raw_text: ABBOTT, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. Crown Agents' office after compet. exam., Oct., 1912; asst. head, finance dept., 1929; dep. head, 1933; asst. sec., E. African currency bd., 1922; sec., 1930.
ABBOTT, CHARLES, Barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn).—Res. Mag., Kenya, Feb., 1930; crown coun., Oct., 1930; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Sept., 1932.
ABDUL HADI, RUHI BEY, O.B.E.—B. 1885; held various offices in the Turkish diplomatic service, 1905-20; admve. offr., Palestine, 1920; asst. sec., 1930.

ABRAHAM, HENRY CHARLES, F.L.S.—B. 1887; ed. Taunton's Schl., Southampton and Southampton Univ. Coll.; survr. prob., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; survr., grade II, topo. br., Apr., 1912; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Sept., 1915; lieut.-July, 1917; asst. aspt., topo. br., Jan., 1919; rel. comm., Mar., 1920; ag. aspt., topo. br., June, 1927; ag. aspt., surveys, Penang Feb., 1928; Empire conf., survey offrs., Southampton and London, July, 1928; sen. asst. supdt., topo. br., Jan., 1929; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Medal, Nov., 1929; senr. asst. supt., topo. br., May to Dec., 1930; do., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., Feb., 1932; supt., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oxon.). M.B.E. (1918).—B.1889; ed. Keswick and Wadham Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nyassaland, 1911; O.C. Details, Fort Johnston; attd. 1st K.A.R., 1914; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1916-13; men. in desps., three times; admin. offr., 1st gde., 1923; ag.asst.ch. sec., Nov., 1926; N. Nyassa native reserves comm., 1928; sp. duty, Tanganyika, Nov., 1929; asst. sec., native affs., Nyassaland, Jan., 1931; asst. ch. sec., July, 1931; chmn. native languages bd., 1931; supt., census, 1931; ag. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; gov.'s dep., July, 1932; senr. prov. commr., Nov. 1933; nom. mem., exec. and leg. couns.

ABRAHAMS, Sir SIDNEY SOLOMON, Kt. Bach. (1936), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1916; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil adminstn., Jan., 1920; advoc.-gen. and regis. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appeal, Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just., Uganda, Feb., 1933; do., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; joint edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1868-1918; sole edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1924.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in desps.; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comnr., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. comnr., northern dist., 1925; comnr., lands, 1927.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in desps., Croix-de-Guerre; appd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clc., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte. on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte. on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-sec., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.

ADAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, D.Sc. (N.Z.), A.I.A. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1870; ed. Christchurch Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll., Otago Boys' High Schl., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1900; ch. trigonometrical computer and dir., tidal survey some years; gov't. astronomer and seismologist since 1911; Martin Kellogg fellow in astronomy, Univ. of California, 1915, and assoc. in astronomy, Yale Univ., 1916.
ADAMS, C. H.—Ast. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comnr., Mar., 1917.
ADAMS, CYRIL OCTAVIUS.—B. 1907; ed. Sherborne; sub.-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1930; 3rd cls. inspr., 1932; ast. comnr., pol., Nigeria, 1935.
ADAMS, F.V., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1904; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; acctnt., Nyasa land, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; ast. comnr., income tax, 1921; senr. ast. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas. in 1921, 1925 and 1927; dep. treas., Oct., 1929; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930; dep. treas., Palestine, 1933; mem., rly. bd., road bd., harbr. bd., cent. town planning comnr., wages comte., 1930; ag. treas., mem., exec. and advisory couns., currency offr., June-Oct., 1934.

ADAMS, JAMES WILMOT, F.R.C.S. (Eng.), B.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab).—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; med. offr., S.S., Feb., 1913; surg., Tan Tock Seng's hosp. and gen. hosp., 1915 to June, 1917; surg., Penang, Aug., 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1929 to Nov., 1929.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1903; ast. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., K Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st ast. sec., 1919; ag. 1st ast. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., 1920; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regar. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II, chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; Br. res. Selangor, Dec., 1933.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confes. of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offr. reps. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibts., Letchworth, 1906, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Gov't. since 1914; hon. mem., Instn. of Mun. and Cty. Engrs. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914; mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, MAJOR WILFRID CARNE, M.C.—B. 1894; sup't., constab., N. Borneo, Dec., 1919; adjt., Apr., 1922; ag. offr. commdg., constab. and offr. mem., leg. coun., May, 1927; commdt. and offr. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1931.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1906; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925-31; vice-pres., educnl. sec., Br. Assn. (centenary meeting).

ADDERLEY, CHARLES J. H., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.I.—B. 1884; Chinese govt. rly., 1890-16; war serv., R.G.A., France, 1916-19; road survr., Nigeria, 1920; exec. engr., 1923; senr. exec. engr., 1926; ag. ast. dir., in 1927-30; ag. D.P.W. in 1927-35; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1927 and 1929; ag. supt., nat. admin. wks., 1931 and 1933; ast. D.P.W., 1934.

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (hons.), 1923; ast. dist. comnr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. ast. sec., elk. of couns., edr., offl. gazette, Mar., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regist., Pemba in 1931 and 1932; dist. offr., Pemba, Apr., 1934; seconded to D.O., June-Oct., 1933; dist. comnr., Pemba, Nov., 1934.
ADDO, J. A., B.A.—B. 1903; 4th cls. teacher, Gold Coast, 1922; 2nd divn. teacher, 1923; cadet inspr., schls., 1927; inspr., schls., 1928.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADSHEAD, PERCY WILLETS, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed. Tettenhall Coll.; R.F.A. (T.F.), 1914-19; France, 1915-19; capt. and adjt., 1916-18; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Uganda, Nov., 1926; mem. cotton enquiry comnr., 1929.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, registr. of sup. ct., e-ceator gen., provost marshal, regar. gen., regar. of titles and legal adviser to govt. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comnr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag.-atty-gen., Dec., 1923 to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925.

AHEARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1896; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S.S., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; ast. asst., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; ast. asst., contrlr., lab., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr., lab., Klang, 1914; dep. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, 1916; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; ast. asst., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; ast. regiar., impts. and expts. (war dept.), 1918; offr., cl. iv., 1919; ast. advr. to govt. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr. lab., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1923; dep. treas., S.S. (cl. ia), 1928; contr., lab., Malaya, 1930; leader S.S. deleg., Simla shipping confce., July, 1931; chmn., S.S. retrenchment comtee., Feb., 1932; leader Malay. an deleg., to govt. of India, New Delhi, as to Indian immigr. to Br. Malaya, Oct., 1933.

AINSLIE, JAMES ROBERT, B.Sc. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry (Oxford), F.L.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at Graeme Walker's (Aberdeen); Aberdeen Univ. and Oxford; probationary ast. cons., forests, Imp. Indian Forest Serv. (Oxford), Aug., 1907; ast. cons., forests, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; on active serv., 1914-17 (Egypt and France); attd. to W.O., timber suppliers depot. (XI Forest Divnl. staff), Aug., 1917; dep. cons., forests, Ceylon, May, 1919; senr. cons., forests, Nigeria, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., forests, Nov., 1928; dir., forests, May, 1929; oh. conserv., forests, 1935; author of "The Physiography of Southern Nigeria and its effect on the Forest Flora of the Country" and "Timber Resources of Nigeria."

AITKEN, ERIC COLOQUHOUN.—B. 1884; sear. gen. regis. office, Jamaica, 1902; ast. treas., 1907; ditto, secretariat, 1911; 2nd cls. clk., 1913; 1st ditto, 1919; conf. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1921; ag. col. sec. on several occasions; audr.-gen., 1932.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Giggleswick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; ast. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puiane judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1925; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929; puiane judge, Gold Coast, 1933; ditto, S. Stltime, 1935.

AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1925; ast. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926; ast. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1928; utilization offr., May, 1934.
AITKEN, W. J., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

AKBAR, MAAS THAJOON.—K.C., B.A., LL.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puiane just., Nov. to Dec., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1927; puiane judge, sup. ct. Apr., 1929.

ALABASTER, CHALONER GRENVILLE, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; confirmed in appt., 1931; unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1925; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1844-1912."
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island commnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade commnr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan., 1918; 2nd grade, 1926; 1st grade, Mar., 1931.

ALBURY, WILTON GRANT, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., June to July and from July, 1925.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific civ. serv., 1915-20; asst. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1927; seconded as lab. offr., 1929; dist. offr., July, 1931.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD—Ast. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct. 1912; lands offr., Nyassaland, July, 1922.

ALLAN, ALBERT, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1893; war serv., 1914-19; station supt. and traffic inspr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1920; ast. sec., G.M.R. office in 1921, 1923, 1925 and 1929; ast. sec., bd. in 1924, 1927 and 1930; divnl. supt. (W.), K. Lumpur, Aug., 1932; do. (Mid.) Germas, Mar., 1934.

ALLAN, PETER.—B. 1886; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), 1910; D.P.H., 1912; M.D., 1920; R.A.M.C., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; med. inspr., Union of S. Africa health dept., 1920; major, S.A.M.C., 1921; med. supt., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1923; seconded to tuberculosis research comtee., 1927-30; reassumed M.S., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1930; ast. health offr., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1935.

ALLARD, JULES.—B. 1889; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotory of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit off., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. comm. and comtee., as ast. in, P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comm., 1903, to West Indian quarantine confce., 1904, and to confce. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comm., 1920.

ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B.1890; ed. Christ Hospital; examr., Exchequer and Audit dept., Nov., 1911; ast. audr., Nigeria, July, 1914; seconded, rly. constrn., Oct., 1922-June, 1929; ast. ast. audr., Nigeria, Nov., 1930.

ALLEN, BERTRAM WELLSWOOD.—B. 1888; ed. Repton and King's Coll., Lond.; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., 1909; ast. asst. supt., pol., Aug., 1914; dep. commr., pol., Muar, Johore, June, 1918; ast. asst., pol., Penang, June, 1920; ast. commr., pol., Kedah, Jan., 1923; supt., pol., S.S., July, 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Oct., 1929; comdt., pol., depot, Singapore, Dec., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Penang, 1932.
ALLEN, CONWAY PHILLIPS, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool)—B. 1901; anaesthetist, F.M.S., July, 1927; med. offr. in ch. offr., in addn., Aug., 1932.
ALLEN, E. M.—Ed. St. Etheldreda Coll., Norwich; temp. mistress, kindergarten schl., Tamale, Gold Coast, 1927; mistress, 1928; inspr., schl., 1933.

ALLEN, EDWARD CUNNINGTON.—B. 1902; elk., secretariat and reporter, legis. coun., Nyasaland, 1925; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930; ag. finan. asst., provl. admin., Feb. to June, 1933 and July, 1934-Mar., 1935.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; engnr. asst., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Ilorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engnr., 1924; nativ. adminstr. engnr., 1925; mech. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1926; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1930; ag. D.P.W. and M.L.C., May to Nov., 1934.

ALLEN, GEORGE VANCE, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.), D.T.M. and H.—B. 1894; asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Feb., 1921; senr. bacteriologist, Sept., 1925; bacteriologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. prin., med. coll., Singapore, June, 1929; prin., Mar., 1930; pres., Raffles Coll. in addn., Apr., 1932; mem. dental bd., S.S., Sept., 1933; mem. pharmacy bd., 1934; offl. del., 9th cong. Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

ALLEN, H.—Cashier and acct., E.A.P., July, 1914; on active serv., E. Africa, 1914-16 and 1917-18; tempy. asst. dist. commnt., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920; asst. treas., Zanzibar, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Uganda, 1935.

ALLEN, H. T., C.M.G. (1838)—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. to concessions and finance comtees., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; asst. sec. to trade relations comte., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comte., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 15th Aug., 1931.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high commr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.

ALLEN, JOHN WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; 2nd class hon. mods., 1925, 3rd litt. hum., 1927; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1932; dipl. O.S., 1933; aspt. educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; offl. examr., Swahili, 1934.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., asst. elk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. req., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., N. Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1931; offr., cls. I B, 1932; do., Kinta, Mar., 1933; comsrr., lands and mines, Johore, Mar., 1934.
ALLEN, PERCY DE VERE.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne, Australia; on mily. serv., E. Africa, Egypt and France, 1914-19; lab. inspr., Kenya, Apr., 1925; lab. offr., Jan., 1929.

ALLEN, COL. SIR STEPHEN SHEPHERD, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1901-04 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in commd., 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in commd., Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; lt. ol. O., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, 1928-31; chmn., transport co-ordination bd., 1934; four times ment. in desps.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-gen.l., N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, W. R., B.A.—B. 1895; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, HERBERT MASSY.—B. 1899; ed. Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; commd., Connaught Rangers, Dec., 1918; transf., King's Own Royal Regt., 1922; ret., 1929; admstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.B.E. (1935), M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. elk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. elk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
ALLISON, HERBERT BRUCC.—B. 1893; ed. Neilson Schi., Paisley and R. Tech. Coll., Glasg.; asst. engnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II, Aug., 1924; do. grade I, Aug., 1928.
ALLISON, J. S.—B. 1879; ed. Glenalmond and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin.); LL.B. (Cape); Capelaw dep., 1903; Union dep. of nat. affrs., 1918; under sec. for native affrs., 1929.

ALLISON, WILLIAM FRANCIS DUDLEY, A.M.I. Mech.E., M.I.Loco. E.M.Inst.T.—B. 1882; ed. Glasgow High Schi. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasg.; N.B.R. Co. and L. and S.W.R., 1906; inspr. engr., crown agts., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1911; seconded as loco. supt., mln. rly., Togoland, 1915; asst. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1918; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ch. mech. engrnr., July, 1930.

ALLNUTT, RICHARD BUSWELL, B.Sc. (Agric.).—B. 1907; ed Malvern Coll. and S. Eastern Agrl. Coll., Wye; B.Sc. (agr.) (Lond.) and dipl., agr., 1928; crop. observn. offr., Norfolk agrl. stat., 1928-30; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.
ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1886; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admstr. offr., Aug., 1922.

ALUWIHARE, RICHARD.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandaganuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landmg survr., cust., 1926; comsrr. requests, Kandy, Nov., 1928; asst. attm. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1931; contr., finance and supply, Mar., 1934.

ALVAPPILLAI, KOVINDAPILLAI.—B. 1905; cadet., Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1929; attd. office ast., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1930; office ast., Galle kach., Mar., 1934.

AMBLEDER, CAPT. GOODMAN, M.C., V.D., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Steyning Grammar Schol., Brighton Grammar Schol. and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; ast. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria Inst., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of capt., Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schol., S'pore, Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; capt., Aug., 1930; transf'd, to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; senr. normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931; headmast., Outram schol., Nov., 1932.

AMBLEDER, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. command., Oct., 1917; Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-inspr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk. exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Western Pacific high commn., May, 1921; ag. ch. clk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1927; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high commn., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., Jan., 1932.

AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office ast. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Dandagamunuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office ast. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 1930; office ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 1932; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932.

AMERY, LIEUT.-COL. L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1890-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Imp. War Coun. at Versailles, and as ast. sec. to the War Cabinet and Imp. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admiralty, Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Cols., 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925 to June, 1929; capt. 14th 1st B'ham Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMERY, WILLIAM BANKEN, C.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Christs Hosp.; ent. cust. and excise dept., 1901; ast. sec. (temp.) war trade dept., 1916; estab. offr., ministry of transport, 1919; finance offr. (with rank of prin.) overseas settlement dept., D.O., 1922; rep. of the U.K. govt. in the Commonwealth of Australia under the Empire Settlement Act, 1925; prin., O.S.D., 1929; temp. attd., D.O., 1932.

AMES, CECIL GERAIT.—B. 1897; ed. Elstree Prep. Schol. and Dover Coll.; (schol.); 5th Somerset Light Infy., 1913-19; Law Soc.'s final exam. (hons.), 1921; Nigerian admstive. serv., 1922; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct., Nov. 1932—July, 1933; admitted, solr., Nov., 1933; attd. judl. dept., Jan.—Mar., 1934; ast. judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.
AMHERST, HON. HUMPHREY WILLIAM.—B. 1903; ed. Charterhouse and R.M.C., Sandhurst; regular army, 43rd Light Infy., 1924-26; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; ast. dist. commr., 1930.

ANDERSON, ADAM, B.E., B.A. (R.U.I.)—B. 1888; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry and Queen's Coll., Belfast; ast. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; ast. engrnr., 1919; dep. port engrnr., port development dept., 1924; exec. engrnr., 1926.

ANDERSON, ADMIRAL SIR DAVID MURRAY, K.C.B. (1930), C.M.G. (1918), M.V.O. (1910).—B. 1874; ed. Stubbington and H.M.S. "Britannia"; served with Brass river expdn. and M'Wele exped., 1906; Ashanti, 1906; Great War, 1914-19, including operns. in Rufiji river, 1915, and with Grand Fleet, 1918; capt. supt., Pembroke dockyard, and senr. naval offr., Milford Haven, 1920-22; rear-admnl., 1922; naval A.D.C. to the King, 1921-22; senr. naval offr., Yangtse river, 1923-25; C. in C., China station, Apr., June, 1925; Africa station, 1927-29; vice-admnl., 1927; high commn., S. Africa, June-Dec., 1928; admnl., 1931; admly. rep., L. of N. perm. advisory commn., 1929-31; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1932; do., New South Wales, 1936.
ANDERSON, E. L. B., O.B.E. (1936).—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1925.

ANDERSON, JAMES HENRY (Lieut. R.A.R.O. Seaforth Hrs.).—B. 1895; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., Nyasaland, 1920; trans'd to secretariat, 1922; senr. clk., 1924; ag. ch. clk., secretariat and edr., Govt. Gazette, 1927; ast. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1928; ag. dep. treas., Mar.-July, 1935.
ANDERSON, THOMAS JOHN, M.A., B.Sc., (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1908.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. Kilmarnock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (France) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, Feb., 1925; ag. supt., accts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; sup't., accts. and stores, Jan., 1928.
ANDRE, WILFRID JOSEPH.—B. 1890; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; mag., St. Andrew, &c., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; mag., Caroni, Dec., 1924.

ANDREWES, LANCELOT RUGGLES.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. regist. in 1932-35; dep. regist., sup. ct., 1935.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—R. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Oxford; (schol., Braesnoose Coll., 1919; Bridgeman Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A., 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, adminstr., Uganda, 1925; ast. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; seconded to secretariat, Uganda, 1929; sec., cotton enquiry commn., 1929; ast. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1935.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll. Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guv's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and ast. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schl. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928; med. inspr. (malaria), 1931.

ANSDELL, GEORGE ARTHUR REES WETTENHALL.—B. 1894; ed. Lisard High Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1926; ag. dist. offr., 1925; seconded as lab. offr., 1928; A.D.C. and P.S. to govt., Apr. to Nov., 1927 and Dec. 1928 to Jan., 1929; dist. offr., 1934.
ANTEBI, SOLOMON, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1895; agril. offr., S. dist., Palestine.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust.; Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rlya., 1925; ag. ch. engrn. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ast. prte. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as prte. sec. to govt., Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.O., 23rd July, 1930.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1938). B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1873; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, Dec., 1880 to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, Dec., 1882 to June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), June, 1885 to Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, Feb. to Aug., 1886; administd. the govt. of St. Helena, Nov., 1889 to June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; prin. clk., 1896; ast. under-secr. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; ret., 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; ast. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling comnr., Nsawam, Oct., 1915, to May, 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ag. dist. comnr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. comnr., 1929; prov. comnr., 1932.

APTHORP, MARMDUKE GWYN, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Malvern and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; clk., atty-gen.'s office, Cape Town, Sept., 1900; clk., survr-gen.'s office, Feb., 1905; clk., treas. and prte. sec. to prime min., Apr., 1909; ag. prin. clk., P.W.D., May, 1910; prin. clk., native affrs. dept., June, 1910; on active serv. with E. African expedy. forces, 1916-17; sg. ch. clk., and ag. undersec. for nativ. affrs., 1917; mag., Lady Frere, May, 1918; do., Kentani, June, 1923; sg. clk., ch. mag.'s office, Umtata, and sec. and treas., Transkeian Territories and Pondoland gen. couns., Dec., 1926; ch. nativ. comnr., Ciskei, Oct., 1928.

ARCHER, CLYDE VERNON HARCOURT, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Cambridge Univ.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; clk. to atty.-gen., Barbados, 1930; ag. solr.-gen., and as pol. mag., dist. "A", for various periods, 1930-34; pol. mag., dist. "B", 1936.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1920), O.M.G. (1913).—Ast. colnr., East Africa Prot., Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. comnr., Apr., 1907; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S. 1918, for surveys in E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admstd. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. comnr., Mar., 1913; comnr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., May, 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimber Berriis, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st class); govt., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; govt. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Sept., 1922; govt., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cls. certif.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, as torpedo lieut., 1916; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1916-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. adm'y., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; apptd. under reconstruction scheme ast. prin., min. of lab., Sept., 1920; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1921; prte. sec. to perm. under sec. for Dom. affrs., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. economic mission to Australia and visited Australia, N.Z. and Canada, 1928-29; prte. sec. to partly. under sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; prin., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec., office of high comnr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., 15th Apr., 1932; ag. high comnr., Sept., 1934 to Jan., 1935.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; recd. prmr. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1905; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and expmlst. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Expmtl. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Expmtl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.
ARDRON, Harold Graham.—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transf'd polit. serv. as ast. dist. comsnr., 1925; ast. col. sec., 1930; dist. comsnr., 1932.

ARGYLE, Sir Stanley Seymour, K.B.E. (1930), M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1887; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1920; ch. sec. in four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1932-Apr., 1935; leader of opposition since Apr., 1935.
ARMSTRONG, Andrew Clarence Francis.—B. 1907; ed. St. Edmunds Schl., Ware and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. comsnr., 1931; admstive. offr., 1932.

ARMSTRONG, Arthur Leopold.—B. 1888; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1915; ast. sec. for native affrs., Jan., 1920; resigned, Oct., 1920; dist. comsnr., and prov. comsnr., Dec., 1921; under sec. for native affrs., Oct., 1922; ast. sec., do., Jan., 1927; ag. sec., do., on various occasions, 1927-34; ag. col. sec. (conjoint), June-Aug., 1930; nom. mem., leg. coun. and mem. exco. coun. for various periods, 1929-32; last ast. col. sec., Mar., 1932; ag. col. sec., May-June, 1933 and in 1935; gov'ts. dep., June, 1933 and Sept., 1935; ag. sec., native affrs. in 1934.
ARMSTRONG, George James.—B. 1901; clk., Basutoland, grade II., 1920; grade I., 1927; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1929.
ARMSTRONG, Captain John Andrew.—B. 1892; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1924; dist. comsnr., 1932.
ARMSTRONG, J. S., M.C., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1891; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

ARNDT, Carl Evan.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Aug., 1922; extra office ast., Kandy kachcheri, Nov., 1923; off. ast., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1926; dep. rubber contr., Apr., 1926; ast. gov't. agt., Kurunegala, Sept., 1928; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1929; ast. attimt. offr., May, 1930; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1931 and July, 1932; admstive. sec., income tax dept., Feb., 1932; ast. gov't. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1933.

ARNOLD, 1st Baron (creat. 1924), Rt. Hon. Sydney Arnold.—B. 1878; was partly priv. sec. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the finan. sec. to the treasy., 1914; lib. M.P. for Holmfirth and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly under sec. for the cols., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1924.

ARNOLD, Leslie William, B.A., B.Sc. (Econ.) (Lond.).—B. 1891; Free Schl., Penang, June, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1915; headmast., govt., eng. schl., Penang, Jan., 1927; do., Hutchings schl., Penang, Jan., 1928; do., high schl., Malacca, Dec., 1930; senr. educn. offr., grade A, 1934; headmast., Free Schl., Penang, Aug., 1934.

ARNOLD, William Eric.—B. 1882; ed. Napier public schl., and Napier high schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1901; dist. man., 1920; senr. clk., actuarial br., 1926; office examr., 1927; acctnt., 1929; sec. and dep. comsnr., 1932; comsnr., 1933.

ARTHUR, James Startin Wills.—B. 1881; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.A.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; cadet, S. S., Oct., 1904; attd. to col. audit office, Feb.-Mar., 1911; offr., cls. IV, May, 1916; seconded as ast. advr., Kedah, May, 1916; ag. temp. as advr. in addn., Feb., Mar., 1917; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1921; dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1922; dep. pres., mun. comsnr., Penang, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Malacca, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., Penang, Jan. 1926; ast. advr., Muar, July, 1927; offr., cls. IB.; ast. advr., Johore, 1930; cls. IB.; ag. sec. for postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1930; cls. IA.; dir.-gen., posts and tels., Malaya, May, 1931; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Feb., 1934; do., Penang, Nov., 1934.

ARUMUGUM, Joseph Nalliah, B.Sc.(Lond.).—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prov., May, 1924; pol. mag., Chilaw, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Tangalle, Jan., 1929; do., Badulla, Nov., 1932.

ASHMORE, Jack Haliburton.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., The King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., Apr., 1919; col. pol. offrs. course, R.I.C., May, 1920; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, Oct., 1920; dist. inspr., July, 1923; travelling mag. and warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., Aug., 1923 to Apr., 1924; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1926; county inspr., 1927; ag. detective inspr., for various periods in 1928-28 and 1930-32; local commitd., pol. in ch. crm. invesgn. dept., Cyprus, Dec., 1932; ag. ch. commdt. pol. and inspr. prisons in 1933, 1934 and 1935; dep. ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Jan., 1936.

ASHTON, Daniel Aspinall.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.

ASHTON, Major-Gen. E. C., C.B., C.M.G., V.D., M.D., C.M.—B. 1873; ed. Trinity Med. Coll., and graduated in med., Trinity Univ., Toronto, 1898; comand., Dufferin Rifles of Canada, 1893; gradually promoted to lieut.-col. cmdgd., 1907; at outbreak of war organised and commanded 36th batt., C.E.F., 1915; col., Canadian Militia, May, 1916; after various commands promoted brig.-gen., Apr., 1917; rtd., Canada to be adjt.-gen., Nov., 1917; major-gen., Jan., 1918; qmr.-gen., Canadian Militia, Aug., 1920; do., defence dept on reorganisation, Jan., 1923; D.O.C., M.D., No. 2, Jan., 1930; do., No. 11, Oct., 1933; ch., gen. staff, dept. of nat. defence, June, 1935; three times ment. in despatches, for war services.
ASHTON, Hugh.—B. 1878; served in S. African War; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; inspr., 1914; served in European War with native labour contingent; ast. comsnr., Basutoland, 1923.

ASHWORTH, Henry Tweedale, M.B.E. (1934), M.M.—B. 1886; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clks. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to O.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1927; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1934.
ASKEW, G. G.—B. 1895; ed. St. Bees Sch., Cumberland; on mily. serv., 1914-19; "1914" Star, ment. in desps.; dep. colliery man., Nigeria, 1924.

ASPINALL, SIR ALGERNON EDWARD, K.T. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comte., since 1898 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; pres. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comte. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comte., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agril. Coll. comte., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comte., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comte., 1923.

ASSER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Joseph John. K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdr. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, ARTHUR VINCENT.—B. 1886; ed. King's Sch., Chester, and Queen's Coll., Oxford open maths. schol.; R.F.A., 1915-18 (M.C.), 1917); cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; collr. land rev., July, 1922; sec. to Br. advr., Kedah, Feb., 1930; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Nov., 1930; attd., land office, Penang, Nov., 1931; collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1932; ag. res. coun., Penang, Apr., 1933.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUCHLAN.—B. 1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry comte., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regar., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists., "A" and "B," Antigua, Oct., 1917; mag. dist. B., Antigua, July, 1924; mag. dists., "A" and "B," 1930.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig. gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; chan. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George (1934); Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, VIVIAN BUCHAN.—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; asst. engnr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; asst. ch. engnr., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbors, 1930.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.
ATTERBURY, WILLIAM ALBERT.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; war serv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engnr., 1926.

AUBER, EGERTON LANCKLOT.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Sch., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supernumy, clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treas., Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., Nov., 1911; co-graded 4th grade clk., 1916; 3rd grade clk., July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., Dec., 1918; ch. clk., treas., Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marine acctnt., Feb., 1927 to July, 1929.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; ast. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric. and regar., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Confce., London, 1920; divn., agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agrl. development, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, Aug., 1925; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to rept. on palm oil industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; del. to W. African agric. confce., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land slmtlt. and various trop. agric. subjects: dir., agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCHINLECK, MAURICE M.—B. 1888; treas. and cust., Antigua, June, 1906; col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1912; clk. to commr. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1915; ag. postmr. and harbrm., Antigua, 1919-20; ch. clk., treas. and cust., Antigua, 1921; ag. treas., Dominica, in 1925; treas. and compt., cust., Antigua, and Fedl. treas., Leeward Is., local chmn., income tax commsrs., Antigua; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Antigua, and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1926-30; served on hurricane relief comte., 1928; J.P. and visiting just. of prison; mem., city commsrs. and road bd., Antigua, 1930; secr. ass't. recr. gen., Gambia, 1931; recr. gen., 1933.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1900-01 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and govt. med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Kenya, 1919; Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Vincent, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B. K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regar. of exchequer et. of Canada, 1887; asst. judge, exchequer et., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.
AUSTEN, HAROLD CHOLMEY MANSFIELD, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1878; tempy. harbr. engr., Mauritius, 1923; gen. man., rlys. and harbr. engr., May, 1931.
AUSTIN, T. A., L.R.C.P. and S.I.L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Liverpool).—B. 1895; ed. Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Dublin; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), May, 1924.

AVICE, TRISTAN, O.B.E. (1929).—Ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; matric., Lond. Univ., 1883; ent. civ. serv. Mauritius, 1893; 6th cls. clk., 1894; 5th ditto, Apr., 1899; 4th ditto, Nov., 1899; 2nd ditto, 1903; ch. clk., 1905; asst. regar.-gen., 1912; regar.-gen., recr. of registrn. dues, comr., mortgages, regar. of companies, 1920; nom. mem., coun. of govt., May, 1926.

AYLESWORTH, HON. SIR ALLEN BRISTOL, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.O., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s comsrs. for settltm. of Alaska boundry, 1903; elected to the H. of O. for North York, 1904; postmr.-gen., of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitr. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYRE, CLAUDE FREDERICK CHARLES—B. 1881; asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, Oct., 1907; lent to F.M.S. govt., inspr., schls., June, 1912; grade II, Jan., 1918; sr. mast., Raffles Inst., Oct., 1915; grade I, and prin. High Schl., Malacca; pol. mag., Aug., 1919; census offr., Mar., 1921; prin., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, May, 1922; sr. normal instr., Nov., 1924.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899; clk. in charge of accts., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 29th Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in accts. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. asst. prin., 16th Aug., 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comttee., 1926; prin., 1st June, 1929.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; apprentice, ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls. and clk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1915; senr. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson, May-Aug., 1922; ch. clk., Apr., 1921; asst. col. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, HENRY FLEMING, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., Exhibr.; asst. mast., Portsmouth arm. schl., 1907-8; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., Aug., 1917; res., July, 1925.

BADCOCK, RICHARD DORIAN, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll.; mil. serv., 1914-19; major, R.A.; asst. ch. of staff, O.E.T.A. (S.), 1919-20; sub-dist. gov't., Palestine, 1920; sec. Near East Conf., Cairo, 1921; adm. offr., Safad, 1924; Nablus, 1926; Jaffa, 1928; dep. dir. dept. of immigr., 1930; asst. dist. commr., S. dist., 1934.

BADDELEY, SIR FRANK MORRISH, K.B.E. (1930), KT. BACH (1928), C.M.G. (1925).—M.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripos, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; recd. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. offr. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency commr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt. govt. monopolies dept., S. Sttlmts., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-sec., S. Sttlmts., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Apr.-Sept., 1922, May-Oct., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925, 1927 and 1928-29; govt. dep. for various periods in 1925, 1926 and 1927; ret., 1930.

BAADER, H.E., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law, dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; sec. expenditure advisory comttee., 1932-33; ag. clk., coun., 1933; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Jan., 1934; ag. asst. col. sec. and pvt. sec. to gov't., 1935; col. sec., Leeward Is., 1936.

BAEZA, JOSHUA ISADORE, M.B., B.Ch. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxon.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1890; jr. res. surgn., Labados gen. hosp., Oct., 1913; med. offr., W. Africa, May, 1914; war serv., 1914-16; med. offr. and ag. bacteriologist, Trinidad, 1919-22; ag. sr. health offr., Penang, June, 1923; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1924; health offr., S'pore, Mar., 1924; sr. health offr., Kedah, June, 1929; ag. state med. and health offr., Pahang, Sept., 1934.

BAGOT, EDWARD.—B. 1892; pol. probnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; asst. commr., pol., Oct., 1916; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1918; adjt., pol. depot, June, 1920; 1st asst. commr., Selangor, June, 1922; asst. commr., pol., Ipoh, Feb., 1925; offr. i/c detectives, Perak, Mar., 1925; asst. commr., pol., def. hqrs., Dec., 1929; ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Apr., 1930; dep. commr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1930; commr., pol., Trengganu, Nov., 1930; do., Johore, Jan., 1933.
BAILWARD, A. N.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affairs dept., 1926; pvt. sec. to ag. gov't., 1927.

BAIN, ARTHUR HERBERT.—Clk., Sept., 1895; examr., deeds, April, 1912; 1st gr. examr., deeds, Oct., 1919; asst. regr., deeds, King Williamstown, Nov., 1919; asst. regr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1926; asst. regr., deeds, Cape Town, Oct., 1928; regr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1932.

BAIN, JAMES, M.C., M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1892; ed. Edin. Univ., European mast., S.S. Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan., 1921; headmast., Geylang English Schl., Jan., 1923; ag. inspr., schls., 1923-28; ag. sup't., educn., Kedah, Apr., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B), Jan., 1932; ag. headmast., Penang Free Schl., Oct., 1933; headmr., Eng. Coll., Bahru, Aug., 1934.
BAIRD, ROBERT DOUGLAS, Lieut., R.N.R.—B. 1905; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and H.M.T.S. "Conway"; merch. serv., 1921-28; marine offr., Kenya, 1929; harbour and marine supt., British Guiana, 1932.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE, M.C., B.A., Oxon.—B. 1885; cadet, S. S., Dec., 1908; attched. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; officiating asst. supt., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating asst. offr., assignee and dep. regar., sup. et., Penang, Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; offr., els. III, Oct., 1920; collr., land rev. and asst. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923; commr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; clk. IB; ag. Br. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; Br. advr., Kelantan, July, 1934.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australasia; clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (conjoint apptnt.), 1908; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (conjoint apptnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1913; dist. commr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra, 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; dist. commr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macuata, 1923; ditto, Rewa, 1923.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Schl.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; commr., R. Artillery, 1916; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; press. Jerusalem dist. ot., Aug., 1919; also press. Beersheba dist. ot.; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1935.
BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London,

BAKER, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; junr. draughtsman, off. aspdnt. civ. eng., H.M. dyd., Rosyth, 1914-15; R.F.C., France, Mesopotamia, Persia, 1916-20; studied Scale-Hayne Agr. Coll., 1920-24; agr. off., Zanzibar, 1925; del., Imp. Agr. Res. Conf., 1927.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MASON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educ. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, Oct., 1921; ag. prin., higher coll., Katsina, 1930 and 1932.

BALE, EDGAR GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. King Edward's Schl.; imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-21; asst. to comsnr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Mar., 1921; dep. comsnr., cust., April, 1923; ag. comsnr., cust., for various periods, 1924-29.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-asst., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inspr., 1927; dep. asst. comsnr., 1930.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1911; attd. to Jaffna Kach., Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov. 1911; attd. to Trincomalee Kach., May, 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; asst. comsnr. excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1926; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1926; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927; asst. judge, Anuradhapura, Oct., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Jaffna, Sept., 1930; dist. judge, Negombo, Oct., 1933.

BALFOUR, STEPHEN FRANCIS, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1930; 1st clk., mag., Kowloon, 1932; asst. head, army dept., Feb., 1933; 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1933; 1st clk. and mag., May, 1935; J.P., 1935.

BALLARD, EDWARD, B.A. (Cantab.), F.R.Entom.Soc.—B. 1888; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; govt. entomologist, Nyasaland, 1911; Indian agric. serv. (govt. entomologist, Madras), 1914; on war serv., 1916-18 (France and Italy); advy. entomologist, Long Ashton research station, 1923; C'wealth of Australia cotton pest research, 1924-28; visited Papua and New Guinea for survey of potential cotton areas, 1925; dir., plant prot. sect., Egypt, 1928-32; ch., Egyptian delegn. to Internat. confce. on plant prot., Rome, 1929; entomologist, Palestine, 1932; author of numerous papers on insect pests from S. India, also from England, Australia, New Guinea and Egypt.

BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acctnt., G.P.O., Kenya, Oct., 1919; ch. acctnt., Kenya-Uganda-Tanganyika post. and tel. service, 1933; senr. dep. P.M.G., do., 1935.

BAMFORD, LIEUT.-COLONEL H.W.M., C.B.E., (1933), O.B.E.(1918); M.C.(1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy., bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defence of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.O., Croix de Guerre avec palme; comsnr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol., G. Coast, Aug., 1924.
BANCROFT, EDWARD NATHANIEL, M.C., M.Am.Soc.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B.1889; lieut., R.E., 1916; France, 1917-18; survey engr., Nigerian rly., July, 1927; survr., gen., Jamaica, May, 1929.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE, M.A.—B. 1888; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A. Barbados, 1925; dep. regiar., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928; pol. mag. and coroner, Barbados, 1931.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUS, O.B.E.(1924).—B.1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advc., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1931; circuit judge, Ashanti and N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1934; puisne judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, July, 1935.
BANTING, WILLIAM VILLIERS.—B. 1903; ed. Haileybury Coll.; chartered acctnt. (England and Wales), 1928; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. DAVID WILLIAMS BENTLEY.—Ed. Durham Univ.; B.A. (1910), M.A. (1913), D.D. (1919); asst. curate, St. John's, Barrow-in-Furness, 1906-10; do., St. James-the-Less, Bethnal Green, 1910-14; vicar, St. Mary's, Plaistow, 1914-16; warden, St. Peter's Coll., Jamaica and examining chaplain to bishop, 1917; canon missioner, 1918; asst. bishop of Jamaica, 1919; bishop of Barbados, 1927.

BARKER, H. P., D.S.C. (1917).—B. 1883; master mariner, 1919; asst. marine supt. mercantile marine; ch. instr., Dublin Sch. of Navig. and Seamanship, 1918; port off., Tanganyika, Oct., 1919; marine supt., 1931.

BARLAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pitlochry High Sch. and Thomson's Acdy., Dundee; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1908; ag. govt. schoolmr., June, 1913; asst. teacher, govt. schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Shetlands, Nov., 1914; junr. clk., C.S.O., Apl., 1915; mag., &c., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. Georgia, Apl., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1920; ag. mag. S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1921-27, ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., May, 1922-Feb. 1923; ag. col. postmr. and local audr., June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, Sept., 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schi. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., 2nd cls. hon.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., Dec., 1911; attd., res. comsnr.'s off., Apr., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, Nov., 1912; ag. treas. and collr. cust., Apl., 1913; passed cadet, Apl., 1914; dep. comsnr., July, 1914; dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; ag. res. comsnr., Apl., 1917; dep. comsnr., Fiji, Mar., 1924; judl. comsnr. (temp. and provisional), Feb., 1928, and in 1934; 1st dist. offr., Apr., 1928; chmn., lab. comsnr., 1928; res. comsnr., Gilbert and Ellie Is. Col., Oct., 1934.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, C.M.G. (1936), B.Sc. (Hons.), F.I.C., A.M.I.Ch.E.—B. 1891; ed. Deacon's Schi., Peterborough, Munic. Coll. of Tech. and Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol.); computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; asst. analyst, Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. dir., chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; agril. chem., Nigeria, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., agr., Zanzibar, June, 1927; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1928; prov. off. mem., leg. coun.; dir., agr., Fiji, July, 1929; off. mem., leg. coun.; chmn., coconut comittee.; chmn., livestock record assn.; dir. agr., 1930; dir., agr. and island chemist, Jamaica, 1933.
BARNES, HAROLD CHARLES VERNON BLACK.—B. 1905; ed. Uppingham Schi.; asst. audnr., Kenya, July, 1928.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; elk. and pay and qrtmtr., 1900; sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917 and Sept., 1925; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. comsnr., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Ba, May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., Jan., 1922; prov. mem., leg. coun., Nov., 1925.

BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hons. (Cantab.)—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1930; seconded to Gold Coast as asst. col. sec., 1934.

BARRETT, ARTHUR LUTTLE.—B. 1882; ent. native affrs. dept., Cape, Feb., 1901; elk., native appl. ct., sec. to several deptl. comittees, and as pvt. sec. to Sir Walter Stanford; prot., natives Kimberley, 1913; prin. elk., ch. native comsnr., Natal, 1920; mag., Ngamakwe, 1926; ch. elk., office of ch. mag., Umtata and sec. and treas., Transkeian Territories gen. coun., 1928; ch. elk., dept. native affrs., Pretoria, 1929; seconded as comsnr. of conciliations, 1930; dir., native lab. (Union) and ch. native comsnr., Witwatersrand, 1932.

BARRETT-LENNARD, SIR FIENNESS CECIL ARTHUR, KT. BACH. (1926)—Served in Anglo-Boer War, but invalided through wounds recd. in action, 1899-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1913; served with local mily. forces and as judge, ct. of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1915 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. cts., S. Stlitms. and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Johore, June, 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Kedah, Dec., 1921; ch. just. and keeper of records, Jamaica, Nov., 1925; ret. 1932.

BARRON, GEORGE DESMOND, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1891; surv. probr., F.M.S., Dec., 1913; survr., grade II, rev. surv. br., Jan., 1915; ditto, topo. br., July, 1915; on mil. serv., Nov., 1916-Apr., 1919; M.C., Oct., 1918; asst. supt., rev. surv. br., Jan., 1919; asst. supt., rev. surv., Kedah, Sept., 1922; do., Johore, Jan., 1925; do., Selangor, Oct., 1929; supt., surveys, F.M.S., and S.S., 1933; do., Kelantan, Dec., 1933.

BARRON, JACOB MAURICE, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. High Schi., Dublin, 1899-1910; sen. modr., nat. sci., 1914, T.C.D.; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; labr. dept., 1915-17; passed in Tamil, 1917; dist. offr., Nibong Tebel, 1917; labr. dept., 1917; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1917; supervisor of cust., P. Swettenham, 1917-20; labr. dept., Apr., 1921; supervm. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; dep. contr., rubber exptr., Jan., 1924; town planning admstr. and valuer-gen., K. Lumpur Jan., 1926 and in Mar., 1927 (in addn.); offr., cls. III; ag. dep. contr. lab., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; offr., cls. II; dep. contr. lab., F.M.S., Nov., 1931; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, Mar., 1933.

BARRON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.A., 1909, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., K. Kubu, Selangor, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, N. Sembilan, Aug., 1920; dist. offr., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; asst. advr., B. Pahat, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; collr., land rev., Seremban and registr., titles, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1929; state valuer and town-planning admstr. in addn.; chmn. sany. bd., Seremban in addn., Mar., 1930; chmn., European unemployment local comttee, N. Sembilan, Oct., 1930; Ist asst. sec.(A), S.S., Nov., 1931; ag. sec. to high comsnr., Apr., 1933; offr., cls. IB, 1934; ag. Br. advr., Kelantan, Feb., 1934; do., Selangor, Jan., 1935.

BARROW, JOHN.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge (B.A., 1926, M.A., 1929) abroad and Univ. Coll., Lond.; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1927; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Mar., 1929; asst. col. sec., June, 1929; pvt. sec. to gov., Nov., 1929 and to O.A.G., Feb., 1930; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1930; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., 1932; 1st elk. magistracy and mag., 1932; asst. col. sec., 1933; asst. supt., impts. and expts., May, 1933.
BARRY, CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.—B. 1878; ed. St. Paul's Schi. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; admitted Inner Temple, 1906; admitted advoc., Pretoria, 1906; judge, sup. ct., 1926.

BARRY, EDWARD, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expdy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1888; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; elk., C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; elk., mag. dept., 1901; asst. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; ch. elk., master's office, Cape (insolv. broh.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sur. ct., Natal, 1915; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadhams Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; registr. and prin. registr. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comsn., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1906; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920; ret. 1934.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MUNNAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914—Jan., 1920; admistr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; asst. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; asst. admstr. gen. and asst. registr. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; res. mag., Oct. 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., ag. registr.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. registr., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. registr., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926; asst. judge, high ct., Nigeria, 1934.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; B.A., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; passed cadet, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., Jan., 1912 passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1912; asst. registr., impts. and expts., S.S., Dec., 1915; sec., food control comttee., June, 1917; mem., shipping comttee., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr.'s comttee., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by S. of S. Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comn., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comttee. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Apr., 1922; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926; offr. cls. IB comn., lands, S.S., Dec., 1926; cls. IA; under-sec. S.S., 1930; ag. col. sec., S.S., May to June and Aug. to Sept., 1930; pres., mun. comnrs., Singapore, Mar., 1931; supernmy. offr., cls. IA, S.S., 1932; supernmy. staff offr., S.S., July, 1934.

BARTOLO, PROF. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH. (1929), LL.D., B. Lit., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; ed. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn. Malta branch, Empire Parly. Assocn.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univ. of Empire in gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migrn., Aug., 1927; judge, 1934; author of several works on Maltese hist. and Imp. affra.

BARTON, A. E. V., C.B.E. (1936), O.B.E. (1933).—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. asst., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. cust., Apr., 1923; comprtr. cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust. and excise, Trinidad, 1930.

BARTON, C. J. J.T., O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law tripose; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; asst. dist. comnrs., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comnrs., 1917, 1919, 1921; asst. for native affrs., 1923; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1924-26; ag. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. on various occasions, 1928-33; senr. asst. col. sec., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., 1934; col. sec., Fiji, 1935; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CHARLES MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons. classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; asst. dist. comnrs., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comnrs. to inquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of comnrs. to inquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vety. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929; puisne judge, 1934.

BARTON, GEORGE SAMUEL HORACE, C.M.G. (1935), B.S.A., D.So.A.—B. 1883; ed. Vankleek Hill High Schl., Ontario Agril. Coll., Toronto Univ. (B.S.A.), Laval Univ. (D.Sc.A.); asst. animal husbandry, 1907; lecturer, 1910; prof., 1911; dean, faculty of agr., Macdonald Coll., McGill Univ., 1926; comdr., Agricole Merite, Quebec gov't., 1929; dep. min., agr., Canada, 1932.
BARTON, LANGLLOT ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; asct. treasy., Hong Kong, 1921; cashier, 1924.

BARTON, MAJOR PERCIVAL FULLER, O.B.R. (1935), V.D.—Asst. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; attd. col. sec.'s office, Oct. - Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. asst. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in depts., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; treas., Dominica, 1932; treas. and collr., cust., St. Helena, Mar., 1933.
BARTON, T., M.A.—B. 1904; qualified teacher Scottish educn. dept.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Oct., 1928.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.). Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem. of H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. Senator, 1925; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; asst. stimlt. offr., July, 1923; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1933; comm., agrl. marketing, Jan., 1935.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.C., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa, 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909-13; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., with jurisdiction as a judl. comnrs. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. comnrs. in addn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., Fiji, May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., for various periods, 1924-33; ag. solr.-gen., July to Oct., 1927; puisne judge, Apr., 1934.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.R. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audt. office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transf. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; asst. dir., prisons, Apr., 1926; is also comnrs. of oath for Union; sp. grade supt., Pretoria cent. prison and Pretoria gaol and outstations and warden, Baviaanspoort farm col. and inebriate reformatory, Sept., 1930; do., Johannesburg gaol and outstations and warden, Leeuwkop farm col., Jan. 1934.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1919), B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Sch., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 1st cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1905; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comttee., 1912; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adml. commg. ap. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; asst. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; polit. sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of York during his visit to Australia and N.Z., 1927; asst. under sec. of state for dom. affrs. 25th Jan., 1930; dep. sec., Imp. Confce., 1930; regiar., Order of St. Michael and St. George since 1930.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; addl. to Colombo cust., May, 1920; addl. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1921; office asst., Badulla kach., Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panatuna, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1923; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1927; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., May, 1928; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1928; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32; col. sec., Cyprus, 1934; ag. gov., Mar. and June-Oct., 1935.

BAVERSTOCK, WILLIAM CHARLES BEAT, R. of O., K.R.R.C.—Ed. Wimborne Grammar Schl. and Milton Abbas Grammar Schl., Blandford; overseer, Pacific Phosphate Co. (Br. Phosphate Coms.), 1905; served, Great War, Queen's Own Dorset Yeomanry, Dorset Regt. and K.R.R.C., Aug., 1914 - July, 1919; min. of munitions, 1919; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., W. P. high comm., 1920.

BAVIN, HON. Sir THOMAS RAINFORD, K.C.M.G. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called to bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania, 1900; sec. to prime min. C. of A., 1901-4; M.L.A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and col. treasurer, N.S.W., 1927-30.

BAXTER, JOHN BARINGTON MACAULEY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of cust. in Dom. govt., 1921; prime min., New Brunswick, 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.B., 1931; ch. just., N.B., 1935.

BAYLES, HERBERT LAURENCE.—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; clk., C.A.'s office, 1905; clk., cust. serv., Nyasaland, 1909; P.W.D., 1910; Nyasaland Field Force, 1914; asst. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; asst. treas., Zanzibar, 1921; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924; treas., Trinidad and chmn., agrl. bank, May, 1931; treas., and currency offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; treas., and currency offr., Nigeria, 1935.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1860; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; clk. to commr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901; priv. sec. and clk. to adminstr. Dominica, Aug., 1909; transf. to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. 2nd cl. clk., National Health Insurance Commsn. (England), June, 1912; 1st ols. clk., May, 1913; priv. sec. to sec., ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917; sec., National Health Insurance Joint Comttee., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to parl'y. sec., ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920 to Mar., 1921; asptg. clk., Min. of Health, Oct., (1920); sec., "Discovery" comttee., C.O., July, 1923; col. sec., Leeward Is., Oct., 1929; admistr. govt. of Antigua on numerous occasions; ag. gov., Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1930-34; admistr., St. Lucia, June, 1935.

BAYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PERCIVAL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; clerical asst., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., postoffice, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. clk. to admistr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and aspt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to admistr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr.-Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offl. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica on three occasions in 1929, 1930 and 1931; comsnr., Montserrat, 1932.

BAZELL, CHARLES.—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. mods., 2nd lit. hum.); asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Spore, 1914-17 and 1922; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangar, 1923.

BEARDMORE, ERNEST.—B. 1882; ed. Manchester and Capetown; B.A. (1907); LL.B. (1911); clk., Cape civ. serv., 1900; prof. asst. and ch. clk., solr.-gen., Cape, 1920; solr.-gen., Cape, 1931; crown pros., Witwatersrand, 1933; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1935.
