BEATLEY, FRANK EDWARD MAMERTUS.—B. 1882; ed. Lucton Grammar Schl.; traffic supt., Mauritius govt. rly., 1914; dep. traffic man., Sierra Leone rly., Jan., 1925; traffic man., 1930; gen. man. and traffic man., 1933.

BEATTIE, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (res. of offrs.)—Entd. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Highlands), 1906; transf. on promotion to the Queen's (now The Queen's Royal) Regt., 1908; served in Aden and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; asst. adjutant, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regt. adjutant, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjutant, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in desps., 1914; M.C., 1915; severely wounded, 1915; asst. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offg. staff offr., K.A.R., 1915; promoted major by brevet, 1918; tempy. lieut. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-depl. comttees, 1915-21; ret. with rank of lieut.-col., Sept., 1922; reed. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; asst. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; res. inspr. of schla., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec., bd. of educm. (ex officio), 1922; local secy., Cambridge Univ. exams. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1925; ag. gov. from May, 1926 to Apr., 1927; ch. asst. col. sec., Cyprus, 8th Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-29; col. sec., Gibraltar, Jan., 1930.
BEATTIE, JAMES, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1918-20; M.C., 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commnr., 1922; comdt., pol., Oct., 1928.

BEATTIE, SIR JOHN CARRUTHERS, K.T. (1920), F.R.S.E. and S.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Ed. and Wits.).—B. 1866; ed. Moray House, Edinburgh Univ.; Vans Dunlop schl. in Physics, 1851 Exhib. Sohl., Univ. of Munich, Vienna, Berlin and Glasgow; prof. of physics, S. African Coll., 1897; medallist S. African Assocon. for Advancement of Science, 1910; crossed Africa from Cape Town to Cairo with Prof. J. T. Morrison on magnetic survey; chmn., survey comm., 1921; pres., S. African Assocon. for Advancement of Science, 1928; prin., S. African Coll., 1917-18; prin. and vice-chmn., Univ. of Cape Town since 1918; prin. publs.: rept. of a Magnetic Survey of S. Africa, Cambridge, 1909, and (in conjunction with Prof. J. T. Morrison) "On Magnetic Work in Southern and Central Africa," Washington, 1913.

BEATTY, SIR KENNETH JAMES, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's med. and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. pros., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. asst. res. mag., Mar., 1903; asst. res. mag., Dec., 1905; attd., Natal Carbineers, Natal rebellion, 1906; recd. thanks of Natal govt., med. and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and regis.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on mil'y serv., 1915-18; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; dep. gov. in 1928.; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1931.

BEAUBRUN, IGNATIUS GECIL, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1889; treas., clk., St. Lucia, 1906; asst. audit clk., 1907; rev. offr., St. Vincent, 1912; ag. audit clk., 1910 and 1912; rev. offr., Grenada, 1915; 3rd treas. clk., 1916; ch. clk., treasy. and commr. of income tax, 1922; asst. treas., 1929; col. treas., collr. of cust., supt. of indl. rev., ch. of excise, Grenada, June, 1930; ag. col. sec. and regis.-gen., June, 1932.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th EARL (creat. 1815), VISCT. ELMLEY (1815), BARON BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); WILLIAM LYDON; B. 1872; succeed'd his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNET.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls. math. hons., 1910; headmnr., gram. schl. and sub-inspr., schls., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr., schls., Leeward Is., July-Oct., 1916; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; asst. educn., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1926.

BEAUCHESENE, ARTHUR, M.A., K.C., LL.D., Litt.D., F.R.S.C.—B.1876; ed. St. Joseph's (class. course), Memramcook, N.B.; grad. as B.A., M.A. and Litt.D. valedictorian, 1896; studied law, Laval, Montreal; admitted to bar, Quebec, 1904; K.O., 1914; legal adviser, justice dept., 1913-16; clk. asst., House of Commons, Canada, and commr. to admstr. oath of allegiance to M. of P.; elk., House of Commons, 1925; author of "Beauchesne's Parliamentary Rules and Forms," "Eorivains d'Autrefois," etc.

BECK, DAVID MACDONALD HERBERT, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; temp. lieut., W.A.F.F., Feb., 1917; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1919; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., Aug., 1934.

BECK, EWALD EGERTON HERBERT.—B. 1894; ed. Weymouth Coll., Dorset; pol. probtnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; 1st assnt. commr. pol., Selangor, Feb., 1923; supt., convict estabmt., Taiping and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1923; offr. i/c. pol. dist., Kuantan, Aug., 1927; comdt. pol., depot., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929; sup't., pol., S.S., Oct., 1931; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Mods., 1912; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd ols. clk., C.O., 30th Nov., 1914; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st ols. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. Indies currency comte., 1923; sent to Mauritius as spec. commr. to enquire into and rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1925; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925; ag. asst. sec., 28th Sept., 1931.

BECKETT, HERBERT JAMES RUST, A.C.A.—B. 1885; treas., N. Borneo, Feb., 1911; dist. treas., Apl., 1911; mag., 1st cls., Apl., 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1916-19; dist. offr., Aug., 1919; asst. commr., cust. and excise, Mar., 1921; asst. commr., excise, Feb., 1926; auditor, Mar., 1929; finance contr., Dec., 1930.

BEDINGTON, CYRIL A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., M.I.M. & Cy.E., E.S.I., M.R.San.I., Chtd. Civ. Engrn. and Chtd. Survr.—B. 1889; ed. Univ. of Bristol; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1913; exec. engrn., Ipoh, 1922; ag. senr. exec. engrn., Kuantan, 1927; addt. ch. hydrc. engrn.'s office, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engrn., waterworks, K. Lumpur, Oct., 1930; ar. exec. engrn., F.M.S. & S.S., Klang, 1932; sr. exec. engrn., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1934.

BEECHING, HERBERT.—Seconded from Basutoland service; apptd. ag. sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., Dec., 1920; ag. asst. res. mag., 1921; ag. res. mag., 1923; passed 3rd grade Secwana exam., 1926; sub. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., July, 1927; hon. rank inspr., Nov., 1928.
BEBGIE, RALPH STOCKMAN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—Health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; ag. malarologist, 1931; asst. bacteriologist, 1932; ag. bacteriologist, 1934.
BEKKER, SIMON POTGIETER.—B. 1882; solr., 1906; M.P.C., 1928; mem., ex. co., 1929-33; M.P., 1933-34; admtr., Transvaal Prov., 1934.

BELCHER, SIR CHARLES FREDERIC, KT.Bach. (1931), O.B.E. (1923), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ed. at Geelong Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practice, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodian of enemy property, Uganda, Apr., 1915; mag., Uganda, June, 1916; asst. judge, Zanzibar, June, 1920; judge of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Aug., 1920 and June, 1925; atty. gen., Nyasaland, Sept., 1920; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, high ct., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922; judge, high ct., May, 1924; ch. just., sup. ct., Cyprus, May, 1927; ch. just., Trinidad, 1930.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, K.T. BACH. (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st honos. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp. Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. advsr. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, LILLIE JOSEPHINE, M.B., B.Ch., certif. L.S.T.M.—Lady med. offr., B. Gajah, May, 1921; Ipoh, July, 1921; K. Pilah, July, 1923; Taiping, Apr., 1925; K. Kangsar, June, 1925; lady med. offr., infant welfare centre, Taiping, Aug., 1925; Seremban, July, 1926; K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; Ipoh, Nov., 1927.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jena Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rifles Brig., 1883; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; (ment. in desps., Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. comsnr., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkerstroom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary coman., 1902; sec. in office of high coman. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial sec., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; asst. Imperial sec., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water coman., 1909.
BENZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1899; ed. Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919-25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BERENDSEN, CARL AUGUST, LL.M.—B. 1890; ed. Australian pub. schla., Gore Dist. High Schl. (N.Z.) and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. educ. dept., 1906; lab. dept., 1916; chf. clk., 1917; dep. registr., industrial unions, 1919; Imperial affrs. offr., prime min.'s dept., 1926; perm. head, prime min.'s dept., 1935; also sec. for external affrs., from 1928.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LEO, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bndry. comsn., 1908 (recd. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bndry. coman., 1911; capt., R. of O.; asst. dist. comanr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1912; dist. comanr., March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sen. asst. sec. to gov't. of Tanganyika Territory, Apl., 1921; ag. asst. ch. sec., Sept., 1922 to May, 1923, Dec., 1923 to March, 1924; ag. prov. comanr., Apr., 1930 to May, 1931 and Feb., 1932-Mar., 1934; dep. prov. comanr., Feb., 1931; mem. leg. coun., 1931; prov. comanr., Sept., 1934.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as clk. H. M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfld., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfild., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland savings bank, 1911.

BERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH. (1916); K.C. (1913).—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I, 1890; ditto, part II, 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tancred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admtd. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1903; ag. ch. justice, Sep.-Nov., 1906; chrmn. of coman. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to govt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1925; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BESSBOROUGH, 9TH EARL OF (creat. 1739), Rt. Hon. VERA BRABAZON PONSONBY, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1919), B.A. (Cantab.), B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks. I.Y., 1905-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1915; served in Great War, 1914-18; L.C.O., 1907-10; M.P., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., Co. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1931-35.

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1856; vice-pres. of the exec. coun., Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deakin govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, SIR THOMAS ALKAXDER VAN, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medalist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa admin., 1896; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. comanr., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. res. 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Imp. Educn. Confce, 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1915, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930; ret., 1933.
BETANCOURT, NELSON.—B. 1887; sworn survr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, April, 1909; 1st inspr., mines, Apr., 1921; ag. inspr., mines, May to Sept., 1927 and Mar., 1933.

BETHEL, CHARLES PERCIVAL, I.S.O. (1929).—B. 1891; ag. 4th clk., post office, Bahamas, Apr. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Out islands, Feb., 1910; 3rd clk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ch. clk., C.S.O., Aug., 1919; enlisted for active serv., 1915; 2nd lieut., Mar., 1919; served in France; twice wounded; discharged from Army, Apr., 1919; resumed duties, May, 1919; ag. clk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, Aug. to Sept., 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1923-34; ag. A.D.C. to admstr., Sept. to Nov., 1925; asst. col. sec., July, 1928; ag. col., sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., May, 1934 to Sept., 1935.

BETHELL, WILLIS H.—B. 1875; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1897; customs offr., Mar., 1901; ag. chief clk., audit dept., June, 1912; chief clk., audit dept., Apr., 1913; ag. auditor of pub. acts, for various periods between 1916 and 1923; audr., Dec., 1925; audr., pub. acts, 1st Dec., 1925.

BETTINGTON, DIGBY ROWLAND, ALBEMARLE.—Ed., at United Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; served with Natal Mounted Pol., 1898-1901; with 2nd Kitchener's Fighting Scouts, 1901-1902 (dangerously wounded, ment. in despr., Queen's med. and 5 clasps); inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, 1903; inspr., pol., Sierra Leone, 1906; ag. comsnr., pol. and sheriff on several occasions; dep. comsnr. of pol. and dep.-supt., prisons, Gold Coast, 1910; base comdt., Togoland Field Force, 1914; "1914-15" Star, War and Victory med.; title changed to dep. inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, 1915; inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Gold Coast, 1917; inspr.-gen., pol., Gold Coast on separation of depts. of pol. and prisons, 1920; King's Police med., 1920; ret., 1924; comdt., pol., Bahamas, 1924; resigned, 1926; chief of pol., Bermuda, 1927.

BEVIR, ANTHONY, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Herford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th Batt. King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in despr.; asst. prin., C.O., 9th Feb., 1921; accompanied Mr. Ormsby-Gore on his visit to the West African Colonies, Jan. May, 1926; prte. sec. to Mr. Ormsby-Gore, Feb., 1926; prin., 1st Apr., 1929; sec., C.O. confec., 1930.
BEWES, GEORGE PRICE HOPK.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
BEYERS, LT.-COL. LEONARD.—B. 1894; perm. staff, Union defence forces, 1914-32; dir., prisons, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1932.

BHARUCHA, NAVROJI MANEKJI.—B. 1890; B.A., Cantab. cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1914; office ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1914; attd. Kurunegala Kach., June, 1914; extra office ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., in addn. to his own duties, Aug., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; office ast., govt. agt., E.Prov., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1917; Balapitiya, Nov., 1918; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, May, 1922; asst. sttlmt. offr., May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Jan., 1927; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1932.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; apptd. after compet. exam clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1903; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 4th Sept., 1929.

BIDEN, CLAUDE ERIC DE BEER.—B. 1887; ed., Radley Coll.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1911; on active serv., attd. Nigerian Overseas Contgt., German E. Africa, and W. African Serv. Brig., 1917-19; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1930; mem., rly. retrenchment comtce., 1931; ag. audr., May, 1932; audr., Palestine and Trans-Jordan, May, 1933.
BIDMEAD, KENNETH ANDREW.—B. 1909; pol. probnr., Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; asst. sup., pol., Aug., 1932; attd. Punjab pol., Jan.-Oct., 1935.

BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—B. 1889; ed., Dean Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; head of training coll., Oct., 1921; ch. inspr., educn., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1929; ag. asst. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1931, 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. dir., educn., Nigeria, 1934.

BIGELOW, HENRY VEDDER, K.C., B.A., LL.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Truro, N.S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S. 1896, practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; K.C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., Feb., 1918.

BIGG, WILFRED JOSEPH.—B. 1897; boy clk., G.P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1922; asst. prin., 1st Aug., 1925; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 3rd Feb., 1930; prin., 22nd June, 1931.

BIRCH, HUGH MASSEY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. privately and King's Coll., Dublin; trooper, M.M. Pol., 1897; exec. engr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1907; local capt., E.A. Forces, 1916-18; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1918; B.S.A. Coy.'s, Br. War and Victory meda.
BIRCH, W. C.—Asst. analyst, E.A.P., July, 1914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRCH, WILLIAM SOMERSHIT, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1889; ed., King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; served R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1914-19; sen. asst. med. offr., Jamaica ment. hosp., 1923-24; med. supt., do., 1925-26; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Barbados, 1927; med. supt., do., 1928; ag. ch. med. offr. for various periods, 1933-34.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed., Xaverian Coll.; asst. ch., cust., Zanzibar Prot., Oct., 1914; ag. ch., cust., Jan. to Aug., 1915, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917, and Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zan- zibar (in Prize), Jan. to Aug., 1915 and from Dec., 1917; immigr. offr., Sept., 1915; dep. comptr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1920; ag. comptr., cust., on various occasions, 1921-29; dep. comptr., cust., Nigeria, 1930; comptr., Nov., 1931.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed., Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; war serv., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; asst. dist. judge, S'pore, Aug., 1922; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, July, 1925; asst. advr., B. Pahat, Aug., 1928; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1929; ag. stamp and prob. offr., S'pore, Aug., 1933; ag. dep. treas., S.S., May, 1934; offr., cls II., Nov., 1934.

BIRKINSHAW, FRANK.—B. 1887; asst. agrl. supt. and sc. mast., gram. schl., St. Vincent, Aug., 1912; agrl. instr., Mauritius, Jan., 1915; inspr., co-op. credit socs. in addn., Oct., 1915; agrl. supt., Sept., 1919; asst. agrl. inspr., Perak, F.M.S., June, 1920; agrl. field offr., Perak North, Dec., 1924; ditto P. Wellesley and Penang in addn., Apr., 1926; sr. agrl. field offr., Perak, Sept., 1931.

BIRKITT, FRANK.—B. 1885; Imp. postal serv. (tel.) 1898-1902, mails, 1903-07; admstve. (cler. and acctg.) 1908; staff record offr., 1909; postmr., Nyasaland Prot. (various offices) 1912; (active serv.), 1914-15, N.V.R., Ntnh. Rhodn. Forces), ag. asst. P.M.G. various occasions; 1st cl. clk., 1920; ch. acct. and stkpr., 1923; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1927; temp. M.L.C. in 1932-34; ch. acct., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory postal serv., 1935.

BIRSE, ARTHUR LOUIS.—B. 1893; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; colr., income tax, S'pore, Sept., 1921; treas., exec. cmtee., Br. Malaya, Br. Emp. Exhibn., in addn., Jan., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff Concession, Kubub, Mar., 1926; offr.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers' Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agr. Coll., 1906-15; sec., economic and development comm., Canada, 1915-16; coms., agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., soldier stint., bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigrn. and colonization, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Canadian National Rly., London, 1923; ch. colonisation dept., C.N. rly., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKABY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (London)—B. 1902; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; house surg., R. Infirmary, Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg. offr., dist. infirmary, Aahton-under-Lyne, 1924; house surg., London Lock Hosp., 1925; ast. med. offr., Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. spec., in 1929 and 1930; med. offr., E.A.M.S., Nyasaland, 1931.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Stonehurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; lat. of lat. chs., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; called to Irish Bar, 1912; crown counsel, E.A.P., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1923-31; mem. comm. on riots, S.E. Provs., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1933; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1936.
BLACKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—B. 1906; ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; R. Australian Navy, 1924-30 (paymr.-lieut.); ret., 1930; corrs. elk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931; ag. acct., Jan.-June, 1933.

BLACKWELL, CAPT. FRANCES.—B. 1885; Impl. P.O., 1901-13; ast. supt., posts and tels., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on mil'y. serv., 1914-21; personal ast. to dir., posts and tels., F.M.S., Feb., 1921; supt., posts and tels., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; contrlr., ditto, Jan., 1929; contrlr., Perak and Dindings, Nov., 1929; ag. contrlr., posts and tels., S'pore., Nov., 1930; contr., do., Nov., 1932; ag. dir., posts and ag. sec., Malayan postal bd., June, 1934.

BLACKWELL, KENNETH RAY.—B. 1896; ed. Shebbear Coll., N. Devon; war serv., R.F.A., July, 1915-Nov., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; att'd chd., Pahang secr., Apr., 1921; ast. supt., chandu monop., Ipoh, June, 1922; supvr., cust., Johore, Aug., 1923; offr. cls. V, ast. dist. offr., Gopang and Lampar, Nov., 1923; passed inter. L.A.A., Dec., 1925; ast. dist. offr. Kroh and ast. supvr., cust., Jan., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Nov., 1927; ag. supt., convict estabtnt., F.M.S., in addn., Feb., 1928; offr. cls. III, Nov., 1932; state treas., Perak, Feb., 1934; chmn., sany. bd., Larut in addn., Oct.-Nov., 1934.
BLACKWOOD, A. MC.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—B. 1886; lieut., R.A.M.C., May, 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Jan., Nov., 1932.

BLAINE, CHARLES HERBERT.—Ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, and Dover Coll., (Eng.); served, S.A. War, 1901-02; Great War, 1914-18; elk., ot., Christiana, Transvaal, civ. serv., 1902; pub. pros., mag.'s office, Johannesburg, 1906; 1st clk. to A.R.M., Germiston, Jan., 1908; ditto and ast. mag., Krugersdorp, 1909; ditto, Barberton, 1912; senr. clk. and senr. pub. pros., Johannesburg, 1919; addnl. mag., Roodepoort, 1920; ditto, Durban, 1923; ditto, Johannesburg, 1924; 1st gde. mag. and native commnr., MARIO at Zeerust, 1929; senr. gde. mag., Zoutpansberg at Louis Trichardt and native commnr., 1931; under sec., justice, Pretoria, July, 1934.

BLAIR, HON. ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Wellington Coll., (N.Z.), and Canterbury and Victoria Univ. Colls.; judges associate, 1893-98; admitted barrister and solr., 1899; experience in legal offices, 1898-1910; in practice, 1910-28; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928.
BLAIR, HAROLD ARTHUR.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; ast. dist. commnr., Nov., 1928.

BLAIR, HON. SIR JAMES WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1936), KT. BACH. (1930)—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.I.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1902-15; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministries, 1903-7, also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instrn., Denham ministry, 1912-15; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1925.

BLAIR, ROBERT WESTERUP, A.R.C.S.C.I., F.I.O.—B. 1885; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edin., R. Coll. Sc., Dublin; ast. chem., Imp. Inst., 1906-06; ast. chem., Metro. Water Bd., Lond., 1906-11; ast. chem., Wellcome Trop. Resch. Lab., Khartoum, Sudan, 1911-12; ast. chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., 1912; chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., 1913; 2nd lieut., East Lancs. Regt., 1915; tempy. lieut., 1916; sp. brigade R.E., 1916; ment. in desp., 1918; ag. capt., R.E., 1918; chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Dec., 1922; 1st lieut., July, 1924; extra A.D.C. to high commnr., Nov., 1924; ch. chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., Mar., 1927; capt., M.S.V.R., May, 1927; hon. A.D.C. to high commnr., Mar., 1930.

BLAKE, GEORGE STANFIELD, B.So. Geol. Lond., A.R.S.M. Lond., M.I.M.M.—B. 1876; ed. R. Schl. of Mines, London; on teaching staff, 1896-97; imp. Inst. (mineral survey of crown cols.), 1901; Newfoundland and Canada, 1909-15; munitions inspect., Eng., 1916-19; geol. staff, Imp. Coll. of Sci., 1920-22; geol. advr., Palestine govt., 1922.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; elk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. regiar.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. elk., regiar.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; commr. of sup. ct., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regiar.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAND, CHARLES HERBERT.—B. 1886; ed. pub. and high schl., Pembroke, Ont., and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.A. (1907); ent. Canadian div. serv. at first compet. exam., 1909; served with Can. Field arty., 1917-19; ast. sec., civ. serv. commr., 1919; ch. examr., 1921; civ. ser. commr., 1933.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Indian Army, R. of O., Apr., 1915; active serv., N.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned Indian pol., Jan., 1919; dist. supt., pol., 1920-24; ch. of international pol., Tangier, July, 1922; ast. to res. commnr., New Hebrides, Sept., 1929; ag. res. commnr., New Hebrides, in 1930, 1931, 1934 and 1935; ast. C.O., May, 1933.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1895 to 1902; conf. elk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. elk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. elk. to high commr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; ast. sec. to high commr., 1912; sec. to high commr., 1919; ag. high commr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); mily. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; ast. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comm. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; ast. prin., C.O., 1924; prte. sec. to permit under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; pvtc. sec. to partly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

BLEDISLOE, 1ST BARON (cr. 1918) RT. HON. SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926); K.B.E., (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agrl. Coll., Cirencester (gold medalist and late chmn. of govs.); chanoery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comm. on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. comm., land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930-35.

BLONDIN, LIEUT. COL. THE HON. PIERRE EDOUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, C.M.G., (1934), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-19; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; astd. to Colombo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradhapura Kach., Mar., 1920; ag. office astd., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1920; astd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office astd., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th ast. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regiar. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admstd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1934; ag. gov., Oct.-Nov., 1934; gov.'s dep. on several occasions, 1935.

BLOUNT, AUSTIN ERNEST, C.M.G.—B. 1870; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Chas. Tupper, Bart., 1896-1901; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Robt. L. Borden, prime min. of Canada, 1901-1917; elk. of parlms. and elk. of senate, 1918.

BLOXHAM, CAPT. HUGH FITZGERBERT.—B. 1893; ed. Bradford Coll.; 4th batt., Wiltsire Regt., 1914-15; 102nd K.E.O. Grenadiers, I.A., 1915-24; served, Mesopotamia, 1915-19 and Afghan War, 1919-20; twice ment. in desp.; ast. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ast. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ag. supt., prisons, Apr., 1926; ag. ast. supt., pol., Mar., 1926; ag. supt., prisons, Mar., 1929; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Oct., 1930; prison supt., S.S., 1932.

BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chemy.; B.A., 1910; 1st cls. hons., Latin, 2nd cls. hons., math.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl., ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-26; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BLUNT, DENZIL LATTON, M.A. (Cantab.)—B., 1891; ed. Shrewsbury and King's Coll., Camb.; served under court of wards, Madras, 1913-14; served H.M. Forces, 1915-19; lieut., R.A.S.C.; senr. agrl. offr., Kenya, 1926; asst. to dir., agr. Kenya, 1930; dir., agr., Cyprus, Aug., 1935.

BLYTHE, WILFRED LAWSON.—B., 1896; ed. Birkenhead Inst. and L'pool Univ.; war serv., 1916-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1933.
BOAST, HENRY WALTER—B., 1887; clk., Swaziland, 1909; regiar. and mast., sup. ct. and sheriff, 1928; dep. astt. commr., 1930.

BODENSTEIN, HELGARD DEWALD JOHANNES LL.D. (Leyden), barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—Clk. mag.'s offices, Krugersdorp, Lydenburg, Pietersburg, 1909; Piet Retief, 1910; sec., judge pres., J. de Villiers, 1910-12; ch. clk. and professional astt. to atty.-gen., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1912; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Amsterdam, Aug., 1912; asst. edr., "Die Burger," 1920; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1922-1927; sec. to prime min. and for external affrs., 1927.

BODILLY, COMMDR. RALPH BURLAND, R.N.—B., 1884; ed. Bradford Coll. and H.M.S. "Britannia," 1899; midshipman, China station, 1901; sub. lieut., Atlantic Fleet, 1905; lieut., Home Fleet, Jan., 1907; invalided, 1909; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; on war serv., Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; lieut. commdr., Jan., 1915; promoted commdr. for war services, Nov., 1918; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct. on various occasions, 1927-31; acted in appellate ct. on various occasions, 1928-31; pres., marine and water cts.; ch. mag., Palestine, Oct., 1932; author of "Inter-action between Vessels."

BODKIN, GILBERT EDWIN, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B., 1886; ed. at Repton Sch., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Cambridge Univ. diploma of agric., 1910; govt. economic biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agrl. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; rep. Br. Guiana at W. Indian Agrl. Conf., 1913; astt. dir. of sci. and agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; joint editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; govt. entomologist, Palestine, June, 1922; offr.-in-ch., locust campaigns, 1928-30; pres., Internat. Locust Bureau, Damascus, 1930-31; dir., agr., prin., coll. of agr. and regis., co-op. credit socs., Mauritius, 1932; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana and Palestine, scientific repts. to govt., etc.

BOILEAU, LEWIS HAROLD.—B., 1888; ed. Monkton Combe Sch., Bath and Garrick Chambers (Schoones); astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. prin., astt. sec., Apr., 1923; ag. prin., astt. sec., S. Prov., in 1925, 1926 and 1927; admstr. offr., cls. I, Aug., 1930; prin., astt. sec., Dec., 1930.

BOISSIER, CAPT. JOHN SCOBELL, B.Sc. (hons.), M.Inst.W.E.—B., 1894; ed. Exeter Sch., and Birmingham Univ.; astt. engrn., F.M.S., Aug., 1929; sec. for ser. in Johore, Jan., 1924-Sept., 1929; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Aug., 1929; exec. engrn., rural, S'pore., Nov., 1934.

BOK, DR. WILLEM EDUARD, JUNR.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. to prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; law advsr., Feb., 1916; sec. for just., Aug., 1918; judge, highct., S.W. Africa, Feb., 1930; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa, Griqualand West local divn., 1933.

BOLAND, FREDERICK MALCOLM, LL.B.—Called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mag., County of St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1926; mag., eastern dist., Jan., 1927; astt. mag., St. George West, 1933; mag., Victoria, 1934.

BOLTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Dipl.—B., 1897; ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.); B.A. (hons. chem.), 1920; dipl., educn., 1921; lab. astt., H.M. facty., Sandycroft, 1916; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1923; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BONAVIA, WALTER JOSEPH.—B., 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-1916; clk., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1917; astt. polit. offr. (cadet), Oct., 1920; admstr. offr., 2nd grade, Oct., 1923; ag. ditto, 1st grade, Feb. and Nov., 1923 to Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., in 1926, 1928, 1929-31; dist. offr., Oct., 1931.
BOND, MAJOR B. W., M.C.—Astt. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

BOND, RALPH NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab.)—B., 1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; astt. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office astt., Badulla kach., Jan., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1930; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1931; do., Kandy, June, 1933; do., Kurunegala, Dec., 1934.

BONE, MAJOR CHANDOS ERIC, M.C., F.S.I., L.S. (Vict.).—B., 1889; survr., 1st grade, rev. surveys bch., Perak, Oct., 1913; lieut., B.F.A., May, 1916; capt., Apr., 1918; major, Aug., 1918; astt. supt., rev. surveys Selangor, Nov., 1920; supt., surveys, Trengganu, May, 1925; astt. supt., surveys, S'pore., Sept., 1927; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., 1933; ag. astt. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., May, 1934.

BONE, MAJOR THOMAS, O.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B., 1879; London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1924; 13 yrs. arm.; 3 yrs. A.D.V.S. army divn.; O.C., vety. hosp.; senr. vety. offr., remount depot; dir., vety. dept., Mozambique Territory; govt. vety. surg., Br. Guiana, 1928.

BONHAM-CARTER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR CHARLES, K.C.B. (1935), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917).—B., 1876; ed. Clifton Coll., R.M.C., Sandhurst and Staff Coll.; served S. African war, 1900-01 (Queen's med., 4 clasps); European war, 1914-18; (D.S.O., C.M.G., bvt. col., officer, Legion of Honour, American D.S.M.); maj.-gen., 1926; dir., staff duties, W.O., 1927-31; comdr., 4th divn., 1931-33; lieut.-gen., 1933; dir.-gen., Territorial army, 1933; gov. and comdr.-inch., Malta, 1936.

BONYUN, E. C. B.—B., 1885; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados and privately; copyist, pol. mag.'s courts, Dist. "A," Barbados, Jan., 1903; 2nd clk., registn. off., Barbados, Sept., 1906; ag. dep. registr., from Jan. to Feb., and Sept. to Nov., 1910; ch. clk., registn. off., Nov., 1913; supervisor of customs, Nigeria, July, 1914; astt.

BOOCOCK, JOHN JAMES.—Enlisted in Cape Mounted Rifles, 1897; forester, Transkeian conservancy, Cape, 1903; supt. of plantations, 1904; ast. dist. forest offr., Umtata, Feb., 1906; Butterworth, Dec., 1908; Kambi, Oct., 1909; Umtata, Apr., 1912; dist. forest offr., Kologo, Oct., 1914; conservator of forests, Natal Conservancy, May, 1920; served with Cape Mounted Rifles during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; served with S. African Heavy Arty., Apr., 1917 to June, 1919; conservator, Eastern conservancy, 1926; do., Western conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., W. inspectorate, 1932.

BOON, HERBERT M.B.E. (1935).—Copyist, treas. dept., St. Kitts, May, 1908 to Mar., 1910; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Apr., 1913; ch. clk., post office, St. Kitts, May, 1915; clk. to admstr. and clk. exec. and leg. couns., Decr., 1924; has held various ag. appts.; treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1935.

BOOTH, LEONARD HORACE VERTUE.—B. 1898; ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and R. Naval Coll., Dartmouth; European War, 1914-18; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Dec., 1919; ast. supt., pol., Feb., 1922; ag. ast. habmr., Apr., 1922 and Feb., 1926; supt., pol., 1931; ast. dir., criminal intelligence, 1931; ag. dir., do., 1932, 1934 and 1936.
BOOTH, PEBBY, N.D.A., U.D.A.—Senr. supervisor, agrl. dept., Kenya, May, 1922.
BOOTHE, R. W.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1925; staff offr., 1929.

BORDEN, Rt. Hon. Sir ROBERT LAIRD, P.C. (1912), G.C.M.G. (1914), K.C., LL.D.—B., 1854; ed. Acadia Villa Acad., Horton, N.S.; called to the bar, 1878; head of firm Borden, Ritchie and Chisholm, Halifax; pres. Nova Scotia Barristers' Society, ten years; Q.C., 1900; LL.D., Queen's and St. Francis-Xavier Univs.; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g. e. 1896; re-elec., 1900; defeated g. e. 1904; re-elec., bye-elec., 1905, and g. e. 1908, 1911 and 1917; leader opposition in H. of C. 1901-1911; president of the King's privy council for Canada and prime minister, Oct., 1911; prime minister and S. of S. for external affairs; mem. of the Peace Conference, Versailles, 1919, as representative of the Dominion of Canada; resigned premiership, 10th July, 1920; Canadian rep. to Armaments Conf., Washington, 1921; chan., Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont., 1924.

BORGH, ANTHONY J., M.D., Malta.—B. 1889; medical offr., Fiji, 1916; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Ra., 1917; ag. R.M.O., med. supt., pub. lunatic asylum and visiting med. offr., Suva gaol, 1919; D.M.O., Nadroga, 1920; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Nadi, 1921; ditto, Levuka, 1923; ditto, Labasa, 1924; D.M.O. and dist. comsrr., Ra (conjoint), 1929.

BOSMAN, ANDREW MURRAY.—B. 1888; ed. Rendrecht High Sch., Pretoria; Ontario Agrl. Coll., Univ. of Toronto; lectr., animal husbandry, Potchefstroom Schl. of Agric. 1913-15; vice princ., 1915-17; prof. animal husbandry and dean, faculty of agric., Univ. of Pretoria, 1917-33; ch., divn. of agric. educn. and extension, dept. of agr. and forestry, and prof., animal husbandry, Univ. of Pretoria, 1934.
BOSTOCK, H. K.—B. 1892; ed. Repton; L. & N.W. Ry., Crewe, 1909; on milly. serv., 1914-19; divn. supt., Nigerian ry., 1928; supt., motive power and equipment, 1934.

BOSTON, HUGH MERRICK.—B. 1893; ed. Wallacey H.E. Schl., New Brighton, Liverpool Univ., M.B. Ch.B. (Liv.), 1925; served with 2nd W. Lancs. (98th County Palatine) Field Ambulance (T.F.), 1914-19; med. offr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Apr., 1928.

BOTHA, CHRISTIAN LAURENS.—B. 1870; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein and at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Amsterdam and K.C., 1919; puisne judge, Orange Free State divn. of sup. ct., S. Africa, 1925.

BOTHA, COLIN GRAHAM, V.D., M.A.—B. 1883; ent. atty.-gen.'s dept., Cape Colony, 1901; serv. in offices of civ. comsrr., Cape, and mast. of sup. ct.; in charge, archives of Cape Col., 1912; ch. archivist, Union of S. Africa, 1919; accompanied Sir Reginald Blankenburg as pte. sec. to first assembly of League of Nations, Geneva; hon. or corresponding mem. of several hist., lit. and genealogical socs. of Gt. Britain and Holland; contributed articles to various jnl., mags. and newspapers; author of "Place Names in the Cape District," 1917; "A Brief Guide to the Documents in the Cape Archives, 1852-1806," 1918; "The French Refugees at the Cape," 1919; rept. of tour to various archives in Europe, Canada and U.S.A., 1921; "Social Life in the Cape Colony in the 18th Century," 1927; "Place Names in the Cape Province," 1927; "Public Archives of South Africa, 1662-1910," 1928.

BOTHA, MARTINUS CHRISTOFFEL.—B. 1886; ed. George and Stellenbosch Univ.; ent. dept. of posts and tels., 1902; teacher, 1906; B.A. (Cape), 1914; M.A. (U.C.T.), 1919; int. in Afrikaans, Univ. of Cape Town, 1918; prof. of Afrikaans, ditto, 1921; supt.-gen., educn., Prov. of Cape of Good Hope, Sept., 1929; secy. for Educn., July, 1934.

BOTHA, PHILIP RUDOLPH.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll. (Stellenbosch) and Univ. of Amsterdam; journalist; univ. lecturer; economist, bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa 1927; trade comsrr. and comm. sec., U.S.A., 1929; trade comsrr., Canada, 1933; under-seo., depts. of prime min. and external affs., 1934.

BOTHAMLEY, CHARLES MILDMAY.—B. 1891; ed. Terrace Schl. and Wellington Boys' Coll., N.Z.; comte., clk., N.Z. H. of R., 1917; legis. coun., 1919; 2nd clk. ast., legis. coun., 1924; dep. clk. of parlmts. and clk. ast., legis. coun., 1931; clk. of parlmts., clk., legis. coun., and examr., petitions for pte. bills, 1934.
BOTTOMLEY, JOHN HUBERT, A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1899; ed. The Salt Schla., Shipley, Bradford Schl. of Art, and Bradford Tech. Coll.; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; lieut., H.K.V.D.C.

BOTTOMLEY, SIR WILLIAM CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comtee., 1905; sec. to inter-deptl. pensions comtee., 1907; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 16th Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, 1st Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Lucas, 24th Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 26th Oct., 1911; 1st cls. clk., 1913; principal clk., 8th Dec., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., E. African Currency Bd., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 1927.
BOUCAUD, JOSEPH ERROL.—B. 1904; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad; admitted solr., 1927; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, 1933; dep. registr. and marshal, Jan., 1935.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; 1st grade admnve. offr., Jan., 1924; prov. commnr., 1934.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1934), K.B.E. (1931), C.M.G. (1924).—Ed. Tonbridge and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mag., United Provs., India, Nov., 1908; under-sec. to gov't., Mar., 1913; registr., high ct. Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), Mar., 1918; judl. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; settlement offr., Baghdad, Mar., 1919; polit. offr. Baquba, Oct., 1920; polit. sec. to high commnr., Jan., 1921; dep. sec. to high commnr., Dec., 1921; sec. to high commnr., Jan., 1922; col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1929; O.A.G., Apl. to Sept., 1930; ch. sec., July, 1931; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Uganda, 1932; do., Nigeria, 1935.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDOWALL.—B. 1886; examiner, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1906; asst. auditor, N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; asst. audr., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1920; audr., Somaliland Prot., July, 1921; audr., B. Guiana, Oct., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1927; audr., Sierra Leone, 1930.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALBETT, KNT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen. and escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comm. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst., C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902; ret., 1912.

BOVELL-JONES, LIONEL CLIFFORD.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; served in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; demobil., 1919; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, July, 1920; dist. inspr., 1924; offr.-in-ch., punitive expedit. to Venezuelan bndry., 1925; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1927; county inspr., 1931.

BOWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912, also depts. of col. sec. and registr.-gen.; 3rd cls. clk., atty.-gen's off., 1915; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1918; clk. to registr.-gen., 1919; secy., riot inquiry commn., 1919; ag. registr.-gen. and cust. empty ppty. and contr., L.C.O., 1920; secy., yellow fever inquiry commn., 1921; ag. registr.-gen., 1921, 1923 and 1925; registr.-gen., registr., lands titles, supt., vice-adm'y. and bankey, cts., companies, patents, births, marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp comsrs., dir., pub. offr. guarantee fund; comsrs., income tax, 1927; sec. in arbitrn. between govt. of U.S.A. and Guatemala, 1930; dist. comsrs., Belize, offr., recr. in bankruptcy, offr., admstr., estates, coroner, 1933; ag. ast. col. sec., 1934; postmr. (in addn.), 1935.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean; E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high commnr. for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high commnr., 1894-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; adminstd. govt. on various occasions, 1900-09; ret. 1910.

BOWERLEY, WALTER, C.B.E., (1931), B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripods, 1899); apptd. clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. Depk., Nov., 1901; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1902; ag. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local audr. in 1903, 1904, 1905 and of amalgamated territories, Lagos-S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. local audr., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1907, to Mar., 1908; local audr., Gambia, Apr., 1908; local audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1909; title changed to audr., 1910.

BOWMAN, HUMPHREY ERNEST, C.M.G. (1936), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. mod. hist. 1902); B.A., 1902; M.A., 1906; ent. Egyptian miny. of educn., 1903; seconded to Sudan govt. (dept. of educn.), 1911-13; dir., Egyptian students in U.K., 1913-14; serv. with Br. Army, 1914-20; B.E.F., France, 1915-16; staff capt., H.Q., N. Command, 1916-18; India, 1918; Mesopotamia, 1918-20; ret. with rank of major, 1920; seconded from M.E.F. as dir. of educn., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; seconded to govt. of Palestine, 1920-23; dir. of educn., Palestine, 1920; mem., advisory coun. since 1920; chmn., comm. on crafts and industries, 1922; mem., transliterat. comtce., 1923; chmn., comtce. on transliterat. of geographical names, 1924; chmn., bd. of higher studies.

BOWRING, ARCHIBALD GEORGE.—Ed. Nottingham High Schl. and Nottingham Univ. Coll.; capt., Sherwood Foresters, 1914-19; miny. of lab., Nov., 1919 to Nov., 1920; col. audit dept., Nov., 1920; asst. audr., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; do., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1930; ag. audr., May to June, 1930.

BOWRING, SIR CHARLES CALVERT, K.C.M.G. (1925), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B. C. Africa, Sept., 1895; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., June, 1899; treas., E. A. P., Oct., 1901; mem., exec. and leg. coms., E. A. P., 1907; ch. sec. to gov't., E. A. P., Apr., 1911; ag. gov't., Feb. to Oct., 1912, Aug. to Oct., 1913, Apl., 1917 to Jan., 1919, and from Nov., 1919; gov', Nyasaland, 1923-1924.
BOWRING, HON. SIR EDGAR RENNIE, K.C.M.G. (1934), KT.BACH. (1915).—B. 1858; mem. legis. coun., Newfoundland, 1897; high commnr. for Newfoundland in London, 1919.

BOWYER, ERIC BLACKLOCK.—B. 1902; ed. Whitehill Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; open scholarship, 1920; B.Sc. (with distinction), 1923; G. A. Clarke schol., 1923; M.A. (1st cls. hons. in maths. and nat. philosophy), 1924; ast. inspr., taxes, 1925; appt. to C.O. as ast. prin. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; joint sec., col. survey comte., 1927; seconded for serv. with col. development comte., 1st Oct., 1930; pvt. sec. to parly. under-sec. of State for Dom. affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 22nd June, 1931; do. (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald), 3rd Sept., 1931; prin. 6th March, 1935.

BOYCE, E. A., B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1898; astt. engnr., P.W.D., Uganda Prot., 1925; astt. engnr. in charge Mbale dist., 1926; ditto, N. Prov., 1927; dep. D.P.W., Bahamas, Aug., 1928; ag. D.P.W. in 1930, 1932 and 1935.

BOYD, EDMUND BLAIKIE, C.M.G. (1935). —B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schol. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; apptd. by civ. ser. comsnr. under reconstrn. scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; astt. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., May to Sept., 1921; sec. interdeptl. comte., income tax in cols., 1922; priv. sec. to parly. under-sec. (Mr. Wood, now Viscount Halifax), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec. Imp. Inst. comte. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin. 2nd Nov., 1925; sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec., col. development advisory comte., 1929; pvt. sec. to Lord Passfield as S. of S. for Dom. affrs. and for theCols., 1st Apr., 1930; pvt. sec. to successive secs. of state for theCols. (Mr. J. H. Thomas, 26th Aug., 1931, Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister (now Lord Swinton), 9th Nov., 1931, Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, 7th June, 1935, Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Nov., 1935).

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1883; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1906 to Dec., 1911; postal astt., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. astt., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; acctnt., post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. dep. P.M.G. Jan.-Sept., 1927; ag. P.M.G., Sept.-Dec., 1927; col. postmr. and supt. tels., Fiji, Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1929; nom. comsnr., currency, Feb., 1930; P.M.G., 1930.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A.(1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on war serv., 1915-19; astt. contrlr., labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; astt. dist. offr., Mar., 1923; regisr., co-op. socities, May, 1923; astt. offr. in ch., co-op. socs., S.S., Nov., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; astt. dir., co-opern. F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1927; on ap. deputation to India, Nov., 1928; ag. dir., co-opern., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1930 and Apr., 1933; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dir., co-opern. S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

BOYD, WILLIAM RIDER.—B. 1887; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Dublin Univ.; B.A., Dublin; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1914; astt. dist. offr., May, 1915; dist. offr., July, 1920; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1923; ag. astt. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, May, 1928; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1928; 1st astt. sec. (B), S.S., Mar., 1929; sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addn., Apr.-May, 1931; regisr.-gen., statistcs, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932; pres., munici. comsnr., Penang, Apr., 1932; dist. offr., L. Perak, Feb., 1934.

BOYD-SHANNON, GODFREY ECULESTON.— B. 1907; ed. Wellington and Cambridge; schol., St. John's Coll., 1926; 1st cls. class. tripes, pt. I., 1928, pt. II., 1929; Browne med., 1928; B.A., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., D.O., 27th Oct., 1930.

BOYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served apprenticeship as engnr. at R. & W. Hawthorns & Leslie, St. Peters, nr. Newcastle-on-Tyne; came to S. Africa, 1903; worked as a fitter in the Natal harbr. wks. and also in the S.A. rly. workshops, Durban; stood unsuccessfully as off. cand. for S. African Labour Party for the Greyville divn. of Durban at the first Union parly. elections, 1910; contested by-elect. for same constit., 1912; re-elec., 1915-20, 21 and 24; dep. leader of the S. African Labour Party in parly.; chmn., parly. labour caucus; national organiser of party; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks. in Hertzog cabinet, June, 1924; min. of lab., 1926-29; nominated senator, Sept., 1929.
BOYES, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Prince Albert Coll., Auckland; joined N.Z. pensions dept., 1903; regiur. pensions, 1910; astt. comsnr., 1919; comsnr., 1929.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Stiltns., 1893; resig., 1896; astt. treas., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comsnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admtn., May, 1901 to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; astt. sec., Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comsnr., E.Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comsnr., Jan., 1905; ag. dep. comsnr., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; admstgt. govt., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V.H., O.B.E. (1933), M.B.E. (1920).— B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; astt. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on sp. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. as finan. sec. to high comsnr., Iraq., Mar.-Dec., 1924; acct., 1st Sept., 1930; acct. to exec. coun., Imp. Agrl. Bureau and Oversea Mech. Transp. Coun.; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932.
BRACE, IVOR LLWELLYN, B.L.—B. 1898; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1930; ditto, Nigeria, 1931; crown coun.,

BRADBURN, COL. WILLIAM ERNEST HORATIO. —B. 1884; served, Imp. Yeomanry, 1901; S. African Constab., Jan., 1902 to Oct., 1907; 8th King's Royal Hussars, Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 1st King Edward's Horse, King's Oversea Dominions Regt. (adjutant), Apr., 1917 to July, 1919; local commdt., Cyprus mil. pol., Oct., 1919; mem., prison bd., Mar., 1921; passed exams. in mod. Greek, 1921 and 1923; gov., Athalassa juvenile prison, Dec., 1922 to June, 1924 in admn.; ag. ch. commdt., pol., July to Oct., 1923 and June to Oct., 1926; twice ag. A.D.C. to high comsnr., 1924; dep. ch. commdt., pol., Aug., 1924; ag. dist. comsnr., Aug., 1924; hon. A.D.C. to high comsnr., Oct., 1924; nom. offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1926; insprn.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1927; ag. insprn. prisons in addn., May, 1927 to Nov., 1927; nom. offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928 to June, 1930; offl. mem., exec. coun., May to Dec., 1929; King's pol. med., 1932; insprn., prisons in addn., July, 1933.

BRADFORD, SIR JOHN ROSE, Bart. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S. —B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schlr.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.

BRADLEY, BENJAMIN GERALD.—B. 1899; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and R. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, June, 1932; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. asst. contrlr., lab., July, 1922; passed cadet, June, 1923; offr. cls. V., asst. dist. offr., Sitiawan, Nov., 1924; offr. cls. IV., Nov., 1928; offtcg. dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; ag. asst. audr., sup. ct., Ipoh, Aug., 1932; offr. cls. III., Nov., 1933.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.—B. 1901; ed. Wellington Coll.; clk., col. audit dept., June, 1925; asst. audr., Hongkong, Sept., 1925; do., Uganda, 1936.

BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAS, O.B.E. (1933), L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.; M.D. (Brux.)—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late asst. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; asst. med. offr., Seychelles, Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd divs., South Mahé; med. sup., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr., 1924.

BRADNEY, GEORGE PRESTON.—B. 1877; ed. at Oratory Schl., Edgbaston, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; hons. in hist., 1899; col. audit offr., June, 1901; asst. audr., B.C. Africa, Feb. 1992; asst. auditor, East Africa Prot., Sept., 1904; auditor, Fiji, and Western Pacific Protectorates, 1909; dir. of external audit, S. Sttltns. and F.M.S., Oct., 1914; ag. aud.-gen., S. Sttltns., in addition, Jan. to Feb., 1916; audr.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1919; pres., comsn. to enquire into finan. position, Singapore municipality, Sept., 1921; mem., comsn. to enquire into organization and expend. of govt. depts., F.M.S., etc., Feb., 1922; ag. finan. ad. and treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; auditor, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932; comm., Malayan expenditure comtce., June, 1934.

BRADSTREET, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1884; ont. C.A.'s office, June, 1902; asst. head, checking and insurance dept., May, 1919; do., gen. dept., Jan., 1924; sec., Zambesi Bdg. interdeptl. comtce., 1928-31; dep. head, finance dept., Mar., 1931; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. clk., 1933; head, appts. and passages dept., 1935.

BRADY, Lieut.-Col. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., Feb., 1904; inspr. of schls., Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-20; commanded 4th and 18th batts., K.R.R.C.; ment. in desig.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; ag. dir. of educ., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.

BRAGG, ALGERNON.—B. 1890; col. audit dept., Apr., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915; mily. audt., E. African Expdy. Force, 1916-19; asst. audr., Uganda, 9th May, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Sept., 1924; ag. audr., Uganda, for various periods, 1926-27; prin. asst. audr., Nov., 1928; ag. audr., May to Nov., 1929 and Mar.-Nov., 1933; dep. audr., Kenya, 1931; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917.

BRAITHWAITE, ELDERD CURBURN, M.B., B.S. (Dunelm), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H., B.Hy. (Dunelm), M.S. (Dunelm), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1885; ed. St. Bees Sch., Cumberland, Durham Univ. and Univ. of Durham Coll. of Med.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., S. Nigeria, 1911; served in Cameroons campaign, 1914-16; temp. capt., Army (gen. list), 1914-16; specialist, Nigeria, Apr., 1925.

BRANCH, Sir CHARLES ERNEST ST. J., KT., BACH. (1924), B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1893 to Jan., 1898; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 2 Sept., 1902; chancellor of the Diocese of Antigua, 1903-1909; pres., Antigua defence reserve force, 1903; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1902; apptd. to combined offices of col. sec. and atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., Jan., 1903; deleg. te agric. confocs., Jamaica, 1907; Barbados, 1908; Canadian reciprocity confoc., Barbados, 1908; cotton confoc., Liverpool, 1908; comnr., to prepare a new and revised edition of laws of Leeward Islds., Feb., 1909; admst. gvt. of Antigua on various occasions in the years 1902 to 1909; ag. gov., Leeward Islda., Nov.-Dec., 1907, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Oct., 1909; deleg. to Canada on banana tax question, 1915; deleg. to Indent. Emigrn. Confoc., Trinidad, 1916; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, Apl., 1919; rep. of Jamaica at Canada-W. Indies Trade Comtce., May-June, 1920; puisne judge, sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1921; judl. comnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1922; ch. just., Jamaica, 1923; ch. just., Ceylon, 3rd July, 1925; ret., 1926.
BRANCH, J. R. A.—B. 1894; ed. Antigua Grammar Sch. and McGill Univ.; served in great war (3 medts.); sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., 1927-30; inspr., pol., 1931.

BRANCH, STANLEY, M.B.—Ch.B. (hons.), Edin., 1898; D.T.M., Liverpool, 1907; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1899; Uganda, 1900; St. Vincent, 1903; house surg., St. Lucia, 1904; D.M.O., St. Lucia, 1912; seconded as dir. under Internat. Health Bd. campaign for eradication of hookworm disease, St. Lucia, 1914-24; ch. med. and health offr., St. Vincent, 1925.
BRANCO, ANTHONY NICHOLAS.—B. 1884; clk. secretariat, Cyprus, 1907; asst. regist. and interp., dist. ct., 1913; examr., accts., 1926; local asst. audr., 1927; ag. audr. for various periods, 1928-34.
BRANDON, KENNETH REVIE.—B. 1888; clk. of cts., Jamaica, 1919; ag. regist., sup. ct., Apr. to Dec., 1921; ag. res. mag., Oct. and Nov., 1932 and Nov., 1924 to Aug., 1925; res. mag., Sept., 1925.

BRANIGAN, PATRICK FRANCIS, Barrister at law (Gray's Inn and King's Inn, Dublin).—B. 1906; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Downing Coll., Cambridge (T.A.S. Course); B.A. (T.C.D.), 1928, 1st cls. hons. (gold med.) legal and polit. science; 1st Victoria prizeman, and cert. of hon. at call to bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1927; Eastern circ., 1928; admstv. offr. (cadet), Kenya, July, 1931; ag. crown coun., Kenya, 1931 to 1934; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934; addt., legal advisory staff, D.O., and C.O., Dec., 1934 to Jan., 1935.
BRANSON, VICTOR CECIL, M.C., A.R.C.S.C., D.I.C., B.Sc., A.I.C.—B. 1896; 1st asst. analyst, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. analyst, 1929 and 1932; analyst, Sept., 1932.

BRASHER, WILLIAM KENNETH, B.A., M.I.E.E.—B. 1897; ed. Clifton Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; R.E. (signals) 1915-19; France; engr., Marconi's W.T.Co., 1920; asst. engr., P.O., Br. Guiana, 1922; ch. engr. and elec. inspr., 1928; exec. engr., P. and T., Iraq, 1929; ch. engr., P. and T., Palestine, 1933.

BRASNETT, NORMAN VINCENT, M.A., Dipl. For. (Cantab.)—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schl. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A., 1914-18 (capt., despa.); asst. cons., forests, Kenya Col., 1920; senr. A.C.F., Kenya, 1928; cons., forests, Uganda, 1929.

BRASSEY-EDWARDS, H.—Veterinary offr., E.A.P., Feb., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., 1919; senr. vet. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; ag. dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924; dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924; ag. ch. vet. offr., 1927; ch. vety. offr., 1929; dep. dir. agr. (animal industry), 1931.

BRAWN, ALAN O.—B. 1878; ed. "Queen Mary's" gram. schl., Walsall, and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea (1st cl. Queen's scholarship); teachers' certif. (Bd. of Educ., England); matric. Lond. Univ., 1898; asst. mast., diocesan schl., Hong Kong, 1901-1909; jun. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1910; lecturer, Tech. Inst., Hong Kong, 1910-1912; headmaster, Wantzai dist. schl., 1913; headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1914; headmaster, Wantzai schl., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., 1917; 2nd lieut., 1917; 1st lieut., 1918; served in France, 1917 to 1919; demobilised, 1919; dep. reg.ist., Hong Kong Univ., 1919; headmaster, Wantzai schl., 1919, headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1919; asst. mast., cls. II., 1920; headmr., Victoria Br. schl., 1920; asst. mast., cls. I., 1921; headmr., Wantzai schl., 1921; ditto, Indian schl., 1921; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. and Wantzai schl., 1922; headmr., Wantzai schl., 1923; headmr., Yaumati schl., 1924-28; dir., tech. inst., 1930; inspr., English schl., 1933.

BRAYSHAY, SIDNEY, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1885; ed. Ripon Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; tech. instr., tech. inst., P.W.D., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1914; lieut., R.E., Sept., 1918; exec. engr., Apr., 1920; exec. engr., i/c, tech. schl., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1928; ag. sr. exec. engr., hydraulics, Selangor, June, 1930; exec. engr., Seremban, Mar., 1932; ag. senr. exec. engr., Muar, Jan., 1933.

BRAZEL, MAJOR CLAUDE HAMILTON, M.C., R.E. (T.), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1894; temp. 2nd lieut., R.E., 1914; S.M.E., Chatham, Apr.-June, 1915; lieut., 1915; adjt., R.E., 1916-17; capt., 1917; major, 1917-19; ment. in despa., 1917; Belgian Croix de Guerre, 1917; M.C., 1918; "1914-15" Star, G.S. and Victory med.; France, 1915-19; major, R.E. (T.), 1920; Nat. Defence Force, Apr.-July, 1921; dir., elec. dept., Bahamas, 1926; ch. engr. and man., elec. undertakings, Ceylon, 1934.

BREARLEY, L. J., M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1901; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; staff offr., 7th Apr., 1933.

BREENER, GEORGE GUSTAV RADLOFF, M.A. (Edin.), LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1883; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; pvt. sec. to Gen. Botha, 1916-19, and attended Paris Peace Conf., 1919; pvt. sec. to Gen. Smuts, 1919-21; asst. crown pros., Johannesburg, 1922; 1st prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1924-27; law adviser to crown, Jan., 1927.

BREEN, MICHAEL JAMES.—B. 1884; ed. at Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin: B.A., math., science (honours) in Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1905; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1907; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. regist.-gen., 1911; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1911; ag. supt., impts. and expts., 1913; asst P.M.G., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; asst. asst. col. sec., 1914 to 1916; on mil. serv., 1916-19; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1920 to 1921; asst. treas., 1921; P.M.G., 1924; ag. col. treas., 1929 and 1933; chmn., retrenchment comittee, July, 1930; chmn., economic comittee, 1934; offi. M.I.C., 1935.
BRENNAN, HON. THOMAS CORNELIUS, K.C., LL.D.—Chosen by Victorian Parl., to fill vacancy in senate, Commonwealth of Australia, May, 1931; el. to senate, Dec., 1931; asst. min., Lyons miny., since 1934.
BRETT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseas settl. office, 1919.
BREWIS, HELEN ELIZABETH STUART.—B. 1904; ed. Sherborne Schl. for Girls, Dorset, Froebel Educln. Inst. Training Coll. for Teachers, Roehampton; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1927.

BRIDGE, BLACKE FRANK, B.A. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1930.—B. 1891; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; asst. contr. of lab., June, 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Bentong, Apr., 1922; dep. contr. lab., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; dep. pb. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, June, 1929; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1930; offr., cls. II., 1931; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Nov., 1932.

BRIDGES, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Tom, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—B. 1872; Grenadier Guards; served S. African War, 1899-1901; Somaliland, 1902-4; European War, 1914-16; mil. attache at the Hague, Brussels, Copenhagen and Christiania, 1910-14; lieut.-col., 4th Hussars, 1914-16; brev. col., 1916; maj.-gen., temp. lieut.-col., 1917; col., Dragoon Gds., 1920; commdr., Br. mly. mission to Smyrna, 1920; gov., S. Australia, 1922-1927.

BRIDGES, WILLIAM FRANCIS NOEL, D.S.O.—B. 1890; ed. Melbourne C. of E. Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; surv.-on-agreement, F.M.S., Sept., 1912; served A.I.F., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; attd. survr.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1928; ag. asst. survr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1929 and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931; asst. survr.-gen., Dec., 1931; dep. survr.-gen., June, 1933; seconded as dep. contr., rubber, May-Aug., 1934.
BRIERCLIFFE, RUPERT, C.M.G. (1936), O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., B.S., D.P.H. (Mano.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1889; dep. dir., health, Palestine, 1920; dir., med. and sany. services, Ceylon, July, 1930.
BRIERLEY, FRANK NORRIS.—B. 1887; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., June, 1906; warden, county of Coronii, Mar., 1933.

BRINK, Major-Gen., ANDRIES JACOB ERASMUS, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpfu (native) campaign, 1898; Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1920; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st mounted brig., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; temp. lieut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. lieut.-col., whilst so empd., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., Sept., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr. No. 6 mil. dist., Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as asst. adj.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; lieut.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; maj.-gen., Nov., 1929; sec. for defence and G.O.C., Union Defence Forces, May, 1933; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in desps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, COLONEL CAROLUS, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B.1879; served Jameson Raid, S. African Republic, 1896; Mpefu campaign, Zoutpansberg, S. African Republic, 1898; Anglo-Boer War, Heidelberg commando, staff General Spruyt, 1899-1901; German S.W. Africa campaign, as brig.-major, 2nd Mounted Brig., Northern Force, 1914-15; German E. African campaign as G.S.O.I., 2nd E. African divn., 1916-17; Europe, Western Front, 1917-18; qualified, staff course, Bloemfontein, 1914; qualified, senr. staff course, Cambridge, 1919; qrtmr.-gen. Union Defence Forces; ment. in desps.; Croix de Guerre (French); med., Anglo-Boer War.

BRISTOW, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1882; 2nd Middlesex, R.G.A., 1899-1901; S. African constab., 1902-07; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and clasp); Moulmein Artillery, 1908-10; asst. inspr. of police, E.A.P., Jan., 1911; passed pol. promotion exam., Aug., 1911; passed Kiswahili exam., lower standard; inspr. of police, Mar., 1913; asst. supt. of police, 1st Apr., 1914; during war period 1914, attached to Moulmein Rifles as lieut.; on active service, E.A.P., 1915-17; awarded "1914-15" Star, War and Victory med.; resumed civil duties as asst. supt. of police, 1918; ag. qrtmr., pol. and prison depts., Oct., 1920 to Mar., 1921; immigr. offr., Apr., 1921 to June, 1922; supt. of pol., 1st Jan., 1923; headgr. supt., Jan., 1926.
BRITTER, EDWIN HAROLD.—B. 1886; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; admitted bar, Mauritius, 1909; advoc., sup. ct., Natal, 1911; judge, native high ct. of Natal, 1930.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDW. LEWIS, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; cadet, Straits Stltmts., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; comrnr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907 to Feb., 1908; ag. res., Perak, Apr., 1908; res., Pahang, Nov., 1908; res., Negri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1910; col. sec., S. Stltmts., 1910; chief sec., F.M.S., 1911; ret., Sept., 1920; apptd. agt., Malay States Infrn. Agency, Nov., 1920.

BROMLEY, Rear-Adml. Arthur, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. Farnborough Sch.; H.M.S. Britannia; joined navy, 1892; lieut., 1898; commodr., 1908; capt., 1915; served European War, 1914-18; H.M.S. Courageous, 1916-19; flag capt. to Vice-Adml. Napier commdg. light cruiser force; ret. list, 1922; rear adm., ret., 1926; a gentlemen usher to the King since 1927; pvt. sec. (ceremonial and reception) to the S. of S. (Lord Passfield), 1931.

BROODBANK, ALFRED JOHN BERTIE.—B. 1888; dist. treas., N.Borneo serv., Aug., 1921 to Aug., 1931; asst. audr., in addn., Dec., 1924 to Apr., 1926, and Oct., 1928 to July, 1929; ag. audr., Aug., 1931 to May, 1932; dist. treas., May, 1932; asst. commr. cust., in addn., Mar., 1933; ag. commr. cust. and excise, Apl., 1934; commr. cust. and excise, Apl., 1935.
BROOKE, Neville John.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; seconded to C.O., 1928; census offr., N. Provs., 1930; res., 1930; judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.

BROOKES, VICTOR S.—B. 1888; passed semr. Cambridge local exam., 1906; apptd. copyist, col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1906; cashier, St. John's savings bank, Sept., 1908; acctnt., ditto, June, 1909; ag. acctnt., treasy. dept., June o Sept., 1909; clk. to comrnr. and exec. couns., Virgin Is., Apl., 1911; rev. offr., St. Kitts, Nov., 1914; acctnt., treasy., St. Kitts; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; ag. treas. and ag. mem. exec. and leg. couns., Aug.-Oct., 1934.

BROOKS, R. L.—B. 1900; ed. Charterhouse; Artists Rifles; commd., Guards Machine Gun Regt., 1920; passed inter. exam., Survr.'s Inst.; dipl., forestry, Edinburgh, 1922; asst. conserv., forests, Uganda, 1922; seconded to adminstr., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Trinidad, 1925; ag. conserv., forests in 1925, 1928, 1931 and 1933; conserv., forests, May, 1934.

BROWN, CHARLES CUTHBERT, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schlr., 1909; Bell schlr., 1908; 1st cls. class. tripos, 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; asst. dist. offr., Brusas, 1912-13; asst. sec. to res., Perak, 1913-15; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat and priv. sec. to ch. sec., 1915-19; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1919; dist. offr., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1931; Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933; Br. res., Pahang, 1936.

BROWN, CAPT. ERIC GORDON, B Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Chld. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1890; ed. Burton-on-Trent Gram. Schl., Malvern Coll., and B'gham Univ.; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Oct., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade II., Oct., 1924; ditto, grade I., Oct., 1928; ag. divsnl. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1933.

BROWN, FRANK LESLIE, M.C.—B.1896; ed. Wilson Gram. Sch.; served in France, 1916-19; lieut., M.C., bar (ment.in desps.); prob., N. Rhodesia, Ost., 1919; asst. native comrnr., Feb., 1922; native comrnr., Ost., 1926; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as ass't sec., Aug., 1931; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935.

BROWN, GEORGE PRITCHARD.—B. 1885; served with E. Africa pol. service batt., 1914-17; capt., Aug., 1915; Turkana expdn., 1915 (A.G.S. med.); asst. supt. of pol., Kenya, Apr., 1916; asst. pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; staff offr. to comrnr., pol., 1926; ag. dep. comrnr., pol., July to Oct., 1931; offr., order of St. John of Jerusalem; asst. comsnr., pol., Jan., 1933; ag. comsnr., pol., June, 1934 to May, 1935.

BROWN, GERALD HOBAGE.—B. 1876; ed. Ottawa pub. schls. and Coll. Inst.; journalist and parly. correspondent; asst. dep. min. of labour, Canada, 1909-17; sec., reconstruction and development comte., re-apptd. asst. dep. min. of labour, 1920; Canadian del., League of Nations, Internat. Lab. Conf., Geneva, 1921.

BROWN, HARRY WHIDDEN, B.A., L.L.B.—B. 1868; ed. Acadia Coll., and Dalhousie Univ.; called to Nova Scotia bar, 1893; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1896, as priv. sec. to min. of militia and defence; dir. of contracts, militia dept., 1904; comsnr., war purchasing coman., 1919, returning to militia dept., on the dissolution of that coman., 1920; asst. dep. min. of militia, 1920.

BROWN, HON. JAMES T., K.C., B.A.—B. 1871; ed. pub. and high schls., Huntington, Quebec, and McGill Univ.; called to bar, 1896; K.C., 1907; elec. to Sask. legislature, 1905; judge, sup. ct. of Sask., 1910; ch. just., ct. of King's Bench, 1918.

BROWN, JAMES VICTOR RABURN.—B. 1897; ed. Falkirk High Sch. and Geo. Watson's Coll. Edin.; B.Sc. (Agrio.) Edin., F.S.I.; Agr. and Suth. Hldrs., attd. R.F.C., Egypt, France, Italy, 1916-19; E.C.G. Corp.; post-grad. stud., Camb., 1925; prov. supt.dg., Sierra Leone, 1925; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika, 1931; seconded, Zanzibar, 1931.
BROWN, JOHN BATHURST.—B. 1904; ed. Shaftesbury and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

BROWN, KENNETH VINCENT.—B. 1890; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1916; mag., eastern dist., Trinidad, Dec., 1924; asst. mag., county of St. George West, 1926; mag., county of Victoria, June, 1930; do., St. George West, 1932; ag. judge in 1933.

BROWN, MONTAGUE, M.B.E. (1933), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), A.C.I.B., A.L.A.A.—Office supt. and sec., gen. agrl. coun. and comte., on agrl. economics and marketing; office, commsr., of income tax and super tax, 1915; 39th Bn. R. Fusiliers, 1918-19; Palestine govt. treasy., 1920; dept. of agr. and forests, 1922; sec., Palestine trade deleg., to Egypt, 1930.
BROWN, PHILIP PRENTON, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 2nd cls. hons., philos. and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.
