BROWN, RALPH PHELPS.—B. 1877; ed. pub. and high sch., Nova Scotia and Boston; 2nd cls. elk., militia dept., Can., 1904; 1st cls. elk., 1907; asst. acctnt., 1910; asst. acctnt. and paym. gen., 1917; ch. acctnt., 1918; gen. audr., 1920.

BROWN, ROBERT ALFRED.—B. 1888; draughtsman, Kano and Kaduna waterworks, Nigeria, 1914; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1916; senr. exec. engnr., 1927; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1929-34; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1934; ag. supt., nat. admstrn. wks., 1935.

BROWN, ROBERT ALLAN.—B. 1897; ent. P.O savings bank, Dec., 1912; trans. to gen. bd. of control for Scotland, Feb., 1914; apptd. asst. clk. after compet. exam., Dec., 1914; junr. clk., 26th Apr., 1915; on milly. serv., July, 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., 27th Apr., 1922; seconded as acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1928 to 1930; treasy. acctnt., Gambia, Aug., 1930; asst. recr. gen., 1933; ag. recr. gen., June to Oct., 1934.

BROWN, THOMAS ALGERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Wellington Cadet Coll., S. India; 2nd lieut., 16th Cav., India Army, Feb., 1920; 1st lieut., Feb., 1921; active serv. Dasistan Field Force, Feb., 1920-May, 1922; lieut., regular army R. of O., June, 1922; B.A. (Oriel Coll.), Oxford; barrister-at-law; 2nd cls. hons., jurisprudence; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1926; crown coun., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1934.
BROWN, WILLIAM TWERDIE STODART, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1903; ed. St. Bees Schl., Edinburgh Univ. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. coms., forests, Gold Coast, 1926.
BROWNE, GEORGE GILHAM.—B. 1898; acot., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1924; acot., P.W.D., S.S., Apr., 1932; senr. acot., F.M.S., Aug., 1934.

BROWNE, GEORGE SIMCLAIR, C.M.G. (1931), M.A.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; B. 1880; asst. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1906; 3rd cls. cl., June, 1912; dist. offr., Jan., 1914; 1st cls. ditto, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1920; staff grade, 1920; secy., N.Provs., 1921-25; sec., native affrs., 1929; ag. lient. gov., N.P., May, 1930; ch. comsrr., N.P., 1933.

BROWNE, RAYMOND HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; Midland cir., advoc., Uganda bar, 1925-28; dist. mag. and coroner, Antigua, July, 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., on occasions; offi. mem., legis. coun., Antigua; res. mag., Jamaica, Nov., 1930.

BROWNE, ROLAND PAUL LAMBERT.—B. 1888; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; served as "élève consulaire" at British Consulate, Danzig, June, 1908 to July, 1909; asst. to res. comsrr., New Hebrides, Feb., 1913; comsrr., Cyprus, Jan., 1924; attd., col. sec.'s office in 1932 and 1934; asst. sec., Jan., 1935.

BROWNE, VERNON DENNISON.—B. 1892; secretariat clk., N.E.R. admstrn., May, 1909; mag. clk., N. Rhodesia (Fort Jameson) Aug., 1911; treasy. clk., Mar., 1913; asst. acot., Mar., 1914; served in R.A.F., 1918-19; senr. asst. treas., N. Rhodesia Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., on various occasions, 1927-34.

BRUCE, CAPT. THE RT. HON. STANLEY MELBOURNE, C.H. (1927), F.C. (1923), M.C. (1915).—Member of the H. of R. for Flinders since 1918; served in European War, 1915-17; capt., R. Fusiliers, Dec., 1914; twice wounded and invalided from the Army, Apr., 1917; Croix-de-Guerre, 1916; resigned chairmanship of firm of Paterson, Laing and Bruce, on appt. as Federal treas., Hughes ministry, Dec., 1921; prime min., Commonwealth of Australia, 1923-29; hon. min. and res. min. in London, Lyons ministry, 1932; leader Australian deleg. to Ottawa Conf., 1932; resigned from miny. and apptd. high comsrr. in Gt. Britain, 1933.

BRUCE, THOMAS DUNDAS HOPE, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; final hons., schl. of jurisprudence, 1906; admitted solr., sup. ct. of England, 1911; crown solr., Fiji, Nov., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1915; ag. chief pol. mag., ag. regar. of sup. ct., ag. regar. of titles, ag. curator of intestate and vacant estates, ag. public trustee, May to July, 1916; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1916; tempy. M.L.C., Aug., 1917; on active serv., Dec., 1917 to May, 1919; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1919 to May, 1920; dep. comsrr., Western Pacific, May, 1920; ag. res. comsrr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1920 to May, 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, May to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Apr., 1922; apptd. a judl. comsrr. for the W. Pacific, Dec., 1921; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific High Commr. to preside over certain murder trials in the Solomon Is., Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1922; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1925; solr.-gen., Kenya, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. in 1932 and 1933; King's proctor, Dec., 1928.
BRUCE, THOS. ROBERT.—B. 1862; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., St. Helena, Jan., 1898; postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.
BRUEN, JOHN PATRICK, B.E. (Dub.).—B. 1904; asst. engr., Br. Guiana, 1930; asst. engr., to sea defence bd. of comms., 1934.
BRUMAGE, DOUGLAS O.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1919; 1st grade admstve. off., Kenya, Dec., 1930.
BRUNNEN, JOHN EWART.—B. 1893; admry. and dockyard schls.; H.M. Navy, 1914; admry., 1915; engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. asst.elec.engnr.,1922;ag.sec.asst.elec.engnr.,1924.
BRUNT, ABRAHAM DE VILLIERS.—B. 1879; Cape civ. serv., 1897; Tvl. civ. serv., 1901; commr., Robben Is., 1915; ch. clk., health dept., 1919; under sec., health dept., Union of S. Africa, 1932.

BRYAN, SIR HERBERT, K.B.E. (1925), D.S.O. (1917), C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1865; 2nd lieut., Linc. regt., June, 1892; lieut., Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., July, 1899; brev. major, Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expeditn. to Bassemia (ment. in desp., medal with two clasps); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff off., Gambia expeditn., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., May 1902, to Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, Nov., 1903; col. sec., G. Coast, Feb., 1904; ag. gov., on various occasions, 1904-09 and 1911-12; col. sec. and rev. commr., Jamaica, Aug., 1914; served in France, Flanders and Salonika, 1915-18; Constantinople, 1918-19; bvt. lieut.-col., bvt. col., D.S.O.; ag. gov., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1919, 1922, 1924-25; ret., 1925.

BRYANT, G.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; served with K.N.V.R., Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 24th Aug., 1927.

BRYANT, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (math. scholar); B.A., 1909; M.A., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, S.S., Apr., 1915; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1917; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1918; offr., cls. IV, 1st Jan., 1919; sec. tin mining commr., in addition, Aug., 1919; attd. for sp. duty, Federal secretariat, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; ag. asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Aug., 1924 and Apr., 1927; temp. assist. prin., C.O., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1927; ag. chmn., sny. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. I B, June, 1930; pres., munic. commrs., Penang, Oct., 1930; ag. res. coun., Penang in addn., July, 1932; offr. cls. I A., res. coun., Malacca, Oct., 1934.

BRYSON, HUGH PATTERSON, M.C.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; war serv., 1916-19; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, 1917; France, 1918-19; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attached to dist. office, Kinta, B. Gajah; offr., cls. V, 4th ast. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; offr., cls. V, stlmt. offr., Trengganu, Apr., 1928; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, May, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. advr., Segamat, Feb., 1935.

BUCHANAN, JOHN CHOL RANKIN, M.D. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (E.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; mily. serv., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1928; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
BUCHANAN, LAWRENCE ALEXANDER COCKBURN, M.A. (Edin.), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1903; ed. Edinburgh Acad., Edin. Univ. and Oxford Univ.; teacher's dipl., London Univ., 1929; supervar., educn., Zanzibar, 1929.

BUCHANAN-SMITH, SIR WALTER, KT. BACH. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), M.C. (1918)—B. 1879; ed. Repton Schl.; Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; ag. commr., lands, 1912, 1914 and 1916; attd., Nigeria Regt. in Cameroons 1914-15 and in E. Africa 1916-18; ag. commr., lands, 1916; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1921; res., 1921; ag. prim. asst. sec., S. Prova., 1921, 1923 and 1925; ag. sec., S. Prova. 1923 and 1925; staff grade, 1926; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Prova., 1928-29 and 1930; lieut.-gov., S. Prova., 1930; O.A.G., Nigeria, Sept.-Nov., 1930; ret., 1935.

BUCKLAND, LANCELOT LIDDLE RORKE, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Oundle Schl., Univ. of Cape Town, S.A.; Artists Rifles, 1918; asst. engr., irrig. dept., S.A., 1919-28; asst. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; exec. engr., 1929.

BUCKLE, DOROTHY M., O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1883; ed. Howard Gardens Munic. Secondary Schl., Cardiff and Whitelands Training Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. B. of E. teachers' certif.; asst. prin., Raffles girls schl., Singapore, 1908; ag. prin., 1909; prin., 1911.

BULKELEY, GEORGE VICARY OWEN, A.M.I. Mech. E.—B.1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Owen's Coll., Manchester; 1st cls. rly. operating, Lond. Schl. of Economics; port man., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbors, 1926; gen. man., Nigerian rly., 1933; author "Mechanical Appliances for handling Railway Traffic" (1922), "Railway and Seaport Freight Movement" (1926).

BULL, HAROLD ROBERT, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open entrance scholar, Queen's Coll., 1907; foundation scholar, 1908; 1st cls. hons., Nat. Sci. Tripos, Part I, 1909; 3rd cls. Maths. Tripos, Part I, 1910; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1911; attached to Chinese protectorate, Dec., 1911; passed cadet; offr. collr., stamp duties, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Apr., 1924; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1924; sheriff, Singapore, Mar., 1925; 2nd mag., do., Oct., 1925; asst. offr. assignee, do., Jan., 1928; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Apr., 1930; offr., cl. IB, Mar., 1932; dist. judge, Singapore, Sept., 1932.
BULL, OSWYN BOYS, M.A.—B. 1882; dir., Lertholoth tech. schl., Basutoland, 1931.
BULLOCK, G.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda Railway, Apr., 1919; senr. asst. supt., transportation dept., Mar., 1927.

BULMAN, JOSEPH WAUGH, A.M.I.Loco.E.—B. 1893; ed. Petershill Pub. Schl., Glasgow; served in Navy, 1914-19; loco. foreman, Cyprus govt. rly., Nov., 1919; asst. loco. supt., Oct., 1927; aspt., rly., July, 1935.

BULSTRODE, B. P. E.—B. 1887; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; R.A., 1913; asst. priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1907-1909; chief clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1914; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1915; priv. sec. to admstr., Feb., 1916 to Apr., 1917; seconded from govt. office for sp. duties, Feb., 1917; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Apr., 1917; trans. to Gambia as 2nd ast. revr.-gen., Oct., 1918; offr.-in-charge, secretariat on several occasions between 1920 and 1926; ag. clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1920; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1921; clk., exec. and leg. couns., on many occasions; ag. inspr., schls., Apr. to Sept., 1926; ast. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1931.

BUNBURY, CECIL MOLESWORTH.—Ed. privately; employed at Crown Bridge Works, W. Bromwich, 1895-1898; Great Central, Great Northern and City Rlyws., 1898-1900; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (medal with 4 clasps); Central S. African Rlyws., 1902-1904; ast. engrn., Uganda Rlyw., 15th Jan., 1906; maintenance engrn., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. engrn., 1925.

BUNNY, JOSEPH, F.L.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; ast. conserv., forests, S. Nigeria, 1912; conserv., 1918; senr. conserv., 1922; attended Imp foresty confce., in Australia and N.Z. as del. for Nigeria and Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. dir., forests, Nigeria in 1929.
BUNTING, BENJAMIN, N.D.A., F.L.S.—B. 1889; ast. agriculturist, F.M.S., Mar., 1914; ag. do., Dec., 1914; agriculturist, Mar., 1920; on sp. duty in Ceylon, Jan.-Feb., 1930.
BUNTING, ROBERT HUGH, F.L.S.—Ast. dir. of agriculture, Sierra Leone, 1913-15; ast. dir. of agric. and govt. mycologist, Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions.
BURDITZ, HENRY LEATHAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; war serv., Nov., 1914 to Aug., 1919; ast. engrn., Basutoland, 1921; transferred to Tanganyika as road inspr., 1929.
BURGE, G. H. K.—B. 1890; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Islands, Jan., 1915.
BURGESS, W. C.—S. African civ. serv.; offr.-in-charge, finger print bureau, Kenya, Oct. 1919.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, E. M.O'D.—B. 1887; ed. Belvedere Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; ast. engrn., F.M.S., P.W.D., Jan., 1920; exec. engrn., Jan., 1929; ag. state engrn., Kelantan, Nov., 1934.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, H.J. O'D., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; ast. bacteriologist, 1929; senr. pathologist, 1935.

BURLINGHAM, DONALD.—B. 1892; ed. Norwich schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1911; attached to Punjab pol., 1912-13; passed in Urdu, 1913; J.P., 1914; ast. supt. of pol., New Territories, 1914-17; passed in Cantonese, 1916; 2nd lieut., Indian Army res. of offrs., 1917; on active serv., German E. Africa, 1917; attchd. army headqrs., gen. staff branch, Simla, 1918-1919; ast. supt. of pol., Hong Kong, 1920; side-de-camp to gov., 1921; passed in Punjabi, 1923; ag. dep. supt., pol., Apr., 1925; dep. supt., pol., 1926; divnl. supt., pol., 1930; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Oct., 1934; ag. inspr.-gen., do., May, 1935.
BURNER, ERIC ANDERSON.—Ed. at Felsted Schl.; command., R.F.A., France, Belgium and Germany, 1915-18; ast. dist. consmr., Gold Coast, 1920; dist. consmr., 1924.

BURNETT, BERTRAM DAVIDSON.—B. 1891; ed. Port Elphinston Pub. Schl. and Inverurie Acad.; war serv., 6th Batt. Gordon Highlanders, 1914-19; foreman of wks., 2nd grade, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; headmast., junr. trade schl., Apr., 1924.

BURNETT, CAPT. FREDERICK, M.C., M.A.—B. 1890; ed. Ashwater Schl., Devon, and Oxford Univ.; war serv., R.E., France, 1914-19 (M.C. and bar); divl. agrl. offr., Ceylon, 1925; ast. regisr., co-op. socs., 1926; vice-prin., agrl. coll., Peradeniya, 1926; dep. dir., agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

BURNS, SIR ALAN CUTHBERT MAXWELL, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1887; apptd. to treasy. and cust. dept., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905; clk. to mag., Dec., 1909; ag. mag., Anguilla, 1910; dep. coroner and J.P., St. Kitts, 1911; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; supervisor of cust., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; ast. sec., Nigeria, June, 1914; col. sec., Bahamas, Feb., 1924; el. mem., H. of A., 1925-28; del. to W. Indian confce., London, 1926; admstr. govt. on various occasions, 1924-28; dep. ch. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1929; acting ch. sec., Feb. to July 1929; gov.'s dep. for various periods in 1929 and 1930; gov. and commdr.in-ch., Br. Honduras, 1934; on active serv. with W. African Regt., 1914-15; adjutant, Nigeria Land Contingent, 1915; served with Egba Expedn., 1918; joint compiler, Index to Laws of Leeward Is., 1910; compiler, Nigeria Handbooks, 1917-23; author of "A History of Nigeria" 1929.

BURNS, R. E., LL.B., London (1908).—2nd clk., regisr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town consmr., Basseterre, June to Dec., 1899; clk., P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treasy., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treasy., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. gov't. clk., clk., ex. and legis. couns., clk., bd. of health, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904; tariff clk., treasy., St. K.-N., Oct., 1904; ag. audit clk., May, 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Oct., 1906, to May, 1907; 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Aug., 1907; jun. ast. treas., July, 1908; ast. treas., Mar., 1912; ast. censor (temporary) G. Coast, Aug., 1915; ag. senr. ast. auditor, Apr. to May, 1917; ag. treas., May-July, 1920; dep. treas., Aug., 1922.
BURNS, ROBERT.—B. 1912; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ. (M.A. hons. classics); apptd. after compet. exam. ast. prin., D.O., 7th Jan., 1936.
BURNS, WILLIAM ALOYSIUS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1900; ed. St. Aloysius Coll. and Vety. Coll., Glasgow; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1923.

BURNSIDE, ROBERT EDWARD WARRICK, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Christ's Hosp. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; served R.A.F., 1918; headmast., N. Rhodesia, 1927; inspr. schls., Apr., 1931; ag. dir., European educn., 1932.

BURPEE, LAWRENCE JOHNSTON, F.R.G.S., F.R.S.C.—B. 1873; priv. sec. to three successive mins. of justice in Dom. govt., and for several years librnr. of Ottawa pub. lib.; sec., Canadian section, International Joint Comsn., 1st Jan., 1912; mem. Champlain Soc., Can. Inst., Ontario Hist. Soc., Société Archéologique de France, Hist. Soc. of the Mississippi Valley, Bibliographical Soc. of America; author and editor of numerous works relating to Canadian life and history.

BURR, MAJOR FRANCIS WILLIAM MANFRED, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1884; ed. Bedford Schl. and Crystal Palace Schl. of Eng.; ast. engrn., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1913; lieut., R.E., Jan., 1915; ast. engrn., J. Bahrn., Feb., 1920; exec. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1928; ditto, Kinta, July, 1929; ditto, Penang July, 1932.

BURRILL, HON. MARTIN.—B. 1858; ed. St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint; fruit farmer in Niagara Peninsula fourteen years; moved to B. Columbia, 1900; apptd. mem. bd. of horticulture, B. Columbia; editor, Grand Forks Gazette; mayor, Grand Forks, 1903; fruit commr. and lecturer in England for B.C. Govt., 1907-08; elected to H. of C. Canada, for Yale-Cariboo, 1908; re-elected, 1911 and 1917; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of agric. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; sec. of state and min. of mines, Oct., 1917; joint librn. of parlmt., July, 1920.

BURROWES, BERTRAM, LL.B. (Hons.) Univ. Coll., London.—B. 1883; called to bar. Gray's Inn, 1920; treas., Jamaica, 1903; judl. dept., 1915; clk., cts., 1921; judge, grand ct., Cayman Is., 1924; supernum. res. mag., Jamaica, 1926; ag. res. mag., 1925; res. mag., 1926.

BURROWES, EDMUND, S.—B. 1906; ed. Queen's Coll.; cler. asst., col. sec's office, Br. Guiana, 1924; 6th offr., cust., 1925; 5th clk., col. secs. office, 1926; 4th cls. offr., 1928; asst. clk., leg. coun., 1929; 3rd cls. clk., O.S.O., 1933.

BURROWS, WILLIAM.—B. 1883; ent. "Britannia," 1898; ret. from Navy with rank of commdr., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Jan., 1920; seconded Fiji, Feb., 1922; dist. commr., Fiji, Jan., 1923.

BURT, NORMAN FRANK.—B. 1899; ed. Rugby Sch.; R. Mily. Academy, Woolwich and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1922; cadet, Tanganyika, Sept., 1925; asst. dist. offr., Sept., 1926.
BURT, ROBERT HILLHOUSE.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. scr. and assigned to C.O., 15th Mar., 1915; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Apr., 1923.
BURTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.A.—Plant breeder, agrl. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921.

BURTON, LEONARD LAMMING, L.S.A., L.R.C.P., Lond., M.R.C.S., Eng., M.D., Brux., F.R.C.S., Edin.—B. 1872; senr. med. offr., St. Helena, 1929; publications:—"Keloid Secondary Rash in Smallpox." ("Lancet.")

BURTON, WILLIAM, Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1884; B.A., Cantab, 1st cls., div. III, clas. trip., 1906; 2nd cls., pt. II., hist. trip., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; asst. dist. offr., K. Pilah, cls. V, cont. as mag., K. Baharu, and harbmr., T. Anson, and asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1911; asst. dist. offr., B. Padang, 1913; mag., Ipoh, 1913; asst. regiar., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1914; mag., Seremban, 1916; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1919; registr., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, 1920; offr. assignee in addn., May, 1921; registr., sup. ct., July, 1921, cont. offr. assignee in addn.; offr., cl. I B, May, 1926; offr. assignee, F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1927; dep. legal advr., F.M.S., Apr., 1927; puisne judge, S.S., Apr., 1929.

BURTON, WILLIAM ELLIOT.—M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., 1910; ag. temporary res. surg., Barbados general hosp., 1910; ag. med. offr., St. James parish, Barbados; ag. visiting physician, leper asylum; ag. surg. to police, dist. II., Barbados, 1911; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Leeward Is., 1912; inspr. public health, St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.

BUSH, BERTRAM OLIVER, Assoc.M.Inst.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1890; ed. Hoe Gram. Schl. and Plymouth Tech. Schl.; asst. engr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1920; ditto, K. Kangsar, Feb., 1921; ditto, K. Pilah, Dec., 1924; ditto K. Pilah and Jelebu, Oct., 1925; exec. engr., D.P.W.'s office, Jan., 1929; sr. drainage and irrig. engr., Perak, Jan., 1932; ag. asst. advr., drainage and irrig., Malay States in 1933 and 1934.

BUSHE, SIR HENRY GRATTAN, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.B. (1932), C.M.G. (1927)—B. 1886; called to the bar, 1909; Western circuit, 1910-11; S. Eastern circuit, 1911; Herts and Essex sessions, 1916; sec. to royal comm. on the contracts between the War Office and Sir John Jackson, Ltd., 1916-17; ag. legal asst., C.O., Nov., 1917; legal asst., 1st Jan., 1919; legal adviser to D.O. and C.O., 1st Apr., 1931.

BUSTARD, FRANK HARBOURT.—B. 1888; joined Northumberland Fusiliers, May, 1903; seconded to K.A.R., Aug., 1913; commissioned in field, May, 1916; coy. commdr., Apr., 1917; ment. in desps., 1918; Somaliland campaign, 1920; capt., reg. army R. of O.; temp. asst. supt., Tanganyika pol., May, 1925; cadet, Zanzibar pol., Oct., 1925; supt., pol., May, 1926.

BUSWELL, HENRY HOPE.—(B.D. (Serampore, Calcutta); L.C.P. (hons. English and French), Fellow of L'Institut Francois du Royaume Uni.; asst. chaplain, Quatre Bornes and Moka, Mauritius, 1908; civ. chaplain, Beau Bassin and Rose Hill, Mauritius, 1909; civ. chaplain of Seychelles, 1919; sub. dean, St. Paul's Cathedral, Victoria, Seychelles; chmn., Seychelles free library; mem., bd. of educn., Seychelles.

BUTCHER, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. N.Z. state schls. and Waitaki Boys' High Schl.; ent. regir.-gen.'s dept., 1906; statistical branch, 1912; ch. compiler, census and statistics office, 1915; asst. govt. statistician, 1929; govt. statistician, 1932; N.Z. rep. at Br. Empire Conf. of statisticians, Ottawa, 1935.

BUTCHER, PERCY GEORGE, B.A. (hons. Oxon.).—B. 1890; ed. Perse Sch., Cambridge and Exeter Coll. Oxford; war serv., Devonshire Regt., 1915-19; France, 1916-19; admstv. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, Aug., 1919; Arabic dipl. Schl. of Oriental Studies, 1927; ag. res., Bornu Prov., various periods since 1928; dep. res., Aug., 1934.
BUTLER, CHARLES OWEN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1932.

BUTLER, CHARLES RICE.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and at the Sorbonne, Paris; B.A.; sup't., educn. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ch. inspir., S. Provs., 1930; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1932 and 1935; prin., Higher Comm., Yaba, 1933.
BUTLER, ERNEST CREIL.—B. 1900; clk., Basutoland, 1920; sub.-insp., B.M. pol., 1927; war serv. Apr., 1917-Mar., 1919.

BUTLER, FREDERICK BERRY LEWIS, F.L.S.—B. 1890; ed. Shaw Schl. (Berks); ent. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1912; served in European War, 1915-19; agrl. instr., E.A.P., 1919; man., Mazeras and Kibos experimental farms, E.A.P., 1921; agrl. supervisor, Kenya, 1923; grader and inspir., Kenya, 1925; ch. grader and inspir. of produce, Cyprus, Jan., 1935.

BUTLER, G. G., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., B.C., Cantab. (hons. nat. sci. tripos), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (with distinction).—Apptd., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, Apl., 1911; seconded for sp. investigation under yellow fever comsn., May, 1913 to June, 1914; served Cameroons Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916 to Apl., 1916; temp.-capt. (gen. list) Jan. to Apl., 1916; seconded for serv. with R.A.M.C., 1916-19; dir. of laboratory, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; ag. D.P.M.O., Mar., 1924; dir., med. research inst., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

BUTTERFIELD, HON. RICHARD LATTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—M.P. for Wooroora 1915-18 and since the elections of 1921; leader, partly, liberal party and of opposition, Dec., 1925; premier, treas. and min. of rly., 1927-30; now leader of opposition.

BUTTERFIELD, HARRY ROBERT FREDERICK.—B. 1896; ed. Westminster City Sch.; 2nd lieut., Middx. Regt., Mar., 1915; served Great War, 1914-19; 6th K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1922 to 1925; ret., 1926; admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; capt., 6th K.A.R.R. of O., July, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. registr., June, 1931-Sept., 1932; author, "Index to Laws of Tanganyika Territory, 1935."

BUTTERS, HENRY ROBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow High schl. and Glasgow Univ., 1st bursar, 1916; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1922; asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924; dist. offr., North, 1925; ag. dep. regis. and 2nd pol. mag. in addn., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1926; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1928; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1929; 2nd pol. mag., 1930; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., 1932 and 1933; pol. mag., Kowloon, Sept., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Oct., 1933; extra asst. col. sec., Jan., 1934; dep. clk., couns. in addn., Sept., 1934; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Sept., 1935.

BUTTERS, SIR JOHN HENRY, KT. BACH., M.I.E.E., M.A.M.I.C.E., etc.—B. 1882; ed. Taunton Schol. and Univ. Coll., Southampton; ch. engr., gen. man., govt. hydro elec. dept., Hobart, Tasmania; chmn., Tasmanian state comttee. of the C'wealth advisory coun. of science and industry; pres., Tasmanian Inst. of Engrs.; ch. commr., fedl. cap. couns., Canberra, Nov., 1924; ret.

BUTTON, FRANCIS JENKINSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Burnley Gram. Schl.; war serv., East Lancs Regt. and R.T.C., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1919; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; exec. engrnr., Apr. 1929; ag. sr. exec. engrnr., J'Bahru, Nov., 1932.
BUXTON, C. E. V.—Asst. dist. comsnr. E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

BYNOE, ALLAN KENNETH.—B. 1881; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1900; ch. clk., pol. mag., cts., dist. "A" 1922; ag. pol. mag. of various dists., on several occasions, 1922-26; pol. mag., dist. "C" 1927; do., dists. "D" and "F" 1930.
BYNOE, EVAN BROWN.—B. 1871; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1889; ch. clk., treasy., 1922; provost marshal and sergt. at arms, 1925.

BYRNE, BRIG.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH A. G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1928), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. Military (1916).—B. 1874; joined R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1893; served in S. African War, 1899-1902; wounded; served with central judicial (claims) comm., Pretoria, 1903-05; major, 1914; bt. lieut.-col., 1915; brig.-gen., 1916; asst. adjutant-gen., W.O., 1916; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1916 (C.R. and desps.); inspr. gen., R.I.C., 1916; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1922; gov., Seychelles, 21st July, 1922; assumed govt., 26th Sept., 1922; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Kenya and high commr. for transport, 1931.

BYRON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS WILLIAM.—B. 1900; served in R.E. and Royal Corps Signals, 1918-1924; awarded M.S.M. and ment. in desps. for serv. on N.W. Frontier, India; wireless offr., Gold Coast, 1925; electrician in charge, elec. services, Falkland Is., 1930; broadcast offr., Sierra Leone, 1933.
CAABE, THALES.—B. 1887; clk., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1910; ch. clk., secretariat, 1931; asst. postmr.-gen., 1934; ag. P.M.G. in 1934 and 1935.

CADE, REGINALD COURTNEY.—B. 1891; ed. Academie de Caen and in Germany; joined army, Aug., 1914; served in France, June, 1915 to Sept., 1916 and Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1918; lieut., gen. list, H. Qrs., Home Forces, till Mar., 1919, invalided off farmy; junr. exam., clearing office (enemy debts.) Sept., 1920; senr. exam., June, 1921 to Dec., 1929; temp. admstve. offr., C.O., Feb., 1930.

CADMAN, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.M.G. (1916), M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1877; ed. High Schol., Newcastle-under-Lyme, and Durham Univ. Coll. Sc.; Staff. C.C. scholar, 1895; B.Sc. (Dunelm.), hons., 1899; M.Sc., 1902; pres. Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1900; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffa., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. insp. of mines (H.O.), East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engnr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; ret., 1922; author of prize papers on "Iron Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engnr.

CAHAN, HON. CHARLES HAZLITT, B.A., LL.B., LL.D., K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Yarmouth Seminary and Dalhousie Univ.; leader of conserv. party in Nova Scotia legis., 1890-94; 1st le. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1925; re-elected, 1926 and 1930; sec. of state in Bennett cabinet, 1930.
CAIN, HENRY OLIVER.—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; 21st Lancers 1910; Indian Sig. Serv., 1916-23; qrm. sgt., Somaliland pol. force, 1923; pay and qrmr., 1928.

CAINE, SYDNEY, B.SC. (Econ.)—B. 1902; ed. Harrow County Schol. and London Schol. of Economics; B.Sc. (Econ.), with 1st cls. hon., 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. inspr. of taxes (Inland Rev.), 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Nov., 1926; prin., 8th Nov., 1931 visited W. Indies and B. Guiana as sec., W. Indian sugar commn., 1929-30; sec., managing commtce., Imp. Inst. of Entomology, Oct., 1931.

CAIRNS, THOMAS CRAWFORD, B.SC.—B. 1900; ed. Edinburgh Acad.; B.Sc. Edinburgh Univ., 1924; entomologist, Empire Cotton Growing Corp.; seconded to divn. of entomology, Union of S. Africa; cotton investigator, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1930.

CALDECOTT, SIR ANDREW, KT. BACH. (1935), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1884; ed. Uppingham (schol. and exh.), and Oxford (schol.), 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1901; B.A., Exeter Coll., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; dist. offr., Jelebu, 1911; dep. contr., labr., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. Sec., 1916; seconded for sp. duty in connec. with Br. Empire Exhib. while on leave in England, Jan., 1923; town planning admstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Mar., 1926; chmn. housing and P.W.D. enquiry commtces., Dec., 1926; dep. contr., lab., Malaya but continues ag. under sec., S.S., July, 1927; offr. cls. I.A., sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1928; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1929; ag. Br. res. Perak, Oct., 1930; ag. Br. res. Selangor, May, 1931; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., July, 1931; supernumy. staff offr. of rank of ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; col., sec., S.S., Dec., 1933; O.A.G. S.S. and high commr., Malay States. Feb.-Nov., 1934; gov. and comdr-in-ch., Hong Kong, Dec., 1935, published, "History of Jelebu," 1912.

CALDER, James.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow Univ.; M.A.; war serv., 1916-19; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attd., treasy., S'pore, June, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore and asst. contrlr., rubber in addn., Aug., 1926; ditto, Penang, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1928; asst. dir., co-opern., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., B. Palat, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; mag., Seremban, May, 1935.

CALDER, Hon. James Alexander, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. Ingersoll pub. schls., pub. and high schls., Winnipeg; Manitoba Coll.; hon. grad. in science, Manitoba Univ., 1888 (silver medallist); called to the bar, North-West Territories, 1906; prin. of Moosejaw high schl., 1891-94; inspr. of schls., N.W.T., 1894-1900; dep. commr. of educn., N.W.T., 1901-05; elec. to Sask. ass. at first g.e., 1905; prov. treas. and commr. of educn. in Scott min., Sept., 1905; unsucc. candl. for Milestone div. at g.e., 1908; elec. for Saltcoats at bye-elec., Dec., 1908; min. of imigrn. and colonization in federal union govt., Oct., 1917; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for Moosejaw, Dec., 1917; pres., Canadian privy coun., July, 1920; called to senate, Sept., 1921.

CALDER, John A., M.A.—B. 1889; ed. at Harris Acad., Dundee and Edin. Univ.; Van-Dunlop scholarship in history, 1910; M.A. with 1st cls. hons. in history, 1911; 1st cls. hons. in philosophy, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th Sept. to 10th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited S. Africa, N. and S. Rhodesia, Nyassaland, Tanganyika, Zanzibar, Uganda and Kenya as sec., E. Africa Parly. Coman., Aug. to Dec., 1924; asst. sec., 27th June, 1933.

CALDICOTT, Kenneth Child, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., chtd. civ. engrnr.—B. 1887; ed. Malvern Coll. (sch.) and Edin. univ.; Messrs. Rendel & Robertson, Westminster, 1909; jun. asst. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1911; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1912; dist. engrnr., grade III., 1913; ag. off. asst., gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., 1918; dist. engrnr., grade II., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade I., 1921; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., 1925; gen. dist. engrnr., 1926; senr. dist. engrnr., Apr., 1927; ditto, Ipoh dist., Mar., 1928; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1930; prin. asst. to engrnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1930; divn. engrnr. F.M.S.Rlys., Aug., 1932; ag. maintce. and new wks. engrnr., Oct., 1934.
CALDWELL, Capt. K. F. T.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1918; asst. game warden, Oct., 1922; ag. game warden, Jan. to Nov., 1923 and in 1926; senr. asst. game warden, Jan., 1925.

CALDWELL, Reginald Percival.—B. 1894; held various appts., Fiji, 1910-17; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R., France, 1917-19; 1st cls. clk., treas. and sec., returned sailors and soldiers land settlmt. scheme and returned sailors and soldiers advances bd. of control, 1922; asst. commr. for Fiji, Br. Emp. Exhib., 1924; col. audit dept., Dec., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1925; asst. audr., Uganda, July, 1930; ag. senr. asst. audr., Aug., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Dec., 1935.

CALDWELL, Robert, M.A., (Glasgow).—B. 1894; ed. Irvine Royal Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; served Scottish Rifles and K.A.R., 1915-19; temp. asst. dist. commr., Kenya, 1919-20; supt., eduan., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; asst. dir., eduan., Nyassaland, 1927; ag. dir., eduan., Aug., 1928 to Feb., 1930; dir. native eduan., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; M.L.C., 1933.

CALDWELL, Robert.—B. 1882; European pol. constable, Penang, Feb., 1908; sergt., Aug., 1910; insp., pol., Jan., 1912; sec. for serv. with govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1921-1922; ch. insp., S'pore, Dec., 1922; do., Penang, Jan., 1928; asst. aspt., pol., Jan. 1931; do. S'pore, Feb., 1932.

CALLAN, Hon. John Bartholomew, K.C. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1882; ed. Christian Bros. schl., Dunedin, and Otago univ., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice many years; served in war as capt., 3rd batt., N.Z.R.B.; lecturer in torts, Otago univ., 1912-33; and dean of faculty of law, 1924-33; mem., coun. of legal educ.; formerly pres. Otago law soc.; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1935.

CALTHROP, Lanciot Henry Calthrop.—B. 1904; ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1924; seconded as junr. dist. offr., Weihaiwei, 1926; asst. supt., pol., 1928; J.P., 1928; passed in Mandarin, 1928; attd., Punjab pol., 1930-31.
CALWELL, H.G., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.) (Belf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.) (Gold Md., Diseases of Children, Belfast).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1931.

CAMACHO, Fabien Joseph.—B. 1898; ed. St. Wilfred's Coll., N. Staffs.; army, France 1917-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mag. and legal advr., Montserrat, Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; mag., E. counties, Trinidad, Nov., 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1935.

CAMACHO, Maurice Vivian.—B. 1885; ed. St. Edmund's Coll., Herts.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., in 1919 and 1920; atty.-gen., Dec., 1920; K.C., 1924; judge, West Indian ct. of appeal for hearing of certain appeals, 1924; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1928 to Apr., 1929 and Dec., 1930, to Jan., 1931; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr. to June, 1929 and Jan. to June, 1931; atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1931.

CAMBRIDGE, Ralph.—M.A. Hertford Coll., Oxon.; B. 1878; 3rd asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, Sept., 1901; 1st mast. mast., Apr., 1918; 2nd mast., July, 1920; prin., Queen's Royal Coll., Sept., 1927.
CAME, Frederic Harold Roberts, M.B.E. (1936), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Impl. Coll. of Sci., S. Kensington; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda, 1925.

CAMERON, Sir Donald Charles, G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1872; ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. govt., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. asst. govt. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to govt. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-5-6-7; temp. transf. to S. Nigeria as asst. sec., Jan., 1908; principal asst. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor trade inquiry comtee., 1909; ag. prov. commr. in 1910, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb. to 24th Aug., and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912, deputy gov. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; ch. sec. to govt., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. gov. in 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, Nigeria, 1931.

CAMERON, Sir Edward J., K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury Sch., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lees, gov., Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1883; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Stltmt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec. 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comsrr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1,95, and Apr. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901; admstr., St. Lucia, Apr., 1909; ag. gov. Windward Is., June to Oct., 1909, July to Dec., 1911, and in 1912; representative of St. Lucia, and chmn. of W. Indian delegates at reciprocity conf. at Ottawa, Mar., 1912; gov., Gambia, Feb., 1914; assumed govt., Apl., 1914; ret., 1920.
CAMERON, Katherine Ross, M.B.E. (1926)—Nursing sister, Basutoland, 1911; ag. matron, 1914; nursing sister, Nyasaland, 1919; matron, 1923.

CAMERON, Major Sir Maurice Alexander, K.C.M.G. (1914); C.M.G. (1900), late R.E.—B. 1855; first comen. in Roy. Engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engrnr., S. Stltmt., Dec. 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted about two years and a half as col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown auths. for the colonies, 1895-1920.
CAMILIERI, Luigi A., LL.D.—B. 1892; matric., Malta Univ., 1907; LL.D., 1913; el. for legis. assm., 1921; mag., 1924; judge, 1930.
CAMPBELL, Albert Bernard.—B. 1883; ast. store keeper, Baro Kano rly., 1909; cond. Nigerian Regt., 1917; ast. ch. storekeeper, 1920; ch. storekeeper, Nigeria, 1928.

CAMPBELL, Archibald, A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B. Rly. Co., 1906-11; engnr. design dept., C.A., 1914; war serv., 1916-18; ast. engrnr., 1921; dep. ch. engrnr., (mech.), 1936.
CAMPBELL, Colin Bruce, R.A., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Leatherhead Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1921.

CAMPBELL, General Sir David, G.C.B. (1935), K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1916)—B. 1869; ed. Clifton; joined 9th Lancers 1889; served Boer War, 1899-1902 (desp., bt. maj., Queen's med. 7 clasps, King's med. 2 clasps); served European War, 1914-1918; first in command 9th Lancers then of a cavalry brig. 21st Divn., 1916-19 (desp. six times, bt. col., C.B., promoted major-gen., etc.); G.O.C. Baluchistan dist., 1920-24; mily. sec. to S. of S. for War, 1926-27; G.O.C. in ch., Aldershot Commd., 1927-31; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Malta, 1931; ret., 1936.

CAMPBELL, Col. George Frederick Colin, C.M.G. (1919), V.D. (1908).—B. 1858; ed. Nelson Coll., New Zealand; entd. New Zealand civ. serv., 1874; dep. comsrr. of taxes, 1894-1904; dep. supt., advances to settlers' offices, 1896-7; dep. valuer-gen., 1897-1904; valuer-gen., 1904-9; gen. man., state fire insurance office, 1909-10; comsrr. of taxes, 1910-2; sec. to treasy., recvr.-gen., pay-master-gen. and supt. of state advances office, 1913; contr. and audr.-gen., 1922.

CAMPBELL, James Edward Francis, M.A.—B. 1879; ed. Christ Church, Oxford; double 1st cls. hona.; called to bar, Middle Temple; Order of the Nile; served throughout great war; major, R. Artillery, 1916-18; ent. Palestine admin., Mar., 1919; ast. dist. gov., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1920; dist. comsrr., Jerusalem dist., 1932.
CAMPBELL, J. M., M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. (Liv.) (Alan Milne med.).—B. 1897; med. offr., Kenya, Nov., 1923; senr. health offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1930.

CAMPBELL, Lachlan Bain, M.C. (1918), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1882; ed. Auckland Grammar Schl. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; 30 years' serv. in N.Z. pub. wks. dept.; comsrr., borough of Napier (during restoration period following earthquake), 1931-33; sec., marine, 1933.

CAMPBELL, Owen Neil.—B. 1884; ed. Auckland gram. schl. and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1901; served overseas as lieut. N.Z. Engnr., 1916-19; ch. drainage engrnr., lands dept., 1922; (dist.) comsrr. of crown lands, 1927; dep. under-secr., lands and survey dept., 1934; native trustee and under-secr., native dept., 1935.

CAMPBELL, S. McNeill.—B. 1893; ed. Hamilton Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; M.A. (lst cls. hona. Classics) 1915; G.U.O.T.C., Sept., 1914; on military service, Jan., 1916 to Mar., 1919, with Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) 52nd Div. and R.A.F., Egypt and Palestine; lieut., July, 1917; capt., R.A.F., Nov., 1918; temporary clk., in C.O., Apr., 1919; apptd. by civ. serv. comsrr., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir J. Masterton Smith), Apl., 1922 to Nov., 1923; ag. prin., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin., 1st Oct., 1922; sec., W. Indies Closer Union Comsn., Nov., 1932-Mar., 1933.

CAMPIGLI, Lieut.-Col. George Murray (R. of O.), M.C., M.Inst.T.—B. 1890; ed. pvtv.; Vict. state rly., 1905-14; served in Australian Forces in Gallipoli and Palestine, 1914-18; ment. in despatches; capt. (staff capt.), May, 1917; dep. ast. dir., rly. traffic, 1918; ast. dir., ditto, 1919; offr.-in-ch., Palestine mil'y. rly., 1919; transf'd to civ. govt. as ast. gen. man. Palestine rly., 1920; lieut.-col., R. of O., July, 1921; ag. gen. man. in 1922, 1924, 1926-1927-28; mem., advisory bd. to Palestine govt. for commerce and industry, Mar., 1928.

CAMPION, Lieut.-Col. Sir William Robert, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O. (1917), T.D. (1914).—B. 1870; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; lieut.-col., 4th Batt., R. Sussex Regt., 1915; col., 1924; M.P., Lewes divn., Sussex, 1910-24; gov., W. Australia, 1924-31.

CAMSILL, Charles, C.M.G. (1935), B.A., B.Sc., LL.D., F.R.S.C., F.G.S.A.—B. 1876; ed., St. John's Coll., Winnipeg; grad., Man. Univ., 1894; post grad., geol., of Queen's Univ., Kingston, Harvard, and Massachusetts Inst. of Tech., Boston; geol., Algoma Cent. Rly., 1901; geol., Canadian Northern Rly., 1902-03; geol., geological surv. of Can., 1904; geol., investigations and explns. in Br. Columbia and N. Can., 1904-20; in charge of B.C. and Yukon br. of the geol. surv., 1918; dep. min., federal dept. of mines, 1920; apptd., Dom. fuel bd., 1921.

CANNON, Hon. Lawrence Arthur Dumolin, B.A., LL.D., LL.D.—B. 1877; ed. Commercial Coll., Arthabaskaville, Que. (Can.), Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ.; gov.-gen'l.'s med., A.B., 1896; LL.L., 1899; LL.D., 1927; admitted to bar, 1899; K.C., 1910; M.L.A. (Que.), 1916 and 1919; mem. of comen. to revise and consolidate statutes of Quebec, 1923; bataillon, Quebec bar, 1924; hon. sec., Canadian Bar Assn., 1927; judge, King's bench (Que.), 1927; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1930.

CAPETOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, THE MOST REVEREND FRANCIS ROBINSON PHELPS, D.D., Ed. Keble Coll. Oxford; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1889; D.D., 1916; bishop of Grahamstown, 1915; archbishop of Capetown and metropolitan of the church of the province of S. Africa, 1931.
CARBERRY, JOHN EDWARD DOSTON, LL.B. (McGill).—B. 1893; served in Great War, 1915-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1925; clks. etc., Jamaica, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1927-32; res. mag., Nov., 1932.

CARBONELL, JOHN ROUSE.—B. 1908; ed. Shrewsbury Schol. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; 3rd cls. geog. tripes, 1929; 3rd cls. anthrop. tripes, 1930; B.A., 1930; supt., educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

CARDIN, HON. PIERRE JOSEPH ARTHUR (Richelieu)—B. 1879; ed. Sorel Comm. Coll. and pvt. teachers; studied law, Laval Univ.; bataillon Richelieu bar sec.; first elec. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1911; re-elec., g.e., 1917 and g.e., 1921; mem., privy coun. and min. of marine and fisheries, 1924; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, 1924; re-elec. at g.e., 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; resigned portfolio with King Cabinet, 1926; re-apptd. min. of marine and fisheries, Sept., 1926; min. of pub. wks. in King cabinet, 1935.

CARDINAL, ALLAN WOLSEY.—B. 1887; ed. Winchester Coll. and Heidelberg; Lond. Matric., 1914; asst. dist. comanr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1921; ag. comanr., S. Prov., N.T. Apr. to Aug., 1928; ag. comanr., N. Prov., N.T., Jan. to June, 1929; ret., 1932; comanr., Cayman Is., 1934.

CAREW, WILLIAM DESMOND.—B. 1899; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll., Ireland and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lt., 13th D.C.O. Lancers, Indian Army, 1918; served N.W. Frontier, 1919; cadet, Fiji, 1921; dist. comanr., 1925; ag. ch. pol. mag., for various periods, 1928-31; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1930; ag. atty. gen., sup. ct. criminal circuit, Sept., 1931 and Sept., 1932; dist. mag., N.W. Viti Levu, and dist. registr., sup. ct., in ch. of Lauota dist. regy., 1932-34; ag. ch. pol. mag., registr. sup. ct., pub. trustee and offl. recr. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1934; judl. commr. for W. Pacific, New Hebrides and ag. Br. judge of joint (internat.) ct., condominium govt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1935.

CAREY, CECIL WILLIAM VICTOR.—B. 1887; B.A., LL.B., Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Dublin, 1910; asst. dist. comanr., Uganda, Oct., 1915; mag., 1918; admstr.-gen., custodian of enemy property, offl. recr. in bankruptcy, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1921; ag. solr.-gen. in 1923-30; puisne judge, 1930.
CARLTON, ERIC JAMES WATTS.—B. 1908; ed. Bedford Schol. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; passed in sch. of agr., B.A., 1930; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; asst. dist. ofir., 1933.
CARMICHAEL, FRANK BERNARD—B. 1883; loco. supt., Trinidad govt. rly., June, 1928; ag. supt., rlys., July, 1934 to Jan., 1935.
CARNCROSS, HON. SIR WALTER CHARLES FREDERICK, KT. BACH (1922).—B. 1855; ed. privately; mem., N.Z. House of Reps., 1890-1902; M.L.C. since 1903; ohmn., comte of L.C., 1910-18; speaker, 1918.

CARNELLEY, STEPHEN H., F.R.G.S.—Ed. Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; solr., sup. ct., England; mem. of bar, F.M.S.; asst. dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1910; ag. dist. comanr., 1912; asst. col. sec., 1913; res. mag., E. Africa Prots., 1914; seconded as capt., E. African Forces, 1915-1918; res. comanr., Kenya, 1921; senr. res. mag., Jan., 1923.
CARR, EDWARD THOMAS WHITMORE, B.A., T.C.D.—B. 1883; sub-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, Oct., 1907; inspr., July, 1916.

CARR, FREDERICK BERNARD.—B. 1893; admstr. offr., cls. IV, 1919; asst. sec., S. Provs., secretariat, 1925, 1930 and 1931; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1928, 1929 and 1930; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1930; sec., coman. of inquiry in S.E. provs., 1930; ag. prin. asst. sec., S. provs., secretariat, 1930 and 1932; admstr. serv., cls. II, 1933; ag. res., 1928, 1933, 1934 and 1935.

CARR, JAMES BENJAMIN.—B. 1889; ed. Bedford Mod. Schol. and Culham Training Coll. (Oxford Diocesan); European asst. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1911; with H.M. Forces, Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1920; Raffles Ist., S'pore, Jan., 1921; headmast., T. Kurau govt. eng. schl., June, 1926; asst. inspr., schls., S'pore, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1926; headmast., K. Pilah govt. eng. schl., Dec., 1928; headmast., Rangoon Rd. schl., S'pore, Dec., 1930.

CARRIE, WILLIAM JAMES, M.A., B.Sc. (1914).—B. 1891; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; cadet, civ. ser., Hong Kong, 1914; passed in Cantonese, 1917; on military service, 1918 to 1919; asst. col. treas., 1920; J.P., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1922; sec., Sui On piracy comanr., 1922; sec., stocks and shares comanr., 1923; asst. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1926; head, sany. dept., Nov., 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Oct., 1928; head, sany. dept., 1929 and Nov., 1934; sec., salaries comanr., 1928 and 1929 in addn.; supt., census, 1930-31; senr. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1931; ag. P.M.G., 1933.
CARROW, Lieut.-Commdr. JOHN HINTON, R.N. (Ret.), D.S.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll.; admstr. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, June, 1919; ditto, cls. I, Dec., 1932.
CARRUTHERS, ANGUS WILLIAM.—B. 1879; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edin.; Impl. serv., 1897; O.F.S. serv., 1904; asst. dir., census, U. of S.A., Feb., 1918; dir., census, Jan., 1919.

CARSON, ANDREW HOWARD, A.M.I.E.E., M.I.R.E., Chtd. Elec. Engr.—B. 1895; ed. High Schol., R. Acad. and Educln. Inst., Inverness, Bonn Univ. Tech. Coll.; Br. P.O. eng. dept., Jan., 1912; asst. teleg. engr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; engr., P. & T., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; ag. ar. engr., on various occasions, 1932-34; ar. engr., and sec. to Johore as ch. teleg. engr., July, 1934.

CARSON, JAMES COBURN, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Queens Univ., Belfast), D.T.M. (Meth. Coll., Belfast).—B. 1894; 11th Battn. R. Irish Rifles and 126th Baluchistan Infny., 1915-18; med. offr., S.S., Apr., 1924; gen. hosp., Penang, May, 1924; ag. prof., anatomy, med. coll., S'pore, May, 1926-Jan., 1927; med. offr., S'pore, June, 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1928 and Sept., 1931; med. offr., P. Wellesley, Jan., 1932; do. S'pore, June, 1933; do., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1933.
CARSTAIRS, CHARLES YOUNG.—B. 1910; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; M.A. (hons. in hist.); apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., D.O., 24th Oct., 1934.

CARTER, CYRIL WALTER, Assoc. M. Inst. C.E., M. Int. Nav. C.—B. 1900; ed. Berkhamsted Schol. and Crystal Palace Engng. Schol.; mily. serv., 1918-19; seconded to Tanganyika Govt., 1927; dep. res. engr., Egyptian govt., 1929; exec. engrnr.-in-ch., harbr. wks., Tanganyika, 1930; senr. ast. engr., harbr. dept., Nigeria, 1934; ag. port engr., 1935.
CARTER, EDWARD STANLEY.—B. 1888; ed.; Carpenter's Co. Tech. Inst.; war serv., 1914-19; ast. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; seconded, Kowloon-Canton Rly., 1926-27 and in 1929.
CARTER, HUGH CLAY, M.C., M.E.I. (Canada).—B. 1884; ed.; St. Albans Schl.; ast. engr., Br. Honduras, Jan., 1921; ag. dir., pub. wks., Apr., to Dec., 1923; D.P.W., Br. Honduras, May, 1924.
CARTER, WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; ast. inspr., schl., Barbados, 1834.

CARTER, SIR WILLIAM MORRIS, KT. BACH. (1919), C.B.E. (1918), B.A., B.C.L.—Ed. at King's schl., Canterbury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; open exhib. in mod. hist., Jan., 1892; class. mods. (honours), 1894; 2nd cls. hona. juris., 1896; certif. of honour, bar exam., 1899; awarded spec. prize in English constitutional law and legal hist. by coun. of legal educn., 1899; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1899; registr. and prin. regisr. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1902; ag. town mag., Mombasa, Apl., 1902; mag., 1st Oct., 1902; judge of H.M. high ct. of Uganda and H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Nov., 1903; ag. prin. judge and legal adviser, Apl. to Sept., 1904; ag. prin. judge, Mar. to Oct., 1907, Aug. to Dec., 1909, and Aug., 1911, to Jan., 1912; apptd. comsnr. to enquire into native land tenure, 1906; mem. of comtees. to consider land legislation, 1906; and to draft land legislation, 1909; president, native land settm. comtee., 1911; chief just., Uganda Prot., July, 1912; joint compiler of first edition of laws of Uganda Prot., 1909; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Oct., 1920; ret., 1924; ohmn., cotton enquiry comtn., Uganda, 1929.

CARTMEL-ROBINSON, HAROLD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; probtnr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1912; ast. native comsnr., 1914; war serv., 1917-19; native comsnr., 1918; ast. mag., 1926; ag. prov. comsnr. for short periods in 1928, 1931, 1932 and 1933; prov. comsnr., 1934; ag. senr. prov. comsnr., 1935.
CARTWRIGHT, CRIL GEORGE FOX.—B. 1911; ed. Winchester Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1934; dep. comsnr. (temp. and provtn.), 1934.
CASEY, NORMAN BRANDRETH, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1908; gaoler, 1917; ag. supt., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen., prisons, 1920; supt., prisons, 1923.

CASEY, HON. RICHARD GARDINER, D.S.O., M.C., M.A., M.P.—Polit. liaison offr. in London between Br. and Australian Govts., 1924-31; M.P., Corio, H. of R. of C'wealth of Australia since 1931; ast. treas., 1933-35; treas., 1935.
CASHIN, HON. SIR MICHAEL PATRICK, K.B.E. (1918); B. 1866; M.H.A., Newfoundland; mem. for Ferryland since 1893; mem. exec. coun. and min. of finance and customs, 1909; min. of finance, Jan., 1918.
CASTONGUAY, JULES.—B. 1882; ast. in office of clk. of crown in chancery for Canada, 1908; ast. ch. electoral offr., 1920; ch. electoral offr., 1927.
CATCHPOLE, CAPT. E. K.—B. 1889; Rhodesian pol., 1910-15; K.A.R., 1915-20; ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1920; supt., 1928.

CATOR, GEOFFREY EDMUND, C.M.G. (1936), B.A.—B. 1884; ed. King's schl., Bruton and Selwyn Col., Camb. (Scholar); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1907; passed cadet, June, 1909; offr. cls. V. Mar., 1911; ag. res., Labuan, in addn., Feb., 1917; offr. cls. IV., Jan., 1918; offr. cls. III., Nov., 1919; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1924; mem. stand. advry. comtee. on use opium, Malaya, Feb., 1926; temy. mem., legis. council, S.S., May, 1927; offr. cls. IB. dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1928; ag. Br. res., Perak, in addn., June-July, 1928; offr. cls. I A, June, 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., 1930; M.L.C.; Br. res., Selangor, July, 1932; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1933.

CATT, JOHN EDWARD, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1885; ed. Shoreham Gram. Schl. and Brighton Tech. Coll.; ch. engring. lectr., Seafield Park Coll., Hants, 1906; engrnr.-in-ch., S. Nigeria, 1911; lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; ast. elect. engr., K. Lumpur, May, 1919; ex. elect. engr., F.M.S., May, 1925; elect. inspr., Selangor and Pahang, June, 1925; ag. dep. ch. elect. engrnr. and inspr., in addn., Sept.-Oct., 1925; dep. ch. elect. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., Apr., 1932; ag. dir., elec. dept., F.M.S., June, 1934.
CATTANACH, A.—B. 1880; on mily. serv., Feb., 1915 to Feb., 1918; ast. cashier and cashier, Disposal and Liquidation Comtn., 12th May, 1919 to 22nd July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 24th July, 1922.

CAVE, JOHN REGINALD MANNING, O.B.E. (1932), M.S.E.—B. 1873; inspr., water works dept., Barbados, 1895; ast. man., do., 1916; ag. engrnr. and man., do., for various periods, 1922-31; ast. engng. ast., pub. works dept., 1932.

CAVENAUGH, HERBERT JAMES LAWRENCE.—Ed. Ardingly Coll. and King's Coll., London; addl., R.I.C., Dublin for training, May, 1908; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., Dec., 1908; serv. with H.M. Forces, Sept., 1915, June, 1919; capt.; G.S. and Victory Meds., Order of the Nile (4th cl.); ag. staff offr., local forces, Aug. and Sept., 1919; ag. inspr., Trinidad constab., Sept. to May, 1920; ast. of pol., Br. Honduras, 1st June, 1920; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize, Mar.-May, 1921; ag. supt., prisons in addition to substantive appt., Sept., 1924 to Mar., 1926; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, 1927; dir., ditto, 1930.

CAVENDISH, RICHARD CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1886; served with B.S.A. pol., S. Rhodesia, 1906-11; ast. comsnr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1912; King's pol. med., 1926; senr. comsnr., pol., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1927; ast. inspr.-gen., pol., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1930; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Nigeria, 1931; comsnr., pol., Kenya, 1931.

CENTLIVRES, ALBERT VAN DE SANDT, R.A. (Cape and Oxford), K.C.—Rhodes Schl., 1907; S. African Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc., Cape prov. divn., sup. et., S. Africa, 1911; advoc., high et., S. Rhodesia, 1913; partly draftsman, part. of Union of S. Africa, 1920; ag. judge, high et., S. West Africa, 1922; ditto, Cape prov. divn., 1932-33.

CHALMERS, 1ST BARON, OF NORTHAM, (cr. 1919); RIGHT HON. SIR ROBERT CHALMERS, P.C. (Ireland) 1916; G.C.B. (1916); K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1900)—B. 1868; ed. City of London schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; ast. sec. to the Treasy., 1903; chmn., Ed. of In. Rev., 1907; permtn. sec. of Treasy., 1911; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1913; assumed govt., Oct., 1913; joint sec. to the Treas., 1916; also under-sea. for Ireland, May to Sept., 1916; author of "History of Currency in the British Colonies," "A Translation of the Jataka from Pali," etc.

CHAMBERLAIN, GEORGE DIGBY.—B. 1898; B.A.; St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; war serv., 1917-19; lieut., reg. R. of O.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1925; seconded to C.O., as asst. prin., Oct., 1930.
CHAMBERLAIN, KENNETH SEYMOUR JAMES.—B. 1901; treas., Kenya, 1924-33; dep. treas., Fiji, Apr., 1933; ag. treas. and commr., inland rev., June, 1933-Feb., 1934; treas., Gibraltar, 1935.

CHAMBERS, FREDRICK JAMES—B. 1899; lt.-commdr., R.N. (retd.); ed. R.N. Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth; European War, 1914-18; pvt. sec. to H.M. min., Addis Ababa, 1927-28; commr., III. grade, Somaliland, July, 1928.

CHAMBERS, WILFRED METCALFE, M.D. (Brux.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.)—B. 1882; med. offr., Hants. County Asylum, 1910-13; med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., Apr., 1913; med. offr., European hosp., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1923; surgn., Tan Tock Seng hosp., ag. prot., clin. surgery in addn., July, 1923; C.O. deleg., Br. Social Hygiene Cong., Wembley, Oct., 1925; ch. med. offr., Social Hygiene, Jan., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Singapore, in addn., Sept., 1927; ch. med. offr., Penang, Sept., 1927; S.S. deleg. 4th Imp. Social Hygiene Cong., London, July, 1929; ch. med. offr., S'pore, Oct., 1931; ag. dep. dir., med. and health services, S.S., Jan., 1932; dir., do., 1934.

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (1934),—B. 1896; ed. Sutton Valence; commd., E. Surrey Regt. (S.R.), 1913; served France, Egypt and Palestine, 1914-19; O.T.A., Palestine, 1917-19; dist. offr., Hebron, Palestine cov. admnstr., July, 1920; do., Beersheba, 1924; do., Jerusalem 1927; seconded to Aden as 2nd asst. res. and prot. sec., 1929; ag. 1st asst. res., July to Oct., 1929; dist. mag., Aden, 1932; ag. res. and commdr.-in-ch. (ch. commr.), Aden, Apr.-Oct., 1933.

CHANCELLOR, MAJOR SIR JOHN ROBERT, G.C.V.O. (1924), G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1909), D.S.O.—Major R.E.; served with expdn. to Dongola, 1896, with force at Suakin (medal and Egyptian medal); in operations on N.W. frontier of India, 1897-8, with Tirah exped. force, action of Dargai (ment. in despa.); sec. to col. def. comte., Oct., 1906; asst. sec. to Impl. confere. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; gov., Mauritius, July, 1911; gov., Trinidad and Tobago, Dec., 1915; gov., S. Rhodesia, Sept., 1923; high commr. and commr.-in-ch., Palestine, and high commr. for Trans-Jordan, 1928-31.
CHANDLER, MAJOR HERBERT WILLIAM RALPH, M.C.—B. 1894; customs dept., Gold Coast, July, 1920; sr. collr., cust., June, 1928; inspr. cust., Sept., 1933.
CHANDLER, STANLEY FREDERICK.—B. 1898; entd. C.O., Nov., 1913; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1916; on mil. serv., Sept., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

CHANDLER, SIR WILLIAM KELLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1927), KT. BACH. (1915), C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St. John's Coll., Camb. (1875-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar, Inner Tem., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. commr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of ast. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assem., Mar., 1881; represented parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHANTER, ALFRED BERNARD, M.Inst.T.—B. 1884; ast. traffic supdt. and ag. dist. traffic supdt., S. Indian rly., 1907-17; dist. traffic supdt., E. African Milly. rlys., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), Aug., 1917-19; ag. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., in 1920, 1921 and 1923; traffic man., June, 1923.

CHAPLIN, SIR FRANCIS DRUMMOND PERCY, G.B.E. (1923), K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1866; ed. Harrow and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; barrister, Lincoln's Inn, 1891; pres. of chamber of mines, Transvaal, 1905; M.H.A., Transvaal, 1907-10; M.L.A. for Germiston, Union of South Africa; admtr. of Southern Rhodesia, 24th Dec., 1914, and at N. Rhodesia, March, 1921; resigned both appts., Sept., 1923.
CHAPMAN, HON. SIR FREDERICK REVANS, KT. BACH. (1923).—B. 1849; judge of sup. ct., New Zealand, 1903-21; pres. of ct. of arbit., 1903-7; compiler of statutes, 1921-25.

CHAPMAN, GROFFRY WALTER, B.A. and Dipl. in Forestry (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. Cranbrook Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; hon. exhib. in hist.; nats. nat. sci., 3rd class; post-grad. probationers' course at Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; ast. conserv., forests, 1st grade, Cyprus, Sept., 1930.

CHARLEWOOD, LIEUT.-COMMDR. CLEMENT JAMES, D.S.C. (1914), R.N.R. (Ret.).—B. 1888; ed. Newcastle Prep. Schl., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, and on board H.M.S. "Worcester" from May, 1903 to July, 1906; midshipman in R.N.R., 4th Aug., 1906; employed by British India Steam Navigation Co., Ltd., July 1909 to Aug., 1914; comd. small craft taking part in blockade and subsequent capture of coast of German E. Africa, being selected by Rear-Adm. E. Charlton to rec. surrendr. of Darressalaam on 4th Sept., 1916; attached to Zanzibar govt. to superint. docking and repairs to Zanzibar govt. steamers "Cupid" and "Khalifa," July, 1917 to Apr., 1918; apptd. to H.M.S. "Oroonoo" for escort duties in Nth. Atlantic, Apr. to Nov., 1913; ast. port offr., Zanzibar, Apr., 1919; port. offr., 5th Dec., 1923; lieut. commdr., R.N.R. (ret. list), 10th Apr., 1925; Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 3rd cls., 1934.

CHARNAUD, CECIL ARTHUR ROY, LT. COMMDR. R.N. (Retd.).—B. 1903; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; ret., 1927; lt.-commdr. (retd.), 1933; cadet, N. Rhodesia, July, 1929; dist. offr., Grade III, Aug., 1931; seconded as ast. sec., secre., Jan., 1934; attd., C.O., Feb., 1935.

CHARTER, ARTHUR ERNEST, O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1882; Transvaal land attit., bd., 1901; land dept., 1902; priv. sec. to commr. of lands, 1903; ag. ch. clk., land and irrigtn. dept., 1908; ch. clk., adminstr.'s off., June, 1910; prov. sec., Natal, Feb., 1928.
CHASE, LISLE AUBREY.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, 1908; war serv., 1917-19; supt. govt. indust. schle., 1932.

CHASEN, FREDERIC NUTTER.—B. 1896; trooper, Norfolk Yeomanry (K.O.R.R.), Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., Suffolk Regt., Mar., 1919; asst. curator, Raffles Museum, S'pore, Jan., 1921; curator, Mar., 1924; dir., Raffles Museum and Library, S'pore, Mar., 1932.
CHAU, Kwan Lam, M.A. (Durham), Dip. Ed. (Lond.).—B. 1890; mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1923; inspr., Eng. prte. schls. in addn. 1926.

CHAYTOR, C. J.—B. 1889; ed., King's Schl., Worcester; served in Royal Navy, 1906-12 (asst. paymr.); asst. res., N. Nigeria; attd. as lieut., Cameroons Expdy. Force, Aug., 1914 to May, 1916; seconded to Adm'y., Aug., 1917; served as paymr. lieut.-commdr., H.M.S. Liverpool, Adriatic, Mediterranean and Black Seas; demob., May, 1919; 1st cls. interpreter, French and higher standard Hausa, 1919; lower standard Arabic, 1924; temp. attd. to C.O., 1927.

CHAYTOR, MAJ.-GEN. SIR EDWARD WALTER CLERVAUX, K.C.M.G. (1878), K.C.V.O., C.B. (D.), A.D.C. TO THE KING.—B. 1868; jnd Marlborough Hussar Vol. (N.Z.), 1886; lieut., Oct., 1888; capt., Mar., 1893; maj. in N.Z. Militia, Mar., 1901; lieut.-col., Jan., 1902; temp. col., Jan., 1911; transf'd. to N.Z. Staff Corps, Jan., 1911; prom. col., June, 1913; maj.-gen., Apr., 1917; comdt.; N.Z. mil. forces, Dec., 1919; ret., Mar., 1924; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (severely wounded); mentd. in desps.; (Queen's med., three clasps, King's med., two clasps); left N.Z. with main body N.Z. Exped. Force 1914 as A.A.G. with rank of col.; temp. brig.-gen., Dec., 1918; temp. maj.-gen. while employed as a divil. comdr., Apr., 1917, and maj.-gen. in N.Z.E.F., Apr., 1917; apptd. G.O.C., N.Z.E.F. in Egypt; Order of the White Eagle, 3rd cls. (with swords) (Serbian), 1916; Order of the Nile, 2nd cls., 1918; mentd. in desps. (6).

CHEESEMAN, HAROLD AMBROSE ROBINSON.—B. 1889; ed., Perse Schl.; European mast., Penang Free Schl., Sept., 1907; instruc., Penang normal classes in addn., Aug., 1908; snr. instruc., ditto, Apr., 1909; headmast., Northam rd. schl., Aug., 1922; instruc., schls., Penang, Jan., 1923; supt., edun., Johore, Mar., 1928; inspr., schls., Singapore and Labuan, 1934; del. to Imp. confce. on social hygiene, July, 1934.

CHELMSFORD, 3rd BARON (U.K.), creat. 1885, Frederic John Napier Thesiger, C.M.G., C.M.G. (1912), K.C.M.G.—B. 1868; ed. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. law), 1891; M.A., 1894; Fellow of All Souls, 1892; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1893; mem. of London schl. bd., 1901-4; mem. of L.C.C., 1904; gov. of Queensland, 8th Aug., 1906; gov. of N.S. Wales, 16th Mar., 1909, to Mar., 1913; ag. gov. gen. of Australia, Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; chancellor of the Order of St. M. and St. G., 1914-1916; viceroy of India, 1916; first lord of the adm'y., Jan.-Nov., 1924.

CHEVERTON, REGINALD LESLIE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1901; casualty house surg., 1927; house surg., 1927, and house physician, 1928, gen. hosp., Birmingham; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Dec., 1928; ag. dir., health, Aug., 1933; ag. surg. specialist, June-Sept., 1933; senr. med. offr., Falkland Is., 1935.

CHILCOT, THOMAS CHARLES.—B. 1883; ent. C.A. shipping agts., 1902; apptd. to C.A. office, 1912; asst. head, gen. dept., Mar., 1922; dep. head, pay dept., Nov., 1933; acctnt., E. African currency bd., 1920.
CHILTON, N. B.A. (Hons.), B.M., B.Ch. (Oxf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1900; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1929.
CHINTAMANI, R.—B. 1878; apptd. cls. III Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1934; office asst., Puttalam kach., Feb., 1934.

CHISHOLM, SIR JOSEPH ANDREW, KT. BACH. (1935)—B. 1863; ed. at pub. schls., St. Andrews, Univ. of St. Francis Xavier, Antigonish (B.A. and M.A.), and Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax (LL.B.); admitted to the bar, 1886; mayor of Halifax, 1909, 1910 and 1911; pres. of Union of Canadian Municipalities, 1910; K.C., 1907, puisne judge, sup. ct., Nova Scotia, Feb., 1916; ch. just., June, 1931.

CHRISTIAN, JOHN HAROLD SMITH, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. Alderman Newton's schl., Leicester; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1904; on mil. serv., 4th Aug., 1914 to 8th June, 1919; ment. in desps., Feb., 1915; commanded cavalry corps signals, 1919; loaned to C.O., 17th Jan., 1921; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to C.O., 1st Apr., 1921; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

CHRISTIANI, HENRY PEROT, M.B.E. (1936), F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; 6th cls. clk., cust., Br. Guiana, 1897; 5th cls. clk., treasy., 1903; 3rd cls. offr. and govt. survr., lands and mines dept., 1904; 2nd cls. offr. and govt. survr., 1908; drawing office supp., 1924; sec., lands and mines dept., 1927; sec. and acctnt., dept. of lands and mines, 1929; ag. comnr., lands and mines, 1933.

CHRISTIE, JAMES, C.M.G. (1934), LL.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1880; ed. State schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; schl. teacher, 1897; N.Z. treas. dept., 1901; crown law office, 1904; law drafting office, 1907; asst. law draftsman, 1912; ag. law draftsman, 1916; law draftsman, 1918.

CHRISTISON, FRED HAMILTON, M.B.E. (mily.) (1918).—Asst. treas., G. Coast, 1901-03; temp. coman., army pay dept., 1915; K.A.R. pay dept., July, 1916; ag. ch. paymr., K.A.R., 1917; ment. in desps., 1917; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; senr. asst. treas., May, 1930; ag. dep. treas., Oct., 1932-Apr., 1933.

CHRISTOFFELSZ, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1890; B.A., LL.B., Cantab.; barrister-at-law; local div., Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1915; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Mar., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in addition to his own duties, July, 1915; attached to Kegalle Kachcheri, Apr., 1916; addtl. comanr. of requests and pol. mag., Kegalle, in addn. to own duties, Apr., 1916; ag. extra office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1916; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, 1917; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, Mar., 1919; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala, Sept., 1921; asst. stlmt. offr., Oct., 1926; ag. pol. mag., Panadura, Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. dist. judge, Matare, Mar., 1927; asst. stlmt. offr., Dec., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, June, 1931; do., Puttalam, Sept., 1933.
CHRISTOFFELSZ, BASIL MORRIS.—B. 1886; cls. V, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1931; office asst., Kalutara kach., Feb., 1931; do., Matare kach., Jan., 1934.

CHURCHILL, WILLIAM FOSTER NORTON.—B. 1898; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb. (exhib.), B.A. (1921); 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Aug., 1917; served in France, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., dist. office, Kajang, May, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Apr., 1924; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Apr., 1926; priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; comm. and sec., sany. bd., Larut, Aug., 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; dist. offr., Lipis, Aug., 1934.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER, P.C. (1907).—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906-8; for Dundee, 1908; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1895; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1895 (1st cls. (Spanish) Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (despa., medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspdt., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton Homes, Venter's Spruit, Hussar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Krantz, and Pieters; also operations round De wetshoek, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); parly. under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1906; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; pres. of Bd. of T., 1908; Home sec., 1910-11; first lord of the Admiralty, 1911; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, 1915; min. of munitions, 1917; sec. of state, War Office and Air Ministry, Jan., 1919; S. of S. for the Colonies, 14th Feb., 1921 to 25th Oct., 1922; chancellor of the exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill."

CHURMS, W. H.—B. 1890; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1911; on military service from 5th Aug., 1914, to 19th Jan., 1919; paper room clk. in library, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Apr., 1921.

CILLIERS, CHARL. ANDRIES, B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch (now Univ. of Stellenbosch); clk., defence dept., Union of S.A., 1913; pvtc. sec. to min. of lands, 1916; ditto, to min. of defence, 1917; ditto, to min. of agr., 1921; under-sec. for pub. wks., 1934.

CLARENDON, EARL OF (cr. 1776) Rt. Hon. GEORGE HERBERT HYDE VILLIERS, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G., (1930), J.P., D.L.—B. 1877; ed. Eton; prev., lord-in-waiting to H.M.; hon. lieut.-col. (Hertford Reg.) A.D.C. to Lord Lieut. of Ireland (Lord Dudley); chanc., Primrose League, 1919-21; capt., Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1922-23 and 1924-25; dep. speaker, House of Lords; parly. under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., and chmn., overseas settlement ctes., 9th July, 1925; chmn. B.B.C., 1927; gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1931.

CLARK, ADRIAN JOHN, LL.B. (Lond.), O.B.E. (Milv.)—B. 1889; King's schol. and Goodenough medallist (Fr. and German), Westminster; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; junr. coun. to treasy., 1917; served in Great War, France, 1915-19; capt., 23rd London Regt.; D.A.A.G., Third Army and lieut.-col., R.A.F., (S.O., 1st grade); brevet-major and twice ment. in despa.; mag., Victoria, Trinidad, July, 1924; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Dec., 1924; P.O.S., Trinidad, Jan., 1927; puisne judge, Jamaica, May, 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Nov., 1932; dep. pub. pros., S'pore, Sept., 1934; puisne judge, S.S., 1936.

CLARK, ERNEST MALCOLM, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1908; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., Lond.; late senr. res. med. offr., West Suffolk Gen. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Nov., 1933.

CLARK, JOHN BUCHANAN.—B. 1897; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; S.A. Coll. Schls. and S.A. Coll. (now Univ. of Cape Town); Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.A. (Cape Town), B.A. (Hons.), Oxon.; Rhodes schol., 1917; 2nd lt. and lt., R.A.S.C., France, Aug., 1917 to Feb., 1919; lect. in hist., Univ. of Cape Town, 1921; lect., classics and hist., Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; imprs., schls., June, 1925; ag. dir., European educn., June, 1926-June, 1927; dir., European educn., N. Rhodesia, June, 1927; mem., European educn. comsn., N. Rhodesia, 1929; temp. M.L.C. in 1930, 1931 and 1935; ag. dir. native educn., 1935.

CLARK, J. MCK., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), D.T.M. (Liverpool), Certif., Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.—Apptd. to W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1914; served in Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1915-16; temp. lieut. (gen. list), 1915-16; temp. med. offr., E. Africa, 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 19th Apr., 1918; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1924.
CLARK, RICHARD JOHN BOND, A.R.I.B.A. (chart. archt.).—B. 1896; ed. Cornwall County Schl.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

CLARK, WILLIAM CLIFFORD, C.M.G. (1935), M.A., LL.D.—B. 1889; ed. Williamstown High Schl., Queen's Univ. (Kingston) and Harvard Univ. (Ricardo prize scholarship, Harvard Univ., 1915); Henry Lee memorial fellow and tutor, divn. of hist., govt. and economics, Harvard Univ., 1914-15; successively lecturer, asst. prof., assoc. prof. and prof., economics, Queen's Univ., 1915-23; advr., dept. of lab., Ottawa, 1918-19; prof., commerce, etc., Queen's Univ., 1931-32; finan. advr., Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; dep. min., finance and sec. treasry. bd., Ottawa, Nov., 1932; off. advr., World Monetary and Economic Confce., 1933; author of various papers in economics and finance and collaborator in War-show's "Representative American Industries."

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1930), C.S.I. (1911), C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1876; Ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; clk. Bd. of T., 1899; sec. to ap. mission to Shanghai to negotiate comm'l. treaty with China, 1901; ag. 2nd sec., diplomatic serv., 1902; sec. to royal comsn. on supply of food, etc., in time of war, 1903-5; pvtc. sec. at Bd. of T. to Rt. Hon. D. Lloyd George, 1906, and to Rt. Hon. W. S. Churchill, 1908; and to Rt. Hon. D. Lloyd George as chancellor of the exchequer, 1908-10; mem. for commerce and industry of the coun. of the Viceroy of India, 1910-16; compt.r-gen., comm'l. intell. dept. Bd., of T., 1916; compt.r-gen. dept. of overseas trade, Sept., 1917; high comsnr. for Great Britain in Canada, Sept., 1928; high comsnr. for Basutoland, the Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland and high comsnr. in the Union of S. Africa for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., Jan., 1935.

CLARKE, ARTHUR GRENFELL, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Mountjoy Schl., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1929; passed cadet, Jan., 1932; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Feb., 1932; J.P., 1935.
CLARKE, F. H.—Asst. game warden, R.A.P., 1919; ag. game warden, Kenya, 1932.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FRANCIS GRENVILLE, K.B.E., M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. Scotch Coll., Melbourne, Melbourne Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.L.C. for N. Prov. (Vic.); min. for lands and water supply, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1919; min. of pub. wks. and water supply, Oct., 1919 to July, 1923; pres., leg. coun., Victoria, July, 1923.

CLARKE, Hon. Sir Frederick James, K.C.M.G. (1911), C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. Christ Church Mid. Schl., Barbados, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1879; M.H.A., Barbados, 1887; pres., Barbados agric. soc'y, 1905; mem., cent. poor law board; M.E.C., 1905; J.P.; O.C., Barbados vol. force, 1906; speaker, H. of A., Barbados, 1898-1934.

CLARKE, Francis Priestley Courtney.—B. 1879; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A., Oxford, 1901; LL.B., Cape, 1909; advoc., sup. ct., Cape, 1910; ent. Cape civ. ser., 1903; served in depts. of justice, native affs. and forests; ch. clk., S.W.A. admtn., 1923; asst. sec., 1925; sec. for S.W.A., 1934; chmn., S.W.A.—Angola bdry., and Cunene Water coman., 1929; rep. Union govt. at Geneva at mtgs. of Mandates Coman., 1930.
CLARKE, Geoffrey T. S.—B. 1879; clk. to P.M.O., Basutoland, 1913; clk., treas., 1914; clk. to asst. comanr., 1919; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Police, 1921; war serv., 1916-19.
CLARKE, Joseph Sherwood, B.A. (Harvard).—B. 1903; ed. Rossall Schl. and Harvard Univ.; supt., educn., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; headm., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1931.

CLARKE, Robert William, F.I.C., diploma, food and drugs, agric. chem., biol. chem., and bacteriology.—Analyst to sany. comanrs., Gibraltar, Oct., 1913 to Jan., 1921; gov't. analyst, Cyprus, Jan., 1921.

CLARKE, Stephen Grainger.—B. 1894; 5th ols. clk., P.W.D., Fiji, 1914; 4th ols. clk., audit dept., 1915; on active ser., 1915; 3rd ols. clk., audit dept., 1922; 2nd ols. clk., 1922; acctnt., Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1924; dep. comanr. (temp. and provln.), 1926; treas., collr., cust. and ch. postmr., 1928; dep. comanr., 1929; ag. res. comanr., 1935.

CLARKE, Thomas Lisle Evelyn, M.D., C.M., McGill (1911).—B. 1885; ed. at the Lodge Schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and McGill Univ., Montreal; junr. res. surgeon, general hosp., Barbados, Aug., 1911; med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1912; ag. comanr. and mag., May, 1912; mem. of quarantine bd., health offr., mem. of exec. coun., and J.P., Virgin Is., July, 1912; ag. comanr., Virgin Is., Apr., 1913 to Jan., 1914; ag. comanr., mag. and dep. judge, summary jurisdiction ct., Virgin Is., from Dec., 1914 to May, 1917, and from July, 1917 to Oct., 1918; med. offr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1918; provisional mem. fed. leg. and exec. couns., Leeward Islands, Mar., 1915; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

CLARKE, W. W.—B.1895; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., S. Africa; war serv., Rebellion and German S.W. campaign, 1914-15; S. Africa Overseas Contxt., 1916-18; temp. lieut., 1918; supt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. senr. supt., cust., Feb.-May, 1930; senr. supervisor, cust., 1935.
