Leone, July, 1914; curator of intestate estates, Oct., 1914; regisr., prize court, 1914; 2nd lieut., general list, Mar., 1916; mentioned in desps., 1916; attached S. Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., May, 1916; served in S. Leone, 1916-17; transfd. to G. Coast Regt., 1917; served in Port. E. Africa, 1918; staff capt. with Col. Rose's column, Apr. to June, 1918; staff quartermr., 2nd W. African Service Brig., Oct., 1918; demob., Dec., 1918; resumed duties as regisr., sup. ot., Sierra Leone, May, 1919 regisr., sup. ct., S.S., Nov., 1920.

DAVIS, Sir Charles Thomas, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1923), C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1873; ed. Christ's Coll.; Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol Coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical mods., 1894; Jenkyns exhibnr., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., acct-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 5th, 1896; transf. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr. 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comttee., 1900; priv. sec., to Sir M. Ommannye, June, 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905; principal clk., 4th June, 1916; senr. asst. sec. to Imperial Conference; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1921; perm. under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925; ret., 1930.

DAVIS, Hon. Henry Hague.—B. 1885; ed. Brockville, Ont., Can.; Collegiate Inst., Toronto Univ. (B.A. 1907, gold medallist in polit. sci., M.A., LL.B.); Law Sch., Osgoode Hall, Toronto, 1911; called to Ontario bar 1911; K.C., 1928; judge, supreme ct., Ont., 1933.

DAVIS, Richard Pritchard, M.I.M. & C.E., M.T.P.I., M.R.S.I.—B. 1888; town planning supt., Selangor, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; town planner, Selangor, July, 1932; do., Perak, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Oct., 1933.

DAVIS, Sir Steuart Spencer, Kt. Bach. (1930), C.M.G. (1919)—B. 1875; rev. off., St. Kitts, 1893; 1st clk., treas. and cust., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevia def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; att. col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., July, 1901; acctn., treasy., G. Coast, 1901; asst. treas., 1902; ch. asst. treas., Nov., 1908; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; treas., civil admnstr., German Africa (occupied territory), Nov., 1916; treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. sec. to admnstr., June to Aug., 1919; dep. admnstr., Mar.-Apr., 1920; treas., Palestine, May, 1922; chmn., economy comttee., 1922; ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1923-25; chmn., currency comttee., 1924; del. for Palestine at Constantinople in connec. with Ottoman pub. debt, Oct. to Nov., 1924; chmn., comn. on finances of Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, Dec., 1925; chmn., standing comttee. for commerce and industry from 1928; O.A.G. Palestine and ag. high, comntr., Trans-Jordan, July-Sept., 1930; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., St. Helena, 1932.

DAVIS, Thomas Arthur Warren.—B. 1899; ed. Haverfordwest and Keble Coll., Oxford; Indian army, 1917-22; dipl., forestry (Oxon.), 1923; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. conserv., forests, July-Dec., 1930 and Feb.-Oct., 1934.

DAVSON, Sir Edward, 1st Baronet (cr. 1927), Kt. Bach (1919)—Ed. Eton; pres., associated West Indian Chambers of Commerce, 1916-26; chmn., Imp. Cocoa Comf., 1924; mem., Imp., Economic Comttee., 1925; chmn., federation of Chambers of Commerce of Br. Empire, 1925-28; chmn., W. Indies Comf., 1929; advr. on col. trade to S. of S. for the Cols., Ottawa Comf., 1932 and World Economic Comf., London, 1933; rep. of cols. on Imp. Communications Advisory Comttee. and Imp. Economic Comttee.; a trustee of Imp. Inst., and gov., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agric.

DAVSON, George Charles Wolsley, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engnr., Nigeria, July, 1913; asst. engnr., Nigerian Rly., 1915; cadet as S.M.E., Chatham, 1916; lieut., R.E., 1917; ag. capt., 1919; asst. engnr., S.S., June, 1919; ex. engnr., Nov., 1928; senr. ex. engnr., Sept., 1933.

DAWE, Arthur James, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Berkhamsed Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; scholar, 1910-1914; B.A.; war service, 1914-18; staff, 1917; lieut., R.N.V.R. (R.N.D.); temp. appt. in C.O., Jan., 1918; apptd., under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., June, 1919; asst.prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. Amery, 18th June, 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. E. F. L. Wood, 1921; prin., 1st Dec., 1921; dep. sec. to Imp. Economic Comf., 1923; visited Sierra Leone as sec. to coman. of enquiry into affrs. of Freetown municipality, 1928; accompa. parly U.S. of S. on visit to Palestine and Cyprus, 1930; sec. to Malta royal comn., 1931; sent on mission to Malta, 1933-34; asst. sec., 6th Mar., 1936.

DAWE, Morley Thomas O.B.K. (1925), F.L.S.—B. 1880; asst. botanist, forestry and scientific dept., Uganda, 1902; head of dept., 1903; dir., agric., Mozambique, 1910; agric., advr. to Colombia, 1915; comand. by C.O. to visit and report on agric. conditions and needs of Gambia, 1920; consultg. agric. expert to Fomento Geral de Angola, 1921; comntr., lands and forests, S. Leone, 1922; comntr. repg. S. Leone at British Empire exhibn., 1924; comntr. repg. S. Leone at Canadian Nat. exhibn., Toronto, 1926; dir. agr., Cyprus, 1928; mem., leg., May, 1928 to Nov., 1931; headed Cyprus trade mission, June-July, 1930; rep. govt., 9th Internat. horticultural cong., London, 1930, comf. on fruit production, London, 1930 and 5th Internat. botanical cong., 1930; dir., agr. and forests, Palestine, 1933; mem., advy. coun., chmn., gen. agric. coun. and mem. standing comttee. for commerce and industry.

DAWSON, Christopher William, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); 2nd. lieut., 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; served India (N. W. Frontier, Mesopotamia); demobilised with rank of capt., 1919; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1920; 2nd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1922; 2nd asst. advr., B. Pahat, Nov., 1923; 1st mag. and regisr., sup. ct., Johore, Mar., 1924; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1927; called to bar (Grays Inn), June, 1929; dist. judge, Malacca, Sept., 1929; 2nd asst. sec., S.S., Jan., 1930; 1st asst. sec., S.S., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, July, 1933.
DAWSON, Ernest Edward.—B. 1886; B.A. (engg.) Cambridge with 2nd cls. hon., A.M.I.C.E.; Canadian N. rlv., 1910; asst. engnr., Tanganyika rly., Jan., 1921; dist. engnr., Nov., 1928.
DAWSON, Percy Lee.—B. 1890; ed. Needham House (Ely), Cambs.; office asst., Nigerian Rly., 1920; asst. traffic supt., 1921; dist. traffic supt., Feb., 1935.
DAY, Sir Albert Cecil, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1925), C.B.E. (1919).—Asst. priv. sec. to gov. of New Zealand, 1910-1912; offl. sec. to gov.-gen. since 1913.

DAY, Edward Victor Grace.—B. 1896; ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch and Timaru High Schl., New Zealand; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1921; attd., col. sec. office, Nov., 1921; asst. sec., Aluana Borneo Exhibn., Dec., 1921; A.D.C. to O.A.G., June, 1922; sec., sany. bd., A. Star in addn., Feb., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec., S.S., Nov., 1933.

D'CRUZ, ALBERT WALTER JOSEPH, L.M.S. (S'pore).—B. 1885; asst. aurg., Tan Tock Seng's Hosp., S'pore., Apr., 1908; ag. health offr. quarantine, June 1910 and Nov., 1918; asst. health offr. port, Aug., 1911, Sept., 1915, Mar., 1919 and Apr., 1921; ag. med. offr., Labuan, Dec., 1920; dep. health offr. port, S'pore., Jan., 1923; do., but sr. Jan., 1930; health offr., port, S'pore., Dec., 1930.

DEAKIN, CHARLES SIDNEY, B.A.—B. 1897; ed. at Orme Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne, Christ's Hosp. and Queen's Coll., Camb., schol., Hughes Eng. essay prize, 1st cl. mechan. tripods, engng., 1923; research grant, bridge oscillations, 1923-24; junr., engng dept., science and indust. research, bridge stress comm., 1924-25; John Wimbolt prize, engin. research, 1925; R.A.S.C. (M.T.), 1916; Iraq, 1917; addl. polit. dept. as Q.M.S., Native Militia and hd. clk., Zubair, 1919; demob., 1920; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

DEAN, H. E. S.—African constab., 1902-07; asst., fencing dept., E.A.P., May, 1908; transport offr., Nairobi, 1908-11; office supt., P.W.D., B.E.A., 1911-16; acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1916-21; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1921-25; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; treas., Dominica, 1932.

DEANE, SIR GEORGE CAMPBELL, KT. BACH. (1930).—B. 1873; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; M.A., Oxon (honours in jurisprudence); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1898; practised at the bar. B. Guiana, 1898-1903; ditto, Trinidad, 1903-1910; stip. mag., Arima, Trinidad, Sept., 1910; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, May to Dec., 1911, and from May to Oct., 1912; stip. mag., Port of Spain, 1913; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions, 1914-17; twice apptd. comsrr. under ordece, 1919; capt. and adjutant, Trinidad Light Horse; 2nd puisne Judge Apr., 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Jan., 1923; puisne judge, S. Stilments, and judl. comsrr., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1929; pres., W.A. ct. of appl., 1930; ret., Dec., 1933.

DEANE, Lieut.-COL. ROBERT, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; S.A.War, 1900-02; S.A. constab., 1901; Transvaal pol., 1908; S.A. Permt. Force (Staff), 1912; German S.W.A. (staff capt. and brig. machine gun offr.), 1914; W.F.F. Egypt (brig. machine gun offr.), 1st S.A. Inf. Brigade, 1915; France, 1916; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, 1919; dep. inspr. gen. pol., Mauritius, 1922; inspr.-gen. pol. and supt., prisons, 1926; M.L.C., 1926.

DEAR, EUGENE CLYDE.—B. 1885; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1902; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; clk. to atty.-genl., 1924; ag. sol.-gen. for various periods, 1926-28; registr., 1930; judge, asst. ct. of appl., and regir., of friendly socs., 1931.

De BOER, HENRY SPELDERWINDE, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.).—B.1889; ed. privately and at London Hosp.; served with R.A.M.C., 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine and Syria, ment. in desps.); med. offr., Kenya, May, 1920; senr. health offr., E. African med. serv., Jan., 1926; dep. dir., san. services N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1931; do., med. services, 1933; dep. dir., med. services, Uganda, 1933.

DE CARTERET, VALENTINE EDWARD ROSS.—B. 1883; asst. comsrr., pol., Gold Coast, May, 1915; comsrr., Jan., 1922; col. pol. offr.' course, R.I.C., 1915, and senr. pol. offr.' course at New Scotland Yard, 1927; senr. comsrr., pol., 1929.

DE CHAIR, VICE-ADMIRAL SIR DUDLEY RAWSON STRATFORD, K.C.B. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1933), M.V.O. (1908).—B. 1864; mid. of the "Alexandra," at bombardment of Alexandria, 1882; landed with naval brig., naval attache to courts of the Maritime Powers, 1902; and to N. and S. America, 1903-4; A.D.C. to the King, 1911-12; naval sec. to 1st lord of the adm'y., 1912-14; adm'l. of training service, 1914; comdg. tenth cruiser squadron, 1914-16; naval adviser to F.O., 1916; vice-adm'l. comdg. battle squadron, 1917-18; vice-adm'l., 1917; adm'l. comdg. coast guard and reserve, 1919; gov., New S. Wales, Oct., 1923; ret., 1930.

DECK, S. F.—Asst. dist. comsrr. E.A.P., 23rd Aug., 1907; dist. comsrr., May, 1913; offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1914-15; prin. inspr. of labour, Dec., 1920; supt., native lab., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Oct., 1925; ag. senr. comsrr., 1928; 1st grade prov. comsrr., 1931.

DE COMARMOND, HENRI MAXIME.—B.1899; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1922; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for Mauritius and Rodrigues, Aug., 1925; ag. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., on various occasions, 1925-28; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Jan., 1927; 2nd addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Jan., 1928; subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1932; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., May, 1933; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt.

DE FREITAS, SIR ANTHONY, KT. BACH. (1927), O.B.E. (1918), B.A. (Clare Coll., Camb.).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. chief just., St. Vincent May-Oct., 1904; stip. mag., 1st Dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; ag. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, July, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Apr., 1907; chief just., St. Vincent, May, 1915; chief just., St. Lucia, Sept., 1919; ag. administrator., St. Vincent, 1909, 1915, and Mar., 1917, to Dec., 1918; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, on seven occasions; ag. administrator., St. Lucia, on 15 occasions, including July, 1914, to May, 1915; puisne judge, Jamaica, Aug., 1921; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, July, 1922 to Sept., 1923 and Jan., 1925 to Feb., 1926; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1927; ret., 1934.

DE GLANVILLE, R. J. A. P. G.—B. 1885; called to the bar, Bahamas, 1914; notary pub., 1914; priv. sec. to H.E. Wm. Hart Bennett, Esq., C.M.G., adminstr. of Bahamas, 1909; also to H.E. Sir W. Grey-Wilson, K.C.M.G., gov., 1912; ag. coroner for New Providence, 1911 and 1913; confirmed, 1914; 2nd elk., H. of A., 1912; chief elk., 1913; ag. registr., sup. ct. from 1913; ag. stip. and circuit mag. and coroner on various occasions, 1921-31; ag. atty.-gen., advoc. gen. and adm'y. advoc., contr., local clearing office and temp. mem., exec. coun. on various occasions in 1923, 1924 and 1925; registr.-gen., Apr., 1923; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1922-35.

DE HAVILLAND, THOMAS LYTTON, C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1874; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and R. Naval Schl., London; Bechuanaland campaign, 1897; Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; France and Belgium, 1916-19; serjeant-at-arms, H. of A., Union of S. Africa.

DE HEVENINGHAM, N. W. P.—Ed. King's Coll., London and Worcester Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); ent. home civ. serv. (open compet.), 1901; served H.O.L.G. Bd. and miny. of munitions; practised at bar, 1918-24; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is.; mem., advisory coun. and a judl. comr. for W. Pacific, 1924; dep. registr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; ag. registr., May-Dec., 1931; res., mag., 1932; ag. judge, high ct., 1935.
DE JAGER, SAMUEL JACOBUS, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Camb.—B. 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1902; admitted advocate, O.R.C., 1903; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1911; ditto, Cape Prov., 1929.

DE KOCK, GILLES VAN DE WALL.—B. 1889; ed. Stellenbosch, Pretoria, Capetown and London; M.R.C.V.S., Lond. (1913); vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1914; prof. of anatomy, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920; prof. anat., T'vaal Univ. Coll., 1920; sr. vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. services, Onderstepoort, 1927; D.Sc., Witwatersrand Univ., 1928; dep. dir., divn. of vety. services and animal husbandry, 1931.

DE KOCK, MICHIHEL HENDRIK, B.A. (Cape), M.A. and Ph. D. (Harvard).—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Harvard Univ., U.S.A.; sen. lecturer, economics, Univ. of Cape Town, 1923-24; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924-29; mem., diamond control advisory comttee, 1930; S. African Reserve Bank, 1931; dep. gov., S. African Res. Bank, 1932; author of "Finance Of the Union of South Africa," "Government ownership in South Africa," and "Economic History of South Africa."

DE LA HARPE, PETER HENRY, I.S.O. (1935).—B. 1877; apptd. to cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; extra offr., Galle Kach., June, 1923; ditto to col. sec., Aug., 1928; office asst. to col. sec., Sept., 1929; do. to ch. sec., July, 1931.
DE MEZA, JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; vety. offr., Nyasaland Prot., 1914; vety. bact., 1921; ch. vety. offr., 1935.

DENHAM, SIR EDWARD BRANDIS, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1876; ed. at Malvern and Merton Coll., Oxford (exhibtn.) B.A., 1899; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1904, Mar., 1905; asst. govt. agt. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1905; 2nd asst. col. sec. and sec., agric. bd., May, 1906; priv. sec. to Sir A. Ashmore, O.A.G., Sept., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1905; landing survr., cust., Colombo, June, 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Sept., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1909; and priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1909; seconded as supt. of census, Aug., 1910; priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 111; attached to C.O., 1st Jan. to 13th Oct., 1913; prin. asst. col. sec., Ceylon, 25th Apr., 1914; chief oensor, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1914; capt., administrative section, town guard, Colombo, 1915; ag. govt. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1915; offr. of cls. I., grade II. on app't. as dir. of educn., Aug., 1916; dir., food production, Mar.-Dec., 1920; col. sec., Mauritius, 14th Dec., 1920; admstl. govt. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; col. sec., Kenya Col. and Prot., Aug., 1923; admstl. govt., Feb. to Oct., 1925 and from Jan. to Aug., 1927; gov., Gambia, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1930; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1934; joint author of "Rubber in the East."

DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.V.O. (1909).—B. 1874; ed. at Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offs.; late lieut., Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex I.Y.; served in S. Africa war (wounded, Queen's medal, 3 clasps); lord-in-waiting in ordinary, 1905-7; capt. Lon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1907-11; J.P., Sussex; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Commonwealth of Australia, 1911-1914.

DENNIS, OWEN GREGORY.—B. 1894; ent. Army, King's Own Scottish Borderers, 1915; war. serv. in Egypt and France, 1915-18; seconded serv., W.A.F.F., 1919; asst. commnr., pol., Nigeria, 1922; resgd. Army commr., 1923; invalided from col. serv., Nov., 1923; re-apptd. as local commdt., pol. and asst. commr., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; commr. and local commdt., pol., Dec., 1929.

DE NOBRIGA, John.—B. 1884; 3rd clk., P.W.D., Trinidad, Apr., 1901; asst. clk., water and sewerage dept., Aug., 1903; storekeeper and clk., engnr.'s office, Apr., 1905; clk., mines dept., Sept., 1908; ch. clk., ditto, Dec., 1910; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1921; ch. clk. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; 2nd astl. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. 1st astl. col. sec., Feb., 1931; ag. dep. col. sec., May-Aug., 1934; 1st astl. col. sec., Mar., 1935; ag. dep. col. sec., Feb.-Apr., 1936.

DE NORMAN, ALBERT WILFRED NOEL.—B. 1891; ed. United Services Coll. and Chateau du Rosey, Switzerland; Canadian Expedy. Force, 1914; lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1915; West African Forces, 1916-20; survr., Nigeria survey, 1921; asst. survr.gen., 1930; ag. survr.gen., Apr., Sept., 1933; ag. commnr., lands and survr.gen., Mar.-Sept., 1935.

DE PINTO, Cyril Ernest.—B. 1892; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1914; addl., Galle Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; office asst., Batticaloa Kach., Nov., 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, June, 1933; do., Matara, May, 1934.

DE ROME, F. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.B.E.—B. 1886; B.Sc. Lond. Univ. 1908; Jena Univ. (Germany), 1908-1909; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. Hong Kong, 1910; asst. postal censor, 1914-19; asst. mast., cls. II., 1919; asst. mast., cls. I., 1920; ag. headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1923; ag. headmr., Wansai schl., 1923; headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1925; headmr., Queen's Coll., Mar., 1930.
DEERRIMAN, FRANCIS PHILIP LOUIS.—B. 1901; ed. Ratcliffe Coll.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1924; asst. audtr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; do., Nigeria, June, 1934.
DE SANTOS, ERROL LIONEL.—B. 1890; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., Apr., 1912; asst. treas., Jan., 1929; treas. and chinm., agrcl. bank, 1933.
DE SILVA, JOSEPH MARTIN.—B. 1880; apptd. to Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1926; extra off. asst., Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Oct., 1929; ag. office asst., Colombo kach., Feb., 1932.

DE SILVA, WALWIN ARNOLD, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; addl., Kegalle kach., Jan. and Sept., 1928; addl., Jaffna kach., June, 1928; addl., Kegalle kach., Sept., 1928; addl., Matara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., Galle kach., Oct., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Dec., 1934.

DE SOYZA, GUNASENA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1926; addl., Matara kach., Sept., 1926; addl. to Jaffna kach., June, 1927; office asst., Kegalle kach., Mar., 1928; 2nd landing survr., cust., Nov., 1928; office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1931; asst. gov. agt., Badulla, Aug., 1932; do., Mullaitivu, June, 1933; dep., reglar., co-op. socs., Oct., 1934.

DESPICHT, STANLEY MONK.—B. 1890; ed. Boston Gram. Sch.; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi; war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., 1919; dist. commr., July, 1923.
DESROCHERS, FELIX.—B. 1886; ed. St. Hyacinthe Seminary, Quebec; B.A.(1906); LL.L. (1908); called to bar, 1908; ch. libr., liby., Montreal, 1930; gen. libr., liby. of parlmt., Canada, 1933.

DEVAUX, JUSTIN LOUIS.—B. 1884; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1906 (cert. of hon.); revised stand. of St. Lucia, 1916; ag. mag., 1st dist., St. Lucia, Sept., 1917 to Sept., 1918; asst. legal adviser and pol. mag., Seychelles, Mar., 1919; ag. ch. just., Seychelles, Nov., 1919; crown pros., legal adviser, etc., Seychelles, June, 1920; ag. ch. just., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; ch. just., Apr., 1924; gov't. dept., July-Aug., 1923; O.A.G. in 1925, 1927 and 1928; res. mag., Jamaica, 1929; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1931; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1931-Oct., 1932; atty.-gen., July, 1935.

DE VERE, ROBERT STEPHEN, J.P., B.A., LL.B., barrister.—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (B.A., LL.B.); called to bar, 1898; serv. in Paget's Horse, S. African war (Queen's med.); dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1903-06; leg. advdr. and ag. ch. just., Seychelles, 1906-08; recd. comm. in 6th Batt. R. Sussex Regt., Feb., 1915; mil. censor, B.E.F., Aug., 1915; Br. judge, Anglo-French condominium, New Hebrides, 1922; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Grenada, 1931.
DE VERTEUIL, ARTHUR.—B. 1878; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., July, 1899; warden, county of Coron, July, 1929; do., eastern counties, Mar., 1933.

DE VERTEUIL, ERIC J., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1881; ast. med. inspr. of health, Trinidad, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. surg.-gen., May, 1933; senr. med. inspr. of health, Aug., 1935.
DE VERTEUIL, LEON EUGENE.—B. 1877; ast. draughtsman, P.W.D., Trinidad, June, 1900; 2nd grade dist. offr., Oct., 1903; 1st grade dist. offr., Feb., 1911.

DE VERTEUIL, Sr. YVES DANIEL, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 1875; clk. of works, waterworks, Trinidad, Dec., 1900; head overseer, Arima, Jan., 1903; dist. engrnr., 2nd grade, Aug., 1916; dist. engrnr., 1st grade, Sept., 1916; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, Dec., 1925; warden, St. George, prot. of immigrts. and dir., lab. exchange, Mar., 1933.

DE VILLIERS, ISAAC PIERRE, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. S. African Coll.; atty. and notary, served S.W. Africa, in 1st Eastern Rifles, 1914-15; France and Palestine, in R.F.A., 1916-19; dep. commr. (headqrs.) S.A. pol., Feb., 1928; commr., S. African pol., Dec., 1928.

DE VILLIERS, SIR JEAN ETIENNE REENEN, M.A., LL.D., Kt.Bach. (1923).—B. 1875; ed. S. African Coll. and Cambridge; B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1897; LL.D., 1902; fellow of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1901; judge of water cts. from 1913; ag. judge, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1915, 1916, 1918, 1919; judge pres., O.F.S., 1920; judge of appl., 1933.

DEVINE, A. SIDNEY.—B. 1892; ed. Fran. Coll., Oxon.; enlisted Middlesex Regt., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Warwickshire Regt., Oct., 1915; lieut., 1917; served in France and Italy; wounded; served with disposal bd. in Italy, 1919-20; disposal and liquidation commr., 1920-23; cler. offr., C.O., 19th Mar., 1923.

DEVONSHIRE, 9TH DUKE OF (creat. 1694), VICTOR CHRISTIAN WILLIAM CAVENDISH, K.G., 1916; P.C.; G.C.M.G.; G.C.V.O.; LL.D.; BARON CAVENDISH, 1606; EARL OF DEVONSHIRE, 1618; MARQUESS OF HARTINGTON, 1694; EARL OF BURLINGTON, 1831; BARON CAVENDISH (U.K.), 1831.—B. 1868; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P. (L.U.) Derbyshire W., 1891-1908; treas. of H.M. Household, 1900-1903; financial sec. to treasy., 1903-1906; lord-lieut. of Derbyshire; president territorial forces, Derby; chancellor of Leeds Univ.; civil lord of the Admiralty, 1915; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1916-21; S. of S. for the Colonies, Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1924.

DE WAAL, HON. DANIEL, D.S.O., K.C.—B. 1873; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; B.A., LL. B. (Cantab. and Cape); admitted, advoc., Cape and Transvaal, 1897; practised, Pretoria; German S. W. Africa campaign, 1915; K.C., 1919; ag. judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Oct., 1919; judge, sup. ct., T.P.D., Aug., 1920; judge pres., T.P.D., 1927.

DE WAAL, HON. JAN HENDRIK HOFMEYR, M.P.—B. 1871; ed. Normal and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1897; advoc., Cape bar, 1897; mem., Union H. of A. for Pietermaritzburg since 1915; whip to Nationalist Party, 1915-24; chmn. of comtees. and dep. speaker, 1924-29; speaker, 1929-33; el. pres., Afrikaanse Taal Vereeniging, 1910; formerly chmn., Rega Kommissie and chmn., head comtee, Nationalist Party, Cape; author of "Afrikaner Grammar," and of various works of fiction in Afrikaans; edir. "De Goede Hoop," 1903-14.

DE WAAL, COL. PIETTER.—B. 1899; ed. Zeerust Pub. Schl., and Transvaal Univ. Coll. (now Univ. of Pretoria); lieut., perfom. forces, Union Defence Forces, 1922; S.A. Perfor. Garrison Arty., 1923; attd. Coast Arty. Schl., Shoeburyness, Aug.-Dec., 1923; attd. R.A., S. Area, England, 1924-25; capt., S.A.P.G.A., 1925; seconded to S.A. Staff Corps as staff offr., gunnery, Cape Peninsula, 1925; ag. A.D.C. to gov.-genl., 1925-28; grad. Staff Coll., Camberley, 1930; A.D.C. to C.G.S., U.D.F., Impl. Confoce, London, 1930; attd. W.O., 1930-31; O.C., S.A.P.G.A. and C.G.A., Cape Peninsula, and promoted major, 1931; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Capetown, with temp. rank of lieut. col., 1932; maj. (temp. lieut.-col.), S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; lieut. col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; O.C., Sp. Serv. Batt., 1933-34; O.C., Roberts' Heights commd. and comdt., S.A. Mil. Coll., 1933; dir., mily. operatns. and training, defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1934, and temp. col., 1934.
DEWAR, H. J.—B. 1878; clk., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 1902; clk., Br. refugee aid, 1903; clk., treasy., O.F.S., 1904; clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1910; acctnt., 1916; ch. accnt., 1928.

DEWAR, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. Otago Univ. Coll.; cadet, lands and survey dept., New Zealand, 1901; survr., F.M.S., 1906; ast. supt., surveys, F.M.S., 1911; supt. of surveys, Kedah, July, 1920; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1924; ast. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1927; dep., survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931; survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1933.
DE WET, JOHANNES HENDRIK, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Univ. of Pretoria; educ. dept., 1911-18 Union pub. serv. from 1918; ag. under sec., dept. of interior, 1931; under sec. do., 1932.
DE WET, THE HON. N. J., K.C.—Min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1913-24; puisne judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1932.

DICKENS, K. J., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Christ's Hosp.; schol. and exhib.; open classical scholarship, Keble Coll., Oxford, 1925; final honour schol., English language and lit., 1929; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1929.

DICKENS, WILLIAM SAMUEL, M.B.E. (1919).—B.1891; Indian pol., 1906-16; Indian army R., of Ofra, 1916-21; Iraq pol. force, 1921-30; inspr.-gen., pol., comdt., local forces and supt., fire brig., Barbados, 1932.

DICKINSON, ARTHUR HAROLD.—B. 1892; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; pol. probr., S.S., Nov., 1912; asst. supt., pol., Jly., 1916; adjt., S.S. pol., May, 1920; supt., pol., Jan., 1928; King's Pol. Med., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., crim. intell., S.S. and in addn. ag. dir., pol. intell. bureau, Dec., 1928; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., Jan., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Penang, Nov., 1933.

DICKINSON, BENJAMIN.—B. 1880; ed. Giggleswick and Caitus Coll., Camb.; barrister, at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1904; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1909-16; pol. mag., Accra, 1916-17; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1918; puisne judge, 1926; senr. puisne judge, 1927; ag. ch. just. six times; puisne judge, Kenya, 1930.

DICKINSON, JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—Ed. Liverpool Coll.; solr., 1920; 2nd lieut., The King's Own (R. Lancaster Regt.), 1914; lieut., July, 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; France, 1915-18 (M.C. and Bar); asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921.

DICKSON, WILLIAM MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. St. Mary's Schl. and Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1897; priv. sec. to several solr.-gens. and mins. of just. of Canada, 1914-25; asst. chf., remission br., dept. of just., 1925-30; priv. sec., Hon. G. D. Robertson, and later, Hon. W. A. Gordon, mins. of lab., 1930-33; dep. min. of lab., 1934.

DIEMONT, J. F. W.—B. 1884; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; asst., secretarial branch, poste and tels., O.R.C., 1903; clk., prov. sec., office, O.F.S., 1910; admive., inspr., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1922-29; prin. elk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1929; ch. de. 1935.

DILLON, ALBERT BARROW, I.S.O. (1932).—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; clk. to inspr. of schl., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to audr. and in treasy. and cust., 1893; clk. to col. engnr., 1895; ag.-insp. of schl., 1895-8; 2nd clk., treasy. and cust., 1897; insp. of schl., June, 1898; ag. sup. bot. station, Sept. to Dec., 1904; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., June, 1905 to Jan., 1906; lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, Apr., 1918; ag. audr., May to Aug., 1918 and from Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1920.

DIMES, CAPT. DOUGLAS HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Kingsbridge Gram. Schl. and Schl. of Agr., S. Devon; R. Vety. Coll., London; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1913; war serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

DINNELE, PERCIVAL ALFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.S.M., certif. T.M.H., L.S.T.M.—B. 1881; dist. surg., N. Borneo, Dec., 1911; dep. health offr., July, 1915; civil res. surg., No. 1 London genl. base hosp., Jan., 1917 to May, 1918; P.M.O. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1918; deleg. to 5th cong., Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Sept., 1923; deleg. to L. of N. Health Confce, S'pore, Feb., 1925; deleg. to meeting of advisory coun., eastern bureau, L. of N. health organisation, S'pore, Jan., 1926.

DIVE, MAJOR HUBERT ROY, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), graduate Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., certif. Royal Lond. Ophthalmic Hosp. (Moorfields).—B. 1887; great war, Gallipoli; Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-19; 1st med. offr./c war pensioners., St. Barth, Hosp., Lond., 1919-20; student and house surgn. L.S.T.M., and Albert Dock Hosp. Feb.-Aug., 1920; med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1927; assisting in office of prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1930; ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1933.

DIX, CHARLES CABBY, C.M.G., D.S.O., R.N. (ret.)—Ed. R.N. Schl., Eltham and H.M.S. "Britannia"; R.N., 1898-1919; harbmr., Barbados, 1926; harbmr., Kingston, Jamaica, 1927; dep. mast. attndt., S.S., Sept., 1931; pol. mag., S'pore, Oct., 1931; ag. mast. attndt., S.S., Nov., 1933-Aug., 1934.
DIXEY, FRANK, O.B.E. (1929), D.Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; govt. geologist, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918 to Dec. 1921; dir., geological surveys, Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.

DIXON, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1909; 1st cl. Lit.Hum., 1911; appd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls., cik., C.O., 11th Oct., 1911; priv. sec. to Sir G. Fiddes, 7th July, 1917; 1st cls., clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; aest. sec., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929.

DIXON, CAPT. HAROLD PARSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll. and Merchant Taylors Schl.; mily. serv., 1914-20; served in Salonika, Caucasus and Anatolia; mentd. in despatches; capt., 5th Durham L.I., T.A. (Res.), 1921; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1927.
DIXON, OWEN, K.C., M.A., LL.B. (Victoria).—B. 1886; ed. Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1910; ag. just., sup. ct., Victoria, July—Dec., 1926; a just. of high ct., C'wealth of Australia, 1929.
DIXON, CAPT. OSWALD, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, Dec., 1912; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1920; asst. ch. vety. offr., 1932.

DOBBS, CECIL MOORE, C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 25th Oct., 1906; dist. comsnr., 1910; senr. comsnr. (2nd cls.), Jan., 1925; ditto, 1st cls., Sept., 1927; ag. ch. native comsnr., Nov. 1928.
DOBSON, KENNETH BLAIR AUSTIN.—B. 1907; ed. Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.
DODDS, E. W.—B. 1881; clk., treasy., Natal, 1899; clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasy., 1920; acctnt., treasy., 1928; acct., dept. of posts and tels., Dec., 1931.

DOMMEN, MICHEL-EDOUARD, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1910; ed. Oakfield Schl., Rugby, Collège Classique, Morres, Switzerland, Derby Schl., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; post grad. course, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Aug., 1931.

DONALDSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUNTER, O.B.E., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.). M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif. (with distinc.) L.S.T.M., D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1881; ed. St. Thomas Hosp., London; joined R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; B.E.F. in France as surg. specialist, 15th Casualty Clearing Station, 1915-19; ag. major, R.A.M.C., mentd. in desps.; served in Somaliland in operations against the Dervishes in 1920; med. offr., Somaliland, Nov., 1920; med. offr. in ch., June, 1933.

DONOVAN, THOMAS—B. 1885; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., Cork; Mediterranean Expedy. Force, Apr., 1915-Jan., 1916; B.E.F., Apr., 1916-June, 1919; Q.M.S., Somaliland pol., Oct., 1919 to Mar., 1923; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Apr., 1923.

DONOVAN, WILLIAM, M.Sc. (N.Z.), F.I.C.—B. 1879; ed. Thames (N.Z.) High Schol. and Schl. of Mines, Otago Univ. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; joined N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; asst. dom. analyst, 1919; dom. analyst, 1930.

DOOLEY, EDGAR (CAPT. R. OF O.), A.M.I.Mech.E., M.I.A.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; A.S.C. (mech. transpt.), 1914; engrn. offr., Tank Corps, 1917; R. of O., 1921; asst. mech. engrn., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; transfd., motor transpt. dept., Aug., 1922; engrn. transpt. offr., Nov., 1922.

DOORLY, A. N., B.A. (Oxon), Barrister at Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1887; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; res. mag., Kenya, 1919; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1923; ag. judge, high ct., in 1923, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929; ag. ch. just. in 1926, 1928 and 1929; atty-gen., 1929; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933.
DOORLY, REV. CHARLES STOKELEY, B.A. Selwyn Coll. Camb.—B. 1882; 4th asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, July, 1911; 3rd asst. mast., July, 1914; 2nd asst. mast., July, 1920.

DOORLY, SIR CHARLES W., KT. BACH. (1935), C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1875; 3rd cls. clk., imigrn. dept., Jamaica, 1892; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; Hindustani interpreter, 1897; inspr. of imigrts., 1903; protector of imigrts., 1909; govt. emigrn. agt., Madras, 1913; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1922; admstr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1928; ag. gov., Windward Is. in 1930, 1931 and 1933; ret., 1935.

DORAN, CHARLES FREDERICK GARFIELD, B.A., B.L.—Ed. Royal Belfast Academical Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1916-19; served in France, Nov., 1916 till wounded in 1917; called to Irish Bar (King's Inn) 1913; crown counsel, Kenya, Dec., 1925; ag. solr.-gen. and M.I.C., Mar.-June, 1930 and Dec., 1931-July, 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Sept., 1933-Jan., 1934, res. mag., 1934.

DORION, HON. CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Quebec Seminary; Laval Univ. (L.L.B., 1884); admitted to bar of Prov. of Que., 1884; practised law in Queb. City until 1911; LL.D., Laval Univ., 1894; prof. of laws, Laval Univ., 1898; and since prof. of civ. code; judge, superior et., Prov. of Queb., 1911; judge, ct. of king's bench, 1921.
D'ORNELLAS, GUSTAVR.—B. 1885; 6th cls. clk., reg. office, Br. Guiana, 1905; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1906; 5th ditto, 1910; 4th ditto, 1915; 3rd ditto, 1923; 2nd ditto, 1925; asst. survr., cust., 1927.

DOUGHTY, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1905), M.A., Litt.D.—B. 1860; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., Quebec, Canada, 1896; librarian, Quebec, 1900; Dominion archivist, Canada, 1904; mem. of comsn. on War Records and Trophies, Dec., 1918; joint del. to internat. congress of the history of America at Rio-de-Janeiro, Sept., 1922.

DOUGHTY, LESLIE ROY, B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A.—B. 1902; ed. Derby Munio. Secondary Schl., Midland Agrl. Coll. and Schl. of Agr., Cambridge; geneticist, East African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
DOUGLAS, CHARLES HOMER, M.M.—B. 1884; ed. Wellingborough Grammar Schl.; war serv., R.E., 1915-19; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; land survr., 1933.

DOUGLAS, CUTHBERT SCHOLTO.—B. 1887; temp. survr., irrign. dept., Union of S.A., May, 1917; temp. asst. engrn., Dec., 1919; 2nd gr. prof. asst., Dec., 1927; prof. asst., Nov., 1929; survr.-gen., Bloemfontein, May, 1934.

DOUGLAS-JONES, SIR CRAWFORD DOUGLAS, KT. BACH. (1934), C.M.G. (1920).—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Aug., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volrs., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instr.'s certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. comsnr., Rhodesia, Apr., 1911; ag. res. comsnr., S. and N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1918; res. comsnt., July, 1918 to Sept., 1923; res. comsnr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1923; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; admstd. govt., Sept., 1924 to April, 1925 and from Apr. to July, 1926; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1926; O.A.G. on various occasions, 1927-28.

DOVEY, JOHN EDWARD, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M.&H. (Edin.).—Med. offr., Hong Kong; do., Kowloon, 1927-28; med. offr. in ch., subord. staff and families, med. offr. in ch. venereal disease clinic and asst., Victoria Hosp., 1929; M.O. in ch., Victoria Hosp., May-Sept., 1932 and 1934; M.O. in ch., civil and ment. hosp., 1933.

DOWBIGGIN, SIR HERBERT LAYARD KT. BACH., C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1880; inspr. of pol., Ceylon, Jan., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Apr., 1901; supt. of pol., Feb., 1905; inspr.-gen. of pol., Nov., 1913; on sp. duty, Cyprus, Nov., 1926; resumed duties as inspr.-gen., pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1926.

DOWLAND, CHARLES EDWIN JAMES.—B. 1880; ed. Macandrew Road Schl., normal schl. and Otago Boys' High Schl., Dunedin, N.Z.; ent. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1897; dep. comsnr., taxes, 1928; comsnr., taxes, 1932.

DOWNIE, H. F., O.B.E. (1934), B.A. (1912), M.A. (1914).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hospital and Oxford; Lodge exhibnr. (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class mods., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Dec., 1912; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug. to 19th Oct., 1914, and from 10th June, 1915, to 7th Apr., 1918; ag. 1st cls. clk., 3rd Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited Sudan, Uganda, Kenya, Tanganyika, Nyasaland, N. and S. Rhodesia and S. Africa as sec., East Africa coman. on closer union, Dec., 1927-May, 1928; asst. sec., 21st Oct., 1935.

DOWSE, WILLIAM ROBERT, A.I.E.E.—B. 1891; Br. P.O. engrn. dept., G. Britain, Jan., 1912; Dub. Univ. ex-graduates, O.T.C., Aug., 1914; comsnd., R.E., Jan., 1915; Gallipoli and France, May, 1915; asst. teleg. engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1919; demob. reg. army R. of O., June, 1919; engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S. Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., ditto, May, 1928; senr. engrn., P. and T. Selangor and Pahang, Mar., 1932; asst. engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels., Malaya, Dec., 1932; ag. dir., tels. and telephone and engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1934.
DOWSETT, FRANCIS DUDLEY.—B. 1908; ed. Bristol Grammar Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1931; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.

DOYLE, THOMAS FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed. St. Patrick's Acad., Dungannon; offr. in Imp. cust. and excise serv.; Aug., 1912; inspr., cust., Palestine, July, 1926; ag. dep. dir., cust., excise and trade, periods in 1929 and 1930; dep. collr., cust. and excise comnr., Mauritius, Feb., 1932; ag. collr., cust. and harbrmr., June, 1932.

DRAKE, HAROLD WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. City of London Schl.; entd. Col. Audit Dept., July, 1904; seconded to W.A.F.F., Oct., 1914 (Nigeria and Cameroons); paymr., Overseas Contingent, W.A.F.F. (G.E.A.); Col. Audit Dept., Aug., 1918; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Mar., 1919; senr. asst. audr., Dec., 1921; ag. audr., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; audr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1927; asst. dir., col. audit dept., Dec., 1931; audr., Nigeria, 1933.

DRAKE, THOMAS NORMAN.—B. 1896; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast; pilot, R.A.F., Great War, 1917-19; cadet, R.I.C. and dist. inspr., Ulster spl. constab., 1922; Air Force Offrs. Reserve, 1923; 3rd cls. inspr., Jamaica constab., 1924.

DRAYTON, BERNARD MORGAN.—B. 1886; service in post office, pub. wks. and treasy. depts., Grenada, 1902-14; treasy. asst., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; asst. treasy., civ. adminstr., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), 1917; asst. treasy., Nigeria, 1920; senr. asst. treasy., Nigeria, 1928.

DRAYTON, HON. SIR HENRY L., KT. BACH. (1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comtce., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario govt. comnrs., on Toronto Power Comn., 1911; ch. comnrs., bd. of railway comnrs., Canada, 1912; resig. to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Govt., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-eI. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig. portfolio on defeat of adminstr., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comnrs., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, ROBERT HARRY, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps., 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; miny. of reconstr. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; treasy. solr.'s office, 1919-20; asst. legal sec., govt. of Palestine (continuing as asst. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr.-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1925; mem., finan. comnrs., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. govt. in parly. comn. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftsman, Jan., 1932; called to bar, 1934; comnrs. for revision of laws of Palestine, Sept., 1934; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1934.
DRAYTON, VIVIAN FORTESCUE.—B. 1883; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, 1900; ch. clk., gov's. office, Aug., 1918; commr., Carriacou, Jan., 1932.

DRENNAN, JOHN MATTHEW, D.C.M. (1918).—B. 1891; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 3rd cls., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, Salonica, Palestine); asst. head, appts. dept., 1919; stores dept., 1922; shipping dept., 1930; dep. head, gen. dept., Jan., 1935; shipping dept., May 1933.
DREW, DESMOND, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C.D.).—Med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921.

DREW, RINGROSE CHARLES WILLINGTON, B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1912; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1921; ag. state engrnr., Kelantan, Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrnr., Apr., 1930; state engrnr., Kelantan, June, 1931; senr. exec. engrnr., Muar, Nov., 1934.

DROWER, E. M., C.B.E. (1923).—B. 1880; admitted solr., 1904; practised, consular ct., Cairo, 1904-6; Sudan bar, 1906-14; served European War, 1914-18; pres. of first instance, Hillah, 1918; Mosul, 1918-19; Baarah, 1919; asst. judicial adviser, Iraq, 1921; tempy. judicial adviser, 1921; govt. counsellor, 1921; adviser to min. of just., 1922.

DRUMMOND, DAVID B.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1925; asst. dist. comnrs., Sierra Leone, July, 1923; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; ag. dist. comnrs., on five occasions, 1924-30; pvt. sec. to gov. and ag. gov. on five occasions, 1924-29; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Aug.-Sept., 1930; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. on several occasions; ed. "Sierra Leone Studies."

DRUMMOND, CAPT. THE HON. EDMUND RUPERT, M.V.O. (1922), R.N.—B. 1884; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia"; lieut., 1906; commdr., 1918; capt., 1926; ch. of-staff to commodr. in ch. Portsmouth, 1930-32; commanded H.M.S. "Delhi," 1932 and H.M.S. "Despatch," 1933-35; commodore commodg. N.Z. station and naval advr. to N.Z. govt., 1935.

DRYBURGH, ALEXANDER MITCHELL, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1895; ed. George Heriot Schl., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S.; 1919; govt. rice agt., Tapah, Mar., 1920; 1st asst. dist. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1920; addl. Fedl. sec., Nov., 1920; supersym. offr., cls. V., 1922; cls. IV., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1926; dist. offr., Raub, 1928; sec. res., Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III., 1931; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, June, 1933; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1934; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1934 to Nov., 1935; gov't. dep., Sept., 1935.

DU BOULAY, GEORGE CORNIBERT, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1883; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. audit clk., St. Lucia, Apr., 1899; 3rd clk., govt. office, Aug., 1899; 4th clk., treasy. and customs, Mar., 1903; ag. 2nd clk., treasy., from Mar. to Nov., 1903; chief clk., post office, Jan., 1905; ag. harbr. master, St. Lucia, Sept., 1909; priv. sec. and clk. to gov. of Seychelles, clk. to councils and supt. of printing, Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, May, 1916; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar. to May, 1919; asst. sec., col. sec.'s off., Gibraltar, Dec., 1919; senr. asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. and gov't. dep. on various occasions, 1924-25; ch. asst. col. sec., Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; ag. col. sec., on various occasions; gov't. dep. on various occasions; ag. gov., Apr. to Sept., 1930 and Dec., 1931.

DU CANE, GEN. SIR JOHN, G.C.B. (1928), K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1910).—B. 1865; lieut.-col., R.A., 1884; capt., 1893; major, 1900; lieut.-col., 1902; col., 1908; major-gen., 1915; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen., 1926; D.A.A.G., Staff Coll., 1905-07; G.S.O., 1st grade, hdqrs. of Army, 1908-10; C.R.A., 3rd divn., 1911-12; S.O. to I.G., Home Forces, 1913-14; served S. Africa, 1899-02 (desps., brevet lieut.-col., Queen's med., 5 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European war, brig.-gen., III. Corps, 1914; maj.-gen., R.A., G.H.Q., 1915;

DUKE, EDGAR MORTIMER, LL.B.—B.1895; ed., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; Br. Guiana open schol. in maths., 1911; Andrews' entrance schol. in science, 1913 and Joseph Hume schol. in jurisprudence, 1914, Univ. Coll., London; Campbell Foster Prizeman of Middle Temple; sp. prizeman in crim. law and procedure, 1915; LL.B. (Lond.), 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; admitted to practise Br. Guiana, 1917 and in Trinidad and Tobago, 1921; ag. registr., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Br. Guiana, in 1929, 1930 and 1933; dep. registr., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Jan., 1930; stip. mag. and mem., dist. licensing bds., Demerara and Essequibo, Sept., 1931; registr., deeds, 1933; author, "Law of Immoveable Property in British Guiana."
DUKE, ORKIL ST. A., M.B.E. (1932), M.M.—B. 1896; ent. govt. serv., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1913; serv. European War, 1915-19; inspr., Leeward Is. pol., Sept., 1927; ch. inspr., pol., July, 1935.
DUMANOIRE, JOSEPH WENCESLAS.—Asst., trigonometrical survey, Trinidad, 1902; 2nd asst., survey office, 1903; 1st asst., ditto, 1913; dep. survr.-gen., 1930; ditto and sub. intendant, 1933.

DUMBRELL, HENRY JAMES EDWARD, B.A.—B.1885; asst. mast., Heaton Lodge Schl., nr. Huddersfield; mast., St. Bedes Coll., Eastcourt, Natal; headm., Ompumulo Native Training Coll.; inspr., schls., Natal, 1921; inspr., educn., Bechuanaand Prot. and Swaziland, 1928.

DUNCAN, HAROLD HANDANDY.—B.1885; ed., Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; hons. schl. mod. hist.; M.A.; qualified in compet. exam. for appt. as junr. clk., 1st cls., F.O. (placed 4th in 1908 and 5th in 1909); conf. sec. to London comte., Imp. Ottoman Bank for work relating to Turkish govt. finance and indus. enterprise in Near East from Dec., 1909; on sp. mission to Constantinople, 1912; served in army, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; France and Italy; liaison offr. (intelligenceA.) between Br. G.H.Q., Italy and Br. Mily. Mission, Comando Supremo from Aug., 1918; capt., Norfolk Yeomanry; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1919; S. Eastern circuit; temp. legal asst., Miny. of Health, 25th Apr., 1927; est., 15th Mar., 1928; 2nd asst. legal adviser. D.O. and C.O., 31st Mar., 1930; asst. legal adviser, do., 1st Apr., 1931; ag. legal adviser, Dec., 1931 to Mar., 1932 and Feb., 1933 to July, 1933.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904), K.C., LL.D. (Edinburgh).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; latcls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prin. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906; barrister, Middle Temple and advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; M.L.A., for Yeoville; min. of interior, pub. health and educn., 1921-24; min. of mines, Apr., 1933.

DUNCAN, WILLIAM MACFARLANE.—B. 1896; ed. Craigie Pub. Schl. and Acad.; certifs. in building constr., mechanics, mathematics, etc.; Caledonian rly., Perth, 1911; asst. engrn., Taungavika rly., Feb., 1921.

DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th clk., P.O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1904; clk., record office, 1909; consnr., 4th div., 1912; consnr., grade III., June, 1920; clk., grade III., regisr.'s office, July, 1927; ag. asst. registr.gen., 1928 and from June, 1929.
DUNCOMBE, WALTER KEWALL.—Bahamas treasy, and cust., 1895-1900; Out Island

DURUTY, CHARLES ERIC.—B. 1892; war serv. with 28th London Regt., (Artists Rifles) and The Royal Warwickshire Regt.; awarded M.C.; served audit dept. and pol., Trinidad, 1911-21; asst. comsnr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; comsnr., pol., 1927.

DUSSEK, OMONDE THEODORE, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Univ. of London; B.A., London, 1909; mem. of convocation; senr. ast. mast., Alleyne's Grammar Schl., Uttoxeter, 1909-11; senr. ast. mast., High Schl., Malacca, 1912-14; prin., Malay Training Coll., Malacca, 1914-19; prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., F.M.S., 1920; in addn., ast. dir. of educn. (Malay), and examr. in Malay, Mar., 1924.

DU TOIT, PETRUS JOHANN, Ph.D. (Zurich), Dr. Met. Vet. (Berlin), B.A. (Cape), F.R.S. (S. Africa).—Senr. vety. offr., vety. research divn., Vryburg, Dec., 1919; sub-dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1920; dep. dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1921; dir., vety. services, Pretoria, Apr., 1927; dean of faculty and prof., trop. diseases and protozoology, Univ. of S. Africa, 1927; visited Nigeria at request of C.O. to rept. on stock problems, 1927; del. to Imp. Agril. Research Conf., London, 1927; ohmn., 6th Pan-African Vety. Conf., Pretoria, 1929; dir., vety. services and animal industry, 1929; del., internat. vety. confg., New York, 1934.
DU TOIT, STEPHANUS FRANCOIS, LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. Victoria Coll. Stellenbosch and Univ. of C.T.; translator, Union senate, 1920; gent. usher of black rod, 1926; elk. ast., 1930.

DUTTON, ERIC ALDHelm TORLOGH, O.B.E. (1930), M.A.—Ed., Hurstpierpoint, and Keble Coll., Oxford; major, W. Yorks Regt.; served in Gallipoli (ment. in desp.); temp. appt., Basutoland adminstr., 1918-19; priv. sec. to gov. of Uganda, 1920-22; priv. sec. to gov., Kenya, 1922-30; sec., high comsnr., Zanzibar, 1922-25; priv. sec. to high comsnr., transport, Kenya and Uganda, 1925; prin. ast., ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. ch. sec. in 1931, 1932 and 1934; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1932-35; ohmn., finance comsnr., 1932.

DUTTON, ERNEST GODFREY.—B. 1886; elk., Impl. mil. rly., Apr., 1902; elk., high comsnr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1903 to May, 1916; Swaziland service, Apr., 1907; elk. to ast. comsnr., Maseru, Basutoland, June, 1916; elk. clerk, master of ct. and registr., July, 1919; ag. govt. sec., June to Dec., 1923 and Feb. to Oct., 1924; ast. comsnr., 1924.
DUTTON, STEPHEN BERNARD.—B. 1881; served in stores dept., C.S.A.R., 1902-3; served in Zululand rebellion, 1906; comptroller of stores, Basutoland, Oct., 1909.

DYER, W. H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), cert., Lond., S.H. and T.M. (Distinc.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., 1914-25; surg. specialist, 1918-20; N. Persian forces memorial med., for research, 1924 and 1927; seconded, Nyasaland Aug., 1921-Apr., 1925; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

DYER, ALEXANDER MEIK, M.A.—B. 1910; ed. Dumfries Acad. and St. Andrews Univ.; 1st cl. honos. classics; Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, prov. adminstr. Tanganyika Territory, June, 1934; admntr. offr. (ast. sec.), Cyprus, Apr., 1935; ast. comsnr., July, 1935.

DYER-MELVILLE, SYDNEY WHITMORE, M.I.E.E., S.A.—B. 1885; ed. City of London schl.; asst. dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. dir., Apr. to Nov., 1913, Oct., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Dec., 1919 to Feb., 1925; immigr. offr., Sept. to Dec., 1917; ag. director, Jan. to Dec., 1919; ag. dir., elec. and wireless tel. depts. for various periods, 1925-32.

DYKE, HAMILTON WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1935). M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1905.—B. 1881; ed. South African Coll., Cape Town and Glasgow Univ.; house phys., Glasgow Royal Infirmary, house surg., Glasgow Western Infirmary, Glasgow Ophthalmic Inst. and Glasgow Royal Hosp. for sick children; med. offr., Maseru, Basutoland, 1913; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; served in France, 1915-19; S.M.O., S.A. Native Lab. Cont., Rouen area, 1919; surg. spec., govt. hosp., Jerusalem and govt. of Palestine, 1927; prin. med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1929; do., Basutoland, 1935; author of "An Outbreak of Scurvy among South African Natives in Rouen," Lancet, 1917; "A Case of Madura Foot in Basutoland," S. African Med. Jnl.
DYKE, KENNETH H., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1892; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

DYSON, EDWARD TREVOR.—B. 1886; ed. Ruthin Gram. Schl., U.C.W., Aberystwyth (B.A.), and Jesus Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1912; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Aug., 1913; addtnl. asst. col. sec., Mar., 1918; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1918; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1920; asst. govt. agt. N. Eliya, Feb., 1921; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1926; ag. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Mar., 1928; do., Jaffna, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. I. grade II, 1932; ag. offr., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1934; chmn., munic. coun., Kandy in addn.

EAGLESOME, SIR JOHN EGAN, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—M. Inst. C.E.; served in P.W.D., Rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of Indian govt., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., 1900, as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Baro-Kano rly. from 1907; dir. of rlys. and works, Nigeria, 1912; ret. 1919.

RALEIGH, SHIRLEY, C.M.G. (1935); C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1883; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; 2nd cls. clk., govt.'s office, Transvaal and O.R.C., July, 1902; 1st cls. clk., July, 1904; transf'd to Basutoland service and att. to office of high commr. for S. Africa, July, 1908; prin. clk., Dec., 1911, and asst. Impl. acctnt., Apr., 1915; chief clk., Apr., 1919; mem. of commn. to inquire into establishments, etc., in Bechuanaland Prot., Basutoland and Swaziland, 1921-22; ag. asst. Imp. sec., May-Nov., 1921 and Mar.-Sept., 1922; asst. Imp. sec., 1923; ag. Imp. sec., in 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1931-2; title changed to admvte. sec. to high commr., 1933; mem. for Bech. Prot. rly. commn., 1935.

EARLE, HUGH J. M.—B. 1896; ed. Cheltenham; on war service, 1914-19; capt., 4th Batt. Essex Regt., 1918; ent. dept. of lands and mines, R. Guiana, 2nd Aug., 1921; 6th cls. clk., secretariat, 1st Mar., 1922; sec. for sp. duty in connexion with Georgetown town disturbances, 1924; ag. A.D.C. to O.A.G., May-Nov., 1925; cadet, admvte. serv., Nigeria, 1927.

EARLE, SIR LIONEL, K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1911); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., Univs. of Gottingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., Oxford; asst. sec. to royal commn. on Paris exhibn., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, 1900; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; priv. sec. to lord pres. of the coun. (Earl of Crewe), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), Nov., 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works, 1912; ret.

EASTER, B. H. M., M.B.E., B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Coll., Finchley; served R.N. Dn., 1917; Scot's Guards (lieut.), 1918; Ampleforth Coll., O.T.C., 1919-23; headmnr., St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1923; dir., educn. and headmnr., Grenada, 1928; ag. col. sec. and adminr., Grenada, Dec., 1930-Aug., 1931; local adviser, educn. comtee., 1931; dir., educn., Jamaica, Apr., 1932.

EASTWOOD, CHRISTOPHER GILBERT.—B. 1905; scholar of Eton, 1917 and of Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1923; apptd. to C.O. as ast. prn. after compet. exam., 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded for serv. as pvte. sec. to high commr., Palestine, 11th Feb., 1932; prin., 18th Mar., 1933.

EATON, BERTIE JAMES, F.I.C., F.C.S., F.I.R.I., O.B.E. (Mily. Div., 1919).—B.1883; ed. Dorchester Gram. Schl., Finsbury Tech. Coll. (City and Guilds of London Inst.) and Birkbeck Inst.; ast. research chem., Impl. Inst., 1904-06; govt. chem., F.M.S., 1906-10; govt. agrl. chem., F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; del. to Internat. Rubber Congress and Exhibn., Batavia, 1914; recvd. thanks of the S. of S. and the Govt., F.M.S., for re-searches on rubber, 1918; 2nd lieut., gen. list, adjutant, Civ. Guard, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1916; lieut., M.V.I.; O.C., M.V.I., Selangor, 1917; major, M.V.I., Jan., 1923; del. of F.M.S. and S.S. at 2nd Pan-Pacific Service Congress, Australia, July to Oct., 1923; Br. Empire Exhibn., Apr., 1924; O.C., Selangor Volunteer Corps., Jan.-Dec., 1926; mem. co-opern. bd., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; seconded for serv., Rubber Research Inst., Nov., 1926; del., rubber confere. and 4th Pacific serv. cong., Java, May, 1929; mem., agrl. advy. comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1930; dir., Rubber Research Inst., Malaya, Nov., 1930; author of "The Preparation and Vulcanisation of Plantation Rubber," "The Cultivation and Preparation of Camphor in F.M.S.," contribs. on rubber re-searches to the Society of Chemical Industry, numerous articles on tropical agrl. industries, etc., to the Agrl. Bulletin, F.M.S. and other journals.

EBDEN, WILLIAM SYDENHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; class. tripos, 1909; cadet, S.S., 1911; 2nd lieut., Jan., 1915; lieut., Salonika Front; wounded and p. of w., Bulgaria; dist. offr., F.M.S., 1920; dep. contr., rubber expts., mag., Johore, and recr., Alsagoff concession, in addn., July, 1924; registr., sup. ct., Johore and 1st mag., Johore Bharu, Dec., 1925; asst. adviser, Segamat, Jan., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; asst. treas., Penang, Dec., 1928; dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. I.B., Apr., 1931; commr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1932; cls. I.A., 1935.

ECCLES, LAUNCELOT WILLIAM GREGORY, M.C.—B.1890; ed. Michaelhouse Schl., Natal and Worcester Coll., Oxford; ast. survr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; ast. examr., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from Dec., 1922; dep. dir. surveys and examr., diagrams and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir. surveys in 1925, 1928 and 1931; commr., lands, mines and local govt. and M.L.C., 1933.

ECCLES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; clk., educn. office, Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1907; elk. to comsrs. of currency, June, 1908; customs, Jan., 1911; temp. sub-inspr.ofpol., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1915; on mily. serv., lieutenant, afterwards capt., 1915-19; returned to cuat., Trinidad, July, 1919; on sp. mil. duty to B. Guiana and Trinidad, Aug. to Oct.; asst. recvr.-gen. Gambia, Oct., 1919; relinquished comen. with rank of capt., Sept., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Mr. G. Gwyn, Sept., 1921 and to Mr. C. R. M. Workman, Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. elk., exec. coun., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; offr.-in-charge, posts and tels. dept., Feb. to July, 1924; offr.-in-charge, cust. on various occasions between 1923 and 1928; asst. compt., cust., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. compt., cust. on various occasions, 1929-32; compt., cust., 1932.
ECKBO, NILS BONNIVIE (M.F. Yale).—Offr. in ch., timber seasoning investigations, Pretoria, 1919; apptd. to perm. estab. as timber investigations offr., 1924; ch., Forest Products Inst., 1932.

ECKEL, CAPT. PERCY.—B. 1893; audit dept., Trinidad, Jan., 1912; seconded, W. O., 1915-20; wounded, France, 1917; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., 1920; asst. comsrs., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; comsrs., pol., 1927.

EDGINTON, EDWIN.—B. 1886; sorting clk. and telegraphist, Imp. serv., 1908; resigned, Jan., 1912; re-apptd., Dec., 1912; dist. surv., posts and telegs., G. Coast, Oct., 1913; senr. dist. surv., Feb., 1917; served as offr. in ch., posts and telegs., Br. Zone, Togoland, Nov., 1916 to Aug., 1917; postmas., Cyprus, Feb., 1920; P.M.G., Trinidad, 1922; divul. surv., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, 1927; contr., ditto, 1930.

EDMONDS, B. D.—B. 1890; on mily. serv., 7th Aug., 1914 to 5th Dec., 1919; awarded Mily. Med.; temp. elk., C.O., 2nd Feb., 1920; apptd. after compet.exam., cler.offr., C.O., 5th Nov., 1921; clerical offr., higher grade, 3rd Feb., 1928.

EDMONDS, EDWARD REGINALD.—B. 1901; matric., London Univ., June, 1921; entered C.O., 1917; apptd. after exam., asst. elk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; elk., P.W.D., Uganda, 1926; higher grade offr., C.O., 31st July, 1929.

EDMONDS, HERBERT GLYN.—B.1900; ed. Swansea and Univ. Coll., London; med. offr., Falkland Is., June, 1931; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, July, 1931; ag. prin. med. offr., Mar. to Oct., 1932; M.S.A. (Lond.), 1932; ag. senr. med. offr., Dec., 1934 to Feb., 1935.
EDMUNDSON, K., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.).—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1928.

EDWARD, DOUGLAS SANDERMAN, A. M.I. Struct. E., A. I. A.A., M. Inst. R. A., Chart. Struc. Engrn.—B. 1897; ed. Grove Acad., Broughton Ferry, Tech. Coll., Dundee (Armitstead med.), and St. Andrews Univ.; mily. serv., R.E.'s, 1914-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

EDWARDS, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; dist. comsrs., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps) substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. police, 1907-12; commt.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Apr., 1923.

EDWARDS, CAPT. DAVID, M.A. (1912), LL.B. (Aberdeen), 1914.—Temp. offr., R. Arty., 1915-1920; served at home, N.W. Frontier of India, German E. Africa and Mesopotamia; Scottish sol., 1921; asst. dist. comsrs., Kenya, 1921; ag. regir., sup. ct., Kenya, 1921; regir., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; called to Scottish bar, 1924; res. mag., Kenya, 1928; relieving pres., dist. ct., Palestine, 1935.
EDWARDS, ERNEST JAMES.—B. 1886; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; head mast., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1928; senr. mast., educn. dept., 1934.

EDWARDS, WILFRED HENRY, D.I.C., F.E.S.—B. 1897; lect., zoology and entomology, Coll. of Agr., Mauritius, 1924; ag. entomologist, botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Mauritius, 1928; govt. entomologist, Jamaica, May, 1929.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM STUART, C.M.G. (1935), K.C.—B. 1880; called to the bar, Ontario, Sept., 1909; legal offr., dept. of justice, Canada, Nov., 1910; sec. of dept. of justice, 1913; asst. dep. min. of justice, 1914; dep. min. of justice, 1924.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed., Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); a J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; offl. assignee and regir. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comsrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comsrs., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, July, 1904; gov. and comdr-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prote. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov., B. Guiana, Mar., 1912; ret., Mar., 1917.

EGG, RICHARD TYLER.—B. 1886; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1913; admitted to practise before sup. ct., Br. Guiana, 1913; ag. mag., Oct., 1919; stip. mag., 2nd grade, 1921; ditto, 1st grade, 1924; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1925-35; pol. mag., Gold Coast, May, 1929.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1925).—B.1881; ed., Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; F.R.C.V.S., 1914; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland admtn., May 1903; prim. vet. offr., 1919; agrl. offr., 1928.

ELIAS, DAVID HENRY, M.C.—B. 1882; G.W.R., Eng., May, 1899-May, 1902; S. African Rlys., May, 1902-Sept., 1914; S. African Mounted Rifles, German S.W. Africa, S. African Infy., Brig., France, M.C., Sept., 1914-July, 1919; S. African Rlys., July, 1919; supt. line, Kenya and Uganda Rly., Sept., 1922; ditto, Nigerian Rly., Sept., 1925; dep. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1931; gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., May, 1933.

ELKINS, MAJOR WALTER HENRY, O.B.E. — B. 1883; ed. Cirencester Grammar Sch.; S. African and Rhodesia Rlys.; Mesopotamia Mill. Rlys.; reorganisation rlys. Transvaal; dep. dir., rly. transport, S. Russia, 1919; dep. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., 1928; ag. offic man., Dec. 1928 to Aug. 1929; divnl. pt., F.M.S. rlys., Sept., 1932; ag. transpnt. in., Apr., 1934.
ELLENBERGER, VIVIEN FREDERIC.—Clk. to s. commr., Bech. Prot. 1915; passed 2nd grade cwana exam., 1919; sub-insp., Bech. Prot. I., 1920; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928; res. mag., May, 1934.

ELLIOT, JOHN AUGUSTUS GILBERT.—B. 1881; 1st. reserve of officers; ed. privately and at din. Univ.; enlisted in C.I.V. (M.I.) for S. African war Jan., 1900; 2nd lieut. R. Scots Fus., ug., 1900; dangerously wounded at battle of rederikstad, Oct., 1900; 1st. lieut., May, 1908; served with 8th and 4th regts. of M.I., S. Africa, 1903-4; intell. dept., S. Africa, 1904-1906; resigned 2nd R. Scots Fus., Salisbury Plain, June, 1906; M.I. certif.; musk. certif., Hythe, 1907; ub. health certif., Edin. Univ., 1908; asst. ist. commr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Apl., 1909; ist. commr., Oct., 1917.

ELLIOTT, COLIN FRASER (Fraser), K.C., B.A., L.C., C.E.—Solr., income tax br., dept. of finance, Canada, July, 1919; asst. commr., income tax, dept. of national rev., May, 1926; commr., income tax, July, 1932.

ELLIOTT, FRANK FIELD.—Tempy. asst. registry, survr., O.F.S., Mar., 1903; asst. registry, survr., May, 1903; chief draughtsman and compt., Apr., 1912; addl. prof. asst., Apr., 1914; prof. asst., Sept., 1917; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Nov., 1930; do., Cape Prov., Dec., 1931.

ELLIOTT, HON. JOHN CAMPBELL, B.C.L., D.C.L., K.C. (Middlesex West).—B. 1872; ed. Ekfrid pub. schl., Strathroy C.I., Glencoe High Schl. and Trinity Univ.; B.C.L., Osgoode Hall, 1898; D.C.L., Toronto Univ., 1905; barrister-at-law; elec. Ontario legis., g.e., 1908; re-elec., 1911 and 1914; first elec., H. of C., Canada, g.e., Oct., 1905; mem., Privy Coun. and apptd. min. of lab., 1926; re-elec. by-el., Mar., 1926; min. of health and soldiers' civ. re-estab., Apr., 1926; resigned, June, 1926; re-elec., g.e., Sept., 1926; min., pub. wks., King Cabinet, Sept., 1926; re-ele. by acol., Nov., 1926; re-elec., g.e., July, 1930; resigned portfolio after defeat of liberal admin. at polls, Aug., 1930.
ELLIOTT, PHILIP JOHN.—B. 1892; entd. crown agents' office, Dec., 1911; asst. head, dept., July, 1927; dep. head, stores dept., Nov., 1933.

ELLIS, CAPT. FREDERICK ROBBINS, M.C., Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1924.—B. 1891; ed. Blundell's Schl., Tiverton; 2nd lieut., 4th batt., Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy.; M.C.; served in Mesopotamia with 2nd Dorset Regt., 1915-16; prisoner of war on capitulation of Kut-el-Amara, 1916; escaped from custody, 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; Siberia, 1919; passed solrs. final exam., junr. adminis. offr., S. Leone, 1920; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Jan. to Apr., 1921; J.P., 1921; ag. ass't. col. sec., 1921; ag. commr., pol., 1921; ag. dist. commr., headqrs. dist., 1921-23; dist. commr., Aug., 1928.

ELLIS, JOHN MEDICOTT, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; ed., Haileybury and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schl.); asv.ed., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914-19; asst. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, June, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, March, 1920 and Turkish, July, 1921; ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1923-25; ag. col. sec., Falkland Is., Sept., 1926 to Aug., 1927; col. sec., Aug., 1927; ag. gov., in 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; local audr. from May, 1930 to Feb., 1931; registr.-gen. and mag. from July, 1930 to Jan., 1931; col. sec. and registr.-gen., Grenada, Oct., 1934; asst. ch. sec., Nyasaland Prot., 1935.
ELLISON, EDWARD WILFRID.—B. 1905; ed. Shrewsbury and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; asst. dist. commr., 1931.
ELLISON, F. O'BRIEN, M.D., B.S., B.A.O.—B. 1879; prof. of physiology and registr., Ceylon Med. Coll., Oct., 1923.
ELPHINSTONE, SIR H. G., BART.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921.

ELPHINSTONE, SIR LANCELOT HENRY, Kt., Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1904; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1919; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; pulse judge, designate S. Stiltna., 1924; ag. solr. gen., Ceylon, June, 1924; atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; ch. just., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ret., 1932.

EMBURY, BRIG.-GEN. HON. JOHN FLETCHER LEOPOLD, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; served European War, 28th Can. Inf., 13th Inf. Bde., 2nd Inf. Bde., Can. Contingent; Br. G.H.Q., France (Canadian sec.), 1914-19; ment. in despatches three times, C.B., C.M.G.; judge of King's Bench, Saskatchewan, 1918.
EMBURY, W. E.—B. 1898; on mil. serv., Oct., 1916 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Dec., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., 8th Nov., 1921.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1864; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and La Poile, 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909; registr., supreme ct., 1917.

EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 26th Jan., 1922; promoted ag. asst. prin. under clause 51, 4th Sept., 1929.

KMPSON, CHARLES.—B. 1898; ed. Harrow and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A. (S.K.); joined Iraq civil admin., Aug., 1930; addnl. sec. under sec. to high commr., Sept., 1921; under sec. (consular), July, 1922; H.M.'s consul, Baghdad, Dec., 1924; consular sec., July, 1927.

ENGLAND, WILLIAM WENTWORTH.—Entd. gov't. ser., Antigua, Feb., 1907; clk. to registr.-gen. of births and deaths; ag. treasy. offr., Montserrat, Jan. to Dec., 1908; ag. clk. to commr., Virgin Is., Mar. to Aug., 1909.
ENGLISH, JOSEPH SANDYS, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda Hosp., Dub.), F.C.O.G.—B. 1890; prof. of midwifery and gynaecology, King Edward VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Jan., 1922.

ENGLISH, PATRICK THOMAS.—B. 1902; 5th clk., treasy., Montserrat, 1921; clk. to mag. and regtr., 1924; ag. offr. in ch., prison, 1924; 3rd clk., treasy., 1925; ag. regtr., sup. ct., 1925; jnr. clk. col., sec.'s office, Antigua, 1927; ag. clk. of couns., 1928 and 1930; ag. clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1930; ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., and clk., fed. couns., 1930; clk. and cust. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1931; ag. acct., 1931-33; ag. sec. to govt., June-Aug., 1933.
ENGLISH, ROBERT.—B. 1888; asst. printer, Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. asst. do., 1925; gov't. printer, Sierra Leone, 1930; do., Nigeria, 1932.

ELPSTEIN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1886; ed. Hebrew Inst., Liverpool; Jews Hosp., W. Norwood; inspr., dept. of commerce and industry, Palestine, 1920; dist. offr., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1923; admstv. offr., S. dist., 1925.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; attd., Ratnapura kach., May, 1912; Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayts, Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Avisawella, June, 1915; asst. comrntr., excise, Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Mar., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1918; pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1924; dep. comrntr., stamps, Feb., 1928; comrntr., do., July, 1931; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, May, 1934; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1934.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VIVIAN ARMIT., M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1875; ed. St. Paul's Sch., Lond. and Crystal Palace Schol. of Engnr.; served, P.W.D., Jamaica; asst. engnr., Lagos govt. rly., 1900-01; dist. engnr., Gold Coast Govt. rly., 1901; exec. engnr., P.W.D. E. Africa Prot., June, 1905; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1908 and 1909; D.P.W. Uganda Prot., 1911; Uganda Volunteers, 1914-19; hon. lt.-col., 1916; miny of munitions while on leave, 1917; D.P.W., Jamaica, Aug., 1921; mem., leg. coun., D.P.W., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; mem., fed. coun., chm. electrical bd. and others; mem., rly. bd., immig. comm. and others; ret. July 1928; re-employed as eng. in ch., colonial development fund wks., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

ESPITALIER-NOEL, JOSEPH GEORGES.—B. 1894; ed. R. Coll. Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1916; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1919; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Sept., 1921; addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1922; ag. subat. procureur and advoc. gen. on various occasions, 1924-29; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., Mar., 1929; ag. mast. and regisr., supt. ct., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1932 and Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct. in 1932 and 1933.

EULER, HON. WILLIAM D. (Waterloo N.)—B. 1875; ed. at pub. schls., Berlin (Kitchener), High Schl., Toronto Normal Schl.; mayor of Berlin (Kitchener), 1913-14; pres., Kitchener bd. of trade, 1917; first elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1917; re-ele., g.e., 1921, 1925 and 1926; mem. privy coun. and apptd. min. of national rev. in King cabinet 1926; re-ele. by accl. after assuming office; re-ele., g.e., 1930; resigned portfolio with Mr. King's cabinet, 1930; re-ele. at g.e., 1935; min. of trade and commerce in King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

EVANS, ARTHUR ANTHONY, O.B.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., Lieut., Col., R. Corps of Signals (Res.)—Ed. Brodribb Acad., Carmarthen and Univ. of Wales, Cardiff; served in S. African War (Queen's med., 4 clasps); engnr. branch, G. P. O. 1898-1914; R. E., signals, Gallipoli, Egypt; asst. dir., Army signals and ch. signal offr., Palestine and Syria, 1914-20; four ment. in desps., Order of White Eagle, 5th cls. with swords, O.B.E., M.C., Br. and Allies war meds., "1914-15" Star; ch. engnr., Palestine posts and tels., 1920.

EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES, F.R.A.S.—B. 1887; computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1903; junior asst., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1909; lst asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1912; on military service, 1917 to 1919; ag. ch. asst. for various periods in 1915, 1916, 1920, 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1929; asst. dir., 1932; ag. dir. for various periods in 1934 and 1935.

EVANS, EDWARD WALTER, B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1890; ed. Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class mods., 1st class lit. hum.; asst. dist. comrntr., Zanzibar Prot., Feb., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., June, 1917; temp. comrn. in K.A.R.; ag. 1st asst. sec.; Zanzibar, 1919-20; asst. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1921; senr. asst. sec., 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., July, 1924; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1925; ag. col. sec., Jan. to Mar., 1927; col. sec., Br. Honduras, June, 1927; admstd. govt., on two occasions; col. sec., Bermuda, 1929; col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1932; admstd. govt., Aug., 1932 to Apr., 1933.

EVANS, FRANK DUDLEY, M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1883; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S. 1907; malaria adv. bd. 1911; exec. engr., grade III, 1913; ag. regtl. srgt. maj., M.S.V.R., 1917; 2nd lieut., do., 1917; engr., malaria adv. bd., 1919; ment. in desps., 1919; lient., M.S.V.R., 1919; anti-malaria engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1921; awarded Ccl. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., 1921; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1924; ag. state engrn., Negri Sembilan, 1924; ag. capt., M.S.V.R., 1924; visited anti-malaria wks., Palestine and Italy, 1926; D.D.P.W., Nigeria, 1926; ag. D.P.W., and M.L.C., Nigeria on various occasions 1926-35; malarial comrn., health sect., L. of N., Geneva, 1928; mem. comte. road v. rail transp., 1933; chmn., do., 1935; D.P.W. and M.L.C., 1935.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903), C.B.E. (1918); Comdr. of Order of Isabel la Católica (Spanish), 1912; Knight Comdr., ditto, 1914.—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., admty., Somerset house, Dec., 1867; transf'd. to sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1879 to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr., cust. and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883 and on other occasions; clk. legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov', Lagos, July to Aug., 1883 and May, 1885 to Jan., 1886; dep. gov', G. Coast, Sept., 1883 and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admistr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1893; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ag. gov' on seven occasions; ret. 1914; ag. col. sec., Gibraltar, in 1915 and 1917.
EVANS, H. G.—Asst. dist. comrntr., E.A.P., Apr., 1913; 1st grade admstv. offr., 1925.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., KT. BACH. (1926), C.M.G. (1906)—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, July, 1904 to Oct., 1905; premier and treas., Oct., 1905, to May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker.
