EVANS, LEONARD WINNE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.Sc., certif. L.S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1891; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1922; med. and health offr., P.W., north and central, June, 1923; ch. med. offr., Kelantan, Jan., 1929.
EVANS, NOEL, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1886; asst. mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1921.

EVANS, SAMUEL, Hon. LL.D., Witwatersrand Univ.—B. 1859; pvt. sec. to finan. adviser, Egyptian govt., 1883; ch. contr., Egyptian coast guard serv., 1886; inspr.-gen., Imp. Ottoman Bank, Constantinople, 1889; man., Turkish Tobacco Regie, 1892; during Anglo-Boer War served on staff of Lord Roberts from Kroonstad to Johannesburg, and on staff of mil. gov. of Johannesburg as civ. comsnr.; medal, 3 bars; pub. debt comsnr., Union of S. Africa.

EVANS, SAMUEL ALBAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and R. Vety Coll., Dublin; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; asst. vety. pathologist, May, 1926.

EVELYN, EDWARD ARTHUR, (jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl.; supernumy. rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1922; ch. clk., post office, 1927; dep. harbmr. and cashier, treasy., 1928; gvt. offr., Eastern dist. and clk. to mag., dist. F, Dominica, 1930; ag. appt. in treasy. and post office, Tortola, Virgin Isl. 1932; ag. mag. dist. F, also addnl. mag., dist. E, 1933; J.P., 1934; ch. clk. to admr. and clk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1935.

EWART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Botelor Schl., Warrington and Rossall Schl., Williams Mem. Exhn.; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; pres., O.U.S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (chem. pt. 1); F.C.S., 1923; 2nd chem., pt. 2, 1924; mem., Inner Temple, 1925; asst. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1926; M.A., 1927.

EYBERS, GEORG VON WELPFING.—B. 1887; ed. Boy's High Schl., Beaufort West, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Univ. of Amsterdam and King's Coll., London; B.A. (Cape); M.A., D. Litt. (Lond.); prin., Graaf Reinet Coll. and Hoet Volkskool, 1919-26; mem., Cape provin. educn. comsnr., 1926-27; inspr., schl., 1927; under sec. educn., Aug., 1927; prof. asst., Union educn. dept., July, 1934; author, various works on S. African hist. and travel.

EYRE, JOHN COLES, B. Sc. (Agric.), D.I.C.—B. 1903; ed. Diocesan Coll., S. Africa; B. Sc. (Agr.), plant pathology, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1928; D.I.C., plant pathology, Royal Coll. Sci., 1930; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., 1931; tech. offr., Empire Marketing Bd., 1929; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1932.

EYRE-SMITH, CAIT. ST. JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Edmunds Coll., Ware; served with R.A.S.C., France and Germany, 1915-19; Turkey, 1919-20, ment. in desps.; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1925; asst. sec., native affrs., 1931.

EZECHIEL, SIR PERCY HUBERT, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1875; B.A., Bombay, 1889 (ed.); B.A., London, 1894; major schlr., Tripl. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; sec. to crown agts., for the colonies, May, 1905; mem.of Fair Wages Advisory Comtee., 1909-1920; sec. to Colonial Economic Development Comtee., 1919-20; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., 1919 (resigned secretariatry 1921); crown agt. for the cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, 1921; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire Exhib., 1921-25; chmn., Palestine currency bd., 1926; mem., Hong Kong currency comsnr., 1931.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; arrived S. Africa, 1898; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924; re-apptd., Apr., 1930; chmn., Mar., 1932.
FAIR, HON. ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1885; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown solr., 1921; prin. law offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.Z., 1934.

FAIR, Lieut.-Col. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1885; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff off. to Gen. Gatacre during Atbara campaign, 1898 (desps., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls., Medjidish); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (desps., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. comsnr. and comdt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908-1911; dep. asst. dir. of remounts since 1911.

FAIRBAIRN, SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1926).—B.1855; ed. Geelong Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1924-27.
FAIRBAIRN, H., M.D., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1898; mily. serv. 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; sleeping sickness offr., 1935.

FAIRBURN, HAROLD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1884; pol. prob., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; asst. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., June, 1908; registr., crimin., F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1913, Oct., 1913 and Jly., 1915; asst. comsnr., pol., ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Aug., 1917; dep. comsnt., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; in charge, intell. branch, F.M.S., Apr., 1921 and Feb., 1923; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., Jly., 1925.

FAIRCLOUGH, F.R.—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv. and assigned to C.O., Aug., 1914; on military service from 13th Dec., 1915 to 9th March, 1919; priv. sec. to Sir James Stevenson, 12th Aug., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; staff offr., 15th July, 1933.

FARLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., M.A.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.), B.A., (mech. science tripos) 1913; 6th Bn. E. Surrey Regt., 1914; 1st K.A.R., 1917-19; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engnr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. asst. D.P.W., in 1923 and 1924; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Aug., 1925; ch. engnr., N. Rhodesia, 1935.

FAIRWEATHER, EUGENE ERNEST, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Unvrs. and Dalhousie Law Schl., N.S.; asst. solr., dept. of rlys. and canals, 1912; solr., 1914; counsel, 1919; asst. gen. counsel, Can. Nat. rlys., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, N.D.A.U.D.A.—B. 1896; ed. Montrose Acad., Aberdeen Univ., N. of Scot. Coll. of Agric.; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1921; ag. mun. sec., Malacca in addn., May, 1927-Feb., 1928; agrl. offr., Johore, Dec., 1931; offcgt. prin. agrl. offr., Apr., 1933.

FAIRWEATHER, WILLIAM GEMMEL, O.B.E. (1932)—B. 1886; ed. at Univ. of St. Andrews (B.Sc.), A.M.I.C.E.; asst. survr., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; ch. asst. survr. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ch. survr. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. survr., 1920; dist. surveys, Oct., 1924; mem., leg. coun., 1929-32, 1933.

FALCONER, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen (1st cls. hons.); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; asst. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. regisr., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second asst. advr., Muar, Feb., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1930; do., Raub, July, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. advr., B. Pahat, June, 1934.

FALK, EDWARD MORRIS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl., on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. comsnr., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and retd. to England, 1915; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ag. admstr. of col., 1931.

FANNIN, C. G., M.C., B.A. OXON., Assoc. Inst. C. E.—B. 1890; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, Rhodes schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford in 1908-11; hons. engng. sci., 1911; G. E. C. London, 1911-14; 1/1st Lond. Heavy Bty., R.G.A., 1915-16, adjt., 7th H. A. Group, 1917; (ment. in desps., M.C.); asst. inspr., guns, Woolwich, 1918-19; Kenya survey dept., 1920; dist. survr., asst. land offr., author of paper "Townplanning at Mombasa."

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entl. C.O., Dec., 1914; asst. clk., Apr., 1917; on mily. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925; staff offr., 20th Apr., 1934.
FARQUHAR, EDWARD ALLAN.—Comsnr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NEIL, M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Putalam Kach., June, 1921; office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec., land comsnr., Aug., 1928; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1936; clk. to leg. coun., Mar., 1930; clk. to state coun., July, 1931; contr., establishments, July, 1932; col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1934.

FARQUHARSON, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A. (Oxon), M.Sc., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Schl., Otago Univ., N.Z. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; sen. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engng., 1905; 1st cls. hons., geology and 2nd cls. physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hons. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; govt. petrologist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rept. on geological and mineral resources of Prov., 1923; govt. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; recd. thanks of S. of S. for geol. map of Somaliland; dist. agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926; geol. advr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bdry. comsnr., 1932-33; geologist in ch., water boring scheme under Col. Development Act, 1932-34.
FARR, FRANK JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928.

FARRELL, CAPT. WILFRID JEROME, M.C., M.A., R. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Schl. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schl. and Haileybury Coll., 1911-19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educn. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, miny. of educn., Iraq, 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., educn., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir. on various occasions, 1927-35; Br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929.

FAULKNER, ODIN TOM, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. tripods, 1911); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and agrl. expert to Rubber Growers' Assocs., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr., Nigeria, 1921.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1918)—Asst. auditor E.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. asst. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.

FAWCETT, CAPT. ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., B.A.T., (Dublin), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr.—B. 1890; war. serv., 1916-19, R.E., France, Persia, Afghanistan, ment. in desp.; asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Apr., 1920; dist. engnr., Apr., 1925.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Deputy regisr., high ct., E.A.P., 1910 regisr., high court, Feb., 1919; mast. and regisr., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, July, 1921; ch. regisr. and sheriff, Gold Coast, May, 1924.
FAZAN, S. H., C.B.E. (1934), O.B.E. (1930). Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920; senr. dist. comsnr., 1934.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhibir.), B.A. (lit., hum.), 1902; clk., law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; asst. regisr., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; 1st asst. regisr. and taxing offr., 1923; regisr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.

FEASEY, GILBERT GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. Westminster; served with H.A.C. and Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F.; active serv., Cameroons; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., July, 1934.

FEETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut. 1st battn. Cape Corps., 1916-19; mem., Southborough comte., on Indian Reforms and ohmn. functions comte., 1918-19; mem., India Office comte., on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHIYY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.L., F.A.C.S., D.P.H., Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; health offr. of port and inspir. immigrts., 1931.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; sups., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Mar., 1922.
FELTHAM, STANLEY CRATHERN, A.R.I.B.A., chd. architect.—B. 1896; ed. Northern Polytechnic, London; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

PENDALL, FRANCIS ALAN, L.S., N.Z. King's Coll. Auck. Univ.—B. 1891; lic. survr., N. Z., Mar., 1916; survr., F.M.S., Feb. 1920; do., Kedah, May, 1920; ast. supt., surveys, Sept., 1923; do., F.M.S., Mar., 1928; snr. ast. supt., surveys, S'pore, Dec., 1933.

FEEN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Diocesan and St. Andrew's Colls., S. Africa; clk., staff of gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-28; served, S. A. Arty. and lieut., R.A.F., 1915-19; sub-inspr., Swaziland pol., 1928; inspr., 1931; ch., pol., 1933.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD.—M.H.R. C'wealth of Australia since 1910; mem., standing comtee., of pub. wks., 1914-17 and of joint comtee., pub. accts., 1917-25; opposition whip, 1922-23; min. for trade and cust., 1929; Australian del. to Disarmament Comtee., London, 1930; P.M.G., Lyons' Ministry, 1932; resigned, Oct., 1932.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1891; ed. Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.).; war service, Mesopotamia 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Mendi; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1915; dist. commr., Apr., 1925.
FERGUSON, SIR DAVID GILBERT, KT. BACH. (1934)—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, BART., G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1915); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1898), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, 1883; adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaigns, 1896-98; severely wounded, Rosaries (med. with eight clasps, ment. five times in desps.); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1899 (2nd cls. Medjidie); garrison and dist., Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, 1904-7; brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; inspr., infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; in commd., 5th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps.; mily. gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in desps.); gov. and commdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, Oct., 1924; assumed govt., Dec., 1924; ret., 1930; chmn., W. Indian Closer Union Comm., 1932.

FERNANDO, SIR HILARIAN MARCUS, KT. BACH. (1923), M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.), Fellow of Univ. Coll. (London); J.P.—B. 1864; ed. at the Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. Scholar and Gilchrist Scholar for the East, 1882-83; 2nd medical entrance scholar, Univ. Coll., 1884; Atchison scholar, 1888, 1st. cls. hons. and gold medal, physiology, medicine, and forensic medicine (Univ. of London); ast. col. surg. and regiar., Ceylon med. coll., 1890; 1st physician, general hosp., and lecturer in medicine, Ceylon med. coll., 1898; reported on outbreak of malaria in Galle, 1894, and plague in Bombay, 1897, (rec. thanks of govt.); ret. from govt. serv., 1909; served on Colombo lake development, railway traffic, education, opium, prevention of tuberculosis, and several other commissions and comtees. of enquiry (1909-1918); nominated mem. of legis. coun. of Ceylon as the first low-country Sinhalese representative, Jan., 1917; nom. unoff. mem. leg. coun., 1921-1924; ditto, exec. coun. from 1921.

FERNANDO, PETER OLIVER.—B. 1898; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; astd. to Kurunagalla kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Feb., 1922; ditto, Jaffna kach., Mar., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; astd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1925; office ast., ditto, Mar., 1926; pol. mag., Panadura, Dec., 1926; office ast., Mannar kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1929; ast. attmtn. offr., Jan., 1930 and May, 1934; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1933; astd. office of min. for agr. and lands, Apr., 1934; ast. attmtn. offr. in addn., May, 1934.

FERNANDO, SELLAPERUMAGE SHELTON CLARENCE, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1931; astd., ch. sec.'s office, Dec., 1931; astd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1932; office ast., Matale kach., Mar., 1933.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, KT. BACH. (1930).—Ed. Bedford Schl.; called to Irish bar, 1903; crown coun., Ireland, 1912-21; mem., coun. of bar of Ireland and of standing comtee.; K.C., 1919; coun. to mily. during Sinn Fein rebellion, 1916; coun. to atty.-gen., 1919; coun. to crown, 1920; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; judge, high ct., Punjab, 1922-31; pres., special cts., Swaziland and Bechuanaland Prot., judl. commr., Basutoland, and legal advr. to high commr. for S. Africa, July, 1934.

FIDDES, SIR GEORGE VANDERLEUR, G.C.M.G. (1917), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. moda., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888 to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and accntnt. to Sir A. Milner, high commr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prn. clk.; accounting offr., 1907; ast. under-sec. of state, 20th June, 1909; chrmn., West African currency board, 1912-16; perm. under-sec. of state for the colonies; 10th Mar., 1916; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; ret., 1921.

FIELD, EDGAR JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; ent. survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; clk., office of commr. of crown lands, 1892; ch. clk. and acct., 1909; ch. immigr. offr., 1911; sec. for lands, chmn. of advances to settlers bd. and recr. of rents, 2nd Apr., 1917; chmn., irrig. comm., S. Aust., 1923; dir., lands, 1931.

FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1891; ed. St. John's Schl., Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); on war serv., 1914-18; demob. (capt.) 1919; inspr. and schoolmast., S.P., Nigeria, 1922; supt., educn., 1926; prin., govt. coll., Ibadan, 1932.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMOND, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, Apr., 1915; ag. dist. commr. on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commr., Aug., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925; prov. commr., 1934.

FIENNES, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR EUSTACE EDWARD TWISLETON-WYKEHAM, BART.—B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxford Imp. Yeo.; served Riel rebellion, 1885 (medal with clasp); Egypt, 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star); pioneer expdn. to Mashonaland, 1890; South Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in desps.); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Dardanelles and Home); M.P. (L.), Banbury div. of Oxfordshire, 1906-1910, and 1910-1918; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Seychelles, 6th July, 1918; assumed govt., 8th Oct., 1918; gov., Leeward Is., 29th Oct., 1921; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1921; ret., 1929.
FINCH, A.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1922; supt., pol., 1929.

FINCH, FRANK GEORGE.—B. 1882; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1907; asst. state engrnr., Kedah, 1910-15; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., 1913; state engrnr., Pahang, 1923; state engrnr., Selangor, Mar., 1927; dep. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., July, 1928; dir., advr., drainage and irrign., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl., Aberdeen Univ. and North of Scotland Coll. of Agr.; M.A., B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A., N.D.D.; asst. agr., Nigeria, 1912; senr. supt., 1922; asst. dir., 1927; dep. dir., 1930; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1931; M.L.C., 1932.

FINDLAY, GEORGE HUGO, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; asst. sec., S. Provs., 1922; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1928; ag. admstr. of colony, Lagos, 1930; ag. prin. ass't. sec., 1930; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1931; staff grade, 1932; ag. comsnr., col. and lands, 1934; ag. ch. sec., 1934.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; home cust. and excise serv., 1912-24; war serv. in R.N., 1918; collr. cust., Sierra Leone, 1924; dep. dir., dept. of cust., excise and trade, Palestine, 1928.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1882; ed. Pictou Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914.

FIRTH, OWEN WATTS.—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; asst. dist. comsnr., Nigeria, 1911; polit. offr., Udi-Okiwgi Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1927; ag. prin. ass't. sec., S. Provs., 1928; staff grade, 1932; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1934.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., L.L.B.—B. 1878; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; S.A. Coll., Cape Town, Trinity Hall, Camb.; judge, sup. ct., South Africa, 1929.

FISSET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; staff, adjt. med. corps, Canada, 1902; dir. gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min. militia and defence, vice-pres., the militia coun. since 1906; recd. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches; ret., 1924; mem. for county of Rimouski.

FISHER, BERTIE ST. JULIAN.—B. 1885; Fiji serv., 1909; dist. comsnr., 1918; legal dept., 1920; ag. dep. regt.-genl. and regt., sup. ct., 1921; ag. regt., sup. ct., curator of intestate and vacant estates, pub. trustee and off. recr. in bankpty., 1923-27; regt.-gen. and regt., titles, 1929; regt., sup. ct., pub. trustee, off. recr. in addn., Jan., 1931.

FISHER, JOHN CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Schl. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., July, 1902; asst. auditor, Lagos, Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; asst. aud., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1905; local audr., July, 1909; examnr., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1910; 1st div. clk. (2nd cls.), central office, May, 1910; aud., Malta, Moh., 1913; mem. of control board and hd. of food and commerce control office, Malta, 1917; senr. clk., cent. office, May, 1918; asst. dir., col. audit, Apr., 1928; deputy dir., Oct., 1929.
FISHER, HAROLD MAYSTON, L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1893; mil. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. dental surg., 1930.

FISHER, REV. ROBERT, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Cambridge; asst. mast., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921; house mast., 1922; mast., Achimota Coll., 1926; supt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1928; prin., govt. coll., Umuhia, 1928.

FISHER, STANLEY, KT. BACH (1922).—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; on sp. mission to Cairo under F.O., 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which read. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Laws," 1919.
FISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1926; prin. lab. inspr., Feb., 1928.

FITZGERALD, RICHARD DESMOND, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), certif. (hons.) L.S.T.M., M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Penang, July, 1912; do., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1912; health offr., Malacca, Sept., 1914; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Sept., 1914; served in European war, 1914-19 (ment. in desps., M.C.); med. offr., Tan Tock Seng hosp. and surg., native hosp., S'pore, Dec., 1919; M.O., gen. hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1920; med. offr. in ch., officials, pol. surg. and prison surg., Jan., 1921; prin. med. offr., Johore, June, 1923; offl. del., Far Eastern Aspn. Trop. Med., Calcutta, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. med. and health services, S.S., Feb., in 1932 and 1933; dir. med. and health services, S.S. and advr., med. and health services, M.S., Dec. 1933; dir., med. servs., S.S. and advr., med. servs., M.S., Mar., 1934.

FITZGERALD, THOMAS, O., C.M.G. (1933), O.B.E. (1927).—F.M.S. postal service; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, July, 1920; P.M.G., Jan., 1922; P.M.G., Kenya-Uganda-Tanganyika Territory postal and tel. service, 1933.

FITZGERALD, WILLIAM JAMES.—ed. Blackrock Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.C. and Croix de Guerre (French); admstve. serv., Nigeria, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1924; solr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; atty.-gen., July, 1933; ag. judge, 1934.

FITZGIBBON, HENRY ELLIOT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Stephen's Green Schl., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., B.A.I., Assoc. M. Inst. C.E., M. Inst. M. & Cy. E., M.T.P.I.; inspr., municipalities, Egypt, 1913; war serv. with E.E.F. (ment. in desps.); dir., irrign. and mech. dept., min. of wakfs., Egypt, 1919; Order of the Nile, 4th cls.; munici. and town planning engnr., Kenya, 1928; Pickering prize and gold medal, Inst. M. and Cy. E., 1931.

FITZ-GIBBON, WILLIAM GUERIN.—B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland; B. 1885; rly. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st clk., col. secretariat, Hong Kong, 1917; ag. ch. clk., col. secretariat, 1921 and 1924; J.P., 1924; gen. sec., P.W.D., 1927.

FITZ-HENRY, WILLIAM COOPER, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dulwich Coll.; asst. engr., Nigerian rly., 1908; lieut. R.E., 1915; capt. R.E., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and adm'y., 1918; in India on rly. work, 1920-27; road engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road engrn., N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., Aug., 1930; asst. engrn., P.W.D., 1933.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), 1913; late out-patient offr., asst. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland Prot., 1914.

FITZPATRICK, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES, P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1880; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl't., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906-1918; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908; lieut.-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.
FITZPATRICK, JAMES PATRICK, L.R.C.P. and S. (Ireland).—B.1885; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. med. supt., Central Mental Hosp., T. Rambutan, May-Dec., 1929.
FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.).—Asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; audr., Cameroon Expdy. Force, 1916; audr., Windward Is., June, 1919; audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1925; do., Uganda, 1935.

FLECK, FRANK.—B. 1875; clk., educ. dept., O.F.S., July, 1897; clk., orphan mast., O.F.S., Oct., 1899; clk. (first), educ. dept., O.F.S., Feb., 1901; clk., atty-gen.'s office, O.F.S., Sept., 1901; clk., master's office, high ct., Bloemfontein, 1902; asst. res. mag., Vredesfelt dist. (Parys), June, 1902; asst. res. mag., Fauresmith dist., Aug., 1903; asst. res. mag., Vierfontein, Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag., Bothaville, Feb., 1910; asst. res. mag., Reitz, Apr., 1912; detached asst. mag., Dewetdorp, Nov., 1913; detached asst. mag., Zastron, Feb., 1919; mag., Zastron, Oct., 1919; pub. serv. inspr., Pretoria, May, 1928; dir. prisons, Pretoria, Nov., 1929; mag., Pretoria, Sept., 1932.

FLECKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, EDWARD DONOVAN.—B. 1998; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salonika, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921, att'd, Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; offr. cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chine-e, Taiping, May, 1930 and supt., convict estabm't., in add'n., Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; prot., Chinese, July, 1934.

FLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv., and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd astt. acctnt., 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMING, COL. JOHN GIBSON, C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1916), late R.E.—B. 1880; ed. Haileybury Coll. and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; joined R.E.'s 1898; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's med. 5 clasps); European War, 1914-18 (deeps., D.S.O., bt. lt.-col., C.B.E.); ret. with rank of col., 1924; apptd. fourth Crown Agent and engrn.-in-ch., 1st June, 1932.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd. after compet. exam., S. and T. clk., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1916 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1930), KT. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhbr.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. astt. regis. gen., 1903; J.P., 1904; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1906-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; astt. to atty-gen., 1909; regis., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 111; ag. dep. regis., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treas., 1913; astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns., in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 20 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comte., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high commr. W. Pacific, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 1936.

FLETCHER COOKE, JOHN.—B. 1911; Barham schol., Malvern Coll., 1926; senr. exhbr., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1929; Kitchener schol.; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., C.O., 1934.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clk., 1908; ch. clk., 1915; astt. postmr., 1921; postmr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLEURKET, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1899; mesgr., C.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun. clk., Jan., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk. treas. and cust., Oct., 1919; clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1924; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 1928 to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, Jan., 1931; lieut., Mar., 1934; aide-de-camp to gov., Feb., 1935; ag. comdg. offr. and adjt., May, 1935; ag. col. postmr., July, 1935.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Roval Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; admtn. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; astt. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H. Commr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. clk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. ch. astt. sec., July, 1922; ag. commr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commdt. police, Sept., 1922; ag. ch. astt. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. societies, June, 1923; regis., co-op. societies, July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. credit societies, June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; ag. ch. astt. sec. for various periods, 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade commr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927; treas., Cyprus, 1935.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933)—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student); apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comtee., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comtee., 1924-26; mem. W. Afr. Currency Board, 1928.

FLOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (1921), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. Cranleigh Schl. and King's Coll., London; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn); ent. bd. of agr., 1894; prte. sec. to Sir Thomas Elliott (1904-5); Mr. Ailwyn Fellowes (Lord Ailwyn) (1905) and Lord Carrington (Marquess of Lincolnshire) (1906-7) as presds. of bd. of agr.; head, land branch, 1907-14; asst. sec., 1914-19; perm. sec., min. of agr. and fisheries, 1920; chinm., bd. of cust. and excise, 1927; perm. sec., min. of lab., 1930; high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s gov't. in the U.K., 1934.
FLYNN, J. B., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Drogheda Grammar Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, 1928.
FOGGIE, ALISTAIR, B.Sc. (Forestry)—B. 1908; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., 1932; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1929.

FOLEY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); asst. comdt., Br. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; dist. comm., Police Training Schl., 1926; dist. supt., pol., N. dist., 1927.

FOLLIT, HAROLD HARRY BAILEY.—B. 1883; ed. Bedford, Heidelberg and Cambridge; St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; European War, 1914-18; Egypt, Gallipoli and France; med offr., Nyasaland, 1921; senr. health offr., Tanganyika, 1928; senr. health offr., Nyasaland, 1931; ag. D.M. and S.S., May, 1933 to Feb., 1934 and Mar.-Nov., 1935.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1920; attd. C.O., May-Oct., 1925; asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. asst. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. on various occasions.
FORAN, WILLIAM—B. 1871; entered civ. serv., Canada, 1890; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; sec., bd. of civ. ser. examns., 1895; 1st cls. clk., 1906; sec. of olv. ser. comms., 1908.

FORBES, Rt. Hon. GEORGE WILLIAM, P.C. (1930).—B. 1869; ed. Boys' High Schl., Christchurch; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1908; senr. whip, Liberal party, 1912-22; leader of opposition, 1925; leader of National party, 1925-28; min., lands and agr., 1928-30; prime min., min. of finance, external affrs., cust., and stamp duties, 1930; prime min., min. of rlys. and min. of external affrs. in coalition miny., 1931; atty.-gen., 1933; native min., 1934; min. for Cook Is., 1934; rep. N.Z., Imp. Confce., 1930 and World Economic Confce., 1933; and at H.M.'s Silver Jubilee celebrations, 1935.

FORBES, HARRY.—B. 1873; cust. dept., Fiji, 1911 clk.; of peace, Rewa, Fiji, 1914-18; J.P., 1915; cashier, treas., 1918; ag. acct., Gilbert & Ellioe Col., June, 1921; ag. treas., ch. postmr. and collr. of cust., June-Dec., 1922; acct., Br. residency, New Hebrides, Aug., 1923.

FORBES, LESLIE.—B. 1889; B.A., Oxon. (lit. hum.), 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; lent to Kedah govt., 1914; sec. to adviser, Kedah, 1916; dist. offr., Dec., 1920-1922; offr. dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; collr., land rev., Seremban, Feb., 1931; dist. offr., Klang, June, 1933; ag. advr., land office, Kedah, Feb., 1935.
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug., Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; asst. ch. acct., Palestine rly., Aug., 1921; ch. acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1926.

FORDE, JOHN REID (late Capt., R.A.M.C.), M.B., B.Ch.—On mily. serv., 1915-18; apptd. to W. African med. staff (Gold Coast), 1918; senr. med. offr., 1932; transfd., Gambia, 1934; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; senr. med. offr., G. Coast, 1935.

FORRER, HENRY AUGUSTUS, M.A. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1886; cadet S. S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet, Aug., 1913; supern. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; attd. to censor's office, May 1915; services lent to the F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Dec., 1923; dep. regir., and ast. offr. assignee, Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1926; regir., sup. et., Penang, May, 1927; regir., sup. et., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; offr., cls. IB., Apr., 1930; dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, Jan., 1933.

FORREST, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1892; asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; on active service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; asst. audr. in charge, Somaliland, 1917; ag. senr. asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., in 1917, 1919 and 1920; asst. audr. in charge Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1922; senr. ast. treas., Nov., 1925; treas. acct., Jan., 1926; mem. and sec. Kenya cost of living comnr., 1926; title of subst. post changed to prin. asst. treas., 1928; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ag. treas. in 1930, 1932 and 1934; treas., Oct., 1934.

FORREST, ROBERT ANDREW DERMOD—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct. 1919; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Jan., 1922; attd., educn. dept., Jan., 1922; asst. head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; asst. treas. and dep. estate duty comnr., 1931 and 1935; head, sany. dept., 1934; senr. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1934; supr., impts. and expts., Jan., 1935; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Mar., 1935; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., May, 1935; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1935.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ag. comsr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island comsr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.
FOSBROOKE, HENRY ALBERT, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Pocklington Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.

FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LISLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradfield and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); min. of educn., Egypt, Jan., 1913; asst. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1915; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925; ag. ast. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; ag. prov. comsrr., Nov.-Dec., 1933; olk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan.-Sept., 1934; dep. prov. comsrr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1936.

FOSTER, RICHARD SHEPHERD—B. 1894; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service; temp. employed at Nat. Hi'th. Insurance Comsrr., Mar., 1913 to June, 1914; assigned to C.O., June, 1914; on military service from Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; col. audit dept., 1919; astt. audr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; do., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1923; do., Gold Coast, Nov., 1931; ag. dep. audr., various occasions, 1931-33; senr. astt. audr., Nigeria, 1934.

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCE, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., S.K.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-19; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924; Swahili examr.; dep. dir., educn., Uganda, 1930; ag. dir., educn., in 1931, 1933 and 1936.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1936), M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in G.W., 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmnr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmnr.-gen. on several occasions.

FOULGKR, HARRY CECIL, M.A. (1928)—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; capt., R. Fusiliers; astt. res., Nyassaland Prot., Aug.1914; attd., K.A.R., ag. pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. comsrr., Jan.-Sept., 1935.

FOUND, WILLIAM AMBROSE, D.Sc.—B. 1873; ed pub. schls. and Prince of Wales Coll., Prince Ed. Island; dept. marine and fisheries, Canada, 1898; supt., fisheries in combined dept., 1911; advr. to Br. plenipotentiaries when pelagic sealing treaty negotiated, 1911; mem., Canadian-American Fisheries Comsrr., 1918; dep. min., fisheries, 1928.

FOURIE, THE HON. A. P. J.—Formerly mem., Cape prov. coun. and subsequently mem., H. of A. for Somerset; M.H.A., Gordonia; admstr., Prov. of the Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1926; min. of mines and industries, Aug., 1929; del., Imp. Confce., London, 1930 and del., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932; min., lab., commerce and industries, Apr., 1933.

FOURIE, LOUIS, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edin. Univ.; Berlin; M.B., Ch.B. (Hons) (Conan Doyle Prizeman); D.P.H., F.Z.S., F.R.A.I., Fell. Soc. M.O.H., M.R. San Inst., mem., Amer. Anthropol. Assocn., res. med. offr., R. Infirmary, 1905; city fever hosp., 1906; roy. hosp. sick. child., Edin., 1906-7; h.-surg., Paddington Green Child. hosp., Lond., 1907; act. serv., Boer Forces, 1899-1900; D.S. Taunga, 1913-15; capt., S.A.M.C., 1916-19; M.O. to admtnr., S.W.A., 1920-29; astt. health offr., Union Health Dep., 1930.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; gov'r. off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-06; olk. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, HAROLD ARTHUR LESLIE, B.So. (Mass.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; Institute of Technology; served in Great War, 1915-19; ment. in desps.; demob. with rank of capt., 1919; ch. engr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1926; ag. dir., June-Dec., 1931.

FOXLEE, RICHARD WILLIAM, M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1885; ed. Westminster Schl.; served in engnr's dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; P.L.A., 1915-21; dep. head, engrg. design dept., C.A., 1921; dep. ch. engrg. (civ.), 1928.

FRAMPTON, ALGERNON DEKEWER, C.D.A.—B. 1904; ed. Christ's Hosp., Oxford Schl. of Economics and at Wye Agrl. Coll.; Impl. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. offr., dept. agr., Nigeria, 1927; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1929.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1909; ag. inspr., cust. preven. serv., Mar.-Oct., 1911; ag. inspr., maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; prov. collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, BERTRAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1928)—B. 1878; ed. St. Edmunds Schl., Canterbury, and St. Catherine's Schl., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripos), 1900; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1900, lieut., 1902; inspr. of immigrts., Mauritius, 1908; ag. auditor-gen., Jan. to June, 1908; prot. of immigrts., and poor law comsrr., 1912; temp. capt., R.G.A., Jan., 1915 to 1918; ag. recvr. gen., Feb. to Nov., 1919, Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1922 and from Nov., 1922 to Aug., 1923; chmn., profiteering comttee., May, 1920; collr. of cust. and harmbr., Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1927 to May, 1928 and Dec.-Aug., 1930; ag. astt. col. sec., Aug., 1929 to Dec., 1930.

FRANCIS, CLIFFORD CLAUDE, Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; service in Fiji, 1908-12; cadet W. Pacific serv. (Br. Solomon Is. Prot.), June, 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; dep. comsrr., high comsrr.'s ct., Dec., 1914; lieut., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Flanders, 1917-18; dist. offr., Gizo, Br. Solomon Is., Oct., 1919; ag. res. comsrr. of Prot., Oct., 1922 to Mar., 1923; rep. natives of Gizo dist. in claims before lands comsrr., 1923; judl. comsrr. for W. Pacific in New Hebrides and Br. judge, joint ct., New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; pol. mag., Nigeria, 1930; puisne judge, Uganda, and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, July, 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug.-Oct., 1934.

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE, K.C. (1934)—Apptd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., for various periods, 1908-10; 2nd grade dist. comsrr., May, 1912; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major, Aug., 1914; ment. by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; coy. comdrr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsrr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; registr. gen., regiar., sup. ct., comsrr., stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsrr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. ch. just. in 1921. 1922 and 1923; chmn., coman. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., retrenchment comttee., 1922; chmn., franchise coman., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1934.
FRANCIS, JOSIAH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.
FRANKS, JOHN WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1879; prison comm., H.O., June, 1899; asst. supt., Victoria Gaol, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; asst. supt. pol. res., Feb., 1919; supt., prisons, Dec., 1920.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.B.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin.; Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; deacon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to oh. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to oh. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. asst. advr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regis., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930; offig. mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1933; offr. cls. I.B., 1934.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on mil'y. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1924; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon July, 1931; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; cadet Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, &c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; agt. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAZER, HON. SIR FRANCIS VERNON, KT. BACH (1935), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; asst. pub. serv. comnr., 1920; judge, ct. of arbitn., 1921-35; chmn., dairy industry comnr., 1934; dep. chmn., exec. coms. of Agr., 1935.

FREEMAN, BENJAMIN JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp., London, and Horsham; br. ch., Oct., 1903-Dec., 1913; jur. postal clk. and telegraphist, Kenya, Jan., 1913; 2nd cls. postmr., Apr., 1915; asst. ch. acctnt., Apr., 1918; 1st cls. postmr., Apr., 1920; dep. ch. acctnt., June, 1924; asst. P.M.G., July, 1931; snr. acctnt., S.S., Aug., 1932; contrlr., P. and T., Selangor and Pahang, Agr., 1933.

FREEMAN, GEOFFREY JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. privately and in Clongoweswood Coll., Ireland; served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Royal Munster Fusiliers in the Great War, 1914-17; collr., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; senr. do., Feb., 1932.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls., hons. mod., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; appt. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries comnr., 1921; act. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; prin., 1st April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. Hond. on duty, 1927; seconded for serv. with Col. Development Advisory Comte., 1st Feb., 1930; sec., E. African gov't. contce. and to the high comnr. for transp., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1936.

FREETH, ARTHUR CAUSTON.—B. 1890; ed. Lancing Coll. (exhbr.), St. Mary's Hosp. and Coll. of Med., Newcastle-on-Tyne, M.B., Dunelm, certif., London Schl. of Trop. Med.; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-20; med. offr., Grenada, 1921; do., Trinidad, 1921; do., Uganda, 1924; ag. senr. health offr., Uganda, 1929; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1930; tuberculosis offr., 1933.

FRENCH, LEWIS, I.C.S. (Ret.).—Ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; served as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab, afterwards as dep. comsnr. and joint ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, Palestine, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres., dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; ch. just., Apl., 1914; judge, prize ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NOEL GRAY, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1885; ed. Wainganui Collegiate Schl., N.Z.; and Canleigh Schl.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, July, 1909; dist. comnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., N. Prov., in 1921, 1923, 1926 and 1928; prov. comsnr., Jan., 1929.

FRETZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian adminstr., B.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrols, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; chmn., agr. comsnr., 1924; ag. adminstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1927; asst. judge, Zanzibar, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., 1935.

FREYBERG, CAPT. GEOFFREY HERBERT, R.N. (Retd.), O.B.E. (Mil.)—B. 1881; served in R. Navy, 1895-1926; China med., 1900; ment. in despa. for services at Battle of Jutland when oomdr., H.M.S. "Valiant"; awarded O.B.E., (Mily.) for services afloat during the Great War; King's harbour., Plymouth, from 1919; elec. a younger brother, Trinity House Corpn., 1921; served at admry., 1922; master attendant, S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1926.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND.—Served in the Great War; just of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to comsnr., clk. to leg. bd., sec. to bd. of educn., 1920; sub-insptr., pol., 1922; asst. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., gen. man. of savings bank, regist. of shipping, 1923; ag. asst. comsnr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. comsnr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931; treas., St. Lucia, 1933; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1933.

FUAD, MUSTAFA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schls., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; clk., treasy., Cyprus, 1905; inspr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., sheri. ct. of appeal, 1927.
FUGLES, NORMAN RALPH.—B. 1907; ed. Sir Roger Manwood's Schl.; B.Sc., Wye (Lond. Univ.), dipl agr., Cantab and A.I.C.T.A., Trinidad; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

FULLER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apl., 1910; col. sec., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1910-20; premier, 1922-25; agt.-gen., N.S.W., 1926-31.

FULLERTON, HON. C. P., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. Dalhousie Univ., Dalhousie, Nova Scotia, Canada; admitted to bar, 1896; judge, ct. of appl., Manitoba, 1917-31; ch. comsnr., bd. of rly. comsners. for Canada, 1931; chmn., Canadian National Rly. trustees, 1933.
FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and Suez, France and Italy, 1915-18; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1926.

FURNNESS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD. Kt. Bach. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hone.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regir.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926.

FURNNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. comsnr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932; ag. atty.-gen., in 1933.

FURSE, MAJOR R. D., C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis now Lord Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O. D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apl., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O. gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in desps., asst. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec., (apptmts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1923; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited Canada on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923 as rep. at Imp. Forestry Confco; mem., bd. of govrs., Imp. Forestry Inst.; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

GALEA, ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1930)—B. 1872; ed. Malta Lyceum; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1891; 3rd cls. elk., 1st Jan., 1895; 2nd cls. elk., July, 1901; asst. registr. cts. of judicial pol., Dec., 1913; registr., Jan., 1914; transf'd, secretariat, May, 1918; ch. clk., lieut.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1920; ch. clk. to legislature, Dec., 1921; compt'r., charitable institutions, Apl., 1923; off. sec. to head of ministry, Mar., 1927; asst. sec. to govt., Apr., 1934.

GALEA, PROF. ROBERT V., L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with honrs.), Malta Univ., 1900; graduated as land survr. and architect, 1903; lect. on engng. and architecture, Malta Univ., 1905; prof., ditto, 1915; ag. rector of univ., 1923; el. for 1st divn. in leg. assem., June, 1924; re-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health and charitable institutions, Aug., 1927 and June, 1932, also min. for treasy., Aug., 1929; dep. leader constitutional party, 1932; leader, opposition in L.A., 1932-33; rector, Malta Univ., 1934.

GALIZIA, HON. MAJOR JAMS A., C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1876; elk., Malta civ. ser., 1893; ch. elk., P.W.D., 1905; man., water wks. and elec. light dept., 1913; supt., pub. wks. and man., water wks. and elec. light dept., with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1921; treas., 1930.

GALLOWAY, WILLIAM CHARLES GOMBLETON.—B. 1892; Lond. Institution of City and Guilds bronze medalist, telephony, A.I.E.E.; Br. P., engng. dept., Jan., 1912; on active serv., R.E. (Signals), Nov., 1915; asst. teleg. engr., P. and T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1920; engr., Oct., 1926; ag. sr. engr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May-July, 1928, and Feb-May, 1929; sr. engr., Dec., 1930; ag. asst. engr.-in-ch., Nov., 1934.

GALWAY, 8th Viscount (or. 1727) GEORGE VEBE ARUNDELL MONCKTON-ARUNDELL, K.C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O. (1917), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; Sherwood Rangers Yeomanry, 1900-04; 1st Life Guards, 1904; adjt., 1908; capt., 1911; staff capt., 4th Cavalry Brig., 1914; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., 2nd Cavalry Divn., 1915; A.A. and Q.M.G., 1917; brevet lieut.-col., 1919; commd. Life Guards, 1925-29; col. commdt., Hon. Arty. Coy., 1933; gov.-gen. and commr.-in-ch., New Zealand, 1935.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HENRY LIONEL, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsnr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. comsnr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. comsnr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comsnr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; high expdn., 1895 (desps. medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (desps. clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (desps. medal with clasp); gov., St. Helena, 1902; gov., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1911; gov., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; assumed govt., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

GALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; clk., vety. pathological lab., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, mily. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corps, 1st June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; "1914-16" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory meda.; labour inspr. offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., Dee., 1922.
GAMBLE, F. C.—Asst. dist. coman., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; res. mag., July, 1923; puisne judge, Uganda, 1936.

GAMBLE, JOSEPH SAMUEL.—Ag. sub-inspr., pol., B. Guiana, 1905; sub-inspr., pol., 1906; Hythe musketry certif., 1908; dist. inspr. pol., 1912; county inspr., 1920; ag. dep. inspr.-gen. and supt., fire brigade for various periods in 1920, 1922, 1924 and 1926; inspr.-gen., pol., Fiji, and commdt., Fiji defence force, Oct., 1927; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1928.

GAMBLES, ROBERT MOYLAN, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1910; ed. Westminster Schl. (King's schol.) and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (cl. II nat. sci. tripus, pt. I in 1931); R. (Dick) Vety. Coll., Edin. (C.O. scholarship) and Univ. of Edin.; vety. offr., Cyprus, Jan., 1935.
GAME, FREDERICK HAROLD ROBERTS, A.C.G.I. (1921), A.M.I.C.E. (1925).—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology, S. Kensington; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1925.

GAME, AIR. VICE-MARSHALL SIR PHILIP WOOLCOCKT, G.B.E.(1929), K.C.B.(1924), K.C.M.G. (1935), C.B. (1919), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse; ent. R.A., 1895; capt., 1901; adjt., 1902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., 3rd and 2nd grade, W.O., 1910-14; served, S. Africa, 1901-02 (desps., Queen's Med. five clasps); European war, 1914-18 (desps. six times); dir., training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air. mem. for personnel on air coun., 1923-28; ret. list, 1929; gov., New South Wales, 1930-36.
GANADO, PROF. EDGAR, LL.D.—B. 1884; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; LL.D., 1910; prof. of law, 1921; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., 1928; judge, 1929; ex. in law, 1930.

GANADO, ROBERTO F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag. judl. pol., 1918; examr. laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., July to Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cts., June to Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., Sept., 1925.

GANE, PERCIVAL CARLETON, M.A. (Oxon.), L.L.B. (Cape).—B. 1874; ed. Kingswood Schl., Bath and Jesus Coll., Oxford; admitted advoc., S. Africa, 1903; K.C., 1919; ag. judge many occasions, 1920-34; puisne judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1934.
GARDNER, H. M.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928.
GARDNER, JOHN HTSLOP, B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; asst. engrnr., Basutoland, 1909; gov't. engrnr., Swariland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARDNER, JOSEPH HENRY.—B. 1882; active serv. with Imp. Yeomanry, S. African War; mil. serv., E. African Exped. Force attd. Rly. Corps, 1916-18; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rlys., Mar., 1918; dist. loco. supt., 1930; loco. running supt., 1933.
GARLAND, T. V., B.A., B.A.I. (T.C.D.).—B. 1906; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1930; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1934.

GARLICK, GEORGE HERBERT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1886; med. offr., Johore, 1917; Malayan med. serv., 1923; ag. prin. med. offr., Johore, 1924, 1931 and 1933; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927; prin. med. offr., 1934.

GARNER, JOSEPH JOHN SAVILLE.—B. 1908; ed. Highgate Schl. (senr. foundation schl., 1923-26) and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schl., 1926-29); mod. and med. languages tripus Pt. I, cls. 2 div. 1; ditto, Pt. II, cls. 1; B.A., 1930; asst. med. lang. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1929-30; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1930.

GARNETT, CHRISTOPHER BRADSHAW, M.A. (Agr.).—B. 1903; ed. Clayesmore Schl., Winchester and Peterhouse, Cambridge; B.A. (Agr.), 1924; M.A. (Cantab.), 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907; seconded for service in Oversea Sttlm. Office, Jan., 1919; promoted asst. prin. under clause 51, 1924; asst. to the rep. in the C'wealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1931.

GARRAN, SIR ROBERT RANDOLPH, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT. BACh. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comte. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty.-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901; and partly, draftman; solr.-gen., 1916-32; chmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARROOD, HARRY ST. CLAIR.—B. 1882; ed. St. Polycarp's Schl., Farnham; articled architect; Sudan civ. serv., 1906; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1917; capt., 1918; served with Hedjaz Expedy. Force; Br. agt., Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; M.C., 3rd cls., Order of Nahda from King Hussein; ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; gen. headqrs., Egypt to 1928; crown survr. and engrnr., Gibraltar, 1929.

GARSON, ALEXANDER DENIS.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hons. hist. tripus; M.A.; cadet, Nigeria, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1933.

GARTON, HARRY.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th Aug., 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials offr., R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admiralty, 21st Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Coman. of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr.; C.O., 21st Oct., 1922.

GARVEY, FRANCIS WILCOCKS.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport offr.; served Transvaal town pol., 1904-08; asst. comanr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1909; comanr., pol., 1918; recd. thanks of govt. for investigation and suppression of human sacri- fices to Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; comanr., pol., Lagos, 1922; ag. dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Feb., 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Mar., 1926.

GARVY, RONALD HERBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.A.I.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; ast. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; ast. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932; ag. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934.

GARVIN, SIR THOMAS FORREST, Kt. Bach. (1935), K.C.—B. 1881; Crown counsel, Ceylon, Mar., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1913; ag. addl. dist. judge, Colombo, Feb., 1914; solr.-gen., 1915; ag. puisne just., Nov., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1922 to Dec., 1922; ag. puisne just., sup. ct., Jan., 1923; puisne just., June, 1924; ag. ch. just., July to Sept., 1926; ret., 1936.
GASH, VINCENT GORDON.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1911-15; sub-inspr., 1916; passed 3rd grade Secwana exam., 1923; inspr., 1929.

GATER, BOSSLEY ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; ast. entomologist, dept. of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria resch. offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930; del. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Dec., 1930.

GATFIELD, WILLIAM HUBERT, M.C. — B. 1895; ed. Streatham Gram. Schl. and St. George's Coll., London; apptd. to the 2nd divn. of the Imp. civ. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Rifles, 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France, and demob. with rank of lieut., 1919; cadet, S. S'tltna., Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to study Chinese, July, 1921; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1922; 2nd ast. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1926; offr. cls. III, Dec., 1931; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, May, 1934.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; apptd. ast. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec., 1928.

GEAR, HARRY SUTHERLAND.—B. 1903; B.Sc. (1s cls. hon.) 1925; M.B., B.Ch. Witwatersrand Univ., S. Afr., 1928; D.P.H., Univ. London, 1931; D.T.M. & H., R.C.P.S. Eng., 1932; med. offr., N. Rhodesia med. serv., Aug., 1929; ast. patho., S. Afr. Inst. Med. Res., Mar., 1930; S. Afr. govt. post grad. schol. in pub. health, London Sch. of Hy. and Trop. Med., Sept., 1930; hd., dept. of prev. med. and med. statistics, Henry Lester Inst., Shanghai, July, 1932; ast. health offr., Union of S. Afr., Mar., 1935; published several articles on anthropology and pub. health in various med. juls.

GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. ast. to irrig. engnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature study and agr. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1918; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agr. economics and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under-sec. for agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; comm. coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.

GENEVE, FREDERIC PIERRE, K.C. (1929).—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1922; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag.ch. just., Mar., 1931.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, O.B.E. (1935) D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Perm. Under-Sec. of State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1880; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk., gov.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road survr., Grenada, 1905; ag. supt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; ast. supt., pub. wks., 1916; supt., pub. wks., 1922.

GEORGE, HENRY BERNARD.—B. 1880; clk., Imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; clk., P.W.D., 1903; clk., C. and A.G.'s office, 1903; clk., audit office, Cape admin., 1912; served, Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.F.; offr. in ch., local govt. audit, 1920; prov. audr., Natal Prov., 1932; prov. audr., Transvaal Prov., 1933; author of "Standard Electricity Accounts in S.A."

GEORGE, REGINALD, R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1887; ed. H.M. Schl. Ship "Conway"; seconded for serv., Lake Nyasa, 1915; commodr., marine transport, Sept., 1918; ag. marine supt. in 1922, 1925, 1928 and 1931; marine supt., Oct., 1932.
GEORGE, NANCY MARGARET FANNY.—B.1900; ed. St. Catherine's High Schl., Malta, Devonport Secondary Schl., Froebel Training Coll., Bedford; mistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

GEORGI, MAJOR CHARLES DENIS VICTOR, O.B.E. (Mil.), B.Sc. (Lond), F.I.C., Dulwich Coll.); Univ. of Lond., Univ. Coll.—B. 1890; ast. agrl. chem., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ag. agrl. chem. on various occasions, 1924-31.

GERAHTY, CHARLES CYRIL, K.C. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt. E. Lancs. Regt., 1910; lieut., 1911; capt., 1914; ast. res. and commr., prov. etc., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; war serv. in France, Belgium and W. Africa, 1914-19; restored to civil establt., N. Prova., Nigeria, Nov., 1918; legal ast., land directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1920-26; ag. atty.-gen. and puisne judge on various occasions; joint compiler, 2nd vol., stat. law of Cyprus, 1923; atty.-gen., Dec., 1926; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1929; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Apr., 1932; puisne judge, S'pore, July, 1933; legal adviser. to gov., Malta, 1935.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.); B.A. 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1929; supt., marine and cust., chandu, posts and tels., audr. and pub. pros., Kelantan, Jan., 1931; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1933; regar., titles, Selangor in addn., July, 1933; offr., cls. II., Oct., 1934; dist. offr., Seremban, Feb., 1935.
GERMAN, WALTER.—B. 1885; bldg. inspr., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1913; ag. ch. sany. inspr. in addn., Aug., 1919 to May, 1920.
GERRAND, JOHN KENNEDY.—B. 1899; asst. engnr., Basutoland, 1929.

GETHIN, PERCY EDWARD LOWELL, A.F.C.—B. 1884; ed. Radley Coll.; R. Geographical Soc'y. dipl. in surveying, 1908; explorn. in S. Arabia, 1909; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1910; in charge, topographical surveys in the field, 1912; on mily. serv., 1914-20; major, R.F.C., 1916; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, 1923; ag. reg. gen., 1923; dir., surveys, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; tech. adviser, air bd., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; dir., civ. aviation, 1930.

GETLEY, ALFRED CUPPHEY.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schl., Eastbourne; acott. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paymr.-lieut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit. dept., Apr., 1928; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions; asst. audr., Kenya, 1929; asst. audr., col. audit dept., 1933.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, govt. stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.

GIBBINGS, ALBERT VICTOR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; Kedah, Apr., 1913; ag. exec. engnr., Kedah, Jan., 1917; exec. engnr., Kedah, Mar., 1922; exec. engnr., Johore, Sept., 1925; ag. senr. exec. engnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1929; water wks. engnr., Seremban, June, 1930; senr. exec. engnr., Klang, Apr., 1931; ag. state engnr., Pahang, June, 1931; senr. exec. engnr., Coast, Kuantan, Jan., 1932; do., Seremban, Dec., 1933; ag. state engnr., Kedah, June, 1934.
GIBBON, GEOFFREY MCKAY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1896; ed. Elgin acad., Gordon Coll., Aberdeen and Univ. of Edin.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Oct., 1933.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GIBBS, GEORGE HOWARD.—B. 1889; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920; dist. commr., 1925.

GIBSON, H. D.—B. 1886; ed. Bedale and Lincoln Gram. Schls.; junr. traffic asst., Nigerian rlv., 1910; asst. traffic supt., 1913; dist. ditto, 1920; traffic supt., 1922; divnl. supt., 1927; supt. of line, 1932.

GIBSON, LESLIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schl., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S., attd. Grand Fleet; lieut., R.A.F.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; attd., atty-gen.'s office, S'pore, Apr., 1920; attd., food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir., food prodn., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. regir. and asst. offr., assigned, Penang, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1925; asst. regir., sup. ot., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Aug., 1932; attd. M.L.C., Mar., 1934; ag. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, May, 1934; ag. stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Feb., 1935.

GIBSON, ROY ALEXANDER.—B. 1885; ed. Brandon High Schl. and Brandon Coll.; ent. fed. govt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transf'd. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; asst. dep. min., June, 1919; comsrr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923.

GIE, S. F. N., Ph.D.—B. 1885; ed.
