Africa, 1926; sec., ditto, 1927; min., plenipotentiary, Berlin, Oct., 1934; publications include a history of S. Africa in Afrikaans in two vols.

GILBERT, HUMPHREY RALEIGH.—B. 1899; ent. R. Navy, 1912; served, Great War 1914-18; ret., Jan., 1923; lieut. commr. (ret'd.), Feb., 1928; admtn. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1927; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1933.

GILBERT, SAMUEL MOORE, B.Sc. (Lond.), N.D.A. Dip. Agr. (Wye).—B. 1892; ed. City of Norwich Schl., Wye Coll. and London Univ.; comsrr., The 1st King's Regt.; served in War, 1914-18; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1929; ag. dir., 1929-30; M.L.C., 1933; ch scientific offr., coffee research and experimental station, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1934.
GILBERT, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.R.C.V.S.—Stock dept., N.S.W., 1913; lect., vety. schl., Sydney Univ., 1914 and 1920; mil. serv., 1914-19; s-nr. vety. offr., Palestine, July, 1920; vety. pathologist, 1934.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunswick House private schl., B. Guiana; entd. H.M. Customs, B. Guiana, Dec., 1899; copyist, regir.'s office, Oct., 1900; 6th cls. clk., ditto, Mar., 1902; 5th cls. clk., cust., 1903; asst. commissary, Sept., 1905; clk. to atty.-gen., May, 1906; stip. mag., J.P., coroner, and comsrr. of oaths, 1910; chmn., Rose Hall village partition of lands comsrr., 1911; called to the bar, Grav's Inn, 1914; ag. asst. govt. sec., Mar. to Sep., 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1914-20; recd. thanks of S. of S.; practised at English bar, 1920-21; comsrr. to enquire into disturbance, H.M. penal attlt., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. jnr. puisne judge, in 1923 and 1925-6; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1928, 1926 and 1927; puisne judge, Sept., 1927; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, 1931.

GILKS, J. L., C.M.G. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Medical offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; prin. med. offr., Kena, Feb., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. councils; dir. med. and sanry. services, 1926.
GILL, EDWIN—B. 1896; on naval serv., 1915-19; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926.

GILL, EDWIN LEONARD, D.S.C.—B. 1877; asst., Manchester Museum, June, 1900; curator, Hancock Museum, Newcastle-on-Tyne, July, 1901; asst. R. Scottish Museum, July, 1922; dir., S African Museum, Cape Town, Jan., 1926.

GILL, HENRY SKWELL CURRAN, M.A.—B. 1903; ed. Edin. R. High Schl., Edin. Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford (coll. exhib.) (1st cls. hons. classics); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1929; seconded to secr. as asst. sec., 1935; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934-35.

GILL, RONALD GODDARD.—B. 1896; ed. Haberdashers' Askes Schol. (Hampstead); on mili. serv. overseas, 1917-19; commissioned, Nov., 1917; temp. clk., Miny. of Lab., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., Mar., 1923.

GILLESPIE, JAMES DAVID, B.Sc.—B. 1905; ed. Musselburgh Gram. Schol., George Watson's Boys' Schol. and Edin. Univ.; three years land reclamation and cotton growing Nile Delta; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1930.

GILLMAN, CLMENT, F.G.S., M.S.A.S.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1882; ed. Swiss Federal tech. high schol., Zurich; on rly. constr. and surveys in E. Africa, 1906-14; served E.A.P. Force, 1916-19; capt., rly. corps; exec. engrnr., African military (occupied territory), 1916-18; ch. engrnr., ditto, 1918-19; dist. engrnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1919; senr. dist. engrnr., Apr., 1925; ch. engrnr., Apr., 1928.

GILLMAN, HAROLD, B.Sc., (Agr.), M.Sc., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1908; ed. Dale Coll. and Transvaal Univ. Coll., B.Sc. (agri.); Cambridge and Trinidad, (A.I.C.T.A.); Univ. of Witwatersrand (M. Sc.); agr. asst., Tanganyika Terry., Oct., 1933; dist. agrl. offr., 1934.

GILMOUR, ANDREW.—B. 1898; ed. Royal High Schol., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; served with 14th A. & S.H., B.F.F., 1916-17; M.A. hons. (Edin.), 1920; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attch. to res. councillors' office, Penang, May, 1921; offr., cls. V, asst. contrlr., lab., Penang and asst. prot., lab., Kedah, Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Malacca, Feb., 1926; contrlr., land rev., S'pore, Jan., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; res., Labuan, June, 1928; dist. offr., Jasln, Sept., 1929; ditto, U. Kelantan, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

GILMOUR, GORDON JOHN.—B. 1893; ed. Fremantle schol. (W.A.); supvr. govt. monops., S.S., Feb., 1911; war serv., Jan., 1915-Mar., 1919; supvr., tob. dep., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1919; supvr., grade I, Jan., 1924; confir., Feb., 1930; asst. supt., S'pore, Nov., 1931; do., Penang, June, 1933; in addn., dep. contr., rubber, S.S., and dep. supt., rubber control, Penang Is., June, 1934.

GIMSON, FRANKLIN CHARLES.—B. 1890; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1914; attchd. office of naval intell. offr., in add. to his own duties; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in add. to his own duties; on mil. duty, 1918; addtnl. asst. col. sec., July, 1919; offr. asst. to govt. agt., N. C. Prov., Sept., 1919; addnl. asst. col. sec., Mar., 1920; 4th asst. ditto, Sept., 1920; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1922; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. collr., cust., Mar. to May, 1928; addnl. asst. dir., educ., Mar., 1929; sec. to min., educ., July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Feb., 1932; do., Kegalle, Dec., 1933.
GITTEENS, DARNLEY ERROLL WYNHAM.—B. 1890; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1913; income tax commr., 1931; offr. treas., 1935.

GLEN, JAMES ALEXANDER, M.A.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. dir. of edn., Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. adviser, Min. of educ., Apr., 1922.
GLENDAY, VINCENT G., O.B.E. (1929), M.A. Oxon. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry.

GNANAMUTTU, JOSEPH ISAAC.—B. 1879; office asst., dir., agr., Apr., 1925; sec., rubber and coconut resch. schemes, Oct., 1929; apptd. Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1932; addl. treasy., Oct., 1932; office asst., Batticaloa kach., June, 1934.

GODEBOLT, S. B.—B. 1884; olk. treasy., Cape 1901; Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. olk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; ob. olk., grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; commr., pensions, July, 1932.
GODDARD, HARRY FREDERICK.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

GODDEN, HENRY JAMES, M.B.E. (1929)—B. 1877; telegraphist, Beaufortland, 1902; postmr., 1907; olk. to asst. commr., 1917; ag. asst. commr., 1922; passed 1st grade Scuto, 1922; ch. olk., mast. of ct. and reglar., 1924.

GODFREY, GEORGE CROSBIE.—B. 1880; ed. Waimataitai Schol., Timaru, N.Z.; N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1897; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., 1901; asst. ch. clk., pub. wks. dept., 1908; ch. clk., 1914; asst. under-secr., 1920; sec. marine, 1923; sec. of lab. and commr. of unemployment, 1932-35; commr., transp., 1935.

GODFREY, SIR JOSEPH ED., KT. BACH (1914).—B. 1858; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy registr. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., registr.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., registr.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of poor law commrs., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and Bahamas to Internat. Leprosy Confce., Bergen, 1909.

GODLEY, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Dublin Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1896; major, 1897; colonel, 1906; apptd. comdt. New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gazetted major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1916; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officier (Belgian) 1917; Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Croix de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.

GODSALL, WALTER DOUGLAS, B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attchd. N'Eliya kach., Jan., 1924, ditto, Badulla kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Mar., 1926; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Dec., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Mar., 1929; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Jan., 1930; do., Galle, May, 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt. Matara, in addn., Oct., 1934.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, ROBERT ANNESLEY, F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Rhodesia, 1923; dir., roads, 1927; dep. dir., surveys, Tanganyika, 1929; dir., land registr. and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1933; Br. del. of Evkaf, Mar., 1935.

GOLDBURG, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; entd. crown agents' office, 1902; asst. head, gen. dept., 1919; asst. head, finance dept., 1922; asst. head, shipping dept., 1925; asst. head, finance dept., 1930; dep. head, finance dept., May, 1933; acctnt., W. African Currency bd., 1912-33.

GOLLAN, SIR HENRY COWPER, KT. BACH. (1921), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1886; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, commr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., Jan.-Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admntr., June-Aug., 1900; atty.-gen., Nor. Nig., Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1900-Apr., 1901; ch. just., Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, 1904; compiled proc. of N. Nigeria, 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Ct. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, Dec., 1906; chmn. of produce comm., 1906 and of aliens comm., 1906; chmn. of bd. of educn., Bermuda, July, 1907; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1911; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1918; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1924; chmn., salaries comm., 1928-29; ret., Aug., 1930.

GOMES, STANLEY EUGENE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; ag. S.M., Br. Guiana, 1927 and 1928; S.M., 1929; ag. asst. atty. gen., 1929-33; asst. atty. gen., 1933; ag. atty. gen., 1934.
GONTIER, GEORGES, C.A., L.I.A.—B. 1869; ed. Commissioner's Sch., Montreal and priv. tuition; audr.-gen., Dominion of Canada, Jan., 1924.

GOOCH, STANLEY JOHN WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., chtd. civ. engnr.—B. 1889; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1912; exec. engnr., Johore, May, 1922; sr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., June, 1927; sr. exec. engnr., hydraulic, Selangor, Mar., 1929; res. engnr., civ. aerodrome, Spore, Jan., 1933; senr. exec. engnr., Kinta, Oct., 1933; ag. state engnr., Perak in 1933 and 1934.
GOOD, CECIL ALBERT.—B. 1892; called to bar, 1913; served in Army, lieut. and capt., 1915-20; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1921.
GOOD, EDWIN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Auckland gram. schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1904; advisory offr., 1925; asst. comptr., cust., 1930; comptr. cust., 1935.

GOODALL, EDWARD BASIL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1934), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Longborough Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; asst. native commr., N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1909; native commr., Sept., 1911; asst. mag., Nov., 1921; ag. dist. commr., Apr., 1928; dist. commr., Feb., 1929; prov. commr., Apr., 1929; ag. sec., native affrs., Sept.-Oct., 1931; mem., leg. coun.

GOODMAN, ARTHUR MITCHELL.—B. 1886; ed. New Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; supernumerary mag., Ipoth, June, 1915; pres., rent assessment bd. for Penang, July, 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supermny. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. II; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., S.S., Nov., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. IA, mem., exec. coun., S.S., July, 1931; res. coun., Penang, Apr., 1933.

GOODMAN, REGINALD ERNEST.—B. 1886; clk., Basutoland, 1913; asmtg. clk., 1916; fin. sec., mast. regnr., deeds, Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1927; audr., Basutoland, Bechuanaland Prot. and Swazieland, 1935.

GOODMAN, TRAFFORD FLAVIUS.—B. 1900; ed. Lodge Sch., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1926; pol. mag., dist. "A," Barbados, 1927; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt, etc., and judge, asst. ct. of appl., various periods, 1928-1930; pol. mag., dist. "C," 1930.

GOODWIN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR THOMAS HERBERT JOHN CHAPMAN, K.C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1932), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1898), hon. D.Sc. (Oxon), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1871; ed. Newton Coll., Devon and St. Mary's Hosp., London; comdr., Army Med. Serv., 1993; staff surg., Mohmand Field Force, N.W. Frontier, 1897 (desp., med. and clasp); European War, 1914-18 (3 times ment. in desp., Mons Star, 1914, Allied and Victory medals); A.D.M.S. with Mr. Ballfours mission to U.S.A., 1917; dir.-gen., Army Med. Serv., 1918-23; retired, 1923; comdr., Legion of Honour; comdr., Belgian Order of Leopold; comdr., Italian Order of the Crown; Belgian Croix de Guerre; American D.S.M., Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; gov., Queensland, 1927-32.

GOONETILLEKE, OLIVER ERNEST, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1892; asst. audr., rly., Ceylon, Aug., 1921; asst. col. audr., Feb., 1926; col. audr., June, 1931; audr.-gen., July, 1931; Ceylon govt. del. to Internat. rly. cong., Cairo, Jan., 1933.

GOPSIILL, WALTER LEONARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ed. Alley's Schl., Dulwich, King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; R.F.C. (2nd lieut.), 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918-20; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1926.
GORDON, HON. SIR ALEXANDER, KT. BACH. (1930).—Judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 1910-28.

GORDON, CHARLES AUGUSTINE.—B. 1882; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., St. Edmund's Coll., Herts. and Edinburgh Univ. (faculty of law); writer to the signet (W.S.); war serv., France and Flanders, 1915-19 (The Royal Scots); asst. admntr. gen., Zanzibar, 1925; offr. admntr. and pub. trustee, Nigeria, 1929.

(S.R.), Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-20 (ment. in desp.); asst. dist. wks. offr., Iraq, 1920; dist. wks. offr., 1921; ag.-dep. dir., wks., Baghdad, Aug.-Sept., 1921; do., Basrah, Oct., 1921 to Jan., 1922; dir., wks., Bahamas, 1923; asst. D.P.W., Trinidad, May, 1928.

GORDON-HALL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; lieut., M.V.I., Mar., 1923; dist. offr. Kuala Langat, May, 1926; offr., cls. III., May, 1931; dist. offr., Krian, May, 1932; ag. sec., res., Selangor, July, 1933.
GORDON-SMITH, F.—see Smith, Frederick Gordon.

GORING, GEORGE INGHAM, A.R.T.C. (Glasgow), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Allan Glen Schl.; asst. mech. engnr., col. transport dept., Br. Guiana; ag. ch. mech. engnr., transport and harbrs. dept., Nov., 1932-Aug., 1933; ch. mech. engnr., Sept., 1933.
GORMLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1904; ed. Dover County Schl. and Reading Univ.; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929.

GORSUCH, Leslie Harold.—B. 1897; ed. Ipswich Schol. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A. (1920); war serv., May, 1916-Jan., 1919; lieut., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1920; attchd. to dist. office, Pekan; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1923; 4th asst. sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1932; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1933.

GOSDEN, Minnie, M.B., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—War serv. as V.A.D. nurse, 1914-17; chem. pathology asst., Hale chem. lab., London hosp., 1925; pathology asst., 1926; asst., bacteriological dept., 1927; asst., med. offr., Lingfield Epileptic Colony, 1928; bacteriologist, Cyprus, Feb., 1929.

GOSLING, William Richard, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Sutton Valence and Cambridge Univ.; served in France, the Balkans and Turkey, 1915-1919; demob. with rank of major, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, May, 1920.

GOSTELOW, Cecil, F.I.A. (Lond.).—B. 1891; ed. Fort St. Model High Schol., Sydney, N.S.W.; actuary, N.Z. nat. provident fund, 1921; asst. actuary, N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1922; actuary, govt. life insurance and friendly societies depts., 1923; also sec., govt. life insurance, 1925; govt. actuary, 1926.
GOTT, John Bury.—B. 1890; ed. Lancing Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1922.

GOURLAY, William Newlands.—B. 1894; cadet, S.S., May, 1920; offr. cls. V., Feb., 1922; asst. dist. judge, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Feb., 1926; 3rd mag. Singapore, Jan., 1929; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1931; ag. asst. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Nov., 1931; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1933.

GOULD, Lieut.-Col. Hon. Sir Albert John, Kt. Bach. (1908).—B. 1847; solr.; M.L.A., N. S. Wales, 1881-98; M.L.C., 1899-1901; min. for just. in Reid ministry, 1894-8; returned to the first senate of Commonwealth parlmt., 1901; pres. of the senate, Feb., 1907, to June, 1910; ret. from politics.

GOWERS, Sir William Frederick, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), M.A., Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1898 (1st cls. class. tripus); entd. service of B.S.A. Co., Jan., 1899; asst. native commr., Matabeleland 1900; resigned, 1902; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1902; 2nd cls. res., 1906; 1st cls. res., 1910; ag. chief sec., Sept., 1912; lieut.-gov., N. Nigeria, June, 1921; gov. of Uganda, 1925-32; senr. crown agt. for the cols., 1st July, 1932.

GOWRIE, 1st Baron (Cr. 1935), Brig.-Gen. Hon. Sir Alexander Gore Arkwright Horke-Ruthven, V.C. (1890), G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1928), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Eton; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Inf., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1898; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent operns. (V.C., 4th cls. Osmanieh, English and Egyptian med. with clasp, three times ment. in desps.); sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroys of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov.-gen., C'wealth of Australia, 1908; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1914-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., ment. in desps. five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brig. (Guards); 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928; gov., N.S.W., 1935; gov.-gen. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1936.

GRAEME, Alexander William Sutherland, M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern, pupil, Crewe, 1902-6; held various appts. on Eng. and Scottish rlys.; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., 1915-24; ag. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., May, 1917, Apr., 1919, Mar., 1922, Dec., 1923; loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., Mar., 1924; ch. mech. engr., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1932.

GRAHAM, Major Bernard James.—B. 1882; served S. African war, 1899-1902; Cape Garrison Arty., 1909-10; 2nd lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., June, 1913; lieut., ditto, Apr., 1915; temp. capt., Jan., 1917; temp. major and 2nd in commd., N. Rhodesia Pol. Service Batt., June, 1918; twice ment. in desps.; hon. capt., Aug., 1919; offr. commdg. town and dist. pol., C.I.D. and ch. emigrn. offr., N. Rhodesia and supt., Livingstone prison, July, 1920; offr. commdg. N. Rhodesia pol., supt., cent. prison and dist. commdt., volunteers, Mongu, Barotse, Apr., 1922; ditto, Livingstone, Jan., 1923; ditto, Fort Jameson, Dec., 1923; capt., N. Rhodesia pol., July, 1924; J.P. for N. Rhodesia; ag. commdt., N. Rhodesia Regt., 1933 and 1934; commdt., 1935.

GRAHAM, Rt. Hon. George Perry, P.C. (1925), LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schls., Iroquois and Morrisburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg Herald, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder, elected to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898; 1902 and 1905; provcl. sec. of Ontario, 1904; resig. with the govt., 1906; leader of the opposition in the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the privy coun. for Canada and min. of rlyws. and canals, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brockville, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g.e., 1911; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did not seek re-election at gen.-elec., 1917; re-ele., g.e., Dec., 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of naval service in King admn., Dec., 1921; min. of rlyws. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

GRAHAM, James MacKay.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Gram. Schol. and S. African Coll.; ent. Transvaal civ. ser., 1903; asst. mag., Johannesburg, 1916; mag., Heidelberg, 1927; do., Lichtenburg, 1930; do., Durban, 1933; do., Cape Town, 1934.

GRAHAM, Richard, A.L.A.A., F.R.Econ. S.—B. 1896; U.K. postoffice, 1911-20; supt., P.O., S'pore, Dec., 1920; acctnt., Penang, 1923; supt., mails, Penang, Apr., 1929; senr. acctnt., mails, S'pore, June, 1927; acctnt., P. & T., F.M.S., Sept., 1928; ag. senr. acctnt. various occasions 1929-32.

GRAHAM, Sir Thomas Lynedoch, Kt. Bach. (1920), K.C.—B. 1860; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Camb. (R.A.), barrister-at-law, Inner Tem., 1885; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope; M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min., June to Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Districla local div., Apr., 1913; chrmn. pub. serv. inquiry comm., June, 1918.

GRANNUM, Sir Edward Allan, Kt. Bach. (1928), C.M.G., 1915.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. regis. off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1886, to Nov., 1887; clk. master-in-chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898; asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to Mar., 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor-gen., Mauritius, Apr., 1909; recvr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912; ag.col.sec., on various occasions, 1919-20 and 1922-23; col. sec., Aug., 1923; asmdt. govt. on various occasions, 1924-30; ret., 1931.

GRANNUM, REGINALD CLIFTON, C.M.G. (1925).—B. 1872; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supvr.of cust., G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leone, June 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast, Apr., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. hd. of educ., July, 1903, to June, 1904, Dec., 1904, to Dec., 1905, June, 1906, to May, 1907; Oct., 1907, to Sept., 1908; admitted student of Gray's Inn, Aug., 1908; recvr.-gen., B. Guiana, Sept., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Dec., 1908; ag. govt. sec., July to Oct., 1913; deputy gov., Sept. to Oct., 1913; treas. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Kenya, Sept., 1922.
GRANT, D.K.S.—Asst. conservator of forests E.A.P., Dec., 1912; conservator of forests, Tanganjika Territory, Dec., 1920.

GRANT, EZEKIEL AUGUSTUS.—Ent. British Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., as copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd cls. clk. (asst. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv. with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag. asst. inspr., schls., during 1921 to 1923; asst. inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. attv.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize and offl. admstr. and offl. recr. from Apr., 1931.
GRANT, LIONEL KIRTH.—B. 1875; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1894; ch. clk. P.O., 1913; acctnt., 1926; ag. col. postmr. various periods, 1927-30; ch. clk., audit office, 1931; audr.-gen., 1934.
GRANT, PETER.—B. 1883; Hong Kong pol., Nov., 1906; inspr., ditto, Dec., 1923; ch., ditto, Mar., 1928; King's pol. med., 1933; asst. supt., pol., Feb., 1933.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Schil., Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col. sec., office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls V, dist. offr., N. Tebal, Nov., 1923; offr., cls IV, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; asst. advr., K. Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls III, Nov., 1932; asst. advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A), S.S., Feb., 1935.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HERDER, M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1929; sec. retrenchment comte., July, 1930; asst. to P.M.G. in addn., July, 1930; dist. offr., South, in addn., Dec., 1930; extra asst. col. sec., and sec. to harbr. advisory comte., 1931; 1st pol. mag. in addn., 1933; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of counz., June, 1933; extra asst. col. sec., Apr., 1935; col. sec., Bermuda, 1935.

GRANTHAM, SIDNEY HOWARD.—B. 1883; clk., W.O., Apr., 1901; asst. acct., army accnts. dept., Apr., 1904; served S. Africa, 1904-07 and 1912-14; asst. commr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1915; sent. ditto, Apr., 1930; asst. inspr.-gen., pol., Jan., 1931; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., July, 1934.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl.; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. exec. engnr., May-Dec., 1933.

GRAY, CHAS. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler. asst., offr. recr.'s. office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th cls. offr., col. sec.'s. office, 1921; 5th cls. offr., 1923; 4th cls. offr., 1926; sec. to prisons comte., 1928; 2nd cls. clk., 1933; ag. prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns. in 1932 and 1933.

GRAY, H. W., O.B.E.—Office supt., lieut.-gov.'s off., E.A.P., May, 1908; clk. of the couns., Oct., 1908; asst. dist. comsnr., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; ch. regis. of natives, Jan., 1920.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., M.R., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M., Rot. Hosp. (Dub.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr. surgn., class III, Oct., 1913; ch. med. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1919; ch. med. offr., Singapore, Nov., 1920; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S.S., and tempy. M.L.C., for various periods, 1923-31; state surgn., Kedah, Oct., 1931.
GRAY, JOHN MACGARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (I), L.M. (Rot), D.P.H. (Univ. Dub.).—B. 1898; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1933; ag. M.O.H., 1934.

GRAY, JOHN MILNER.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl. and King's Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; admitted solr., sup. ct., 1923; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1932; war serv. with B.R.F., France (twice wounded); asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 1920; mag., 1924; ag. solr.-gen., 1929; ag. puisne judge, 1933; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1934; O.A.G., May, 1936.

GRAY, R. B. 1880; clk., treas., 1902; clk., rev. dept., 1904; dep. recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; clk., rev. dept., Jo'Burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin.clk., 1921; survr., 1923; auctnt., 1929; recr., rev., E. London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933.

GRAY, SIR REGINALD KT. BACH (1920), K.C. (1908).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled, in 1884, Bermuda laws from 1690-1883; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1690-1902; counsel to statute law consolidation comte. since 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1900; ag. chief just. in Oct. and Nov., 1911; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun., M.L.A., 1896-1897, 1900-1906, and from 1907; ret.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), M.P.C.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. med. offr., 1911; med. supt., Nelson (N.Z.) mental hosp., 1920; med. supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep. inspr.-gen. mental hosps., N.Z., 1925; inspr.-gen., 1927.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ. of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cls. hona., Gladstone Prize and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; some time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech. Coll., Brighton; examnr., Univ. of Edinburgh, Oxford and Cambridge joint bd. and Oxford local exams.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1912; mob., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen staff, Aldershot commnd., Dec., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Educn. Corps, Nov., 1920; major, R.A.O.R., Mar., 1923; comntr., educn., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1924; (appt altered to dir., Nov., 1927); mem., educn. comntr., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free liby., May, 1926; mem., ct. of policy and combined ct., June, 1927; chmn. diamond and gold industries comntr., Sept., 1927; mem. first exec. and leg. couns. under reformed constitution, July, 1928; ag. col. sec. and gov't. dep. on various occasions.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (Tor.), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Milne med. in trop. med. Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1916-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in adml., Bahamas, 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of small-pox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1921; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, K.T. BACH. (1904), K.C.—B. 1857; B.A., Oxon., honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. just., Apr., 1902; ret.

GRECH, WYNNDHAM, LL.D. (Malta), 1913.—Called to the bar (Grays Inn), 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; capt., R.A.F., 1918; sp. serv., Malta and Rome, 1917; attd., Italian Sup. Comd., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); transf., unemployed list (R.A.F.), 1919; asst. legal adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser and crown pros., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922 and in 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to Jan., 1924, Mar.-Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.
GREEN, CLAUDE.—B. 1879; entd. Trinidad civ. serv., Mar., 1896; ch. clk. to gen. man. of rlwys., Mar., 1917.

GREEN, FREDERICK WALTER VICTOR.—B. 1891; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 6th cls. clk., cust., 1914; asst. comntr., 1917; sec., excise bd., July, 1923; 3rd gde. comntr., July, 1925; 2nd gr. offr., dist. admntr., Jan., 1932; ag. ch. commissary and dist. comntr., Georgetown and E. Bank, Demerara, May, 1934; dist. comntr., W. Demerara, Jan., 1935.

GREEN, GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster City Sch.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut. The Buffs; wounded in France; temp. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny. of lab., Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., 1920 to June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st June, 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

GREEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1928)—B. 1897; clerical asst., col. sec.'s office, B. Guiana, 1912; 6th cls. clk., 1913; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; sec. to comntr. for considering disposal of colony lands, 1917; 2nd cls. clk., 1920; sec. to civ. serv. comntr., 1923; sec., B. Guiana section, Wembley, 1924; prin. clk., 1924; sec., rly. comntr., 1925; liaison offr., parly. delegation to Br. Guiana, 1926; sec., Br. Guiana delegation to W. Indian confce., London, 1926; sec. civ. serv. comntr., 1926-27; ag. 2nd astl. col. sec. and clk. exec. and leg. coun., various occasions, 1928-30; sec., Br. Guiana constitution comntr., 1927; prte. sec., O.A.G. in 1928; 2nd astl. col. sec., 1930; astl. col. sec., 1931; prin. astl. col. sec., 1934.

GREEN, HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Queen Mary's Grm. Sch., Hants.; entd. Royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1907; astl. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1911; seconded for mil. serv., 1917-20; supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1920; J.P., 1922.

GREEN, JOSEPH.—B. 1897; U.K. post office, Aug., 1914; on mil. serv., lieut., Indian Army Res., 1915-19; supt., mails, Penang, Feb., 1921; acctnt., Sep., 1924 and Nov., 1926; senr. supt., mails, Sp'ore, Dec., 1925; supt., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1932; ag. contrlr., Penang and P. Wellesley, Dec., 1933.

GREEN, MAURICE JOHN, V.D.—B. 1878; ed. Sea Point pub. schl. and S.A. Coll.; astl. clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1896; clk. of papers, 1897; comntr. clk., Cape leg. coun., 1904, and Union senate, 1910; gent. ush., black rod, 1916; clk. astl., 1926; clk. of senate, 1930; sworn trans. (Eng. Dutch); sec. and shorthand writer various govt. comntrs.; astl. sec. speaker's confce. on future const. senate; war serv., Anglo-Boer, in W.P.M.B.; Great war, in Ger. S.W. Afr. as nav. trnspt. Offr., in Harwich light cruiser. squad. and war cabinet secretariat; comdr., cdmg. Cape Town base.

GREEN, RICHARD THOMAS BONFIELD, M.D., B.S. (Melb.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1897; ed. Melbourne (Church of Eng. Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll.), Univ. of Melbourne; med. offr., F.M.S., June, 1925; pathologist II, inst. med. resch., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; malaria resch. offr., I, inst. med. resch., Jan., 1929; Milne Medal, Liv. S. of T.M., Mar., 1930; rep. S.S. and F.M.S. at 2nd Inter. Cong. on Malaria, Algiers, May, 1930; mem. of malaria comntr., health organizn., L. of N., Dec., 1930; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Resch., Apr., 1932-Feb., 1933; bacteriologist, F.M.S., do., 1934.

GREENBERG, LEOPOLD.—B. 1885; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., LL.B.; called to Transvaal bar, 1911; practised at Johannesburg, 1911-24; puisne judge, Transvaal Prov. divn. sup. ct., Nov., 1924.

GREENE, HON. COL. EDWARD MACKENZIE, C.M.G. (1909), K.C.V.D.—Hon. colonel in army; served in Zulu war, 1878-1879 (medal, with clasp); S. African war, 1899-1902, in command of Natal Carbineers (Queen's medal, with three clasps; twice mentioned in despatches); mem. of Natal legis. assm., 1893 to date of Union; min. for rly. and harbours, 13th July, 1908; Natal deleg. to S. African National Convention, Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; represented Natal at Impl. Confce. on defence, 1909; mem., rly. and harbours bd., Union of S. Africa, 1910 to 1916.
GREENE, CAPT. GORDON STRATTON.—B. 1887; ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; dip. forestry (Oxon); war serv., 1914-19; astl. cons. forests, Gold Coast, 1920; prov. forest offr., 1930.
GREENE, MAURICE CHERRY.—B. 1881; ed. at Rathmines's schl. and St. Stephen's Green

GREENFIELD, Lieut.-Col. Herbert, O.B.E., R. of O.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African War, 1901-02; joined R.A.V.C.; lieut., May, 1903; served in India, 1903-08; capt., May, 1908; attd. staff coll., 1910-12; ap. serv. and research work, N. and S. China, 1912-14; served in Great War, 1914-18 Tsingtau opera.; major, July, 1915; D.A.D.V.S., 10th divn., Gallipoli, Greater Macedonia, Serbia, Bulgaria, 1915-16; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; D.A.D.V.S., France and Belgium, 1917-18; ment. in despa.; A.D.V.S. (lieut.-col. temp.) Rhine Army, 1919; served in Waziristan opera., 1920 as D.A.D.V.S. and D.D.V.S. (temp. lieut.-col.) Wazir Force, 1920-21; ment. in despa.; D.A.D.V.S., India and in Burma, 1921-24; exec. duty, Aldershot, 1925-28; vety. offr., Barbados, Oct., 1928.

GREENHALGH, N., B.Sc., Hons. (Vict.).—B. 1888; agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1910; lecturer in agric. science to students of training coll., Antigua; asst. inspr. of schls., Barbados, 1912; lecturer in educ. and master of method, training inst., Codrington Coll.; ag. chief inspr. of schls., Trinidad, June to Oct., 1915; inspr., schls., Barbados, 1934.

GREENHILL, Marcus Arthur.—B. 1889; entd. C.O., Sep., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mily. serv., May, 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928; staff offr., July, 1934.

GREENIDGE, Charles Wilton Wood.—B. 1889; educ. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Nat. Sci. Tripos) 1910; LL.B., 1911; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1912; 1st cls. hon. criminal law; admitted to bar, Barbados, 1912; parochial treas., St. James', Barbados, 1915; mag., dist. D. St. Kita, 1919; mag., dist. D. Barbados, 1923; judge, ct. of appl., Barbados, 1925, mag., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1928 to June, 1929; ag. puine judge in 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. regis., Oct.-Nov., 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Jan., 1931; ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1932; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1936.

GREENLAND, Stanley Arthur.—Served with Natal Field Force, Rebellion, 1906; clk., govt. sec. dept., Papua, Mar., 1912; J.P., Sept., 1912; ag. asst. res. mag., Iona, May, 1913; ag. asst. res. mag., Kairuku, Mar., 1915; inspr., dept. native affairs, Mar., 1917; ag. curator, intestate estates, registr.-gen., etc., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. inspr., native affairs, 1918; ag. Papua govt. agt., Sydney, N.S.W., Dec., 1919; travelling inspr., native affairs, July, 1922; J.P. for N.S.W., Oct., 1923; ag. mem., land bd., Papua, Nov., 1924; ch. inspr., native affairs, Dec., 1924.
GREENSHIELDS, Hon. Robert A. E., LL.D., D.C.L.—B. 1861; ed. Danville Acad. and McGill Univ., Canada; ag. ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1929; ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1933.

GREENWAY, Percy James, F.L.S.—B. 1897; ed. East Anglian Inst. of Agr., Chelmsford; R. Horticultural Soc., Wisley, Ripley, and R. Botanic Gardens, Kew; mily. serv., France and Italy, 1916-19; herbarium asst., R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1922-25; do., and ag. demonstr. in systematic botany, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1925-28; systematic botanist, E. African agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

GREENWOOD, Geoffrey Martin, M.A. (Cantab.).—B, 1908; ed. Rugby and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; tropical African serv. course, 1930-31; cadet, Uganda, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1933; asst. dist. commr., Palestine, 1935; admstr. offr. (ast. sec.), Cyprus, July, 1935; ag. commr., Sept., 1935.

GREGG, James Reali (B.A., Cantab.)—B. 1899; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Harvard and Cambridge Unive.; called to N. Ireland bar, 1923; mem., Gray's Inn (Lond.), and King's Inn (Dublin); chmn., ct. of referees under Min. of Lab., N. Ireland, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., St. Vincent, May, 1929; atty.-gen., Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; govt. rep. on St. Vincent arrawroot bd.; attd. to C.O. for short period, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Jan., 1933.

GREGOROWSKI, Reginald Addington.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s office, O.F.S., Dec., 1896; priv. sec. to state pres., Mar., 1896; landdrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landdrost clk., Wepener, Jan., 1899; prin., govt. schl., Reddersburg, O.R.C., Oct., 1900; astd. clk., educ. dept., Mar., 1901; sh. clk. and regis., educ. dept., July, 1901; prin. clk., col. sec.'s off., June, 1909; ch. clk. to admstr., O.F.S. prov., Aug., 1910; prov. sec., O.F.S., Oct., 1924.
GRELL, Jessie Mitchinson Potter, M.B., B.S. Cantab., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1880; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, June, 1908; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912.
GRENFELL-HICKS, J. B.—B. 1891; Rhodesian pol., 1911-16; lieut., Rhodesia Native Regt., 1916-19; Rhodesian adminstr., 1919-20; asst. supt., Kenya pol., Nov., 1920; staff offr., 1923; sup't., 1926.

GRETON, Ronald Henry.—B. 1907; B.A. (Br. Col. Canada); trop. African serv. course, Oxford, 1928; cadet, Nigeria, 1929; seconded, Gambia, 1931; pvte. sec. to ag. gov., 1931 and 1933; astd. col. sec., 1932; ag. commr., 1934.

GRICE, Norman, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1893; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; astd., Chinese sect., Perak, Feb., 1923; astd., cust. dept., Mar., 1923; astd., Chinese sect., Jan., 1925; astd. prot., Chine., Selangor, Jan., 1925; astd. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Feb., 1927; astd. contr., rubber, S'pore, Mar., 1927; astd. dist. judge, S'pore, in addn., Aug., 1927; cl. III, Oct., 1929; 2nd mag., S'pore, Sept., 1933; offr. cl. II., Oct., 1934.

GRIER, Sir Selwyn MacGregor, Kt. Bach. (1934), C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1878; ed. at Marlboro' Coll. (classical scholar) and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (classical scholar); 2nd class tripos, 1900; 1st class French and English special, 1901; B.A. 1900; called to the bar, Nov., 1910; astd. rea., Northern Nigeria, 1906; passed in Hausa, 1907; 3rd class rea., 1st Oct., 1908; in charge of Zaria prov., April, 1910; and from May to Nov., 1911; sec., native affrs., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1921; ag. gov. for various periods 1929-1934; gov. and commodr.-in-ch., Windward Is., 1935.

GRIERSON, Charles Henry Alexander.—B. 1889; ed. privately and St. Paul's Schl.; served local forces and 2nd Batt. Loyal N. Lancs. Regt. in E. African campaign, Aug. 1914 to Jan., 1920; Turkana expdn., 1915; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1916; adminstr. offr., 1st grade, Apr., 1921; dep. prov. commr., Jan., 1926; prov. commr., Feb., 1930.
GRIERSON, John Darbyshire, M.B., Ch.B., U. Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin., F.R.C.S., Eng.—B. 1898; med. ser., B. Guiana, 1927; res. surg. and surg. spec., pub. hosp., 1931.

GRIST, DONALD HONEY, Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agric.—B. 1891; ed. Huntingdon Grammar Schl., Camb. County Schl. and Camb. Univ. Sch. of Agr.; agric. instr., agric. dept., F.M.S., June, 1914; on duty in Ceylon, Feb., 1921; ag. agric. instr., Sultan Idris Coll., Tanjong Malim, in addn., Dec., 1922; agric. economist, 1924; dep. regisr.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., June, 1931; agric. economist and dir., S.S. and F.M.S., Sept., 1931.

GRIST, MAJOR H. G., R. of O., R. Fusiliers.—B. 1881; ed. Aldenham Schl., Herts.; 2nd lieut., 7th bn., R. Fusiliers, 1899; lieut., 1900; capt., 1903; R. of O., 1907; S. African War, Queen's med.; capt., 14th (S.) bn., Northumberland Fusiliers, Nov., 1914; B.E.F., France, 1915-19; major, Oct., 1915; ag. lieut.-col., July-Oct., 1916; staff capt., Abbeville area, 1918; ment. in despatches; ch. of pol. and offr. commdgs. local forces, St. Vincent, Jan., 1924.
GROBLER, HON. P. G. W., M.P.—Rep. Rustenburg elec. divn. in Union of S. Africa parlmt. since 1915; min. of lands, 30th June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min. for native affrs., Mar., 1933.

GROOM, HON. SIR LITTLETON ERNEST, K.C.M.G. (1924), K.C., M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to 1st hse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comm., on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., July, 1905; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; min. of external affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910; min. of trade and customs, June, 1913-Sept., 1914; hon. min., Feb., 1917; vice-pres., exec. coun., Nov., 1917 to Mar., 1918; min. for works and railways, Mar., 1918; ag. atty.-gen. from Apr., 1918; atty.-gen., Dec., 1921; atty.-gen., Bruce ministry, 1923; speaker, house of reps., Jan., 1926-29; re-el., M.H.R., Dec., 1931.

GROSSKOPF, JOHANNES FRIEDRICH WILHELM.—B. 1885; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; M.A. (Stellenb.), 1906; studied Univ. of Berlin, Vienna, Leiden, Zurich; Jur. Dr. (Leiden), 1913; prof., Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920-34; mem., Carnegie poor-white investgn., 1929-30; mem., co-opern. and agr. credit comm., 1933; ch., div. of economics and markets, dept. of agr. and forestry, Pretoria, 1935.

GROSSMITH, C. A.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., E. Lancs. Regt., 1916; lieut., 1918; served Mesopotamia and N. Persia, 1917-19; ment. in despatches; demob., 1919; attd., Br. mission in Czechoslovakia, 1919; temp. employed, clearing office, enemy debts, Berlin, 1921-23; enemy debts dept., London, 1924-26; cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934.

GUILLERMARD, SIR LAURENCE NUNNS, G.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.B. (1910), K.C.M.G. (1923), C.B. (1905).—B. 1862; ed. Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll. Camb.; entered Treasury, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Harcourt and Sir M. Hicks-Beach, 1892-1902; deputy chmn., board of inland rev., 1902; chmn., bd. of customs and excise, 1909; gov., S. Stllmte., 7th Feb., 1920; ret., 1927.

GULLETT, HON. SIR HENRY SOMER, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1878; served European War, 1915-18; dir., Australian War Museum and offic. historian, A.I.F. in Palestine; dir., Australian immigrn. bureau, Oct., 1920; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia from 1925; min. for trade and customs, 1928-29; dep. leader of opposition, Nov., 1929; min. for trade and customs, Lyons' ministry, 1932; Ottawa Conf., 1932; min. without portfolio for negotiating trade treaties, 1934.
GULLIVER, E. G.—Cust. asst., Kenya, July, 1916; collr., cust., Jan., 1926.

GULLOCH, WILLIAM SUTHERLAND.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1914; lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1916; lieut., Gold Coast Regt., 1918; capt., Gold Coast Regt., 1919; served France and Togoland; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1919; ag. commr., pol., Eastern prov., 1924-5; commr., pol., Cent. prov., 1926; ch. pol., Gibraltar, Nov., 1927.

GUNARATNA, WALTER DANDRIS, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Galie kach., Jan., 1928; attd., Kandy kach., May, 1928; ag. pol. mag., Dumbara, Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; extra office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1929; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; pol. mag., Puttalam, Oct., 1931; office asst., Badulla kach., Aug., 1932.

GUNASEKARA, SEPTIMUS THEODOSIUS, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.), L.M.S. (Ceylon).—B. 1881; 1st house surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Sept., 1904; med. offr., Kandy, Jan., 1907; ag. 3rd surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Oct., 1908; dir., ankylostomiasis campaign, Dikoya and Amella, Oct., 1917 to Mar., 1920; sany. offr., Apr., 1920; ag. sany. commr., Oct., 1922; asst. dir., sany. services, Oct., 1925; dep. dir. med. and sany. services, 1935.

GUNAWARDANA, DON CHARLES RAJAKARUNA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1924; attd., Matara kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, Jaffna kach., Apr., 1926; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1927; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Jan., 1930; do., Colombo, Jan., 1931; do., Batticaloa, Mar., 1931; office asst., Kandy kach., Dec., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1934.

GUNDY, JOHN ARNOLD, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Bees Schl. and Faraday House; engrn.-in-ch.'s office, G.P.O., Lond., 1920; asst. engrn., posts and tels., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; engrn., Mar., 1929; divn. engrn., Apr., 1933.
GUNN, HON. JOHN.—B. 1885; M.P. for Adelaide 1915; leader, parly. Labour Party, 1918; premier and treas., S. Australia, Apr., 1924-26; mem., migration and development comm. of Australia, 1926.

GURNEY, HENRY LOVELL GOLDSWORTHY.—B. 1898; asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, Feb., 1933; resigned, Aug., 1935; temp. attd., C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1935.

GURUSINHA, KANKANAN TANATURU SUMATHIRIPALA.—B. 1877; apptd. cls. V Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1925; office asst., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1925; addnl. asst. commr., stamps, May, 1930; attd., Galle kach., Oct., 1933.

GUTHRIE, HON. HUGH, P.C. (Can.), K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Coll. Inst. Guelph and Osogo Hall, Toronto; el. to H. of C., Canada at g. e., 1900; re-el. at g. e., 1904, 1908, 1911, 1917, 1921, 1925 and 1926; sol. gen., Canada, 1917; min. of militia and defence, 1920; min. of national defence in Meighen admntr., 1926; min. of just. and atty.-gen. in Bennett admntr., 1930; ch. commr., rly. comm., 1930.

GUTSCHE, Hon. Clemens, B.A., LL.B., C.B.E., V.D.—B. 1877; ed. Dale Coll., King Williams Town and S. African Coll., Cape Town (gold med., lit. and philos.); on active serv., Anglo-Boer War (2 meda., clasps); admitted to Cape bar, 1904; edr. Cape Town repts., 1908-10; and joint edr., Repts. of Cape Prov. Divn.; served in Great War, 1914-18; offr. in comm., Table Bay defences; Gen. Serv. and Victory meda., twice ment. in desps., C.B.E. and V.D.; resumed professorship, law, S.A. Coll., 1919; first judge, high ct., S. West Africa, 1919; judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., Grahamstown, 1930.

GYBON-MONYPENNY, Richard Montagu, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Jan., 1928; office ass't., Kurunegala kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1933; attd., rubber contrlr.'s office, July, 1934; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Aug., 1934.
HAARKER, Alec Ernest, F.L.S.—B. 1896; ed. Birkenhead Inst., Battersea Gram. Schl. and Royal Horticultural Coll. (dipl. hort.); dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; agril. lect., 1930.

HACKING, Rt. Hon. Douglas Hewitt, P.C. (1929), O.B.E., M.P.—B. 1884; ed. Giggleswick Schol. and Manchester Univ.; coman., E. Lancs. Regt., 1914; served two years France (ment. in desps.); M.P., Chorley Divn. of Lancs since 1918; parly. sec. to Sir James Craig (now Lord Craigavon) at miny. of pensions, 1920, and Adm'y., 1920-21; parly. pvt. sec. to Sir L. Worthington-Evans, Bart., M.P., S. of S. for War, 1921-22; vice-Chamberlain, royal household, 1922-24, and 1924-25; conserv. whip, 1922-25; parly. under sec. of state for home dept. and rep. of office of works in H. of C., 1925-27; sec. D.O.T., parly. sec., B. of T. and parly. under sec. of state for foreign affrs., 1927-29; parly. under sec., H.O., 1933-34; finan. sec., W.O., June, 1934; parly. under-sec. of state for Dom. affrs., 29th Nov., 1936.
HADDRILL, Cyril F.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th Apr., 1920.

HAIG, Rupert Alexander.—B. 1904; ed. Glenalmond and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cl. hon. schol., jurisprudence), 1925; called to bar, 1927; asst. dist. comanr., Nyassaland Prot., 1928; ag. asst. atty.-gen., various periods, 1929-34; ag. town mag., 1931; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1933; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1934.

HAILES, Everett Olive.—B. 1876; entd. N.Z. pub. trust office, 1894; offr. in charge, wills branch, 1914; inspr., 1919; local dep. pub. trustee, 1920; asst. pub. trustee, 1922; supt., N.Z. state advances office, 1931; pub. trustee, 1934.
HALFYARD, Hon. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Fogo, Newfoundland, 1913; min. of agric. and mines, 1917; M.E.C., 1917; col. sec., 1918.

HALIFAX, 3rd Viscount (cr. 1860), Hon. Edward Frederick Lindley Wood.—B. 1881; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls, Oxford (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragoons (M.P.), Ripon divn., Yorks, 1910-25; under sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educn., Oct., 1922; created 1st baron Irwin, 1925; viceroy of India, 1926; pres. bd. of educn., 1931.
HALL, Guthrie.—B. 1897; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1915; lieut., 1917; served with Waziristan Field Force, 1919; asst. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comanr., 1925.

HALL, J. Hathorn, C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931) D.S.O., M.C., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgium).—B.1894; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (1913-14); B.A.; on mily. serv., France and Belgium, Dec., 1915 to Dec., 1918; Egyptian civ. serv., miny. of finance, Sept., 1919 to Feb., 1921; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 17th Feb., 1921; ag. prin., 11th July, 1923; prin., 11th July, 1927; on various occasions accredited rep. to Permit. Mandates Comanr., 19.6-32 and mem., Br. delegn. to coun. of L. of N., 1929-32; seconded, F.O., Oct., 1932 to June, 1933; ch. sec., Palestine, June, 1933.

HALL, Julian Dudley, B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, July, 1913; superny. offr., cls. V; asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1914; superny. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1920; cls. III., Nov., 1922; pvt. sec. to gov. and high comanr., Nov. 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1927; adviser, land office, Kedah, 1930; offr., cls. IB., Apr., 1930; see to high comanr., Aug., 1930: Bradvr., Kedah, Mar., 1932.

HALL, Kenneth Lambert.—B. 1887; ed. Brasenose Coll., Oxford; asst. req., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. 2nd asst. sec., N. P., 1919; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1924; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1925; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1925; ag. dep. ch. sec. in 1927 and 1928; ch. sec., Nyassaland Prot., 1930; ag. gov., May-Nov., 1932, June-Aug., 1933 and Mar.-Sept., 1934.

HALL, Percy, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1885; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1913; do., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1916; prov. engrnr., 1921; seconded as res. engrnr., Achimota Coll. constrn., 1925-27; senr. pub. health engrnr., 1927; asst. D.P.W., 1928; dep. D.P.W., 1932.
HALL, Robert.—B. 1880; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1912.
HALL, Robert De Zouché.—B. 1904; ed. Willaston Schol., Liverpool Univ. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., June, 1932.

HALL, Roger Evans.—B. 1883; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; 3rd cl. hons. mods.; 2nd cl. hons. final jurisprudence; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1908; asst. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, 1910; crown counsel, 1914; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1914; senr. crown counsel, 1915; ag. solr. gen., 1916, 1917 and 1918; circuit judge, Ashanti (temporary appt.), 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1919; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920; puisne judge, 1921; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1925-31; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 1931; ag. gov., Feb. Mar. and June-Aug., 1934; ch. just., Uganda, 1935.

HALL, Thomas Donald Horn, LL.B. (N. Z.).—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schol., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; N. Z. dept. of agric., 1901; asst. law draftsman, 1921; clk. of house of reps., 1930.

HALLIDAY, L. G.—B. 1883; ed. Holt Schol. and Liverpool Univ.; mily. serv., Mesopotamia, 1916-1920; ch. engrnr., "Lord Milner," Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; in charge, reconditioning "Liamba," Dec., 1925; ch. engrnr., "Liamba," 1927.

HALLIER, Capt. Frank Collings, M.C., Reg. Army, R of O.—B. 1888; U.D.F. and Cape Corps, 1913-18; S. African Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; G.E.A., 1916-18; seconded as asst. pol. offr., civ. admin., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918; admin. offr., 1st gde., Feb., 1922; dep. prov. oomanr., Jan., 1933; prov. oomanr., Nov., 1935.
HALLIFAX, JOHN GORDON, C.S.L.L.—B. 1883; clk., law dept. (Cape), 1903; clk., pub. serv. comm., 1913; ch. clk., ditto, 1927; pub. serv. inspr., 1930; sec., pub. serv. comm., Oct., 1935.

HALLINAN, MAJOR THOMAS JOHN, C.B.E (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Dublin Univ.).—B. 1886; ed. Archbishop Tenison's Schol. and Charing Cross Hosp. (exhbr. and Pereira prize); oommd., R.A.M.C., 1910-25; served, B.E.F., Egypt, 1914-16; Mesopotamia, 1916-20; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Amarah, 1918; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Basrah, 1918-20; thricement in desps.; seconded, govt. of Iraq, health offr., 1920; inspr.-gen., civ. and health services, 1922; ret., R. of O., 1929; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1933; M.L.C., 1934.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1851; ed. at public schls.; arrived in New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, New Zealand, 1890-1908; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier, col. treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1906; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolios of pub. wks. and rlys. in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; high comsrr. in Lond., 1908-1912; M.L.C. since 1913.

HALLOCK, ANANDA RAJAH.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1927; attd. Puttalam kach., Dec., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., ditto, Oct., 1929; ditto., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1931; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1932; office asst., Mannar and Mullaitivu kach., Oct., 1934.

HALLOWES, BRAZON HENRY COLLIS, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1884; ed. Corrig Sch., Kingstown and Trinity Coll., Dublin; survr., valuation and bindry. survey of Ireland, 1910; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; capt., R.F.A., 1914; 1st cls. survr., 1921.

HAM, GORDON LUPTON.—B. 1885; ed. King Edward's Schol. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; lent to Kelantan, Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S.S., Jan., 1918; clk. of couns., Sept., 1919; 1st ast. col. sec., S.S. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ast. offl. assignee, Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high comsrr., Feb., 1921; ag. asst. adviser to govt. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. 1st ast. sec. B. S. S., Apr., 1922; ag. dep. treas. (B.), S.S., Dee., 1924; ag. dep. treas. (A.), Mar., 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1925; sec. to res., Perak, Nov., 1925; cls. IB., Jan., 1927; pres., munic. comsrr., Penang, May, 1927; Br. advr., Trengganu, Mar., 1931; offr., cls. I.A., dep. pres., munic. comsrr., S'pore, Nov., 1932; under sec., S.S., June, 1933; ag. col. sec., S.S., Dec., 1933.

HAM, HENRY SOMERSET MCKENZIE.—B. 1878; clk., recr. of rev., O.F.S., 1900; clk., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1901; ch. clk. and examr., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1904; ast. regisr., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1912; ditto, Pretoria, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, Oct., 1928; regisr., deeds, Pretoria, 1933; do., Cape Town, Feb., 1934.

HAVENGA, HON. NICOLAAS CHRISTIAAN.—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. counr. for Fauresmith, O.F.S. and mem., exec. comttee for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Fauresmith from 1915; min. of finance, from June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932; del., World Economic Confce., London, 1933.
HAWES, RICHARD BRUNEL, M.B., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1893; med. offr., Kedah, June, 1923; prof. of medicine, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schls., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd cls. clk., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; asst. dep. min., Apr., 1920.
HAWKER, CHARLES ALLAN SEYMOUR.—Served European War 1914-17 (wounded); M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; min. for markets and repatriation, Lyons' ministry, 1931-2; resigned, Oct., 1932.

HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hona., mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd astt. collr., treas., June, 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., Nov., 1927; astt. head, sanry. dept., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., South, May, 1932; 3rd astt. col. sec., May, 1933; sec., economic comnrr., July, 1934.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; on milly. serv., 1914-20 (capt.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; astt. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; sec., Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Mar., 1928; cls. III, May, 1928; assessor, wild life comnrr., Aug., 1930; astt. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933; astt., co-op. socs. dept., Sept., 1934.

HAY, ALISTAIR WILLIAM.—B. 1899; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. V, ag. inspr. Chinese schls., Nov., 1924; passed cadet, June, 1925; 2nd astt. dir., educn. (Chinese), Sept., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1928; 3rd inspr., S'pore, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, astt. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1933.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1928), M.V.O.—Undersec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, MORTIMER CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); M.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930; cls. II, Nov., 1930; Br. advr., Perlis, Dec., 1930; ag. legal advr., Johore, Mar., 1933.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, KT. BACH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbit., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puian judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just.,
FRENADA, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; classed in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (gold medalist); admitted solr., sup. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; regiar., high ct., Uganda, Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admstr.-gen., rev. authority, regiar. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927; offi. recr. and regiar. trade marks and patents, Hong Kong, Apr., 1934.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford Modern Sch.; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B.G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928; hon. A.D.C. to gov., Feb., 1929; sec., colonization comittee., Jan., 1929 to Mar., 1930; ag. prin. clk., C.S.O., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; ast. comsrr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1931.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL, WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engrns. (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; ast. dist. comsrr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1926; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., 1933.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1880; ed. at Collegiate Sch., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis., for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis., during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, GARY, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class tripso, 1900, law tripso, 1906); dist. comsrr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; regiar., sup. ct., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Coun. of legal educn., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from May, 1916; sec. to gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1919-21; employed in C.O. since 1924; temp. ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. ast. land offr. in addnl., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1928; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treasry. solr. in addnl., 1926-30; ag. dep. regiar. in addnl., Apr., 1928 to Feb., 1929; pol. mag. in addnl., July to Sept., 1929; regiar., sup. ct., offi. admstr. and regiar., companies, in 1929 and 1932-36; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1932; crown solr., 1935.

HEAD, ERNEST WILFRED.—B. 1876; loco. foreman Ceylon rly., Dec., 1902; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1905; ast. loco., carriage and wagon supt., Nov., 1912; supt., do., Dec., 1915; dep. gen. man., Oct., 1924; gen. man., Aug., 1932.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia, 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929; staff offr., 18th Dec., 1934.

HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ast. sec., Somaliland Prot., 1919; attd. for duty to C.O., 1926; resumed subst. appt., Somaliland, 1926; ast. sec., Tanyanika Territory, 1929; col. sec. and regiar.-gen., Grenada, 1935.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; ast. dist. comsrr., Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; senr. mag., Nov., 1924; ag. puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929; main., H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930; chan. of diocese, 1932; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. ch. just., 1935.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his gov't., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint comsrr. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transfd., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; appd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.
HEATH, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1890; ast. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. govt. printer, 1920, 1921, 1923 and 1932.
HEATH, HENRY WYLDE EDWARDS.—B. 1912; ed. Close Coll., Cheltenham; sub-inspr., pol., Leeward Is., Sept., 1931; ast. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Jan., 1935.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A" 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; pres., defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-chg. of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut., defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. comsrr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918; med. offr., Antigua, 1934.

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT.—B.1896; ed. privately and Oxford Univ.; lieut., 1/1st Kent Regt. and R.F.C., 1915; H.M. Borstal instr., 1924; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927; comsrr., prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

HERDEN, GEORGE BRENTNALL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Sch.; tal. constr. ast. engr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909; supt.g.t.l.engr., Jan., 1914; ast. engr.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; dir., posts and tels. to C.E.F., 1915; engr.-in-chg., 1921; P.M.G., 1928.
HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prir

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.R.S.N.Z.—B. 1880; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geol. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, RICHARD McNEIL, M.I.C.E., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Allen Glen's Inst. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; senr. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engrnr., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Hong Kong, Jan., 1928; J.P., 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; D.P.W., 1932.
HENDERSON, W. W., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Vic.), capt., R.A.V.C. (R. of O.).—Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; M.C. and Bar; seconded to Gambia as prin., new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929.

HENDRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in desp., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.C., 1926; 3rd cls., Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1928; M.E.C., 1932.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, July, 1888; asst. to conservator, Mar., 1890; dist. forester, offr., King William's Town, June, 1893; Stutterheim, Aug., 1898; asst. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, Oct., 1905; asst. conserv., W. Conservancy, July, 1907; prin. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturship, Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; asst. conserv., forests, Midland Conservancy, Feb., 1909; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, Apr., 1912; conserv., forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; tranfil., Rhodesian forest serv., Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 29th May, 1930.

HENNIKER HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1928).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regir. gen.'s off., Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjt., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. clk., native dept., and ag. asst. native commr., July, 1906; apptd. asst. native commr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offr. recvr. in bankrupt, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining hd., Apr., 1910; commr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec. Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigts. and poor law commr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comm'd., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1926; col. sec., Cyprus, Sept., 1929; ag. gov., for various periods, 1929-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Falkland Is. and Dependencies, Jan., 1935.

HENRY, GEORGE HEWITT, L.M., L.C.H., L.A.O. (Dub. Univ.).—B. 1906; med. offr., Nigeria, Aug., 1929; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Lai Chi Kok prison, Kowloon mortuary and astt. M.O., Kowloon hosp., 1935.

HENRY, ROBERT NOEL, M.A., higher dipl. edcu., T.C.D.—B. 1898; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on staff of St. Columba's Coll., Dublin, 1924-28; war serv., 1916-19; astt. dir., edcu., Cyprus, 1930; ag. dir., edcu., for various periods, 1929-35.

HENWOOD, SYDNEY WILLIAM.—B. 1898; on war serv., 1917-18 (France); serv., in India, 1919-21; R.M. Schol. of Music, 1921-22; bandmr., The Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1923; served in India, 1923-31; Sudan, 1931-32; bandmr., Br. Guiana Militia Band, May, 1935; lieut., May, 1935.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1887; ed. Latymer, Liverpool Inst. High Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schls., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schmstr., 1919; ag. astt. dir., S. Provs., 1927 and 1928; ag. dep. dir., S. Provs., 1929; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1929; ag. astt. dir., edcu., S. Provs., 1934.

HERDMAN, SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, KT. BACH. (1929).—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915-18; judge of N.Z. sup. ct., 1918-35.

HEROLD, THOMAS HARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; astt. master, June, 1902; comsnr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn.), 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chrmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924-30; mem., land bank bd., 1930.

HERON, GEORGE, A.M.I.N.A., A.M.I.Mech. E.—B. 1882; temp. surv. ships, Singapore, Nov., 1915; astt. surv., Jan., 1918; ag. surv. ships, May, 1921; survr., ships, Singapore, Jan., 1922 and Aug., 1924; sr. survr., ships, Dec., 1923; ag. survr.-gen., ships, S.S. Apr., 1927 and Sept., 1931; dep. survr.-gen., ships, May, 1928; survr.-gen., ships, S.S., Nov., 1932.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1905; ed. Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (chem.) (hons.), B.A. (forestry); astt. conservator, forests, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comdl. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., nat. party govt., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; do., coalition govt., Mar., 1933.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1890; astt. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1914; ag. asm. astt. auditor, Jan. to Mar., 1919; ag. astt. col. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Col. Audit Dept., July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, Sept., 1926; audr. N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEWSON, ROBERT WARBENE DALE.—B. 1880; ed. Repton Schol., Edinburgh Univ. and R. Colls., Edinburgh; L.R.C.P. & S. Edinburgh, L.R.F.P. & S. Glasgow; served, S. African war, Queen's med.; served, European war, capt., R.A.M.C.; med. supt., Jamaica lunatic asylum, Nov., 1926; joint author with Capt. R. M. Stewart, "Case of Acute Febrile Polynearitis," B.M.J., 1919.

HEYMANS, Dr. G. M. A.—B. 1870; M.D. (Brussels) 1895; personal physician to late Pres. Kruger, 1899-1904; consul for Belgium, Transvaal, 1908-29; vice-consul for Spain, Transvaal, 1907-29; shmn., med. bd., Pretoria, 1912-29; S. African trade commr., Milan, 1929; 1st sec., S. African legn., Rome, 1929-34; chargé d'affaires, Rome, 1933-34; min. plenip., Rome, Sept., 1934.

HEYWOOD, GRAHAM SCUDAMORE PERCIVAL.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; B.A. and B.Sc., 1926; meteorological research astt., war dept., Leafld, 1928; professional astt., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1932.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V, Mar., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July 1926; offr. cls. IV, Mar., 1928; astt. emigrn. comsnr. for Malaya; astt. dir., co-opern., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; astt. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1931; offr. cls. III, Mar., 1933; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., offr. assignee and ag. regisr., companies, Apr., 1934.

HIBBERT, JOHN GEOFFREY, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. schl. mod. languages (French), 1912; prte., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) Divn.; ment. in desps.; attd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr. clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; astt. claims clk., Jan., 1922; attd., Br. govt. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; temp. admvte. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., Feb., 1930; astt. pvtc. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols., Oct., 1930; astt. prin., 1st Oct., 1934; prte. sec. to parl. under sec. of state for cols. (Lord Plymouth), 23rd Sept., 1935.

HICKS, ARTHUR HAXWELL.—B. 1894; ed. Cardiff High Schl.; entd. C.A. office after compet. exam., 1913; mil. serv., 1914-19; seconded to G. Coast as sec., harbr. dept., 1925-28; astt. head, pay dept., Feb., 1930; appts. dept., Oct., 1930; finance dept., 1932; sec. to colonial savings banks comtee., 1934; dep. head, stores dept., 1936.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1917; 1st grade admvte. offr., Apr., 1921; seconded as dep. lab. comsnr., 1930-31; dep. prov. comsnr., Sept., 1934; ag. prov. comanr., Apr.-Aug., 1935.

HIDE, EDGAR WALTER—B. 1893; ed. Muncl. Secndy. Schl., Eastbourne; P.O., Gr. Britain, 1910; R.E. Sp. Res., France, 1914-19; supt., grade II, P.O., Penang, Nov., 1919; do., S'por- Nov., 1920; do., Malacca, Dec., 1924; ag. contrlr., P. & T., Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1927; do., N. Sembilan and Malacca, July, 1930; do., Perak and Dinding, Feb., 1932; contrlr., Nov., 1932.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., May, 1905; ast. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HILDYARD, LT.-GEN. REGINALD HENRY THOROTON, C.B. (1928), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; entd. army, 1896; capt., 1904; maj., 1915; col., 1919; maj.-gen., 1929; lt.-gen., 1934; employed with S.A. constab., 1900-3; A.D.C. to lt.-gen., S. Africa, 1904-5; to G.O.C., Africa, 1905-8; gen. staff offr., 3rd grade, W.O., 1911-13; brig.-maj., S. commd., 1913-14; P.S.C.; served S. Africa, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. 4 clasps, King's med. 2 clasps); European War, 1914-18 (desps. seven times. D.S.O., bt.-lt.-col., C.M.G., bt.-col.); brig. comdr., 2nd Rhine Brig., 1928-29; ocmdr., 43rd (Wesser) divn., T.A., 1930-34; gov. and ocmdr.-in-ch., Bermuda, Apr., 1936.

HILL, ALEXANDER GEORGE GLENDO, B.A., Agr.B., A.R.C.Sc.I.—B. 1900; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; botanist, Nigeria, 1926; sen. geneticist, sugarcanes research stn., Mauritius, 1929; sent to Java to study cane breeding methods, 1930; ag. dir., agr., Mauritius, Oct., 1930 to Mar., 1932.

HILL, GILBERT.—B. 1889; war serv., 1914-19; offr. in ch., wireless stations, Scapa Flow, 1919-20; wireless engrnr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. astt. dir., Somaliland, Oct., 1926; ag. dir., posts and tels. in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; 8th clk., admtr.-gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1896; 5th cls. clk., police dep., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr., pol., Dec., 1900; dist. inspr., pol., Apr., 1903; ag. adjut. and musketry, July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of pol., Nov., 1909 to Apr., 1910 and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qtrmtr. from Sept., 1911; pay and qtrmtr., Jan., 1912; commd. paymr. and staff offr. for admtrv. duties with local forces, 1914-18; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Apr., 1923; ag. dir., co-op. socs., July to Dec., 1923; ch. clk., audit dept., Jan., 1925; ag. audr. on various occasions, 1926-27.

HILL, HON. LIONEL LAUGHTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—B. 1881; comnr. of pub. wks., min. of educn. and min. of industry, S. Australia, 1924-26; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1926-27 and since Apr., 1930.

HILL, REGINALD DYKERS RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. clk. and 2nd A.C.S. on various occasions, 1928-29; mag., 1933.
HILL, STANLEY OLIVER, L.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I., A.R.San.I.—B. 1893; ed. Royal Grammar Schl., Guildford and Liverpool Univ.; mily. serv., R.F.A., 1915-19; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.
