HILLARY, DENIS.—B. 1886; pol. probr., S.S., Dec., 1908; inspr., pol., Malacca, Mar., 1912; ch. inspr., pol., K. Star, Kedah, July, 1915; ag. astt. comsnr., pol., N. Kedah, Sept., 1919; do. S. Patani, Apr., 1926; do. S. Kedah, Dec., 1927; astt. supt., pol., S'pore, July, 1928; J.P., S'pore, Oct., 1928; ag. comsnr., pol., Kelantan, Sept., 1934.

HILLIER, SIDNEY CHARLES.—B. 1888; London Schl. of Economics (Brunel med.); G.W.R., 1902; mily. serv., 1916-1921; ment. in desps. (twice); G.W.R., 1921; astt. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., Tanganyika rly., on various occasions, 1925-29; ag. ch. acct., 1928; dep. ch. acct., July, 1930; ch. acct., 1930.

HILTON, JOHN ROBERT, A.R.I.B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Marlborough Coll. (junr. and senr. scholarships and leaving exhib.) and Corpus Christ Coll., Oxford (maths. schol.); 1st cl. hons. in schl. of philosophy, politics and economics; John Locke scholarship in philosophy, Oxford; dipl. of Bartlett Schl. of architecture, London Univ.; dir., antiquities, Cyprus, Oct., 1934.

HIMSWORTH, ERIC.—B. 1905; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Feb., 1929; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; dep. regir., Feb., 1931; addt. treasy., 1932; astt. head sany. dept., 1932 and 1935.

HINCKS, MAURICE THORP.—B. 1885; ed. Stonegate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; comsnr. for oaths, 1914; on mily. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regir., Gold Coast, on various occasions; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922; ch. regir., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927; offi. admtr. and pub. trustee in addn., 1932-33; regir., high ct., Zanzibar, June, 1934.

HINGLEY, ANTHONY CAPPER MOORE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1908; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1931; addt., finan. sec.'s office, Jan., 1932; do., Kandy kach., Aug., 1932; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, June, 1933; pvt. sec. to gov., Apr., 1934.

HIPWELL, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1888; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, July, 1914; lieut., R.E., Oct., 1918; astt. engrnr., F.M.S., July, 1919; Johore, Oct., 1919-Feb., 1924; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1928; ag. sr. exec. engrnr., S'pore, Nov., 1932, and July, 1934.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1919; addt., treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDN.—B. 1880; astt. engrnr., water wks., Trinidad, Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, Sept., 1906; divisional engrnr., Apr., 1925.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1906; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; astt. regir., prize ct., 1914; astt. regir. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. astt. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1929; law advr., Pretoria, 1936.
HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1884; gov't schlmr., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educn. and headmr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928.

HOARE, HENRY SAMUEL MALORTE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929; pte. sec. to gov't., Feb., 1931; landing survr., cust., Nov., 1932; ag. dep. collr., cust., July-Sept., 1933.

HOARE, COL. FRANCIS RICHARD GURNEY, C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1879; ed. Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 9th Middlesex Regt. and 4th Suffolk Regt. (Volunteers), 1894-1900; served S.A. War, 1899-1900; Queen's Med. and two clasps; ent. Transvaal Horse Arty., 1912; seconded to Army Ordnance Dept. as temp. ord. offr., 4th cls. with rank of capt., 1914; do., 3rd cls. with rank of maj., A.O.D., 1915; depot commdr., 1915; temp. lt.-col., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., air equipment to R.A.F. training brig., Canada and U.S.A.; maj. (temp. lt.-col.) (tech.), R.A.F., 1918; inspgr. ord. offr. (lt.-col., Active Citizen Force), Union of S. Africa, 1919; lt.-col., staff offr., 1st grade, Permt. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; staff offr., air equipment, in addn. to duties of inspgr. ord. offr., 1920; lt.-col., S.A. Ordnance Corpsa, 1933; dir., tech. services, U.D.F., May, 1933; col., S.A. Staff Corps, Sept., 1933.

HOBBS, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; R.I.C., 1906-13; inspr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; supvr. cust., F.M.S. (Kuala Lumpur), Sept., 1921; ch. supt., prev. br., Oct., 1923; ag. dep. comsnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Oct., 1923-May, 1924 and in May, 1927; dep. comsnr., trade and cust. (excise), F.M.S., Sept., 1927; ditto, prev. br., June, 1930; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., Perak, Jan., 1931.

HOBDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd. col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo kach., April, 1913; Trincomalee kach., Nov., 1913; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs, May, 1919; 4th asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1925; ditto, Kegalle, Feb., 1927; ditto, N'Ellya, Sept., 1928; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1932; ag. contr., lab., July, 1932.
HOBSON, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1905; ed. Sherborne; admitted solr., 1928; prin. offr. and 2nd dep. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, 1935.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1865; apptd., after compet. exam. elk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the comsnr. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf'd. to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. regir., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-elk., first grade, 1911; supt., printing dept., Dec., 1924.

HODGE, HENRY PANNELL, CAPT., R.A.M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), L.M.S.S.A. (London), Grad., L.S.T.M.—B. 1887; med. offr., S.S., May, 1923; med. offr., S'pore, June, 1923; do., K. Lumpur, July, 1923; do., Coast Selangor, Aug., 1923; do., Pahang, May, 1925; do., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1928; med. offr., Coast, Selangor, Aug., 1933.
HODGE, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1911; ed. Taunton Schl.; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1931; asst. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1935.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926; senr. dist. comsnr., 1935.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS.—B. 1884; Impl. civ. ser., acnt. gen.'s dept., G.P.O., London, 1901; ch. acnt.'s office, G.P.O. O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924; ag. treas., in 1926, 1927, 1928, 1931 1933; treas., Jamaica, 1934.

HODGES, WILLIAM CARNLEY, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed. King's Schl., Grantham; lieut., 54th (E.A.) Divul. Cyclist Co., July, 1917; resigned coman., Dec., 1921; ch. acnt., govt. monops., S'pore, Dec., 1921; ag. asst. supt., govt. monops., Penang, Oct., 1928.

HODGKINS, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. N.Z. pub. schls. and Otago High Sch.; entd. N.Z. reglar-gen.'s dept., 1901; registr., births, etc., 1907; dep. ch. electoral off., 1913; ch. electoral off., 1928; also dep. registr.-gen., 1932.

HODGSON, ANTHONY GEORGE OWEN.—B. 1890; ed. Rugby; asst. res., Nyassaland, 1913; 2nd lieut., 1st London Bde., R.G.A. (T.F.), 1912; lieut., 1914; capt., 1916; R. of O., 1922; attd., 1st Bn., K.A.R., N.F.F., G.R.A. campaign, 1915-17; ag. A-de-C to govr., 1919; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1920-22; 1st gde. admin. offr., Nyassaland, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1930-31 and in 1933; ag. ch. sec., June to Oct., 1933; prov. comsnr., May, 1934.

HODGSON, DUDLEY HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Forestry.—B. 1899; asst. conserv. forests, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; conserv. forests, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. dir., forestry, S.S. and F.M.S., in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educn., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educn. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instn., W.O. schl. of educn., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.

HODSON, SIR ARNOLD WIENHOLT, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1922).—Ed., Italy, Mulgrave, Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damaras war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. asst. comsnr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. comsnr. and 1st cls. mag., Somalland, 1912; H.B.M. consul for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, Dec., 1926; ditto Sierra Leone, May, 1931; ditto, Gold Coast, 1934; auth. "Trekking the Great Thirst," "Seven Years." Southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, BERNARD.—B. 1876; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1894; clk., mag.'s office, Dundee, 1894; clk., min., native affrs., 1895; capt. and mil'y. mag., Volksrust, Transvaal, 1900; prin. clk., Durban, 1905; addnl. mag., Durban, 1910; mag., Nongoma, Zululand, 1915; mag., Mpfanoa, 1917; mag., New Hanover, 1921; mag., Pinetown, 1921; mag., Dundee, 1926; ch. mag., Maritzburg, 1929.

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; asst. settlmt. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordece, June, 1918; on mil. duty, 1917; addnl. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1919; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. asst. dir., educn. Mar., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926; do., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1931; do., Prov. of Uva, Nov., 1931; govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Oct., 1932.

HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRICK, M.A., D.Sc., M.P.—B. 1894; ed. S.A. Coll. High Schi., S.A. Coll., Cape Town, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; prof. of classics, Univ. Coll., Johannesburg, 1917; prin., univ. of Witwatersrand, 1919; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1924; M.P., Johannesburg (North), 1929; min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1933.

HOGAN, HON. EDMOND JOHN, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian parly. lab party, Apr., 1926; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and from Dec., 1929 to May, 1932.

HOGAN, GERALD (GEORGE)—B. 1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1915; served Australian Field Arty., Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; ment. in desps.; stip. sp. mag., N. Territory, Australia, 1919; dep. judge, sup. ct., N. Territory, 1920; crown law offr., mandated Territory of New Guinea, 1922; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1933.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, Aug., 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1933.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M. Inst. C.E., Whitworth Schi.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schi. and Imp. Coll. of Science; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; asst. engrnr., 1919; dep. head, engnrng. inspn. dept., 1920; ch. insp. engrnr. and head, engnrng. inspn. dept., 1934.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mil'y. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMOR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Peterel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov't., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harmbr., Hong Kong, 1924; comdr., 1927; offl. mem., leg. coun., 1929; dir., air services, 1929; head, Hong Kong delegn. to Shipping Confec., Simla, 1931.

HOLFORD, GEORGE TILSTONE, A.I.M.M.—B. 1835; ed. Brighton Coll. and Camborne Schi. of Mines; inspr., mines, F.M.S., Sept., 1911; 2nd lieut., R.H.A. & R.F.A. (S.R.), June, 1915; France, 1915-19; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1920; warden, mines, Pahang, June, 1930; do., Selangor, Mar., 1933; do., Perak, 1934.

HOLGATE, MAXWELL RUSSELL, M.A.(N.Z.)—B. 1896; asst. mast., Raffles Schi., July, 1920; sr. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Perak, Nov., 1922; ag. prin., do., Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1930; asst. inspr., schls., Singapore and Labuan, Nov., 1930; headmast., King Edward VII Schi., Taiping, Dec., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., Malacca, Nov., 1934.

HOLLAND, FRANCIS GEORGE LEOPOLD.—B. 1884; headmr., govt. educn. scheme, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1920; dep. commr. (temp. and provnl.), 1929; supt., educn., 1931; ag. dist. offr. at various times, 1928-33.
HOLLAND, JOHN JAMES, B.Sc., B.E., M.Inst.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy.E.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Galway; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, Aug., 1928; dist. engrnr., P.W.D. Cyprus, Mar., 1930.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Missahohe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee. regnr. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-29; admstr.-gen. and offl. recr., Uganda, 1930.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, M.B.E. (1935), B.A. (Lond.), F.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Schi., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmr., teacher's training schl., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; author "Milango ya History" (Vols. I, II and III); "Jiogiria ya Unguja na Pemba," "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," "Njia za Kufundisha," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schi. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab.), 2nd cls. hist., tripes; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1910; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., Jan., 1913; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1915; senr. dist. comsnr., Jan., 1931; ag. prov. comsnr., cent. prov., 1922, 1928, 1929 and 1930.

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1910).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstr., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1904-1; and Nandi expdn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affrs. and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. govr., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admstr., German E. Africa (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; g. gov., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. rea., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; 1st cls., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1929; g. gov. and commdr-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 6th Jan., 1930; assumed govt., 22nd Mar., 1930; ret., 1936; author of works on the Massai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., J.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; etc., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; lir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932 and World Econ. Confce., London, 1933; economicadvr., treasy., Jan., 1934.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Arty., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; B.A. (lit. hum.) July, 1921; M.A., June, 1930; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1928; asst. pte. sec. to S. of S. for dom. affs. and for cols. (Mr. L. S. Amery), Feb., 1928; accomp. Sir S. Wilson on tour to W. African cols., Aug. to Nov., 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 24th Jan., 1930; U.K. delegation to Monetary and Econ. Confce., London, 1933; Imp Defence Coll., 1934.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. pte. sec. to gov., Mar., 1927; office asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1929; pte. sec. to O.A.G., Apr. to Sept., 1930; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1931; dist. judge, Tangalle, Sept., 1932; asst. contr., establs., Apr., 1934; dep. regiar. co-op. socs., July, 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1934.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney). Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schl. of Mines (London) in Mining, Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schl. of Mines, 1901; mining engnr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HOLT, CAPT. VIVIAN.—B. 1896; the Middlesex Regt. (T.A.R.O.); joined Iraq civ. admin. as asst. polit. offr., Sulaimani, Dec., 1919; A.D.C. to high commnr., Iraq, Oct., 1922; pte. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1923; oriental sec. to high commnr., Aug., 1926.

HOLTUM, RICHARD ERIC, M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1895; ed. Bootham Schl., York and St. John's Coll., Camb.; asst. dir., gardens, S.S., Mar., 1922; ag. dir., Feb., -Oct., 1923 and in Feb., 1925 dir. May, 1925.

HONE, CAPT. HERBERT RALPH, M.C., K.C., LL.B. (Hons.).—B. 1896; ed. Vardean Schl., Brighton and London Univ.; enlisted, Inns of Court O.T.C., 1915; 2nd lieut., 18th Lond. Regt., July, 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Jan., 1918; attd. H.Q. 60th Divn., Sept., 1915; adjt. 1/18 Lond. Regt., Feb., 1916; served twice B.E.F., France 1916 and 1917-18; capt. R. of O. (T.A.), 1922; asst. treas., Uganda, Sept., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; went S. Eastern Circuit; offl. recr. in bankruptcy, and registr., H.B.M.'s high ct., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. res. mag., Zanzibar, Jan., 1926 to Feb., 1927; res. mag., Zanzibar, Mar., 1928; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; ag. solr.-gen., Mar. to Sept., 1930; ag. asst. legal advr., D.O. and C.O., Jan.-Aug., 1933; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Aug., 1933; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; K.C. (Gib.), 1934; commr., revised laws, 1935.

HONEY, SIR DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedit. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admstr., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acctng. offr., Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admstr., Mar., 1907; res. commnr., Swaziland, Jan., 1917; gov., Seychelles, 1928; ret., 1933.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vetry. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLESS, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. honors., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; asst. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for col., 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. supt., schl., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; asst. dir., educn., 1927; tutor, Cent. Med. Schl., May, 1930.

HOOGENHOUT, P. IMKES.—B. 1884; ed. Stellenbosch and Cape Town, B.A. and prof. teachers' certifa., 1904 and 1906; headmnr., Jespe Educn. Inst., Johannesburg, 1908, and Hoogenthout High Schl., Bethal, 1910; inspr., educn., S.W. Transvaal, 1921; ch. examr. to Trans. educn. dept., 1927; sec. for interior, 1929.

HOPE, WILLIAM MANNFIELD SANDERSON.—B. 1881; asst. supt., refugee camps, O.F.S., 1901; supt., do., 1902; sec. and acct., repatriation dept., O.F.S., 1903; inspr. of accts., do. and govt. relief dept., 1903; examr. of accts., audit dept., 1904; 2nd cls. clk., do., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1909; senr. clk., dept. of contr. and audr.-gen., 1912; prin. clk., do., 1921; senr. inspr., do., 1930; ch. inspr. (expend.), do., 1933.

HOPE-JONES, ERNEST.—B. 1877; clk., col. secs. dept., Cape, 1892; dept. of agr., 1899; contrlr. and audr.-gen. office, 1905; acct., dept. of lab., Union of S. Africa, 1925; sec. pub. debt. commnr., 1932.
HOPKINS, FRANK ARTHUR.—B. 1886; home prison dept., 1910; Cyprus prison dept., 1919; supt., prisons, 1921; asst. supt., prisons, Hong-Kong, Jan., 1931; ag. supt., prisons in 1931 and 1933.
HOPKINS, ISAAC MORELAND, B.Sc. (Glasgow).—B. 1902; ed. Bellahouston Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, Jordan Hill Coll. for Teachers; prin., govt. training coll. for teachers, Trinidad, 1925.
HOPKINS, J.G.—Aast. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. ass't., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto, 1923; 3rd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926; sec., comrnt. of currency, 1928; 1st cls. olk., treasy., 1933.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1922). M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-01; W. African med. staff, 1904 and served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; recd. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for rept. on cerebro-spinal fever in N. Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for rept. on sleeping sickness in Volta River dist. of G. Coast, 1910; seconded for sp. serv. at C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the F.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comsn. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and san. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and San. Comtee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; consulting physician to C.O., 1928; mem., exec. comtee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, ARTHUR JOHN WARD, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. King Edward's High Schl., Birmingham Univ. (B.Sc. 1914) and Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ.; R.F.A., Sept. 1914 to Oct., 1919; agrl. chemist, Nyasaland, Sept., 1921; asst. dir., agrl. and agrl. chemist, Apr., 1931; ag. dir., agrl., June, 1933 to Jan., 1934.

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY ERTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. — B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agrl. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in despe.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schl. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services and vety. pathologist, 1930; ag. dir., vety. serv., Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; dir., vety. serv., Jan., 1933.
HORNE, EDWIN NORL.—B. 1902; appt. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 14th Apr., 1930.

HORNE, W. K.—B. 1883; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; war serv., 1915-19; ch. just., Tonga, 1925; compiler, revd. edn., "Laws of Tonga," 1928; ag. ch. just., Fiji and ag. ch. judicial comsnr., W. Pacific, Feb.-Sept., 1928; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1930; puisane judge, Kenya, 1933.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schl.; C.A., Jan., 1908; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engrg. contracts, 1920; tech. offr., "Discovery Comtee," 1923.
HORSFIELD, WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Denstone and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hon. mech. sci. trip., 1921; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921.

HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL, O.B.E. (1931). D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., desps.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922; polit. offr. with Anglo-Italian (Somaliland) bdyv. comsn., Aug., 1929-Sept., 1930; head, Anglo-Italian claims sub-comsn., Mar.-Sept., 1930; O.A.G., Somaliland for various periods, 1932-33.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schl., St. Helena; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwk., Accra tech. schl., Apr., 1920; ag. prin., Dec., 1920 to Mar., 1921; house mast., Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., Apr., 1926.

HORWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec., 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comsn. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newfld. centre of St. John Ambulance Assocn.; chrmn. of comtee. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfld. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comen. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSEGOOD, THOMAS WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1886; younger brother of Trinity House, master mariner; ed. St. Paul's Cathedral Choir Schl.; habmr. and naval agt., St. Lucia, 1918; ag. ch. of pol. on various occasions; asst. habmr., marine mag., etc., Hong Kong, 1927.

HOSKING, ETHELBERT BERNARD, O.B.E., (1932).—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Wadham Coll., Oxford (class schl.); asst. dist. comsnr. E.A.F., Dec., 1913; dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1925; ag. prin. asst. to commr., local govt., etc., 1932; ag. comsnr., mines, 1933; senr. dist. comsnr., 1935.

HOUGHTON, H. E., F.R.A.S.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high comsnr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transf'd. to Basutoland serv., Nov. 1923.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical asst., Basutoland, Oct., 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regiar., July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., July, 1919; dep. asst. comsnr., 1923; asst. comsnr., 1927; ag. govt. sec., Bech. Prot., Mar., 1932 to Apr., 1933.

HOWARD, HON. ERATUS EDWIN.—B. 1888; ed. Iroquois High Schl., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in maths. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist) 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept.,

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. ast. acctnt., army scots. leapt., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. ast. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. ast. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supermny. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation comm. treas., as ast. contr. of deptl. finances until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921; prin., 27th Aug., 1928; attended Imp. Defence Coll. course, 1933.

HOWARD, JOHN CURTOIS, K.C.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhbr.), hona., nat. sci.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1916; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; pres. dist. ot., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1925; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1927-30; temp. addl., legal advisory staff, C.O., Nov., 1931; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, Mar., 1933; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1936.

HOWARD-FLANDERS, WILLIAM EDGAR.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1927; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-18; called to bar, M.T., 1923; mem., south-eastern circ. and cent. crim. ct.; mag., St. Vincent, June, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1928; income tax commnr., 1928; mem. educn. bd., 1928; ag. col. sec., July, 1928; atty.-gen., Sept., 1928; ag. ch. just., May-Dec., 1928; admtd. govt., Feb., 1930; ast. regis., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1930; ag. regis., 1935.

HOWAT, CLARENCE HUGH, M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1908; ed. Edin. Acad. and Univ. of Edin.; medallist in med. and in clinical med.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1932; med. offr., Nyassaland Prot., 1933.

HOWE, HON. CLARENCE DECATUR, B.So.—B. 1886; ed. Public schls. and Massachusetts Inst. of Technology; prof., civ. engnr., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S., 1908; ch., engnr., bd. of grain commrs. for Canada, 1913; el. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1935; min. of rlys. & canals and min., marine in Mackenzie King cabinet, 1935.

HOWE, GERARD LEWIS.—B. 1899; ed. Armagh Royal Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; served, R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1916-17; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1917-20; ment. in desps., France, 1918; called to bar, Kings Inn, Ireland, 1923; called to bar, N. Ireland, 1924; practised, N. Ireland and I.F.S., 1923-30; res. mag., Kenya, June, 1930; arbitrator, Mombasa munio. valuation appls., Jan. to July, 1934; crown coun., Gold Coast, Aug., 1934.

HOWE, JOHN WALLACE, Dp. Ag., B.S.A., M.Sc.—B. 1901; ed. Westward Ho! and Univ. of Alberta; in ch. grain experimental plots, prov. dept. of agr., Vermilion (Alta.) 1921; cream grader, dairy branch, Alberta dept. of agr., 1922; instr., dairying, schl. of agr., Alberta, 1925; instr., animal husbandry, Univ. of Alberta, 1930; headmr., govt. farm schl., and supt., govt. stock farm, Hope, Jamaica, July, 1931.

HOWELL, ALLENE GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk. corrpdg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. commr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1905, and 1907.

HOWELL, CHARLES GOUGH.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., Cantab.; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-17; served in France and Flanders, May to Dec., 1915 and ret. on account of wounds in Aug., 1917; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1921; mem., S. Wales circuit; crown coun., Kenya, Nov., 1925; ag. solr. gen., June to Oct., 1930; mem., leg. coun.; atty.-gen., Fiji, July, 1931; ag. ch. just. and ch. judl. commr., W. Pacific, Apr., 1932 to Jan., 1933; legal advr. and pub. pros., F.M.S., July, 1933; seconded to C.O., 1934.

HOWELL, E. H., M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; promoted, wit h gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk. 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; deptl. astl., 16th Jan., 1923; clk. for legal instruments, 26th Feb., 1923; staff-offr., gen. dept., 6th Apr., 1933.

HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. coun. and exec. comtee., 1925.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statu., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and off. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan. June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1927 and Sept. to Oct., 1928; puisne judge, Straits Stltlms. and judge, F.M.S., Jan., 1934.

HOWITT, CHARLES ROBERTS.—B. 1894; ed. Carlisle Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; seconded to Johore, Dec., 1920; 1st cls. mag., 1922-1924; lieut., V.R., Aug., 1924; 3rd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1926; ag. 2nd astl. sec. (B), S'pore, May, 1926; offr., cls. IV., June, 1926; dist. offr., Jasir, May, 1927 and May, 1928; ag. 1st astl. sec. (B), S'pore, July, 1930; offr., cls. III., June, 1931; ag. 1st astl. sec. (B) and clk. of couns., Aug., 1932; attd. Malayan establmt. office, Aug., 1934; ag. Br. advr., Perlia, Apr., 1935.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORST.—Ck., control and audit office, Cape, 1895; examr., 1895; astl. acctnt., 1903; astl. acctnt., contrlr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1916; sec., office of high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1928; sec. for finance, Nov., 1931.

HUBBARD, PERCIVAL CYRIL.—B. 1902; ed. Mill Hill and Caius Coll., Cantab. (mod. languages scholarship); B.A. hons., 1923; LL.B. hons., 1924; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., May, 1928; ag. asst. dist. offr., Malaita, Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Ysabel, Jan., 1929; ch. mag. and legal advr., Br. Sol. Is. Prot., Feb., 1929; ag. Br. judge, joint ct. and judl. comnr. for New Hebrides, 1930-32; ch. mag., Palestine, 1934.

HUCKS, GEOFFREY WILLIAM YOUNG.—B. 1906; ed. Highgate Schi. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripos, pt. I, 1927; 2nd cls. anthropol. tripos, 1928; B.A., 1928, M.A., 1932; cadet, Taunganyika Territory, July, 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1931.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo kach., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Mar., 1921; asst. settlmt. offr., 1922; office asst., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; asst. sttlmt. offr., May, 1925; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1929; do., Matara in addn., Sept.-Dec., 1932; asst. sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1934.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM C.B.E. (1932), O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privately and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff, cent. telegraphs, London, 1902; dept., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory admin., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUETT, FRANCIS JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Anerley Coll.; ag. supvr., cust., P. Swettenham, July, 1921; do., P. Buntar, Aug., 1921; do. T. Anson, Apr., 1923; supt., cust., P. Swettenham, Mar., 1924; do., P. Dickenson and barbmr. in addn., Jan., 1927; supt., chandu monop., N. Sembilan and supt., cust., in addn., Nov., 1930; sr. supt., trade and cust. dept., F.M.S., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; dep. comnr., do., Apr., 1933.

HUGGARD, SIR WALTER CLARENCE, Kt.Bach. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honourman and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. puisne judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. puisne judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C. 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revd. edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec. and gov.'s dep., Aug., 1927; atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., June, 1929; ch. just., 1933.

HUGGINS, HASTINGS DUDLEY, M.Sc., Dip. Agr., I.C.T.A.—B. 1905; ed. Antigua Gram. Schi. and Cornell Univ.; asst. mast. Antigua Gram. Schi., 1923; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1923; asst. field man., sugar expt. stn., Br. Guiana, 1926; asst. agrl. supt., dept. agr., 1928; agrl. supt., 1934.

HUGGINS, CAPT. JOHN, M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schi. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov. 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, May, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, Oct., 1920; 4th asst. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; passed cadet Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; pvt. sec. to high comnr., May, 1926; offr. cls. III., Aug., 1928; sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. II., Aug., 1933; asst. advr., Kedah, July, 1933; ag. Malayan estabmt. offr., May, 1934.

HUGHES, CLIFFORD BRYNMOR.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; Machine Gun Corps, Salonica, 1916-19; asst. acctnt., Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1919; acctnt., do., Nov., 1924; senr. acctnt., do., July, 1931; ch. acctnt., do., Sept., 1934.
HUGHES, FREDERICK EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1906; ed. Dean Close Schi., 1924 and Worcester Coll., Oxford, 1928; asst. conser., forests, Gold Coast, July, 1928.
HUGHES, GEORGE SPENCER, B.A. (Dub.), LL.B. (Dub.)—B. 1897; Barrister-at-law; asst. dist. comnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; dist. offr., cls. II., 1919; ag. res., 1923; cls I, admstve. serv., 1930.

HUGHES, HERBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, (schol.), and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A. (1911), M.A. (1914); ordained, 1915-16; apptd., educn. dept., Jamaica, 1929; examr., 1929; secy., bd. of educn.; ag. asst. dir., educn., various periods; supervising instr., secondary schls., sec., Jamaica schls. commn. and Rhodes scholarship selection commn., 1933.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1905; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; mag., Sept., 1911; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1923; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IB, comnt., lands, F.M.S., continues ag. as above July, 1928; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Oct., 1930; comnt., lands, F.M.S., May, 1931; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, July, 1931; Br. res., Mar., 1932.

HUGHES, RT. HON. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907 atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., Oct., 1915; Canadian privy councillor, Feb., 1916; prime min. and atty.-gen., Nov., 1916; ditto, Feb., 1917; ditto, Jan., 1918; resigned Feb., 1923; min. for health and repatriation, Lyons miny., 1934 and vice pres. federal exec. coun.; resigned, 1935.
HUGO, FRANCOIS DANIEL, B.A.—B. 1883; inspr. schls., Natal, Apr., 1916; ch. inspr., schls., Jan., 1931; supt., educn., Aug., 1931.

HUME, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1897; Imp. post office, 1912-1919 (war serv. R.E. Signals, G.E.A., 1915-19); postmtr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. postmtr., do., 1923; dist. survr., posts and tels., Gold Coast, 1930.
HUMFREY, ERIC BERTRAM.—B. 1903; ed. Cranleigh; constable, Kenya pol., Jan. 1926; asst. inspr., 1929; ag. asst. supt., 1931-32; local commdt., pol., Cyprus, June, 1933; ag. comnr., July-Nov., 1935.
HUMPHERSON, LESLIE HERBERT.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Schi., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1925; dist. comnt., Jan., 1931.

HUMPHREYS, LIEUT.-COL., SR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1932), G.C.V.O. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. African War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); ent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; pvt. sec. to ch. comnr., N.W.F.P., 1904; asst. comnr., Hazara, 1907; dep. comnr., Bannu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N. Waziristan, 915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (desp.); served European War in Europe as pilot R.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khyber, 1919; dep. foreign sec. to govt. of India, 1921; 1st envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary at the court of H.M. the King of Afghanistan, Jan., 1922; high commr., Iraq, 1929.

HUMPHRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treas., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevia, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treas., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, WILLIAM EDGAR, B.A., C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. Warwick Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; ast. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; ast. col. sec., Lagos, 1910; dist. offr. (2nd cls.), 1917; res., 1924; staff grade, 1929; mem., comm. of inquiry into disturbances in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs. in 1930-33; commr. of col. and lands, 1934; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1934.

HUNTER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Archibald, K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1886).—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Royal Lancaster. 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1889; col., 1894; maj. gen., 1896; served in Egypt, and twice wounded; gov. of Dongola Prov. and commdt. Frontier Field Force, 1895-99; gov. of Omdurman, 1899; Egyptian medal and other orders; in command of 1st cl. dist. in India; lieut.-gen. on staff, commanded 10th div. S.Africa, 1900-01 (twice ment. in desps.); commanded in Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corps, India, 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, Indian Army, 1907-9; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1910-1913.
HUNTER, Hon. James Aitchison Johnston, M.P.—El. to H. of Rs., C'wealth of Australia for Maranoa (Queensland), since 1921; ast. min., Lyons miny., 1934.

HUNTER, James Blake, B.A.—B. 1876; ed. public schls. and Colleg. Inst., Woodstock, Ont.; Toronto Univ. (B.A. 1899); ent. Dominion pub. serv., 1899; pte. sec. to successive ministers of crown; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; 1st cls. clk., 1903; ch. clk., Apr., 1907; ast. dep. min. of pub. wks., 1908; dep. do., 1908.

HUNTER, James Stanley.—B. 1889; ed. Hawera High Schl.; entd. N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; post and tel. dept., 1905; pub. serv. commr.'s office, 1914; pte. sec. to various ministers, 1916-24; off. sec., N.Z. rly., 1924; ast. gen. man., N.Z. rly., 1928; commr., transport, 1929-35; sec. of lab. and commrs., unemployment, 1933; pte. sec. to Sir Francis Bell at League of Nations gen. assem. and also at Imp. Conf., 1928.

HUNTER, John.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; clk. to patronage comtee., Nov., 1907; staff-offr., 1st Apr., 1922; librarian, 6th July, 1933.

HUNTER, John Adams.—B. 1890; ed. at Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (hist. tripes cls. 2, I); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; cls. V, Sept., 1918; ast. dist. offr., Oct., 1919; addt. to chief sec.'s office, Jan.-Mar., 1919; seconded for serv. in food control dept., Mar., 1919; ag. 3rd ast. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., Oct., 1920; 4th ast. sec. to govt., Oct., 1921; cls. IV, offg. dep. contr., lab., Seremban, Nov., 1921; ditto, K. Lumpur, July, 1922; on spl. duty, rubber census, July, 1923; offr. in ch., govt. toddy shops in addn., July, 1923; 3rd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Oct., 1926; Class III, Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1927; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addn., Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1933; ag. under sec., S.S., Mar., 1935.
HUNTER, K. L.—Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

HUNTER, Louis Lucien.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; att. to Colombo kach., Sept., 1914; addtl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1915; addtl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Panadura, Sept., 1916; office ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chilaw, Sept., 1919; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1922; ast. attit. offr., Jan., 1923; addnl. ast. dir. educn., July, 1928; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, Jan., 1930; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1934; do., Matara, Mar., 1934; ag. govt.-agt., N. Cent. prov., Sept., 1934.

HUNTINGFIELD, 5th Baron (Cr. 1796) William Charles Arveduchne Vanneck, K.C.M.G. (1934).—B. (Queensland) 1873; M.P., Eye divn., Suffolk, 1923-29; partly. pte. secy. to pres., B. of T., 1927-28; gov., Victoria, 1934.
HURST, Raymond George.—B. 1877; Indian State rly., 1900; E. African rly., 1917; ast. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., July, 1925.

HUSBAND, Charles Thomas Main, F.S.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1891; ed. Box Hill Grammar Schl., Melbourne; 1st grade survr., F.M.S., 1913; served France, 1915-18; ast. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ast. supt., Selangor, July-Nov., 1920; Perak, Feb., 1926; Johore, Sept., 1927; ast. surveys, F.M.S. & S.S., July, 1929; do., Kedah, Apr., 1930 and Jan., 1932; do., Malacca and N. Sembilan, May, 1933.

HUSBAND, Thomas Gibson, B.Sc. (Edin.), A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1890; ed. Heriot Watt Coll. and Edin. Univ.; ast. engrn., Jasir, S.S., July, 1919; ast. exec. engrn., Malacca, May, 1923; ast. engrn., Penang, Dec., 1924; ditto, P. Wellesley, Jan., 1927; exec. engrn., South, P. Wellesley, Apr., 1929; snr. exec. engrn., P.W.D., June, 1931; ditto, rural and marine, Spore, July, 1931; snr. exec. engrn., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, Oct., 1932; do., Town, Apr., 1934.

HUSSY, Eric Robert James, C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1885; ed., Repton (Schl.), open exhibn., Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1904; B.A., 1908 (lit. hum.), M.A., 1923; Sudan civ. serv. as tutor, Gordon Coll., Khartum, Sept., 1908; dist. commr., 1912; ch. inspr., educn. dept., 1918; educn. mission to Somaliland, 1920; educn. mission to Uganda, 1924; dir. educn., Uganda, 1925; educn. mission to Kenya, 1928; dir. educn., Nigeria, 1929; mem., stat. comm. of inspecn., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, 1932.

HUSSY, John Drew.—B. 1900; 2nd lient., Devonshire Regt. & Tank Corps, Apr., 1918-May, 1919; pol. probr., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. ast. commr. pol., various occasions, 1922-24; adjt., F.M.S. pol., Dec., 1925; A.C.P., various occasions, 1925-31; dep. commr. pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1933.
HUSSEY, THOMAS MACDONALD.—B. 1888; ed. Long Ashton Coll., Somerset; off. censor, cinematograph films, S.S., F.M.S. and Johore, Dec., 1919.

HUTCHINS, ERNEST ELWIN.—B. 1886; ed. Exeter Schl.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 1903-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest demarcator, Kenya, 1909; asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-16; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; admstve. offr., 1st grade (now dist. offr.), Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MENTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Mily. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Miny. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; prin., 1st, 1st Oct., 1930.

HUTCHISON, PATRICK HAMILTON.—B. 1902; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Edin. Univ.; M.A., 1924; LL.B., 1925; admitted solr., Feb., 1927; asst. admstr. gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. admstr. gen., off. recr., pub. trustee, regis., companies, trade marks, patents, designs, trade unions, regis. gen., births, deaths, marriages, house tax and estate duty comanr., 1933-34.

HUTSON, Sir Eyre, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), R.A.—B. 1864; 2nd clk. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1885; asst. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1885; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; col. sec. and regar.-gen., Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comanr., 1902; chrmn., St. George’s harbour comanr., 1903; col. sec., Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., educn. comanr., Apr., 1909; rep. Fiji and W. Pacific high comanr., at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep. gov., on various occasions, 1909-12; comanr., currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov. Fiji and high comanr., for W. Pacific, Aug., 1915 to May, 1916 and from June to Oct., 1918; gov., B. Honduras, Nov., 1918; gov., Fiji and high comanr., W. Pacific, 1925; ret., July, 1929.

HUTT, ALEXANDER MACDONALD BRUCE.—B. 1904; ed. St. Andrew’s Coll., Grahamstown, S.A., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (English lit.); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; ag. dist. offr., May, 1932; seconded, cert., Mar., 1935.

HUTTON, THEODORE RALPH, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ag. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1922-27; exec. engrnr., 1928-32; ag. sr. exec. engrnr., Coast, Pahang, July, 1933.
HUTTON-MILLS, ALEXANDER, B.A.—B.1900; ed. Dulwich and Keble Coll., Oxford, hons. in jurisprudence; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; practised in Gold Coast, 1924-30; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1930.
HUXHAM, HAROLD JAMES.—Comanr., income tax, Ceylon, Apr., 1932; finan. secy., July, 1934.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, O.B.E. (1931), F.R.Hist.S.—B.1875; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Grnn. Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1895-98; supplementary estab., secy.’s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of sec., miny. of infrn., Apr. to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; asst. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrlr., lab. Dec., 1921; in charge, dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrlr., permits, Apr., 1924; ch. immigrn. offr., Oct., 1926; sec., cent. town planning comanr., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comanr., chmn., comanr. on rates of wages for govt. employees; dep. chmn., lab. standing comanr.; dir., immigrn., Jan., 1932; author, “A History of the Jews in England,” “Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People,” “Palestine, Old and New,” “British Projects for the Restoration of the Jews,” etc.

HYDE, ANTHONY, M.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1900; ed. Westminster Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford (exhibir.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; attd., dist. office, Alor Gajah, Malacca, 1925; pvte. sec. to Gov., 1929; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Oct., 1931; ag. pvte. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols., Aug., Sept., 1933; ag. 2nd ast. sec. (B.) S.S., Nov., 1933.

ILSLEY, THE HON. JAMES LORIMER, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (Digby-Annapolis-Kings).—B. 1894; ed. Somerset pub. schl., Berwick High Schl., Acadia Univ., Wolfville (Arte), and Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax; degrees of B.A. (Acadia) and LL.B. (Dalhousie); barrister-at-law; elec. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of national rev. in King’s cabinet, 1935.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. regiar., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

INGLEDOW, CEDRIC FRANCIS.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Aug., 1920; attd. to Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1920; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1924; ditto to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, N’Ellya, Dec., 1927; astt. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Oct., 1930; astt. attltm. offr. Mar., 1931.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., Sept., 1914 to May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., Dec., 1916; min. of nat. serv., Dec., 1917; min. of lab., demob. and resettlement dept., Nov., 1918; F.Z.S., May, 1919; astt. dist. comanr., Zanzibar, July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Societe d’Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif., acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; 2nd astt. sec., Jan., 1925; pvte. sec. to ag. high comanr., Apr. to June, 1925; ditto to Br. res., Nov., 1925 to Aug., 1926 and from Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. pvte. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, Nov., 1925 to June, 1926; clk., prot. comanr., Feb. to June, 1925; cdr., Offl. Gaz., in 1924 and 1927; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug. to Oct., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; mem. Pemba educn. comanr. and teaching of Koran comanr., 1924; mem. museum comanr., 1924 and ag. chmn., Aug., 1926; cls. IV., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1927; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1927; chmn., museum reorganisation comanr., 1928; chmn., comanr. of enquiry, med. and health dept., 1928; mem. schls. comanr., 1928-29; vice pres., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1929; mem., R. Coll., comanr., 1929-31; ag. astt. prin., C.O., 1929-30; ag. recr.-gen., June-July, 1932; ag. col. sec., Jan.-May, and from Aug., 1932; polit. advr., Aden, 1934; ag. polit. secy., Sept., 1934; part author, “Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History” (1924), “Schl. History of... Zanzibar" (1925), and "Aya Za Kuraani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate," (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926), "Guide to Swahili Examinations" (1927), "Zanzibar, Its History and People" (1931), "School History of Mauritius" (1931), "School Geography of Mauritius" (1932), and various pamphlets and articles.

INNES, CAMERON STAR.—B. 1892; Imp. serv., male learner, post office, 1908; seconded col. serv., 1921; war serv., 1914-19; ent. Nigeria serv., as asst. surv., posts and tels., Mar., 1921; divn. survr., Apr., 1933.

INNESS, Rt. Hon., Sir James Rose, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reptd. Cape div.; atty.-gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comte. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. just. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., Mar., 1927.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, C.M.G. (1929), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Schol. and St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; house physician, Bethlem Royal Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1905; senr. sanv. offr., Nigeria, 1914; asst. dir. of med. serv., Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and sanv. services, Sierra Leone, 1924; dir., med. and sanv. serv., Gold Coast, Mar., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns.
INNISS, KNOLLS ULRIC ALEXANDER, M.B., Ch. B. Edin.—B. 1882; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912; ag. surg.-gen., Aug.-Dec., 1934.
INSKIP, WALTER.—B. 1887; 14th Hussars, 1906-13; home prison serv., 1914; on active serv., 1914-18; ment. in desps., 1917; seconded to prison serv., Zanzibar, 1924; aspt., 1925; transf., 1926.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—Cik., admnstr.'s off., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to admnstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; dir., B.S.A. Co., Apr., 1919; gen. man., B.S.A. Co., S. Africa, June, 1922; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion, 1896 (medal), and Rhodesia field force, 1900 (medal and 2 clasps); joined R.A.S.C., 1914; O.C., R.A.S.C., Harwich dist., 1914-17; S.S.O., 48th divn., France, Flanders, Italy, 1917-19; Croce di Guerra, Italy; ment. in desps.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and Bucks. L.I., May, 1915; invalided with rank of capt., Jan., 1918; asst. priv. sec. (appts.), to Lord Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, Feb., 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amey, Nov., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1903; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.Sc. Ag., Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; R.Sc., agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1894; ed. Dunfermline High Schl.; bd. of agr., Scotland, 1913; served in France, 1913-18; capt., Lancs. Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; prte. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V., July, 1921; asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, May, 1923; dist. offr., Jebu, Nov., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr. cls. IV., July, 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., May, 1926; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B) S.S. and olk. of couns., Mar., 1928; dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1929; offr. cls. III., July, 1930; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A) S.S., Jan., 1934.

IRVINE, HON. SIR WM. HILL, K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.—B. 1858; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. H. of R., C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept. 1914; chief just., Victoria, Apl., 1918; liet.-gov., Victoria, May, 1918; ag. gov. from June, 1931 to May, 1934; ret., 1935.

IRVING, FRANCIS ERNEST.—B. 1886; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Schl., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; German S.W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1914-15; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to admnstr. of Mafia Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916, to May, 1919; asst., cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. asst. compt., cust. for various periods, 1920-22; asst. compt., cust., Dec., 1922; ag. compt., cust., for various periods, 1924-34.
IRVING, HUGH CRAUFURD.—Asst. dist. commr., Somaliland Prov., June, 1914.

IRWIN, HENRY MARK, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Haileybury and Selwyn Coll.; Cambridge exhibitioner; Haileybury exhibitioner; hons. class. tripoe, 1908; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; attached to secretariat, July to Dec., 1911; priv. sec. to ag. gov., C. L. Temple, Esq., C.M.G., June, 1912.
IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928.

ISAACS, Rt. Hon. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1906-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., Mar., 1930; gov. gen. of the C'wealth of Australia, Jan., 1931.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon) (hons.).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, July, 1913; Nigerian Land Contingent, 1914; attd. polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika admnstr., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. senr. commr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 to Mar., 1928; ditto and mem., exec. and leg. couns., in 1927, 1929, and 1931; Swahili examr.; dir., educn., 1931.

ISLINGTON, 1st BARON (cr. 1910), KT. HON. SIR JOHN POYNDER DICKSON-POYNDER, 6TH BART., P.C. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1913); K.C.M.G. (1911); D.S.O. (1900); J.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; major and hon. col., Wilts Yeomanry; served in S. Africa. 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); late lieut., 3rd Royal Scots; mem. L.C.C., 1898-1904; M.P., Chippenham, Wilts., 1892-1910; gov., New Zealand, 1910-1912; chmn. of Indian civ. ser. comm., 1912; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 1914; parly. undersec. for India, 1915.
IVES, Hon. William Carlos, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta.; Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.

IVEY, Lieut. Commdr. A. V. P., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; Cameroons expdy. force, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; commanded H.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, China, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. mast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 1930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935.

IZARD, Godfrey Wallace.—B. 1891; ed. Uppingham; B.S.A. Co., 1909-14; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., 1914; invalided; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; admstve. serv., Apr., 1930; dep. commsr., lands, 1930; prin. asst. sec., N. Provs., 1931.
IZARD, H.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.

JACK, Henry Walter, M.B.E. (1930), D.Sc., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork (scholar); B.A. (econ.), 1911; B.Sc. (biol.), 1912; agr. inspr., F.M.S., 1914; agr. instr., 1915; ec. botanist, S.S. & F.M.S., 1919; mem., comte., on rubber research, 1923; D.Sc. by research, 1925; rep. of Malaya, Pac. Sc. Cong., Japan, 1926 and in Java, 1929; economic botanist, Mar., 1929; mem., coconut and oil palm research comte., 1929; hon. sec., rice cult. comte., Malaya, 1930; dir. agr., Fiji, 1934; nom. M.L.C.; publsn. on rice, coconuts, oil palm, cotton, rubber, etc.

JACK, Murray M.—B. 1893; ed. Peterhead Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; solr., sup. ct., Scotland, 1920; H.B.M. consult. cts., Egypt, 1922-25; war serv., Gordon Highs. and I.A.R.O., 1914-19 (capt., ment. in desps.); dep. registr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1925; asst. admstr. gen. and offr.-recr. Tanganyika Territory 1926; registr., sup. ct., Kenya and E.A. ct. of appl., July, 1929; rep. and coll., E. A. Law Repts., 1930-32; ch. registr., sup. ct., Palestine, 1935.

JACKLIN, S., M.C.—Actnt., repat. dept., Christians, Aug., 1902; rev. clk., law dept., Nov., 1902; clk., civ. comms. off., Pretoria, Nov., 1903; clk., rev. dept., Mar., 1905; prin. clk., Oct., 1906; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1912; inspr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. commsr., Johannesburg, Sept., 1919; ch. clk., estabtms., treas., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1920; sec. pub. ser. comms., Oct., 1921; ret. Oct., 1926; treas., League of Nations, Oct., 1926.

JACKS, Philip—B. 1877; ed. Plymouth Coll.; admtd. solr., sup. ct., England, 1900; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1905; J.P., 1905; commsr. for oaths, 1905; ag. land offr., 1907 to 1908; ag. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1908; ag. land offr., 1911; and from 1913-1914; land offr., 1916; ag. crown solr., 1919 and 1920; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1922; registr., marriages in addn., 1927; ag. puisne judge on several occasions, 1927-34.

JACKSON, Sir Edward St. John, Kt. Bach. (1933), O.B.E. (1918). K.C.—Ed. Stonyhurst and Beaumont Colleges and Brazenose College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, Oct., 1912; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Oct., 1918; judge of high ct., July, 1920; pres. of land commsr., 1920; pres., finance commsr., 1923; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1924; atty.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1929.

JACKSON, Major Sir Francis Walter Fitton, Kt. Bach. (1936), C.M.G. (1928), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. Dover Coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., July, 1904; dist. commsr., Northern Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1907; ast. col. sec., G. Coast, Mar., 1911; provincial commsr., G. Coast, Jan., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., 1915 to Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1915 to June, 1918; ment. in desps. 3 times; O.C. British forces, and senr. polit. offr., Togoland, July, 1919; represented gov., in final handing over of Lome and that portion of Togoland ceded to France by the Franco-British declm. of 10th July, 1919; dep. ch. commsr., Ashanti, 1928; ch. commsr., N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1930; do., Ashanti, 1932.
JACKSON, Gildart.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Sch., Guildford and Westminster; ast dist. commsr., Sierra Leone, Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, John.—Ed. St. Mark's Coll. Schl., Chelsea, and King's Coll.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1934; on serv. 1914-19 (U.K., France and N. Russia); admstve. serv., Nigeria, Dec., 1920; transf. to col. legal serv. as ast. judge of high ct., Nigeria, Apr., 1935.

JACKSON, Robert Best, M.B., D.P.H., B.A.O., M.D., T.C.D., L.M. Rotunda Hospital, Dublin, certif., London Sch. Trop. Med.; R.A.M.C., 1914-17; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; M.O.H., Negri Sembilan, 1921; do., Pahang West, 1926; malarialogist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930.

JACKSON, Sir Wilfrid Edward Francis, K.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G.—B. 1883; ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (classical scholar), B.A. 1905; personal sec. to gov., Trinidad, 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and clk. to exec. coun., Bahamas, Sept., 1906, to Oct., 1907; ast. collr., Uganda, Nov., 1907; ag. dist. commsr., Toro, July, 1911 to Apr., 1912; dist. commsr., Apr., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., July-Dec., 1912; 1st ast. sec., Dec., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., Aug., 1913 to Feb., 1914, May to Nov., 1914 and from Apr., 1915; col. sec., Bermuda, 19th Feb., 1916; col. sec., Barbados, 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Feb., 1926; ag. gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Mauritius, Mar., 1930.

JACOBS, Charles Ernst Harvey (late R.A.), B.Sc. (Chem.), B.Sc. (Bot.) Vict., L.C.P. Diploma in Educn.—B. 1886; ed. Brewood Gram. Schl., Staffordshire and Victoria Univ. (Queen's Coll.), Manch.; ast. master, Marlborough Coll., Cranleigh Schl. and Reading Schl.; sr. sc. master, Raffles Inst., S'pore, Feb., 1921; sr. normal instr. in addn., Apr., 1929; sr. master, Raffles Inst., Nov. 1930; headmast., High Schl., B. Mertajam, Aug., 1931.
JACOBS, Kenneth Charles, A.C.A.—B. 1904; ed. Bedford Schl.; ast. forest dept., Cyprus, 1928; offr. recr. (companies), 1930; custodian, deportees' property, 1931; treas. ast., Gambia, 1934.
JACQUES, Evelyn Bessie, M.B., Ch.R. (Bristol), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—lady med. offr., Seremban, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; do., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1922; do., Ipoh, Apr., 1924; do., Taiping, June, 1926.

JACQUES, FREDERICK VIEL, M.B., Ch.B., (Bristol), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1891; ed. Gresham's Schl., Bristol Univ. and Lond. Hosp.; midshipman and sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1909-15; surg. sub-lieut., 1916; North Sea, 1914-15; E. Mediterranean, 1916-17; med. offr., Malayau med. serv. (Seremban), 1921; health offr., K. Lumpur, 1922; Kinta, 1924; Perak North, 1926; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1927; ag. senr. health offr., Perak, 1928 and 1931; ag. dep. state med. and health offr., Perak, 1932; state med. and health offr., Perak, 1934; senr. health offr., Johore, Sept., 1934.

JAMES, JOSEPH EDWARD, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls. and Mico Coll., Antigua; ag. educn.-offr., Antigua, Nov., 1901; clk., educn. dept. and educn. offr., Antigua, Apr., 1904; schlmast., St. John's Training Schl., Antigua, Feb., 1913, to June, 1917; sub-inspr. schls., Antigua, Apr., 1914; sub-inspr., schls., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1919; local instr., schls., Mar., 1927; ag. fedl. instr., schls., July, 1929 to Jan., 1929.
JAMES, MARSHALL NICOL.—B. 1875; served under the condominium govt., New Hebrides until Mar., 1922; govt. agt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, S. I.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1919; ent. W.O. (temp.) Nov., 1919; ent. C.O. as clerical offr., July, 1926; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Mar., 1931; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1932.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, KT. BACH. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schls., admitted as bafrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. conven., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C. 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904-1906; chan., univ. of W. Australia.

JAMESON, CLAUDE FRANCIS SPURRELL, M.C., A.C.S.M., A.I.M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Wellington Coll. and Camborne Schl. of Mines; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.E., Jan., 1916; instr., mines, Selangor, Oct., 1919; asst. warden, mines, F.M.S., Oct., 1928; asst. instr., mines, Ipoh and S. Siput, Feb., 1931; do. K. Lumpur, Mar., 1933; asst. warden, mines, Selangor, Apr., 1933; do. N. Sembilan, in addn., June, 1933.

JAMISON, ROBERT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Ireland), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1880; asst. med. offr., Hiatiikulu, Swaziland, 1910-13; med. offr., Swaziland, 1913; served, S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; prin. med. offr., 1918.

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; chmn., Saamwerk Unie van Nataalse Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; mem., Union H. of A. for Vrheid since 1921; speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924-July, 1929; min. of native affrs., June, 1929 to Mar., 1933; speaker, H. of A., May, 1933.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; att. Kandy kach., Sept., 1914; att. Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Batticaloa, in addn., Ang., 1915; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Jan., 1917; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1925; addnl. dist. judge, Avissawella, in addn., Oct., 1927; asst. sttlmt. offr., Sept., 1928; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, Apr. to June, 1930; ag. sttlmt. offr., Mar., 1931.
JANSZ, REGINALD BELFRAN.—B. 1878; cls. V Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1928; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1928; attd., Colombo kach., Mar., 1934.

JARDINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910 B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; pvt. sec. to ag. high commnr., July to Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. asst. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admn., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expdy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in desp.); senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; gov't. dep. on several occasions since 1927; ag. gov't., in 1929, 1931 and 1933; gov't., North Borneo, 1933; accredited rep. to Perm't. Mandates Comsn., 1929, 1930 and 1931; joint-editor "Hand-book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FRIER, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917; asst. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admvte. instr., Mosul prov., 1923; on sp. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admvte. instr., Sulaimani, 1925; do., Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; author "Grammar of Bahdinan Kurmanji (Kurdish)," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY, K.C.—B. 1895; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; asst. dist. comnr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr. gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; ag. admvtr. and ag. ch. just., St. Vincent in 1930 and 1931; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1933; ag. ch. just. and O.A.G., 1934; dep. gov., 1935; col. sec., Aug., 1935.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWLSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Feb., 1916; cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, 1930; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Pemk, May, 1931; ag. Br. advr., Trengganu, June, 1933; offr., cls. I B, 1934; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1934.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes schol., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec. ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926; atty.-gen., Natal, 1933.

JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office asst., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Mar., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1934.

JEFF, CAPT. JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., attd. Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1922; asst. prot., Chinese, Seremban, Dec., 1923; attd., cust. dept., Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Apr., 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1930; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932; ag. asst. sec., Chinese affs., S.S., Aug., 1933.

JEFF, RICHARD HEDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. asst. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1913; dist. engrnr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supervm. dist. engrnr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engrnr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engrnr., grade I, Jan., 1924; ag. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1928-Aug., 1929; sr. dist. engrnr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931; divnl. engrnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932.

JEFFERY, CECIL ALBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Clyde schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; clk., govt. house, 1914; pvt. sec. to various ministers, 1915-26; ch. prot. sec. to prime min., 1926-35; clk., exec. coun. and sec. to cabinet, 1935; served with N.Z. expd. force.

JEFFERY, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archology of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inad. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; relinquished appt. July, 1919; reapptd. curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, Feb., 1921; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," "Cyprus under an English King," "Historic Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem, edr., new edition (1929) of "An attempt at a Bibliography of Cyprus."

JEFFREY, PAY COMMDR. A., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1892; ed. Glas. Univ.; R.N., 1914-19; probationer, N. Rhodesia, 1919; J.P., 1920; clk. of ct. and under-sheriff, Batoka dist., 1920; asst. nat. commr., 1921; nat. commr., 1926; passed higher Chinyanga; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory 1927; asst. secy., Jan., 1930; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934-35; clk., exec. and leg. coun., 1935; admstive. offr., 1st gr., Zanzibar, Aug., 1935; asst. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Aug., 1935.

JEFFREYS, ROBERT SYDNEY.—B. 1888; ed. Cape Town (S. Africa), Bedford Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B. (1909), Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn, 1910); joined adminstr., of N. Rhodesia, June, 1914; asst. native commr., and J.P., July, 1916; resigned to proceed on active serv., Feb., 1918; rejoined N. Rhodesia adminstr., May, 1919; native commr., July 1921; seconded to secretariat, Nov., 1921-Dec., 1922; asst. mag., Apr., 1926; dist. offr. (grade II) with powers of mag., Apr., 1928; seconded as asst. to sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1927-Oct., 1928; ag. crown coun., Nov., 1928-Mar., 1929; ag. pol. mag., May, 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltshire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land slmt. selection board, 1919; asst. sec., E. African currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comttee. on governors' pensions, 1927-28; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.
