JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. asst., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; 1st asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief asst., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929; dir., 1932.

JERKILL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1885)—B. 1846; lieut.-R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comttee., 1878; sec. to the roy. comn. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comttee., 1885; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comn. for Melbourne centennial exhib., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comn. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JELF, SIR ARTHUR SELBORNE, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (extrh.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admstive. and exec. posts in Malay States, 1900-10; asst. gen. adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., man. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., gen. staff, S. Stlttna command; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (milit.int.l.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comttee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., June, 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, cls. I.B., Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malaya opium comttee., Dec., 1923; pres., estates health comn., Apr., 1924 (reed. thanks of S. of S.).; col. sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., for various periods 1925-32; lieut.-col. local forces, Oct., 1927; ret. 1935.

JENKINS, EDWARD ENOCH.—B. 1895; ed. Peterhouse, Cambridge, B.A., LL.B., 1922; M.A., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1924; joined South Wales circuit; war serv. (France) 1914-19; lieut., R.F.A.; cadet, Nyassaland, Sept., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Apr.-July, 1927; ag. asst. atty.-gen. July-Oct., 1927; asst. regt., high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1927; crown coun., Apr., 1930; ag. solr.-gen. or ag. atty.-gen., 1931-35; ag. judge, high ct., 1934.

JENKS, J. C. A.—Ed. Bristol Gram. Schl. and Angers, France; awarded Croix-de-Guerre (Palm) 1917; Order of Saint Stanislaus with Swords (2nd cls.), and med. in desps., 1919; colonial audt. serv., 1920; asst. audr., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1923; senr. asst. audr. in charge, Jan., 1925; mem., retrenchment and rev. comtee., Zanzibar, 1927; Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar (5th cls.), 1928; senr. asst. audr. in charge, Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbors, 1929; audr., Mauritius, 1933.

JENNER, FRANK.—B. 1878; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland campaign, 1896-97; S. African war, 1899-1901; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1901; inspr., 1910; supt., leper distnt., 1914; comntr., 1920.
JENNINGS, F. G.—Asst. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919.
JENNINGS, PRUDENTIAL HENRY.—B. 1903; ed. Christ's Hosp.; coll. audit dept., 1926; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1927; asst. audr., Mauritius, 1931; ag. audr., 1932.

JEPSON, FRANK PRICE.—B. 1885; M.A., Pembroke Coll., Camb.; exhibr., medallist, and dip. in agri. (hons. in entomology), Wye Agrio. Coll., Kent, 1906; trop. disease research fund student in med. entomology, Camb. Univ., 1907; assisted in investg. by L.G.B.O. on flies as carriers of infection, 1908 and 1909; govt. entomologist, Fiji, 1909; offl. visits to Hawaii, 1911, Samoa, 1912, Java, 1913; asst. entomologist, Ceylon, 1919; ag. entomologist in addn., July, 1923 to Mar., 1924 and Oct. to Nov., 1925; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Conf., Lond., 1927; on sp. serv., U.S.A. (termite investg.), 1928.

JERMYN, LANCASTER AMBROSE SCUDAMORE, B.A.(Oxon.), M.R.S.T.—B. 1886; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; headmast. Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; do., Northam rd. Eng. Schl., Penang, Jan., 1923; do., govt. Eng. Schl., B. Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1926 and Mar., 1931; do., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, July, 1930; do., High Schl., Malacca, Sept., 1934.
JERRAM, JAMES HAVELOCK.—B. 1879; ed. Thames High Schl. and Auckland Gram. Schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance office, 1905; acct., 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. man., 1923.

JESSE, WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served Indian Army R. of O., 1916-19; Afghan campaign, 1919; Calcutta Light Horse, 1921-22; apptd. treas., Kenya, 1924; asst. treas., Uganda, Jan., 1925; asst. currency offr., Uganda, 1927-28 and 1933; asst. treas., Zanzibar, Nov., 1935.

JEWELL, MORTON, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1890; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; conf. clk., 1911; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., establishments, 1st June, 1928.
JINADASA, HETTIARACHCHIGE, B.Sc. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1934; attd. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1935.

JOBLING, GEOFFREY LIONEL.—B. 1899; ed. Bowen Schl., Brisbane; called to bar, Jan., 1926 (Gray's Inn); served German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A. Co., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; asst. regisr., high ct., N. Rhodesia; asst. offl. recr. in bankey., asst. admin. gen., asst. regisr., lands and deeds, asst. regisr. births, deaths and marriages, asst. regisr. companies, asst. custodian enemy property, asst. contr. local clearing office, asst. regisr., high ct., etc., Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933 and 1934.
JOHN, SAMUEL SPEDDING.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Four Courts, Dublin, 1914; war serv., 1914-20; admivte. serv., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; asst. judge, high ct., 1934.
JOHNS, BRIAN MAURICE, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Ed.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1901; med. offr., S.S., Mar., 1923; prof. clin. surgery., King Edward VII Coll. of Med., Spore, Aug., 1928.

JOHNS, ROBERT.—B. 1903; ed. Monmouthshire Agr. Inst., Usk, and Univ. Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth; imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; N.D.A., O.D.A., C.D.D., A.I.C.T.A.; stock inspir., Tanganyika, 1926; C.O. agr. research scholar, Trinidad, and Univ. of Reading, 1929-30; agr. offr., Zanzibar, 1931.

JOHNSON, CHARLES WALTER TREVOR.—B. 1893; Fiji serv., 1910; col. sec.'s office, 1915; lst cls. clk. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1920; ag. pvt. sec. to ag. gov. on three occasions; seconded for serv. in W. Pacific high comn. as dist. offr., Fanning Island, Feb.-Nov., 1923; ch. clk., secretariat, 1927; ag. prov. and dist. comntr., June-Sept., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1929; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1931; ag. 1st asst. col. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. prin. asst. col. sec. in 1934 and 1935; mem., Fiji mining bd. and mem., advy. bd., cent. med. schl.

JOHNSON, E. T.—B. 1875; ed. Sedbergh Schl. and Trinity Hall (Camb.); B.A., LL.B. (Law Tripos, 1902); res. mag., K. Africa Prot., 1915; senr. res. mag., Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, in 1924-25 and 1928-29; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929; ag. ch. just., 1932; judge, Nyasaland Prot., 1934.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M., M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1873; asst. clk., post office, Sept., 1914; ch. clk., Jan., 1924; ag. postmr. in 1925 and 1930; asst. postmr., June, 1926; ag. postmr., Aug. to Sept., 1932.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, I.S.O. (1932), A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspir. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of acots. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties; is also a dep. comntr. of the high comntr.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regisr. of high comntr.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas. and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., Nov., 1919; offl. mem., advisory coun., 1927.

JOHNSON, CAPT. GEORGE BENJAMIN, M.R.S.T.—B. 1884; ed. Ryder's Schl., Devonport and Cheltenham Training Coll. (dipl. educn.); Leicestershire Regt. and gen. staff, 1915-19; ag. vice-consul, Le Havre, 1920; headmr., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1920; ditto and inspir., schl., 1925; ch. inspir., schl., 1928; ag. dir., educn. for various periods, 1920-33; temp. mem., L.C., 1928, 1930, 1932-33; temp. mem., E.C., 1932-33.

JOHNSON, JOHN-COLEMAN DE GRAFT, O.B.E (1935).—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Schl., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; asst. inspir., W. and M., 1914; asst. sec. for native affrs. Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.

JOHNSON, SIR WALTER BURFORD, Kt. Bach. (1935), L.R.C.P., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schl. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1912; seconded for sp. investg. work under yellow fever comn., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1913 to May, 1914; temp. capt., gen. list, 1914-15; tsetse investigator, 1921; specialist, 1921; seconded for serv. on Tsetse fly comn.; dir., med. and sanv. services, Nigeria, Mar., 1929.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B.1862; speaker of the H. of R., C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-23; ret. from politics.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in L.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; ment. in desps., 1917; transf'd. to depot, 11th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mil'y. acts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. acts. offr., O.E.T. admtn., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admtn., July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and advisory couns. for various periods, 1923-28; mem., comttee. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comttee. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926; mem., rly. bd., 1928; currency offr., Jan.-June, 1928; treas. and currency offr., 1932; chmn. income tax comttee., 1934; head, trade delegn. to Egypt, 1935.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist., S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, Hon. Harold Featherston, K.C. (1930), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1875; ed. Wanganui Coll., N.Z. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn (1897) and N.Z.(1898); many years legal practice; pres., Wellington dist. law soc., 1928; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1934; judge ct. of review of mortgagors' liabilities, 1935; served in France with London Scottish (1916-18).

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROKE.—B. 1898; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTON, Sir REGINALD FLEMING, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.M.G. (1928), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; M.A. Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901), Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prox. acc. Lord Rector's essay, Edin.; hon. in English lit., nod. hist. and constitutional law, Edin.; exhib., Magd. Coll., Oxford, 1894; ment., hon. causa Stanhope Essay, 1898; B.A., 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of couns., 1899; pvt. sec. to gov't., 1900-01; ag. ast. col. sec., 1900-04; J.P., June, 1902; sec. to gov't., Weihaiwei, 1904; dist. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906; seconded for serv. as tutor to Manchu Emperor (ex-Emperor of China), 1919-26; recd. from Emperor button of highest offl. grade and sable robe, 1922; warden, Summer Palace, Peking, 1924; brought Emperor into safety in legation quarter, Peking, Nov., 1924; sec., Br. China indemnity delegation, 1926; comsrr., Weihaiwei, 1927-30.

JOHNSTON, WILLIAM—B. 1890; ast. clk., G.P.O., May, 1910; offr., cust. and excise, Aug., 1911; dep. collr., cust., Mauritius, Dec., 1920; sec., harbr. bd., 1922-25; chmn., ditto, 1923-24; sec., tariff advisory bd., 1927-31; chmn., ditto, on various occasions, 1927-30; comsrr. of excise for tobacco in addn., 1927-31 and ag. ast. supt., inland rev., May to July, 1929; ag. collr., cust. and harbrmr., M.I.C., on various occasions, 1923-30; collr.-gen., Jamaica, Jan., 1932; col. sec., treas. and collr., cust., Br. Honduras, 1935.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNCAN.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1908; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; ast. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cls. II., polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comsrr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922; prov. comsrr., Apr., 1928; comsrr., S. Prov., Aug., 1929; ag. ch. comsrr., N. Territories in addn., Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGU CHOLMELEY.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. ast. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; ast. supt., 1927.

JOHNSTONE, SIR ROBERT STEWART, KT. BACH. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comttee., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. attv.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES.—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A. 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to ast. advisor's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd ast. advisor, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; ast. state astur., chmn., conservancy bd., Khota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; sec. to rivers comsrr., in addn., Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III., June, 1930; 2nd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; hon. A.D.C. to high comsrr., Mar., 1931; ag. pub. trustee and offl. admstr., F.M.S., May, 1931; offr., cls. II., June, 1935.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mil'y. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matare, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment comsrr., Jan., 1922; addnl. ast. dir., educa., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Eliya, Apr., 1925; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925; ast. col. treas., Sept., 1923; ast. gov't. agt., Matale, June, 1929; dep. collr., cust., Sept., 1933; ag. prin. collr. cust. and chmn., Colombo port comsrr. in addn., Oct., 1934.

JONES, GEORGE ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1934).—R. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; ast. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comttee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1919; promoted under O.-in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWYNN ARTHUR.—Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1909; ast. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ast. sec. to permanent exhibitn. comttee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and a director of the Victoria museum, 1915.

JONES, CART. NELSON, M.C. and Bar.—B. 1895; ed. Glasgow Univ.; comsrr., Cameron Highlands, 1915; dischg'd, on account of wounds, Apr., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ast. contr. lab., Penang, Mar., 1920; ditto, K. Lumpur, May, 1921; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; attd. dist. office, Klang to act as 1st cls. mag., 1923; 3rd mag., S'pore., July, 1924; offr., cls. IV Oct., 1926; asst. advr., Kukup, May, 1929; ag. emigrn. comnr., Malaya, Madras, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1931; mag., J. Bahru, Jan., 1933; offr. admstr., Johore, Nov., 1934.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYMAN.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cape, 1898; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1924; edited "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly with H. O. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S.A."; judge, Aug., 1926.

JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regtr., 1930; chief judge, native land ct., since 1919; was undersec., native dept., 1922-33 and native trustee, 1933.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.R.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.)—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Schl.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY BOSCAWEN.—Ch. acct.'s office, Beira and Mashonaland and Rhodesia Rly., Bulawayo, 1912-18; 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1914-17; asst. native comnr., and native comnr., N. Rhodesia, 1918-28; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Oct., 1929.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. hona. hist. (1908); barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); cadet F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917; do., K. Kangsar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu; Jan. 1920; do., K. Lipis, May, 1920; localexamr. in law and Malay, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1923; attd. to fed. sec. on sp. dutv., Aug., 1924; localexamr. in Malay, Selangor, Sept., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1928; offr., cls. IB, May, 1931; comnr., lands and mines, Johore, Nov., 1933.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. comnr., Dec., 1918; dep. prov. comnr., Jan., 1924; prov. comnr., July, 1927; sec. nat. affrs., 1930; ag. col. sec., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov't. dep., Sept., 1932; ch. comnr., N. Territories, 1933.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.)—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V, offr. as asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1916; lieut., M.V.I., Dec., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Raub (Bentong), Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Oct., 1926; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1930; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Dec., 1930; mem., Br. deleg. to L. of N. Opium Conf., Bangkok, Nov., 1931; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1932; sec., Chinese affrs., Malaya; offr., cls. IA, Oct., 1933.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOSEPHS, HECTOR ARCHIBALD, R.A., LL.M. (Trinity Hall, Camb.), K.C. (Jamaica, 1911, Br. Guiana, 1921).—Called to Bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1896; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1907, 1908, 1909, 1910; asst. atty.-gen., 1912; atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1914-24; temp. atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1921 to Apr., 1923; ag. puisne judge, Jamaica, 1924-25; referee of titles, Jamaica, 1924-25; nom.-mem., leg. coun., Jamaica, 1924-25; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. ch. just., 1932; chmn., several select and other comtees, Jamaica and Br. Guiana.

KANNANGARA, EDWARD WILMOT.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; att'd. to Kandy kach., Aug., 1919; att'd. Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, Avissawella, May, 1921; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, Nov., 1924; ditto, Balapitiya, June, 1926; asst. coms'r., stamps, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1930; sec. to min., agr. and lands, Nov., 1931; clk. state coun., and sec., bd. of ministers, June, 1933.

KANTAWALA, MOHAN HARGOVINDAS.—B. 1890; M.A., Cantab.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1915; att. Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, in addl. July, 1915; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, July, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1918; office asst. to gov't. agt., E. Prov., Oct., 1919; pol. mag., Negombo, Jan., 1921; ditto, Panadura, June, 1923; ditto, Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1927; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1928; asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; dist. judge, Negombo, Mar., 1930; pol. mag., Galle, June, 1933; dist. judge, Matara, Nov., 1933; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, May, 1934.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; att'd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1920; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balaputiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; chinm., munic. coun., and addnl. gov't. agt., Kandy, Oct., 1931.

KAUFMANN, JOHN ERNEST.—Forester, Umtata, 1900; clk., conservator of forest's office, Umtata, 1901; senr. clk., ditto, 1902; asst. dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1903; transf'd. to Kokstad, 1912; dist. forest offr., Kokstad, 1912; transf'd. to Knysna, 1925; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, eastern conservancy, 1926; conserv. of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1927; ditto, Midland conservancy, 1929; ditto, Eastern conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., E. inspectorate, 1932.

KAUNTZE, W. H., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., Ch.B. (Vict. Univ.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H. and T.M.; (1st place and med.), D.P.H. (Vict. Univ.)—W. African med. staff, Jan., 1914 to Jan., 1919; served with W. African Forces, Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1916; seconded for serv., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1916 to Jan., 1919; ment. in despa.; pathologist and asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Jan., 1919; dir. laboratory services, Kenya, Apr., 1919; dir. med. and san'y. services, Uganda, Nov., 1932.
KAY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1887; Hong Kong educn. dept., 1913; head, Queen's Coll., 1933; headm., King's Coll., 1934.

KAY-MO'AT, JOHN RICHARD, M.A., Oxon and Hon. Cana Adelaide, M.Sc., M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Bristol.—B. 1881; formerly asst. demonstr., physiology, Oxford Univ.; demonstr., pathology, Bristol Univ. and pathologist to R. Infirmary, Bristol; temp. surg. lieut. to R. Naval Divn., Aug., 1914; ag. surg. lieut. comdr. for anti-malarial work, Sierra Leone, May, 1918; demob. to act temp. as prof. of pathology, Bristol Univ., April, 1919; asst. prin. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Mar., 1921; ag. prin. in 1921, 1922 and 1926-29; prof. of physiology, Nov., 1921; lect. in pharmacology, June, 1923; del. to jubilee, Adelaide Univ., Aug., 1926.

KEAST, MAJOR SYDNEY BANKS, R.E. (R. of O.), M.C. and Bar, French Croix de Guerre.—B. 1884; ed. City of London Schl.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906-13; suptg. san'y. engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1913; Cameroons E.F., 1915-16; B.E.F., 1916-19; senr. pub. health engrnr., 1924; asst., D.P.W., 1926; dep. D.P.W., 1928; D.P.W., 1932; lt.-col. commdg. Gold Coast Defence Force, 1931.

KEATES, CAPT. E. H. O., M.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1896; ed. St. Peter's Schl., York; on mil'y. serv. France and Belgium, 1913-18, Germany, 1919; adminstv. offr., N. Nigeria, 1921; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1925.

KEATINGE, WILLIAM MATBURY, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (Jellett prizeman); B.A., LL.B. (1900); LL.D. (1903); solr., 1904; admstr.-gen., regis. of deeds, and sec., Wafk oman, Zanzibar, Mar., 1915; joint custodian of enemy property and liquidator of enemy firms, Nov., 1915; offl. trustee, 1919; contr., local clearing office, Nov., 1920; registr.-gen., pub. trustee and offl. recr., Kenya, June 1924.

KEEGAN, R. J.—B. 1901; ed. R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Indian Army; served, N.W. Frontier and Iraq; asst. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), New Hebrides, Sept., 1927; ag. acct., Jan. to June, 1928 and Mar. to Nov., 1930; ag. dist. agt., Efate and commdt., constab., Nov., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. commdt., constab., Jan.-June, 1933; ag. asst. res. coms'r., Dec., 1934-June, 1933.
KEEN, KENNETH.—B. 1907; ed. King's Schl., Rochester, Guy's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; cadet, Hong Kong, 1930; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1933.

KEET, JOHAN DIEDERIK MOHR.—Asst. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knysna, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knysna, Apr., 1917; conserv. of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1923; ch. forest offr. (ch., divn. of forest management), 1932.

KEEVIL, L. G. M., M.I.R.S.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1895; ed. privtly.; served with R.A.S.C. (TF) and R.E. Sigs. (TF) in France, German East Africa and Turkey, 1914-20; R.A.R. of O. and capt., Gold Coast Defence Force; engnr. asst., posts and tels. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; asst. tel. engnr., Gold Coast, 1925; tel. engnr., 1929; ag. asst. suptgd. tel. engnr. in 1933 and 1934.

KEIR, ALEXANDER, M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1884; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A. (Aberdeen), 1905; diploma, agr., Cambridge Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1906; inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay training coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schls., Perak, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. confee. on educn., 1923; ag. ch. inspr., English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., in 1923, 1924, 1928 and 1931; inspr., schls., Perak, Sept., 1924; del. for British Malaya to Imp. Educn. Confee., June to July, 1927; inspr., schls., Perak, Apr., 1929.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd Lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1916; Bimbashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan, Aug., 1916; Darfur Expedn. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd ols. mag., 1916; dist. commnr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st ols. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstrn., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1928; asst. dist. commnr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1926; dep. dist. commnr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of the Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1839; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernum. offr., cls. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III., 1925; currency commnr., Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; European judge, Kedah, July, 1933.

KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Seconded from London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd asst. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1924; post. asst. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. D.P.T., June-Oct., 1927; survr. posts and tels., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; ast. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika postal serv., 1935.
KELLY, EDWARD JAMES GLEESON, M.C.—B. 1894; admstve. offr., cls. IV., Nigeria, 1919; ag. Br. consul, Boma, 1929; admstve. serv., cls. II., 1933; ag. res., 1934; admstve. offr., cls. I., 1934.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—B. 1858; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, and Toronto Univ.; called to bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; pres., York Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; chmn., Toronto pub. lib. bd. for 3 years; a gov. of Toronto Univ., 1906-1912; jud., high ct., Ontario, 1911.

KELLY, JOSEPH ANGELO.—B. 1887; ed. O'Brien Coll., Marino, Dublin; R.A.F., France, Belgium and Egypt, 1915-19; qmr., 8th Wing, R.A.F., ment. in desp., 1918; polit. dept., Palestine, 1920; secretariat ast., C.S.O. Gold Coast, 1923; acct., transport dept., 1926.

KELLY, ROBERT ALSOF, C.M.G. (1935), A.S.A.A.—B. 1881; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; elk., inland rev., 1898; S.A. war, 1900-02 (Queen's med., 5 clasps); ast. acct., N. Rhodesia, 1911; ast. audr., 1913; ag. audr., 1914-15 and in 1919; lieut., N.R. Rifles, 1916; ag. adjt., 1917-18; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. treas., 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1925, 1927 and 1928; ag. commr., taxes, 1928; treas. and currency offr., mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gold Coast, 1929.

KELLY, ROBERT WILLIAM CREIGHTON, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.)—B. 1888; med. offr. i/c schuls. and venereal diseases, S.S., Apr., 1923; med. offr., social hygiene, May, 1926; health offr., May, 1927; chief med. offr., social hygiene, S.S. Feb., 1928.
KELVIN-STARK, DAVID.—B. 1905; ed. Taunton; B.A. (Lond.), 1928; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1928; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1931; ast. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1934; snr. ast. col. treas., 1936.
KEMP, ALFRED.—B. 1904; ed. Manchester Cent. High Schl.; matric., 1919; G.P.O. engng. dept., 1920; ast. traffic supt., G.P.O., 1923; traffic supt., G.P.O., 1932; ch. telephone supt., Palestine, 1935.
KEMP, GEN. THE HON. JAN C. G.—M.P., Union of S. Africa since 1920; min. of agr., June, 1924; min. of lands, Jan., 1935.

KEMPE, JOHN ERSKINE.—B. 1888; ed. Radley Coll. and University Coll., Oxford (2nd cl. hon. sch. mod. hist.); B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; ast. dist. offr., Feb., 1915; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Feb., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Pahang, Apr., 1919; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; relief ast. sec. to gov't. F.M.S., Mar., 1927; elk., fedl. coun. in addn., Apr., 1927; fedl. examr., Malaya, Jan., 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Larut, and regar., titles, Perak, 1929; offr., cls. IB, Jan., 1931; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Apr., 1931; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932; ag. Br. res., Pahang, July, 1933; ag. Br. advr., Trengganu, Apr., 1935.
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; elk., Basutoland, 1913; war serv., 1916-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1926.

KENNAN, THOMAS BRERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Collet Court, Lond. and Worcester Cathedral Schl.; ast. asst., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War; commnr., 1929.

KENNEDY, DONALD GILBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Waitaki Boys High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; housemr., Dannevirke High Schl., N.Z., 1920; ast. mast., Grammar Schl., Suva, 1921; headmr., Banaban Schl., Ocean Is., 1922; headmr., Ellice Is. Schl., Vaitupu, 1923; headmr., King George V. Schl., Bairiki, Tarawa, 1931; ag. admstve. offr., Ellice Is. dist., 1932; admstve. offr. and nat. lands commnr., Ellice Is. (conjoint), 1934.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apr., 1917; London matri., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher gr., 1st June, 1928.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., LL.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and solr., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law soc'y., 1924-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schl., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ast. head, sany. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; ast. sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; ast. supt., impts. and expts., 1926; ast. treas., 1927; dep. estate duty commnr., 1931; senr. ast. treas., 1933; dist. offr., S., 1935.

KENNIFF, VICTOR, B.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (AUST.), M.I.M. AND C.R. E.—B. 1895; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; ast. eng., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ast. ast. commnr. of wks., 1927; ag. commnr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, May to Dec., 1927; ast. D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1930-33; ast. D.P.W., Palestine, 1935.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONEY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rly., Dee., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stltmtn. and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1916.
KERR, ALLAN PEARCE, B.A. (Hons.).—B. 1910; ed. Stowe and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1933; asst. dist. commr., 1934.

KERR, ROBERT ASHFORD.—B. 1870; ed. pub. sch., Swellendam; served Matabele war and rebellion and seriously wounded in latter; formerly mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate since 1921.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G. (1929) (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B. 1871; A.D.C. to gov. gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1905-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Elliotson-Macartney), 1913-15; priv. sec. to gov. gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWIN POMEROY, M.C., A.C.S.M., M.I.M.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for mily. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931; warden, mines, Pahang, 1934.

KERWIN, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1889; ed. Sarnia Separate Schl., Sarnia Collegiate and Os- goode Hall; called to bar of Ontario, 1911; K.C., 1928; mem., Ontario parole bd., 1932; just. sup. ct. of Ontario, Sept., 1932; just., sup. ct., Canada, July, 1935.
KEY, WALTER JOHN SMITH, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Dundee High Schl., Dundee Tech. Coll. and St. Andrews Univ.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KIBBLE, EDMUND DAVIN, M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Highgate Schl. and City & Guilds (Engng.) Coll., S. Kensington; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; ex. engnr., P.W.D. F.M.S., 1914; senr. ex. engnr., 1923; attd., govt. town planning dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; state engnr., Pahang, June, 1927; ch. hydraulic engnr., Aug., 1930; state engnr., Pahang, Jan., 1932.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; seconded for mily. serv. 1915 to 1919; sec. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., censu., Kedah, Oct., 1920; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comtec., Kedah, Oct., 1922; attd., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comtec., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., Muar, Sept., 1932; offr., cls. I B, 1934.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDSON, ERNEST O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P., F.R.S. N.Z.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian C.walth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; asst. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927; N.Z. rep. at Empire and internat. meteor. confere., 1936.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd asst. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa and ag. prov. comsnr., Naitsiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; gov't. sec., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Nov., 1922; or. spl. duty as judg. comsnr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqrs. on several occasions; ag.-res. comsnr. on various occasions, 1925-1932; offl. mem., advisory coun., 1927; judg. comsnr. (temp. and provisional), Nov., 1928.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Dobensoch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch) sup. of, S. Africa, 1912; assessor app'td. by admstr., Cape exec. comte., elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; rec'd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."

KIMBER, GUTH. —B. 1906; ed. Perse Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripos, pt. I, 1926; 2nd cls. pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928; temp. ag. prin., 18th Mar., 1932.

KINCAID, PARCY FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Wynberg High Schl.; Cape govt. rly. serv., 1902; transf'd., col. sec.'s dept. (Cape), 1909; clk., dept. of interior, 1925; comsnr. for immigr. and Asiatic affrs., 1933.

KING, CLMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. clk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. to prin. clk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire brig., July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. and supt. fire brigade, Aug., 1919; comsnr. pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 1921; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1925-27.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD, I.S.O. (1932).—Ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. clk., regisr.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from 30th Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 4th July, 1928.
KING, JOHN GEORGE MAYDON.—B. 1908; ed. Oundle Schol. and S. Eastern Agril. Coll., Wye, Kent; dipl. agr.; C.O. Agril. schol., Cambridge, 1929-30; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1931.
KING, LOUIS HERBERT, M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; chf. engrnr. and mantr., elec. wks., Dar-es-Salaam, 1919; chf. elec. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KING, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Seminary, Quebec and Mungrit Coll., Ireland; passed final exam., Law Soc.; ag. offl. recr., pub. trustee and crown solr., Br. Guiana, in 1920, 1921 and 1922; offl. recr., pub. trustee and crown solr., 1923; ag. asst. col. sec. in 1932; ag. col. sec. in 1934 and 1935.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schol.; served in R.F.A., 1916-1920 (coman., Aug., 1916); cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr. cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; asst. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1928; dep. pres., town bd., in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. pros., Johore, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., Aug., 1933; ag. 1st do. (B), S.S., Mar., 1935.

KING, STUART EDGAR.—B. 1898; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; attd. Chinese prot., S'pore, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. V., July, 1924; asst. prot., Chinese and asst. contrlr., lab., S'pore, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV., asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, July, 1928; ditto, Malacca, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III., July, 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Sept., 1933; dep. immigrn. offr., S'pore, Mar., 1935.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schol.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attchd. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; J.P., 1908; asst. emigrn. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; mem., bd. of examrs., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1912; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., various occasions, 1921-29; dir., crim. intell., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar., Oct., 1932 and 1934; inspr.-gen., pol., Apr., 1935.

KING, WILLIAM CYRIL CAMPBELL.—B. 1891; ed. Cranbrook schol.; prob., Ceylon pol., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1914; on active serv., 1917-19; supt., pol., 1921; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., 1931-32; ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Cyprus, Aug., 1933.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; apptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; regis. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confce., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930; prime min., 1935.

KINGDOM, FRANCIS WINDSOR PELHAM.—B. 1890; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate and Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; col. audit dept., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, Feb., 1916; ag. sen. asst. audr. on various occasions; asst. censor, 1918-19; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Apr., 1933.

KINGDON, SIR DONALD, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal asst. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty-gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsnr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—Asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for research, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.
KINLOCH, JOHN BAXTER.—B. 1906; ed. Kirkcaldy High Schol. and Edin. and Oxford Univ.; B.Sc., forestry, Edin., 1927; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1927.

KINNEARD, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.I.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls. certif., Regina Normal Schol., 1912; prin., Earl Grey and Govan. Pub. Schls. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. D., Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932 and 1935.
KINSMAN, CYRIL WILLIAM.—B. 1887; offr. sec. to gov., Victoria and clk., exec. coun., 1930.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darling-
KIRBY, A.C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade res., Mar., 1925; ag. prov. comsnr., June, 1933.

KIRK, JAMES Balfour.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool., Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.), I.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; asst. pol. offr., Hai, 1918-19; civ. surg., Arabian, 1919; asst. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., Mauritius, 1922; dir., med. and health dept., 1927; author of "Pub. Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Hints on Equipment and Health for intending Residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.

KIRKHAM, G.H.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Infy. (Overseas Brig.) 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in desps., France, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; King's pol. med.; dep. comsnr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918; comsnr., 1929.

KIRKPATRICK, Thomas Winfrid, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Colony, 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Research Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

KIRSOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; comptr., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924-31; treas. and comptr., cust., Jan., 1932; comsnr., cust., Kenya-Uganda, 1934.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY—Jun. elk., col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., Jan., 1907.
KIRWAN, Sir JOHN Waters, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B.1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Australia since 1908; chmn. of comtees., 1923-26; pres., leg. court, 1926.

KITCHING, A.E.—Ed. Oundle Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. asst. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; asst. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affrs., Apr. to Dec., 1931; dep. prov. comsnr., Apr., 1932; prov. comsnr., 1934.

KITCHING, GEOFFREY Charles, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. Oundle Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. polit. offr., Iraq, 1920; miny. of int., Iraq, 1920-39; dep. dir., development, Palestine, 1931; govt. secy., St. Helena, 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Camb.; surrey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1924; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officg. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June and Dec., 1932; supt., surveys, Johore, Mar., 1933.

KITTERMMASTER, Sir Harold Baxter, K.C.M.G. (1936), K.R.E (1928), C.M.G. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1902-07; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comsnr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; secy. to adminstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Honduras, 1931; do., Nyasaland Prot., 1934.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory med.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CURTHOSS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1880; ed. Sydney Gram. Schl., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agrl. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supt., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; regis. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in lands comsnr., 1920 and 1921; ag. res. comsnr. during 1920; comsnr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engrnr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; comsnr. of lands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924; chmn., mining bd., 1927; attended Imp. Survey Confce. and Internat. Geog. Confce., 1928.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1854; ed. Grenada Grammar Schl.; 5th clk., treasury, Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G.K.—B.1891; ed. Radley Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cls. hons., sohl. of jurisprudence), 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ment. in desps., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., 1920; ag. res. mag., Mombasa, 1923; res. mag., Uganda, 1925; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; ag. judge, Zanzibar, for various periods, 1927-35; ag. atty-gen., 1930 and 1933; edited Zanzibar Law Repts., Vol. IV.

KNOLLYS, Lancelot Erskine.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll. and S.E. Agric Coll., Wye, Kent (dip. agric.); asst. supt., cotton cult., Uganda, 1909; asst. dist. comanr., 1914; 1st grade admin. offr., 1925; senr. asst. sec., 1931; asst. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; ag. dep. ch., sec., various periods, 1932-35.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schl. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; ohmn., agric. experiments comtee., 1916; ohmn. food supply comtee., 1917; ag. gvt. entomologist (conjoint), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.

KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1877; joined Trinidad govt. serv., 1898; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, Dec., 1925; do., St. Patrick-Victoria, May, 1933.

KOLLER, Thurstan William, A.S.A., C.A. (S.A.), ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; elk., O.F.S. civ. serv., July, 1900; senr. inspr., Union audit dept., Feb., 1929; chf. inspr., rev., do., Sept., 1931; provi. audir., Cape Prov., Dec., 1932.

KOTZE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, KT. BACH. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the comsrs. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; ohmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; reed. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. couns., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admstr. S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dists. ct., 1904; ohmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of C. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisane judge, C. of G. H. Provl. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate divn., June, 1922; ret., 1932.

KOTZE, PETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape). Dip. For. (Oxon.).—Asst. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Concordia, 1914; forest offr., Storms River, 1918; ditto, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forests, Midland conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., Midland inspectorate, 1932.

KOTZE, SIR ROBT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schi. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.: asst. engrnr. and consulting engrnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; gvt. mining engrnr., Transvaal, Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chan., Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., Nov., 1926.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAGOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1906; advoc., sup. et., S. Africa and high et., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. gvt., Witwatersrand, 1900; ohmn., mining regns. coman., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; ohmn., hoop. inquiry coman., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation coman.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ot., Transvaal prov. divn., Apr., 1923; judge-pres., O.F.S. prov. divn., 1933.

KREFT, HERMANN HEINRICH GERHARD, (B.A.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal Coll., Capetown and S.A. Coll., Capetown; inspr., schls., Cape Prov., 1916; dir., educn., S.W. Africa, 1923; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1935; sec., Transvaal educn. divn., 1936.
KROGH, ANDJARLOUIS, M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1887; govt. ms., pftr., Dec., 1920; dist. med. offr., Tobago, 1922.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-19; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., exec. comtee., nat. industrial coun. of the printing and newspaper industry of S. Africa.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. comanr. since 1925; pub. debt. comanr. since 1928; a comanr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, GEORG ADOLF CARL, (M.A.).—B. 1892; ed. Normal Coll., Johannesburg, Transvaal Univ. Coll. and Univ. of S. Africa (B.A.), Stellenbosch Univ. (M.A.); teacher, Transvaal, 1912; prin., Transvaal, 1921; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1927; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1933; dir., educn., Transvaal, 1934.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F.G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stoiberg Gedenk Schi., 1916-24; organising inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. registr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; ohmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comens. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admstr. and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admtd., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schi., Norwich, and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripes; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyassaland Prot., 1930.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., O.B.E. (1935), LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920; dir., educn., Nov., 1933.

LANG, HON. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1916).—B. 1852; mem. of H. of R., New Zealand, 1893-1905 and 1906-22; chmn. of comtess., Aug., 1912 to June, 1913; speaker from June, 1913 to 1922; M.L.C., 1921-31.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt.; premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and 1930-32.

LANGLEY, CARLETON GEORGE, K.C. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schl.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on mily. serv., Malta, France and Ireland, 1914-21; prte. practice, 1921-30; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1931; comsrr., sup. ct., J.P.; ag. puisne judge, 1931-3; ag. col. sec., 1934-35; ag. admstr., Antigua, 1935.

LANKTREE, CHARLES JOSEPH DANIEL, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inns, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; admstr. sec. to dir. of med. and sanv. serv., Oct., 1925; addnl. asst. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; admstr. sec. to dir. elec. undertakings, July, 1928; astt. attit. offr., Jan., 1932; ag. man., elec. undertakings, Apr., 1933; addnl. man., do., July, 1934; astt. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1934.

LANSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—Clt., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Alwal N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. chf. clk. to sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. astt. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sent., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; adtnl. legal advr., July, 1907; prof. astt., atty.-gen.'s off., Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advr., dep. of justice Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law advr., 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., Oct., 1931.

LARTER, LESLIE N. H., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—Ed. Imp. Coll. (Lond.), schol. and medalist, graduated (1st cls. hon.) 1929; col. agril. schol., Fitzwilliam House, Camb. and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1930-32; geneticist, Jamaica, Nov., 1932.

LASCELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod., hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; sup. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divnlt. ct., sup. ct., 1923.

LATHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schl., Cape Town; B.Sc. (agric.) Univ. of S.A., 1922; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agril. offr., 1930.
LATHEM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

LATHAM, Rt. HON. SIR JOHN GREGG, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schl., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confce., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter- allied comm. on Czechoslovak affrs. at confce.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confce., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affrs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932; leader, goodwill mission to Japan, 1934; resigned from parl't., 1934; ch. just., high ct., 1935.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.
LAURIE, WILLIAM, M.Inst.M., M.Cy.E.—B. 1890; astt. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1920; ag. exec. engrnr., Johore, Jan., 1925; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; do., Kedah, June, 1933.

LAVILLE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripes, B.A., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S. Sttlmtn., Dec., 1912; attd. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet; supv. officer, cls. V., Jan., 1916; lent to govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offg. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supv. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; offg. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, July, 1920; offg. collr., income tax, Singapore, Apr., 1922; mag. and astt. regisr., sup. cls. Seremban, Dec., 1923; D.P.P., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regisr., sup. ct., Sp'ore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II., Dce., 1929; offg. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. locl advr. and judl. comsrr., Kelantan, June, 1932; offr., cls. IB., Nov., 1933; dist. judge, snore, Feb., 1935.
LAW, ALFRED NOEL, M.C.—B. 1895; mil. ser., 1915-18; inspr., dist. admstrn., Palestine, 1920; ag. astt. dist. comsrr. on various occasions, 1925-30, and once as dist. comsrr.; asst. sec., 1932.

LAW, CHARLES EWAN.—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1905; Burma bar, 1905; staff capt. (tempy. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res., of offs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem., legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1923; ag. judge, Kingston ct. on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 1925 and 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comttee. of res. mags., June, 1926; mem., comanr. of enquiry into admistr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just., from May, 1932 to Mar., 1933; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; mem., comanr. of enquiry into admistr. of just. in crim. matters in E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. just., Zanzibar, Mar., 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa; chmn., agrl. indebtedness comanr., Aug., 1934.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888 asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem., bd. of examns., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; re-apptd. mem., bd. of examns., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban districts in addn., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.S.J. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep.-adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; administr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. administrator, Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high comanr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.
LAWLOR, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—B. 1880; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll. Feb., 1908; asst. inspr., schls., Natal, May, 1910; inspr. of schls., Apr., 1912; ch. inspr., schls., Aug., 1931.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR SIR ARTHUR SALISBURY, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931), D.S.O.—Major, late reserve of officers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desps.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1903-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desps.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. comanr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in desps.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. consul., Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; admstr. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-32; sec. to gov't., 1926; comanr. and commdr.-in-ch., June, 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., May, 1935.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES TREVOR, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1876; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Sch.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-05; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. asst. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. asst. prov. comanr., Cent. Prov., 1912; pte. sec. to gov't., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st asst. sec., S. Prov., 1916; addl., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prin. asst. sec., 1921; comanr., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1923; ag. lieut.-gov'r., S. Prov., 1927; staff grade, 1928; admstr. of col., 1930.

LAWSON, HON. SIR HARRY SUTHERLAND WIGHTMAN, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1875; M.L.A. for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat continuously in State Parlmt. from 1899; comanr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr. gen. and min. of public instrn., premier and atty.-gen. 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth senator for Victoria.
LAWSON, R. A.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., Apr., 1919.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schl., Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. acct., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to comanr. for lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; asst. registr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (recd. thanks G.O.C.L. of C.); registr. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. registr., crown lands, 1920; dep. registr.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audlr., mem., bl. of comanrs., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; comanr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1914; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; on mil'y serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., secretariat, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1923; office asst. to dir., med. and sani. services, Apr., 1929; asst. sttlmt. offr., Jan., 1930; asst. gov't. agt., Badulla, Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., 1932.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed. High Schl., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.Sc. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student, 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch., office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and asst. forester; ch. forester, comanr. of conservn. and bl. of rly. comanrs., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. comanrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively, pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engnr.

LECHMEREGUPPY, GARRETH EVERARD.—B. 1874; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, May, 1895; seconded for special serv. to water authority and sewerage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of imgrts. in 1909, 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and librnr., col. secretariat, Trinidad, July, 1915; sec. income tax comttee., estd. comttee., tenders comttee., and civ. serv. comttee.; J.P. for the col. and Tobago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, Dec., 1920; sub.-intend., crown lands, Jan., 1925.

LECHMEREGUPPY, P. (Junn.).—Super-numerary clk., audit offr., Trinidad, 1889; 4th clk., sub-treasy., San Fernando, Jan., 1892; landing waiter, customs, May, 1894; clk., col. treasy., Mar., 1898; acted as warden, Tacarigua, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-recr., postmaster, collr. of cust. and harbr. mat., Tobago, Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water nipa of Trinidad for British Museum.
LECK, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; colliery man, Nigeria, 1914.

LECKIE, Capt. Peter Martin, L.S.(Vict.) B. 1890; survr., dept. lands and surveys, Dec., 1907; staff survr., May, 1913; war survr., Sept., 1915-18; survr., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1920; do., Kedah, Apr., 1925; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; do., S'pore, Mar., 1929; do., Kelantan, May, 1932.

LE CONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser dependencies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regisr., sup. ct., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law consnr., 1914; war survr. in France and Macedonia, 1916-19; food survr., May, 1920; puisne judge, July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1933.

LEDEBOER, CLAUD MARIUS.—Served in S. African Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. Prot. police, from 1907; sub-insp. Bech. Prot. police, 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed Cape civ. surv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade Sechuana exam.; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1919; ag. res. mag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape civ. surv. higher law exam., 1923; ag. asst. res. comantr., 1930.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripus, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripus, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyasaland adminstr., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvtv. sec. to S. of S. for theCols., 4th Aug., 1933.

LEE, HARRIE BERTIK, D.S.O., M.C., M.B., B.S. (hons.) Melbourne, D.T.M. and H., Eng.; certif. London S.T.M. (with distinc.), B. 1888; ed. Sydney and Melbourne Univ.; four years serv. with A.I.F. in field as R.M.O.; field ambulance offr. and D.A.D.M.S., 1st Australian Divn., 1914-18; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; specialist, Nigeria, Aug., 1933.

LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. surv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; asst. P.M.G., 1932; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, Feb., 1934; asst. dist. offr., North, Jan., 1935; pvtv. sec. to O.A.G., May, 1935.
LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; asst. engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engrnr., 1927; supt. engrnr., June, 1933.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), Kt. BACH (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.
LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LEELE.—B. 1893; regisr. clk., Western Pacific high comsnr., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1920; sec. to res. comsnr., 1922; sec. to govt., 1931.

LEES, ABBEY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Milly Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., Iraq (air staff intell., spec. surv.) June, 1924; admistr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvtv. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admistr. offr., Palestine, 1929.

LEESE, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932).—Asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transf'd. to treasy. dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior asst. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921; and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925 and from Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927; gov.'s offr. on various occasions in 1926 and 1927; treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1928.

LEGGE, CHARLES ARTHUR LIONEL.—B. 1879; clerical astt., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted captn., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHERWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty.-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams., 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922; passed govt. Hindi exam.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH.—B. 1891; on milv. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; attd., Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; attd. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office astt. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1928; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Matara, Dec., 1929; astt. govt. agt., Matara, Feb., 1930; admistr. secy., med. dept., and sec. to min. of health, Sept., 1932; ag. tea expt. contr., Dec., 1934.

LEMAIRE, ERNEST JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1874; ed. Sherbrooke (Que.), Coll.; ent. privy coun. office for Canada, Jan., 1894; priv. sec., prime min., 1904-11; post office dept., 1912-23; clk. of privy coun. for Canada, Aug., 1923.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on milv. surv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. capt., Sch. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; col. audit dept., Oct., 1921; astt. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; senr. astt. audr., Kenya, 1933.

LENDRUM, SAMUEL JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. St. Luke, Cork, and pte. tuition; clk., Imp. milv. rly., S. Africa, 1900; conf. clk., govt. comsnr.'s office, 1901; clk., Transvaal pol., 1902; ag. ch. clk., do. pol., 1903-8; supt. clk. do. pol., 1908; sub-inspr., S.A. pol., 1913; inspr. do. pol., 1918; senr. inspr., 1928; dep. comsnr., 1929; ch. dep. comsnr., 1933.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. surv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st clc., clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk's office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., Apr., 1921; astt. sec., Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from July-Dec., 1925; P.M.G., Apr., 1926.

LE ROUX, JONATHAN JOHAN DU PRÉ.—B. 1900; ed., B.H.S., Paarl, Univ. Capetown, Royal Coll., Edin., and Univ., Witwatersrand; L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Wit.), 1933; med. offr., leper inst., Pretoria, Nov., 1927; med. supt., Jan., 1933.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; asst. engnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; divnl. engnr., Apr., 1933; ag. engnr.-in-ch., 1932 and 1934.

LESTER, A. R., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.)—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-22; mily. serv., India and Persia, 1918-22; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch., sp. sociological investgn., Kahama, 1927-31; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agr.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schl., Capetown; B.Sc. (agr.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, Sir GORDON JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Schl., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Univs.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affras., Nov., 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933; do., Leeward Is., 1935.
LEVINKIND, LEOPOLD.—B. 1892; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.Se. (engnrg.); corp., C.P. Rifles, S.W. Africa campaign; ent. dept., irrig., Union of S. Africa, 1917; hydrographic surv., 1934.

LEWRY, MAJOR A.W., M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—Ed. St. Paul's and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; Middlesex Regt., European War, 1914-18; Gallipoli, Egypt, Flanders, Palestine, severely wounded; pol. mag., Gambia, Dec., 1929; aide-de-camp and pvt. sec., Gambia, Jan.-June, 1930; ag. legal adviser, and prov. mem., exec. and leg. couns. on several occasions; crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1932.

LEWIN, CLAUD JOHN, M.C., B.Sc.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ.
