Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; sup't., agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; senr. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir., agr., July, 1933; temp. M.L.C., 1935.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E. —B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, BERTRAM.—B. 1887; Br. P.O., sect's office, C. and A.G.'s dept. and London & prov. audit, 1903-17; asst. acctnt., dep. ch. acctnt., Kenya and Uganda, P. and T., 1917-21; ch. acctnt., Palestine, 1924.
LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; govt. printer, Feb., 1933.

LEWIS, ERNEST HARRY, C.B.E., C.A. (S.A.).—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; on war ser. (S.A.), 1899-1900; 1st grade clk., audit dept., Transvaal, 1902; acctnt., educn. dept., 1907; ch. clk. and acctnt., Transvaal prov. admin., 1910; ch. paym. and acctnt., S. African pol., 1910; asst. finan. sec., defence dept., 1916; finan. under sec., 1919; finan. advr., S. African pol., 1929.
LEWIS, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1907; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man and Keble Coll., Oxford (B.A.); asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnr.; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahrû, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1934.
LEWIS, MALCOLM STUART.—B. 1889; H.A.C., 1914-16; R.G.A., 1917-19; treasy. asst., Nigeria, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1934.

LEWIS, WILLIAM EDWIN.—B. 1886; ed. West Monmouthshire Grammar Schl.; traffic asst., Gold Coast rly., Aug., 1911; seconded for war serv., Cameroons, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Apr., 1921; traffic man., Feb., 1929.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., D.M., R.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1894; schol. student, Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. pathologist I, Mar., 1928, June, 1929, and May, 1931; pathologist II, Jan., 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLARY, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; comen. in R. Engnr., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C., to inspnr. gen. of fortifications and engnr., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical scen., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestinesurvey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATES.—B. 1898; clk. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; 1st clk. treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and colnr. cust., man., savings bank, rec. of wrecks and regist., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, Sept., 1930; ag. dep. treas. in 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Nov., 1935.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE, Pay-Lieut. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.)—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admity. war staff (allied naval conv.), 1918-19; staff, comdr-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; "1914-15 Star," Brit. War Med., Victory Med.; librarian, col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Oct., 1920; office asst., col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Jan., 1922; offr.-in-ch., records revision, in addn., Feb., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., S'pore, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. asst., do., S.S., June, 1933.

LIESCHING, PERCIVAL, C.M.G. (1932)—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; R.A. 1920; pvt., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Afr., 1916; ment. in desps., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 9th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to perm. under see for Dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin. affrs., 1927; prin. 1st Apr., 1929; accompanied U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1927, to signature of Pact of Paris, 1928 and to Comp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932; seconded sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1928-32 and as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high commr. for U.K., Aug.-Dec., 1933.

LIGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office ast. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Jan., 1929; asst. attnt. offr., Jan., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933; do., Kalutara, Apr., 1934.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd olk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec. bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. comsnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; olk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., on various occasions, 1929-35.
LILLEY, CYRIL CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; capt., Worcestershire Regt.; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
LILLEY, RONALD, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Bedford and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audit dept., Apr., 1930; asst. audr., Tanganyika, July, 1930.

LINDON, NORMAN LIDSTONE.—B. 1892; ed. Bristol Cathedral schl., and Christ's Hosp.; pol. probt., S.S., 1912; asst. supt. pol., S'pore, Mar., 1917; dist. offr., Christmas Is., 1921; comdt., pol., depot, S'pore, 1929; dep. commr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1929; comdt., pol., depot, K. Lumpur, 1931; ch. off. pol., Malacca, July, 1932.

LINDOP, REGINALD ALEXANDER ERKINE.—B. 1897; war serv., 1915-19; Leeward Is. pol. force, 1919-24; dep. impr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1924; ag. impr.-gen., pol. and supt., fire brig., various periods, 1925-34.

LINDOW, ERIC DRAFIELD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond., certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Christ's Coll. Blackheath, Lond. Univ., King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1923; do., T.T.S. hosp., Dec., 1923, Oct., 1928 and Apr., 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1928 and Apr., 1929; ag. suppy, ch. med. offr., A. Penang, Dec., 1934.

LINDSAY, JAMES.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian sanatorium for consumption; late clinical asst., Royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and diseases of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M.S., Apr., 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921.

LINDSELL, HAROLD OFFLEY.—B. 1884; ed. at Bradfield Coll. (exhibitioner) and All Souls' Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1909; dist. offr., 1917; res., 1925 res., Kano, 1929.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripods, part I, cls. I, div. 2; called to bar, 1930; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. regisr.-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1916; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1915 to 1916; ag. asst. P.G., 1916; ag. asst. supt., imports and exports dept., 1918; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., 1923; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st pol. mag., on various occasions, 1922-30; ag. puisne judge, 1931 and 1933; ag. asst. atty.-gen., Feb., 1932; puisne judge, 1934.

LINEHAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. schol. and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. hons., class., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Jan.-April, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1928; ag. asst. Br. advr., supt., lands and regisr., titles, Kelantan, Feb., 1931; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1933; dist. offr., Seremban, Jan., 1934; ag. asst. advr., Muar, Mar., 1935.

LINFORD, GEOFFREY BESIDE.—B. 1891; pol. prob., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; asst. commr., pol., June, 1918; 1st asst. commr., pol., Ipoh, Jan., 1923; paymaster, Jan., 1925; regisr., criminals, S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1925.

LINNELL, CAPT. THOMAS FORESTER, R.E. (res. of offr.), A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Denstone Coll., 1899-1904; articled pupil, civ. engrnr., 1905-09; lieut., Royal Engineers, S.R. of O., 1910; temp. asst. engnr., P.W.D., Br. E. Africa, 1911; transfd. to Uganda rly., Apr., 1914; war serv. on Uganda rly. and in German E. Africa, 1915-18; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen Serv. meda.; dist. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rlys., June, 1924.

LINTON, REGINALD DUKK, B.Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1904; ed. Mountjoy Schol., Dublin, Belfast Univ. (B. Agr.) (distinct.), 1927, Oxford Univ. and Imp. Coll., Trop. Agr. (A.I.C.T.A.), 1929; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929.

LISSAMAN, ARTHUR EVELYN, F.S.I., M.Inst. M. & Cy. E.—B. 1897; ed. Luton Modern Schl. and Coll., Estate Management, Lond.; mil. serv., R.E.'s, 1915-19; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; engrnr., 1923; ag. ex. engrnr., Feb.-Nov., 1935.

LIVERPOOL, EARL OF, Rt. HON. ARTHUR FOLJAMBE, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912), M.V.O., (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Hasselbech.—B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; capt., 1897; staff capt., Dublin district, July, 1900 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906-08; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; governor-general of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LIVINGSTON, WILLIAM, A.M.Inst.C.E., P.A.S.Inst.—B. 1890; ed. Schl. of Science & Art, Kirkaldy and Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; war ser., Gallipoli, Egypt and France, 1914-19; asst. dist. engrnr., Trinidad, 1919; dist. engrnr., 1920; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, 1924; seconded for duty on reconnaissance survey to interior, Br. Guiana, Mar.-Aug., 1927; D.P.W., Bermuda, Sept., 1929.

LLIWELLYN, JOHN LIONEL BRETHERTON LLWELLYN, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915-June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.
LOYD, EDGAR BRINLEY.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land asst., 1920; ag. regisr., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schol. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in desps., 1917; apptd. under re-construction scheme, ass't. prin., min'y. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as ass't. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., D.O. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded for serv. as sec. to Palestine comm., 1929.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; puisne judge, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925, and from July, 1928; puisne judge, sup. ct., Nigeria, 1930; ag. ch. just. July-Oct., 1933.

LLOYD, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Hampden Gunnery Schol.; asst. acctnt., open lines, Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; transf'd. to adm'tive. ser., Apr., 1917; adm'tive. offr., cls. I, Dec., 1932.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newf., 1891; editor, "Evening Telegram" 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., becoming attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., B.A., Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tombridge and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Dublin Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in desps.; dep. registr., sup. ct., Kenya, Oct., 1920; registr., July, 1921; asst. atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1925, 1926, 1927, 1929 and 1932; solr.-gen., Palestine, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., in 1933 and 1934.

LOCK, FRDERICK JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. King's Schol., Peterboro and Quernmore Coll., Upper Norwood; temp. acct., Nyasaland, July, 1915; acct., July, 1918; asst. treas., Feb., 1923; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas., in 1922, 1923, 1924, 1932-33; ag. treas., Somaliland, Apr. to Sept., 1932; dep. treas., Nyasaland, Apr., 1933; chmn., nat. civ. ser. bd., 1933-34.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, maths. tripes, 1898; 1st cls. mech. sciences tripes, 1899; ent. C.A. as tech. asst., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1913.

LOCK, GEORGE WINSLOW, N.D.A., N.D.D.—Dip. Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Latymer Schol., London; Mid Agril. Coll.; econ. div., M.A., I.C.T.A., 1929; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSDALE, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in desps.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. asst. treas., May, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1923; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; treas., Apr., 1933.
LOCKHART, F. R., M.B., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1897; mil'y. serv., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924.

LOCKHART, SIR JAMES HALDANE STEWART, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1898); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.; LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.),—B. 1858; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; cadet Hong Kong 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; asst. col. sec. and asst. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. registr. gen., 1884 and 1885; registr.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chmn., bd. of exams. in Chinese, 1891; ditto governg. body Queen's Coll., dep. registr., sup. ct. in prize, Nov., 1894; 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and registr.-gen., Mar., 1895; sp. comnr. to rept. on extension of bndry. of H. Kong, 1898; rep. of Gt. Br. to delimitate bndry's. for both of which recd. thanks of S. of S., 1899; rector of coll. of med. for Chinese, 1895-1902; comnr. of Weihaiwei, Jan., 1902; assumed govt., May, 1902; ret., 1920.

LOCKHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—B. 1899; copyist, adm'tive. office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd clk., registr. office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and harmbr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treas., 1917; is also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. bd. of health; govt. trade agt. to inquire into and rep. on conditions re W.I. labourers at Santo Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, May, 1920; ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. dept., July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force; ag. 1st clk., treas., May-Sept., 1922; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1922; income tax comnr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.

LOCKHART-SMITH, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham; 1st clk., Kowloon Magistracy, Hong Kong, July, 1924; att'd. land office, Nov., 1926; admitted solr., sup. ct., Eng., Nov., 1932; dep. O.R. and reg. tr. marks in addn., Aug.-Sept., 1933; O.R. and reg. tr. marks, Sept., 1933-Apr., 1934; asst. crown solr., Jan., 1934; asst. land offr., Aug., 1935.
LOCKIE, JAMES REACH.—B. 1904; ed. Campbeltown and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (forestry 1st cls. hon.); asst. conservtr., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1926.

LOGAN, SIR EWEN REGINALD, Kt., Bach. (1928), J.P.—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1899; joined Middlx. and N. London sess. and cent. crin. ct., 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 65th squad., I.Y., 1901; Queen's medal and four clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902 to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1905; chief just., Seychelles, 1914; adm'tive. govt. from July, 1916 to Mar., 1917 and from May to Oct., 1918; drafted in conjunction with Alfred Gellé, Esq., barrister-at-law, code of civil procedure for Seychelles; recd. thanks of sec. of state; puisne judge, G. Coast, June, 1920, with seniority from Mar., 1920; senr. puisne judge, Dec., 1922; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and Feb. to Mar., 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn., bd. of educn., Aug., 1925; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, May, 1927; ret., June, 1931; J.P., County of London, 1935.

LOGAN, WILLIAM MARSTON, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Hulmeexhbr., Bridgeman prizeman, B.A.); asst. dist. comnr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., cent. contrv. of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., unattached list, 1915-17; ag. dist. comnr., 1918 and Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sept. to Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; lit grade adm'tive. offr., June, 1924; sen. asst. sec.

LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); int nat. sci. tripos, 1910; hist. tripos, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cls. V, 1914; mily. serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouc. Reg.; hfr., cls. IV, 1919; sec to res., Pahang, 1920; hfr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts., S.S. and F.M.S. and dep. supervisor, rubber F.M.S., Nov., 1924; cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. finan. comnr. and andr.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1930; sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930; cls. IB, Apr., 1931; ax. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; ag. Br. res., N. Sembinoh, June, 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1934; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1934.
LONEY, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1871; ed. Timaru Pub. Schl.; ent. N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1895; supt., 1922; govt. printer, 1933.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt, Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1918; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Jigjiga, Sept., 1927; consul, Oct., 1930.
LOOS, W.T.—B. 1886; apptd. cls. III., Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1934; office ast., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1934.

LORD, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1898; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Schl., Clifton; mily. serv. with H.A.C. and R.A.F., 1916-18; offr. Imp. cust. and excise, Jan., 1920; astt. to comnr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Oct., 1924; senr. collr. of cust., Jan., 1935, ag. dep. comnr., cust., on various occasions since 1930.
LORD, GEORGE ERIC LUDWIG, B.A.—B. 1907; ed. Felsted Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; col. andit dept., June, 1930; astt. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1930; do., Palestine, 1936.

LORD, LINTOT-COL. J., E.C., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. serv., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. Tas. A.I.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov.-gen.; comnr. of pol., Tasmania.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Chatham, New Brunswick; astt. audr.-gen., 1902; audr.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schl. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa; B.A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; el. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African comnr. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high comnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, Mar., 1929; min. pleni-potentiary, Washington, Oct., 1929; do., Rome, Nov., 1933; do., Paris, 1934.
LOUW, JAN DANIEL, B.A.—B.1888; ed. Victoria Coll. and Univ., Stellenbosch; educ. serv., Cape Prov., 1912-1930; govt. translator, Pretoria 1930.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vety. offr., May to Dec., 1926; ag. dir., vety. serv., in 1932 and 1933; ag. dep. dir., do., in 1933.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCETTE, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1922; attd., col. sec's office, Nov., 1922; pvt. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Feb., 1929; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1931; astt. regis., co-op. socs., Nov., 1931; astt. govt. ast., Kegalle, Oct., 1932; regis., co-op. socs., Oct., 1934.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Temp., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; col. sec's office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Stlitms, 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in desps.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; astt. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in med. Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929; chmn., oil and water bd., 1931; puisne judge, Kenya, 1931; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec. Westmoreland hurricane relief comnr., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease comnr., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. clk., col. secretariat, 1929; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders), 1915-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W. O. staff, 1919; edr. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUCKMAN, CYRIL HERBERT MORTON.—B. 1881; clk., cust., Johannesburg, 1903; 1st grade clk., Capetown, 1912; senr. clk., 1915; 2nd grade prin. clk., Pretoria, 1922; 1st grade ditto, 1926; acctnt., Pretoria, 1930; ch. clk., 1933; ch. inspr., 1933.

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M.Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apr., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; astt. comnr., excise, July, 1923; astt. dist. offr., Nov., 1926; offr., cls. I., grade II, 1933; govt. ast., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEIGHTON LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st comsn., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880; medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, “Tofrek” Khedive’s Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; admnstd. Uganda; went to Borgu in cond. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apr., 1895. Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; appptd. comsnr., and comit., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comsnr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gen.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates comsnr. since 1922; author of “The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa” and other works.

LUIGG, HARRY CAMP.—B. 1882; ed. Greytown; clk. and Zulu interp., mag.’s office, Bulwer, Dec., 1890; Msinga, Jan., 1901; Greytown, Feb., 1902; clk., civ. comtn., Zululand, Aug., 1903; native affrs., Natal, Nov., 1903; Zulu interp. and asst. registr., native high ct., July, 1908; sworn Zulu interp., sup. ct., Aug., 1909; sen. clk. and asst. mag., Durban, Dec., 1916; mag., Ndudwe, Dec., 1918; Ingwavuma, May, 1920; Mapumulo, Aug., 1921; 2nd grade mag., Richmond, Apr., 1923; 1st grade mag., Verulam July, 1928; senr. mag., Verulam, Aug., 1930; pres., native appl. ct., Natal and Transvaal, Oct., 1932; ch. native comsnr., Natal, May, 1933.

LUKE, Sir HARRY CHARLES, Kt. Bach. (1933), C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. med. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; B. Litt., 1919; priv. sec. to the govr. of Sierra Leone 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the govr. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comsnr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Goold-Adams, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of exec. comtn., Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comsnr., Paphos, 1917; comsnr., Famagusta, 1918; sec., Cyprus defence comte., Aug., 1914; served in H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; political offr. to Admirals Lord Wemyss and Christian and govt. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to June, 1916; (lieut.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.); recd. thanks of admv.; Italian med. for mily. valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comsnr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; asst. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; mem. of comsnr. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffa disturbances, 1921, and finan. comsnr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; col. sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. gov. on four occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; lieut. gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hon.); author of “A Bibliography of Sierra Leone,” 1910 and 1925; “Cyprus under the Turks,” 1921; joint editor, “Handbook of Cyprus,” and “Handbook of Palestine and Transjordan.”

LUKE, S. E. V., B.A., 1927.—B. 1905; ed. St. George’s, Harpenden and Oxford, Bracegirdle exhib., Wadham Coll., 1924; 2nd cls. med. hist., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. inspr., taxes (inland rev.), 1927; apptd. after further compet. exam., junr. clk., House of Commons, Feb., 1930; transf. to C. O. as asst. prin., Sept., 1930; joint sec., col. survery comte., 1931-33; visited Br. Hond. as sec. to finan. comsnr. (Sir A. Pim), 1933; asst. pvt. sec. to Sir P. Cunliffe-Lister (now Viscount Swinton) 7th Apr., 1934, to Mr. Malcolm MacDonald, 7th June, 1935; to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 27th Nov., 1935; seconded to Palestine adminstr., 4th Jan., 1936.

LUMLEY, JAMES MADDY, L.S.O. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; served R.H.A., 1895-1901; S. African war, 1899-1902; wounded; Queen’s medal, four clasps; King’s medal, two clasps; S. African constab., 1901-08; pub. pros., and just. of the peace, Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1904-08; asst. supt. of pol., E.A.P., 1909; served in E. Africa, 1914-18; King’s pol. medal, 1919; supt., pol., Kenya, 1919; ag. asst. comsnr., Kenya pol., various periods, 1920-24; asst. comsnr., pol., 1925; dep. comsnr., pol., 1926; ag. comsnr., pol., 1927.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LUPPRIAN, ERNEST VICTOR, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Dip. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Bishop Currier Gram. Schl. and St. Mary’s Coll., Madras; Baldwin High Schl. and Aldham Coll., Bangalore Univ., Madras; med. coll., Madras; St. Mary’s hosp. med. schl.; L.S.T.M. and Hygiene; med. offr., S’pore. port health, Apr., 1923; do., Mlym. emigr. depots, India, July, 1923; med. offr., P. Wellesley, Mar., 1928 and Jan., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, June, 1932.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland adminstr., Sept., 1902; asst. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. secy., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927.

LYALL-GRANT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, Kt. Bach. (1934), M.A. (Aberdeen), L.L.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; as. judge, high ct., 1909, 1910, 1912 and 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising comsn., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prots. defence O. in C., 1916-19; judge advoc. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty.-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem., exec. audleg. councils; puisne just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Jamaica, 1932.

LYNCH, FRANCIS CHRISTOPHER CHISHOLM.—B. 1884; ed. at pub. schls., priv. tuition, McGill Univ.; assoc., Eng. Inst., Canada; assoc. mem., Town Planning Inst. of Canada; gen. engnr., 1902-05; apptd. to interior dept., 1906; asst. supt., rly. lands br., 1911; supt., nat. resources intell. br., dept. of int. Can., Dec., 1911.
LYNDE, WALTER MIGHELLS, F.S.I. (Eng.)—R. 1883; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; quantity survr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1910; in charge sp. wk., Kabete, 1918; contracts offr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1923; ag. D.F.W., 1926.

LYONS, Rt. Hon. JOSEPH ALOYNIUS.—B.1879; ed. State and convent schls.; M.H.A., Tasmania, 1909-29; treas., min. for educn. and min. for mines, 1914-16; premier, treas. and min. for rly., 1923-24; premier, treas. and min. for mines, 1924-27; premier and treas., 1927-28; el. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; P.M.G. and min. for wks. and rly., 1929; prime min. of the C'wealth, 1932.

MAARTENSZ, LEWIS MATTHEW.—B. 1876; barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple; addtnl. crown counsel, Ceylon, Apr., 1903; crown counsel, July, 1906; addtnl. dist. judge, Kandy, Oct., 1910; ditto, Colombo, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apr. to Oct., 1916 Apr.-Dec., 1920, Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; comsnr. of assize, Apr. to June, 1925; ag. puisne just., July to Sept. and from Oct., 1925; dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1927; ag. puisne just., June, 1927.

MABB, CAPT. VICTOR LEOPOLD, M.C., Chevalier de la Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1889; joined "Fort Garry" Regt., 18th Canadian Reserves, Aug., 1914; served in France as comntr. to Tank Corps, Dec., 1916; left Army, July, 1920; ment. in desps.; admstr. serv., N. Prova., Nigeria, 1920; transf. prisons dept., S. Prova. as supdt., prisons, 1924; ag. inspr., prisons, 1925; dep. dir., prisons, 1926; dir., prisons, S. Prova., 1932.

MACASKIE, CAPT. CHARLES FREDERICK CUNNINGHAM, barrister-at-law, F.R.G.S.—B. 1888; cadet, N. Borneo, Aug., 1910; 1st cls. mag., Mar., 1913; on mil. ser., 1916-19 (ment. in desp.); asst. govt. sec., Jan., 1920; legal advr. in addn., July, 1920; prot. lab., Mar., 1922; prot. lab., sec. for Chinese affrs. and legal advr., June, 1925; res., July, 1929; judge, sess. ct., Aug., 1930; judge, high ct., Dec., 1930; O.A.G., Dec., 1930-Jan., 1931; res. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1931; seconded as govt. sec., Sarawak, Sept., 1932; dep. gov. N. Borneo and ag. ch. just. in addn., Dec., 1934.
McBRIDE, ANDREW HOWIE, B.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Dumbarton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.

MACBRIEN, MAJ.-GEN. SIR JAMES HOWDEN, K.C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1878; ed. Port Perry High Sch., Ontario; brig.-gen. comdr., 12th infy. brig. during war; ch. of gen. staff, O.M.F.C., ch. of staff, dept. of national defence, Canada; comsnr., R. Canadian Mounted Pol., 1931.

MACCALL, THOMAS VERNON.—B. 1884; ed. Vicker's sch., Portsmouth, and Edin. Univ.; joined London Metro. Police, Feb., 1905; served in C.I.D., New Scotland Yard; resig. Mar., 1911; served in B. of T. labour exchanges, June, 1911 to Mar., 1914; asst. supt. of police, R. Honduras, Apr., 1914; J.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July-Aug., 1914; visiting just., dist. prisons, 1914-19; dep. atty.-gen., Corosol sessions, sup. ct., Sep., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, Feb.-Mar., 1915; comsnr. of sup. ct., Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., B.H. Volks, Dec., 1915, attld., Corosol; lieut., Jan., 1917; served in Cayo Exped. Force, on Guatemalan frontier, Apr. to July, 1916; capt., B.H.T.F., May, 1917; dep. atty.-gen., Corosol sessions, sup. ct., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Corosol from Nov., 1918.

McCALLAN, E.A.—B. 1874; ed. Ontario agrl. coll.; clk., postoffice, Bermuda, 1903; clk., works dept., 1904; rev. offr., 1907; agric. asst., 1913; dir. of agric., Bermudas 1920; del. to Washington 1920 and 1928; mem., immigrt. lab. bd.

McCALLUM, ALAN.—B. 1890; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; G.E.A. campaign, 1916-18; trooper, Tanganyika Territory pol., 1918; asst. inspr., 1919; inspr., 1920; asst. supt., 1923; supt., 1928; ag. dep. comsnr., pol. and prisons, 1931.

McCALLUM, ANGUS, M.C.—Ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin. and Aberdeen Univ.; capt., B.E.F., France, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1918, M.C. and bar. ment. in desps.; lieut., 3rd K.A.K., Nov., 1918 to June, 1922; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1923.

McCARTER, HARRY BERTRAM ADAIR.—B. 1883; entd. civil serv., Cape Col., 1899; served S. African war in Pingoland native levies, 1901-02; law dept., Transvaal civil serv., 1902; clk., Swaziland adminstr., June, 1907; clk. and dep. asst. comsnr., Apr., 1911; passed Cape Univ. civ. serv. lower law exam., 1911; asst. comsnr., 1928; ag. dep. res. comsnr., Mar.-Sept., 1935; dep. res. comsnr., Oct., 1935.

McCORMICK, ALEXANDER.—B. 1892; asst. clk., G.P.O., London, 1912; 2nd divn. clk., accounts office, G.P.O., Dublin, 1914; asst. acct., G.P.O., Lagos, 1926; survr. and ag. ch. acct., for various periods until apptd. col. postmr., Gibraltar, July, 1930.

McCORMICK, SPRNCER ACKLON.—B. 1878; ed. Komga Pub. Schl. and pvt. tuition; joined Cape civ. serv., 1896; native affrs. dept., Transvaal, Feb., 1897, and served in Tembuland and Transkei transfld. to head office. Cape Town, 1900; asst. mag., Lichtenburg, June, 1901; transfld. to various country stations between 1902 and 1906; stationed Johannesburg as 2nd civ. mag., 1907; civ. mag., Pretoria, Aug., 1908; ch. mag., Pretoria, May, 1924; under-secr. for just., 1929; judge, native high ct., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1930.

MCCOWAN, HUGH WALLACE.—B. 1885; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana (Guiana schl., 1905), and St. John's Coll., Cambridge (maths. tripos), 1908; asst. mast., Aldenham, 1909 (temp.); asst. mast., Wellingborough Grammar Schol., 1910 (temp.); maths. and sci. mast., Boys High Schol., Salisbury, Rhodesia (ag. prin., 1913), 1911; headmnr., Boys' Schol., Grenada, B.W.I., 1917; inspr., schls., S.P., Nigeria, 1923; asst. dir., educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1927; ch. inspr., S. Provs., July, 1929; asst. dir. in ch. S. Provs., Apr., 1930; ag. dir., educn., 1935.
McCRACKEN, ALBERT VICTOR NEILSON.—B. 1886; dep. regist. and asst. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, Jan., 1925; asst. crown solr., May, 1931; regist. and marshal and regist.-gen., 1932.

MOCAE, WILLIAM MOIR, M.M.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-18; engrnr. survr., S.S., 1920; asst. engrnr., Jan., 1928; exec. engrnr., Nov., 1929; ag. snr. exec. engrnr., K. Kangsar, Feb., 1933; exec. engrnr., Klang, June, 1933.
MCCURDY, NEVIN RUSSELL.—B. 1900; asst. dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Feb., 1928; dir., July, 1930.

MCDAVID, E. F., M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; sec., excess profits tax bld., Br. Guiana, 1920; 1st cl. clk., treasy. and sec., E.P.T. bd., 1921; ch. acct., col. transp. dept., 1923; ag. dep. col. treas., 1927-29; commnr., income tax, Apr., 1929; dep. col. treas., June, 1929; ag. col. treas., for various periods, 1930-34; served on various committees, 1929-34; temp. M.L.C., 1930-34; prov. M.E.C., 1932 and 1934; col. treas., May, 1935.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER GORDON, F.G.S., M.I.M.M., B.E., N.Z., A.O.S.M.—B. 1885; inspr., mines, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; supernvy., inspr., mines, Jan., 1912; supernvy. asst. warden, mines and ag. warden, mines, Oct., 1920; asst. warden, mines, Dec., 1921 and May, 1925; 1st cls. mag. and collr. land rev., in admn., Nov., 1930; senr. inspr. mines, Selangor, Jan., 1931; warden, mines, Pahang, 1933; do., Selangor, 1934.
MACDONALD, ALLAN RONALD, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attld. secretariat, Jan., 1930; pvt. sec. to gov., Feb., 1932; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1934.

MACDONALD, HON. DANIEL ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. privately and at gram. schl., comcl. coll. and Prince of Wales coll., P.E.I.; admitted to bar, P.E.I., 1883; Manitoba bar, 1885; practised law in Manitoba; judge, ct. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1906; ch. just., ct. of King's bench, Manitoba, 1927.

MACDONALD, F. H. E.—Ast. supt. pol., B. Honduras, July, 1919; ag. supt., pol., July, 1919 to July, 1920; supt., Belize fire brig., Oct., 1919; on milly. service in Egypt, Mesopotamia, and India, 1915-19 (ment. in desp.); Meritorious Ser. Medal, 1918; dist. commr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

MACDONALD, FARQUHAR VICTOR, A.M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Dingwall Acad.; water and hbr. wks., 1904; G.N.R., 1909 and 1917; Gordon Highlanders, 1914-17; ast. engrnr., Gold Coast rly., Jan., 1920; dist. engrnr., 1929; ch. engrnr., Nov., 1932.

MACDONALD, HON. HECTOR Y.—B. 1876; ed., common schls. of Nova Scotia, and Univ. of St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Antigonish, N.S. (B.A. 1900); studied law in the law schl. of Dalhousie Univ.; called to bar of N.S. 1903; practised in Sydney, N.S., removed to Regina, 1906; called to bar of Sask., May, 1906; law clk. in atty.-gen.'s dept.; city solr., Regina; K.C., 1913; justice, ct. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.

MACDONALD, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. coll. inst., Stratford, Ontario, Toronto Univ., Osgoode Hall, Toronto; called to Ontario bar, 1889; Brit. Columbia bar, 1897; K.C., 1905; bencher, Brit. Columbia Law Societr., 1906-7; elec. to legis. Brit. Columbia, 1903; re-elec. 1907; leader of the opposition, 1903 to 1909; ch. just., ct. of appeal, Brit. Columbia, 1909.
MACDONALD, JOHN, D.F.C., B.Sc. (hons.).—Mycologist, agri. dept., Kenya, Sept., 1920.

MACDONALD, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1874; min. of pub. wks. and highways, P.E.I., 1923-25; dir., national serv. and hon. fuel and food control admstr. for P.E.I. during war; 1st el. to P.E.I. legis., 1908; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1925; min. without portfolio in Meighen admtrn., 1926; min. without portfolio in Bennett cabinet, 1930; mem., senate, 1935.
MACDONALD, J. H., M.B., Ch.B. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; mil. serv., 1922-25; med. offr., Tangan-yika Territory, Sept., 1926.

MACDONALD, MALCOLM, M.P. (Bassetlaw).—B. 1901; ed. Bedales Schol., Petersfield and Queen's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; partly under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 3rd Sept., 1931; re-apptd., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for Cols., 14th June, 1935; S.of S. for Dom. affrs., 27th Nov., 1935.

MACDONALD, PATRICK DONALD.—B. 1909; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. hon., 1931; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1932; dep. commr. (temp. and provtn.), 1934; ag. sec. to govt., 1935.

MACDONALD, ROBERT ARTHUR SCRYMGEOUR.—B. 1898; D.Sc., Edinburgh Univ.; M.R.C.V.S., Royal (Dick) Vetv. Coll.; war serv., Machine Gun Corps, 1917-18; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1922; vety. research offr., 1927; ag. ch. vety. offr., 1927; ag. dep. dir. animal health, 1932-33; dep. dir., animal health, July, 1933; publications, "Cattle Management, a Textbook for use in Native Schools in Africa" in collab. with Fell; "Notes on Rabies."

MACDONALD, RONALD, M.C. and 2 Bars.—B. 1895; app'd. to C. A. for Cols. on passing p. appts. (intermediate) open civ. serv. exam., June, 1914; war service, France and Flanders, 1915-19; seconded from C.A. Office for tour of duty with audit dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; asst. audr., grade A, F.M.S., Aug., 1926; ag. asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S. and asst. audr. (commercial) in addn., Sept., 1926; ag. asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., in addn. May, 1931-Jan., 1932; asst. audr., S'pore, Apr., 1934; ag. senr. asst. audr., Palestine, 1936.

MACDONALD, W. G. MACLEOD.—Imp. post office, 1899; transf. to combined post and tel. serv. of E. Africa and Uganda, 1908; dep. postmr., 1910; ch. clk. and storekeeper, 1913; postmr., 1913; in charge radio tels., Lake Victoria area, 1914; 1st cls. postmr., 1918; asst. postmr.-gen., 1922; ag. P.M.G., 1926; dep. P.M.G., 1931; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda-Tanganyika Territory post. and tel. serv., 1933.

McDONALD, W. M., O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (London).—B.1870; ed. Liverpool coll., and St. Bart's Hosp., London; house physician, St. Bart's Hosp., 1896; med. offr., Montserrat, 1897; ditto, Antigua, 1899; med. offr., Montserrat 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp, Klerksdorp, Transvaal, 1902; med. offr., Antigua, 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1906; med. sup't, hosp., etc., St. Johns, 1913; med. offr. of health, St. Johns, 1914; surgeon-lieut., Antigua defence force, 1914.

MACDONELL, Sir PHILIP JAMES, KT. BACH. (1925), B.C.L., M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Clifton Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol.; 1st cls. hist. schls., 1894); of Gray's Inn, utter barrister; Bacon school.; I. of C. prizeman, constitutional law; called to the bar, 26th Jan., 1900; pub. pros., Transvaal, Apr., 1906; asst. crown pros., Transvaal, Feb., 1907; legal adviser and pub. pros., N.W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1908; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1911; ag. judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Apr. to Oct., 1914, Apr. to July, 1916, June to Nov., 1917; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 13th Sept., 1918; chmn., Native Reserves Coms., 1924-25 (East Luangwa) and 1926-7 (Railway Dist.); ch. just., Trinidad, 1927; ch. just., Ceylon, 1930.

McDONNELL, Sir MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH, KT. BACH. (1929), M.A.—B. 1882; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pres., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; asst. dist. coms., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, Apr., 1918; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Apr., 1920; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927 and ex officio pres., full ct. of appl. of Egypt, 1930; compiler, revd. edn., Sierra Leone Laws, 1925; edw. law repts. of Sierra Leone, 1925; edw., law repts., Palestine, 1934.

MACE, CLIVE, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. Welingtonborough Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; pt. I., maths. trips; pts. I. and II., geog. trips; supt., surveys, Cyprus, Mar., 1932; ag. inspr., mines and lab., in 1933, 1934 and 1935.

MOELDERRY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cls. hons., maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. reg., sup. ct., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dep. reg., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; attd. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Cola. on opium question at internat. red cross conf., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Conf., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec., for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., Feb.-Oct., 1934.

MELWAINE, PERCY ALEXANDER, B.A.(Hons.), LL.B.(1907), K.C.—B.1884; ed., Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to Irish bar (King's Inn) 1908; Alberta bar, 1913; temp. lieut., Royal Irish Rifles, B.E.F., 1916-19; stip. mag. and coroner, 2nd and 3rd dists., St. Lucia, 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Apr., 1923; senr. crown coun., Jan., 1926; ag. solr.-gen., Kenya and M.L.C., Oct., 1925 to Mar., 1926; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1927; called to Inner bar, N. Ireland, Feb., 1929; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1930; atty.-gen., Sept., 1933.

MEWEN, JOHN HARRY, F.S.I.—B. 1886; ed. Kimberley Pub. Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Kimberley; land survey dept., E.A.P., 1918; resigned, 1918; estates offr., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbrs., 1926; prin. asst. to gen. man., Nigerian rly., Feb., 1934.

MACFADYEN, QUENTIN ALLISON ASHBY, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Berkhamsted schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford cls. (3rd hons. hist.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1927; asst. supt., impta. and expts., 1930; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931; J.P., 1931; ch. asst do., Mar., 1933; dep. reg., sup. ct., May, 1933; 2nd pol. mag., 1934; 1st clk. and mag., Kowloon, Jan., 1935; 2nd pol. mag., 1935.

MCFALL, JOHN LYND, Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1922).—B. 1888; educ. Coleraine Acad. Inst. and Queen's Coll., Galway; B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1909; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1910; offr. cls. IV., Aug., 1920; attd. to cust. dep., F.M.S., Aug., 1922; supermuy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; D.P.P., Perak, Apr., 1923; prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Dec., 1924; asst. reg., sup. ct., Ipoh, Apr., 1925; asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; D.P.P. Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Oct., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1927; asst. dist. judge, S'pore, Mar., 1928; D.P.P., Penang, Sept., 1928; offr., cls. IB, dist. judge, S'pore, Dec., 1928; dist. offr., P.W., Nov., 1930; ag. judge, sup. ct., F.M.S., Jan., 1932; dist. offr., P.W., Oct., 1934.

McGAVOCK, Alexander Douglas.—B. 1877; ed. Park Schl., Invercargill, N.Z.; N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1891; conservator, state forest serv., 1921; asst. dir., 1930; dir. and sec. of state forests, 1931.
McGEAGH, William Ryder, M.A.—B. 1901; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; dist. offr., Kenya, 1927; ag. asst. sec., secretariat, Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; admstve. offr., Zanzibar, 1934.

McGILL, Ernest Arthur, S.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Univ. of Leeds (Brown entrance schl.); lect. in engnr., S.A. Schl. of mines (now Witwatersrand Univ.), 1912; hon. sec., S.A. Eng. standards comm., 1912; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1915; lieut., 1917; capt., 1918; served in Mesopotamia, 1915-19; asst. engnr., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engr., 1935.

McGILL, Major Harold Wigmore, M.D., M.C., V.D.—B. 1879; ed. Manitoba Univ. (M.D.); served as M.O. with 31st Batt. Infy. and 5th Canadian Field Ambulance during war; pres., Alta. Mil. Inst., 1928; first elec. to Alberta legis. assem. at g.e., 1930; resig. on appmt. as dep. supt. gen., Indian affrs., 1932.

MacGILLIVRAY, John Walker, O.B.E. (1934), F.S.I.—B. 1884; 4th asst., trig. survey, Trinidad, 1901; 3rd asst., 1903; 2nd asst., 1904; 1st asst., 1906; qual. by exam. for diploma or sworn land survr., 1905; survr., Trinidad rly. extensions, 1909; survr., roads and traces, P.W.D., 1912; topographical asst., survey dept., 1917; crown survr., 1920; survr. gen. and sub-intendant, crown lands, Oct., 1930.

MacGREGOR, Sir Alasdair Duncan Atholl Kt. Bach. (1935), K.C.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., M.A. (Edin.), class. hons.; B.A. (Oxon) class, hons.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1908; dist. commnr., S. Nigeria; polit. dept., Mar., 1912; asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, S. Nigeria, Mar., 1913; statn. mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; crown coun., Mar., 1921; solr.-gen., Sept., 1923; atty.-gen., Trinidad, July, 1926; atty.-gen., Kenya, 1929; ch. just., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1933.
MacGREGOR, Cecil John.—B. 1898; ed. S.A. Coll. Schl., Capetown; mil. serv., 1916-19; dip. agr., Elsenberg Agr. Coll., Cape Prov., S.A., 1921; dist. agric. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1928.
MacGREGOR, Ernest S.—B. 1867; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1890; Out Island commnr. (4th div.) and teacher, 1917.

MacGREGOR, James, Assoc. R.T.C.—B. 1888; asst. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1911; ag. asst. engr., 1st grade, 1919; ag. asst. dir., 1927-28 and 1935; ag. senr. exec. engr., 1928-32; senr. exec. engr., 1934.
MacGREGOR, Malcolm, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1929.

MacGREGOR, Capt. Robert Barr, R.A.M.C., M.B.Ch. B. (Edin.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), B. 1896; med. health offr., Singapore, Jan., 1920; ag. prof., physiology, in addn., June, 1920-July, 1921; med. offr. i/c. schls. and v.d. clinics, Jan., 1922; med. offr. i/c. Tan Tock Seng's hosp., Apr., 1922; ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, Jan., 1928; do., S'pore, June, 1930; regiar.-gen., births and deaths, F.M.S., July, 1924.

McGUSTY, Victor William Tighe, M.B. Dublin and L.M.Rotunda Hosp., D.T.M. and H. Liverpool.—B. 1887; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; med. offr., Fiji, 1912; dist. commnr., conjoint, 1914; gov'ts commnr. for sp. duty, 1931; sec. for Indian affrs. conjoint with the duties of inspgr. med. offr., 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., 1934.

MACHAN, John William.—B. 1890; ed. Marconi Wireless Schl., Lond.; Marconi Internat. Marine Communication Co., Lond., Sept., 1913-Aug., 1914; operator, S'pore, Aug., 1915; asst. engr.-optrn., S'pore, May, 1920 and Aug., 1926; supt., P. and T., S'pore and F.M.S., May, 1927; engr.-optrn., Penang, Oct., 1929 and Mar., 1932; engr., wireless, P. Lebar, S'pore, July, 1932.

McHARDY, W.—Asst. local auditor, E.A.P., 1909; asst. traffic man., Uganda-rly., 1914; deputy traffic man., 1917; supt. (admstve.), Jan., 1924; London rep., Kenya and Uganda, rly. and harbors, Oct., 1927.

MACHTIG, E. G. S., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; educ. at St. Paul's and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (scholar) also at the Sorbonne, Paris; Ist cls. 2nd div., class tripes, Pt. I., 1911; B.A. (Cantab.), 1911; appntd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 15th Oct., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Nov., 1917; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., D.O., 24th Jan., 1930; asst. under S. of S., D.O., 1st Feb., 1936.

McINTOSH, Andrew Elphinstone Sinclair, B.Sc., Ph.D. (Edin.).—B. 1903; ed. Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (1st cls. hons., botany); asst. plant pathologist to bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1925-27; Ph.D. (Edin.), 1927; geneticist to dept. of sci. and agr., Barbados, B.W.I., 1928.

MACKAY, James Murdoch, M.C., M.R. Ch.B. ( Glas. ); D.P.H. (Lond.); certif., London S.T.M.—B. 1892; ed. Hutchison's Grammar Schl., Glasgow and Glasgow Univ.; M.O., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1920; served, R.A.M.S., 1915-19; ag. M.O.H., Nov., 1923; S.S.O., June, 1926; A.D.H.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1930; transf'd. to Nigeria, Feb., 1932.
MACKAY, R., M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Aberd.)—B. 1898; war serv., R.N.R., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; malaria research offr., 1931.

MACKAY, William Harold.—B. 1893; offr., cust. and excise, Cyprus, 1913; asst. collr. and havmr., 1916; collr., cust., 1921; ag. ch. collr., cust. and excise, June to Oct., 1925; ag. comptr., cust. and excise, June to Oct., 1928 and June to Oct., 1931; ag. regr., companies, ag. regr., trade marks, ag. regr., patents, in 1925, 1928 and 1931; ag. comptr., cust. and inland rev. on various occasions, 1933-35; ag. commnr., Sept., to Oct., 1928; dep. comptr., cust. and inland rev., 1931.
McKEAG, Capt. V. M.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.
McKEAN, J. D.—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926.

McKENZIE, A., M.B., B.S., L.M.S.S.A., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), Cert. Lond., S.H.T.M. (distinct. and Duncan Med.)—B. 1898; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Cook Is., 1922; do., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

MACKENZIE, Capt. Angus Durham—B. 1885; cust. dept., Gold Coast., Dec., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., 1915; served in France, 1917 and with Gold Coast Regt., 1917-19; contrl. enemy property and offr.-in-ch., treasy. and cust.,
GOLAND, 1920; prov. collr., cust., Feb., 1924; ntrlr., enemy property, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; p. comptlr., cust., June, 1928; pres., Accra own coun., 1928-29; comptlr., cust., Feb., 1933.
MCKENZIE, CHARLES JOHN, A.M.I.C.E. (London).—B. 1878; ed. Otago Boys' High Sch.; nt. N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1897; ast. engrnr.-in-ch., 1920; engr.-in-ch. and under-sec., 1932.

MACKENZIE, HON. IAN ALISTAIR, M.A. (Edin.), LL.B.—B. 1890; ed. Edin. Univ.; came to Canada, June, 1914; served overseas, 1918; el. prov. assem., Br. Columbia, 1920; prov. sec., 1928; min. of immigrn., Mackenzie King govt., 1930; min., nat. defence, Mackenzie King govt., 1935.
MACKENZIE, MAJOR WALTER JOHN EASTON, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., D.T.V.M.—B. 1889; ed. Royal (Dick) Vety. Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; war serv., 1914-19; col. vet. surg., Hong Kong, 1920; J.P., 1926.
MACKENZIE-INGLIS, MAXWELL IAN HECTOR.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; ast. dist. comsrr., Nov., 1928.

MACKENZIE-KENNEDY, HENRY CHARLES DONALD CLEVELAND, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1889; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1911; probr., dist. admstr., N. Rhodesia, Dec., 1912; ast. nat. comsrr., June, 1915; nat. comsrr., July, 1918; ag. ast. mag., Apr.-June, 1923; seconded to dept. of nat. affrs., Apr., 1925; ast. mag., Apr., 1926; rep. N. Rhodesia at Imp. educn. confce., 1927; prin. ast. ch. sec., Sept., 1927; ag. D.P.W., Apr., to May, 1928; ch. sec., Oct., 1930; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. gov., Aug., 1931 and from Sept. to Nov., 1932; ret., 1933; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1935.

McKERRON, PATRICK ALEXANDER BRUCE.—B. 1896; ed. Fettes Coll., Edin.; 2nd lieut. Gordon Highrs., 1914; capt., 1916; transf., I.A.R.O., 1917; capt., 20th Punjab, 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; cadet, S.S., Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; dist. offr., N. Tebal, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; Br. res., Brunei, May, 1928; attd., fedl. sect., Oct., 1931; sec., retrenchment comn., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; supt., convict estabm't., Taiping, Oct., 1933; ag. ast. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1934.

MACKIE, GEORGE DOUGLAS, M.B.E. (1923).—Ag. 2nd clk., offr. office, St. Vincent, Dec., 1890, to Apr., 1891; 2nd clk. and cashier, treas., Apr., 1891, to May, 1892, and from Jan., 1897 to Oct., 1898; clk., crown lands and survey office, May to Dec., 1892; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Dec., 1896; 2nd. clk. and acctnt., treas., Grenada, Nov., 1898; ch. clk., Jan., 1903; ch. sub-collr. and revenue offr., treasy., St. Lucia, Jan., 1911; ag. treasur. on several occasions in 1911 to 1915; treas., Nov., 1915; ag. col. sec. in 1923, 1924 and 1925; admstd. govt., July to Sept., 1925.
MACKIE, JAMES RICHARD, B.Sc. (Agrio.)—B. 1896; ed. Sexey's Sch., Bruton, Somerset and Univ. Coll., Reading; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1921; dep. ast. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1928; ast. dir., agr., 1929.

MACKIE, JOHN BEVERIDGE, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1901; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; ast. med. offr., Victoria hosp., Aug., 1931; med. offr. in ch. offr. families and Lai Chi Kok prisons, Nov., 1932; ast. med. offr., Kowloon hosp., Dec., 1933; med. offr. in ch., New Territories and offr. families and Kowloon mortuary, Feb., 1934.

MCKINNEY, E. H., M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1877; ent. Bahamas civ. ser., 1898; 2nd clk., P.O., 1901; res. just., Exuma, 1904; Out Island comsrr. (2nd div.), 1909; comsrr. (1st div.), 1912; senr. comsrr., 1928; sr. stip. and circuit mag. in 1931, 1932, 1934 and 1935; ag. assl inspr., Nov., 1936.

MACKINNON, WILLIAM LUNAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), 1911.—B. 1888; ast. audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; ag. senr. ast. audr., in 1918 and 1919; ag. ast. treas. (in addition to his own duties), May to Sept., 1919; ag. senr. ast. audr., Oct., 1919; ag. dep. audr. on various occasions, 1926-29; ag. audr., May-Oct., 1930; senr. ast. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; prin. do., 1934.
MACKLEY, GABRIEL HERCULES.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. rlv. dept., 1900; ch. clk., 1928; ast. gen. man., 1931; gen. man., 1933.

MACKLIN, MAJOR RODRICK WILLIAM, M.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1891; serv. European War, 1914-18; with army of occupation, Germany, 1919; major, R.A. (R.O.); travelling comsrr., Gambia, Mar., 1920; do., South Bank prov., 1920; do., Macarthy Is. prov., 1924; comsrr., North Bank prov., 1930.

MACLACHLAN, HON. ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. Adelaide Univ.; called to bar, 1895; el. C'wealth senator for S. Australia, Nov., 1925; hon. min., Aug., 1926; ag. atty.-gen., 1926-27; plenipotentiary for Australia for signing of Kellogg Pact, 1928; vice-pres., exec. coun., Lyons' ministry, 1932; resigned, 1934; P.M.G., 1934.

MACLACHLAN, JAMES BOYD, M.C., M.Sc., B.E., A. M. I. C. E. Chartered Civil Engrnr.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway and Royal Univ. of Ireland; on active serv., Canadian F.A., 1915-19; M.C., 1917; mentd. in desp., 1918; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1919; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrnr., pub. wks. serv., Malaya, 1935.

MACLAREN, JOHN SUTHERLAND.—B. 1900; Fettes Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A. (hons. class.), 1921; Fettes exhib., Lamfane schol., Ritchie schol., 1st equal Gray Essay Prize, 1922; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed in Cantonese, Dec., 1925; ast. treas. and ast. assessor, 1926; ast. estate duty comsrr., 1927; 2nd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; ast. offr., N. and S. dists., New Territories, 1929; ast. treas., 1930; dist. offr., South, 1931; ast. treas. and dep. estate duty comsrr., 1933; senr. ast. treas., July-Sept., 1934; dep. estate duty comsrr., 1935.

MACLAREN, COL. THK. HON. MURRAY, C.A.M.C., C.M.G., L.L.D., M.D., C.M., M.R.C.S., F.A.C.S., Commander of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, also of Order of Avis Portugal.—B. 1861; ed. Grammar Sch., St. John, N.B.; univ. of New Brunswick; Edinburgh univ.; Vienna; served overseas, 1914-19; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1921; re-el. at g. e., 1925 and 1926; min. of pensions and national health in Bennett admntr., 1930; lieut. gov., New Brunswick, 1935.

McLAUGHLAN, ALLAN HEARTLEY PHILIP, P.A.S.I., chartered survr.—B. 1903; ed. English schl., Cyprus, and pvt. tuition; tracer, P.W.D., Cyprus, 1920; temp. foreman, P.W.D., 1925; draughtsman and record keeper, P.W.D., 1926; asst. engr., P.W.D., 1929; ag. divnl. engr., P.W.D., on various occasions, 1930-35.
MACLEAN, ADRIAN JOHN.—Ed. at Selwyn Coll., Camb., B.A., 1895; M.A., 1901; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1901; collr., Apr., 1905; recorder of titles, 1911; senr. comnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

MACLEAN, F. H.—B. 1889; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to bd. of educn., May, 1908; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1911; on military serv., from 25th Apr., 1917, to 23rd Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 24th Jan., 1922.

MACLEAN, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1888; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Mar., 1918 to Mar., 1919; temp. capt., Mar., 1919 to June, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; sleeping sickness offr., Apr., 1926; D.D.M.S., 1935.

MACLEAN, SIMON JAMES, M.A., LL.B., Ph.D.—B. 1871; comnr. of the Canadian govt. to report on rly. rate grievances, 1901; advar. to comnr. of govt. to draft Railway Act, 1903; prof. of polit. econ., Toronto Univ., 1906-1908; apptd. a mem. of rly. comnr., Canada, 1908; re-apptd., Nov., 1918; asst. chief comnr., Aug., 1919.

MACLENNAN, FINLAY MALCOLM.—B. 1887; ed., Tarbutt Public Sch.; Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., High Schl.; ent. serv. of dept. of rlys. and canals, Can., as office man, Sault Ste. Marie Canal, Sept., 1910; transferred, Port Nelson on Hudson Bay terminals work, as jnr. engr., 1912; asst. audr., dept., rlys. and canals, Ottawa, 1st Apr., 1915; audr., 1917; ch. audr., 1918; asst. comptlr., 1920; comptr., 1930.

MACLENNAN, IAN MORRISON ROSS.—B. 1909; ed. Hymers Coll., Hull and Worcester Coll., Oxford; schol., 1928; 2nd cls. hon., mods., 1930; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; Gladstone research student, St. Deiniol's liby., Hawarden, 1932-3; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1933.

MACLENNAN, NORMAN MACPHHRSON.—B. 1895; ed. Waitaki College (N.Z.) and Aberdeen Univ.; M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.); war serv., 1915-18; res. med. offr., City Hosp., Aberdeen, 1922; bacteriologist, James Mackenzie Inst., St. Andrews and M.O.H., St. Andrews, 1923; asst. M.O.H. and tuberculosis offr., Woolwich, 1925; health offr., Kenya, 1927; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1929; senr. health offr., N. Rhodesia, 1931; tuberculosis expert, Palestine, 1933.

MCLEOD, DAVID WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Cent. Training Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; senr. asst., King's Coll., Seychelles, 1910; prin., King's Coll. and inspr., schls., Seychelles, 1913; on active serv., 1914-19; senr. asst., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Feb., 1920; prin., Jan., 1922; prin., Free Schl., Penang, Dec., 1930; prin., Raffles Inst., S'pore, Feb., 1932.

MACLEOD, J. R. S.—B. 1887; ed. Scots Coll., Ballarat and Cambridge, B.A., LL.B., F.S.A., Scotland; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1921; lieut., Australian Citizens Forces, 1914; served overseas as 2/lieut., Australian Imp. Forces, 1915; capt., 1918 (wounded, desps. twice); temp. clk., F.O., 1921; asst. comnr., national savings comtee., 1922; after compet. exam. apptd. asst. prin., Oversea Sttlmt. Dept., 1924.

McLUCKIE, WILLIAM HARLEY, M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1895; ed. Greenock High Schl., Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; mily. serv., 1915-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; exec. engr., 1928; ag. dep. dir., pub. wks. for various periods, 1930-35; ag. dir., pub. wks. in 1933.
MACLUSKIE, HECTOR, C.D.A.—B. 1904; univ. dipl., agr. (Aberdeen); Imp. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1929-30; agrl. supt., dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

McMAHON, CECIL, O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—Ent. S. Rhodesia civ. serv., Nov., 1909; served S. African rebellion and German S.W. African campaign, 1914-15 with 1st Rhodesian regt.; E. Africa campaign with 8th S. African Inf., 1915-18; capt. and adjt., "1914-15" Star and M.C.; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1918 dist. offr., June, 1926; dep. prov. comnr., 1934; ag. prov. comnr., Aug., 1933-Nov., 1934 and Aug., 1935.

MACMICHAEL, SIR HAROLD, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1882; ed. King's Lynn and Bedford Schls. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); joined Sudan polit. serv., 1905; successively inspr. in provs. of Kordofan, Blue Nile and Khartoum; polit. and intell. offr., expedy. force, Darfur reoccupation, 1916 (three times ment. in desps., D.S.O.); sub.-gov., Darfur prov.; asst. civ. sec., Sudan govt.; civ. sec., do., 1926; gov. and cmndr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1934; Order of the Nile (2nd cls.), 1930; Burton Memorial Med. of the R. Asiatic Soc'y., 1928; Star of Ethiopia (2nd cls.), 1930; author of "The Tribes of Northern and Central Kordofan" (1912), "A History of the Arabs in the Sudan" (1922), "The Anglo-Egyptian Sudan" (1934).
MACMILLAN, HON. CHARLES EDWARD DE LA BARCA.—B. 1872; ed. Brighton pub. schl. and pvtly.; ent. N.Z. H. of R., 1923; min. of agr. and mines, 1932.
MCMILLAN, L. B.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schls., Charlottetown, Prince Edward Is.; messenger, P.E. Is. govt., 1890; clk., 1893; sec. for pub. wk., P.E. Is., 1900.
MACNAB, ALEXANDER CHRISTY, M.I.MAR.E., M.Liv.E.S.—B. 1884; survr., ships, S.S., May, 1920; ag. survr.-gen., various occasions, 1920, 1931 and 1934.
McNAMARA, GEORGE, C.B.E. (1932).—B. 1881; entd. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1896; supt. of staff, 1916; 2nd asst. sec., 1920; 1st asst. sec., 1923; sec., 1926; dir.-gen., 1933.

McNAUGHTON, MAJOR GEN. ANDREW GEORGE LATTAN, C.B. (1935), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1918), Hon. LL.D., McGill Univ. (1920)—B. 1887; ed. Lennoxville, McGill Univ. (B.Sc., M.Sc.), R. Staff Coll., Camberley, Imp. Defence Coll. (Lond.); served European War, 1914-13 in France & Belgium (wounded twice), ment. in desps. three times, '1914-15' Star, two med.; ch., Canadian gen. Staff, 1929-35; pres., nat. research coun. of Canada since 1935.

MACNIE, WILLIAM ALexANDER.—B. 1899; 2nd lieut., Seaforth Highrs., 1918; lieut., 1919-20; served, France, 1918; sub-inspr., pol., B. Guiana, 1921; dist. inspr., 1925; ag. detective inspr., 1925; ag. pol. inspr., 1926, 1928-31; seconded to col. secretariat as addnl. ass't. col. sec., May, 1931; dist. commr., Jan., 1932.

McNULTY, THOS. SYDNEY.—B. 1870; ed. at Marist Coll., Sydney; clk., aceta. branch, wks. dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1896; ch. corres. clk., ditto, 1897; mem. mun. coun., Claremont, 1901-3; sec. to goldfields water supply admin., 1903; under-sec. for agric. and industries, 1909.
McPHEE, SIR JOHN CAMERON, K.C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1878; premier of Tasmania since 1st June, 1928; ret.

MACPHERSON, BARBARA.—Ed. Durness H.G. Sohl. and Skerry's Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after compet. exam. typist, Lond. tel. serv., 1912; transf'd, to C.O., May, 1918; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. off'r., Jan., 1923.
MACPHERSON, COLIN, OF O., K.A.R.—B. 1898; ed. Alleyne's Coll., Dulwich; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. off'r., Jan., 1929.

MACPHERSON, JOHN STUART.—B. 1898; ed. Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ. (M.A., 1921); 2nd lieut., A. and S. Highlanders, Jan., 1918; France, Apr., 1918-Jan., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. 4th asst. sec. to gov't. F.M.S., Aug., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; ag. asst. dist. off'r., K. Salangor, Aug., 1922; ag. dist. off'r., Raub, May, 1923; off'r., cls. V, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. off'r., Bentong, Oct., 1925; off'r. cls. IV, dist. off'r., Bentong, Mar., 1928; relief asst. sec. to gov't. F.M.S., Feb., 1930; off'r., cls. III, Mar., 1933; attchd. C.O., Sept., 1933.

McPHILLIPS, HON. ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1861; ed. St. Boniface and Manitoba Colls.; barrister; K.C., 1900; elected to leg. assem., B.C., 1898; atty.-gen., 1903; subsequently pres. of coun.; judge, ct. of appeal, B.C., 1913.

McQUADE, JOHN HERRBERT.—B. 1893; highest marks in U.K. in entrance exam. for cust. and excise, Mar., 1913; imp. cust. and excise, July, 1913; war serv., Flanders with R.A., 1917-18; asst., cust., Kenya, July, 1920; collr., cust., Aug., 1924; sec., tariff comte., 1930; dep. compt'r., cust., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; census off'r., 1931; sec., econ. advay. bd., 1931-33; compt'r., cust., Nov., 1934.

MACQUARIE, EDMUND JEFFREY.—B. 1883; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana and Caiau Coll., Cambridge; B.A., L.B.B., 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1906; ag., stip. mag., B. Guiana, June to Nov., 1906; and July, 1907 to Mar., 1914; dormant commr. as stip. mag. and apptd. J.P. and commr. of oaths, Dec., 1907; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1915; crown counsel, 1916; ag. chief registr., Apr.-May, 1917; az. senr. crown counsel, 1917; ag. pol. mag., Nov., 1917 to Apr., 1918; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1919; ag. ch. registr., Apr. to Oct., 1920; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1927; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1931.
MACQUILLAN, CECIL JAMES, M.B., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C.Dublin).—M.O., R.A.F. (res.) served U.K. and Irak, 1924-26; R.M.O., Kent and Canterbury hosp., 1926; medl. off'r., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1927.
MACRAE, LACHLAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1886; dir., educn., Ceylon, Jan., 1922; M.L.C.

McROBERTS, CAPT. R. A.K.—Natal mounted pol., 1903-08; Natal rebellion, 1906; Usutu operations, 1907; inspr., veterinary pol., E.A.P., 1909; asst. inspr., pol., Mar., 1910; asst. supt., Sept., 1911; capt., Gordon Highlanders, Aug., 1915; France, 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1917; att'd, Highland Light Inf'y., Mesopotamia, Mar., 1917; seconded, polit. dept., dist. mag. and dep. mily. gov., Basrah, 1917-18; mily. mission to Siberian govt., 1919; supt., pol., Kenya Colony, May, 1920; mag., Zanzibar Prot., Jan., 1921; ag. asst. judge, in 1922 and 1924; ag. atty-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1925; pol. mag., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926.

MAFFEY, SIR JOHN LOADER, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.B., (1934), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1921), C.S.I. (1920), C.I.E. (1916).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby and Christ Ch., Oxford; ent. I.C.S., 1899; transf'd, polit. dept., 1905; served with Mohmand Field Force, 1908 (med. and clasp); polit. agt., Khyber, 1909-12; dep. commr., Peshawar, 1914-15; dep. sec., foreign and polit. dept., gov't of India, 1915-16; ch. polit. off'r., Afghanistan with N.W. Frontier Field Force, 1919; prte. sec. to viceroy, 1916-20; ch. sec. to Duke of Connaught, 1921; ch. commr., N.W. Frontier prov., India, 1921-24; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1926; perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., Nov., 1933.
MAGILL, CHARLES VICTOR, O.B.E. (1934).—Asst. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Nov., 1919; dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Apr., 1926.
MAGLADERY, THOMAS.—B. 1876; M.L.A. for Temiskaming, Ont., 1914-24; promoted in war serv. to capt.; dir., soldier settl. hd., 1931; dep. min., immigrn. and colonization, 1934.

MAGRATH, CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1860; ed., priv. tuition; irrign. and development work in S. Alberta from 1878; mem. for Lethbridge in N.W.T. assem. for several years, and finally mem. of prov. cabinet; ed. to H.C., Can., 1908, for Medicine Hat; def., 1911; fuel contr., Can., 1917-20; chmn., Internat. Joint Coman., Can. sect., 1921.
MAHABIR, F.—B. 1888; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Apr., 1915.
MAHABIR, JULES.—B. 1891; called to bar, Gray's Inn, July, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1934.
MAHON, WILLIAM GRAVES.—B. 1879; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1900; ch. clk., pol. magts., dist. "A", 1919; pol. mag., dist. "E", 1919.

MAINGOT, ELLIOT FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed. Ushaw Coll., Durham; served articles of clerkship in London and qualified as solnr., 1914; crown solnr., admtr., gen. and off'r. receiver, Trinidad, Dec., 1922; pub. trustee, May, 1931.
MAJOR, ALFRED REGINALD SEYMOUR.—B. 1909; ed. St. John's, Leatherhead; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; as-t. supt., pol., Dec., 1931; att'd. Punjab pol., 1933-34.

MALAN, RT. HON. FRANCOIS STEPHANUS, P.C. (1929).—B. 1871; ed. in S. Africa and Camb.; B.A. Science, Cape univ.; LL.B., Camb.; advocate of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1895; M.L.A. for Malmesbury, 1900; re-elected, Feb., 1904; min. of agric., Cape Colony, 1908; min. of educn., Union of South Africa, 1910; attended Imp. conf., 1911; min. of mines, industries and educn., 1912; min. for agr., 1920; min. of mines and industries, 1921-24; senator, 1927.

MALAN, FRANCOIS STEPHANUS.—B. 1882; ed. Normal Coll., Capetown and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, B.A.; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1916; prin., Normal Coll., Ermelo, Transvaal, 1919; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1924; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1935.

MALAN, HENDRICK JACOBUS, B.A. (Cape).—B. 1879; Cape civ. serv., 1900; war serv., capt., Pretoria Rgt., S.W.A., 1914-15; asst. mag., Johannesburg, 1918; mag., Britstown, 1919; Ladysmith, 1922; Johannesburg, 1927; Senekal, 1930; Potchefstroom, 1933.

MALAN, WOUTER DE VOS.—B., 1892; ed., Boys High Sch., Huguenot Coll., Wellington, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., New York; B.A. lit. and phil., 1911; B.A. (hons.) hist., 1916; Ph.D., Col. Univ., 1923; princ., Ceres High Sch., 1924-28; prof., educn., Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1929-30; prof. ast.; supt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1930; supt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1934.

MALCOLM, HAROURT GLADSTONE, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1918); K.C. (1910).—Priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson when admnstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; mem. of H. of A., Bahamas, 1900; deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904 and 1911; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1905; Sept., 1906 to Feb., 1907; July to Nov., 1910; Apr. to Dec., 1911; May to July, and Aug. to Nov., 1913; ag. chief just., 1913 and 1927; speaker, H. of A., 1913; re-elected, 1913, 1919, 1927 and 1928; thanked by S. of S. for assistance to atty.-gen. in drafting bills; thanked by legis., for obtaining copies of early records; Bahamas del. to Canada-W. Indies conferees at Ottawa, 1920 and 1925; Bahamas rep. during visit of Prince of Wales, and partly, tercentenary celebrations at Bermuda Oct., 1920; Bahamas del. to W. Indian conferees, London, 1926; prepared 1929 revised edition of the laws of Bahamas.

MALHERBE, ERNST GIDEON.—B., 1895; ed., Stellenbosch Univ. and Columbia Univ.; B.A. Hons., M.A. (phil.) (Cape), M.A. and Ph.D. (educn.) Columbia; fellow, Teacher's College, Columbia Univ., 1923-24; teacher, Cape Town Training Coll.; lect., educn. psychology, Univ. of Stellenbosch; senr. lect., educn., Univ. of Cape Town; ch. investigator educn. sec., Carnegie Poor White Commn. of research, 1928-32; dir., National Bureau for Educn. and Social Research, 1929; author publications on educn. subjects.

MALHERBE, JOZUA FRANCOIS.—B., 1880; clk., dept. of just., Transvaal, 1900; clk., commr. of pol., 1902; 1st gce. clk. and A.R.M., dept. of just., 1908; pub. pros., Kimberley, 1912; senr. clk., dep. of just., 1920; mag., Wynberg, 1921; admt. mag., Pretoria, 1925; mem., wage bd., 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1930; workmen's compensation commr., 1934.

MALLEY, THOMAS.—B., 1891; G.P.O., Glasgow, Dec., 1909-16; R.N.V.R., Dec., 1916-Jan., 1920; G.P.O., London, 1920-23; asst. supt., P.&T., F.M.S., Feb., 1923; sec., Malayan w/less comtee., June, 1926; supt., P. & T. Dept., Oct., 1926; sec., comtee.—teleg. & telep. commun., S.S., F.M.S. & U.M.S., Oct., 1930; dep. acctn.-gen., P. & T., Feb., 1933.
MALONE, DENIS KERON.—B., 1903; ed., Cooper's Company Schl.; cler. offr., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921.

MANDERS, RICHARD HENRY DAVID, B.A. (Cantab.), B., 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Feb., 1927; attd., Kandy kach., Oct., 1927; office ast., ditto Feb., 1929; ditto, Kandy kach., May, 1930; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1931; asst. ch. sec., Apr., 1932; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1933; pol. mag., Galle, Nov., 1933.
MANGAN, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1910-14; clk., 1914; ag. ast. res. mag., 1921; passed 2nd grade Seewana exam., 1917; ag. res. mag., 1922.

MANGIN, THORLEIF RATTRAY ORDE.—B., 1896; ed., Marlborough Coll.; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R. and attd. to Royal Naval Dvn., 1915; qualified, gunnery course, H.M.S. Excellent, 1916; apptd., to H.M.S. Repulse and served with Grand Fleet, 1916-1919; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1919; dep. prov. commr., 1934.
MANN, SIR FREDERICK WOLLASTON, K.B.E. (1933), M.A., LL.M.—B., 1869; called to bar, 1896; served, Boer War; judge, sup. ct., Victoria, 1919; ag. ch. just., 1933-34; ch. just., Oct., 1935.

MANN, GEORGE ERNEST, M.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B., 1896; ed., St. Olave's Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; ast. agrl. inspr., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; agrl. field offr., Nov., 1924; agr. instr., Dec., 1926; vice-prin., schl. of agr., Malay., Apr., 1931.

MANNING, RICHARD JOSEPH.—B., 1883; ed., Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; sub-inspr. of police, B. Com., Oct., 1909; dist. inspr., Sept., 1912; ag. stip. mag., W. Coast Demarara, June, 1919; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1925; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1927; ag. ch. regis. on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N.Territories judiciary, July, 1930; 3rd puisne judge, Trinidad, 1932; higher certif. in Hindi, Mar., 1916; higher certif., Swahili, 1922; lower certif., Ga., 1928; compiler, "Br. Guiana Police Manual," Dec., 1918.
MANNING, WILLIAM.—B., 1892; on active serv., Sept., 1914 to Feb., 1919; inspr., pol., Zanzibar, Mar., 1919; supdt., 1924; ag. ast. commdt., on various occasions, 1925-35.

MANSERGH, N. S., M.B.E.—B., 1889; ed., Wellington Coll.; B.S.A. pol., 1910; Rhodesia Native Regt., 1917; lieut., 1917; demob., 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1919; operns. against Mad Mullah, 1920; ast. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1926; commr., pol. orim. investgn. dept., 1927.
MANSON, HENRY HINDLE.—B., 1896; ast. elec. engnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; dir., elec. dept., Bahamas, 1934.

MANTON, CYRIL COWAN.—B., 1877; 2nd cls. clk., collectorate, Black River, Jamaica, 1897; 1st cls. treasy. clk., 1903; junr. ast. collr. taxes, 1912; ast. collr., Kingston, 1920; dep. stmp commr., 1920.
MARCH, HARRY THOMAS.—B., 1881; ed., King's Coll., London; 2nd divn. clk., post office, 1900-07; acctnt., post office, Nigeria, Apr., 1907 to Jan., 1912; postm.-gen., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1912.
MARCHANT, W. S.—Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926; dep. prov. commr. and ast. sec., Oct., 1935.

MARCI, HON. CHARLES, LL.D., Ottawa Univ.—B., 1860; elec. to H. of C. of Canada for Bonaventure, 1900; dep. speaker of H. of C., 1905-1908; re-elec. at gen. elec., 1908, 1911, 1917, 1921, 1930 and 1935; speaker of the H. of C., 1909; mem. of P.C. for Canada, 1911.
MARILLIER, ERIC STANLEY, O.B.E. (1935).—B., 1890; clk., cust., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1912; collr., cust., Apr., 1915; ast. collr., cust., Oct., 1926; contr. cust., Mar., 1928; mem., leg. coun.
MARITZ, G. J.—B., 1890; ed., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1912; pres., sp. income tax

MARSHALL, C. W.—Imp. serv., 1897-98; clk., atty.-gen.'s dept., Cape Town, 1898; examr., accts., 1899; clk., res. mag., King William's Town and distr., stamps, 1900; sec., plague admstr., 1903; 2nd cls. examr., control and audit dept., Cape, 1905; inland rev. dept., 1910; contr. and audr.-gen.'s dept., 1912; senr. clk., 1912; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1916; survr., rev., Pretoria, 1921; Cape Town, 1929; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1930; ditto, Johannesburg, 1932.

MARSHALL, EDWARD FOSTER, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; addl. Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1913; Badulla kach., Mar., 1913; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1917; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1918; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Dec., 1923; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Avisawella, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Avisawella, Jan., 1928; ditto, Badulla, Feb., 1929; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1930; asst. stintm. offr., Nov., 1931; dep. tea contr. in addn., May, 1933.

MARSHALL, CAPT. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry.—B. 1893; exhibr. Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1911; hon. mods. (maths), 2nd cls. hon., 1912; 2nd lieut., Worc.
