NOAD, JOHN ELLIOTT MONCKEPP.—Ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth and Training Cruiser, H.M.S. "Cornwall"; midshipman, R.N., Aug., 1910; II.M.S. "Prince of Wales," Flagship, Atlantic Fleet; resgd., 1911; Edinburgh Univ., Oct., 1911 to Aug., 1914; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., Aug., 1914 to Nov., 1918; interned in Holland from fall of Antwerp to armistice; while in Holland passed for A.M.I.C.E., Feb., 1917; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, Mar., 1920; asst. engrn., Kenya and Uganda rly., Apr., 1924; senr. asst. engrn., P.W.D., 1930; exec. engrn., 1934; "1914" Star, War and Victory meda.

NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Jan., 1911; conf. clk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff clk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comttee., 1st Jan., 1918.

NOBBES, CAPT. HOWARD.—B. 1891; ed. University Coll., and Inst. of Educn., Lond.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1913 (hons. in chemy.); M.Sc. (Lond.), 1919; A.I.C. (1918); F.I.C. (1923); 2nd lieut., Northamptonshire Regt., Apr., 1914; lieut., 1915, capt., 1916; war serv. in France, 1st battn., Northamptonshire Regt. 1914-15 and with R.E., 1916-18; chemy. mast., R. Coll., Mauritius, 1926; prin., Queen's Coll. Br. Guiana, 1931.
NOBLE, G. S. P., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), Certif. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distinc.).—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

NOBLE, CAPT. JAMES MORTON, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1890; asst. engrn., F.M.S., Nov., 1919; do., Kedah, Jan., 1920-Feb., 1924; exec. engrn., F.M.S., Nov., 1928; ag. snr. exec. engrn., various occasions, 1929-31 and 1934; ag. state engrn., N. Sembilan, in addn., July, 1934.

NOBLE, PETER, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Elgin and Aberdeen, Scotland; asst. civ. engrn., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1906; ag. dist. engrn. in 1907 and 1909; dist. engrn., Demerara, B. Guiana, Aug., 1910; ag. in addition as chief draughtsman, 1911, and as ast. col. civ. engrn. in 1912; col. engrn. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Aug., 1912; nom. mem., Roseau Town Board, offl. mem. legis. coun. and road board; on war service as mem. commrs. of valuation (min. of munitions); reed. thanks of sec. of state for work in connection with roads of Dominica, 1916; dist. engrn., N. dist., Palestine, Aug., 1922.

NOOTT, P. GORDON.—B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasp); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expedn., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; comm. 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Terr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916 served on Cayo expedn., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.
NORRIS, C. G.—B. 1890; ed. St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; cadet, Solomon Islands, Jan., 1915.
NORRIS, J. A.—B. 1872; ed. Ballygun and Williamstown, Ireland; acot. to treasy., Victoria, Mar., 1914; audr.-gen., Mar., 1919.

NORRIS, JOHN WILSON A.C.G.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Lond. Univ. Imp. Coll. So. and Technology; active service B.E.F. (France) 1914-19, major R.R., mentioned in despatches; joined C.A. as engr. inspr., 1919; asst. engr., 1920; visited Gold Coast 1924; services loaned to consult. engr. reporting on Takoradi Harbour; seconded for duty as asst. engr. Gold Coast harbours staff. Takoradi 1924; rejoined C.A. 1926; dep. to ch. insp. engr., 1929.

NORRIS, VICTOR HUGH, L.M.S.—B. 1893; ed. Raflles Inst. & Coll. of Medicine, S'pore; asst. surgn., S'pore, Sept., 1915; ag. med. offr., various occasions, 1917-24; asst. med. offr., Sept., 1925; dep. med. offr., Oct., 1926; med. offr., S.S., Dec., 1930.

NORTH, ROLAND ARTHUR CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Blundell's and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. with Samoa govt., 1921; studied Japanese in Japan, 1922; 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924 and 1930; sec. for Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927 and 1931; extra ast. col. sec., Feb., 1931; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., June, 1931 and Jan., 1934; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., in 1933 and 1934; 1st cls. cadet offr., Apr., 1935.

NORTHCOOTE, SIR GEOFFREY ALEXANDER STAFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931).—Ed. at Blundell's Schi. and Balliol Coll. Oxford; B.A. (honours) and Newte and Huish exhibnr.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1904; ag. collr. and ag. dist. comnr., S. Kavirondo, 1904-06; polit. offr., Kisil Patrol, 1906; polit. offr., sleeping sickness comnr., 1908-09; dist. comnr., 1909; in charge, Kikuyu Prov., 1911; ag. prov. comnr., May to Oct., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. col. sec., Apr., 1921; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1921, Aug. to Sept., 1922 and Mar. to Sept., 1923; gov.'s dep., July to Aug., 1923; mem., legin. coun., 1923; ag. col. sec. in 1925 and 1926; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Feb., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1930; ag. gov., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1934.

NORTHCOTE, MAJOR LEONARD Augustus Stafford.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1900; res. of offr., Feb., 1914; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Salonika and Palestine, 1914-19; local commdt. of pol. and asst. comsnr., Cyprus, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek; ag. comsnr., for various periods 1924-27 and 1929-34.

NORTHCOTE, R. C.—Ed. Blundells and S. African Sch. of Mines and Technology, Johannesburg; Rhodesian admtn., B.S.A. pol., 1914-16; Rhodesian Native Regt., 1917-19; German E. Africa campaign (ment. in despatch, mily. med.); asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; dist. offr., Feb., 1932.

NORTHCROFT, Hon. ERIMA Harvey, D.S.O. (1919), V.D.—B. 1884; ed. Hokitika dist. high schl., Well. Coll., and Auckland univ. coll., N.Z.; joined N.Z. pub. trust off. as cadet for short period; admitted to bar, 1907; served overseas, 1916-19 with N.Z.F.A., with rank of lt.-col.; dep. judge-advoc.-gen., 1927-33; judge-advoc.-gen. with rank of col., 1933-35; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1935.

NORTHCROFT, LESLIE ARTHUR, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to secretariat, Jan., 1924; ditto, Putalam kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, N’Eliya kach., July, 1925; office asst., Jaffna kach., May, 1926; temp. attd., C.O. Sept., 1928 to Mar., 1929; addnl. asst. col. treas., Apr., 1929; ag. asst. gov’t. agt., Matara, Oct., 1929; asst. attlit. offr., Nov., 1929.

NORTHWAY, MAJOR-GENERAL Sir EDWARD; G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expedn., 1891; Miranzai expedn., 1891; Isazai expedn., 1892; in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasaland Rhodesia field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col.), 1915; King’s R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high comsnr., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTH-HUNT, HAROLD.—B. 1894; ed. Ipswich Schl. and St. Catharine’s Coll., Cambridge (exhib.); war serv., 1914-20; (ment. in desps.); cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; 2nd asst. dist. offr., July, 1920; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1921; 2nd asst. adviser, B. Pahat, Johore, Apr., 1922; offr., cls. IV, Aug., 1925; dist. offr., Lipis, June, 1930; offr., cls. III, Aug., 1930; ag. asst. advr., supt., lands and registr., titles, Kelantan, Aug., 1933; offr., cls. II., Aug., 1936; registr.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1935.
NORTHMORE, Hon. Sir JOHN ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1932), LL.B.—B. 1865; educ. sup. ct., W. Australia, 1914; ch. just., 1931; lieut-gov., 1932.

NORTON, CUTHBERT HUGH.—B. 1878; ed. pte. schls. and Weymouth Coll.; ent. govt. ser., Br. Guiana, 1913; asst. comsny., taxation, 1914; 3rd gde., do., 1917; 2nd gde., do., 1920; 1st gde., do., 1927; seconded as census comsnr., Sept., 1930 to Dec., 1931; 1st gde., offr., dist. adminstr., 1932; ch. comsny. and dist. comsnr., Georgetown and East Bank, Demerara dist., Jan., 1934.

NORTON, HENRY JOHN STEPHEN, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1896; cler. asst., Gibraltar, 1913; crane and wharf clk., 1914; treas. clk., 1914; passed compet. exam., 1917; clk.-in-charge, impta. and expts., and clk. to food contr., 1914 to 1920; dep. asst. shipping master, 1921; asst. shipping master, 1922; sec. and treas., sailors’ home, 1920; clk.-in-charge port dept. and ag. Trinity agt., in 1924-26; asst. sec., col. sec’s. office, 1927; pvte. sec. and interpreter to gov., 1929.

NORVAL, WILLIAM DIXON.—B. 1893; ed. Wepener, O.F.S.; clk., dept. of lands, 1913; clk., dept. of educn. (Union), 1919; pvte. sec. to min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1924; senr. clerk, dept. of agr., 1926; mining comsnr., Namaqualand, 1929; do., Barberton, 1930; do., Barberton and Pilgrims Rest, 1931; under-sec. for lab., 1932.
NORWOOD, LEONARD ROBERT OSWALD.—B. 1896; ed. Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 2nd Nov., 1921.

NOSWORTHY, Hon. Sir WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1867; ed. privately at Boys High Schl., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1928-28; jun. whip for reform party and later sen. whip; sen. whip for Nat. gov’t.; min. of agr., 1919-26; min. of immigrn., 1920-28; min. of finance and stamp duties, 1925-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1926; min. of external affrs., 1926-28.

NOTT, CHARLES ROBERT HARLEY, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Christ’s Coll., Camb.; cadet, Fiji and W. Pacific services, Mar., 1926; col. sec’s. office, Fiji, Apr., 1926; passed cadet, Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., 1930; ch. clk., col. sec’s. office, Jan., 1931; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., in 1932, 1934 and 1935.

NOWELL, WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1936), C.B.E., D.I.C., F.L.S.—B. 1880; ed. munici. tech. schl., Halifax; R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; on mily. serv., S. Africa, 1900; asst. supt., agr., Barbados, 1911; mycologist and agril. lecturer, Imp. Dept. of Agr. for W. Indies, 1913; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1920; mem., agril. relief comtee.; original mem., bd. of man., govt. agril. bank; mem., advisory bd., Emp. Cotton Growing Corp., cotton research sta.; dir., sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1926; dir., E. African agril. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1926.
NUNAN, P. F., M.B., Bsc.-Surg., M.D.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1924; do., Kenya 1925.

NUNN, GEOFFREY NOEL NAPIER.—Ed. Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; B.A. (hons.); cadet, Gold Coast, July, 1929; asst. dist. comsnr., 1930; seconded, Gambia, Dec., 1931; ag. comsnr., 1932; 2nd lieut., R.A.R.O., July, 1929.

NUNN, MAJOR REGINALD LEWIS, D.S.O., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B.1892; ed. Brighton Coll.; waterwks. engrnr., Trinidad and Tobago, Apr., 1921; res. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1924; asst. dir., P.W.D., Nigeria, Feb., 1927; dep. col. engrnr., P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1930; dep. D.P.W., S.S., Jan., 1932; seconded for aerodrome planning, July-Nov., 1934; D.P.W., S.S. and advr., pub. wks., Malay States, 1935.

NUROCK, MAX, O.B.E. (1932), M.A. (Dublin)—B. 1893; mily. serv., 1915-19; asst. pvte. sec., Palestine, 1920; junr. asst. sec., 1922; asst. sec., 1926; sec. to standing comtee. for commerce and industry, 1928; ag. asst. ch. sec. on various occasions and once as ch. sec.; clk., exec. coun. in addn., 1934.

NUTMAN, FREDERICK JOHN, A.R.C.S., M.Sc., D.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Newton Abbot and R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; timber mycologist, forest products research lab., Oxford, 1926; plant physiologist, E. African agril. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

OAKES, ARTHUR PODMORE, M.M., R. of O.—B. 1892; Europ. War, 1914-18; N. Staffordshire Regt., 1912; Northamptonshire Regt., 1917-28; K.A.R., Nyassaland and Somaliland, 1921-26; pay and qrtmr. Somaliland pol., 1926; dep. comdt. pol. and dir., prisons, 1928.

OAKLEY, PHILIP DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1883; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; senr med. offr., July, 1928; ast. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1928; dep. dir., Feb., 1932; dir., med. and san. services, Sierra Leone, 1933.

O'BRIEN, ARTHUR JOHN RUSHTON, C.M.G. (1929), M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H., B.Hy. (hons.), (Durham), Certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1883 joined W.A.M.S., 1911; served in Great War, 1915-19; awarded M.C. and bar, twice ment. in desp., and recvd. thanks of S. of S. for services in Cameroons; surg. specialist, Gold Coast, 1921; R.M.O., Gold Coast hospital, 1923; sec. to C.A.M.S. comte., Dec., 1929; visited West Indies at request of S. of S., 1930; assumed duty as sec., C.A.M.S., comte., June, 1930.

O'BRIEN, BYAN JUSTIN.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. mods.; 2nd cls. lit. hum.; ast. sec., col. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Apr., 1927, ag. clk., exec. coun., for various periods, 1927-31; clk., exec. coun., Aug., 1931; ag. ch. ast. sec. for various periods, 1927-36; ag. ast. comanr., Jan. to Mar., 1928; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1933; joint edr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

O'CONNOR, ALEXANDER JAMES, Dipl. Forestry (Capo).—Clik., conserv. of forest's office, W. conservancy, 1903; forester, Uitvlugt, W. conservancy, 1905; ast. dist. forest offr., Tokai, 1908; seconded to gen. comm., Transkei, 1908; ast. dist. forest offr., Knysna, 1909; ditto, working plans, Midland conservancy, 1910; ditto, Woodbush, Transvaal conservancy, 1910; dist. forest offr., Woodbush, 1918; ditto, Pietersburg, 1924; ag. conserv., forests, Natal conservancy, 1926; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, Natal conservancy, 1927; prin. forest offr. (forestry inspr.). Transvaal and O.F.S., 1932.

O'CONNOR, CHARLES ANTHONY.—B. 1883; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; sup't., Reduit gardens and plantations, forests and gardens dept., Mauritius, Sept., 1904; ch. overseer, dept. of agr., Aug., 1913; enlisted, 2nd Artists' Rifles, Aug., 1917; reed. comm., Royal Munster Fus., Sept., 1918; inspr. of plantations, Zanzibar Prot., Aug., 1919; agrl. instr. and inspr., co-op. credit societies, Mauritius, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, HUGH JOSEPH, F.Z.S.—B. 1887; Lieut., Gambia Artillery (V.), 1910; mem. of navigation and pilotage board, Gambia, 1914; ast. commnr. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1916; ag. staff instructor, Mar., 1916, and June, 1918; ag. commnr. of police for various periods in 1917, 1921 and 1922; O.C. Togoland mil. police, and O.C. Togoland prisons, 19th Aug., 1919; capt., G. Coast Rifles, 1922; commnr. of police, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad; clk., goods dept., rly., Jan., 1914; clk., gen. man. office, July, 1914; 6th clk., col. secret., Jan., 1919; 6th ols. clk., do., Jan., 1920; 5th do., May, 1920; 4th do., Mar., 1922; 3rd do., Sept., 1925; 2nd do., Jan., 1928; 1st do., Jan., 1930; senr. clk., do., Jan., 1931; 2nd ast. col. sec., Mar., 1935.
O'DONOGHUE, LAURENCE.—B. 1895; ed. Newcastle Schl., Co Tipperary; R.I.C., 1917-22; staff sergt.-major, Jamaica constab., Feb., 1922; sub-inspr., Feb., 1931; 3rd cls. inspr., Nov., 1932.

O'FARRELL, EDWARD JOSEPH.—Ed. Belvedere Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; solr., supt. et., Ireland, 1905; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; dep. offi. recr. for Coast Prov. and ex-officio agt. at Mompassa for pub. trustee, Kenya, 1930.
OGG, ROBERT CUTHBERT, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1901; rel. med. offr., Basutoland, 1927; med. offr., 1929.
OGILVIE, J.—B. 1886; ed. Fordyce Acad., Banffshire and Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; war serv., 1914-19; ast. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1919; divil. engrnr., 1923.

O'GRADY, WILLIAM MICHAEL, B.L.—B. 1886; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin, admitted solr., Ireland, 1908; mily. serv., Egypt, 1916; Salonika, 1916-17; Palestine, 1917-18; Egypt, 1919; called to bar, Ireland, 1920; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. puisne judge, high ct., on various occasions, 1927, 1932 and 1934-35.

OKE, HARRIS RENDELL, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newfoundland; studt. at law, 1909-14; The Royal Newfoundland Regt., 1914-15; The Royal Scots, 1915-20; served France, 1916-18 (M.C. and ment. in desps., 1918); served N. Russian Relief Force, 1919 (bar to M.C.); admstve. offr., Nigeria, 1920; transf'd. to admstve. serv., the Gambia, 1932; ag. col. sec., May-Aug. and Dec., 1933; ag. gov., Jan.-Apr., 1934; senr. ast. col. sec., Apr., 1934; col. sec., 1934; ag. gov., May-Oct., 1935.
OLDFIELD, H. G.—Tempy. ast. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919.

OLIPHANT, JOHN NINIAN, M.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. Glenalmond Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford, B.A. and dipl. forestry, 1909; imp. forest service, India, 1909-23; dep. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Dec., 1923; conservator, forests, 1924; dep. dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., 1929; ag. dir. forestry, F.M.S. and S.S. in 1931 and 1934.

OLIVER, GEO. A.—B. 1898; ent. G.P.O., Dec., 1914; apptd. ast. clk., Apr., 1916; wireless operator, R.N.V.R., Jan., 1917 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., June, 1923; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.
OLIVER, WALTER REGINALD BROCK, D.Sc., F.R. Soc. N.Z.—B. 1893; ed. Tauranga High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1900; Dom. museum staff, 1920; dir., 1928.

OLIVIER, 1ST BARON (created 1924), Rt. Hon. SIR SYDNEY OLIVIER, P.C. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1938), B.A.—B. 1859; ed. at Tombridge schl.; open exhibnr., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1878; 2nd class in final classical schl., 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford, 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890, to Apr., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Earl of Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Roy. comm. to inquire into condition of W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on sp. serv. in connect. with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, Apr., 1907; assumed gov't., May, 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913; mem. of comte., for settm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; asst. comptr. and audr., exchequer and audit dept., 1917; S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924.

O'MORCHOE, THE (Capt. ARTHUR DONALD MACMURROGH).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined Leinster Regt. (Royal Meaths), Aug., 1914; served in the great war; regt. signalling offr., 1915; asst. adjt., 1916-17; A.D.C. to maj. gen. Hon. E. J. Montague Stuart-Wortley and camp commdt., 65th divn., 1917-18; provost marshal's dept., 1918-22; junr. pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; pub. pros., 1924-25; supt., pol., Feb., 1926; ag. asupt., C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; adjt., 1928; ag. asst. commdt., 1930; A.D.C. to Br. res., Aug., 1930; pvt. sec., May, 1931; comsrr., pol., Gold Coast, 1934.

ONRAET, RENE HENRY DE SOLMINIHAC.—B. 1887; pol. prob., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1907; passed final exam. in Hokkien, July, 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1911; asst. supt. of pol., July, 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Jan., 1919; asst supt. of pol., Singapore, Mar., 1919; supt. pol. and dir. crim. intell., S.S., Mar., 1922; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1925, and June 1931; dir., crim. intell., S.S., Oct., 1926; dep. comsrr. pol., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1931; ag. inspr. gen. pol., S.S., Mar., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Oct., 1933; inspr.-gen., pol., S'pore, 1935.
OORLOOF, CEDRIC JAMES, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1930; attd., Kalutara kach., July, 1931; office asst., Hambantota Kach., Oct., 1932.
O'REILLY, HARRY ALLAN OSWALD.—B. 1893; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1924 (cert. of hon.); dep. regier. gen., Trinidad, 1934.

O'REILLY, JAMES GALLUS.—B. 1883; ed. Hughes' Acad., Belfast; R.I.C., May, 1902; inspr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; inspr., wghts. and mars., Aug., 1911-Dec., 1912; supvr., cust., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; supt., cust. prev. br., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; supvr., ext. duties, F.M.S., Prai and S'pore, Nov., 1925 and Jan., 1927; ch. supt., prev. br., cust. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore, June, 1930; dep. comsrr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1932; do., Selangor and Pahang, Oct., 1933; asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., Penang, Nov., 1934.

ORMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE ARTHUR, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Shropshire Yeomany; intell. officer Arab bureau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917-18; asst. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British mem., Permanent Mandates Coms., League of Nations, 1921-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied C.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; partly under sec. of state for Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 1924 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., partly, coms. to E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and rept. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926.

ORR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. and Woolwich Acad.; 1st comn., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 1904; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral relief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian frontier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expdn. force, 1897-99 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. African war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; 1st cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admnstr., N. Nigeria, May to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; admnstr. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to Aug., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill health, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918-19; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on several occasions; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1926-1931; ret.

ORR, LIONEL ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1905; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin, B.A., LL.B.; solr., sup. ct., I.F.S.; asst. compt., lands, Gold Coast, Aug., 1929 to Aug., 1932; asst. admistr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1932; ag. admistr.-gen., ag. offl. recvr., ag. regisr., high ct., ag. regisr. of companies, ag. regisr. of patents, trade marks and designs, ag. regisr. of deeds, Mar.-June, 1934; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; st. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. reg.-gen. in addn. June to July, 1924.

OSTLER, HON. HENRY HUBERT, K.C. (1925), LL.B. (1904).—B. 1876; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr. N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-10; 1st asst., crown law office and crown pros., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1926; examr. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

O'SULLIVAN, TIMOTHY ADOLPHUS, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1894; ed. King's Hosp. and Trinity coll., Dublin; asst. mast., col. for blind, Worcester, St. Christopher's, Eastbourne, Ranelagh sch., Athlone, 1913-15; Inns Ct. O.T.C. and R.A., 1915-19; asst. mast., Raffles inst., Singapore, 1919; inspr., schls., Pahang, Feb., 1922; do., Malacca, Nov., 1924; do., Selangor, Nov., 1924; senr. educn. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., schls., Penang, Nov., 1931.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V, Apr., 1923; asst. dist. offr., T. Malim, Feb., 1925; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot., lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1932.

OTWAY, J. H., O.B.E. (1935).—Ck., treasy., Grenada, 1897; 2nd audit clk., 1899; audit clk., St. Vincent, 1901; audit clk., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub oll. and dist. offr., Soufriere; J.P. for the col.; cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; ch. clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Vincent, 1918; ag. ch. clk. and pvt. sec. to gov., Grenada, 1923; col. treas., contr., cust. and ch. of excise, St. Vincent, 1923; ag. admistr. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, ALBERT EDWARD.—Asst. acct. N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1915; attd., lands dept., Oct., 1915 to May, 1922; treasy., May, 1922; ch. clk., dept., agr., Jan., 1927; treasy., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., in 1928, 1929 and 1931; contr., stores and transport, Dec., 1932.

OWEN, A. H., B.A. (Camb.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), Cert. of S.T.M. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ap. serv. (venereal diseases), Uganda, 1912; active serv., K.A. Campaign, and tempy. capt., U.M.S., Jan., 1915 to May, 1918; ment. in desps.; senr. med. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1921; sen. sany. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; dep. dir., sany. serv., 1928; dir., med. and sany. services, 1932.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schol. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; ast. sec., local defence com- tee, 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. comm., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Conf., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S.W. Knagge in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G.R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J.R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T.A.V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; ast. col. sec., clk. to exec. coun., combined et. and ct. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1931; ag. gov. in 1932, 1933 and 1935.

OWEN, GEORGE HUMPHREY RICHARD ST. JOHN.—Cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; 2nd grade admstr. offr., July, 1922; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Feb. to Aug., 1929; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; ag. ast. admstr.-gen., 1933.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown agt.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to comm. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916; returned to crown agt.'s office, Sept., 1919.

OWEN, JOHN HENRY, D.S.C. (1917).—Younger brother, Trinity House, London; master mariner; ed. Univ. Coll. Schol., London and H.M.S. "Worcester"; served in war, 1914-18; lieut., naval reserve; port mine-sweeping offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1918; harbour. and naval agt., Kingston, Jamaica, Jan., 1919; harbour., Malacca, 1927.
OWEN, WILFRED HERBERT, B.Sc.Tech., A.R.I.B.A., Chart. Aroh.—B. 1896; ed. St. John's, Cheetham, Manchester, Manchester Gram- mar Schol. and Manchester Univ.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.

PAGE, HON. EDWARD.—B. 1877; ed. Well- ington Coll., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice some years; mayor of Eketahuna, 1909-12; stip. mag., 1912-35; chmn., many inquiry comm.; judge, N.Z. ct. of arbitn., 1935.

PAGE, RT. HON. EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schol. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Develop- ment League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fed. parl., 1930; min. for commerce and dep. leader, Lyons min., 1934.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD.—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk. mgte. off., July, 1902; ast. mgte., Mar., 1913; rgt., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, May, 1928; mag., Pieterburg, Sept., 1928; ditto, Durban, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PAILTHORPE, WULFRED ARTHUR BOYNTON.—B. 1875; ed. King Edward VI Schol., Chelmsford, and Gray's Coll., Essex; serv., S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. and 6 clasps, King's med. and 2 clasps); registrn. clk., E.A.P., 1904; registr. of docs., 1910; collr. under Stamp Act, 1910; ex-oficio ast. for admstr.-gen., 1911; ag. prin. registr. of docs., crown lands, etc., 1915-19; ch. controlling rev. authority, 1918; dep. registr.-gen. of titles, Kenya, 1919; prin. registr. of docs., crown lands and land titles, 1920.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. mast., St. George's Schol., Windsor Castle, 1913; ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., 6th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926; res. mag., 1929.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compt. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 8th Mar., 1920; second ast. acctn., 5th Nov., 1928.

PALMER, HON. HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; commr., native rev., N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res., Bornu Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educn. proposals, 1918; ag. lieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; lieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1925; gov., Gambia, 1930; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOHN CLIFFORD, F.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Schol. and Queen's Coll., Oxon.; survr., Can. Pac. Rly., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., 1913; adjt., R.E., ord. surv. office, Southampton, 1918; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1920; ex. offr., land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land offr. and survr., Gambia, 1934.

PANES, JOHN NELSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dean Close Schol. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (M.A. hon.); mast., King Edward VI's Schol., Norwich, 1914-22; ast. mast., King's Coll., Lagos, Nigeria, 1922; supt., edcn., S.P., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. inspr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1931.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence comtee., 1898; priv. sec. to late Sir F.W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims of applicants for the fenian raid volunteer bounty, 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd.; ment. in desp. for serv. ended in Can., 1914-1918.
PAPAFIO, M.K.Q.—Ed. Accra Grammar Schol. and in England; 6th grade teacher, 1911; 5th grade do., 1912; 2nd cls. do., 1919; 1st cls. do., 1920; headmistress, 1921.

PAPATETROU, ANTONI, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1878; clk., land registn. and survey dept., Cyprus, Oct., 1903; inspr., land registn. and survey dept., Apr., 1919; ast. regr.-gen., Mar., 1927; title altered to ast. dir., land registn. and surveys, July, 1927; ag. dir., land registn. and surveys for various periods, 1928-35.

PARHAM, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I., Chartered Archt. and Quantity Surv., mem., Town Planning Inst.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll., London; temp. architectural asst., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1915; 1st ast. archt., 1918; ag. ch. archt., in 1919, 1923, 1925, 1927 and 1929; addl., town planning divn., Miny. of Health, 1926; ast. commr., income tax, Ceylon, 1934.

PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, O.B.E. (1933), M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Sch.; European War, 1914-17; lieut. 6th (Serv.) Batt., Border Regt., M.C., desps.; capt., Somaliland Camel Corps, 1917-20; operns. against Mullah, desps.; dist. police offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARKE, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; R.I.C., July, 1908-May, 1913; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. ast. commr., pol., Nov., 1923; ast. commr., detect. br., Perak, Nov., 1924; dir. crim. intell. br., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1927; do., F.M.S., June, 1932; dep. commr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1932; dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., Oct., 1934.

PARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Coman., 7th Batt., Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream Guards, 1911; on war ser., Uganda and India; 1913-18; selected by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Infy., Aden, 1919-20; cadet, Zanzibar pol., 1923-25; ast. finan. advr. and comdt., pol., govt. of Bahrain, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr. to govt.); 1st grade inspr., Mauritius pol., 1933.

PARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C., M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ast. engr., Peru, 1910; ast. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; lieut., R.E. (Field Co.), 1915-19; served in France and Italy; ment. in desps., 1917; M.C., 1918; war and victory meda.; ex. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1920; sen. ex. engr., Apr., 1927; state engr., Perak, Jan., 1930; state engr., Perak, Oct., 1933; dep. D.P.W., S.S., 1935.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmr., Bethesda, 1901; schmr., govt. boys' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, LANCELOT—B. 1879; clk., cust., Joh'burg, 1902; survr., Cape Town, 1915; served R.F.A. in Great War; cust. investigation offr. for Union in New York, 1926; collr. cust. and shipping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Durban, 1933.

PARKER, PELHAM.—B. 1888; Mediterranean and Egyptian Expedy. Forces, 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Salonika, Palestine and Egypt); 2nd lieut. (Gen. List), 1917; lieut., 1918; miny. of P.W., Egypt (Sudan irrig. serv.), 1921-22; ast. accntnt., P.W.D., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; ag. accntnt., June to Sept., 1930 seconded to treas., Nov. to Dec., 1930; ast. accntnt., 1st gr., Jan., 1932; ch. accntnt., Nov., 1934.

PARKES, EDWARD, M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1906; clk. of exec. coun., 1925; sec. to premier, 1925; ofl. sec., Tasmanian agt.-gen.'s office, London, 1927-28; under sec. and ch. electoral offr., 1930; state organizer, visit of H.R.H. the Duke of Gloucester, 1931.
PARKHILL, ROBERT ARCHADE.—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1927; min. for home affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932; P.M.G., 1932-34; min. for defence, 1934.

PARKINSON, SIR ARTHUR CHARLES COSMO, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class. demy, Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. moda., 1906; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. clk., acctut.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf'd. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comtee. of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comtee. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-14; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920; ast. sec., 1st Oct., 1925; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929; ast. under-sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

PARMELEE, JAMES GRANNIS, O.B.E.—B.1876; inspr., iron and steel bounties, Sydney, Nova Scotia; trade and commerce, Canada, 1902; clk., dept. T. and C., Apr., 1911; on mly. serv., 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); head clk., Apr., 1919; ch. clk., Jan., 1921; ast. dep. min. T. and C., Apr., 1923; on sp. duty to U.K., 1923; dep. min., trade and commerce, Nov., 1931.

PARNALL, JOHN.—Solr.; ast. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; ast. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, Sept. 1919 to July, 1920; regsr. and offr. assignee, H.B.M. ot., Zanzibar; regsr. of patents, designs and trade marks, Feb., 1920; ag. admstr.-gen. and regsr. of docs., Apr. to Oct., 1922; ast. admstr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1923; ag. admstr.-gen., Oct., 1923 to Jan., 1924; admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, regsr. of docs. and land offr., Zanzibar, May, 1924; ag. atty.-gen., June-Dec., 1929; also offr. assignee, regsr., patents, designs and trade marks, Jan., 1934; ag. treas., Nov., 1934.

PARR, HON. SIR CHRISTOPHER JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1938), K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-26; min. of educn., 1920-26; min. of health, 1920-23; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1925-26; high commr. for N.Z. in England, 1926-29 and from 1934; M.L.C. and leader of leg. coun., 1931-33.
PARRY, J. H.—B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. commr. in Indian med. serv., Mar., 1915 to Mar. 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 'Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1928.
PARRY, REGINALD FRANCIS SCARLETT.—Ed., St John's Schl., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1934.
PARSONS, HON. SIR HERBERT ANGAS, KT. BACH (1936), LL.B.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARTRIDGE, GEORGE JAMES, B.A. (1915), M.A. (1921), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhibr. (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripos, part I., 1913, B.A. (War), 1915; temp. commr., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; ast. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admstr., German E. Africa, Dec. 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; ast. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. senr. ast. sec., on various occasions, 1921-23; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr.-ast. sec., Aug., 1925; ag. ast. ch. sec., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; ast. ch. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-33; 1st mast. ch. sec., 1934.
PASCHALIS, Neoptolemos, K.C. (1931).—B. 1880; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1902; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods, 1927-35.

PASKIN, Jesse John, B.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed., King Edwards Sch., Stourbridge and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripes, pt. I., 1913; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf'd. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; app'td. under reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. elk., miny. of transport, Jan., 1920; asst. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 4th Sept., 1929.

PASSFIELD, 1st Baron (CB. 1929) Rt. Hon. Sidney James Webb, P.C., 1924, LL.B. (Lond.), Hon. Doc., Polt. Economy (Munich), etc.—B. 1859; ed. London and abroad; ent. W.O. by open compet., 1878; surv. of taxes, 1879; C.O., cls. I by open compet., 1881; resigned, 1891; sometime hon. lect. on polit. economy at City of London Coll. and Working Men's Coll. and subsequently hon. prof., public adminstr., London School of Economics, 1912-27; M.P. (Seaham Divn.), 1922-29; pres., Bd. of Trade, Jan.-Nov., 1924; sec. of state for dom. affrs. and the cols., June, 1929; has served on numerous royal comsns. and deptl. comtees.; author of many works on politics and economics.

PASSINGHAM, Frank Selwood.—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hons. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914; supt. of printing, Jamaica, Mar., 1924; supt., govt. printing office, Cyprus, Oct., 1930.

PATENAUIDE, Joseph Oscar, I.S.O.—Ed. Longueuil Coll., Quebec; cls., Dom. govt. stationery office, 1889; asst. supt., Dom. govt. stationery, Ottawa, 1899; supt., stationery, 1911; contr., purchases dept., pub. printing and stationery, 1920; King's printer of Canada, 1933.
PATERNON, Capt. Gavin, M.M.—B. 1891; cust. dept., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; senr. collr., cust., Apr., 1927; prov. collr., cust., June, 1928; title changed to asst. comptlr., cust., Jan., 1932.

PATERNON, Henry Sibbald.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol.) and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhbr.), B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; seconded for war serv., Mar., 1915; active serv. in France and England, 1915-19; ment. in desp.; demob. with rank of major, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1919; seconded as asst. adviser, Trengganu, July, 1920; supernumy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; asst. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; ag. advr., land office, Kedah, Apr., 1933.

PATERNON, Ralph Stanley Watson, M.C.—B. 1894; ed. Perth Acad. and Tech. Coll., Dundee; val. dept., inland rev., 1913; R.E., 1915; county surv., Zetland, 1919; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; tech. secy., D.P.W., 1932.

PATERNON, Hon. Thomas, M.P.—El. mem. of H. of R., C'wealth of Australia for Gippsland, 1922; initiated voluntary butter stabilization plan known as the Paterson scheme, 1926; min. for markets and migrn., 1926-22; min. for markets and transp., 1928-29; min. for interior, 1935.

PATERNON, Francis Tucker, Dip. civ. Engngnr. (Bristol), M. Inst. C.E.—E. 1883; Axminster and Lyme Regis rly., 1901-03; Cape Col. water supply investgn., 1903-06; pub. wks. constr., England, 1907-13; circlc engrnr., irrign. dept., Union of S. Africa, 1914-19; engrnr., Kamanassie irrign. bd., 1919-24; suptg. engrnr., irrign. dept., 1924-26; asst. dir., irrign., 1926; ag. dir., irrign., Aug., 1925.
PATERNON, John Robert.—B.1892; B.A. (Cantab.); asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. snr. res., Bornu Prov., Sept., 1925; admstv. offr., cls.I, Aug., 1930; prin. asst. sec., Oct., 1930.

PAUL, Robert James.—B. 1888; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Sept., 1912; 2nd lieut., Trinidad local forces, Jan., 1913; pay and qr-mr., constab., and snr. supply and transport offr., Trinidad local forces, Nov., 1914, to Oct., 1916, and from Apr. to May, 1917; ag. inspr. of constab. and cap. local forces, Aug., 1915; asst. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1915, and from Feb. to Mar., 1919; ag. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1915, to Sep., 1916; J.P. for the colony; ag. chief of police, comdt. local forces, asupt. of prison, St. Vincent, Aug., 1919; J.P. for the colony; supt. of pol., Nyasaland, Mar., 1923; ag. contr., pol. and prisons, May, 1925 to Sept., 1926; ag. ch. comsnr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, in 1926, 1929 and 1932.

PAULIN, George.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rlys., New Zealand, with city engrnr., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrnr. and engrnr.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrnr.-survr., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrnr., Fiji, 1912; res. engrnr., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAVLIDES, Stelios.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1916; crown coun., Cyprus Dec., 1927; ag. solr.-gen., for various periods, 1928-35.
PAWAN, James Lenox, M.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., Mar., 1916; bacteriologist and pathological registr., Nov., 1923.

PAYNE, Ralph Lavington.—B. 1886; ed. privately; col. sec.'s, office, Grenada, 1903; supervsr., cust., Nigeria, 1911; admstv. offr. (N.P. secretariat), 1915; on active serv., 1917-19; resumed duty, 1919; ag. prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Apr., 1930 to Aug., 1931; dep. res., July, 1934.

PEACH, Leonard William, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M. & Cy. E.—B. 1888; dist. engrnr., addt. to prov. engrnr., N.W.P., Ceylon, 1914; on war serv., 1914-19; dist. engrnr., Ceylon, 1919; exec. engrnr., Nigeria, P.W.D., 1924; senr. exec. engrnr., 1930.

PEAKE, Harold Gordon, B.Sc. (hons.), Lond., A.C.G.I., M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Eltham Coll. and Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London; admry. engrgr. staff, Rosyth dockyard, 1912; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; mily. wks. services, India, 1919; engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1924; senr. exec. engrnr., 1928; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1933.

PEARCE, Alexander Hugh Blaxell, L.R.C.P. & S., Edin., L.R.F.P.S., Glas. (1904), L.M., Dublin (1906); D.P.H., Dublin (1918), certif., L.S.T.M., F.R.I., pub. health F.R. Soc. Trop. Med. and Hygiene.—B. 1882; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin., Univ., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; junr. med. offr., Montserrat, 1907; D.M.O., St. Kitts, 1908; res. med. supt., charitable inst., Antigua and M.O.H. Antigua, 1908; col. surg., Falkland Is., 1912; M.E.C. and M.L.C., J.P.; dormant comsnr. to admtr. govt., Falkland Is.; pres., bd. of health, surg.-capt., F.I. Vol., Aug., 1914; seconded, R.A.M.C., 1916-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; served in France and Far East; C.M.O., Bahamas, 1918; M.L.C. and J.P.; D.M.S. and M.E.C., 1928; ch. med. offr., Fiji; M.L.C. and chmn., cent. bd. of health, Nov., 1930; rep., G.B., Fiji, and W. Pacific at Internat. Pacific Health Conf., Sydney, Sept., 1935.

PEARCE, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE FOSTER, K.C.V.O. (1927), P.C. (1921).—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlmt.; sec. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comtees; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence Sept., 1914; rep. Australia at the Washington Disarmament Conf., 1921; min. for home and territories in Bruce ministry, 1923; vice-pres., exec. coun., June, 1926; min. of defence and govt. leader in senate (Lyons' ministry), 1932; resigned mem. of defence and apptd. mem. for external affrs. and min. in ch., territories, 1934.

PEARCE, HENRY JOSEPH, M.C., M.Inst. Struct.E., L.R.I.B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Castle House Schl., Weymouth and Weymouth Tech. and engrg. Schl.; asst. engnr., Hong Kong, 1919; dep. head, bldg. ord. office, 1925; exec. engnr., bldgs. ord. office, 1930.

PEARCE, PHILIP GEORGE.—B. 1888; ed. Mt. Albert Schl. and Coromandel dist. High Schl., N.Z.; joined N.Z. valuation dept., 1904; justice dept., 1919; lands and survey dept., 1921; mines dept., 1921; superv., pub. serv. comsrs.'s office, 1926; under-sec., native dept. and native trustee, 1933-35; comsrs., stamp duties, and sec., lands and deeds, 1935.

PEARSON, COLIN BATEMAN, M.A. Cantab.—B. 1889; capt., R.A.R. of O., Durham L.I.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; European mag., Tonga, Sept., 1928; ag. land ct. judge, 1930; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Oct., 1932; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, May to Sept., 1934; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., July, 1935.

PEARSON, JAMES RAH., C.I.E.—B. 1871; schol., St. Paul's Schl. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ent. I.C.S., 1892; held various Indian appts., 1892-1926; ret., I.C.S., Aug., 1926; sec. for Indian affrs., Fiji, 1927.
PECK, MAJOR GEORGE EDGAR, M.C., R.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1875; ed. City of London Engng. Coll.; engrn. and man., water works dept., Barbados, 1923; dir. P.W., 1934.

PEDLER, FREDERICK JOHNSON, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Watford Grammar Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; major schol., 1927-30; Goldsmith's exhib., 1929; cls. I. divn. 1, hist. tripos, pts. I. and II; B.A., 1930; Schuldhaim plate prizewinner; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; seconded to prov. adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934 to Jan., 1936; pvt. sec. to perm. U.S. of S. for the cols., 11th Jan., 1936.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.), Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1912; Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy, to study Hokkien, Sep., 1913; Chinese Prot., S'pore, Mar., 1916; treasy. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, S'pore, in addition, Mar. to June, 1918; pol. mag., S'pore, Aug., 1919; supervm. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; astt. to atty.-gen., S'pore, Dec., 1920; ag. dep. treas., S'pore, Jan., 1921; offr., astt. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, June, 1921; pol. mag., Penang, Oct., 1921; mem., rent assessment bd., Penang, and munie. comsrs., George Town, Nov., 1921 and Oct., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1924; prot. Chinese, Penang, Deo., 1924; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; dep. pres., munie. comsrs., George Town, Penang, (cls. IB.) in addn., Oct., 1926; regiar., sup. ct., in addn., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, June, 1929; do., S'pore, Sept., 1929; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Dec., 1930; ag. solr. gen., S.S., 1931-34; dist. judge, S'pore, 1933; offr., cls. I.B., Dec., 1933; ag. atty.-gen., S.S.and mem., exec.and leg.couns.; solr.-gen., S.S., Oct., 1934; ag. judge, F.M.S., Dec., 1935.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. comsrs., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEL, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), B.A., 1896, M.A., 1931—B. 1875; ed. Queens' Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripos., 1896; cadet, S. Stiltns., Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ditto, Tampin, F.M.S., June, 1908; seconded as pres., mun. comsrs., Penang, Jan., 1911; offr., Class III., but remained seconded, Jan., 1912; ag. res. councillor, Penang, in addn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. comsrs., Singapore, July, 1918; offr., Class II., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; joint passage contrlr., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1919; seconded as food contrlr., Malaya and chmn., shipping ctee., May, 1919; chmn., European unemployment comte. in addn., Feb., 1921; contr., lab., F.M.S. and S. Stiltns., Feb., 1922; Br. adviser, Kedah, Oct., 1922; res. coun., Penang, July, 1925; ch. sec., F.M.S., June, 1926; offr. administering govt., S.S. and high comsrs., Malay States, May to June, 1927; on ap. duty, London, Mar., 1928; gov. and commodr-in-ch., Hong Kong, Mar., 1930; ret., 1935; kt. of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; hon. fellow, Queens Coll., Camb., 1932.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; clk., audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasy. dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and astt. examg. offr., customs, 1914; compt. of cust., Apr., 1921; ag. recvr-gen., treas. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1925-28; ag. postmr., Sept., 1928; has held numerous other ag. appts.; postmr., Sept., 1928.

PEGG, HUGH HANDLEY.—B. 1889; ed. Ironville Schl., Derbyshire and pvtly.; astt. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; astt. engrn., Jan., 1921; mem., Kowloon communication comte., 1924; dep. head, rds. office, Jan., 1925; exec. engrn., Dec., 1932.
PELLISSIER, S. H., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; vice prin., Boshof, 1914; prin., Ficksburg, 1917; dir., educn., O.F.S., Dec., 1926.
PELLY, RUSSELL STEELE.—B. 1910; ed. Mercer's Schl. and Edin. and Oxford Univs.; B.Sc. (Forestry Edin.), 1931; astt. conservator (forests), Br. Hond., Oct., 1932.
PEMBLETON, EDGAR STANLEY, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1888; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 1911; res., 1928; senr. res., Aug., 1934.

PENGILLEY, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp. (exhbr.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); lieut., 23rd Batt., Royal Fusiliers, 1917-19; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1924; astt. dist. offr., Rembau, Feb., 1924; tempy. astt. for duty in C.O. as astt. prin., Nov., 1925 May, 1926; astt. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and dep. regiar., titles, Selangor, June, 1926; offr., cls. IV., Jan.

PENNEFATHER-EVANS, JOHN PENNFATHER.—B. 1894; pol. prob., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; A.C.P., Jan., 1918; adjt., fed. hqrs., June, 1921; ag. comdt. pol., depot, Mar., 1924; offr., supt. cir., Taiping, Apr., 1926; ag. comsnr., pol., Kedah, Mar., 1931; supt., pol. S.S., Sept., 1932; comdt., pol. depot, S'pore, Dec., 1934.
PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

PENTZ, HERBERT FREDERICK, M.C., M.M.—B. 1886; clk., col. sec., s.offr., Cape, 1903; clk., Cape adminstr., 1910; clk., ditto, 1928; pub. serv. inspr., 1930; served in S. African infy. brg. in Great War (overseas); sec. for pub. wks., Aug., 1931; prov. sec., Transvaal, Apr., 1934.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1885; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1908; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; do., Pusat Puteh, June, 1915 and Jan., 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1918; offr., class III., Nov., 1920; fed. sect. for sp. duty, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1925; ast. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; offr. cls. I.B., comnr., lands and mines, Johore, Jan., 1928; offr., cls. I.A., under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; supt., govt. monopolies, S.S., Mar., 1932, and comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S. in addn., Apr., 1932; mem., S.S. trade comnr., in addn., Feb., 1933; comnr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., 1934; gen. advr., Johore, 1936.

PERCY, MAJOR GEORGE REGINALD, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr., Chtd. Survr.—B. 1890; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., May, 1919; exec. engrnr., Feb., 1928; ag. exec. engrnr., Aug., 1929; ag. prin., trade schl., K. Lumpur, in addn., Aug., 1930; sr. exec. engrnr., wks. and bldgs., June, 1932; exec. engrnr., Malacca, Jan., 1934; muncl. engrnr. in addn., Feb., 1934.

PERCY, H. VANE (formerly Baumgartner)—B. 1883; ed. Bedford Schl.; clk., land settl. dept., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; clk. and typist audit dept., Dec., 1902; clk., audit dept., July, 1904; examr. of acct., audit dept., Jan., 1908; jnr. ast. treas., G. Coast, Jan., 1914; ast. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. ast. treas., Nov., 1919; ag. dep. treas. on several occasions, 1921-28; dep. treas., 1929; ag. treas., in 1930, 1932 and 1934.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; pol. prob., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; passed in Chinese, 1915; ast. to Punjab pol., 1915-16; passed in Urdu, 1916; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army res., 1916-19; J.P., 1920; ast. supt., pol., 1920; ast. census offr., 1921; dir., crim. intell. 1930; divnl. supt., pol., 1930; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar., 1935.

PERERA, BENJAMIN FRANKLIN.—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; astd., Colombo kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., Apr., 1926; office ast., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1928; office ast., Kandy kach., Nov., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Matara, Aug., 1930; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Galle, in addn., Mar., 1934.

PERERA, CONRAD BONIFACE PETER, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; astd., Badulla kach., Mar., 1927; astd., Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1927; office ast., Puttalam kach., Apr., 1928; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1930; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, June, 1933; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1933; ast. attm. offr. and ast. to rubber investigating offr. in addn., May, 1934.
PERERA, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1885; Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1930; extra office ast., Ratnapura kach., Nov., 1930; office ast., Kalutara kach., Jan., 1934.
PERERA, HENRY SAMUEL, M.A. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1885; divnl. inspr., schl., Ceylon, Dec., 1922; prin., training coll., Sept., 1932.

PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN, LL.B. (Lond.)—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; astd. Kurunegala kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Avisawella, July, 1921; ast. comnr. of stamps, July, 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Oct., 1926; ast. comnr., stamps, Nov., 1926; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1929; ast. to income tax adviser, Sept., 1929; sec. to min. of agr. and lands, July, 1931; ast. comnr., income tax, Feb., 1932.

PEREZ, JOSEPH LEON MATHEU, LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. St. George's Coll., Weybridge, Surrey; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; dep. regt.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1927; ag. regt. gen., regt. sup. ct. and marshal in 1928 and 1929; ag. solr.-gen., July to Nov., 1929; ag. ast. mag., St. George West, Nov., 1929 to May, 1930; mag., St. George West, June, 1930; do., Victoria, 1932; ast. law offr., 1934.

PERIES, HERMAN ERIC, B.A. (Cantab), B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; astd. secretariat, July, 1929; astd., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1930; astd., rubber contr. office for sp. wk., July, 1934; office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Aug., 1934.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, Mar., 1917, served during war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; 1st-lieut., Oct., 1918; 1st clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade supvr. of cus., Nigeria, Feb., 1920.

PERKY, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE HALSEY, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1933). K.C.M.G. (1915). B. 1857; ed. Ottawa gram. schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ.; B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C. Canada (Argenteuil), 1904; re-elec., 1908 and 1911; chief conser. whip, 1911; mem., privy coun. for Canada and min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high comnr. in London, 1914; min. of overseas mil. forces, 1916; high comnr., 1917; joint comnr. and plenipotentiary of Canada to sign Peace Treaties, 1919; Canadian del. to L. of N. Assem., 1921; resigned high comnrship, 1922; re-el. to H. of C., 1925; sec. of state in Meighan Cabinet, 1926; re-el., g.e., 1926; min. without portfolio in Bennett adminstr., 1930; re-el., g.e., 1936.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—B.1869; prin. civ. med.off. and inspr.gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.

PERRY, B. C. G.—B.1886; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; regisr., C.O., 7th Sept., 1931.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Aast. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb. to Mar., 1894; ag. aast. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanai mountains; sen. aast. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.
PERSSE, CLARE, EDMUND MATURIN, M.C. (1918), F.R.G.S., R. of O., W. Yorks. Regt.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Mod. Sch.; aast. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Oct., 1919; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1925.

PETERKIN, EDMUND MURRAY.—B. 1886; agr. aast., dept. agr., Barbados, 1909; agr. instr., dept. sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1915; hort. supt., bot. gdns., 1917; clk. and sec., bd. agr., 1918; ag. aast. bot. and mycologist, 1922-23; agr. supt., 1928; investigated rice conditions, Br. Honduras, 1933.
PETERS, BERNARD R., M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Aast. engr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engr., Tanganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 1927; D.P.W., Nyasaland, Aug., 1928.

PETRIDES, SIR PHILIP BERTIE, KT. BACH. (1936)—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brussels; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns., ag. chief just., July, 1916 to Mar., 1917, May, 1918 to Oct., 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. empld., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, June, 1920; admstd. govt., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1924; ag. judge, May to Dec., 1925; puisne judge, Nigeria, Sept., 1926; ch. just., Mauritius, May, 1930; do. Gold Coast, Dec., 1935.
PETTMAN, P. A. H.—B. 1896; aoct., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1925; aast. treas., Uganda, 1936.

PHEAR, HAROLD WILLIAM, M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Chord Schl.; war serv., 1914-19; pol. inspr., pol. Singapore, Dec., 1919; det. inspr., pol. Aug., 1921; aast. supvr., cust., F.M.S. and ag. supvr., ext. duties, Singapore, Aug., 1923; supr. cust., prev. br., Selangor, Nov., 1923; do., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1924; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; head, prev. ser., Singapore, Feb., 1932; J.P., Singapore, Mar., 1932; dep. supt., rubber control, S'pore, June, 1934; aast. supt., petroleum rev., July, 1934.
PHILLIPS, C. H., L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—Tempy, lieut., R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915 (General List); med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treas., customs and P.O. depts., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treas. and cust. dept., May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1900; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 1909-10; to gov. Collett, 1911-13; to ag. gov., Walter, 1916; ag. comsnr. of currency, Feb., 1911; ag. treas., collr. of cust., 1911-14 and 1917-20; ag. col. sec., 1920; Br. Honduras del. to Canada-W. Indies Conf., Ottawa, June, 1920; aast. col. sec., Apr., 1921; comsnr. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., June, 1923.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM JAMES ELBERTY, M.C., M.B., B.S., D.T.M. & H., Lond.—B. 1891; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide and Adelaide Univ.; on war serv., major A.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Malayan med. serv., 1923; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Feb., 1935.

PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY, M.A. (Manchester).—B. 1892; on mily. serv., May, 1915; cadet Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attached to Kandy kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Matara, Sept., 1922; office aast., Kurunegala kach., July, 1924; aast. contr., rev., custodian, enemy property and contr., local clearing office, in addn., Jan., 1925; sec., salaries comm., Nov., 1926; sec., land comm., in addn., July, 1927 to March, 1928; aast. sec., sp. comm., on constitution in addn., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; addnl. aast. col. treas., Nov., 1929 and June, 1930; contr., estab., July, 1931; contr., supplies and finance, July, 1932; contr., estab., Feb., 1934.
PHILP, DAVID.—B. 1893; aast. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1919; ag. constrn. acct., Mar., 1925; ag. dep. acct., Oct., 1930 and Apr., 1932; ag. aast. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931.
PICKERING, SIR GEORGE HUNTER, KT. BACH. (1932).—Town mag., E. A.P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1915; puisne judge, 1917; ag. ch. just., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ch. just., Zanzibar, 1928; ret., 1932.

PICKWORTH, CECIL HENRY.—B. 1890; war serv., in France, 1914-19; attd., experimental dept. of tanks, miny. of munitions, 1917-19; res. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1921; res. engr., Singapore municipality, 1924; elec. engr.-in-ch., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.
PIDDUCK, H. B.—Ed. Harper Adams agrl. coll., Univ. of Wales; Shropshire Yeomanry, 1914-19; science mast., Dominica Grammar Schl., 1922; headmast., 1923; agr. offr., 1929.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES GUSTAF EBANK.—Clk. of ct., Lichtenburg, July, 1902; ag. pub. prosecutor, Feb., 1903; pub. prosecutor, June, 1903; ch. clk. and ag. aast. res. mag., Nov., 1906; addnl. aast. res. mag., Krugersdorp, Sept., 1906; res. mag. and native comsnr., Waterberg, Nov., 1910; res. mag., Ermelo, Apr., 1912; secr. mag., Germiston, May, 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921; mag., Louis Trichardt dept. of just., Aug., 1924; sec. for int., Dec., 1924; chmn., pub. serv. coman., Aug., 1926; chmn., Union tender and supplies bd., June, 1934.

PIENAAR, MAJOR FILIPPUS FOURIE, D.T.D., O.B.E.—Civ. serv., S. A. Republic; dep.-ohmn., miner's phthisis bd., Witwatersrand; accredited rep. of Union of S. Africa to League of Nations; cons-gen. for Union of S. Africa, Laurencio Marques, 1934.
PIERS, FRANCIS A.—B. 1882; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; inspr., 1923.

PIERS, HAROLD HENRY ROSS.—Cler. aast., Peddie, Cape Prov., 1895; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1895; R.M. clk., Peddie, 1898; on active serv., served Boer War, 1899-1900; sur. clk., comsnr., Kimberley, 1902; first clk., Calvinia, 1903; Albany, 1907; aast. mag., Albany, 1907; 3rd grade mag., Keiskama Hoek, 1914; ditto, Hopefield, 1919; 2nd grade mag., Molteno, 1920; 1st grade mag., Greytown, 1927; senr. grade mag., Windhoek, S.W.A., June, 1929; sp. "B" mag., E. London, Mar., 1933; sp. "A" mag., Pretoria, 1935.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, Apr., 1906; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts" and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed astt. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th May, 1919; assigned to C.O. as astt. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
PILKINGTON, CHARLES WILLIAM McDONALD.—B. 1877; clk., cust., Cape Town, 1895; ch. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Pretoria, 1933; collr., cust., Johannesburg, 1934.

PILLING, HENRY GUY, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. King's Schl., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Comsn., 1920; ag. astt. col. sec., 1920; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comsn., Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high comsn., 1926; astt. to high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1929; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Dec., 1929; ag. gov. in 1930 and 1931; dep. col. sec., Kenya, 1933.

PINDER, RICHARD FRANCIS, (R. of O.).—B. 1898; ed. St. Bees, Cumberland; served, European War, Flanders, Germany, 1916-19; astt. audr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920; ag. audr., Nov.-Dec., 1920; astt. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1929; ag. audr., Feb. May, 1930; sen. astt. audr., Uganda, Aug., 1931; ag. audr., July-Dec., 1932; audr., Fiji, Solomon Is., Prot., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Col., Aug., 1934.

PINKERTON, WILLIAM JAMES DUNLOP, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrnr.—B. 1890; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engrnr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1923; sr. exec. engrnr., in addn., Jan., 1930; offtd. as sr. exec. engrnr., Kinta, in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1930; senr. drainage and irrign. engrnr. Malay States, Jan., 1932; irrign. engrnr., Kelantan, Jan., 1932.

PINNEY, CAPT. CHAS. ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Schl.; draughtsman, lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transf'd. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsman, Papua, 1920; staff survr. in addn., 1924; ag. oomsnr., lands, etc., 1927; comsnr., lands, dir., mines, dir., agr., dep. chmn., land bd., mem., exec. and leg. couns., July, 1930; adminr., Norfolk Is., July, 1932.

PIRIE, D. A.—B. 1886; clk. Burgher camps, Transvaal, 1903; treas'y., 1903; prin. clk. treas'y., Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. milly. pensions bd., treas'y., 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1928; ch. clk., grade I, 1932.

PIROW, HANS, D.Sc., M.Inst.M.M., D.I.C., vice-pres., Geol. Soc. of S.A.; mem., coun., Chem. Met. and Min. Soc., and S.A. Assoc. for the Adv. of Science.—B. 1892; ed. S. Africa and on continent; grad., S.A. and London; various offl. capacities on Witwatersrand gold mines, 1911-1919; govt. research schol., 1919-20; astt. inspr., mines, 1920; dominion sci. schol., London, 1924; G.M.E., Dec., 1926; is also mem., mining leases bd. and elec. control bd., assessor mem., research grant bd. and statistical coun.; ag. sec., mines and industries, 1927.

PIROW, OSWALD.—B. 1890; ed. South Africa, Germany and England; admitted barrister, 1913; K.C., 1924; ent. parl., of the Union of S. Africa, 1924; min. of justice, June, 1929; min., rly. and harb's and min. defence, Mar., 1933; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933.

PITBLADO, DAVID BRUCE.—B. 1912; ed. Strand Schl. and Cambridge; schol. of Emmanuel Coll., 1930-34; hist. triplos part 1 and law triplos part 2, cls. 2 divn. 1; hist. triplos part 2, cls. 2; B.A., 1933; supervisor in hist., Emmanuel Coll., 1934-35; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. prin., D.O., Oct., 1935.

PITCAIRN, ANDREW.—B. 1894; ed. Edinburgh Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., 1915-19 (France, Mesopotamia and Palestine); lieut., R.F.A.; ment. in desps.; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; astt. dir., agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; ag. dir., agr., on various occasions, 1930-35; ag. dir., animal husbandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June-Sept., 1932.

PITMAN, CHARLES ROBERT SENHOUSE, D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1890; ed. Royal Naval Schl., Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; att'd. R. Warwickshire Regt.; 27th Punjabis; capt., 1915; war serv., Egypt, 1914-15; France, 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916-18 (wounded, des't twice, M.C., D.S.O.); Palestine, 1918-21; gen. staff, 1919-21; game warden, Uganda, 1925; seconded to N. Rhodesia for sp. duty, 1931-33.

PITT, WALTER JAMES, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and medieval language triplos); ent. Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; headm'r., Cape Coast Govt. Schl., 1910; inspr. of schl., 1912; prov. inspr. of schl., 1914; att'd. to polit. serv., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; astt. dist. comsnr., July, 1917; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1921; ag. prov. comsnr., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1920.

PITTMAN, WILLIAM B.—1878; ed. S. African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (1904) and Transvaal (1905) bars; dean of faculty of law, Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa eastern dists. local divn., 1925; mem., delimitation coman., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

PLACE, ALAN WHITLEY.—B. 1888; native affrs. dept., S. Rhodesia, 1910-19; milly. serv., 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1915-16, 4th K. A. R., 1916-24; cadet, Uganda, 1924; astt. sec., 1927; temp. serving in D.O. and C.O., 1928-29; senr. astt. sec., Uganda, 1934.

PLANT, GEORGE FRDERIC, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1877; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll. (scholar, 1896); 1st cl. class. mods., 1898; B.A., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1910; off. astt., Kurunagala, 1902; off. astt., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; astt. gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in C.O. London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., oversea settlement contect., Jan., 1919; permanently transferred to Home civ. serv., Sept., 1919; mem., oversea settlement deleg't. to Canada, 1924; astt. sec., D.O., 11th Nov., 1935.

PLATT'S, SHELLEY ABDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; "King's India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th Gurka Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; att'd. 2/11th Gurka Rifles and 4/70th Burma Rifles, Burma, 1921; cadet, admive. service, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926; a.st dist. offr., July, 1928.

PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZHERBERT, O.B.E (1928) — B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripes); asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; pol. offr., Turkana Expedn., 1915 (A.G.S. Med. with clas); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.B.M. Vice-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.B.M. Consul on transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; transfd., Somaliland, 1922; H.B.M. chargé d'affaires, Addis Ababa, Dec., 1925 to Jan., 1926; mem., mission to Abyssinia for coronation of Emperor, 1930; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; asst. comsnr. and pol. offr., Br. Somaliland; Ethiopia bndy. comsnr., 1931-33; sec., govt. of Somaliland, 1933; ag. comsnr., May-Oct., 1934; has passed E. Africa Prot. law exam. and in Swahili, Boran Galla and Amharic languages.

PLUNKETT, JOHN OLIVER, M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1888; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1914; demob. with rank of capt., Sept. 1919; asst. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; do., S.S., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1929; exec. engr., F.M.S., Oct., 1933.

PLYMOUTH, 2nd Earl of (cr. 1905) and 15th Baron Windsor (cr. 1529) IVOR MILTON WINDSOR-CLIVE.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; D.L., Worcestershire; lord-lieut., Glamorganshire, 1923; capt., hon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1925; M.P., Ludlow divn., Shropshire, 1922-23; parly. under-sec. of state for Dominion affrs., Jan., 1929; kt. of grace of St. John of Jerusalem; parly. sec., min. of transport, Sept., 1931; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 30th Sept., 1932.

POLE-EVANS, ILLIOTT BULLER, C.M.G. (1921), M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Wales), F.L.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cowbridge Gram. Schl. and Univs. of Wales and Cambridge; mycologist, Transvaal, 1905; rep. Transvaal govt. at 4th Internat. Botanical Congress, Brussels, 1910; ch., divn. of mycology and plant pathology, 1911; ch., divn. of botany and plant pathology, 1912; sp. mission to P. E. Africa, coconut palm disease, 1913; dir., botanical survey of S. Africa, 1918; pres., S. African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1920; ch., divn. of botany, horticulture and entomology, 1927; ch., divn. of plant industry, 1929; author of many scientific papers.

POLLARD, ARNOLD.—B. 1893; ed. Hele Schl., Exeter; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 1915-19; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, May, 1924; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions in Tanganyika and Kenya; audr., Windward Is., Jan., 1926; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Jan., 1929; ag. audr., Apr., 1929; audr., Trinidad, June, 1931; audr., N. Rhodesia, 1935.

POLLOCK, CAPT. JAMES HURY HAMIL (R. of O.).—B. 1893; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; war serv., 1914-20; Gallipoli, Macedonia, Egypt and Palestine; men, in desps.; staff capt., O.E.T.A.; inspr., grade 3, Palestine, July, 1920; dep. gov., grade 3, Ramallah, Oct., 1920; admive. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1923; asst. sec., secr., Lagos, Dec., 1927; admive. offr., Palestine, Jan., 1930; asst. dist. comsnr., 1932.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 22nd Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Aug., 1933.

POPE, GEORGE WILLIAM, L.L.M.R.C.P., and S.I. (Ireland), D.P.H., R.C.P. and S.I.—B. 1891; med. offr., Kenya, 1920; 2nd health offr., port med. dept., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. asst. bacteriologist, 1924; asst. health offr., 1925; health offr., 1926.

POPE, LAWRENCE HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Uppingham; served European War, 1915-19 (France and N. Russia); asst. P.S. to govr., S. Australia, Sir W. E. E. A. Weigall, K.C.M.G., 1920; P.S. to govr., Tasmania, Sir W. L. Allardyce, G.C.M.G., 1921; col. audit dept., 1923; asst. audr., Fiji, 1923; ag. audr., 1924-28; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; do. Nigeria, Apr., 1934.

POPE, STEAD.—Br. post office, Feb., 1903; dist. survr., post and tels., Gold Coast, Dec., 1913; on active serv., Togoland Field Force, Aug., 1914; and later in charge posts and tels., Togo-land to May, 1915; imp. army serv., Sept., 1915-19; lieut., R.E. and R.A.F.; dist. survr., Gold Coast, June, 1919; div. survr., Jan., 1927; P.M.G., Nyasaland, Mar., 1930.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW, C.M.G. (1935), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; comsnr., 13th Light Infy., 1900; served, S. African War (2 med.); seconded, G.Costregt., W.A.F.F., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. govr., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. govr., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., July to Aug., 1913; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland polit. admtn., Nov., 1914; senr. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920 to Feb., 1921; dep. prov. comsnr. and ag. comsnr., W.Prov., Feb., 1921; prov. comsnr., Apr., 1922; ret'd. (invalided) May, 1923; comsnr., Cyprus, Oct., 1925; admstr., Dominica, 1933; mem., exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1933.
POPLEWELL, GEOFFREY DOUGLAS, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1904; cadet, provincial adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, April, 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1931-32.

PORTELLY, JOHN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), Certif. Lond. Schl. of Hygiene and T.M.—B. 1889; ed. govt. secondary schl. and Univ., Malta; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, S.S., Mar., 1922; med. offr., S. Kedah, and asst. supt., prisons, inaddn., Jan., 1925; med. offr., in ch. gen. hosp., J. Bahru, Apr., 1927; port health offr., S'pore, Feb., 1929; ag. senr. health offr., Penang, Apr., 1929; offr. rep. on L. of N. Interchange Tour for port health offrs. in the East, June, 1930; ag. senr. health offr., Johore, Sept., 1932; registr.-gen., births and deaths, Johore, Oct., 1932; ag. state med. and health offr., N. Sembilan, June, 1933; do. Perak, Oct., 1934.
PORTER, HENRY ARTHUR, F.R.I.R.A. — B. 1884; senr. archt., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1925.

POSTMUS, JOHANNES.—B. 1877; ed in S. Africa and at Sneek, Holland; gen. man., Netherlands Bank of S.A., 1920-26; dep. govr., S. African Reserve Bank, 1927-31; govr., S. African Reserve Bank, Jan., 1932; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933.

POTTER, JOHN WAGNER.—B. 1873; ed. at Uppingham Schl. (class. schol.); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Crown Agents office, Oct., 1896; asst. registr., inscribed stock, Mar., 1905; asst. head, gen. stores dept., Jan., 1913; ditto, gen. dept., Jan., 1920; ditto, pay dept., Jan., 1924; dep. head, pay dept., Feb., 1930.

POTTINGER, JOSEPH HECTOR, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (L'pool).—B. 1898; on war serv., 1915-18; med. offr., health, W. African med. staff, Gold Coast, 1928; asst. govt. med. offr. of health, Br. Guiana, 1932; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1935.
POTTS, HUGH CUTHBERT MILLER.—B.1907; ed. Durham Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; B.A. Law.; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; asst. dist. offr., 1932.

POTTS, JOHN WILFRED.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot., pol., 1912-16; clk., 1915; ag. asst. res. mag., 1920; ag. res. mag., 1920 and 1922-23; passed civ. ser. lower law exam., 1919; passed 2nd grade Seswana exam., 1922; sub. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1924; inspr., Mar., 1927; res. mag., Apr., 1929.

POUGNET, EDUARD DESBARRIERES, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Mch., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Mch., 1911; supt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apr., 1914; in service of French govt., in France during the war.

POULIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A. Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; att. Galle Kach., Aug., 1919; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1922; addtl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1924; addtl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1925; comsnr., requests, Kandy, Oct., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1931; do., Mannar, Oct., 1934.

POULTON, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1900; ed. R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; R.A., 1918; Iraq campaign, 1920; Somaliland Camel Corps (K.A.R.), 1927; comsnr., 3rd grade, Br. Somaliland, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1932.

POULTON, WILFRED FRANK, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1888; ed. Bishop's Stafford Coll. and Royal Vety. Coll., London; vety. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1914; war serv. in E. Africa, 1914-17; dir., vety. services, Uganda, Feb., 1925.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African war; dist. comdr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv. dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade comsnr., Durban, S.
