Africa, 1909; trade comsnr., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for activeserv., 1914; returned Can., 1919; inspr., trade comsnr. serv.; sp. trade comsnr., India, 1919; dir., comm. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade comsnr., Trinidad, 1924.

POWELL, BASIL WELSTEAD BARNARD.—B. 1881; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. and Highfield Coll.; senr. engrnr., P. and T. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Penang, Mar., 1913; ditto, K. Lumpur, July, 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Dec., 1928.
POWELL, HARRY CREWELL, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1881; mily. serv., France, 1916-19; traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rly., Jan., 1921; dir., do., Oct., 1930.

POWER, HON. CHARLES GAVAN, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (Quebec South).—B. 1888; ed. in Loyola Coll., Montreal; grad., 1907; B.A., LL.B., Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1910; enlisted Jan., 1915; served overseas, twice wounded, invalided from serv., 1918; capt. and ag. major, M.C.; el. to H. of C., Canada, Dec., 1917; re-el., g.e., 1921, 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of pensions and nat. health, MacKenzie King's cabinet, Oct., 1935.

POWER, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; Indian Army, 1917; seconded, I.C.S. as polit. res., Perim Is., 1922-23; col. audit dept., Apr., 1924; asst. audr., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dep. audr., July, 1928; audr., Windward Is., Feb., 1929; senr. asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1936.
POWER, G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1914; asst. dir., educn., Gold Coast, May, 1929; dir., ditto, Feb., 1933.
POWERS, HON. SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1929).—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913; ret.

POYNTON, ARTHUR HILTON.—B. 1905; schol. of Marlborough Coll. (1918-23) and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (1923-7); lst cls. hona., moda., 1925; 2nd cls. hona., lit. hum., 1927; M.A., 1933; apptd. asst. prin., dept. of scientific and industrial research after compet. exam., 31st Oct., 1927; transf. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1929; joint sec., Col. Development Advisory Comtee, 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1930; sec., do., 1st April, 1932; pte. sec. to partly. under sec. for cols.(Sir R. Hamilton), 18th April, 1932 and to (Lord Plymouth), 30th Sept., 1932; seconded to Sierra Leone admtn., Apr., 1933-35; ag. prin., D.O., 9th Sept., 1935; do., C.O., 4th Nov., 1935.

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON, D.S.O., K.C. (Barbados).—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1906; served, European War, 1914-20, Yorks. L.I., and Loyal N. Lanc. Regt. (depts. thrie., D.S.O., 1917); puisne judge, Leeward Island, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1926; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1928; puisne just., Ceylon, 1933.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.II., L.L.D., F.R.S E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram.schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1894; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1895-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and aspt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1905; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR, B.A.(Cantab.).—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class, tripods), B.A., 1905; baccalaureat(B.L. 1st part) Lyons Univ., France, 1908; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911; ag. supt., schla., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1921; promoted cls. I, grade I, admstve serv., Feb., 1928.

PRASAD, MURARI, U.B.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; addtl. office ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apr., 1919; office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag.; Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1927; comsnr., stamps, July, 1932; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Oct., 1933.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verandrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1886; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910; ch. just., Dec., 1929.
PRESCO'TT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—App'd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. regis. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PRETHEROE, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A.; LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; admnstr. offr., 1922; asst. commr., lands, 1924; ag. commr., lands, 1924; asst. sec., S. Prova., 1924; pol. mag., 1928; ag. ch. regis., sup. ct. on various occasions; crown coun., 1930; ag. solr.-gen. on various occasions.

PRETORIUS, JAN LODEWKYK.—B. 1878; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; joined O.F.S. repub. civ. serv., Mar., 1887; asst. mag., Edenburg, 1902; Vrede, 1904; Lindley, 1908; dist. asst. mag., Dewetsdorp, Feb., 1910 to Oct., 1913; Reitz, Nov., 1913; mag., Reitz, 1918; Houtenad, Feb., 1919; Boshof, Oct., 1919; Frankfort, Oct., 1923; Senekal, June, 1929; Vereeniging, Feb., 1930; Lichtenburg, Mar., 1933; Port Elizabeth, Feb., 1934.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S.S., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and asst. adjt., J.V.R., May, 1918; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; Br. res., Brunei, Mar., 1923; offr., Cls. III., Nov., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, June, 1928; ag. sec., high commr., July, 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Schl., Somerset University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; pvte. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th July, 1927 (ag. from 6th June, 1927); temp. ag. prin., D.O., 22nd July, 1927; pvte. sec. to parly. under sec. for dom. affrs., Jan., 1928; asst. sec., Imp. wireless and cable confce., Jan., July, 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 15th Sept., 1928; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1928 and 1932; U.K. delegn. to London naval confce., 1930; attd. to sec., Imp. confce., 1930; U.K. delegn., disarmament confce., Geneva, Feb.-Dec., 1932.

PRICE, HENRY HENRY, M.B.E. (1936).—2nd clk. to asst. commr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. commr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., 1911-12; regis. of brands, 1913; ch. clk. and regis., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seswana exam., 1927; asst. govt. sec., Apr., 1935.

PRICE-JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; dist. commr., Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., 1928; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., Sept., 1935.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM, K.C. (1923), B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class. schol., 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag. Trinidad, Aug., 1910; puisne judge, Mauritius, Feb., 1915; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1916; ag. proc. and advoc.-gen., Oct., 1916 to July, 1917; chmn. of oman. on Royal College, 1916-1917; chmn., sugar sales comte., Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1920 and Feb. to Apr., 1921; ag. proc. and advoc. gen., April, 1921; confirmed in appt., Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. from Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., and judge, F.M.S., June, 1927; ag. sr. puisne judge, S.S., in 1928 and 1932.

PRIESTMAN, HAROLD EDDEY, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1888; mil. serv., 1914-19; temp. admin. offr., min. of lab., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1921; attbd. to C.O., 1930; seconded as ag. col. sec. and senr. asst. col. sec., Gambia, 1931; resumed duties, C.O., 1932; resumed substantive appt., Nigeria, 1933; admin. sec. to high commr. for Basutoland, Bech. Prot. and Swaziland, 1935.

PRING, HARRY.—B. 1883; ed. Watford Schl. and King's Coll., London; Imp. serv., 1898-1903; col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1903; dept. of interior, Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., 1925; under sec., 1927; pub. serv. commr., 1931.

PRINGLE, LEONARD DUNGAN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1906; med. offr., F.M.S., 1931; Hong Kong, 1932; ag. radiologist, Jan.-Aug., 1933; M.O. in ch. of officers' families, Victoria Gaol, infant dist. hosp., Victoria mortuary and remand house, 1934.

PRIOR, A. C. VINCENT.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1905; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Jan., 1915; ag. ch. just., Jan. to June, 1915; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1926; ag. atty.-gen., May to Nov., 1926; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1927; ag. ch. just., June to July, 1928 and June to Oct., 1929; atty.-gen., Nigeria, Oct., 1929.
PRITCHARD, NEIL.—B. 1911; ed. Liverpool Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford (hist. schol., 1929-32); hist. schol., 1st cls.; B.A., 1932; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1933.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, July, 1904; gov., Barbados, Nov., 1910; gov., Jamaica, May, 1918; ret., 1924.
PROPSTING, HON. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly.s., Apr., 1916; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

PROUT, SIR WILLIAM THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1928), KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1905), O.B.E., M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1886; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1887; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief surg., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1896; title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and corrpdg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect. schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory. med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; senr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; author of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Fil. Volucolous (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYER, JOHN HAWKES.—B. 1894; ed. Bedford Harpur Trust; mech. transport, B.E.F., France, 1918-19; cadet pilot, R.A.F.; asst. storekeeper, Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1920; asst. ch., do., Aug., 1929; ch., do., Mar., 1932.
PUCKERIN, MERVYN ALLEYNE.—B. 1891; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, 1909; war serv., 1916-19; supt., govt. indust. schls., 1924; tov., Glendairy prison, 1932.

PUCKRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1895; ed. Exeter Schl.; 2nd lieut., Devon Regt., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 5th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comn. of Control, Germany, 1920; resigned comn., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. comntr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comntr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engnr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, Dec., 1927.

PUGHIE, S. R., M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confl. clk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916; staff offr., 1st Apl., 1922; on service in Sierra Leone, Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; sec., Discovery comttee., Jan.-June, 1928.

PULLICINO, Sir PHILIP, KT. BACH (1934) B.Litt., LL.D.—B. 1886; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1900; B.Litt., 1903; LL.D., 1907; advoc. for poor, 1918; judge, 1928; pub. pros. and treasy. coun., 1929.

PULLON, BERTRAM.—B. 1882; asst. printer, N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. govt. printer, 1924-25; asst. govt. printer, 1925; ag. govt. printer for various periods during 1927-29, 1931 and 1932; govt. printer, 1932.

PURCELL, HUBERT KENNEDY.—B. 1884; ent. Crown Agents office, Oct., 1901; asst. cashier, July, 1914; head of pay dept., Dec., 1920; dep. head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; dep. head, pay dept., 1924; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. clk., 1930; dep. head, gen. stores dept., Jan., 1933; head, pay dept., May, 1933; head, gen. dept. and ch. clk., Nov., 1933.

PURCELL, VICTOR WILLIAM WILLIAMS SAUNDERS.—B. 1896; ed. Bancroft's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Camb.; B.E.F., France, 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; passed cadet, July, 1924; offr., cls. V, July, 1923; inspr., Chinese schls., S.S. and asst. dir., educn., June, 1924; asst. prot., Chinese, Penang, July, 1925; dep. registr. sup. ct., Sept., 1926; offr., cls. IV, July, 1927, dep. registr. and asst. offr. assignee; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Aug., 1930; offr., cls. III, July, 1932; mag., Seremban, July, 1934.

PURLL, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1891; conf. shorthand writer, B. of T., Aug., 1909; conf. clk. to gen. man., lab. exchange dept., B. of T., Oct., 1909; conf. shorthand writer to S. of S., H.O., Jan., 1911; cler. offr., 21st Jan., 1925; accompanied the Rt. Hon. L. S. Amery, M.P. (S. of S. for Dominion Affairs) on his tour of the Dominions July, 1927-Feb., 1928; transferred to C.O., June, 1928; accompanied Br. economic mission to Australia, Aug., 1928-Feb., 1929; seconded for serv. with Newfoundland R. Comsn., Mar.-Oct., 1933.

PURVES, ALEXANDER BRUCE, M.I.C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Kirkcaldy and George Watson's Coll., Edin.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engrnr., Tytam Tuk scheme, 1915; asst. water engrnr., 1916; engrnr., Shing Mun Valley scheme, in addn., 1923-29; dep. water engrnr., 1925; exec. engrnr. in ch. waterworks, 1930.

PUTTICK, LEONARD JAMES.—B. 1885; ed. at High Schl. and Huguenot Coll., Wellington and S. African Coll., Cape Town; served Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; draughtsman, Swaziland, 1910; clk. of lands, 1916; ch. clk., 1919; ag. reglar. and mast., sp. ct. and sheriff, 1919; sec., Swaziland—Portuguese bndry. comsn., 1922; sec., undeveloped minerals comsn., 1927; ag. dept. asst. comntr., 1925 and 1930; beacon inspr., 1927; ag. govt. sec. in 1927 and 1928; asst. govt. sec., 1928; reglar. and mast., sp. ct., sheriff, reglar. births, marriages and deaths and comntr., protocols, 1932; ag. govt. sec. and finan. sec., Mar.-Sept., 1935.

QUIGLEY, EUGENE PARTRICK, M.C.—B. 1889; served six years R.I.C.; joined army, 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, 1915; served in France, Egypt and Palestine; organised pol., C.I.D., Palestine, 1919; D.C.P., grade I, 1921; dist. supt., pol., Apr., 1926; dep. comdt., C.I.D., 1930.

RAE, Sir JAMES STANLEY, KT. Bach (1933)—B. 1881; articled and conf. clk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; dist. comntr., Belize, B. Honduras, 1914; offr. recr. in bankruptcy, offr. admstr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; registr.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. comntr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916; press censor during war; pvt., B. Hond. Territorial Force; attd. to staff as mily. censor; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Apl., 1919; cross of Chevalier of the Order of the Crown, Belgium, for relief services during war, May, 1919; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, June to Oct., 1919; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. just., June, 1921 to Feb., 1922 and from Sept., 1922 to June, 1923; ch. just., St. Vincent, June, 1923; ag. admstr., June, 1926 to Feb., 1927; ch. just., Grenada, 1927; revised and consolidated laws, 1926; ch. just., Leeward Is., Apr., 1931.
RAE, NORMAN.—B. 1892; entd. Crown Agent's office, May, 1911; asst. head of dept., May, 1919; dep. head, stores dept., Oct., 1932; dep. ch. clk., July, 1935.

RAEBURN, COLIN, O.B.E. (1934), D.Sc., F.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; lieut., Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; Salonika, 1915-18; geologist, Scottish Spitzbergen syndicate, 1919; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1920-29; asst. dir., 1929; author of "Alluvial Prospecting."

RAFFLES, MAJOR STAMFORD CECIL, O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919.—B. 1888; ed. All Saints Schl., Bloxham, Royal Naval Academy, Gosport, and in Germany; 2nd lieut., 4th Suffolk regt., 1903; lieut., 1903; transfd., K.S.L.I., 1906; resig. comrn., 1908; supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, 1913; seconded for mily. serv., Jan., 1915; lieut., 3rd R.W.F., Mar., 1915; capt., Sept., 1915; staff offr., 3rd cls., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., 2nd cls., R.A.F., 1918; tempy. major, 1918; transfd. unemployed list, R.A.F., Apr., 1919; resumed duty as supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, and supvr. cust., Kinta, Dec., 1919; supvr. cust., Selangor, Dec., 1920; dep. comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; dep. comnr., excise, F.M.S. in addn., Apr. July, 1929; dep. comnr., trade and cust., Selangor and Pahang, June, 1931; dep. comnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Apr., 1932; ag. comnr., do., in addn., Dec., 1932; senr. dep. comnr., cust. and excise, F.M.S., July, 1932.

RAIKES, H. R.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl., Dulwich Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (William exhib.), Abbot Schol., 1911, final hon. schl. of nat. science (chem.), class I, 1914; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1919; served European War, 1st Batt. The Buffs, R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1914-1919 (A.F.C.); fellow, Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1919-27; sub sector, 1924-27; demonstr. and treas., Balliol and Trinity Coll. laboratory, 1920-27; ch. instr., Oxford Univ. Air Squadron, 1925-27; wing commander, R.A.F.O. since 1926; prin., Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1928; publs.—papers in Jnl. of Chem. Socv. and Faraday Socv.
RAINSFORD, HUME FRDERICK.—B. 1902; ed. Dublin Univ.; B.A. and hons. B.A.I., civ. engnr., 1925; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

RAJA MUSA, BIN RAJA HAJI BOT, Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1897; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay astt., grade III, July, 1914; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; astt. dist. offr., U. Langat, Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; ag. reg., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1933.

RAJA UDA, BIN RAJA MUHAMAD.—B.1894; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay astt., C.S.O., grade III, Sept., 1913; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; offr. cls. V, astt. dist. offr., Kuantan, Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; dep. chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1930; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Mar., 1933.
RAJENDRA, MURUGESYEN, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1911; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv. and attd., Hambantota kach., Dec., 1934.
RAKE, EUSTACE OLPHERTS.—B. 1886; ed. Bradfield and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; astt. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1914; prov. comnr., 1930.

RALSTON, JAMES.—B. 1890; M.A. (Glas.), 1911; ast. mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1918-19; headmast., Indian Schl., Hong Kong, 1925; 2nd mast., King's Coll., 1928; headmast., Ellis Kadoorie Schl., 1929 and 1933; ditto, Wanteai Schl., 1930; external examr. in method, Hong Kong Univ., 1930; ag. inspr., schl., 1931 and 1935; offr., J.P., 1932; headmr., Cent. Br. Schl., 1934.

RAMAGE, RICHARD OGILVY.—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Acad.; R.E. 1914-19 (capt. T.A. R. of O.); admstve. offr., cls. IV, 1920; polit. offr., anti-teaute duties, 1926; astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1930-32; pvte. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., 1931; ag. prin. astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1932 and 1933; ag. prin. astt. sec., S. Prov., do., 1933-34; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1934-35; admstve. serv., cls. II, 1934; personal astt. to lieut.-gov., Malta, 1936.

RAMBAUT, ARTHUR EDMUND, B.A.—B.1886; ed. Magd. Coll. Schl. and B.N.C., Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1905; 4th cls. bot., 1908; dipl. forestry, 1909; astt. con., forests, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; do. mil. duty; 2nd lieut. North Fus. (T.F.), 1915; lieut., 1916; temp. capt. 1916; served in France; intel. corps., 1917 (France, Belgium, Germany); rtnd. to F.M.S., Aug., 1919; dep. consrvr., forests, Perak, South, Nov., 1926; ag. per. astt. to consrvr., forests, Dec., 1927 and Aug., 1928; consrvr., forests, Jan., 1930.

RANASINHA, ARTHUR GODWIN, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; attd. to Kegalla Kach., Mar., 1921; attd., Jaffna kach., Oct., 1922; pol. mag. Pt. RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsmen; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDS, P. E.—B. 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 20th Dec., 1928.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER STEWART, B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1897; ed. Burgh and County High Schl., Peebles; mily. serv., R. Arty., 1916-19; B.Sc., Edinburgh Univ., 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930.

RICHARDSON, CECILY.—B. 1889; ed. The Laurels, Rugby and Royal Holloway Coll.; B.A., London, 1912; asst. mistress, govt. girls' schl., Penang, 1914; asst. mistress, Raffles girls' schl., Singapore, 1915; ag. prin., 1920 and 1924; prin., St. George's girls' schl., Penang, Sept., 1928.

RICHARDSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GEORGE SPAFFORD, K.B.E. (1925), C.B. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1867; enlisted in Royal Artillery, Aug., 1886; attached to N.Z. Forces as instructor in gunnery with rank of master gunner, Aug., 1891; capt., N.Z. militia and dir. of artillery services (N.Z.), Apr., 1907; transf. to N.Z. staff corps, Jan., 1911; major, 1912; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1919, and brig.-gen. with seniority as from Dec., 1915; N.Z. representative at War Office, London, and attached Impl. gen. staff, Dec., 1913; attached to Admty. staff, Aug., 1914; attached Royal Naval Divn. as asst. adjutant and qtrmr., Nov., 1914; active service at Antwerp with Royal Naval Divn.; subsequently served as A.A. and Q.M.G. on Gallipoli; selected as D.A. and Q.M.G. of an Army Corps at Salonika with temporary rank of brig.-gen.; comdt., N.Z.E.F. in U.K., Mar., 1916; G.O. in charge admtn., N.Z. Forces, May, 1919; admstr., W. Samoa, 1923-28; also admstr., Tokelau Is., 1926-28; rep. N.Z. before mandates committee, L. of N., 1928; Legion of Honour (Croix de Chevalier) conferred by Pres. of the French Republic, 1916; ment. in despatches (3).

RICHARDSON, ROBERT BOOTH.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A.; 2nd cls. juris-prudence, 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1927; cadet, Tanganyika Territory admntrn., May, 1924; asst. sec. Dec., 1924; attd. as asst. prin., C.O., Feb. to Aug., 1927; ag. crown coun. in 1929 and 1932; ag. asst. admstr. gen. and registr., companies, 1929; ag. res. mag., 1934; ag. sec., dept., lands and mines, 1935; sec., Tanganyika soil erosion and gold mining industry comtees.

RICHES, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1885; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., London Scottish, Sept., 1917; apptd. to army audit staff, Eastern command, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 17th July, 1922.

RICHMOND, ERNEST TATHAM, F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1874; ed. High Croft Schl., Clifton Coll., Bristol, Architectural Schl., Westminster and R. Acad. Architectural Schl.; archt. to pub. wks. miny., Egypt, 1900; subsequently dir.-gen. state bldgs. dept.; attd., dept. of fortifns. and wks., W.O., Dec., 1914—Oct., 1916; in ch. grenade sec., trench warfare dept., miny. of munitions, Mar., 1917; lieut. R.N.V.R., Mar., 1917; archt. attd. to war graves serv., France, 1918-19; asst. civ. sec., Palestine, 1920; dir., antiquities, 1927.

RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., PH.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario, 1919; ch. of agril. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B. 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths, Normal Schl., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench dvn., 1906; judge, appellate dvn. (second divi. ct.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assc., and of Bar Assocs. of several states; R.F. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univs.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll., (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary jnl.

RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1841; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-secr., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-secr. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of commn. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comttee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., et. of dirs., H. R. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. J.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; supt., pol., 1935.

RIGBY, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Bury Gram. Schl.; served in France, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1918 and June, 1918-Nov., 1919; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attchd., Chinese Prot., Penang, Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1923; asst. supt. govt. monops., S'pore and asst. contrlr., rubber, Mar., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; 3rd mag., S'pore, June, 1930; inspr., prisons, in addn., Sept., 1931; relief asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1932, offr., cls. III; asst. Malayan estabmt. offr., Sept., 1933.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1886; Scots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT. G.B.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

RINFRET, HON. FERNAND, B.A., M.P.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Quebec (B.A.); ed. St. James divn., Montreal bye-el., 1919; re-el. 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1930; sec. of state, Sept., 1926; re-el. by accl., Nov., 1926; mayor, Montreal, 1932-34; re-el., H. of C., 1933; sec. of state, Oct., 1935.

RINFRET, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B. 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1897), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-22; prof., munic. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.
RING, JULIUS, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Birm.), F.S.I., Chart. Surveyor, A.M.I.Struc.E.—B. 1892; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; asst. assessor, rates, 1928; 1st asst. do., 1933.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1895; lieut. R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonica, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, CAPT. A. T. A., O.B.E. (1936), M.C., B.A. (Oxon)—Game warden, Kenya, 1923.
RITCHIE, J. D.—Ck., agril. dept., Kenya, Aug., 1912; head ck. and acct., cust. dept., Sept., 1922.
RITCHIE, T. M.—B. 1895; foreman of wks., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; inspr. of wks., 1928; mast., Accra Tech. Schl., 1930.

ROBERTS, ANTHUR HALLAM, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1888; ed. Aldenham schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1914; cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1915; priv. sec. to gov., Mar., 1915; ag. 1st cls. clk., col. sec's office and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., Jan., 1916; passed cadet, June, 1916; priv. sec., Oct., 1916; 2nd grade dist. commr., Oct., 1916; ag. crown solr., July, 1917; assigned dist. of Sava Savu, Aug., 1917; act. serv., June, 1918; dist. commr., Navua, Dec., 1919; resigned, Oct., 1920; el. mem., leg. coun., 1923-26; registr., sup. ct., taxing master, curator, pub. trustee and offr. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1927; ag. atty. gen. in 1931, 1932 and 1935; ch. pol. mag., Aug., 1931.
ROBERTS, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ent. Admty., 1902; seconded as exec. engrnr., Falkland Is., 1925; col. engrnr., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931.
ROBERTS, GEORGE FORSE.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. olk., exchequer and audit dept., treas., Gold Coast, Feb., 1929; capt., G.C. Defence Force.
ROBERTSON, COLIN BROWN.—B. 1893; ed. Leven higher grade; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920.

ROBERTSON, IAN DONALD, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1886; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jly., 1914; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., irrigation br., Aug., 1916, Aug., 1919 and Nov., 1921; dir., wks., Kelantan, Jan., 1923; exec. engrnr., Jan., 1925; sr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1933.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1875; ed. Glenalmond, M.E., Ch.B., Edin.; formerly house surgeon and physician, Swansea hosp. and Victoria Park hosp. for chest diseases; civil surg., S. African Field Force, 1901-2 (medal and three clasps); astt. comsnr. and gen. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1905; dist. comsnr. and gen. med. offr., Caicos Is., 1905; gen. med. offr., Grand Turk, 1909.

ROBERTSON, SIR W. C. F., K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1885 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr., schls. G. Coast, 1898; dir. educn., 1899; ag. comprtr., cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ag. sec., native affairs, June to Nov., 1905; prov. comsnr., Nov., 1905; sec. for native affairs, May, 1907; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1908; chief astt. col. sec., Jan., 1909; ag. col. sec., in 1909, 1910, 1911, 1912, 1913; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April to May, 1913; ag. gov., May to Aug., 1914; dep. gov., Aug. to Sept., 1914; col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1915; liut.-gov. and chief sec. to govt., Malta, May, 1917; offr. admstg. govt., Malta, for various periods, 1920-25; gov.'s dep., in Oct., 1920; liut. gov. by comsnr. under new constitution, Apr., 1921; gov.'s dep., for various periods in 1920 and 1922; hon. L.L.D., Malta Univ., 1925; gov., Barbados, 1926; attended C.O. conferees, 1927 and 1930; ret., 1932.
ROBERTSON, WILLIAM.—B.1875; ed. Palmerston Dist. High Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. dept. of lands and survey, 1892; inspr., 1919; astt. under-sec., 1926; under-sec., 1931.

ROBINSON, CAPT. ARTHUR GORDON, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. Asaph gram. schl., N. Wales and Birmingham Univ.; astt. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1912; seconded, Kedah, July, 1912; B.E.F., France, Mar., 1917-Jan., 1919; capt., commanding Indian labt., liut., R.E.; ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1921; sen. ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., July, 1928; ch. hydraulic engrnr., 1930; astt. advr., drainage and irrign., Malay States, Jan., 1932.

ROBINSON, AUBREY CHARLES.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; comsnr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compd. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (publcn. deferred during the war); regist.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in July and Dec., 1918 and from Mar., 1919; regist.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. regir., supt. et. and marshal, June to Sept., 1920; rec. mag., Jamaica, Sept., 1923; regist., sub. ct. and regist.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1928; 2nd puisne judge, Aug., 1931.
ROBINSON, GEORGE GILMOUR—B. 1894; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxon., B.A., M.A.; war serv., 1914-19 (France); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1924; practised, 1924-30; res. mag., Kenya, 1930.
ROBINSON, INNES LOVELL.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (2nd cls. hons. classics); cadet, Tanganyika, 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1929; ag. dist. offr., in 1932-34.

ROBINSON, MONTGOMERY BLAIR.—B. 1878; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1901; LL.B., Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, 1909; elk. law dept., Cape, 1903; mag., 1921; ch. clk., dept. of just., 1929; addnl. mag., Pretoria, June, 1931.

ROBINSON, S.—B. 1883; ed. Barnsley Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Sheffield; B.A., 1914; 1st cls. hons. mod. lang.; M.A., 1919; on milly. serv., 1914-19; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. clk., bd. of cust. and excise, Sept., 1919; asst. prin., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O. as priv. sec. (appts.) to successive secs. of State, 1923-24; asst. pvt. sec. to Lord Passfield, Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

ROBINSON, LIEUT.-COL. SIR THOMAS BILBE, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH (1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commnr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comtee. for settm. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

ROCK, ALFRED MILTON.—B. 1888; ag. 3rd outdoor offr. and harbour., treasy., Dominica, 1913-15; clk. and interpreter to mag., dist. "E," Mar., 1915; clk., mag., regiar. and provost marshal "A," comsnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Montserrat, Sept., 1916; clk. to mag., dist. "A" and "B," dep. coroner, Antigua, Nov., 1920; senr.-clk., regiar.'s office, clk., sup. ct. law library, "A" comsnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Dominica, May, 1927; prin. for duty as regiar. and provost marshal, regiar. of titles, admir. of estates and regiar.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Apr., 1929.

RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementary clk., col. audit dept., Apr., 1914; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 1919; regiar., 1st Apr., 1928.

RODDA, GEORGE CHARLES, M.B.E. (1919), F.R.A.N.Z., F.L.A.N.Z.—B. 1878; ed. Stafford schl., Hokitika dist. high schl. and Victoria univ. coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. govt. insur. dept., 1894; treas., 1902; offr. in ch., war expenses, 1914-19; sec., local govt. loans bd. 1927-30; asst. sec., treasy., 1930; sec., treasy., recvr.-gen., paymr.-gen., 1935; also chmn. local govt. loans bd., finan. advr. to govt., and ex officio dir., reserve bank, 1935; attd. N.Z. deleg., world economic confce., 1933.

RODEN, SIR ROBERT BLAIR, KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1860; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; clk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clk. to the gov., Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. clk., and as clk. to the logs., and exec. couns., and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to different govs., Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1895 and 1896; mag., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legis. couns., 1891; mem., fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1896-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903; visiting justice, Glendairy prs., 1904; ch. just., St. Vincent, 1912; admnstd. govt. of St. Vincent from Oct., 1912 to Feb., 1913, and for short periods in Apr., Aug., and Dec., 1913; also from Mar. to Nov., 1914; ch. just., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1915; chmn. of food control comtee., Jan., 1918; ret., 1921.

RODGER, ROBERT, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Mech.E., A.M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., M.I.Struc.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1893; ed. Wishaw High Schl., Wishaw Tech. Schl. and Glasgow Tech. Coll. (affiltd.); on active serv. with Lanarkshire Yeo., (Lowld. Mtd. Bde., Soot., T.F.) 1914-20; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1924; road engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, on "Tarmet" Scheme 1926; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, on road development schemes, 1930; road engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1930; ag. asst. dir., pub. wks., in 1931 and 1932.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd. Kurunegala Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., W.Prov., May, 1915; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1917; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuva, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ditto, Matara, May, 1922; asst. comsnr., excise, Sept., 1924; dep. comsnr., excise, Sept., 1927; contr., finance and supplies, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, July, 1932; ag. govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1933.

RODWELL, SIR CECIL HUNTER, G.C.M.G., (1934), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high comsnr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov., Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1918; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., B. Guiana, 1925; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Southern Rhodesia, Sept., 1928; ret., Dec., 1934.

ROE, ROBERT JESTIN, M.R.C.V.S. (Williams Memorial Prize), D.V.S.M.—B. 1897; ed. Drogheda and R. Vety. Coll., Ireland; war. serv., 1915-16; vety. surg., Cyprus, June, 1921; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Jan., 1923; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Confce., 1927; ag. dir., animal husbandry, July-Oct., 1928; title changed to ch. vety. offr., Jan., 1930; rep. govt. Internat. Dairy Cong., Copenhagen, 1931.

ROGERS, FRANCIS MERVYN.—B.1890; student, R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1915; milly. serv., 1916-19; sub-foreman, Kew, 1919-20; head gardener, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; supt., plantations, E. African agr. research stn., Amani, 1927.

ROGERS, HON. NORMAN McLEOD.—B. 1894; ed. Amherst pub. and high schls. and Acadia Univ.; served Great War; B.A., Acadia Univ., 1919; cl. to Rhodes scholarship at Oxford; B.A. (hist., polit. sci. and law); prof., hist., Acadia Univ.; admitted to Nova Scotia bar; sec.; Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King in his capacity as pres., P.C., 1927; prof., polit. sci., Queen's Univ., 1929; coun. for govt. of Nova Scotia before R. commn. of econ. enquiry, 1934; el. M.P., Canada, g.e. 1935; min. of lab., Mackenzie King cabinet, 1935.
ROGERS, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1864; ent. civ. serv., S. Australia, 1882; 2nd clk., audit dept., 1912; audr.-gen., S. Aust., 1923.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B.1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd. Kandy kach., Jan., 1913; addtl. office asst., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Arissawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., N.W.P., Feb., 1930; astt. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1931; addnl. govt. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1933; govt. agt., S.P., Oct., 1934.

ROLSTON, CECIL MICHELL.—M.D., C.M., Univ. of Manitoba, Canada, 1896; L.R.C.P., Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin.; L.R.F.P. and S., Glasgow, 1898; mem. Coll. of Phys. and Surgeons, 1903; F.R.I.; pub. health, London; late civil surg., attached to R.A.M.C. during S. African war, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal, 2 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); apptd. to Leeward Is. med. serv., 1903; med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, and ag. med. offr. of pub. instit., 1903; dist. med. offr. of health, Vancouver, B. Columbia, 1910; med. offr., dist. 8, Nevis, 1914; ag. mag., Nevis, May to June, 1915; med. offr., dist. 6, Nevis, Sept., 1915.

ROOKE, CHARLES EUSTACE, M.Inst.T., F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Dover Coll.; astt. traffic man., Uganda Rly., Oct., 1914; served with Indian Expdy. Force, 1915-17; 2nd lieut., Indian Army R. of O.; astt. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., rly., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; traffic man., Tanganyika Rly., 1935.

ROOTHAM, J. ST. J., M.A.—B. 1910; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge (class scholarship); 1st cls. class, tripes, Pts. I and II; apptd. after compet. exam. astt. prin. min. of agr. and fisheries, 1933; transf. to C.O., July, 1934.

ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and City and Guilds Engng. Coll., S. Kensington; held various engng. appts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1908; astt. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag. dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1922.

ROPER, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1891; entd. C.A. office, 1907; seconded to Falkland Is., Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to admstr. and clk. to couns., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; ag. local audr., Nov., 1914; retnd. to C.A. office, 1915; served European war (M.G.C., France), 1916-1919; 1st cls. clk., Nyasaland, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. clk., July to Dec., 1921; edr. Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd astt. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., in 1922, 1925 and 1926; organising sec., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Sept., 1926; ag. ch. transport offr., Dec., 1926 to May, 1927; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1927; sec., govt. tender bd., Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. staff offr., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Apr. to Nov., 1929; ag. senr. astt. sec., May, 1929 to June, 1930 and Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; supt., census, 1930; astt. sec., Apr., 1931; ag. astt. ch. sec., Apr. to Aug., 1935.

ROSE, ALAN EDWARD PERCIVAL, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; ed. Aldenham Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; temp. 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., B.E.F., 1918-19; 2nd cls. hons., law tripes, Cambridge, 1921; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, Jan., 1923; mem., S.E. circuit; ch. pol. mag., Fiji, July, 1929; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1930 to June, 1931; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1931; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1931-35; ag. atty.-gen. in 1934 and 1935.

ROSE, FREDERIC GARDINER, B.A., 1907, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., 1911, M.B., B. Chir., U. Camb., D.M.R. & E., Camb., M.D., Camb., M.R.C.P., Lond.—B. 1885; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; med. ser., B. Guiana, 1915; govt. bac. and path., 1915; ag. G.M.O.H. and port health offr., 1917; ag. G.M.O.H. and port health offr., 1919; govt. radiologist, 1921-22; hon. con. radiologist, 1923; ag. port health offr., 1924; dep. port health offr., 1925; med. supt., lep. hosp., 1926.

ROSE, HON. SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906, C.M.G., 1896.—B. 1857; held various appts. in Natal civ. serv., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (deeps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (deeps.); variously employed under Zululand Gov't., 1888-95 (deeps.), when he was apptd. spec. commn. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. commn. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. commn. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Comsn., 1902, and commn. for native affairs, 1906; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, PHILIP HENRY NORMAN.—B. 1890; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; successively 6th, 5th, 4th, 3rd and 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1911-24; sec., bd. of industrial training, 1930; acctg. clk., P.W.D., 1931; sec., advisory comte., on sea defences, 1932; sec., sea defence bd., 1933.

SAUNDERS-JONES, DAVID WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1899; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon; Indian Army, 1917-23; on service in Waziristan, Egypt and Palestine; wounded in Palestine, 1919; asst. dist. commn., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. pvt. sec., Br. res. in 1925 and from Aug., 1930-May, 1931; ag. prov. commr., Pemba, June, 1930 and July, 1932-Feb., 1933; asst. sec. and pte. sec. to Sultan, 1935.

SAUVE, HON. ARTHUR, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1875; ed. in Seminary of Ste. Therese and L'Univ. de Montreal; 1st el. to legis. assem., Quebec, g.e., 1908; re-elec., g.e., 1912, 1916, 1919, 1923 and 1927; el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; P.M.G., in Bennett Cabinet, 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930; mem. senate, 1935.

SAYAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; supt. of regis-trn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; supt. of mails, ditto, Jan., 1915; supt. mails, Penang, but cont. ag. senr. supt. mails, July, 1917; supt., grade II., Jan., 1919; supt., grade I., Jan., 1920; supt., posts and teles., Malacca, Sept., 1920; contr., P. and T., S'pore, Oct., 1926; senr. acct., G.P.O., S'pore, contd. to act as contr., P. and T., Jan., 1929; contr., P. and T., F.M.S., Aug., 1929; ag. P.M.G., Sept., 1929; P.M.G., Johore, July, 1930.

SAYARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; regis. and marshal and regis- gen. and prin. regis., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925 and 1926; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1928; ag. ch. just. in 1932 and 1934.
SAVILE, ARTHUR HUGH, Dip. Agr. (Wye), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1905; ed. Clifton Coll., Wye Agr. Coll. and C.O. Sch., course Oxford and Trinidad; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CABMON, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Long Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; command., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps., Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1925-32; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1929; ag. survr.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934.

SAWTELL, WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. at King Edward VII's Sch., Bath, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; H.M. Transport Serv., 1915-17; Indian govt. tel. ship, 1917-18; Indian Army R. of O., 1918-22; wharf-mast. and pilot, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; marine offr., 1932.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1905; paymr.-lieut.-comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. commr., Accra, and ag. trav. commr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1926; promoted pay commdr., ret. list, 1927; dep. prov. commr., 1930.

SAYER, GEOFFREY ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (foundationer) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhibr. in classics and Fitzgerald exhibr.; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. lit. hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs. 1914 and 1915, in addn. to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addn. 1915; attd. to crown solr.'s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1915; ag. chief asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1915; on mily. serv., 1915-19; dep. registr. and appraiser, Hong Kong, 1920; head, sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921; attd. to treas-y, 1924; asst. treas. and commr. of estate duties, Apr., 1925; supt., impts. and expts. dept., June, 1927; P.M.G., Apr., 1928; head, sany. dept., Oct., 1928; offr., dis. 1, Aug., 1934.

SAYERS, ELDERD FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; dist. commr., Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, GERALD FLEMING.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1916; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; asst. sec., Tanganyika territory, July, 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923; attd. to C.O., Aug., 1924 to Sept., 1925 and from July to Sept., 1930; clk., leg. coun., 1926-32; ag. asst. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1927-31; asst. ch. sec., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Nyasaal Prot., 1933; ag. ch. sec., Feb., 1934; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1935; mem. exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1935; edr., Handbook of Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

SAYLE, LIEUT. COMMDR., ARTHUR REGINALD WILLIS, R.N.R., Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—Ed. Merchant Taylors Schl. and cadet H.M.S. "Worcester"; Royal Navy, May, 1914; R.I.C., 1920; admtrve. offr., S. Nigeria, May, 1921; station mag., Ibadan, S. Nigeria, Aug., 1928; pol. mag., Oct., 1928; crown coun., 1930; ag. solr. gen. in 1932, 1933 and 1935.

SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916, min. for rlys., mines and industry, 1930.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; asst. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. commr., Canada, 1915; asst. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-estabmt., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; sec., pensions divn., dept. of pensions and national health, 1931; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHARFF, JOHN WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.A.O., B.Ch., D.P.H. (T.C.D.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1895; surgn. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., July, 1915; health offr., S.S., Nov., 1920; lect. in biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine in addn., May, 1921; hon. sec. for Malaya of the Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Aug., 1921; health offr., Malacca, June, 1925; tr. health offr., Penang, May, 1928; offl. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Bangkok, Dec., 1930.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; asst. examnr. of acts., Jan., 1894; examr., May, 1898; ch. examr., July, 1907; inspr. of actsa., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec. educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, Sept., 1926; contr. and audr.-gen., 1929; rector, Pretoria univ., 1935.

SCHMITT, LOUIS JOHN, A.I.C.A., F.C.A.A.—B. 1885; ed. Victoria Parade coll., Australia; accnt. for some years; sec., N.Z. bd. of trade price investig. tribunal, 1919; investig. accnt., bd. of trade, 1921; sec. to advay. coun., N.Z. sect., Wembley exhibn., 1924; dep. govt. commnr., Dunedin internat. exhibn., 1925; advay. accnt., industries and commerce dept., 1927; mem. (rep. employers), N.Z. ct. of arbitn., 1928; N.Z. govt. tourist and trade commnr. in Australia, 1930-35; sec. and gen.-man. N.Z. dept. of industries and commerce, tourist and publicity, 1936.
SCHNEIDER, SIR GUALTERUS STEWART, Kt. Bach (1928), K.C.—B. 1864; ag. puisne just., Ceylon, Jan., 1921; commnr. of assize, Nov., 1921; puisne just., Dec., 1921; ag. ch. just., Sept.-Dec., 1926; ret.

SCHNEIDER, GEORGE ALBERT WILHELM.—B. 1884; ed. Pretoria High Schl. and S.African Coll., Cape Town; solr., sup. ct., Transvaal; civ. gen., Transvaal, 1907; ch. clk., dept. of lands, 1931; under-sec. for agr., 1935.

SCHOCK, AUGUST ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional asst. to atty-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dists., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, dept. of just., Pretoria, Sept., 1925; senr. law adviser, Oct., 1931.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; asst. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, Feb., 1915; J.P., 1915; passed law exam., 1916; ag. reg. and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs, in 1919, 1920 and 1921; dist. offr., South, in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923; mag., Hong Kong, 1926; mag., Kowloon, 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1929-30; dist. offr., South, 1930; 1st pol. mag., 1931; offr., cl.s., I.1935.
SCHOKMAN, CLIVE CONRADE.—B. 1897; 4th supt., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1924; 1st do., Apr., 1928; inspr.-gen., prisons, Mar., 1932.

SCHOLEFIELD, GUY HARDY, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan High prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly lib. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILLIAM.—Dep. clk., res. mag's. ct., Feb., 1917; asst. commnr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, July, 1919; ag. commnr., ditto, Sept. to Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCHUMANN, THOROD E. WERNER, B.A., M.Sc., Ph.D.—B. 1896; ed. Stellenbosch, S.A., Gottingen, Germany and Yale, U.S.A.; lect. in physics and math., Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1918-19; and at Stellenbosch, 1920-21; dir., fuel research inst. of S. Africa, June, 1931, reassg., Feb., 1933; ch. meteorologist, Union of S.A., Sept., 1933

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., M.B.E. (1935), L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regis., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; libr., Malta public library, July, 1923.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELEANOR.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M., & H. (Camb.); Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; prosector, 1894; asst. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; asst. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willesden Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1918-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII, mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge hosp., Aldershot, 1916; govt. pathologist, dir., serum inst. Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect., trop. disease, Westminster hosp. med. schl., 1926; examr., D.M.T. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research comte, 1928.

SCOTT, HENRY SEPTIMUS, C.M.G. (1935).—B.1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educn. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1924; dir., educn., Kenya, 1928.

SCOTT, SIR JOHN, K.B.E. (1932), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; prin. astt. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; admstd. govt. for various periods, 1924-29; col. sec., S.S., 1929; admstd. govt., Oct., 1929 to Feb., 1930 and from Oct., 1930 to June, 1931.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1926.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in despatches, 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924; dep. dir., sany. serv., 1932.

SCOTT, WALTER RICHARDSON.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; asst. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1925; attd. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu, 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; prte. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C. to gov., 1929; ag. dir., comm. intell., 1932-33; supt. pol., 1933; ag. divnl. supt., do., Mar., 1935.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERICK.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; command. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; asst. ch. engnr., Palestine rly., 1920; engrnr., ways and wks., Palestina rlys., 1924.
SORVENOR, T. V.—B. 1908; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1930; apptd. temp. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930 prior to serv. as an admstr. offr., N. Rhodesia.

SCULLIN, Rt. Hon. JAMES HENRY, P.C.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R., for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1929-31.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915, to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Deco., 1921; commt. constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. asst. to res. commr., on various occasions, 1923-33; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) Apr., 1925.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripes, pt. I., 1914 and pt. II., 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; asst. prin., C.O., 1922; prte. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929; prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; ed. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wonesh, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audt. dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., July-Sept., 1926; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also prte. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macclesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv., Cheshire Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; apptd. to air miny., 29th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as asst. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; prte. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the Cols. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June 1929; prin., 17th Sept., 1929.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; asst. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man.-sec., agric. bank, Jan., 1928.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ast. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. ast. to ast. D.P.W., Jan., 1925.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G., (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1906), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-86; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-96; succeeded his father, 1896; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1896; chmn., Pacific cable comtee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELBY, JOHN ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1900; venereal diseases offr., med. dep., Hong Kong, 1930.

SELLS, MARTIN PERONNET O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. ast. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rly., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in despatches); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engnr., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1920; ch. mech. engnr., Gold Coast rly., 1922; ch. mech. engnr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SELWYN CLARKE, PERCY SELWYN, M.C., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.F. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Eng.) with distinc., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—B. 1893; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health, W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. sany. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., sany. serv., May to Aug., 1927; del. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever confee., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; asst. dir., sany. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Médaille d'Argent des Épidémies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930; ast. dir., med. serv., Gold Coast, Jan., 1932; dep.-dir., health serv., 1933.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B'calo Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura and addnl. asst. govt. agt., Kegalle, Jan., 1929; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Jan., 1930; ditto, Anuradhapura, Sept., 1930; do., Ratnapura, Apr., 1931; asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Apr., 1934; asst. settlmt. offr., May, 1934; addnl. asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., in addn., Oct., 1934.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd., Matara kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927; asst. sttlmt. offr., Apr., 1929.

SENNETT, CEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A.), 1913); cadet, S.S., 1914; asst. regisr., impts. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; asst. dist. offr., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. sup't., govt. monopolies, Penang, Nov., 1923; dep. regisr., sup. ot. and asst. offr., assignee, Penang, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1926 (offr., cls. III.); 2nd mag., asst. dist. judge and coroner, Penang, Nov., 1926; collr., land rev., Penang, Apr., 1927 and Mar., 1930; offr., assignee and asst. regisr., coys., Penang, July, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; dist. offr., L. Perak Apr., 1933; 1st mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1933; asg. dist. offr., Kinta, Apr., 1935.

SERTSIOS, B. D., O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; asst. king's advoo., June, 1924; title changed to asst. atty.-gen., May, 1925; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Sept., 1934.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT, M.C. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Lealeham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; wounded, 1918; twice ment. in desps.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926; pres., dist. ct., Haifa, 1931; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1934; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1935; publication "Legislation of Transjordan, 1918-1930."

SEVERN, SIR CLAUS, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. clk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; asst. dist. offr., Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; sec. to high commnr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admnstd. govt. on various occasions, 1912-13, 1916-19, 1921-22; ret., 1926.

SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, Mar., 1921.

SEWELL, CHARLES YULE BLACKWOOD.—B. 1888; govt. roads dept., New Zealand, 1906; surv., rev. survey dept., Perak, Oct., 1908; surv., 1st grade, Apr., 1909; asst. supt., surveys, Kedah, Aug., 1917; supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ditto, Selangor, July, 1929; senr. supt., surveys, S'pore, May, 1933.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schol. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; asst. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Mullaittivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; asst. govt. agt., Mullaittivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; asst. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; regisr. gen., Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926; O.A.G., Fiji and high commnr., W. Pacific, for various periods, 1927-33; gov.'s dep. for various periods, 1930-35; col. sec., Trinidad, 1935.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CHARLES F.R.S.A., F.R.E.S., M.R.A.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1918; capt., H.M.A.Z., 31, 1918; demob., 1919; asst. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. asst. admistr.-gen., 1926-27; ag. asst. sec., pvt. sec. to Br. res., clk. of couns. and edr., offr. gal., 1928; ag. prov. commnr. (with seat on leg. coun.) 1929, 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1934; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935; assisted in revision of Madan's Swahili Dictionary, 1931.

SHACKLES, DEREK HOLMES.—B. 1900; ed., R.N.C., Osborne and Greshams Schol., Holt; solr.; asst. commnr., landa, Gold Coast, 1929; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. regisr., 1933; regisr., high ct., Uganda, 1934.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed., Univ. Coll. Schol. and King's Coll., London; comprtr. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Congt., 1914-18; ch. acct., P. and T., Nigeria, 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G. in 1928-30; ret., Oct., 1930; reappd. col. postmnr., Barbados, July, 1931 and in 1934.
SHARPE, H. B.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919; ag. dist. commnr., 1926.

SHAW, BARNABAS JAMES DOLD.—B. 1877; elk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1897; senr. elk., Pretoria, 1915; prin. elk., 1920; ch. elk., Cape Town, 1928; collr., cust. and S.M., E. London, 1932; do., Johannesburg, 1934; do., Port Elizabeth, 1934.

SHAW, FREDERIC LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; ag. headmast., English Coll., Johore Bahru, Feb., 1921; headmast., govt. English Schl., Muar, June, 1922; 1st asst. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Nov., 1925; asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Jan., 1926; inspr., schls., Selangor, Apr., 1926; asst. inspr., schls. Singapore and Labuan, Mar., 1927 and Dec., 1928; headmast., Victoria Inst. K. Lumpur, Aug., 1929.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed., Clifton Schol., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schl., Kingston; asst. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, May, 1921; dist. commnr., May, 1928.

SHAW, JOHN VALENTINE WESTAN.—B. 1894; ed., Reston Schol.; service with M.E., 1914-19; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928; seconded for duty at C.O., Mar.-Sept., 1930; asst. sec., grade F, Palestine, 1935.
SHAW, ROBERT PHILLIP, A.R.I.B.A. (Chartered Architect).—B. 1888; ed. Bathgate Acad. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edinburgh; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT. BACH.(1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908, to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admtd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., R. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stittinta., May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian Schla., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Sept., 1917; demob., May, 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; sec., rubber restrn., Oct., 1922; supernum. off., cls. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contrlr., rubber expts., Johore, in addn., Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; dist. offr., Temerloh, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; dist. offr., K. Pilah, Nov., 1933.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND., C.M.G.(1934).—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; capt., M.S.V.R., 1915; off. assignee and pub. trustee, 1923; chmn., trade statistics comttee., Aug., 1926; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1926; treas., S.S., 1929; ag. dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S. and pres., Raffles Coll., Dec., 1929; Br. res., Perak, June, 1932; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1933.
SHELLISH, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1881; jun. clk., postal dept., Cyprus, 1902; asst. island postmtr., 1921; postmtr.-gen., 1934.

SHELSWELL-WHITE, GROFFREY HENRY, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schl.; comand. with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. comanr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res., ag. 1st asst. sec., secretariat, clk. to prot. coun. and edr., off. gaz. at various periods; higher standard Swahili, 1925; pres., Pemba Swahili exam. bd., 1929; dist. registr., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; ag. res. mag., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; called to bar, 1930; pte. sec. to Sultan, 1930; asst. sec., secretariat, 1930; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1931; edr., Offl. Gaz., 1931-33; trades licensing omsnr., 1934; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934; ag. asst. ch. sec. and prov. comanr., 1935; author of "A Guide to Zanzibar."
SHELTON, C. F., M.D., B.S., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.P.—B. 1888; civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921; junr. med. specialist, 1934.

SHELTON, JAMES, F.I.C.—B. 1886; asst. rech. and analytical chem. in the sc. and tech. dept., Imp. Inst., G. Britain, Mar., 1909; 2nd asst. govt. analyst, Singapore, Feb., 1915; chem., geol. dept., F.M.S., Feb., 1917; in charge, of geol. dept., June, 1917; on sp. duty at Inst. Med. Resch., Jan., 1923; in charge, trade and cust. labv., Mar., 1930 and July, 1933; ag. ch. chem., Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Mar., 1931.

SHELTON, LAWRENCE HUGH, M.A. (Cantab).—B. 1891; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Tenbury, Hereford Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1915; cls. I, admstv. serv., 1932; ag. dep. ch. sec., Nigeria, in 1934 and 1935; prin. asst. sec., Jan., 1935.

SHEPPARD, CHARLES HENRY, O.B.E.(1932).—B. 1897; European War, 1914-1918; ment. in desps.; Kurdish gendarmerie, 1919-21; inspecting offr., Iraq pol., 1922-31; Iraq gallantry med., 1932; asst. commdt., pol., Somaliland, 1931.

SHEPSTONE, FRANCIS ALLAN, K.C.—B. 1879; ed. Harrow; rly. constr., Pretoria-Pietersburg, 1898-99; Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1901; barrister, Inner Temple, 1905; Transvaal bar, 1907; Natal, 1908; farming, 1912-22; S.A.H.R., 1918; solvr., 1922-29; Natal bar, 1930; ag. judge, native high ct., 1932; K.C., 1934; judge, native high ct., 1934.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, SIR JOSEPH, KT. Bach.(1932).—B. 1882; ed. Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Ireland, 1907; went Connaught circuit; judicial clk. (title subsequently changed to asst. to atty.-gen.), Nyasaland Prot., 1908-13; ag. atty.-gen. on different occasions, 1909-13; ag. judge, high ct., Dec., 1909 to Jan., 1910; res. mag., Kenya, 1913-20; ag. puisne judge, Kenya, and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1919-20; puisne judge, Kenya and mem. ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1920; ag. ch. just., Kenya, 1928-29; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ch. just., Kenya, 1934.
SHERINGHAM, ANTHONY ILEX.—B. 1890; ed. Brighton Coll.; Ceylon pol., 1909-31; on active serv., 1917-18 (lieut., 8th Hussars); comanr., pol., and comanr., prisons, Zanzibar, Apr., 1931.

SHERLOCK, DAVID THOMAS JOSEPH, K.C.(1922), B.A.T.C.D., M.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; judl. comanr. and offl. mem., leg. coun., N. Borneo, Dec., 1920; ch. just., Feb., 1926; ret., Aug., 1935; judge, ct. of appl., Jamaica, 1935.

SHRREWELL, ARNOLD GUY.—B. 1891; ed. St. Edward's Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A. (schl. of jurisprudence, hons. cls. III); called to bar (Inner Temple), 1914; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1914; mily. serv. in Africa, ment. in desps., 1915; practised at bar, 1920-25; dist. judge, Sudan, 1925; asst. advoc. gen. and legal advr. to Sudan govt., rlys. and steamers, 1928; govt. advoc., Palestine, Apr., 1929; relieving pres. of dist. cts., 1930; pres. dist. ct., 1936.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFRY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.

SHIELS, DR. T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch. B.); served European War as offr. R. Scots.; in command with rank as capt. one of trench mortar batteries of 9th (Scottish) Divn. (severely wounded); was a mem., Edinburgh town coun.; M.P., East Edinburgh since 1924; mem., sp. comanr., Ceylon constitution, 1927; parly. under sec., India Office, June, 1924; parly. under-sec. for cols., 1929-31.

SHILL, A. T.—B. 1895; on active serv. with Rifle Brig. and Dorset Regt., 1915-17; wounded; Imp. cust. and excise dept., 1919; also dep. supt., mercantile marine, recr. of wreck, regsr. shipping, Lascar transfer offr., etc. asst. recr. gen., Gambie, Aug., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1929 and from July, 1931; contr. food and fuel, May, 1931; supervisor, cust. and exc. dept., Mauritius, Apr., 1932.

SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. elk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff elk., 1911; registr. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., promotions, 19th Nov., 1928.

SHIRLEY, EVELYN PHILIP SEWALLIS, O.B.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columbus Coll. Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll., Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1923; 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyik and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade commr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; priv. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. elk., store dept., I.
