O., Oct., 1900; transf'd. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permitt. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924; dep. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

SHUTE, GEOFFREY GAY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Schl., Eastbourne, Uppingham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; cls. I, admstive serv., 1930; priv. asst. sec., S. Prova., 1931; sec., S. Prova., 1933.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripso, pt. I, 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf'd. to C.O. as asst. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comttee., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comttee., 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 15th Sept., 1928; pvt. sec., permitt. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929, priv., 24th Jan., 1930; accomp. permitt. under S. of S. for the Cols. (Sir J. Maffey) to W. Indies, 1936.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., June to Oct., 1925.

SIKES, HOWARD LECKY, C.B.E. (1936), B.A., B.E. (hons. and extbr.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Botham Schl., York; asst. engrn., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engrn. Mombasa water wks., 1911; engrn. in ch., Magadi water wks., constrn., 1913; res. engrn., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneers, 1915; capt., 1916; govt. hydraulic engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.

SIMMONS, BENJAMIN ROBERT—Cltk. in conservator of forest's office, E. conservancy, Transkei, 1898; ag. dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1902; dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1906; ditto, George, 1914; 2nd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1929; prin. forest offr. (forestry inspr.), Natal and Transkei, 1932; Western inspectorate, 1935.

SIMMONS, GEORGE STANLEY, M.I.Mech.E., Chartered Mech. Engrn.—B. 1892; ed. Battersea Polytechnic; asst. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks rly.; loco. supt., Togoland Milly. rly., Apr., 1917; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., May, 1920; dist. loco. supt., do., July, 1925; asst. ch. mech. engrn., do., Dec., 1926; ch. mech. engrn., do., Apr., 1934.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resign., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct. Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMMS, HENRY, O.B.E. (1935), M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; sany. engrn., Gold Coast, 1908; sany. engrn., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1925.
SIMPSON, JOHN.—Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1892; transf'd., post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1893; survr., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Aug., 1921.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH, C.B.E. (1936).—B. 1878; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1918.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine coman. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.L., 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admstn., 29th Dec., 1921; senator, June, 1930.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and 1918-32; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comsn., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBECK, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc.—Cltk., atty.-gen's. office, 1893; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf'd., dept. of agr., 1907; transf'd., dept. of int., 1911; govt. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf'd., dept. of agr., Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, Mar., 1926.

SINCLAIR-BURGESS, MAJOR-GEN. SIR WILLIAM LIVINGSTONE HATCHWELL, K.B.E. (1935), Kt. Bach (1934), C.B., C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916)—Lieut., N.Z. Staff Corps, Mar., 1911; adjt., 16th Waikato Regt. and O.C. No. 4 group, Mar., 1911; capt., June, 1913; proceeded as exchange offr. with Australian mil. forces, 1913; brig.-major, 6th mil. dist., 1913-14; left Australia with A.I.F.; O.C., 9th Batt., 1914; major (A.I.F.), 1914; commdr., Australian F.A. Ele., with rank lieut.-col., Feb., 1916; commdr., 4th Australian Divl. Arty. with rank brig.-gen., Aug., 1917-19; major, N.Z. staff corps, 1919; lieut.-col., Aug., 1919; arty. staff offr., cent. commd., Nov., 1919; G.S.O. (I) at G.H.Q., Apr., 1922; dir., mily. training and intell., Jan., 1924; ch. of gen. staff, Apr., 1924 with temp. rank col. on staff; ag. commdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Sep.-Dec., 1925; temp-brig., June, 1928; major-gen. and apptd. commdt. N.Z. mily. forces, Apr., 1931; Croix d'officier of Legion of Honour; awarded American D.S.M.; six times ment. in deeps.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT McDONNELL, KT. BACH (1927)—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; ag. res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov't., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apl., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, do., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, do., Manchester, Apl. to June, 1904, do., St. James and Trelawny in 1904, 1905 and 1906; regiar-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. comsrr. of Belize, Apl., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.-gen., 1919-20; seconded, puisne judge, Jamaica, 1920; lat puisne judge, 1921; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. rules, 1926; apptd. arbitrator by govt. of U.S.A. and Guatemala in case of P.W. Shufeldt v. the govt. of Guatemala.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIRAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; attd., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; ditto, Puttalam, Oct., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Nov., 1929; dist. judge, Kegalle, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1932; do., Batticaloa, May, 1934.
SKAN, D. A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., B.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; war serv., N.R.V.R., 1918-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

SKEETE, CEOL CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hona. nat. sci. tripos pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schl. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Barbados, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1924 and 1925; asst. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir., sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 15th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; asst. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. comsrr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., Dec., 1922; dep. prov. comsrr., July, 1927; prov. comsrr., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., native affrs. Apr., June, 1930 and Apr. to Oct., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., June-Sept., 1930.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., Apl., 1918 to Feb., 1920; clk. to comsrr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; lat clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927; ag. prin., Prince of Wales Coll., July to Sept., 1929; asst. col. sec., Sept., 1931; ag. clk., couns., May to June, 1932.

SKINNER, HUGH WELBY.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; served in France, India, Mesopotamia; capt., T.A. res., gen. list, R.A.; apptd. coll. audlt. dept., 1923; asst. audlr., Nyassaland, 1923; asst. audlr., Nigeria, 1926; coll. audlt., 1930; asst. audlr., Gold Coast, 1935.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—B.1890; Gloucestershire R.F.A., 1908 to 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 1910 to 1913; E.A.P. pol., Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1920 to 1923; supt., pol., Nyassaland, 1922; ag. ch. comsrr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 1923 to 1924; asst. commdt., pol., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. comsrr., pol. and gov., prisons on various occasions, 1925-35.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL, M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsrr., income tax, May, 1927; pvt. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., May to Sept., 1930; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Mar., 1931; offl. mem., fedl. exec. coun. and gen. leg. coun.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Antigua; dep. treas. and comsrr., income tax, Trinidad, Mar., 1934.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Asts. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional astt. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd astt. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. astt. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsrr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on sp. duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1916; ag. gov'r., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1932; resigned, 1934.

SLEEP, CAPT. ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Manchester).—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; attd. land office, Kelantan, Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1931; 2nd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1931; ag. astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, May, 1934.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; astt. comsrr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsrr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. commnr., Dec., 1900; res. commnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SLOMAN, ARTHUR ERIC PENROSE, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Bradfield Coll., Berks., Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. of Neuchatel, Switzerland; served in Great War, 1915-19; lieut., R.F.A.; mem., Br. pol. mission to Greece, 1920-27; insp., secondary schls., Cyprus, 1934.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM, M.A., B.Sc. (Glas.).—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V, sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1914; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1923; town planning admstr., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. Ia, finan. commnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, June, 1927; offr., cls. Ia, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Dec., 1930; treas., S.S., June, 1932.
SMALL, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (Mily.), ment. in desps.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. of St. Andrews; D.Sc.; botanist, Uganda, 1913; mycologist, 1920; mycologist, Ceylon, 1926; dir. of agr., Nyasaland, 1930.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra.; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examr., physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1929; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; ag. dep. dir., med. and sanry. services, Feb., 1928 to Feb., 1929; senr. med. offr. and in ch., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1931.

SMALLWOOD, MARGARET, M.B., B.S. Grad. L.S.T.M. and Hyg. Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.—B. 1898; lady med. offr., inf. wel. centre, Ipoh, May, 1924; do., hosp. Ipoh, Nov., 1927; do., women's hosp., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1933; do., hosp., Ipoh, Sept., 1934.

SMART, CAPT. ARCHIBALD Guelph Holdsworth, M.B.E. (Mil.), M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1882; med. offr., i/c quarantine stn., Singapore, May, 1912; attd., govt. lab., Singapore, Jly., 1913; lent to Kedah for anti-cholera work, Jan.-Apr., 1914; asst. health offr., Singapore, June, 1914; on war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-18; med. offr., S. Kedah, Oct., 1920; health offr., Kedah, Nov., 1921; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1927; ditto, Perak, May, 1928; State med. and health offr., Selangor and registr.-gen., births and deaths, F.M.S., Jan., 1932; mem., S.S. and F.M.S. med. coun., Feb., 1932; ret.; senr. med. offr., St. Vincent, 1935.

SMART, HAROLD PHILIP.—B. 1899; ed. Michaelhouse Diocesan Schl., Natal, Glen Schl. of Agr., O.F.S., (Farmers Weekly gold medallist), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; (Farmers Weekly schl.), B. Sc. Agr., Balliol Coll., Oxford, Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, A.I.C.T.A.; served with R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1918-20; lecturer in dairy ing. dept. agr., Union of S. Africa., 1926; agr. offr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

SMART, LESLIE MASON, C.B.E. (1936)—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., Mar., 1927; gen. man., Gold Coast rly. and hbr. authority, Takoradi hbr., Oct., 1933.

SMART, V. IRVING.—B. 1875; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; graduated from Queen's Univ.; engaged on surveys for the Dom. govt., 1897-98; ent. rly. serv. 1900; prot., rly. engnr. and opern., McGill Univ., Montreal, 1907-14; sp. engnr., Canadian National Rlys., 1923-28; gen. supt., trans. Western region, C.N.R., 1928-30; dep. min. of rly. and canals, Dominion of Canada; also govt. dir., Canadian National Rlys., 1930.

SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PRACY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, May, 1929; ditto, Tanganyika Territory, 1934.
SMIT, DOUGLAS LAING.—B. 1885; Cape civ. serv., 1903; pub. proscr., 1912; mag., 1927; under sec. for just., Pretoria, 1933; sec., nat. affrs., 1934.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comm., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., May-Oct., 1924; sec. for lands, Feb., 1934.

SMIT, JACOBS STEPHANUS.—B. 1873; D.T.D. (Dekoratie Trouwe Dienst), Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; pte. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen. Transvaal, 1908; asst. mag., Transvaal, 1909; asst. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resign., 1915; el. mem. for Klipdorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925; admstr., Transvaal, 1929-34; chmn., diamond control bd., 1934.

SMITH, LIEUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harthr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, Feb., 1926.

SMITH, ALLAN CHALMERS, B.A., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Warwick Acad., Bermuda, Rossall Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; war serv., France and Italy, 1915-18 (three times ment. in desps.); pol.-mag., W. dist., Bermuda, Jan., 1928; M.L.A., 1929; pol. mag., cent. dist. and chmn., licensing authorities, Oct., 1931; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1935.
SMITH, A. H. W., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.M. (Univ. of Lond.)—Med. supt., St. Ann's lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Nov., 1934.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. asst., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions comm., 1920; sec., deptl. salaries comttee, 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comttee, July, 1923; transf'd to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt., sup. ct., Oct., 1925; ch. acct., G.P.O., Dec., 1932; ag. P.M.G., 1935.

SMITH, CHARLES JOHNSTON, C.B.E., (1935), M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1880; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1911; med. offr., grade III, Aug., 1912; surgn., native hosps., S.S., Aug., 1914; sr. surgn., Singapore, May, 1917.

SMITH, CHARLES ROBERT.—B. 1887; N. Borneo serv., Aug., 1913; astt. dist. offr., May, 1916; mag., 1st cls., Jan., 1918; dist. offr., July, 1919; astt. gov't. sec., Oct., 1926; ag. under sec., July, 1929; ag. res., May, 1930; res., Jan., 1932; ag. gov't. sec., Mar., 1934; addl. sess. judge, May, 1934; ag. judge, high ct., June, 1934.
SMITH, CLIVE ALEXANDER.—B. 1903; ed. Malton Grammar Schl.; R. Navy, 1919; sub. inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1924; 3rd cls. inspr., May, 1930.

SMITH, CUTHBERT, Dip. in Forestry (Oxon.).—B. 1890; astt. conserv., forests, grade II, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; astt. conserv., forests (new scheme), Jan., 1919; dep. conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; ag. conserv., forests, Johore, May, 1930; conserv., forests, Johore, 1934.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. exped. force, 1917-18; barrister, solic., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928; examr. in law, univ. of N.Z., 1920-27; appointee of U.S.A. as non-national mem. perm. comm. to settle disputes between U.S.A. and Peru; chmn., native affrs. comm. (N.Z.), 1934; author of "Adoption of Children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct., 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, EDWARD PAUL.—B. 1884; Cape mounted pol., June, 1901; clk. to regr., deeds, Cape Town, 1903; clk. to high sheriff, C.T., 1907; 2nd gr. clk., pub. ser. comm., 1913; on mily. serv., Great War, 1915-18 (wounded); 1st gr. clk., ditto, 1919; 1st gr. clk., treasry., 1921; senr. clk., P.S.C., 1922; prin. clk., ditto, 1927; ch. clk., ditto, 1930; pub. ser. inspr., 1931.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.
SMITH, ERNEST ALLEN.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; astt. audr., Gold Coast, 1924; audr., Leeward Is., 1927; senr. astt. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. audr., 1934; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1935.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.—prizeman in constit. law and hist.; astt. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1908; astt. pol. mag., Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., Jan., 1914; transf. to legal dept., Sept., 1914; ag. solr. gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921 and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Aug., 1922; puism. judge, G. Coast, June, 1925; ch. just., Seychelles, 1935; compiled 1928 edition Laws of Gold Coast, Ashanti, etc.

SMITH, ERNEST JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. E. London Tech. Coll.; mily. serv., Gold Coast Volunteers, 1914-18; astt. acct., constrn., Gold Coast rly., 1920; ch. acct., 1922; dep. ch. constrn. acct., Nigerian rly., 1929; astt. ch. acct., 1930; ch. acct., 1933.

SMITH, FRANCIS EDWARD VINEY, B.Sc. (Hons.)—Ed. Colston's Schl. and Univ., Bristol, 1921; dept. sci. and indust. research, 1921; senr. astt. mycologist, min. of agr. and fisheries, Eng., 1924; govt. microbiologist, Jamaica, 1927; Jamaica del., Impl. Mycological Conf., and Impl. Meteorological Conf., London, 1929; banana industry aid bd, 1932; ag. dir. agr., 1933; sec., W.I. Fruit and Veg. Conf., 1933; mission, Honduras and Florida on citrus industry, 1933.

SMITH, FREDERIC GORDON—B. 1886; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa, with N. R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia, with B.S.A. Co's adminstr., including registr., high ct. etc., and astt. legal adviser and pub. pros.; astt. atty-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927; ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1927 to Mar., 1928 and in 1931; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1932; puism. judge, S. Stlntms., 1935.

SMITH-FOLLETT, ROBERT REDVERS, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.—B. 1901; ed. Skinners Schl. and Impl. Coll. Sci. and Tech. (B.Sc. hons. chem.); Impl. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1925; research astt. to prof., chem. and soil sci., 1927; chemist-ecologist attd. to col. comtee., Br. Guiana, 1929; chemist-ecologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914).—C. M. G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, Apr., 1873; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; astt. to ch. sec., Dec., 1883; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1886 to Nov., 1891; commr., Papho, Nov., 1891; regnr.-gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., June, 1895; as sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comtee., 1887; pres. mun. comm., Papho, 1892; ag. Br. del. of Evraf, under conven. with Turkey, Mar., to Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903 and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; Br. del. of Evraf, July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1909; col. sec., Mauritius, June, 1910; administered govt., Apr., to Nov., 1911; gov., Nyasaland, May, 1913; assumed govt., Sept., 1913; ret., Dec., 1923.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub.-inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18; inspr., 1930.

SMITH, HARRY LESTER, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., O.R.C., May, 1903; clk., col. sec's office, O.R.C., Aug., 1903; clk., gov't. office, O.R.C., 1907; clk., gov'-gen's. office, Union of S.A., May, 1910; prin. clk., July, 1924; ch. clk., Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to gov.-genl., Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1927; ch. clk., off. of high commr. for S.A., Jan., 1928; sec. Swaziland finan. and econ. comm., 1931-32; ditto, Basutoland, 1934-35; astt. admin. sec., 1934.
SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pocklington Schl. and Queen's Coll. Cambridge; astt. res., N. Nigeria Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub.-insp., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES DAVID MAXWELL, M.A. (Aberdeen).—B. 1895; cadet, F.M.S., July, 1920; astt. collr., land rev., Lower Perak, Oct., 1920; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1922; astt. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1925; offr., cls. IV., Sept., 1926; offr., cls. III., Sept., 1931; retrenchment offr., F.M.S., May, 1932; ag. legal advr. and judi. commr., Kelantan, Apr., 1934.

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, July, 1891; draftsman, survey off., Sept., 1893; warden and asst. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895 to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., Apr., 1902; comnr. of crown lands, June, 1904; survr., crown lands, 1904; ag. supt., wks., June, 1906 to Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton gin- nery, Oct., 1912; ool. engr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt. pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1883; ed. New Vety Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reappt., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir., animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Provs., Mar., 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., Sept., 1923.
SMITH, JOSEPH MARIO, O.B.E. (1927), M.R.C.V.S.—Vet. inspr., min. of agric., Egypt, 1912-14; lieut., R.A.V.C., 1914; mily. service, 1914-19; major, 1919; ch. vety. offr., Palestine, July, 1920.
SMITH, L. J. D.—B. 1893; ed. Rastriek Grammar Schl.; U.K. oust., 1914-16; mily. serv., 1917-19; supt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. supervar., cust., 1935.

SMITH, LAWRENCE DELPRÉ.—B. 1905; ed. Christ's Hosp., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (class. exhibr.); B.A. 1928; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1931; ag. crown coun., Jan., 1933; Apr., 1934; res. mag., Apr., 1934.

SMITH, LEO ANDREW, A.R.S.M., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1886; inspr., mines, Perak, June, 1916; asst. warden, mines, June, 1926; sr. inspr., mines, Larut, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Selangor, Aug., 1932; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1933; ag. warden, mines, Perak, Sept., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; enkd. regisy. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923; higher grade cler. offr., 1st Mar., 1930.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regiar. of shipping) comnr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regiar. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. asst. comnr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apl., 1913.
SMITH, M.G., M.A.—B. 1904; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Dec., 1926.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedburgh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk.of couns., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. asst. postmstr.-gen., 1914; ag. postmstr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; reed. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; supt., impta. and expts., 1921; attd., Geneva opium confoe., 1923; head of sany. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., 1930; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1931; dir., educn., 1932; sec., Chinese affrs., and mem., exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1934.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.E. (Lond.)—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrookes, archs. and survrs., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as asst.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contrs., Lond.; asst. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engrnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engrnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engrnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engrnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1916.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, ROBERT KNOWLTON, LL.B., K.C.—B. 1887; ed., St. Francois Xavier Univ., Antigonish, N.S.; grad. Dalhousie Law Schl.; admitted to Nova Scotia bar, Mar., 1911; K.C., Jan., 1929; crown pros., Cumberland County, N.S. Aug.—Oct., 1925; mayor, Amherst, 1923; el. to H. of C., Canada, Oct., 1925, re-elec. at g.e., 1926 and 1930; dep. min. of marine, Aug., 1935.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1892; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 29th Oct., 1930.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, C.B.E. (1929), F.R.Aus. H.S.—B. 1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N. S. Wales, June, 1912; asst. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, STEPHEN JOHN.—B. 1887; N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; internal affrs. dept., 1913; sec., Samoan admnstr., 1915; ch. clk., external affrs. dept., 1920-30; ch. clk., Cook Is. dept., 1920-28; sec., 1928.

SMITH, HON. SYDNEY GEORGE.—B. 1879; ed. public schl., New Plymouth, N.Z.; mem. N.Z. H. of R., 1918-25 and since 1928; chmn., comm., 1928-30, 1931-34; min., lab. and immigrn., 1930-31; min., educn., 1934, and of empymt., 1936.

SMITH, TRAFFORD.—B. 1912; ed. City Boys Schl., Leicester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1930-34 (state schl., senr. schl., Gestion Exhibr.); B.A., 1933 (1st cls. pts. I and II mod. and med. languages tripos); apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1935.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; asst. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Stiltns., Jan., 1908; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engrnr., grade III, July, 1908; senr. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; do., Kuala Lumpur Town, Apr., 1925; do., Taiping, Dec., 1928; do., Kinta, June, 1929; do., waterwks., Selangor, Mar., 1932; state engrnr., N. Sembilan, Ncv., 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM FORGAN, M.L.A. (Queensland).—B. and ed. in Scotland; M.L.A., Queensland since 1915; successively min. without portfolio, sec. for pub. wks. and sec. for agr. and stock; premier and treas., June, 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Calcutta).—ed.Trinity COLL., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; ag. D.D.S.S., on various occasions, 1930-33; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb.; Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripods, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Dec., 1903; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., Jan., 1915; wounded, Mar., 1917; relinquished commission owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903-29; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.O. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripods, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comttee. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confce. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.P. for Standerton, 1924; leader, opposition, 1924-33; pres., Br. Assn. for Advancement of Science, 1931; min. of just. and dep. prime min., coalition govt., 1933; leader, S. African delegation, World Economic Confce., 1933.

SMYTH, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH. (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911; ret., 1929.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Kings Coll., London; clk., bd. of educn., 1906; clk., C.A., 1910; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supt., 1917 and 1919; land asst., 1919; sec., land tenure coms., 1920; warden of mines, 1920; registr. of titles, etc., 1922; asst. land offr., 1922.

SNELL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; asst. post office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; asst. G.P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., acct. branch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apl., 1912; income tax office, Pretoria, Sept., 1914; senr. clk., income tax office, Cape Town, July, 1915; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Mar., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; ch. clk., income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; assr. comptur. inland rev., Sept., 1929.
SNYMAN, NICOLAAS.—Prof. asst. to survr.-gen., O.F.S., 1920; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Dec., 1931; ditto, Natal, May, 1934.

SOLLIS, CLIFFORD GEORGE, M.A.—B. 1895; ed. Bristol Grammar Schol., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mast., Bukit Zahrah Sch., Johore Bahru, Dec., 1920; ag. inspr., schls., Pahang, Oct., 1925; do., Penang, Mar., 1928; supt., educn., Kedah, Dec., 1930; inspr., schls., Selangor, Aug., 1931.

SOLOMON, NEVILLE STAFFORD VICTOR, O.B.E. (1936)—B. 1874; clk., G.P.O., Bahamas, 1891; tel. dept., 1892; treas. and cust., 1895; supervisor, cust., 2nd gde., Gold Coast, 1902; ag. ch. regisr., sup. ct., 1906; finan. asst., treas. and cust. dept., Nigeria, 1907; supervisor, 1st gde., cust. dept., 1910; collr., 1917; admry. reporting offr., various ports, Nigeria, during great war, 1914-18; ret. on pension, 1922; asst. postmstr. (temp.), Bahamas, Jan., 1924; resigned, Nov., 1925; recr. gen. an treas., and mem. exec. coun., Bahamas, 1928; mem., leg. coun., Feb., 1931; chmn., licensing authority, Mar., 1931; has held various ag. appts.; dep. gov., July, 1935.
SOLOMON, SAUL.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; asst., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colomba Kach., Dec., 1922; office asst. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office asst., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th asst. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colomba, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1931; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1932; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, June, 1933; dep. contr., lab., May, 1934.

SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; gov't. pilot, Kenya, 1924; asst. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., on various occasions, 1929-35.

SOPER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., 1926; agrl. offr., 1928.

SOUTH, FRANCIS WILTON, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; mycologist and lect. in agrl. sci., Imp. dept. of agr. for W. Indices, Mar., 1909; ch. agrl. inspr., agrl. dept., F.M.S., May, 1913; ch. agrl. field offr., Nov., 1924; ag. sec. for agrl. S.S. and F.M.S., for various periods 1927-31; rep. of Malaya at mycological confce. in Lond., Sept., 1929; ag. dir., agrl. S.S. and F.M.S. for various periods, 1929 and 1931-32.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W.W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1886-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec. rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic coman., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions coman., 1906; mem. of comans. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal coman. on electoral reform, 1909; regiar. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development coman., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, SIR WILFRID THOMAS, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; offr., asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; asst. gov't. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov', Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. asst. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port coman., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1923 and exec. coun., 1923; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., for various periods, 1927-36.
SPAFFORD, H. H., B.A.—B. 1899; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

SPARROW, HERBERT ROBERT, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; asst. elec. engrnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; elec. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr. in addn., various periods, 1928-31; ag. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., June, 1934.

SPARRER, WILLIAM LABARTE.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, 1927; seconded to Weihaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; passed in Mandarin and Cantonese; attd. Punjab pol., 1931-2; passed in Urdu, 1932.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripso, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.
SPIERS, R. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1925.

SPIEGEL, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, C.M.G. (1934), M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary asst. supt. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; asst. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I, 1923; comanr., pol., Kenya, June, 1925; comdt., pol., Palestine, 1930.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schl. (Som.); served in engnr. dept., G.W.R., 1896 to 1910; joined engnr. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stthmts., 1920; oh. engrnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., and ch. engrnr., rly., F.M.S., 1926.
SPOONER, ARTHUR CASSWELL.—B. 1906; ed. Epsom Coll.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; asst. dist. comanr., 1930.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GAETH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engrnr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engrnr., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engrnr., grade I, Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engrnr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; senr. dist. engrnr., J. Bahru, Mar., 1927; ditto, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; engrnr., ways and wks., June, 1929; ch. engrnr., do., Nov., 1934.

SPOWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; do., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; do., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. supt., surveys, Johore, Jan. 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S., and S.S., June, 1933.

STACPOOLLE, GEORGE WENTWORTH.—Asst. comanr., lands survey dept., Gold Coast, 1925; transf'd. to lands dept., 1927; ag. dep. comanr., July, 1928-Apr., 1929; ag. comanr., lands, in 1932 and 1933; comanr., lands, Oct., 1933.

STAINES, CAPT. EDMUND ALFRED.—B. 1882; P. and T. dept., G. Britain, 1889; Br. sea post offr., U.K.-U.S.A., 1907; asst. supt., P. and T., F.M.S., Sept., 1911; mil. service, 1915-18; ag. acct., P. and T., F.M.S., 1921-1925; contrlr., P. and T., Parak and Dindings, June, 1927; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, June, 1928; do., Penang and P.W., May, 1931.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913)—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov. gen., 1913; resident comanr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comanr., for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, Aug., 1927; H. M. high comanr. for S. Africa, and first high comanr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1931; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., S. Rhodesia, 8th Jan., 1935.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (med. and clasps); ag. trav. comanr. Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig., comnr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comnr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. comnr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, Sir AMBROSE THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1929), M.D., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1875; sen. house surg. (to Sir Patrick Manson), hosp. for trop. diseases, 1906; astt., Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1907; bacteriologist, de., 1908; dir., gov. laboratories, F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser, C.O., 1926; chmn., col. advisory med. comte., and managing comte., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1926; author of publications on "Beriberi," "Malayan Culicidiae" and "Meloidiosis."
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.
STATHAM, Hon. Sir CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. parl. since 1915; speaker since 1923.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., astt. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; astt. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. barbmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; regis. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; astt. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan., 1919 to Mar., 1920; refugee comnr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; regis. of patents, Aug., 1920; ob. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Française, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. comnr., Larnaca, for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; regis. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; regis. of companies, July, 1922; regis. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; regis. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. regis.-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; regis., trade marks and patents, Apr., 1928; ag. regis., companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; astt. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.B., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immigr. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.

STEELLE, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, July, 1899; mag. dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regis.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920; pol. mag., Grenada, Oct., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1931.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined R.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; comand., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in desps.; ch. comnr., pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1869; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1894; mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, Jan., 1912, and mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, Feb., 1912; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Feb., 1925; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Naval Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edtn.); astt. editor of 11th edtn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter parties," etc.; contributor to the Encyclopedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENS, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, M.C. (1918)—B. 1887; 4th Canadian Mounted Rifles, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., 5th Batt. East Lancs. Regt., Sept., 1915; served France and Flanders, 1916-1918; astt. treas., Gold Coast, May, 1921; ag. dep. treas., Sept.-Oct., 1932, Apr.-Sept., 1934 and July-Sept., 1935.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914 to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; astt. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; astt. priv. sec. to S. S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923; astt., secretariat, Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; prin., 12th Dec., 1924; U. K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1925; astt., secretariat (economic sec.), Imp. Conf., 1926; int. sec., confce. on open. of Dom. legislation, 1929; admtrv. astt. sec., Imp. Conf., 1930; astt. sec., 1st Feb., 1936.

STERLING, HERBERT HARRY, C.M.G. (1935), LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. Christchurch (N.Z.) Boys High Schl., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. rlya. dept., 1901; mem., rlya. bd., 1924; ret., 1926; gen. man., rlya., 1928; chmn., rlya. bd., 1931.

STEVEN, GEORGE HENRY, M.B., Ch.B., U. Edin, 1901.—B. 1877; ag. res. surg., St. Lucia, 1919; bac. M.O.H. and port health offr., Grenada, 1920-21; C.M.O. (col. surg.) St. Vincent, 1921-25; med. ser., B. Guiana, 1925; G.M.O., pub. hosp., ag. astt. surg., pub. hosp., Georgetown, 1925; ag. bac. and path., 1925-26; govt. bac. and path., 1926.
STEVENS, BERTRAM SYDNEY BURNSDALE.—B. 1889; treas., N.S.W., 1929-30; premier and treas., 1932.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schl. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. homs., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; M.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; att. Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avissawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1926; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1932; agt. gov't., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1934.
twin_text: STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; M.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; att. Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avissawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1926; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1932; agt. gov't., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1934.

raw_text: STEVENSON, CECIL WILSON, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., F.B.Met.Soc.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; capt., R.E., 1915; adjt., 1916; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mono Star" ment. in desps.; exec. engr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. D.P.W., Apl. to Dec., 1920; ag. dep. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1921-30; D.D.P.W., 1931; ag. D.P.W., 1931-33; D.P.W., Jan., 1934.

STEVENSON, DUNCAN.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Schl., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1918; lieut., 1919; France, 1918-19; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conserv., Apr.-Oct., 1927; senr. asst. conserv., forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Apr., 1934.

STEVENSON, MAJOR HUBERT CRADDOCK, M.C., O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1888; ed. Glengorse Meads, Eastbourne and Harrow; war serv., 1915-19 (commanded batty., R.F.A.); admvte. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1920; asst. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; ag. prin. asst. sec., do., 1927; asst. sec., do., 1927; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; ag. res., 1933-34; admvte. serv., cls. I, 1934.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924; conservator, Jan., 1930.
STEVENTON, L. E.—B. 1897; ed. County Schl., Wolverton and Elstow Schl., Bedford; on mily. serv. in E. Africa, Egypt, Salonika and Asia Minor; asst. divnl. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928.
STEWARD, PHILIP FRANCIS, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1925; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1926; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1933; ag. puisne judge, Mar., 1934.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922.
STEWART, CHARLES DUNCAN, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; supt., meteorological br., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1929.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apl., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and govt.'s commr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-16; ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to admnstr., June, 1912; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examnrs., Fijian language, Dec., 1913; asst. native commr. and govt.'s commr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; asst. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; lieut., Fiji defence force, July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov't., June, 1918; asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; prin. asst. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; agt. gen. imigrn. and editor "Rajdut" in 1922 and 1923; ag. col. sec., etc., Sept., 1922 and Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov't. dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. commr., W. Pacifico H.C. and ag. asst. high commr. and gov't. dep., Sept.-Oct., 1926; gov't. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns. and exec. comtee. also ch., hd. of dirs., sugar industry agrl. bank; ag. gov't., Apr.-July, 1927, Feb., 1929 and June-Sept., 1930; rep. col. at lst standing W. Indian Conf., 1929 and at Inter-col. Trade conferee, Trinidad, June, 1931; admvte., St. Christopher and Nevis, July, 1931; admvte. govt., Leeward Is., 1936.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory medals; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1923; prin. vety. offr., 1930.

STEWART, CAPT. MALOOLM JOHN.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Lovat Scouts Yeomanry, 1914; Indian Army, 1916-22; capt., 1919; incl. Schl. of Instr., Zeitoun, Cairo, 1919-21; coy. offr., coy. commdr., 53rd Sikhs F.F.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; ag. senr. asst. treas., 1930, 1931, 1932, 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, Sept., 1933-Mar., 1934.
STEWART, NEIL, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; supt., 1927.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmth.; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROBERT STROTHER, M.A., B.C.L., B.Lit., Durham Univ., Gladstone prizeman.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynemouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr., commdg., Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1916-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser, C.O., July to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, Jan., 1930; mem., privy and nominated couns. and coun. of health; examr. in English lit. and hist., Univ. of Malta; chmn., comtee. to enquire into motor transport, 1930; dep. gov't., July-Aug., 1932; chmn., traffic control bd., 1930-32; chmn., trade union council claims bd., 1932; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933.

STEWART, WILLIAM.—B. 1881; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; asntd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1899; commr., crown lands and ch. survr., Christchurch, 1926; valuer-gen., 1934.

STEWART, WILLIAM GORDON, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B. 1893; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; do., Johore, Sept., 1929; exec. engrnr., Nov., 1929; do., F.M.S., May, 1930; do., S.S., Aug., 1934.

STEYN, DANIEL, B.A. (Cape Town), LL.B. (Univ. of S. Africa).—B. 1893; senr. mast., Boshoff Schl. and Gymnasium, Paarl, 1917; judge's reger., O.F.S., 1920; pyt. sec. to prime min., 1924; Union consul-gen., Lourenco Marques, Sept., 1929; accredited rep., Union of S. Africa to L. of N., Mar., 1934.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912; prin. med. offr., Bech. Prot., 1935.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; Holmes exhibnr., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (lst cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science. Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1906; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to W.I. agric. conferee, 1907 and 1908; ast. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. comm., 1909; mem., banana comm., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comte., 1911; commr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regis., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irrigation comte., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regis., co-op. socie- ties and vice-pres., agric. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comte., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to imp. entomological confce., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to imp. botanical confce., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management, tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dists., N. India with Ceylon delegation, 1925; ast. agric. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several repts., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON.—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. commr., Aug., 1922; senr. dist. commr., Jan., 1931; prov. commr., 1932.

STOKES, GEOFFREY.—B. 1880; ed. Bedford Grammar Schl.; probationer, N. Rhodesia, May, 1909; ast. native commr. and J.P., Oct., 1910; native commr., Dec., 1912; ag. ast. mag., Feb., 1917; ast. mag., Feb., 1924; ag. dist. commr., Apr., 1924; prov. commr., Dec., 1933.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (or 1925), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C. (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to Sir U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, despa., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (despa., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. mem., Air Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transport, 1922-24; 1st commr. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 1925-31.

STOOKEY, GEORGE BERESFORD, F.R.G.S., F.R.Econ.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1897; ed. Bath; paymr. cadet, R.N., Jan., 1914; ast. naval sec., Malta Dockyard, 1915; ast. sec., rear admn. Sir D. R. L. Nicholson, 1917; do. to Adml. Sir L. Bayley, 1918; transf., emergency list of offrs., R.N., 1919; cadet, Sarawak oiv. serv., 1920; ast. sec., 1924; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. clk. of leg. coun., 1931; clk., exec. coun. in addn., 1933; ast. treas., Mauritius, 1933; author of various papers on anthropological subjects.

STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN.—B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; ast., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; surv.'s staff, N.distr., Dec., 1901; engr. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmtr., Feb., 1904; postmtr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmtr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; surv.r., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. surv.r., 1916; surv. surv.r., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G., various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 8th Oct., 1920; mem. legis. coun., July, 1926; P.M.G., N. Rhodesia, 1931; M.L.C., 1932-33.

STORRS, ST. RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.T. BACH (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Ist cls. clas. tripes, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mins (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; ast. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (despa.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comte., Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc'y, Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local gov't. comm., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc'y.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, Sept., 1926; do., Northern Rhodesia, Nov., 1932; ret., 1934; rep. Cyprus at 17th cong. of orientalists. Oxford, 1928; rep. Cyprus at C.O. conf., 1930; joint edr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

STOURTON, I. H. E. J.—B. 1901; ed. Stonehurst Coll., Lancs.; prob. inspr., pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1921; A.D.C. and pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923; inspr., Jan., 1924; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July-Dec., 1932; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1933.

STOUTE, Cyril Eustace.—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and oh. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., for various periods, 1926-32; man., govt. savings bank, 1932.

STOYLE, Herbert Blin.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Battersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. commr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rlys., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923.
STRACHAN, Peter Donald, C.B.E. (1934), M.D.—B. 1872; med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921-23; supt., Basutoland leper asylum, 1923.

STRACHEY, Sir Charles, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtce., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 26th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., lat Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtce., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtcees, for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; asst. sec., lat Apr., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.

STRADBROKE, (3rd Earl of) (cr. 1821), George Edward John Mowbray Rous, Baron Rous (cr. 1796), Viscount Dunwich (cr. 1821), K.C.M.G. (1920), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.

STRAHAN, Frank, C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B.—Sec., prime min.'s dept., C'wealth of Australia; sec., Australian delegn. to the Imp. conf., 1923; govt. dir., Amalgamated Wireless (Asia) Ltd.; sec., Br. Australian and N. Z. Antarctic research expdn.

STRAHAN, Kenneth Cyprian.—B. 1880; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and King's Coll., Cambridge; Ceylon govt. rly., 1908-15; R.A.S.C.M.T., 1915; capt., G.H.Q., Inspection Branch, 1917; ment. in desps.; ch. mech. engrg., Tanganyika rlys., 1920; ch. mech. engrg., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbrs., 1930.

STRATFORD, James.—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony; and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon.; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, England, 1895; Transvaal, 1902; practised, Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. division, 1st March, 1921; judge of appeal, 1927.

STREAK, M. J.—B. 1879; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; C.S., L.L.B.; ast. mag., Jacobsdal, 1900; Hoopstad, 1903; Heilbron, 1904; Senekal, 1908; Harrismith, 1913; mag., Bothaville, 1916; addnl. mag., Bloemfontein, 1919; mag. and mining commr., Namaqualand, 1928; addnl. mag., Pretoria, 1929; mag., Ermelo, 1931; 1st civ. mag., Johannesburg, 1932.
STREET, Sir Philip Whistler, K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ch. just., 1925; lieut-gov., 1930.

STRICKLAND, 1st Baron (cr. 1928), Sir Gerald Strickland, Count della Catena, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and L.L.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law trip; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera comm., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comtce. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtce. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrs.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrs., N.S. Wales; held dormant comm. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1927.
STRINGER, Hon. Sir Thomas Walter, K.T. Bach (1928).—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONACH, John Clark, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Dublin, Dublin Univ. and Eng. Sch. T.C.D.; ast. engr., Indian pub. wks., 1911; exec. engr., 1919; New Delhi constr., 1913-26; seconded to Kenya as supdg.-engnr. (bdgs.), 1926-29; transf. Kenya, 1930; ag. dir., P.W., 1930 and 1934.

STRONG, Arthur Nesbitt.—B. 1890; M.A., Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addn., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Ratnapura, in addn., Dec., 1914; addtl. commr. of requests, Ratnapura, in addn., May, 1915; on mily. duty, 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1919; landing surr., Colombo cust., Nov., 1920; dep. food contr. in addn., July, 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliva, Feb., 1924; ast. govt. agt., Matara, Apr., 1925; temp. attd., C.O., Nov., 1927 to Apr., 1928; dep. collr., cust., May, 1928; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port comm., Mar., 1934.
STRONG, Ernest Edward.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; inspr., 1929.

STRONGE, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.T. BACH. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—B. 1875; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., July to Aug., 1914, Sept. to Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; ree. thanks of Tongan govt. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comsn., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; ch. just., Cyprus, 1931.
STROTHER STEWART, see STEWART, ROBERT STROTHER.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mily. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19, deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hendy Rise, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD CARLETON.—B. 1881; ent. R.N., 1895; lieut. (navigating offr.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron navigating offr., First Battle Squadron, 1920-23; asst., King's harbour, Portsmouth, 1923-25; in command, H.M.S. "Agamemnon," 1925-27; ret. with rank of capt., 9th Mar., 1927; master attendant, Colombo, 12th Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cls. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Sttlmts., F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comttee., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; offr. administering the govt., Jan. to Oct., 1913; Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed govt., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. LL.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt.-gen. and gov. in ch., Jamaica, 1926; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Cyprus, Dec., 1932, do., Ceylon, 1933; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.

STUBINGTON, ROBERT ALAN, P.A.S.I.—B. 1888; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; surv. prob., F.M.S., 1912; asst. supt., surveys, 1919; ag. supt., rev. surveys, Pahang, 1928; Registrar, 1929; sup., revenue surveys, Pahang, 1930; do., Selangor, Dec., 1933; ag. senr. do., Perak, Dec., 1934.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT, C.M.G. (1932).—A.I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas., since 1923.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, K.T. BACH. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr., Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-86; sup't and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observv., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc. Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comttee., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20; ret. 1929.
STUPPEL, RUDOLPH, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1904; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1927; dist. comnr., 1928; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Mar., 1933.
STURDY, DONALD, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; agrl. course, 1923-26; B.A., 1926; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926.

STURGES, ROBERT SIMON MANSFIELD, O.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. Radley and Christ Ch.; asst. pol. offr., 'Iraq civ. admin., Apr., 1920; external oriental sec. to high commr., 'Iraq, Feb., 1921; asst. sec., Apr., 1921; polit. sec., Oct., 1924.

STURROCK, GEORGE, M.Inst.C.E., F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1880; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edin.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1907; ex. engrnr., grade III, Jan., 1913; ex. engrnr., Jan., 1914; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; state engrnr., Kedah, Oct., 1926; dep. col. engrnr., S'pore, June, 1927; ag. col. engrnr., S.S., Dec., 1927; M.L.C. and M.E.C., S.S., Oct., 1929; col. engrnr., S.S., Jan., 1930; pres., bd. of architects, Jan., 1930; D.P.W., S.S., advr., P.W., Malay States, Jan., 1932.

STURROCK, SIR JOHN CHRISTIAN RAMSAY, Kt. Bach. (1934), C.M.G. (1927), M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; tutor to H.H. the Kabaka of Buganda, Mar., 1905; dist. comnr., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1914; prov. comnr., June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., June, 1924; res. comnr., Basutoland, 1926; ret., 1935.
STUTTAFOORD, RICHARD.—B. 1870; M.P. for Newlands, Union of S. Africa from 1924; min. without portfolio, 1933.

SUARES, ALFRED RADIMOND.—B. 1874; 3rd cls. clk., regir. gen.'s dept., Jamaica, 1891; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; supervisor, census, 1911; asst. regir. gen. and asst. dep. keeper, records, 1913; supervisor, census, 1921; regir. gen. and dep. keeper of records, 1922.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office ast. to gov't. ast., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; office ast. to gov't. ast., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927; ast. gov't. ast., Kandy, Nov., 1928; on sp. duties in connexion with new constitution, Jan., 1930; adustive. sec., income tax dept., June, 1932; clk., bd. of review in addn.; admstive. sec., amalgamated depts. of income tax, estate duty and stamps, and clk., bd. of review, Oct., 1933.

SUKUNA, JOSEVA LALABALAVU VANAALALI, B.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—ed. Wanganui, N.Z. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; held various appts., col. secretariat and native offices, Fiji 1907-12; served in France with the Foreign Legion and awarded the Mily. Med., 1914-16; ag. asst. sec. for native affrs. and aide-de-camp, 1916; cadet, Fiji, 1917; served in France with Fiji Labour Corps, 1917-18; called to bar, 1921; passed cadet, 1921; native lands comnr., 1922; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct., 1922; ast. comnr. for Fiji, Br. Empire Exhib., 1924; ag. chmn. native lands comnr., 1927; dist. comnr., Lau, 1932; nom. native mem., leg. coun., 1932.
SULLIVAN, JOHN.—Engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1910; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; engrnr. adviser to French at Duala, 1916; supt. engrnr., marine dept., 1927.

SULLIVAN, JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C., B.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; captn., R. of O. barrister SOLR. AND ATTY., FORMER VICE-PRES., MONTREAL BAR; MEM., GEN. COUN., QUEBEC BAR; MEM., BD. OF EXAMS., DO.; EL. TO H. OF C., CANADA, JULY, 1930; DEP. P.M.G., AUG., 1935.
SULLIVAN-TAILYOUR, EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1886; SUPT., POL., KENYA, JUNE, 1923.

SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—B. 1870; ENTD. CROWN LANDS OFF., S. AUST., JULY, 1887; CLK., AGRI. OFF., MAY, 1892; CHIEF CLK., JULY, 1902; AG. SEC. TO MIN. OF AGRI., FEB., 1910; SEC. TO DIDTO, JULY, 1911; ALSO SEC. TO MIN. OF INDUSTRY AND SEC. TO MIN. OF MARINE, 1915.
SUMNER, JAMES A. W.—B. 1903; ENTD. L.P.S. (INLAND REV.), MAY, 1919; APPTD. AFTER COMPET. EXAM. CLER. OFFR., LONDON TELEPHONE SERV., APR., 1921; TRANSFT., C.O., OCT., 1923.

SURRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ED. FELSTED COLL. AND DOWNING COLL., CAMBRIDGE; B.A.; SERVED, N. LANCE REGT., 1914-17; ASST. SEC. CH. SECS. OFFICE, CYPRUS, MAR., 1918; PASSED EXAM. IN MOD. GREEK, NOV., 1918; AG. CH. ASST. SEC., JAN., TO APR., 1919; LOCAL COMDT. POLICE, APR., 1919; AG. COMSNR., LARNACA, JUNE, 1919; AG. A.D.C., JULY, 1920; AG. ASST. SEC., OCT., 1920; PASSED EXAM. IN TURKISH, AUG., 1922; AG. COMSNR., AUG., 1922; AG. ASST. SEC., SEPT., 1922; AG. COMSNR., DEC., 1922 TO OCT., 1923; COMSNR., JAN., 1924; REGISTR., CO-OP. SOCS., 1934.

SURRIDGE, ERNEST REX EDWARD.—B. 1899; ED. FELSTED AND ST. JOHN'S COLL., OXFORD; MOD. HIST. (HONS.), B.A., 1922; LIEUT., 7TH BATT., THE DUKE OF CORNWALL'S LIGHT INFY., 1917-20; FRANCE AND GERMANY; ASST. DIST. OFFR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, 1924; SECONDED TO SECRETARIAT, 1925-28; AIDE-DE-CAMP AND PVTE. SEC. TO AG. GOV., 1928; SECONDED TO C.O. AS ASST. PRIN., SEPT., 1930-FEB., 1933; SECONDED TO SECRETARIAT, 1933; AG. DEP. CH. SEC., 1935; ASST. CH. SEC., 1936.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ED. STE. MARIE COLL., MCGILL AND LAVAL UNIVS.; CALLED TO BAR, 1896; VICE-PRES., JUN. BAR ASSOC., 1902-03; PRES., 1903-04; MEM. OF COUN. OF QUEBEC BAR, 1904-05; LECTR., LAW, MCGILL UNIV., SINCE 1905; JUDGE, SUPERIOR CT., QUEBEC, 1ST JAN., 1920; ED. OF PRACTICE REPTS., QUEB., SINCE 1896; ALSO OF POCKET EDNS. OF CIV.
