CODE AND CODE OF CIV. PROCEDURE (THE LATTER IN BOTH LANGUAGES); PRES., CAN. CLUB, MONTREAL, 1909-10; VICE-PRES., ALLIANCE FRANCAISE, MONTREAL.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.SC. (DURHAM).—AST. DIST. COMSNR., KENYA, MAY, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, HON. DONALD MATHEWS, P.C. (CAN.), M.B., D.S.O.—B. 1879; 1ST EL. TO REP. S. OXFORD IN ONT. LEGIS., G.E., MAY, 1902; RE-EL. G.E., JAN., 1905; DIR., COLONIZATION AND IMMIGR., ONTARIO, MAR., 1909; EL. AT G.E., DEC., 1921; RE-ELEC., G.E., 1925 AND 1930; MIN. OF NATIONAL DEFENCE IN BENNETT CABINET, AUG., 1930; RE-ELEC. BY ACCL. AFTER ASSUMING OFFICE, AUG. 1930.
SUTTON, G., A.M.C.T.—B. 1902; MAST., ACCRA TECH. SCHL., GOLD COAST, JUNE, 1927.

SUTTON, GEORGE GERHARD, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1880; ED. DALE COLL., KING WILLIAM'S TOWN, UNION OF S. AFRICA, MANCHESTER GRAMMAR SCHL. AND EMMANUEL COLL., CAMBRIDGE; CALLED TO BAR, MIDDLE TEMPLE, JUNE, 1903; ADMITTED TO BAR, SUP. CT., CAPE TOWN, OCT., 1903; K.C. FOR UNION OF S. AFRICA, SEPT., 1921; AG. JUDGE, CAPE PROV. DIVN., ON VARIOUS OCCASIONS, 1922-28; JUDGE, CAPE PROV. DIVN., FEB., 1929.
SWABEY, CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1906; ED. WINCHESTER COLL. AND EDINBURGH UNIV.; YOUNGER SILVER MED. FOR FORESTRY, 1927; B.SC. (FORESTRY), 1928; AST. CONSERVATOR, FORESTS, TRINIDAD, 1928.

SWAINE, CAPT. DAVID RICHARD, M.A. (WALES), M.C., B. 1889; AST. MAST., PENANG FREE SCHL., SEPT., 1913; ON MILY. SERV., 1915-19; TWICE MEN. IN DESPA., M.C., AST. MAST., PENANG FREE SCHL., FEB., 1920; AR. NORMAL INSTR., PENANG, IN ADDN., MAR., 1920; HEADMAST., NORTHAM RD. SCH., DEC., 1924; EUROPEAN MAST., CLS. IB; AST. HEADMAST., FREE SCHL., PENANG, APR., 1928; PRIN., KING EDWARD VII SCHL., TAIPING, SEPT., 1929; AST. INSPR., SCHLS., PERAK, JUNE, 1931; AST. SENR. INSPR., ENG. SCHLS., S.S. & F.M.S., SEPT., 1934.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B. (MIL.) (1904); GRAND OFFR. OF ORDER OF THE CROWN OF ITALY (1905).—B. 1863; ED. AT NAVAL COLL., GOSPORT, AND ABROAD; R.M.C., SANDHURST, 1882; ENTD. WELSH REGT., 1883; BENGAL STAFF CORPS, 1884; ADJUT., 1888; CAPT., 1894; LOCAL LIEUT.-COL., 1900; BREVET LIEUT.-COL., 1901; BREVET-COL., 1903; TEMP. BRIG.-GEN., 1903-06; RETIRED FROM ARMY, 1906; SERVED WITH BURMA EXPEDN., 1886-86 (MEDAL WITH CLASP); INTLL. OFFR., HUKONG VALLEY RECONNAISSANCE; IN COMMAND OF ADVANCED BASE EXPLORATION, TURONG RIVER AND SING PHO COUNTRY, NOV., 1895 TO MARCH, 1896 (McGREGOR MEDAL); EXPLORATION TO SOMALAND, FEB. TO JULY, 1891 (THANKED BY RESOLUTION OF BOMBAY GOVT., AND APPRECIATION BY GOVT. OF INDIA); GILDESSA AFFAIR ON ABYSSINIAN FRONTIER, 1892 (COMMENDATION BY RESOLUTION OF BOMBAY GOVT.); ENGAGED ON DELIMITATION OF INTERNAT. FRONTIER BETWEEN FRENCH AND BRITISH SOMALAND, 1892; SERVED FOR FIVE YEARS ON INTLL. STAFF, INDIA, AS STAFF-CAPT., AND D.A.Q.M.G.; APPTD. 2ND-IN-COMMAND, UGANDA RIFLES, ON ACTIVE SERV. IN JUBALAND, E. AFRICA (MEDAL WITH CLASP); ON ACTIVE SERV. IN UGANDA, 1899; SELECTED TO COMMAND SOMALAND POLICE FORCE, 1900; RAISED AND ORGANIZED LEVIES, AND COMMANDED EXPEDITIONARY FORCE IN SOMALAND, NOV., 1900; DEFEATED AND ROUTED MULLAH (MENT. IN DESPA., MEDAL WITH CLASP, BREVET LT.-COL., THANKED BY GOVT.); ORGANIZED 6TH BATT. UGANDA RIFLES, SECOND EXPEDN., 1901; AGAIN DEFEATED MULLAH (C.B., MENT. IN DESPA.); APPTD. COMSNR., CONSUL-GEN., AND COMDR.-IN-CHIEF, SOMALAND PROT., AND JUDGE OF ITS HIGH COURT, APRIL, 1902; ON SPECIAL DUTY IN EGYPT, 1904; ARRANGED EVACUATION OF SOMALAND, AND ORGANIZED CONTROL OF TRIBES; BRITISH DELEG. TO CONFERENCE WITH ITALIAN DELEG. AT LYONS IN CONNECTION WITH AGREEMENT WITH ITALY RESPECTING SOMALAND; GOVT. AND COMDR.-IN-CHIEF, BRITISH HONDURAS, 1906-13; SPEC. DUTY TO CANADA, WITH REFERENCE TO INDIAN LABOUR (THANKED BY GOVT. OF INDIA) 1908; SPEC. DUTY TO MEXICO, 1909.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E.B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ED. SOMERSETSHIRE COLL., BATH, AND AT BALLIOL COLL., OXON.; EXHIBITIONER, WORCESTERSHIRE COLL., OXON., JUNE, 1876; 2ND CLASS CLASSICAL MODS., JUNE, 1878; 3RD CLASS IN MOD. HIST. FINALS, JUNE, 1880; B.A., JULY, 1880; M.A., 1881; CLASSICAL PROF. AT THE ROY. COLL., MAURITIUS, JUNE, 1881; PRECIS WRITER, COL. SEC.'S OFFICE, FEB., AND 2ND AST. COL. SEC., APR., 1886; AG. COL. SEC., 1889; AG. COL. SEC., BR. HONDURAS, MAR., 1893; ADMST. THE GOVT. OF THAT COL., APR. TO NOV., 1893; COL. SEC., BR. HONDURAS, 1894; ADMST. GOVT., APR. TO NOV., 1895, AGAIN IN 1897; AG. 1ST CLASS CLK., C.O., JAN., 1898; ADMST. OF THE SEYCHELLES IS., AUG., 1899; ASSUMED GOVT., NOV., 1899; GOVT. AND COMDR.-IN-CHIEF, OCT., 1903; GOVT., BR. HONDURAS, APR., 1904; GOVT., LEEWARD IS., JAN., 1906; RECEIVED CORONATION MEDAL, 1911; GOVT., FIJI, AND HIGH COMSNR., W. PACIFIC, MARCH, 1912; ASSUMED GOVT., JULY, 1912; RET., 1918; REP. OF FIJI AT BR. EMPIRE EXHIBN., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, Sir Frank Athelstan G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Stltmts., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and comnr., ct. of requesta, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. comnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; ment. in desps.; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; comnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. comnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, Jan., 1896; admstd. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comnr. on Mauritius, 1909.

SWINBOURNE, Major Charles Augustus, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt.rlys., May, 1900; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; clk., pay and qtrmr., with rank of sub-inspr., Fiji constaby., Oct., 1909; capt.; Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; comdg. offr., Fiji cadet corps, Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., Apr., 1911; attd. 1st Loyal North Lance. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fiji 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, Sept., 1915; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., Oct., 1916; ment. by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active serv., July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lance. Regt., Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., Jan., 1918; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Feb., 1920; dep. comnr., W. Pacific, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Ellice Is., Aug., 1922; 1st dist. offr., Jan., 1928; seni. admstd. offr., July, 1931; ag. res. comnr. in 1928 and 1932.

SYDNEY, Lord Archbishop of, Most Rev. John Charles Wright, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1885-1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1889-1893; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1895; St. George's, Leeds, 1895-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to Bp. of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop of Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYERS, Cecil George Lewis.—B. 1903; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Oxford; classical schol., Balliol Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls. classical moda., 1923; 1st cls., lit. hum., 1925; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 21st Oct., 1925; pte. sec. to perm. under-secr., for Doms. affrs., 14th Nov., 1928; asst. pte. sec. to S. of S. for Dom. Affrs. (Mr. J. H. Thomas), 17th June, 1930; ditto, to Mr. J. H. Thomas as S. of S. for Dom. affrs. and for theCols., 26th Aug., 1931; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; prin., 1st Jan., 1933; accompanied U.K. air mission to Canada and U.S.A., Nov.-Dec., 1935.

SYKES, George Rognvald M.A.—(Liv.)—B. 1889; cadet, S. Stltmts., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attd. censor's office S'pore; passed cadet, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Apr., 1917; asst. regis. impts. and expts. (war trade dept.) S'pore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., S'pore. in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore Jan., 1920; supermny. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. supr., govt. monopolies and asst. prot. of Chinese, Malacca, June, 1921; asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1925; prot. Chinese, Kedah, Dec., 1926; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, June, 1929 and Sept., 1933; offr., cls. II., prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1930; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Sept., 1933.
SYMES, James Kerr Godfrey.—B. 1903; ed. Durham Schol.; cadet and asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 1928.

SYMES, Lieut.-Col. Sir George Stewart, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps); Aden, 1903-04 (desps., D.S.O.); attd. Egyptian Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expdn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt. col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov.-gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times ment. in desps., meds., brevet lt.-col.); attd. staff of high comnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd. miny. of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov., Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admstd. govt., Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept., 1927; accredited rep. to mandates comnr., 1926 and 1928; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1933.
SYMONETTE, W. J.—B. 1878; pub. schl. teacher, Apr., 1898; 4th divn. comnr., June, 1921; ag. ch. clk.; recvr. gen. in treas. dept., Nov., 1922; confirmed in appt. Feb., 1924.

SYMONS, Claude Trevine.—B. 1879; B.A. Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. gov't. analyst, June, 1912; ag. gov't. analyst, in addn. Dec., 1916; govt. analyst, June, 1919; ag. regis., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TADMAN, John Ladbrooke, B.A. (Oxon.).—Ed. Norwich Schol. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford (Goldsmiths' Exhibn., 1929); col. aud. dept., Feb., 1931; asst. audr., Uganda, Apr., 1931; asst. audr., Mauritius, Feb., 1935.

TAIT, Hugh Nimmo.—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripos, Parts I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripos, Part I.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911; 2nd cls., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian Comnr. 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. sec., 24th Jan., 1930.

TATT, Horace Vibart.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd clsr. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offr., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd asst. inspr. of schools in 1914, 1915 and 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. offr., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. class. mast., Queen's Coll., Apr.-July, 1917. ag. asst. mast., do., Mar., 1930; asst. mast., 1934.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR PERCY, M.C., 1914 (Mons) Star.—B. 1892; ed. Colchester Royal Grammar Sch.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1926; served with R.N.V.R., R.N.D., R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1914-19; severely wounded, 1917; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; asst. commit. Northern Territories constab., Sept., 1920; transfd. to Gold Coast pol. as ast. commnr., pol., Nov., 1923; commnr., pol. and in charge, C.I.D., 1926; seconded to Ashanti judiciary as ag. pol. mag., May, 1927; pol. mag., sup. ct., Gold Coast, June, 1928; crown coun., Sept., 1932; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; elk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treas., ast., Uganda, Jan., 1909; ast. treas., civil admin., German East Africa (occupied territory), Jan., 1917; treas., Seychelles, May, 1919; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1923; ag. treas., May 1923 to 1924; senr. ast. treas., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1925; ag. treas., in 1925, 1926, 1928 and 1929; title altered to dep. treas., Jan., 1930; mem. Freetown temp. munic. bd., Dec., 1926; pres. city coun., Freetown in addition, May, 1928; dep. treas., Hong Kong, June, 1930; treas., June, 1931.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918, France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; attch. dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V, May, 1924; ast. dist. offr., Rembau, Dec., 1925; offr., cls. IV, May, 1928; ast. dist. offr., L. Perak, Dec., 1928; ag. ast. offr. assignee and regist. companies, S'pore, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1933; ag. D.P.P., Penang, Mar., 1934; offr. assignee and regist. companies in addn., May 1934.

TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practised in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc., 1917; just., ct. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.
TAYLOR, GREVILLE POWER.—B. 1899; ed. Elstow Schl., Bedford, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; ast. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, March, 1925; dist. commnr., Mar., 1931.
TAYLOR, H. A., F.I.C.—B. 1888; ast. analyst, Hong Kong, 1913; ast. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1914; ag. supt. in addn., in 1930.
TAYLOR, PERCY JOHN.—B. 1881; ast. govt. marine surv., Hong Kong, 1921; ag. govt. marine surv., Mar., 1934-Jan., 1935.

TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, O.B.E. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; exhib., Charing Cross Hosp., London; D.M.O., Cue, West Australia; M.O.H., Cue and M.O.H., Day Dawn, West Australia; M.O., Uganda, Sept., 1914; active serv., E.A. campaign and temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1916; S.M.O., Somaliland, May, 1922; P.M.O., Somaliland, July, 1925.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schl. and King's Coll., London; elk. in crown ag'ts. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); ast. treas., Uganda, 1910; ast. treas., Somaliland, Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1920; ag. treas., Dec., 1921 to May, 1922; treas., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1922; ag. ch. sec., Apr. to June, 1927; gov't. dep. 10th to 27th May, 1927; govt. dir., Nyanza Salt Mines, Ltd., Meat Rations Ltd. and Dar-es-Salaam and Dist. Elec. Supply Co., Ltd.; chmn., estate duty commrs., trades licensing commrs., house tax commrs., economic advisory bd. and road control bd., mem.rly.advisory coun., currency offr., stamp duty commrs., mem., exec. and leg. couns.
TAYLOR, ROYDEN ERNSCLIFFE.—B. 1889; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, Oct., 1905; seconded for war serv., Sept., 1915 to May, 1919; J.P., 1919; regiar., sup. ct., Grenada, May, 1931.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, C.B.E. (1933), D.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B.1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; ast. govt. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909, govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; offi. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1908 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised govt. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agril. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agr. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agril. and indus. exhibtn., 1926; chmn., various comtees; dir., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1929; pres., bd., R.R.I., Malaya, Apr., 1929; visited Java as del., 4th Pacific Sci. Cong., June, 1929; prin., schl. of agr., Malaya, in addn., May, 1930; chmn., comtees, rice cult., Malaya, July, 1930; del., confce. of col. dirs., of agr., Lond., July-Dec., 1931; author of numerous papers and rept. on agril. chem., cane sugar production, agril. educn. and trop. agr.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RYAN.—B. 1898; on mili. serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Matale, Dec., 1927; ast. govt. agt. Badulla, Jan., 1929; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1932.

TENNANT, MATTHEW PETTIGREW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Montrose Academy; ch. ast. engrn., Jamaica govt. rly., 1905; ag. loco. supt., ditto, July, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ag. ch. engrn., Mar.-Nov., 1913; senr. inspgr. engrn., P.W.D., Jamaica, Apr., 1914; ag. ast. dir., P.W.D., Dec., 1918 to Sept., 1921; ag. dir. of pub. wks., July-Oct., 1920; exec. engrn., Uganda, July, 1924.

TERRELL, ARTHUR KOBERWEIN A BECKETT, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1906; war serv., 1914-19; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Jan., 1931; judge, ct. of appl., Kedah, May, 1931; ag. ch. just., F.M.S., Feb., 1933; puisne judge, S'pore, Aug., 1934.
TESTER, L. M.C.—B. 1891; war serv., 1915-18; treas. asst., Nigeria, 1919; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1930; Mauritius, July, 1932; ag. col. sec., 1934; treas. and compr., cust., Zanzibar, Nov., 1934.

TETTEY, VICTOR ADOLPHUS, B.A. (Durham).—B. 1891; ed. govt. primary schl., Accra Grammar Schl., Fours Bay Coll., Sierra Leone (Durham Univ.); 2nd cls. teacher, Accra Training Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; 1st cls. teacher, Apr., 1921; asst. headmast., Apr., 1923; inspr., schle., July, 1925.

TE WATER, CHARLES THORODE.—B. 1887; ed. Normal Coll., Bedford Grammar Schl., and Cambridge, B.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, and mem. of Pretoria bar; repd. Pretoria (Cent.) in Union parl., 1924-29; high commr. for the Union of S. Africa in London, 1929.

THACKER, RANSLEY SAMUEL.—B. 1891; ed. Choir Schl. of H.M. Chapels Royal, St. James', Dulwich Coll. and Univ. of London; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; ch. just. and pol. mag., St. Vincent, Nov., 1930; rep. St. Vincent at conf. of W. Indian sea island cotton growers, Barbados, Mar., to Apr., 1932; temp. attd. legal advisory staff, C.O., Sept.-Oct., 1933; atty-gen., Fiji, Nov., 1933; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., Sept., 1935.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. schl.; examr. of loan accts., audit dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1904; examr. of accts., ditto, July, 1904; prov. aud., O.F.S. Prov., Apr., 1912.

THEILER, SIR ARNOLD, K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; state dipl. as vety. surg., Switzerland, 1889; dr., med. vet., Berne, 1901; hon. assoc., R. Coll. Vety. Surgeons, 1908; C.M.G., 1907; K.C.M.G., 1914; D.Sc., Cape, 1911; D.Sc., Syracuse, 1923; D.Ph., Berne, 1923; D.V.Sc., S. Africa, 1925; came to S. Africa, 1892; dir., lymph farm, Johannesburg, 1893; vety. surg. to sany. bd. and mine sanitation dept., Johannesburg, 1894-95; sp. mission to Rhodesia on outbreak of rinderpest, 1896; govt. vety. surg. to Transvaal Republic, 1896-1900; vety. surg. to Transvaal State Arty., 1898; dir., bacteriological lab. and vaccine inst., S.A. Repub., 1898; govt. vety. bacteriologist, 1900; dir., vety. research, Union of S. Africa, 1910; dir., Lamsiekte research, 1919; dir., vety. educn. and research, 1920; dean of faculty of vety. sci. and prof. of infectious diseases, Union of S. Africa, 1920; sp. mission to Europe to represent Transvaal and Natal govts. at Baden Baden, 1899; sp. mission to Rhodesia in connection with Rhodesian tick fever, 1902; repd. Transvaal govt. at 8th International Vety. Congress, Budapest, 1905, at 9th International Vety. Congress, The Hague, 1909, and at Pan African Vety. Congresses, Bloemfontein, 1902, Cape Town, 1905, Pretoria, 1909, and at World's Dairy Confce, Syracuse, 1923; ret.
THEIS, S. E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.

THERON, Lt.-Col., Francois Henri.—B. 1891; ed. St. George's Cathedral Grammar Schl., Cape-town; ent. civ. serv., O.F.S., 1909; lieut., Enalin's Horse, 1915; asst. staff offr., 4th Mounted Brig., G.S.W.A. campaign, 1915; A.D.C. to Gen. Smuts and attd. Permt. Force (Staff), 1915; A.D.C. to Gen. Botha and granted temp. rank of capt., 1916; served, G.E. Africa, July to Sept., 1916; attd. war cabinet staff as ast. staff offr. to Gen. Smuts, 1917; attd., 2nd S.A. Infy., France, 1917; served in France, 1918; comy. commdr., Nov., 1918; ment. in desps. twice; 1914-15 Sta. Br. War and Victory med.; Peace confce., Paris as A.D.C. to Gen. Botha, Mar.-June, 1918; lieut. (temp. capt.), Permt. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; graduated Staff Coll., Camberley, 1921; staff offr., G.S. a.c., defence headqrs., Pretoria, 1922; posted to S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; A.D.C., O.A.G. of Union, 1923-24 and in 1928; ag. adjt., S.A. Milly Coll., Roberts' Heights in 1924; offr. instr., S.A. Milly Coll., Roberts' Heights in 1924; offr. instr., S.A. Milly Coll., Roberts' Heights in 1924; temp. maj., 1932; temp. lt. col., 1932; staff offr. (G.L.), gen. staff sec., defence headquarters, Pretoria, 1933; major, Apr., 1933; lt. col., Apr., 1933 with seniority from 1st Jan., 1933; dir., milly. opera. and training, Oct., 1933; O.C., Transvaal commd., 1934; O.C., Roberts Heights and Transvaal commd., 1934; temp. col., 1934.
THRUNISSEN, E. D.—Asst. supt. pol., Kenya, Nov., 1922.
THIBON, CLEMENT GABRIEL.—Foreman of wks., P.W.D., Antigua, Mar., 1916; ast. town clk., St. Kitts, Apr., 1916; ag. supt. of pub. wks., St. Kitts, 1916.

THOMAS, A. MUSGRAVE, Barrister-at-Law.—Crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Nov., 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-28; 2nd puisane judge, Trinidad, May, 1929, re-transf. to Cyprus as puisane judge, Dec., 1929; ag. ch. just. for various periods, 1930-34.

THOMAS, CHARLES CLIFFORD.—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's; 2nd lieut., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1909; India, 1914-15; Aden Field Force, Jan., 1915; Egyptian Expedy. Force, Jan., 1916-19; adjt., 1/4th D.C.L.I., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; Territorial Decoration; adjt., 4th D.C.L.I. (T.F.), 1919-24; pte. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1925.
THOMAS, FREDERICK WIKR, C.D.A. and C.D.F., Glasgow Univ., B. 1900; Allan Glens Schl., Glasgow; Dutch East Indies, 1923-29; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

THOMAS, HAROLD BEKEN, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll.; C.P.R., engnr. dept., 1907; Uganda Prot. survey dept., 1911; senr. staff survr., 1915; capt., E. African Prot. Forces, German East Africa, 1917-18; dist. survr., Uganda, 1921; dep. dir., surveys, 1924; ag. dir., surveys, land offr. and commr. of mines for various periods, 1924-35.

THOMAS, HUGH WHITELEGGE.—B. 1887; ed. Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham and Jesus Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1913; ag. dist. commr. at various times, 1913-17; dist. commr., June, 1919; ag. ast. coll. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. commr., cent. prov., May to Aug., 1924; dep. prov. commr., May, 1926; ag. commr., W. prov., Apr., 1927 to Mar., 1928; dep. sec., for native affrs., Apr., 1928; sec., native affrs., 1933.

THOMAS, Rt. Hon. JAMES HENRI, P.C. (1917), M.P., J.P., LL.D., Cambridge.—B. 1875; served as cleaner, fireman and driver, G.W.R.; has served in turn as organiser, ast. sec., gen. sec. and pres., Nat. Union of Rlymen; gov. of Dulwich College; mem., Balfour mission to America, 1917; M.P. for Derby since 1910; J.P., Surrey, 1920; S. of S. for S. cols., 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924; Lord, Privy Seal, 15th June, 1929; S. of S. for Dom. Affrs., 13th June, 1930; S. of S. for Dom. affrs. and for the Cols., 28th Aug., 1931; S. of S. for Dom. affrs., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for the Cols., 27th Nov., 1935; author of "When Labour Rules" and other works.

THOMAS, Leslie Arding.—B. 1891; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Sch.; pol. probt., S.S., Nov., 1910; asst. supt., pol., Sept., 1914; ch. pol. offr., P.W., Mar., 1921; dep. comrn., pol., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; asst. supt. pol., S.S., Oct., 1926; adjutant, S.S. pol., Nov., 1926; commdt., pol., depot, S.S., and ag. supt., pol., Jan., 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, July, 1930; do., Selangor, Feb., 1932; dep. comrn., pol., Perak, 1935.

THOMAS, Sir Samuel Joyce, Kt. Bach. (1935).—B. 1875; ed. King's Coll., London; Inglis schl. and early English Text Soey prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) King's Coll., 1897; B.A., Lond., 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1898; enlisted, 1915; commissioned, R.A.S.C. (M.T.), and attd. to R.G.A. (145th Siege Battery), France, Flanders, Italy; invalided, 1919; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1919; ag. col sec. and admstr., St. Vincent, Feb. to Dec., 1920, and on three subsequent occasions; thanked by S. of S. for index of laws St. Vincent, 1921; mem., W. Indian ct. of appl., 1921-23; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923; chmn., oil and water bd., 1923; ag. atty.-gen., 1923; ag. 1st puisne judge on various occasions, 1923-26 and in 1928; puisne judge, Kenya, 1929; ch. just., F.M.S., Oct., 1933.

THOMAS, Sir Thomas Shenton Whitelegg, K.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1929), O.B.E. (1919).—ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons. 1901; asst. dist. comrn., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd ass't sec., Apr., 1911; sen. asst. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. asst. ch. sec., Aug.-Oct., 1913, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917-Jan., 1919; clk. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917-19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. comrn., 1918; asst. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development comrn., 1919; gov.'s dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., in 1925 and 1926; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1925-26; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1927; gov., Nyasaland, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gold Coast, 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Straits Sttlms. and high comm., Malay States, 1934.
THOMAS, W. E., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1884; rly. engrnr., Sudan, Brazil, F.M.S., New South Wales, and India; dep. res. engrnr., Benue bridge constrn., Nigeria, Aug., 1930.
THOMPSON, Aubrey Denzil Forsyth.—B. 1897; ed. Weenen County Coll., Natal and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); lieut., R.F.A., 1917-19; cadet, Uganda Prot., June, 1921; A.D.C., July, 1923; dist. offr., 1933.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Asst. dist. comrn., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. comrn., 1933; senr. dist. comrn., 1934.
THOMPSON, Downing Charles, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1892; mil. serv., 1914-19; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1921-29; asst. sec., Palestine, 1929.

THOMPSON, Edwin Archibald.—B. 1898; cadet, govt. lab., Leeward Is., Dec., 1914; 2nd jun. asst., govt. lab., Apr., 1915; 1st do., Aug., 1917; senr. clk., cust., Antigua, Apr., 1927; transf., govt. lab., Leeward Is., 1928; milk analyst, Antigua, June, 1930.

THOMPSON, Henry Burford.—B. 1880; cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Apr., 1905; acctnt., ditto, Apr., 1906; ag. 1st treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug.-Nov., 1906; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Antigua Dec., 1906; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1907 to Mar., 1908; 3rd outdoor offr., treasy., Jan., 1908; student, Middle Temple, 1912; cashier, treasy., May, 1914; ag. 1st clk., regis.'s office, May, 1915; comrn. to admstr. oaths, Antigua, July, 1916.

THOMPSON, Herbert W.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

THOMPSON, John Harrison.—B. 1892; diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; appt. after compet. exam., asst. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library asst., C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; asst. librarian, 10th Oct., 1925; registr., col. laws and asst. librarian, 19th Nov., 1928.

THOMPSON, Capt. Keith Wentworth.—B. 1887; ed. Charterhouse; H.A.C.; Ceylon Mounted Rifles; Gold Coast Volunteers; attd. Gold Coast Regt., R.W.A.F.F., Aug., 1914; comrn., R.F.A., July, 1915; demob., 1919; service in Togoland and France; Gold Coast cust., 1913; asst. comrn., pol., July, 1919; comrn., pol., 1922.
THOMPSON, Walter Philip.—B. 1909; ed. St. Lawrence, Ramsgate; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; asst. supt., pol., Dec., 1931.

THOMPSTONE, Capt. Eric Westbury, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury Schl.; 2nd lieut., King's Shropshire Light Infy., 1915; served in France and Italy, 1916-19; wounded; silver med. for valour (Italy); adminis. offr., N.P., Nigeria, July, 1919.

THOMSON, James White, M.C. Ch.B., Glasgow, D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), certif., London Schl. Trop. Med.—B. 1882; ed. Allen Glen's Schl., Glasgow; M.O., W.A.M.S., N. Nigeria, 1912; served in Cameroons campaign, 1914; temp. capt., Army (gen. list), 1914-15; ag. astt. entomologist, London S.T.M., 1915; ag. dep. dir., L.S.T.M. May to Sept., 1915; S.M.O., Nigeria, Oct., 1926; A.D.M.S., Aug., 1929; D.D.M.S., Feb., 1934.

THOMSON, Reginald Francis, F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ch. inspr., native labourers for Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Dec., 1920; ag. temp. dist. offr., Shortland, and dep. commr. for W. Pacific in Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1925-27.
THOMSON, Robert.—B. 1892; ed. High Schl., Greenock and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; serv. with H.M. forces, 1914-19; astt. engrnr., Zanzibar, June, 1925; ag. dist. engrnr. on various occasions, 1927-31.

THOMSON, Robert Leslie.—B. 1880; ed. Perth Acad.; astt. commissary, Br. Guiana, 1907-20; served with H.M. Forces in France, 1914-18; astt. inspr., dists., 1925; 2nd gde. offr., dist. admstr., Jan., 1932.

THOMSON, Walter Morris, M.A.—B. 1905; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931; 3rd astt. col. sec., 1933; 1st clk., mag., Feb., 1934; J.P., 1934; senr. astt. col. treas., Feb., 1935; 1st clk. and mag., Mar., 1935; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, May., 1935.

THORBY, Hon. Harold Victor Campbell, M.P.—M.L.A., N.S.W. for Wammera, 1922-27; Castlereagh 1927-30; min. for agr., 1927-30; M.H.R. of C'wealth of Australia for Calare, N.S.W., since Dec., 1931; asst. min. in Lyons miny., 1934; visited Gr. Britain on occasion of Silver Jubilee.

THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 29th Oct., 1930.

THORNHILL, GEORGE KRAAL, F.S.I., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S., F.R.Met.S.—B. 1884; supernmy. survr., Ceylon, July, 1901; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1908; supt., surveys, 2nd grade, Sept., 1911; do., 1st grade, Oct., 1914; on mily. surv., 1918-19; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1927; dep. survr.-gen., Nov., 1930; survr.-gen., Sept., 1932.

THORNHILL, WILLIAM JOHNSON, M.I.C.E., A.R.T.C.—B. 1880; head overseer, P.W.D., Ceylon, Sept., 1899; inspr., Mar., 1900; dist. engr., 3rd grade, Jan., 1902; 2nd grade, Mar., 1903; 1st grade, Dec., 1910; on mily. surv., 1915-19; prov. engrn., grade II, Oct., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Sept., 1923; dep. dir., pub. wks., Mar., 1929; D.P.W., June, 1932.

THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (TEMP. COL.), SIR EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., commdr. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague surv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. surv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosp. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt., inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br., sec. I, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C.S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtce. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comtce. management, Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtce. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; senr. asst. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for Union, 1920; sec. for pub. health and ch. health offr. for Union, 1932.

THORNTON, GEORGE EDWIN, M.B.E. (1932). B. 1899; war surv., 1916-17 (E. Africa); clk., N. Rhodesia, 1917; asst. comptnr., taxes for periods, 1926, 1927 and 1928; asst. comptnr., taxes, Apr., 1928; comptnr., income tax, Apr., 1932; ag. treas., 1933.

THORNTON, HUGH CHOLMONDELEY, C.M.G. (1929), C.V.O. (1929).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Viscount Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, R.W.—B.1881; ed. Graaff Reinet and Elsenburg Schl. of Agr., Cape Prov. (dipl. with hon.); served in Anglo-Boer War as Scout and intelligence offr.; joined dept. of agr., Cape Prov. field crops and experiment and research section, 1905; offr.-in-ch., agril. research branch, agril. dept., Cape prov., 1908; prin., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr. and experiment station, 1910; sent by govt. to England, Europe and Australia to study agril. educn., experiment station organ. etc., took charge of expdn. to W. Central Africa, in connection with ostrich industry of Union, 1912; active serv. in France, 1917; resumed charge of Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., 1919; dir., field and animal husbandry, Oct., 1923; dir., nativ. agr., dept. of nativ. affrs., Apr., 1929; dir., agr., Basutoland, and agril. advr. to H. Cr., 1934.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb.; war surv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Ipoh; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Feb., 1926; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. contrlr., lab., Klang; asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, May, 1931; ag. emigrn. comntr. for Malaya, May, 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s offr.; treas. and aud. offr., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmtr. on various occasions, 1899-1907; postmtr. of Antigua, and federal postmtr. of Leeward Is., Apr., 1907; liby. trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908 and in 1913-15; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. asst. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city comntr., Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch., Antigua—Montserrat flashlight surv., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; ag. comntr., Virgin Is., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; warden and mag., Nevis, Oct., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. comntr., Montserrat and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., May to Aug., 1927 and Jan. to Apr., 1928; rep. Dominica at gen. leg. coun., 1928.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.).—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. coun., Br. Hond. Nov., 1922; asst. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Hond., Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Hond. Defence Force, Apr., 1928; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Oct., 1934.

TICKLE, ARTHUR GEORGE WARNHAM, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I.—B. 1887; ed. Holy Trinity, Bansbury, Polytechnic and Royal Acad., London; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engrn., 1920; tech. sec., D.P.W., 1923-4 and 1928-32; J.P., 1931; asst. D.P.W., 1932; ag. D.P.W., 1933.

TILNEY, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon)—B. 1909; cadet, Ceylon civ. surv., Jan., 1932; attd., office of dir., med. and san. services, Feb., 1932; attd., Galle kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Ratnapura kach., June, 1933; admstve. sec., dept. of med. and san. services and sec. to min. of health, Mar., 1934.

TIMMS, CHARLES GORDON, M.C. (three bars), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.Ed., L.R.F.P.S. (Glasgow), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1886; ed. Watson's Coll. and R. Coll., Surgeons, Edin.; served with R.A.M.C., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; M.O., Uganda, Jan., 1922; transf., Somaliland, Dec., 1922.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Dec., 1922.

TINTWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; mil. surv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; asst. acctnt., F.M.S. riya, 1921; ag. dep. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rly., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933.
TISDALE, E. G. S.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

TISDALE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.C., A.M. INST. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schol. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schol.), eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; asst. engnr., engrng. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engrng. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, HENRY LAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; asst. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.

TODD, CEDRIC LINDLEY.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schol.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1929; senr. audr., audr., 1933.

TODD, CHARLES DOUGLAS—B. 1901; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923-25; resigned; re-apptd., 1929; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, June-Oct., 1935; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Leeward Is., 1936.

TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1927; asst. supt., impta. and expts., 1930; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1933.
TOLMIE, GEORGE MACKENZIE, B.Sc. (Edin.)—B. 1904; ed. Inverness Royal Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forest Inst., Oxford; asst. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1926.
TOMKINSON, C.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, SIR GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903-04; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. Nigeria, 1911; 3rd cls. res., 1911; ag. res., 1912; 2nd cls. offr. (change of title), 1914; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1913; ag. sec., N. Provs., 1919; asst. sec., native affrs., 1921; ag. sec., do., 1921 and 1925; staff grade (supermny.), 1925; ret., 1928; asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1911; served with R. Engnr., France, 1915-19; res. engnr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1925; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engnr., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1928; supt., Trinidad govt. rly., 1933.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, Kt., BACH. (1925).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.: B. 1777; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.E.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCEY, O.B.E. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhibr.); asst. dist. commnr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. commnr., Aug., 1923; lab. commnr., May, 1930; dist. offr., Oct., 1931; senr. dist. offr., 1936.

TONKIN, SYDNEY, W.B., Eng. (Liverpool), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr., Chtd. Mech. Engnr.—B. 1886; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engnr., Dec., 1925; do., F.M.S., govt. factory, June, 1929.

TOPP, WM.—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transf. to the Gambia, Sept., 1910; 1st. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cls. apptmnt., Jan., 1920; ag. clk., leg. couns. on various occasions, 1916-22; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1925; J.P. and commnr., ct. of requests, July, 1925.

TORY, GEOFFROY WILLIAM.—B. 1912; ed. King Edward VII Schol., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol., 1931-35); med. and mod. languages tripod, pt. 1 cls. 1, pt. 2 cls. 1; B.A., 1934; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1936.

TOGHILL, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's Schol.; Ontario Agr. Coll. (Toronto Univ.); B.Sc., Agr., 1910; Cornell Univ., U.S.A., Graduate Schol., 1913, 1914; Harvard Univ., D.Sc., 1922; agt. and expert, U.S. Bureau of Entomology, 1910-11; entomologist, entomological branch, Ottawa, 1911-24; seconded as dir., Levuana campaign, Fiji, for two years, Nov., 1924; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, May, 1926; dir., Levuana campaign and agr., 1926; dir., agr., Uganda, June, 1929.

TOUGHER, ROBERT ALEXANDER HUNTLEY.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; war serv. Dardanelles, Egypt and Syria, ment. in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; solr., Ireland, 1920; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1879; ag. off. ast., Matara kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist temporalities ord. comm., and extra off. ast. to dir., educn., Nov., 1918; apptd. to civ. serv., July, 1919; addnl. ast. commnr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; cls. IV, Mar., 1926; asst. commnr., stamps, Mar., 1930; dep. commnr., stamps, July, 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Oct., 1934.

TOVEY, DUNCAN LORIMER PATTERSON.—B. 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; asst. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dec., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. ast. priv., C.O., Oct., 1930.

TOWNLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.) in Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Belgium and Germany 1914-19; astt. govt. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. govt. printer, May, 1927 and Nov., 1929; govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1931.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1925.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM., C.M.G. (1930).—Ed. at Clifton Coll., schir. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. ast. land offr. in New Territories, and regir. of land et., 1907; mem. of land et., 1907; 2nd ast. regir.-gen., 1909; 1st ast. regir.-gen. 1911; asst. dist. offr., N.T., (South), 1912; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; head of sanitary dept., 1917; supt. of imports and exports, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. col. treas., 1920, 1922 and 1924; dist. offr., N. Territory, 1920; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; 1st cls. cadet offr., ag. ast. col. sec., and clk. of councils, 1926; title changed to prin. ast. col. sec., 1930; senr. Hong Kong rep. to Bangkok opium confce., 1931; ag. col. sec. in 1933, 1934 and 1935.

TRATMAN, EDGAR KINGSLEY, L.D.S., M.D.S.—B. 1899; ed. Clifton Coll. and Bristol Univ.; prof. dental surgy., coll. of med., S'pore, Oct., 1929; mem. dental bd., F.M.S., Apr. 1931; do., S.S., July, 1931.

TRAVERS, MICHAEL F.—B. 1897; ed. St. Mary's and O'Connell's Schls., Dublin; boy olk., Irish land comm., Dublin, 8th May, 1913; ast. olk., board of educn., 22nd Apr., 1915; war serv., R. Irish Regt., 19th Apr., 1916 to 5th Dec., 1919; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1921; transf'd to C.O., 7th Apr., 1926.

TREMAYNE, ARTHUR GEORGE, A.I.E.E.—B. 1884; Br. P.O., inspr., London engng. dist., July, 1903; City and Guilds, London Inst., 1st hons. telegraphy, 1909; ditto, 1st final telephony, 1912; war serv., 1916-18; ast. teleg. engnr., P. & T., F.M.S., May, 1920; teleg. engnr., Johore, Sept., 1925; ch. teleg. engnr., Nov., 1926; engnr., P. & T., Seremban, Sept., 1930; sr. engnr., P. & T., S.S. & F.M.S., Perak and Dindings, Mar., 1931; do., Penang and P.W., Oct., 1933.

TREMELTT, FREDERICK THOMAS GEORGE, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 1903; attached Lancs. Fusiliers, 1903; sub.-inspr. constab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1908-1911; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 1908; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 1912; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-1912; passed mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1912; transf'd. to Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius volr. force, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comd. Mauritius labour batt., 1917; comd. Mauritius volr. force, Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, in 1917 and 1919; major, 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., supt., prisons and O.C. Mauritius Vol. Force, Feb. to Sept., 1922; dep. comsnr., pol. and prisons, Uganda, Nov., 1922; ag. comsnr., pol. and prisons, on many occasions, 1922-30; comsnr., pol. and prisons, Uganda, Dec., 1930.

TREMELTT, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL SCHWEIDER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Harrow Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.
TRENCH, ARTHUR DONALD LE POER, O.A.C. (dip.)—Senr. coffee offr., agrl. dept., Kenya, Oct., 1916.

TREVOR, IVAN BERNARD.—B. 1900; ed. Tyttendanger Lodge, Seaford; Rossall exhib., 1915 (not taken up); cadet, Midland rly., 1917; traffic ast., Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; ag. traffic supt. and ch. storekeeper in addn., 1925 and 1927; ag. ch. acct., 1928; passed Cantonese, 1928; traffic man. and ch. storekeeper, 1932.

TRINNICK, COMDR. GEORGE WILLIAM, R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1889; merchant serv., 1904-13; extra master's B.O.T. certif.; R.N., 1914-19 (H.M.S. "Diana," Home waters, near and far East, H.M.S. "Temeraire," North Sea and Russia); marine offr., Nigeria, 1922; lieut. comdr., 1924; comdr., on retired list, 1934; ag. dir., marine, 1935.
TRIPE, WILLIAM BORROWDALE.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch, N.Z.; Victoria Univ. Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929.

TROUGHTON, EDWARD PERCEVAL—B. 1899; ed. Forest Schl.; R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-18; olk., ast. comsnr., Basutoland, 1920; elk. finan. sec., 1923; ast. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; senr. ast. treas., Sept., 1935.

TROUGHTON, JOHN FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, B.A. (schol., senr. moderator), LL.B.; admstve. cadet, Kenya, 1926; dist. offr., 1928; on sp. duty with Kenya land comm., 1933; ag. olk., leg. coun., and clk. exec. coun., 1933.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schl., Toronto, Ontario; ast. olk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coal comm., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comm., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regiar. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TRUPHET, JOHN SYDNEY.—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; ast. head, stores dept., 1919; do., insurance and checking dept., 1920; do., shipping dept., 1922; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927; dep. head, stores dept., 1931; head, do., 1933; head, shipping dept., 1936.

TRUSTED, HARRY HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1888; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1913; served overseas (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, East Indies), 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1925; atty.-gen., 1927; comsnr. to revise the federal acts, Leeward Is., 1928; K.C., Leeward Is., 1929; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug., 1931; atty.-gen., Palestine, 1932.

TRUTTER, SIR THEODORUS GUSTAFT, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ. ser., Dec., 1892; chief examr., accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (med. and four clasps); ast. mag., Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermelo, Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, Apl., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, July, 1910; comsnr., S. African pol., Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa; ret., 1928.

TUCKER, D.E.A., M.C.—B. 1896; ed. at St. Francois de Sales Fathers, Givisiez (Fribourg) and Florimont Coll., Lancy (Geneva), B.-es-Lettres (1re partie) Grenoble Univ., July, 1914; Wilts. Regt. and M.G.C., 1914-20; 2nd lieut., June, 1917; H.M. cust., U.K., 1923; inspr.-in-ch., preventive serv., Tanganyika cust., 1929.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, C.B.E. (1932), F.I.A.A., certified accotr.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf'd. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; ast. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; ast. audr., Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (ment. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volrs., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt., Nyassaland Field Force, 1919; ast. auditor, Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; dep. treas., Dec., 1921; ag. treas., Apr., 1923; treas., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1931 and Oct., 1933 to Sept., 1934; ag. gov. in Feb., 1934.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Instn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hussey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and mem., bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apr., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; chmn., gen. purposes comttee, Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922; asst. dir., educn., Jamaica, 1930.

TUCKER, L. M.—Ed., Mod. Schl. for Girls, Exeter, Homerton Coll., Cambridge and Univ. Coll., Exeter; mistress, Prince of Wales' Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; organiser, female and infant educn., Apr., 1928; inspr. schls., Apr., 1933.

TUCKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Ed., (Cape town).—B. 1892; ed. Wilsons Grammar Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripes pt. I (hons. chem. and zoology) 1913; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripes, pt. II (zoology) 1914; asst. S. African museum, Cape town, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921-28; entomologist, Barbados, 1928; publis. include various papers on Araneae and Acari, and economic papers on control of Acarid and insect pests.

TULL, JOHN CADMAN.—M.D. (McGill), F.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1884; capt., Canadian Army Med. Corps (mentd. in desps.), France, 1915-19; pathologist, Rangoon gen. hosp., 1919; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, Jan., 1921; pathologist, Penang, July, 1921; lect., pathology, S'pore, June, 1926; govt. pathologist, S'pore, Aug., 1926; deleg. from King Edward VII Med. Coll. to Intern. cong. on cancer, London, May, 1928; deleg., leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930.

TUPPER, HENRY DE BEAUVIORE, A.M., R.N., Capt., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1883; naval cadet, 1898; commdr., 1917; capt., 1928; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Otranto barrage and adm'y.; Legion of Honour (France), Order of Aves (Portugal) and Al Valore (Italy); harmbr., Br. Guiana, 1925; do., Jamaica, Oct., 1931.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUNNELL.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. comnr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-22 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 as. atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., 1933.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; elk. post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot srgt.-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, May, 1923; ch. of pol., Grenada, Jan., 1927.
TURNER, CAPT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.
TURNER, HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Oamaru South Pub. Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. pub. trust office, 1899; asst. pub. trustee, 1923; under-sec. of defence 1930.

TURNER, JAMES POOGSON.—B. 1871; elk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; elk., regiar.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; comnr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; elk. to mag. dist., "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st elk., regiar.'s off., Antigua, May, 1906; comnr. to admin. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; elk. regiar. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regiar. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regiar., Sept., 1917; comnr. income tax, July, 1923; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1926; addnl. mag., dist. A., Antigua, Jan., 1928; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1929; J.P., May, 1930; comnr. sup. ct., Oct., 1931.

TURNTON, NEVILLE, K.C.—B. 1889; ed. at Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. audr., S. Leone, 1912; ag. audr. on four occasions; volunteer, R.G.A., Sierra Leone; asst. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921; 1st cls. mag. and judge in addn.; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, June, 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; chmn., income tax comnr., 1924; mem., educn. bd., 1925; ag. col. sec., 1925 and 1926; ag. ch. just., 1926, 1926, 1927 and 1928; admstd. govt., Sept., 1926; solr.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1928; temp. attd., legal advisory staff, C.O., Oct. to Dec., 1931; atty.-gen., Oct., 1933.

TUTE, RICHARD CLIFFORD.—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Schl., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet. exam. for Indian civ. serv., 1898; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly, Meerut and other dists., from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major, R.G.A.; dist. judge, Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1920; pres., land ct., Samaria, 1921; pres. land ct. Jerusalem, 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, 1931; admstd. govt. in Aug., 1932; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code," and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam.

TWEDSMUIR, 1st BARON (cr. 1935) Rt. HON. JOHN BUCHAN, C.H. (1932), M.A. (Oxon), hon. D.C.L. (Oxon), hon. LL.D. (Glasgow and St. Andrews), J.P. (Oxfordshire), D.L., J.P. (Peebleshire).—B. 1878; ed. Glasgow Univ. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol., 1896, M.A., hon. fellow); Stanhope historical essay, 1897; Newlight prize, 1898; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1899; pres. Union, 1899; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1901; pvt. sec. to Lord Milner, 1901-03; on headqrs. staff Br. Army in France, 1916-17 (temp. lieut.-col., offr. of the Crown of Belgium and the Crown of Italy); dir. infrn., 1917-18; M.P., Scottish Unive., 1927-36; lord high comnr., church of Scotland, 1933 and 1934; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 2nd Nov., 1936; author of many novels and of historical and other works.

TWELLS, JOHN.—Ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., 1907; examr., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; asst. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., July, 1912; senr. asst. mast. EDUCAN. DEPT., KENYA, 1919; AG. HEAD MAST., NAIROBI SCHL., JUNE TO DEC., 1920; SENR. AST. MAST., APR., 1921; AG. HEAD MAST. IN 1924, 1925 AND 1927.

TWEMLOW, GEORGE BROWNBILL.—B. 1897; ED. AT ROSSALL SCHL.; MILY. SERV., 1916-20; DEMOB. WITH RANK OF CAPT.; RE-ENLISTED, 1920-23; JOINED BOTANICAL AND FORESTRY DEPT., HONG KONG, 1923; AST. SUPT., BOTANICAL AND FORESTRY DEPT., HONG KONG, 1927.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.SC. (ENG.) WITH 1ST CLS. HONS., CIVIL ENGRNG., LIVERPOOL UNIV., 1909; 2ND LIEUT., R.E. (AP. RES.), 1910; AST. ENGRG., P.W.D., KENYA, 1911; EXEC. ENGRG., 1917; CAPT., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., GAMBIA, DEC., 1920; AST. D.P.W., NIGERIA, 1930; DIR., WKS. AND TRANSPORT, TRINIDAD, MAY, 1934.

TYNDALE-BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN, M.C.—B. 1896; ED. GLENALMOND, TRENT AND JESUS COLL., CAMBR.; WAR SER., R.A., 1914-18 (FRANCE AND PALESTINE); M.C., 1916; INSTR. IMP. SCH. OF GUNNERY, 1918; POLIT. OFFR. NAZARETH; PASSED INDIAN CIV. SERV. EXAM., 1919; SUPT., EDUCAN., TANGANYIKA, 1924; AG. DEP. DIR., EDUCAN., 1932; AG. DIR., DO., MAY-NOV., 1932 AND FEB.-SEPT., 1935.

TYNDALL, ARTHUR, A.M. INST. C.K., M.AM. SOC. O.E., M.N.Z. SOC. C.E., SOLR., SUP. CT., N.Z.—B. 1891; ED. BLUE SPUR SCHL., LAWRENCE DIST. HIGH SCHL., OTAGO UNIV. AND MASSACHUSETTS INST. OF TECHNOLOGY; JOINED N.Z. PUB. WKS. DEPT., 1909; AST. ENGRG., 1913; ENGRG.-IN-CH., PUB. WKS. DEPT., W. SAMOA, 1920; HIGHWAYS ENGRG., N.Z., 1924; UNDER-SEC., MINES, 1934.

TYRRELL, SIR FRANCIS GRAEME, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ED. PEMBROKE COLL., OXFORD (B.A.); CADET, CEYLON CIV. SERV., OCT., 1900; AG. PRIV. SEC. TO H.E. THE GOVERNOR, AUG., 1901; PRIV. SEC. TO H.E. THE OFFR. ADMSTG. THE GOVT., APR., 1902; OFF. AST. TO GOVT. AGT., CENT. PROV., JAN., 1903-DEC., 1904; SECONDED SUPT. OF POL., N.W. PROV., AUG., 1905, CENT. PROV., MAR., 1907; DIST. JUDGE, BADULLA, DEC., 1908; AST. GOVT. AGT., MATALE, FEB., 1909; DEP. COLL. OF CUSTOMS, NOV., 1911; ON MIL. DUTY, JAN., 1915; GOVT. AGT., N.W. PROV., MAY, 1921; PRIN. COLL. OF CUSTOMS, APR., 1926; MEM., EXEC. AND LEG. COUNS.; CONTR., REV., JUNE, 1927; DEP. CH. SEC., JULY, 1931; CH. SEC., MAY, 1932; O.A.G., SEPT.-DEC., 1933.

TYSER, SIR C. R., KT. BACH. (1909).—ED. AT RUGBY AND TRIN. COLL., CAMBR., M.A. (1878); CALLED TO BAR, IN TEM., 1873; AST. AG. JUDIC. COMSNR. AND LEG. ADVAR., CYPRUS, 1880-1; ATTY.-GEN., LEEWARDS, 1886-9; PRES. DIST. CT., KYRENA, CYPRUS, 1895; PUISNE JUDGE, CYPRUS, JAN., 1902; CH. JUST., 1906; RET., 1919; AUTHOR OF "MARINE INSURANCE LOSSES," 1894; WROTE TRANSLATION OF OMAR HILMI EFFENDI'S EVKAF LAW, 1899; AND OF THE MEJELLI, 1901.

UECKERMAN, ROBERT MASON.—B. 1876; 2ND CLS. CLK., R.M.R., NOV., 1902; 1ST CLS. CLK., JAN., 1903; EXAMNR., JAN., 1909; EXAMNR., R.M.J. OFFICE, JOHANNESBURG, OCT., 1919; AST. REGISTR., DEEDS, KIMBERLEY, APR., 1924; REGISTR., DEEDS AND COMPANIES, WINDHOEK, JULY, 1930; AST. REGISTR., DEEDS, CAPE TOWN, JAN., 1932; REGISTR., DEEDS, BLOEMFONTEIN, FEB., 1934; REGISTR., MINING TITLES AND RAND TOWNSHIPS REGISTR., JOHANNESBURG, 1935.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAROLD, O.B.E. (1934), D.OEC., M.E.F.A., F.R.G.S., M.C.S.F.E.—FORESTRY BRANCH, DEPT. OF INTERIOR, CANADA, 1903-4; AST. CONSERVATOR AND SENR. CONSERVATOR OF FORESTS, NIGERIA, 1904-17; OFFICE OF WOODS, TIMBER SUPPLY DEPT., ENGLAND, 1917-19; PRIN. FOREST OFFR., CYPRUS, 1921; REP. CYPRUS AND PALESTINE AT EMPIRE FORESTRY CONF., AUSTRALIA AND N.Z., SEPT.-NOV., 1928; REP. CYPRUS AT INTERNAT. CONG. OF EXPERIMENTAL FOREST STATIONS, STOCKHOLM, JULY-AUG., 1929; TITLE CHANGED TO CONSERV., FORESTS, JAN., 1932.
UPSDELL, GEORGE EDGAR SKINNER, M.A. (CANTAB.), H.C.F., C.I.E.E.—B. 1886; AST. MAST., EDUCAN. DEPT., HONG KONG, 1919; HEADMSTR., INDIAN SCHL., 1931; YAUMATI SCHL., 1932; CENT. BR. SCHL., 1935.
URICH, LEO GOTTFRIED WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (ENG.), L.R.C.P. (LOND.), D.C.M. & H.—B. 1899; MED. AST., CHACACHACARE LEPER SETTLMT., TRINIDAD, JAN., 1933.
USHER, CAPT. C. G., M.C.—AST. DIST. COMSNR., KENYA, MAY, 1920; AG. SEC., SECRETARIAT, 1927.

USHER, WALTER WHITBREAD.—B. 1886; ED. AT MARIST BROS. COLL., UNTENHAUSE; SERVED ANGLO-BOER WAR, 1901, S.W. AFRICA, 1914-15, FLANDERS, 1916-18; CLK., SWAZILAND, 1910; REGISTR. AND MASTER, SP. OF SWAZILAND, AND SHERIFF, 1916; DEP. AST. COMSNR., 1919.
UTTLEY, DENYS PLURAT, B.A.—B. 1906; ED. ST. JOHN'S, LEATHERHEAD AND CHRIST'S COLL., CAMBRIDGE; COL. AUDIT DEPT., MAY, 1930; AST. AUDR., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, AUG., 1930.

UTTLEY, KENNETH HARRISON, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.D., B.CH. (CAMBR.), D.T.M. & H. (LOND.).—B. 1901; MED. OFFR., HONG KONG; AST. MED. OFFR., VICTORIA HOSP., 1929; MED. OFFR. IN CH., KOWLOON AND NEW TERRITORIES, LAI CHI KOK PRISON, KOWLOON MORT.; AST. MED. OFFR., KOWLOON HOSP., IN CH. OFFRS.'FAMILIES, KOWLOON, AND POL. SURG., KOWLOON, 1930; MED. OFFR., DITTO, 1933.

VAITHIANATHAN, KANTHAN, B.SC. (LOND.)—B. 1896; CADET, CEYLON CIV. SERV., JUNE, 1923; ATTD., COLOMBO KACH., JULY, 1923; DITTO RATMAPURA KACH., MAY, 1924; AST. OFFICER, DITTO, SEPT., 1924; EXTRA OFFR. ASST., DITTO, APR., 1926; POL. MAG., DANDAGAMUWA, SEPT., 1925; DITTO, AVISSAWELLA, FEBRUARY, 1926; OFFICER ASST., CALLE KACH., NOV., 1926; ADDNL. AST. GOVT., AGT., HAMBANTOTA, DEC., 1928; OFFICER ASST., COLOMBO KACH., MAR., 1929; ADDNL. AST. GOVT., AGT., COLOMBO, NOV., 1929; AST. COL. SEC., MAR., 1930; SEC. TO MIN. FOR HOME AFFRS., JULY, 1931.

VALENTINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, M.C., M.R.C.S. (ENG.), L.R.C.P. (LOND.), M.B., B.S. (LOND.), D.P.H. (LOND.), D.T.M. AND H. (ENG.).—B. 1892; ED. PALMER'S SCHL. AND LOND. HOSP.; TEMP. COMEN., K.O.S.B., 1914-16; R.A.M.C.—1917-19; MED. OFFR., MED. DEPT., HONG KONG, 1919; IN CH., CIV. HOSP., 1925-28; IN CH., KOWLOON HOSP., 1929; IN CH., VICTORIA HOSP., 1930; AG. D.D.M.S., 1931, 1932 AND 1935; M.O. IN CH., CIV. AND MENT. HOSP., JAN., 1934.

VAN BROEKHUizen, DR. H. D.—B. 1872; ED. FICKSBURG, SENEKAL, GREY COLL., BLOEMFONTEIN, VICTORIA COLL., STELLENBOSCH, EDINBURGH, Utrecht AND LEYDEN; D.D. LEYDEN; MIN., DUTCH REFORMED CH., PRETORIA, BEFORE AND AFTER ANGLO-BOER WAR; M.P., PRETORIA, 1924-33; MEM., S.A. ACAD.; HISTORICAL SOC., LEYDEN AND Utrecht; MIN. PLENIP., FOR UNION S. AFRICA, THE HAGUE, AUG., 1933.

VAN DER LINGEN, PIETER MOLL, B.A.—B. 1879; ED. KROONSTAD AND STELLENBOSCH; HEADMSTR., JEPPE EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION, JOHANNESBURG, 1903; DO., MIDDELBURG SECONDARY SCHL., 1908; INSPR., EDUCAN., TRANSVAAL, 1913; PUB. SERV. COMSNR., 1934.
VAN DER MERWE, HERMANUS ZULCH, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. at Ceres High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; inspr., schls., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Cape Prov., 1934.

VAN DER SPUY, LT.-COL. ADRIAN JOHANNES, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Cape Town).—B. 1889; ed. Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Glasgow Univ. and Capetown Univ.; H.S. and H.P., Western Infmy., Glasgow, 1914-16; capt., S.A.M.C. (Overseas), 1916-20; served in France and Belgium, 1918; Br. War and Victory medals; O.C. mil. hosp., Wynberg, as capt. (temp. maj.), S.A.M.C. (A.C.F.), 1920; maj., S.A.M.C., (Perm. Force), 1923; defence headqrs. (med. services sect.), 1926; O.C. mil. hosp., Roberts' Heights and senr. med. offr., Roberts' Heights, 1926; transf'd, to dept. of public health as astt. health offr. for Union, 1927; lt.-col., R. of O. (med. br.) U.D.F., 1927; dep. dir., med. services, U.D.F., attd. to Staff Corps from R. of O. (inadmn. to civ. duties as astt. health offr. for Union), 1927.
VAN DER SPUY, MICHIEL LOUW.—2nd gr. compr., Natal, Feb., 1913; ch. draughtsmen and compr., Apr., 1914; prof. astt., Apr., 1917; survr.-gen., Natal, Sept., 1931; do., Pretoria, May, 1934.

VAN DE VELDE, MARCEL ARTHUR MAURICE—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; elk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., May, 1900; tempy. astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., Mar., 1903; astt. audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 1904; local audr., Uganda Prot., Nov., 1904; audr., Uganda Prot., May, 1910; audr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.

VAN LANGENBERG, WILLIAM JAMES ALBERT—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; attd., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1930; attd., Anuradhapura kach., May, 1930; ag. office astt., Nuwara Eliya kach., Oct., 1931.

VAN NIEKERK, CHRISTIAAN ANDRIES.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commdt. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del. peace confce., Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation coman., till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S., 1907; leg. assem., Union., 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances coman., chmn., sp. pension bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; pres., 1930.
VAN RENEN, R.—B. 1877; elk., treasy., 1895, and income tax office, 1914; recr., rev., Benoni, 1921; do., Krugersdorp, 1929; do., E London, 1932; do., Bloemfontein, 1932.

VAN RYNEVELD, COL. SIR PIERRE HELPERUS ANDRIAS, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—B. 1891; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Royal N. Lances Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Senussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhino army of occupation, 1919; ment. in desps. six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., Feb., 1920; col., S.A. staff corps, Nov., 1929; O.C., troops, Roberts Heights and commdt., S.A. Milv. Coll. in addn. to duties as dir., air services, Dec., 1929; brig.-gen. and apptd., ch. gen. staff, May, 1933.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, JOHANNES ZACHARIAS, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; astt. Bethlehem; vice-prin., Ficksburg, 1908; do., Normal Pretising Schl., Bloemfontein, 1910; prin., model schl., Bloemfontein and vice-rect., Normal Coll., 1913; inspr. of schl., O.F.S., Nov., 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland, Germany and Austria; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn. since 1921.
VAN ZEYLEN, FREDERICK CORNELIUS.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engr., draughtsman, harbour engrn.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; astt. civ. engrn., 1925; dep. civ. engrn. 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, HENDRIK STEFANUS, L.L.B. (Camb.), B.A., L.L.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1905-10; parly. draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation coman., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

VAN ZYL, GIDEON BRAND.—B. 1873; ed. Normal and South African Coll., Capetown; atty-at-law and notary pub., 1898; conveyancer, 1897; astt. dir., war recruiting, 1918; dep. chmn., comtes., Union of S.A. H. of A., Jan., 1934.

VAN ZYL, JACOB ERNEST SIERRER.—B. 1885; prob. elk., R.M., Hanover, 1904; elk., deeds, Vryburg, 1904; elk., R.M., Calvinia, 1905; elk., master's off., C.T., 1906; 2nd gr. elk., Union educn. dept., 1910; 1st gr. elk., do., 1914; senr. elk., do., 1917; 2nd gr. prin. elk., do., 1920; 1st gr. prin. elk., 1921; 2nd gr. ch. elk., 1926; 1st gr. ch. elk., 1928; pub. serv. inspr., 1931; under sec., educn., July, 1934.

VAN ZYL, L. J., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Boys' High Schl., Wellington, and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; head, High Schls., Vredesfort, Smithfield, Ladybrand, 1910-21; inspr., educn., 1921-35; sec., educn., O.F.S., 1935.

VASKESS, HENRY HARRISON.—B. 1891; elk., Western Pacific high coman., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., June, 1915; elk. in ch., accts., W. Pacific, Sept., 1919; ch. elk. and acctnt., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high coman. for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1926 and 1927-29; sec., Western Pacific high coman., Dec., 1929.

VASSALL, HENRY HOLLAND.—B. 1887; ed. Bedford Grammar Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; astt. res., Nyassaland, 1911; res., 1919; admstve. offr., 1st grade, Dec., 1923; ag. prov. comanr., Jan., 1929 to June, 1930 and July, 1930 to Feb., 1931.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L. S. & A.—B. 1891; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1928; govt. exhibn. for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate work at Univ. Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinical asst., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (Duncan med.); temp. commn., R.A.M.C. (ment. in desps.); med. offr., Uganda, 1919; res. surg. offr., Zanzibar Prot., 1925; seni. specialist offr., 1935; 4th cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1931.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripus), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regis., high ct., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; res. mag., July, 1929; ag. judge in 1931, 1932 and 1933; asst. atty.-gen., 1933 and 1934; ag. atty.-gen. in 1934 and 1935; comsnr. for revision of laws, 1934; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; author of "The Dual Jurisdiction in Zanzibar" (1938).

VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC, B.A. (Dublin).—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1917; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1921; 2nd mag., S'pore, 1923; 2nd asst. sec. (A), col. secr., S'pore, 1924; Br. ren., Brunei, 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; mag., Seremban, 1927; dist. offr., Upper Perak, 1929; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; Br. advr., Perlis, July 1932.

VENNING, GUY, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENOUR, LOUIS STEPHEN DOUGLAS HAMILTON.—B. 1885; ed. privately; capt., 5th batt., Middle Regt.; asst. comsnr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; comsnr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1922; ag. comsnr., pol. C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; seni. comsnr., pol., 1929; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., on various occasions, 1925-33.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and cust., Br. Honduras, 1908; 3rd cls. clk., registr. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen's office, 1912; resig., 1913; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1918; dep. clk., cts., St. Catharine, Jamaica, 1918; clk., cts., Trelawny, 1920; ag. res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag. for various periods 1922-26; res. mag., Hanover, 1926; ag. judge, Kingston ct., 1927.
VERNALL, RICHARD JOHN, A.R.I.B.A.—B. 1901; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; ag. tech. sec., P.W.D., 1934.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES, C.B. (1924).—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; schol. of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cls. mods., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cls. lit., hum., 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. insec's dept., inland rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 13th Aug., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot. comsnr., 1903; mem., managing comte. of E.I.O.; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin., Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; read. S. African Union medal, 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov.-gen. of Australia, 1911-12; 1st cls. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited and reported on British Solomon Islands, Nov., Dec., 1911; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-French New Hebrides confce., May-Aug., 1914; transf. to treasy., Oct., 1914; lent to Miny. of Munitions, June, 1915; asst. gen. sec., 1916; visited France and Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; asst. sec., Irish Convention, June, 1917 to Mar., 1918; retd. to treasy., May, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., B. of E., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East dept. as asst. sec., finan. and economic, Apr., 1921; visited Egypt, Palestine and Trans-Jordania on ap. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv. at Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Nov. to Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transf. to permanent staff of C.O. as asst. sec., Jan., 1924; seconded for service as finan. adviser to govt. of Iraq, 1925; returned to C.O., 1st Oct., 1928; mem. Palestine Cur. Board, 1928.

VIADER, RENE, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; regiar., col. sec's office, May, 1919; finan. asst., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec's office, Apr., 1925; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1926-30 and Apr., 1932.

VICKERS-HAVILAND, L. A. W.—B. 1893; lieut., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; seconded, 2nd K.A.R., 1918; asst. polit. offr., 1920; admstve. offr., 2nd grade, 1922; asst. dist. offr., 1926; attd., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 1930; dist. offr., 1931; seconded as asst. to dir., agr., 1932-33.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., R. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; lat grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924; seni. dist. comsnr., 1934.

VIGNE, CHILLOW.—B. 1900; M.Sc. (Yale), B.A. (Oxon.), dip. for. (Oxon) Worcester Coll.; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; asst. consvr., forests, Gold Coast, Mar., 1922; commonwealth fund fellow, 1930-32, Yale Univ.; sylviculturist, Gold Coast, Oct., 1933.

VILJOEN, PHILIPPUS RUDOLPH, M.R.C.V.S., Dr. Med. Vet. (Berne).—B. 1889; ent. dept. of agr. as asst. vety. bacteriologist, Onderstepoort, Pretoria, 1913; vety. research offr., Onderstepoort and Vryburg, 1914; seconded to dept. of defence, Oct., 1914; retd. to dept. of agr., June, 1915; resigned, Jan., 1919; reapttd. dept. of agr. as seni. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. research and prof. in vety. medicine, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Apr., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services, 1927; under-sec., agr., Jan., 1931; sec. agr., May, 1933.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., Lond.—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's schl., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNES, C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1918)—B. 1876; ed Switzerland and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; res., 1905; dir., educn., 1908-18; lieut., Gen. List, 1915; capt., 1915; major, 1916; Étoile Noire, 1917; Legion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Italy, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; demob., 1919 with rank of major in G.R.O.; mem. and sec., S. of S.'s advisory comte. on native educn. in Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.

VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; asst. mag. and ag. civil comsnr., 1902-05; clk.-ast. Cape House of Assembly, 8th Apr., 1907; clk.-ast., House of Assem., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assembly, 1st Oct., 1920.
VISSER, DR. THOMAS CHRISTOPPEL—Mem. Union assem., to 1929; mem., Union senate, 1930; chmn. of comtes., 1930.
VISUWALINGAM, VELLUPILLAI.—B. 1883; ent. Ceylon civ. serv. as ag. extra office ast. Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1934; extra office ast. Jaffna, Jan., 1935.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD.—B. 1890; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 1917; 3rd asst. sec., fed. secretariat, 1919; asst. dist. offr., 1920-21; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, 1922; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; attd., coll. secs. office, S'pore, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1926; ag. dist. offr., B. Padang, July, 1927 to Sept., 1928; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1929; supt., 1931 census, Malaya, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. II., Dec., 1931; economy offr., S.S. and P.M.S., Aug., 1932; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., 1934.

VOIGT, FREDERICK CAREL MARTHINUS, B.A. LL.B.—B. 1879; clk., dept. of just., 1901; pvt. sec. to min. of nat. affrs., 1910; senr. clk., nat. affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1912; dept. of just., Feb., 1913; ast. mag., Krugersdorp, 1916; prof. ast., atty.-gen's. office, Bloemfontein, 1921; ditto, Cape Town, 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1928; ast. prov. sec., Cape adminstr., 1930; prov. sec., ch. local govt. inspr. and contr., educn. finance, Mar., 1933.
VOLLET, DENYS HENRI RENE.—B. 1898; served in Great War; med. offr., Basutoland, 1927.

VON BONDE, CECIL, Ph.D., M.A.—Ed. Univ. of Capetown; senr. lect. in Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1918; awarded "Purcell Memorial Prize" for Zoology in 1924; as prof., Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1926; dir. fisheries survey and govt. marine biologist, Union of S. Africa, 1928; seconded for marine biolog. survey to confce. of E. African govs. in Kenya and Zanzibar, 1928.

VOSS, GEORGE DE TOIT, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Advocate of the Sup. Ct., of S. Africa.—B. 1884; ast. regisr., sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal prov. div.), 1918; regisr., high ct. of S.W. Africa, 1919; atty.-gen., S.W. Africa, 1925; solr.-gen., Eastern dists., Cape Prov., 1931; law adviser, dept. of just., Nov., 1931.
WADDELL, CECIL JAMES, B.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen's Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; war ser., 1915-18; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; seconded, Kowloon-Canton rly., 1931-32.

WADDINGTON, E.J., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1919).—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. commr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. ast. sec., secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; ast. sec., govt. confce., 1926; res. commr., Mombasa, 1928; col. sec., Bermuda, 1932; do. Br. Guiana, 1935.

WADE, A. DE V., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtnr. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; ast. dist. commr., E.
