Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native commr., Kenya, 1926; prin. ast., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928; ch. native commr., 1932; ag. col. sec., 1933; col. sec., 1934.

WADE, CECIL HENRY, O.B.E. (1929), B.A. (hons.), 1909.—Ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtnr.) and Keble Coll., Oxford; 3rd grade res., Nyasaland, Aug., 1911; ast. ch. sec. July, 1920; 1st ast. sec., June, 1921; examr. in Chinyanja language; ag. ch. sec., May, 1926; ag. treas., July to Dec., 1927; ag. ch. sec. May 1929 to Jan., 1930 and Apr., 1930; prin. ast. sec., Nigeria, 1931.

WADE, CAPT. T. G. ROCHFORT, M.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1901; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Columba's Coll., Dublin; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1926; M.A., 1930; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, May, 1931; C.O., St. Lucia Volunteer Force.

WADIA, SIJAVAX HIRJI, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attd., Jaffna Kach., June, 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1915; finan. ast. to chrmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; ast. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addnl. ast. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; ast. commr., stamps, Jan., 1926; ast. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1931; ast. govt. agt. Matara, Dec., 1932; ag. excise commr., Mar., 1934.
WADLEY, W. J. D., M.A.—B. 1903; ed. City of London Schl.; class schl., Lincoln Coll., inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Mekran, S. Persia; transf'd. to Bengal pol., 1901; on sp. famine operns., 1908-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pol., Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAKEFIELD, ARTHUR JOHN, B.Sc., N.D.A., N.D.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Schl., Harper Adams Agrl. Coll., Reading and Edinburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Infy., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Aug., 1933; dep. dir., agr., 1935.

WAKEFIELD, COL. HUBERT STEPHEN, O.B.E. (Mil.) 1919—B. 1883; ed. privte. schl. and Ellesmore Coll.; joined Cape Mtd. Riflemen, 1901; S.A. war, 1899-02; (Queen's med., 3 clasps) lieut., C.M.R., 1912; lieut., 1st regt., S.A.M.R., 1913; operns. in G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15; adjt., 1st Regt., S.A.M.R., 1915-16; capt., 1st S.A.Infy. (Overseas), 1917; operns. France and Belgium, 1917-18; capt., 1st Res. Batt., S.A. Infy. and seconded for sp. employment, 1918; 1st A.Q.M.G. and temp. major, 1918; operns., Persia, 1918-19; ment. in desps., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory med.; on staff of O.C., Union garrison troops, Cape Peninsula, with temp. rank of capt., 1921; D.A.A.G. (later staff offr.) defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1921; capt., 1922; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; maj. do., 1929; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Cape Town, with temp. rank of lt.-col., 1931; lt.-col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1931; O.C. No. 4 dist., Johannesburg, 1932; adjt.-gen., U.D.F., 1934; temp. col., 1934.

WALCOTT, EDWARD KEITH.—B. 1892; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; war serv., 1915-18; solr., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1921; solr.-gen., Barbados, 1930; ag. att.-gen., various periods, 1930-34; also M.H.A.
WALCOTT, JAMES EYRE, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1894; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Glasgow Univ.; gov't. bacteriologist and pathologist, Barbados, 1934.

WALKER, COL. CHARLES WILLIAM GARNE, C.M.G. (1933), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1882; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; staff coll., Camberley, 1912-13; European War, 1914-18 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., bvt. lieut.-col.); col., 1923; ast. sec., commt. of Imp. Defence, 1921-25; ret., 1927; sec., confce. of E. African govs., Dec., 1925 and sec., high commr. for transport, Kenya and Uganda, Feb., 1926.
WALKER, HUBERT EDMUND, M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ast. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1919; ag. senr. exec. engr., 1926; supt., native admnstr., whk., 1929.
WALKER, IVAN LAWRENCE.—B. 1883; ch. inspr., dept. of lab., Union of S. Africa, Nov. 1924; under-sec. for lab., Sept., 1929; sec. for lab., May, 1932.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt.; Coorga Nagpore rifles; served with Yorks. I.Y. in S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1908; mentioned in despatches, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1910.
WALKER, J. W., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1929.

WALKER, CAPT. REGINALD DAVID, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., M.Inst.C.E., Chtd. civ. engrnr.—B. 1892; ed. R. Coll. Science, and City and Guilds Engng. Coll., Whitworth Exhibr., 1913; field coy., R.E., France, Salonika, Egypt, 1915-18; capt., 1917; R.E., Egypt, 1918-20; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1920; ag. dist. engrnr., 1923; dist. engrnr., grade II, 1925; ag. asst. engrnr., ways and wks., 1926 and 1930; dist. engrnr., grade I, 1929; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., 1932; ag. estate and survey offr., F.M.S.R., 1933; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton Rly., Hong Kong, 1934.

WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S., Rlys., 1914-18; asst. acct., do., 1918; dep. acct., Jan., 1926; ag. chief acct., various periods, 1926-34; ch. acct., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbrs., 1935.

WALKER, RICHARD PHILIP SEBASTIAN, B.A., (Oxon.).—B. 1898; attd. Eng. Coll., Johore, Nov., 1920; attd., govt. Eng. schl., Muar, Feb., 1921; Eng. coll., Johore Bahru, June, 1922; Raffles Inst., S'pore, Apr., 1924; Malay coll., K. Kangsar, May, 1924; govt. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1925; King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Sept., 1926; headmait., Eng. schl., Johore, Dec., 1930; prin., King Ed. VII schl., Taiping, Oct., 1931.
WALKER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER RIDDELL.—B. 1906; ed. St. Alban's Schl. and Glasgow Univ.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; asst. dist. comsnr., 1931.

WALL, ERNEST, M.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Worcester Royal Grammar Schlr.; R.E., 1915-19; capt.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nyasaland, 1921; exec. engrnr., 1929; ag. D.P.W., May, 1931 to Feb., 1932 and 1934-35.
WALL, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1900; supt., prisons, Trinidad, Nov., 1932.
WALLACE, CYRIL R., L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.M. (Ireland).—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-18; medical officer, Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918.

WALLACE, GEORGE BRUNTON, B.Sc. (agr.), Ph.D.—B. 1899; ed. George Heriot's Schlr., Edinburgh; mily. serv. R.G.A., 1918-19; B.Sc., 1922; Ph.D., 1925; sen. demonstr. in agr. and forest entomology, univ. of Edinburgh, 1922-23; asst. in plant pathology, bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1924-25; microscopest, game pres. dept., Tanganyika territory, 1925; mycologist, dept. of agr., 1927.
WALLACE, SIR LAWRENCE AUBREY, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. 1910.—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, May, 1907; ag. admtr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admtr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.
WALLINGTON, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1924.
WALLIS, KENNETH.—B. 1901; ed. Epworth Coll., Ryland Univ. Coll., Nottingham; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1923; A.I.C., 1924; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad and Tobago, 1929; govt. analyst, Br. Guiana, 1933.

WALMSLEY, CUTHBERT HORNER, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.M. and Cy.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1882; ed. Eccles Grammar Schlr.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906; Kenya, Magadi water wks., 1913; war serv. in B.E.A., G.E.A. and P.E.A.; ment. in desps., 1916, 1917, 1918; ent. col. serv., 1922; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1922; ag. contracts offr., 1924; exec. engrnr., 1925; ag. asst. dir. in 1926 and 1930-31; ag. hydraulic engrnr., 1931-32; ag. suptgd. engrnr. in 1933 and 1934.

WALSH, DUDLEY J.C.—B. 1895; ed. Merchant Taylors Schlr.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

WALSH, GEOFFREY, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1884; Imp. cust. serv., Apr., 1906; collr. of cust., Mombasa, May, 1913; seconded to occupied territory of German E. Africa to organise cust. serv., with rank of capt., 1916-17; in charge of cust. dept. of occupied area, German E. Africa, Jan. to July, 1917; dep. comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1920; comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda and mem., leg. coun., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WALSH, PATRICK JOSEPH STANISLAUS, Ph.D., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. Clermont and Paris (France), Innsbruck (Tyrol), Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, King's Inns, June, 1898; K.C., Feb., 1910; Ph.D. (metaphysics and ethics, ancient languages and comparative philology, maths.); junr. and senr. crown pros., N.W. circuit and winter assizes, 1906-20; war serv., 1915-1918; ag. county ct. judge for two circuits, midland counties; dep. recorder, Galway; dep. county ct. judge for counties Kerry, Tipperary and West Riding of Cork; pres., R. pensions appl. tribunal (Ireland), Mar., 1920-May, 1929; pres., dist. ots. Cyprus, 1929-31; ch. just., Seychelles, 1931; O.A.G., Seychelles, Dec., 1931-May, 1932.

WALTER, A., O.B.E. (1935), F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal coman., 1909; census coman., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imgrts. and poor law comsnr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs.' confoc., 1926; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., Dec., 1911; att. Galle kach., May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle kach. Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matare, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929; ditto, N. W. Prov., Mar., 1931; ols. I, grade II, 1933; P.M.G. and dir., tels., Sept., 1933.

WALTON, Bryan Stagg.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S.S. Nov., 1913; ag. ast. dist. judge and 4th mag., Spore, Mar., 1914; ast. censor, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., Spore V.R., Jan., 1916; ag. ast. supt. govt. monops., Spore, Nov., 1916; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Dec., 1917; ag. ast. supt. govt. monops., Penang, Aug., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., K. Selangor, May, 1924; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; supt., lands, Kelantan, Aug., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1928; ag. collr., land rev. and ast. treas., Malacca, May, 1929; cls. II, Nov., 1930; 1st ast. sec. (a), S.S., Aug., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Jan., 1934.

WALTON, Hon. Sir Edgar Harris, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high comsnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, Sir George O'Donnell, K.T. Bach. (1925).—B.1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1916; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926; ret., 1931.
WALWYN, Algernon Edward Vere.—B. 1888; ed. Bath Coll. and Peterhouse, Camb.; ast. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, Apr., 1915; res., Sept., 1932.

WALWYN, Vice Admiral Sir Humphrey Thomas, K.C.S.I. (1923), C.B. (1928), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1879; ed. H.M.S. Britannia, Dartmouth; went to sea, H.M.S. Camperdown, 1895; gunnery lieut., 1904; commdr., 1912; H.M.S. Warspite, 1915-17; capt., 1916; in commd., destroyer flotillas and snr. off., Mediterranean destroyers, 1923; dir., gunnery divn., naval staff, admv., 1924-26; commanded, H.M.S. Valiant, 1926; flag offr. commdg. R. Indian Navy, 1928-34; rear-adm., 1928; vice-adm., 1932; ret. list, 1934; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1936.

WAMSLEY, R. W., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; olk., civ. comsnr.'s office, Kimberley, 1903; inland rev. dept., 1911; prin. clk., 1921; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1921; recr., rev., Bloemfontein, 1931; ch. rev. offr., 1932; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1933.

WARD, Esquire Rudul La Tourette, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; pol. mag., dist. "A," Barbados, 1931; ag. regiar., and judge, ast. ct. of appl., various periods, 1932-33.

WARD, Espine Francis, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. lieut., gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroon Expedy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secr. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri, Aetiology, Symptoms, and Treatment," "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.).

WARD, E. R.—B. 1902; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Coll. of Estate Management, Lincoln's Inn Fields; cadet, Nigeria, 1926; ast. dist. offr., 1929; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., S. Prova., Nov. 1930-May, 1931; ditto to gov., July, 1931-Mar., 1934.
WARD, Frank Dobbington, F.R.I.B.A., M.R.S.I.—B. 1885; ed. Kent Coll., Canterbury; P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1920; prov. architect, S.S., Nov., 1928; regir., bd. of archts., S.S., Jan., 1932.

WARD, Joseph Corbet, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.) I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Soitk expdn. (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. reglar., docs., land dept., E.A.P., 1905; attd. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1915-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. lieut. col. (despa. 3 times, 3 war medals); staff offr. and intell-offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencies, London, 1926.

WARD, Lennox, K.C., B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope)—Called to bar, 1909; prof. astt., atty.-gen., Cape, 1910; prof. astt., solr.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926; judge-pres., native high ct., 1933.

WARD, Leonard Keith, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queensland govt. exhibtioner, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; ast. govt. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.
WARD, Leonard Sumner, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1890; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; attd., N. Provs. secretariat, Nov., 1929; res., Oct., 1930.

WARD, Wilfred Arthur.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served, France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lncs. Fusiliers, May, 1915; seconded, M.G.C., France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; ast. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. ast. treas. F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1933; pub. trustee and offr. admstr., F.M.S., Mar., 1934.

WARD, William Ernest Frank, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. hona., B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDLE, Brian.—B. 1891; ed King William's Coll., Isle of Man; Indian pol., 1911-22; King's Pol. med., 1919; seconded as intell. offr., Dublin, 1920-22; supt. in charge, C.I.D., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. asst. commdt. in 1930 and 1933; supt., pol., Tanganyika Territory, 1934.
WARDLE, D. S.—Temp. asst. treas., Kenya, June, 1920; acct., med. dept., Apr., 1921; asst. treas., July, 1923; senr. asst. treas., 1930.

WARDLE, James Morey, B.Sc., C.E., M.E.I.C.—B. 1888; ed. Br. Columbia Pub. Schls. and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.Sc., 1912; engrnr. on locn. and constrn., Canadian N. Pac. Rly., Br. Columbia; highway engrnr., nat. parks, Canada, dept. of intr., 1914-18; supt., Banff nat. park, Alta., 1918-21; ch. engrnr., nat. parks, Canada, 1921-35; western supervsr., nat. parks, 1932-35; dep. min., dept. of intr., Ottawa, 1935.

WARD-PRICE, Henry Lewis, B.A., Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Caterham Schll. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 1912; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1924; asst. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; cls. I, grade I, admvte. serv., 1929.

WARDROPER, Percy Redesdale, O.B.E. (1934), M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1892; B. S. A. pol., 1912; clk., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1914; seconded to N. Rhodesia pol., 1915; lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., Jan., 1917; capt., Jan., 1927; comsrr., pol., Dec., 1926; comsrr., prisons, 1927; King's pol. med., 1929.

WARE, Thomas Walter, M.B., Ch.B., Bristol, D.P.H. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Bristol; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, in ch., subord. staff and families, Kennedy Town infectious diseases hosp. and asst., Victoria hosp., 1927; in ch., civ. hosp., 1928; visiting med. offr., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries, 1930; 2nd port health offr. and inspr. immigrts., Sept., 1932 and Nov. 1933; ag. port health offr., etc., 1933.

WARNER, Brodrick Ashton.—B. 1888; ed Winchester Coll.; asst. dist. comsrr., Uganda Prot., 1912; lab. comsrr., 1922; 1st grade administrative offr., 1923; senr. asst. sec., 1924; dep. prov. comsrr., 1929; ag. dep. ch. sec., Oct., 1931; prov. comsrr., June, 1933; late capt., 5th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, Edgar Herbert, M.B.E. (1930).—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customs, Nyasaland Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also immigr. offr., 1913; principal immigr. offr., May, 1914; asst. contr., cust., Apr., 1920; ag. comptr., cust., July, 1922; comptr., cust., Mar., 1925; ag. treas., June, 1933.
WARREN, Fredk. Drummond.—B. 1881; stock immigr., Basutoland, 1914; subj.-immigr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19.

WARREN, John Joseph.—B. 1889; R.I.C., 1910-13; immigr., pol., F.M.S. Med., May, 1913; asst. comsrr., pol., Mar., 1921; dir., crim. intell., May, 1926; head, prev. serv., S'pore, Nov., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., Sept., 1929; senr. asst. supt., govt. monops., S.S., July, 1932; senr. dep. comsrr., excise, 1934; ag. comsrr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., 1935.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918 twice ment. in despa.; French Croix de Guerre; asst. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. comsrr., Apr., 1924.

WASE-BAILEY, Basil Noel Victor, M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1926, D.P.H. (Eng.), 1929.—B. 1899; med. serv., B. Guiana, 1926; G.M.O., pub. hosp.; ag. gov't. bacl., 1926; asst. G.M.O.H., personal asst. to surg.-gen. and dep. port health offr., 1927; gov't. med. offr. of health, surg. gen.'s dep. and port health offr., 1931; immigr. agt.-gen., regr.-gen., 1935; ag. chmn., poor law comsrs., 1935.

WATERER, Ralph Ronald, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1902; passed B.A. in estate management and dipl. forestry, Cambridge Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, lat gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1928.

WATERMEYER, Ernest Frederick.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; maths. schol. Caius Coll., 1899; maths. tripso., 1902; law tripso., 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922.

WATERMEYER, Theodork Heinrich.—B. 1873; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Old Gymnasium, Stellenbosch, Bath Coll., Bath and Mason's Univ. Coll. (now Birmingham Univ.), M.Inst.C.E.; appt., Cape govt. rlys., 1902; asst. ch. civ. engrnr., 1920; rep. S. African Rlys. at Internat. Rly. Congress, Rome, 1922; asst. gen. man., Bloemfontein, 1925; asst. gen. man. for Cape suburban electrification, Capetown, and consulting engrnr., 1927; asst. gen. man., Johannesburg, 1928; gen. man., 1933.

WATERS, Harold Bertram B.A., Oxon.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Guildford and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; served in The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regt. and Signal Serv., R.E., 1914-19; aspt., agric., Nigeria, Mar., 1921; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to report on oil-palm industry, 1926; dep. asst. dir., agric., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; comsrr., Canadian national exhibit., 1928; dep. dir., agr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928; dir., agr., Kenya, 1933.

WATKINS, Charles Arthur Albrecht.—B. 1883; ed. Br. Comml. Schl., Smyrna and prte. tuition; survr. and asst. irrign. supt., Cyprus, 1901-04; survr., ch. survr. and asst. prov. engrnr., Sudan, 1905-08; engrnr., Egypt, 1st class, and asst. directing engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, July, 1929; divnl. roads engrnr., P.W.D., Apr., 1930; divnl. engrnr., P.W.D., Mar., 1932.

WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Schl.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925; med. offr., Kenya, 1934.

WATSON, Andrew D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baldeock Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; dep. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, Donald Francis, O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admtr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtee., 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., ag. chmn., profiteering comtee., July, 1920 to Apr.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B., 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Cik., gov. of Zululand's office, 1896; ag. sec. for Zululand at different periods, 1896; olk., gov't. office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf. Dep. civ. serv. and apptd. olk., gov't. office, Mar., 1901; ch. olk., July, 1904; olk., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., major-gen. E.S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, K.C.V.O., Dec., 1909; asst. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; olk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A., 1902, B.Sc., 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Spore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr. of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923; ag. dir. eduon., S.S. and F.M.S., 1930-31.

WATSON, JAMES GILBERT.—B. 1889; ed. Kgl. Preuss. Akademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; dep. dir., forests, Malaya, 1938.

WATSON, OSWALD, L.S.—B. 1891; survey cadet, N.S.W. and N. Guineas, Sept., 1910-14; milt. serv., survey sec., R.E. and topo. sec., A.I.F., 1915-18; lic. land surv., Australia and N.Z., 1919; surv., S.S., Apr., 1920; do. F.M.S., Jan., 1921; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; ag. asst. supt., Nov., 1933.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treas. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. asst. treas., 1930.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec., and parly. head of depts. of post office, telegraphs, educ., rly., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comttee., Diocesan Synod, 1910; ag. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. and the Coronation, 1911; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1913; hon. priv. sec. to gov., 1916; asst. priv. sec. to gov., 1918; hon. corres. sec., Royal Col. Inst.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E., M. Coun. Inst. Loco. Engrs.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engnr. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; mln. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr. (mech.), 1928; ch. engnr. (contracts), 1935.

WATT, MICHAEL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1936), M.D. (N.Z.), D.P.H. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ. (N.Z.); med. offr. of health, 1917; asst. ch. health offr., 1920; dep. dir.-gen. of health, 1925; dir.-gen., 1931.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educ., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1918; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATTS, BERTRAM T., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1906-08; inspr. Egyptian govt. survey, 1912; on sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir., sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regir. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919, 1923 and 1926; regir. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish; title altered to dir., land registration and surveys, July, 1927; dir., surveys, land offr. and comsrs., mines, Uganda, 1930.

WATTS, CLIVE.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; ag. asst. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan., 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., for various periods, 1928-33; offr. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar. to June, 1931; Br. del. of Evcaf., Nov., 1933; treas., Mauritius, 1934.

WATTS, GUY TRACY.—B. 1892; ed. Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Apr., 1914; war serv., 1915-18; crown counsel, Nigeria, Mar., 1925; pol. mag., Calabar, 1928; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1930; chmn., bd. of educ., mem., leg. coun.; ag. ch. just., in 1930, 1931 and 1932; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1933.

WAUCHOPE, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ARTHUR GRNFELL, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), C.I.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1906).—B. 1874; ed. St. Ninian's, Moffat and Repton; 2nd lieut., 4th Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, 1893; 2nd Batt. The Black Watch, 1896; capt., 1901; served in Boer War (desps., Queen's med. with clasp, King's med. with two clasps); European War, 1914-18; major, 1914; commanded 2nd Batt. Black Watch in France and Mesopotamia, 34th Brigade and temporarily 7th divn., Indian Corps (desps.); bvt.-lieut.-col., afterwards col., 1922; major-gen., 1923; mily. mem., Overseas Stltmt. delegation to Australia and N.Z., 1923; ch. of Br. sec., mily. inter-allied coman. of control, Berlin, 1924-27; G.O.C., 44th Home Counties divn., T.A., 1927; G.O.C., Northern Ireland dist., 1929; high commr. and comdr.-in-ch., Palestine, 1931.

WAYLAND, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Schl., City of London Coll., R. Coll. of Science and R. Schl. of Mines; nat. sci. studentship (C.L.C.), 1906; nat. schl. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.Inst. M.M.; F.G.S., London, etc.; asst. min. survr., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. in 254 Tunelling Coy., R.E., as... forward demolition reconnaissance offr. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Boring Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir., geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; asst. sec., col. sec's office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. pvt. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29 and 1932; ag. ch. asst. sec. in 1930 and 1933; ag. commnr., in 1931, 1934 and 1935; commnr., Mar., 1935.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVELYN, C.M.G. (1934)—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's med. with 5 clasps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Prot., Apr., 1910; ag. dist. commnr., 1914; dist. commnr., 1919; ag. prov. commnr., 1924; lab. commnr., Uganda, Jan., 1925; ag. prov. commnr., Sept., 1925; prov. commnr., 2nd grade, 1927; prov. commnr., 1st grade, 1927; ag. ch. sec., 1932-33; ag. gov., 1935; seconded to Seychelles, 1935.
WEAVING, L. A.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, June, 1921; ag. sec., 1930.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres. dist. ct., Samaria, 1921; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933.

WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, June, 1923; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.

WEBB, OLIVER STANLEY, M.C., M.Inst., M. & Cy. E., M. I. Struct. E. Chtd. Struct. Engrn.—B. 1889; ed. Kingswood Coll., Grahamstown and Rhodes Univ. Coll., S. Africa; sapper, R.E., Dec., 1914; mentd. in desp., capt. commdg. 31st divan. signal co., R.E., Jan., 1919; asst. engrn., P.W.D., S.S., Feb., 1920; exec. engrn., Jan., 1927.

WEBB, SYMOYE HOPPE MITCHELL, B.A., B.L.—B. 1906; ed. Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen and Trinity Coll., Dublin; trop. African services course, Oxford, 1928-29; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1931; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1932.

WEBB, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1888; ed. Christ Coll., Blackheath and Guy's Hosp.—M.B. (hons.); B.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.S., (Eng.); L.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda, 1913; sp. serv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. capt., Uganda med. serv., 1914-16; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1918-19; senr. med. offr., 1923; spec. offr., med. supt., Mulago hosp., 1926; dep. dir. med. serv., Uganda, 1928; dir., med. services, Zanzibar, 1934.

WEBBER, MAJOR JOHN WASHBOURN, M.C. (R.A.R. of O.).—B. 1884; ed. Merchiston; posts and tels., Transvaal, 1903; Defence Force of Union of S. Africa, 1913-19; war serv., Natal Rebellion, 1910, German S.W. Africa, Egypt, France and Flanders, 1914-18; Gold Coast posts and tels., dist. survr., Feb., 1920; ch. acct., Apr., 1929; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, Jan., 1931.

WEBLEY, H. L.—B. 1884; ed. pvtly., Bloxham; E. African pol., Feb., 1912; served in European war, 1915-19; asst. commnr., pol., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1919; seconded to act as commnr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, Gambia, May, 1920; asst. commnr., pol., Gambia, 1920; ag. commnr., pol., etc. also as pvt. sec. and A.D.O. on several occasions, 1920-29; ag. crown pros. on several occasions, 1920-29; commnr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, June, 1929; King's pol. med., 1934.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admstr., Oct., 1916; adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. commnr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1926; offr. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. commnr., Feb., 1928; prov. commnr., junr. grade, May, 1929; senr. prov. commnr., Nov., 1934; offr. mem., leg. coun., 1935.

WEBSTER, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1887; Br. post office, 1903-14; war serv., 1915-20; twice ment. in desp.; asst. dir., postes and tels., Palestine, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G., numerous occasions; dep. P.M.G., 1935.
WEBSTER, H. J.—Asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audt., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Nov., 1929.

WEBSTER, CAPT. JOHN SUTTON, M.B., Bach. Surg. (Vict. Univ. Manch.), D.P.H., D.M.R.E.—B. 1884; med. offr., S.S., July, 1909; grade I, Apr., 1910; med. offr. for surg. wk., gen. hosps., Penang, July, 1910; cls. IV, Jan., 1914; med. offr., P.W., Sept., 1914; W.O., Apr., 1915 (mentd. in desps.); prof. of med., Coll. of Med., S'pore, Sept., 1922; offr. deleg., 6th Cong. Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Tokyo, Sept., 1925; radiologist, S.S., Apr., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, in addn., Apr.-June, 1935.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matare, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settlmt. offr., Jan., 1911; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settlmt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settlmt. offr., July, 1920; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. gov't. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; gov't. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929; cls. I, grade I, 1931; gov't. agt., W. Prov., Mar., 1931; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; offr. appld. to report on poor relief, Aug., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1934; ag. ch. sec., Oct., 1934.

WEGENER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.A. Inst. C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Crosley Schl., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rly., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S.rly., 1922; worksman, loco. dept., F.M.S. rly., July, 1928; asst. ch. mech., engrn., Oct., 1932; ag. ch. mech. engrn., July, 1933.

WEIGALL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ERNEST GEORGE ARCHIBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Horncastle Div., Lincs., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem., select comtee. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., fedrn. of county agric. comtes.; J.F., Kent and Linca.; gov., S. Australia, 1920-22.

WEIR, ANDREW HERON WILSON, B.Sc. Edin.—B. 1893; ed. Viewpark Sch., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (R.F.A., U.K., India and Mesopotamia); men. in desps.; aast. conserv., forests, Nigeria, Aug., 1920; conserv., forests, col. forest serv., May, 1935; ag. ch. conserv., forests, Nigeria, July, 1935; author of "Gum Arabic in Bornu Province," and "Report on Gum Arabic in Anglo-Egyptian Sudan."

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripom.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ast. dist. offr., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., May, 1920; mag., July, 1922; ag. aast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929; attd. pub. trustee's office, Dec., 1930; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., offr. cls. II, Nov., 1931; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1933.
WELBY, H. E.—Aast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; ag. prov. comsnr., 1930-31; senr. dist. comsnr., 1933; prov. comsnr., 1934.
WELCH, T. B.—B. 1898; ed. Brighton Coll.; ast. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; ast. divnl. supt. (loc.), 1927.

WELLINGTON, ANTHUR ROBERTS, C.M.G. (1934), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.P.H. (Camb.)—B. 1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1905; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selaungar, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., Mar., 1919; offr. del. to 4th cong., Far Eastern Assn., Trop. Med., Java, Aug., 1921; also offr. del., 5th cong., Spore, Sept., 1923; offr. del., L. of N. Eastern Health Confce., Spore, Feb., 1925; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1925; offr. del., 6th cong., F.E.A.T.M., Japan, Sept., 1925; offr. rep., L. of N. interchange of health offrs. in Japan, Korea and Manchuria, Nov., 1925; offr. mem. advisory coun., L. of N. Eastern Health Bureau, Spore, Jan., 1926; offr. del. for F.M.S. and S.S. at Internat. Health Confce., Melbourne, Dec., 1926; on off. duty, Panama, Feb., 1927; ch. health offr., F.M.S., Feb., 1927; offr. del. Far Eastern Assn., Trop. Med., India, Dec., 1927; offr. mem., L. of N. Eastern Health Bureau, Delhi, Dec., 1927; dir. med. and san. services, Hong Kong, 1929.

WELLS, THOMAS EUSTACE, A.M.In.T.—B. 1888; ed. Lincoln Cathedral Choir Sch.; statnmr., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1913; ast. traffic inspr., Tampi, Sept., 1915; wharf supt., Prai, Oct., 1916; ast. traffic man., July, 1918; ast. traffic man., July, 1926; senr. ast. traffic man., July, 1927; ast. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., Jan., 1932.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; prof. assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. ast. atty-gen., Cape, 1918; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1921; law advr., 1926; atty-gen., Transvaal, 1929.

WELSH, GORDON JAMES.—B. 1894; ed. Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schol., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pte., 1/4th Batt. The Buffs and 2nd lieut., The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; clk. to govr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. clk., Apr., 1923; ast. sec. to govt., Somaliland, Mar., 1929; ast. dist. comsnr., Burao dist., Aug., 1929; ast. sec. in ch., secretariat on various occasions, 1929-1934.
WENTWORTH, M. F. G., B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., London (teacher's dipl., Lond.); inspr., schls., Aug., 1930.
WERE, MARY JOSEPHINE, L.R.C.P., S.L., D.P.H.—Lady med. offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; do., infant welfare centre, May, 1923.

WESSELS, RT. HON. SIR JOHANNES WILHELMUS, P.C., Kt. Bach.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town, Downing Coll., Cambridge, Middle Temple; admitted to Cape bar, 1886; Transvaal bar, 1887; legal adviser to Lord Roberts, 1900-01; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; judge pres., Transvaal prov. divn., 1920; judge of appl., 1923; ch. just., S. Africa, 1932.

WEST, HAROLD.—B. 1882; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath; land surrv., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1910; lieut., R.G.A., 1917-19; 1st cls. land surrv., 1920; prin. land surrv., Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; 1st ast. supt., crown lands, 1926; ast. supt., crown lands, 1932.
WESTON, BERTRAM JOHN, M.Sc. (Agr.) S. Africa, M.A. (Cantab), F.R.H.S.—B. 1907; ed. Ashford Grammar and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; horticulturist, Cyprus, 1931.

WESTON, ERNEST ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1880; B.A. Cantab., 1902; served with R.E. during Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1914; lieut.-col., 1916; ast. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1907 and served in various posts under Nigerian govt. until 1927 when retd. on med. grounds; on staff of Imp. Economic Comte., 1927-31; comsnr., Cayman Is., Dec., 1931; comsnr., Cyprus, 1933.

WESTON, HARRY CHARLES, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond., Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war serv., 1914-19; ch. ast. engrnr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engrnr., Dar-es-Salaam, 1925; prin., Acra Tech. Schl., May, 1929.

WHEATLEY, MARK.—B. 1888; ed. Nottingham Univ. Coll.; ast. mast., Victoria Inst., Selaungar, Jan., 1911; headmast., govt. Eng. Schl., K. Pilah, Apr., 1927; headmast., King George V Schl., Seremban, Jan., 1928; do., Batu Rd. Schl., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1930; ag. headmast., Maxwell Schl., K. Lumpur, in addn., July-Dec., 1930.

WHEBBELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; on war serv., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in desps., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W. M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; ast. comdt., pol., Bahamas, Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., Aug., 1926.

WHEELER, LEONARD RICHMOND, M.Sc., B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1888; science master, Letchworth Schl., 1909-12; Gram. Schl., Antigua, 1912-14; ent. ovl. serv. as science master, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad Dec., 1914; oomn. in 1st batt., B.W.I.R., from 1st Trinidad contingent, Sept., 1915; pilot, R.A.F., 1917, served in Egypt and Salonica, 1916-19; returned to Trinidad, 1920; coman. for Boy Scouts, Trinidad, 1920; transf. to S.S. and F.M.S. as European mast., Nov., 1921; science and maths. mast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1921; vice-prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., T. Malim.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Thomas, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls, Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario lawclsh.; appd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.
WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Miny. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cler. offr., C.O., 24th Apr., 1922.
WHITEHEAD, John.—B. 1894; ed. High Schl. and Tech. Coll., Greenock; associate mem., of Inst. of Naval Architects; naval architect, Nigerian marine dept., 1928.

WHITEHORN, Roger Herbert, B.A. Cantab.—B., 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1911; attd. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1911; attd. Colombokach., Aug., 1912; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th ast. col. sec., Sept., 1914; on military duty as ast. censor, Nov., 1914; 4th ast. col. sec., Aug., 1916; priv. sec. to gov. in addn. Nov., 1916; on military duty, 1918; extra ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1922; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1924; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, 1924; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1927; chmn., munic. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1932; tea export contr., May, 1933; govt. agt., W. Prov. in addn., Oct.-Nov., 1934.

WHITELEY, Gerald Charles.—B. 1891; ed. Bradford and Jesus Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); 2nd cls. hons. mods., 3rd cls. lit. hum.; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. res., Muri prov., 1925; admstv. offr., cls. I, Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1931; ag. ch. sec., June, 1931; dep. ch. sec., 1934; ag. ch. sec. in 1934 and 1935.
WHITLAMSMITH, Gerard Kime, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Westminster and Christ Ch., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons., hist., 1925; admstv. offr. (cadet), Tanganyika Territory, 1926; ast. dist. offr., 1928.

WHITLEY, Major Norman Henry Pownall, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1883; called to bar; N.Circuit, 1907; on war serv., 7th Manchester Regt., T.F., 1914-18; demob. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; dep. pub. pros., S.S., June, 1921; puisne judge, S.S., Feb., 1930; judge, F.M.S., Feb., 1930; puisne judge, S'pore, Nov., 1930; ag. sr. puisne judge, Penang, Apr., 1931; puisne judge, S'pore, Apr., 1932; do., Penang, Mar., 1934.
WHITLEY, W., B.A. (Oxf.), A.I.C.—B. 1902; analytical chem., Tanganyika Territory 1928.

WHITTAKER, Harry Stanley, A.M.I.M. & C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1882; prev. serv., Br. Honduras, 1911-20; ast. engrnr., Br. Guiana, 1920; seconded sea defences, 1920-22; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., 1922; dist. commr., in addn., 1933-38.

WHITTING, Charles Edward Jewell—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, July, 1922; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1931; supt. educn., Nigeria, 1933.
WHITTINGDALE, William.—B. 1885; ch. draughtman and compr., surveys, Cape, Mar., 1917; prof. ast., Sept., 1920; 1st grade prof. asst., trig. survey, Nov., 1928; dir., trig. survey, May, 1933.

WHITTINGHAM, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub-inspr., pol., Br. Guiana, 1924; ex. officio J.P., 1925; dist. inspr., 1927; ag. commnr., N.W. dist. in 1929; county inspr., Dec., 1932.

WHITTLE, REGINALD ALFRED, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. math. mods., 1919; B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served in France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda, 1921; asst. sec., 1922; senr. asst. sec., 1932.

WHYBRO, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhainsted Schl., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1922; law tripes, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WHYTE-SMITH, THOMAS STODART.—B. 1883; ed. Merchiston Castle Schl. and Edinburgh Univ.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., the Royal Scots, 1915; lieut., 1916; capt., K.A.R., 1918; served in Nyassaland and Portuguese E. Africa, 1917-19; 2nd asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1923; J.P., 1924; asst. crown solr., 1926; ag. mag., Kowloon, 1929 and 1930; asst. crown solr., 1930; ag. land off. and regisr., marriages, 1934; land off., 1935.

WICKHAM, CAPT. DENIS HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; Connaught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., France, 1915-18; mentd. in desps.; transf'd to Queen's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th K.A.R. Kenya, 1922-25; admstve. offr., Kenya col.; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

WICKHAM, DUDLEY LEE BOSSERT.—B. 1909; ed. Br. Guiana and Barbados; cler. asst. secretariat, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1927; asst. clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1930; 4th cls. clk., secretariat, Jan., 1933; prte. sec. to O. A. G., May to Oct., 1932; ag. clk., leg. coun., June and July, 1933; has been secy. to various comtees. and bds.; clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1935.

WICKHART, W. A. F.—B. 1888; clk. Crown Agents' office, 1906; asst. head, gen. dept., Mar., 1922; do., stores dept., July, 1931; dep. head, shipping dept., Nov., 1934; asst. sec., W. African currency bd., June, 1914; secy., Dec., 1918.

WICKRAMASINGHE, REGINALD HUGH, R.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1930; attd. Badulla kach., Nov., 1930; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1932; do., Kegalla kach., Aug., 1934.

WICKRAMASINHA, SENA PANDUKABHAYA, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; attd. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office asst., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1927; ditto, Point Pedro, Apr., 1929; dist. judge, Tangalle, 1932; asst. stintm. offr., Apr., 1934; dep. rubber contr. in addn., May, 1934.

WICKREMESINGHE, CYRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; att., Badulla kach., Apr., 1912; Matara kach. Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; addnl. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; asst. gov't. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928; ag. gov't. agt., N.C.P., July, 1931; addnl. commnr., lands, Sept., 1934.

WIDDUP, MAJOR CHARLES PONSONBY, V.D.—B. 1884; clerical asst., treasy., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub.-inspr. of police, 1904; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustan exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen., and supt., fire brig. B. Guiana, Dec., 1920; internat. prison confce., 1925; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces in 1924, 1926, 1927, 1930 and 1933; ag. inspr., prisons in addn., July Nov., 1933; King's pol. med., 1934.

WIGGETT, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS.—B. 1884; law and income tax depts., Cape Col., 1901-10; census office, 1911; accts. br., dept. of int., 1912; income tax dept., 1914; inland rev. dept., 1916; recr., rev., Kimberley, 1931; acct., 1931; recr., rev., E. London, 1932; ch. clk., income tax, Feb., 1933; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1933; ch. rev. offr., June, 1934.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, Apr., 1914; ag. res. commnr., 1926.

WIGLEY, WILFRED MURRAY, O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical apptmts. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; 1st puisne judge, Leeward Is., Jan., 1936.
WILCOCKS, C., M.D., Ch.B. (Manch.)—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1927; tuberculosis research offr., 1930.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLEER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Comm'l Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmith including Jaggersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rly. and harbs. bd., 1924; pub. debt. commnr., 1925; admtr., O.F.S., Mar., 1929.

WILFORD, SIR THOMAS MASON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1929).—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-29; chmn., Wellington harbr. bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; leader of opposition and liberal party, 1920-25; min. of just. and defence, 1928-29; high commnr. for N.Z. in London, 1929-33.
WILKIN, B. O., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Edin.).—B. 1889; mily. serv., 1914-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1926.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war serv. (Navy), 1914 to 1919; asst. dist. commnr., G. Coast, July, 1919; dist. commnr., April, 1924.
WILKINSON, PAUL BIDDULPH, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond., M.B., B.S., Lond., M.R.C.P., Lond.—B. 1901; W.A.M.S., 1930; 2nd med. offr., St. Helena, 1934.
WILKINSON, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1906; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1929; att., Kandy kach., Jan., 1930; office asst., Jaffna kach., Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Puttalam, Oct., 1934.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1896; ed. Kendall Gram. Schl. and Jesua Coll., Oxford (open exhib.), lieut., R.G.A.; M.O., June, 1917; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1923; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1927; ag. regis., dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Jan., 1932; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1932; ag. dep. legal advr., F.M.S., July, 1934.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schol. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, Jan., 1912.

WILLBOURN, ERIC STEWART, M.A. (Cantab.), F.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. geologist, F.M.S., May, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.E. (Signal), Apr., 1918; demob., May, 1919; ag. geologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1922-Jly., 1923 and Jly., 1927-Feb., 1928; dir. geology survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1931.

WILCOCKS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice ment. in despatches); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; (placed on R. of O.); A.D.C., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., May, 1928 to Sept., 1930; asst. commr., prisons, Apr., 1932; commr., prisons, 1933.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B., O.B.E.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926; dep. D.M.S., 1928; dir. med. and sanry. services, Nyasaland, 1933.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; asst. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lancs.; inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rly., 1898; Crown Agent's office, 1900; asst. man., Robt. Stephenson, loco. wks., Darlington, 1901; dep. ch. inspgr. engrn., Crown Agts., 1904; staff capt. W.O., 1915; section dir., Miny. of Munitions, 1916; major, R.E., D.A.D., W.O., 1918; tech. head of engrng. inspection dept., C.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspgr. engrn., 1922; pres. Inst. of Loco. Engrs., 1933-34.

WILLIAMS, MAJOR C. T., O.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. John's Leatherhead; late 1st Batt. The E. Surrey Regt.; ret. with rank of major, 1925; serv. in France, Belgium, Italy and Russia; twice ment. in despatches; adjt., N. Territories constab., Apr., 1926; ag. comdt., N.T.C., for various periods; commr., pol., Gold Coast on disbandment of N.T.C., Apr., 1929; staff offr., Gold Coast pol., June, 1931.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES HOLMAN BARKER, M.A., Dip. Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1897; agronomist and later dir., Guadeloupe sugar planters' expt. station; agronomist, Sophia expt. station, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1929.
WILLIAMS, D. L. Gwynne.—B. 1886; solr. sup. ct., Eng., 1914; served European War, 1915-18; ot. martial offr., 1918; staff capt. (legal), 1919; conveyancer and regis. of titles, Uganda, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BRIDGEWATER.—B. 1886; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1910; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Sept., 1918; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Coast, Nov., 1920; collr., land rev., Seremban, Mar., 1922; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1926; pub. trustee, F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1927; offr. assignee, F.M.S., May, 1927; offr., cls. I.B. legal advr. and judic. commr., Kelantan, June, 1928; commr., lands, F.M.S., Apr., 1932; ag. legal advr. in addn., Jan., 1933; regis., companies and offr. assignee, S.S., Jan., 1934.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREGEAR, B.A. (Oxon)—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1913; attd., col. secs. office, Dec., 1913; dist. offr., Oct., 1917; offr., cls. IV., S.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 1st astt. sec. (A), S.S., May, 1924; offr. cls. III., Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; ag. commr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1932; offr., cls. IB, 1934.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; asst. to sec., for Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. dist. offr., New Territories, Aug., 1928; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, 1932; ch. astt. to sec., Chinese affrs., Apr., 1935.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST STUART, M.A.—B. 1896; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; prin., diocesan boys' high schl., Rangoon, 1924-25; lect. and examr., Rangoon Univ., 1925-26; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, FRANK LESLIE, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Perak, Dec., 1921; passed cadet, May, 1924; offr., cls. V., asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1924; attchd. Chinese prot., Ipoh, July, 1925; do., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1927; do., S'pore, Aug., 1927; offr., cls. IV.; dist. offr., X'mas Is., Nov., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese Selangor, and Pahang, July, 1929; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1933; prot., Chinese, Kedah, Mar., 1935.

WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. asst. audr., July, 1924; ag. dep. audr., on various occasions from 1926; ag. audr., Mar. to Aug., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Palestine, Apr., 1931; auditor, Nyasaland, 1935.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1902; commr., income tax, Barbados, 1924; judge, asst. ct. of appl., and inspr., prisons, 1925; ac. atty.-gen., and ch. just., various periods, 1928-34.

WILLIAMS, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough, McGill Univ., Montreal and Cambridge; colonial exhibitioner, Trinity Coll., 1924-26; 2nd cls., nat. sci. tripos, pt. I., 1927; 2nd cls. pt. II., 1928, B.A. 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 17th Oct., 1929; seconded to N. Rhodesia admstr., 1935.
WILLIAMS, J. H., M.I.C.E., M.A.M.Soc.C.E.—B. 1873; res. engrn., Singapore govt. rly.; engrn.-in-ch., Canton-Hankow rly.; res. engrn., Benue Bridge constrn., Nigeria, 1928.

WILLIAMS, OWEN GEORGE REVELL, C.M.G. (1938)—B. 1886; ed. Lancing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. moda., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls.

WILLIS, MAURICE A., M.B.E. (1933)—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. offi., 1st Jan., 1920; transf., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf., C.O., May, 1924; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.

WILLIS, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. London; olk., treasy. and cust. house, Turks and Caicos Is., Jan., 1925; ag. ast. treasy., postmtr. collr. of cust., regis. of shipping and gen. man., savings bank, May, 1928 to Aug., 1928; clk. to comsrr., Aug., 1928.

WILLMOT, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1881; ed. Lancang Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; ast. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. ast., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engrn.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rlyw. constrn., Dec., 1913; served with 4th K.A.R. in E. African Campaign, May, 1915 to Dec., 1918 (twice ment. in desps.); ag. D.P.W., Oct., 1920 to Apr., 1922; D.D.P.W., June, 1923; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1923-24, 1926-27 and 1929; D.P.W., 1933.

WILLMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P., V.D.—Asst. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913; senr. ast. health offr., Union of S. Africa and ast. dir., med. services (defence) ; col., S.A. Med. Corps.

WILLOUGHBY, GODFREY POUNTNEY, M.Sc. Tech., M.Sc., M.I.R.E.—B. 1888; ed. Taunton Schl. and Victoria Univ., Manchester; B.Sc., 1st cl. hons., elect. engng., 1909; Schuster Schol., 1909; tech. ast., post office, Union of S. Africa, Mar., 1913; lect., elect. engng., phys., math., Durban Tech. Coll., Apr., 1919; ast. engrn. and tech. educn. offr., post office, Union of S. Africa, Dec., 1920; elect. inspr., Kenya, and ch. teleg. engrn., Kenya and Uganda, Apr., 1926; ch. tel. engrn., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1933.

WILLS, DANIEL.—B. 1898; ed. King's, Rochester; R.M.C., Sandhurst; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1921; ag. ast., supt., govt. monop., S'pore., Feb., 1922; ag. ast. to air dist. offr., P. Wellesley, Nov., 1923; offr. cls. V, Oct., 1924; ag. sec. rubber restrn., Johore, Apr., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Oct., 1928; ast. advr., K. Tinggi; do., Kukup, Apr., 1930; offr. assignee, Penang, Apr., 1931; ast. dist. offr. B. Mortajam, Aug., 1931; offr. cls. III, 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1933.
WILLS, JOSEPH ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1894; on war serv., R.F.A., France and Italy; ast. consr., forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1921; provl. forest offr., Oct., 1931.

WILMOT, AUBREY CHARLTON.—Cape civ. serv., 1900; transf., Transvaal, 1902; ch. olk., mast. sup. ct., 1910; ch. olk., land bank, 1912; ast. gen. man., 1921; gen. man., 1924; man. dir., 1930; chmn., farmers' spec. relief bd., 1931; chmn., farmers' assistance bd., 1935; chmn., immigrts. selection bd., 1935.

WILSON, CECIL SAMUEL, B.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Dub. Univ.)—B. 1897; med. offr., F.M.S., Jly., 1925; surg., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; surg., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1931; do., Perak, July, 1932 and Nov., 1933; do., Selangor, Aug., 1933.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES, Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns).—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (siz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V, May, 1916; lieut., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in desp., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., M.V.I., Perak, Apr., 1921; emigr. agt. in India, Aug., 1922; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; acomp. contr., lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1930; ag. regist., sup. ct., S'pore, Jan., 1931; ag. offl. assignee and regiar., coys., S.S., Dec., 1932; offr., cls. I.B., 1934; emigr. comanr., Malaya, Madras, Oct., 1934.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and olk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. colir., cust., May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. comanr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. res. comanr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, D. R., M.B., B.Ch. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.
WILSON, DONALD BAGSTER, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar. 1929.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed Amersham Gram. Schl.; olk. acctnt-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, FRANK KERSHAW, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1891; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; attd. ool. sect., Dec., 1914; attd., censor's office, S'pore, Jly., 1915; supt., posts and tels. and ast. supt. lands, K. Bahrul, Aug., 1917; sheriff and dep. regist., sup. ot., S'pore, Oct., 1925; chmn., rubber assessment comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1936; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1929; ag. regist., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; mag., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1933.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, O.B.E. (1935). B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec's. off., 1904; ag. ast. sec., ch. sec's. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. comanr., on various occasions, 1923-35; ch. colir., cust., and excise, July, 1924 (now styled comprtr., cust. and inland rev.); regist., Br. ships, regist., Cyprus ships, impr., fisheries; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31; regist., partnerships, Apr., 1929.
WILSON, GEOFFREY STUDHOLME.—B. 1913; ed. Radley Coll.; pol. probr., Hong Kong, Oct., 1932; ast. supt. pol., Mar. 1935.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; ast. dist. comanr., G. Coast, Aug., 1914; dist. comanr., Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy, capt., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1927.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1885; called to bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cota., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high commr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., in 1903, 1904 and 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (rep. O.R.C. on rly. comitee); ret.; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.
WILSON, J., M.A.—B. 1903; mast., educn. dept., N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1929; inspr., schls., Apr., 1931.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN WOLSELEY, M.C., V.D.—B. 1876; man., Tobago farm, 1899; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., 1908; inspr., 1907; senr. inspr., 1916; served with Imp. Army, 1914-19; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., 1920; ag. commdt., local forces on several occasions.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. RT. HON. SIR LESLIE ORME, G.C.S.I. (1929), G.C.I.E. (1923), P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1900), etc.—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1895; lieut., 1896; capt., 1901; served S. Africa, 1899-01; (severely wounded), desps., Queen's med. 5 clasps, D.S.O.; A.D.C. to Gov., N.S.W., 1903-09; temp. lieut.-col., R.M. and served through Galipoli operations, 1914-15 (desp.); served in France (severely wounded), 1915-16; M.P. (Reading), 1913-22; S. Portsmouth, 1922-23; partly. ast. sec. to war cabinet, 1918; chmn., national maritime bd., 1919; partly. sec. to miny. of shipping, 1919; partly. sec. to treasy. and ch. unionist whip, 1921-23; Gov., Bombay, 1923-28; Gov., Queensland, 1932.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WHIR.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schl., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; ast. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, July, 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. comanr., 1921; dist. comanr., Aug., 1928; ag. ast. ch. col. sec., Apr.-July, 1935.

WILSON, MARK.—B. 1896; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; first schol. (hist. and pol. sci.), 1921; B.A. (senr. modr. and gold medallist), 1921; LL.B., 1922; served with R.A.F., 1918-9; called to bar, King's Inns, Dublin (1st ols. hon. at final examn.), 1924; cadet, admin. serv., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; mag., Uganda, 1926; ag. admstr.-genl. and offl. recr. on various occasions, 1926-9; ag. puime judge for various periods, 1933-4; chan., Uganda diocease, 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1934.
WILSON, PERCIVAL DOUGLAS, M.Inst.C.K.—B. 1887; ed. Swansea Gram. Schl.; ast. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engnr., 1924.

WILSON, REGINALD CHARLES, D.Sc., F.G.S., Assoc.Inst.M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers and R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese E. Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geological survey, Nigeria, 1919-27; dir., 1927; publna. on E. Africa and Nigerian Geology.

WILSON, RONALD ERIC.—B. 1896; ed. Faversham and Sandhurst; cadet, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; attchd. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Jan., 1922; doc., cust. dept., Aug., 1922; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1923; passel cadet, Dec. 1923; ast. ast. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1924; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1925; 4th ass't sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr. cls. IV, sec. to res., Pahang, Sept., 1928; ass't dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Oct., 1930; do., Kuantan, Oct., 1932; offr., cls. III, Sept., 1933; sec. to res. N. Sembilan, May, 1934.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B. (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff cffr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 19th Nov., 1918; gov., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1926; ret., 1933.
WILSON, WILLIAM GLASSFORD WALKER, M.I.L.—B. 1894; ed. Kilmarnock Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; asst. loco. suptd., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. ch. mech. engr., 1929.

WILTSHIRE, ERIC.—B. 1893; ed. Monmouth Grammar Schl.; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; dist. engrnr., Nov., 1929; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1931.

WILTSHIRE, H. G., M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; exhib., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (coll. prize); L.S.T.M. certif. with distinc. (2nd place); war serv., 1914-19; France, Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (1914 Star with clasp); bacteriological specialist, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1924; do., Zanzibar, 1930; pathologist, 1932.
WINSER, CYRIL LEGH, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govs. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, VICTOR HANOTT, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Titfins Schl., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrg.); 2nd cls. engr., G.P.O., engrg. dept., Oct., 1909; asst. engrn., July, 1911; suptd., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; suptd., posts and tels., Jan., 1922; senr. engrn., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1930; asst. engrn.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1930; engrn.-in-ch., do., 1933; dir., tels. and phones, and engrn.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934.

WINSTEADT, SIR RICHARD OLAF, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comtee. for Malay studies in addn. Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., asst. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., June, 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., S'pore, June, 1921; offr. as sec. to high comsnr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. Ia, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comtee. in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech. educn. comtee. in addn., Nov., 1925; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. conferee., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comtee., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malayan delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. conferee., June, 1930; gen. advr., Johore, Oct., 1931; ret., 1935; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.

WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan.-Apr., 1927; senr. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934.

WISE, KENRICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C. Lond., Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and regisr.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919.

WISE, WILFRED, M.Inst.C.E., B.Sc. (Hons. Lond.), M.I.Mun.Cy.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ed. pvtly. and City and Guilds of London Cent. Tech. Coll., Univ. of Lond.; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Fiji, May, 1921; ag. comsnr., wks., Aug., 1923; comsnr., wks., Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Tonga, May-July, 1930; chmn., cent. road bd., M.L.C.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept. Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comtee., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comtee., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comtee., May, 1917; priv. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Amery), 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; accompanied parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Hon. E. F. L. Wood) on visit to W. Indies, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; rep. U.K. govt. at W. Indian confee., Ottawa, June-July, 1925; asst. sec., 18th Sept., 1927; D.O., 1928.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, O.B.E. (1932), M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WOODEHOUSE, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSCELIN HENEGAR, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsnr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; comdl. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toaki; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and claps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanian; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, R.E., O.B.E., M.D., D.P.H.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls. and Toronto Univ.; on mil. serv., Canada, France and England, 1914-19; prov. dist. med. offr., Fort William and Port Arthur in 1912 and again in 1919; exec. sec., Canadian Tuberculosis Assoc., 1921; dep. min., dept. of pensions and national health, Dom. of Canada, 1933.

WOODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office astt. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; astt. commr. of excise attd., the office, excise commr., Apr., 1913; astt. commr., excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; astt. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; attd., ch. censor's office, Sept., 1915; astt. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. commr., excise, Nov., 1926; commr., excise, Sept., 1927; govt. agt., S.P., Nov., 1930; prin. collr., cust., and chmn., Colombo port commr., Nov., 1931; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1934 to Mar., 1935.

WOHLHANN, WARD GEORGE, M.V.O. (1935), I.S.O. (1934).—B. 1873; ed. N.Z. state schls.; N.Z. permanent army, 1894-95; N.Z. pol., 1895; sergt., 1906; senr. sergt., 1914; sub-instr., 1918; inspr., 1921; supt., 1926; commr., 1930; was commr. of pol., Samoa, 1920-22.

WOLFE, HENRY, O.B.E. (1935), M.Sc. (Agric.), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. astt. dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Kenya, 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1933.

WOLFE, PHINEAS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Acad.; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land astt., 1919; registrar, titles, doc. and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOMACK, OSWALD CRICIC, P.A.S.I.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Leeds Univ.; temp. capt., R.A.S.C. and Rifle Brig., 1915-20; astt. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1921-24; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; land survr., do., 1932.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; astt. conserv. Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1918; conserv. of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; reverted to Indian forest serv., Oct., 1927; conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., bd. of examrs., Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ag. off. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928; chmn., local govt. bd., 1929-30; off. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1930; do., leg. coun., Jan., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1932.
WOOD, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—B. 1887; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Schl., Wakefield; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Kong Kong, 1914; 1st cls. ditto, 1921.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); astt. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. astt., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. astt., ion observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.
WOOD, JAMES HERBERT, A.M.I.Struct.E., etc.—B. 1897; astt. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1921; ag. senr. exec. engr., 1929; senr. exec. engr., 1934.

WOOD, CAPT. JOHN CALDER, M.C., F.R.G.S., R. of O., R.E., Qual. Land Surveyor, S. Africa—B. 1886; ed. Robt. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; astt., R.
